  database_type: 'Databricks Unity Catalog'
  Note: 'Lakehouse data model for Pharmaceuticals - Version 1'
}

Table discovery.molecular_target {
  molecular_target_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the molecular target record in the discovery data product. Primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.']
  fto_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.fto_analysis. Business justification: FTO analysis is conducted on molecular targets before committing R&D investment. IP teams run target-level FTO to assess freedom to work on a biological target. A pharma IP expert would expect this li']
  indication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Molecular targets are validated for specific disease indications. Linking to product.indication enables indication-level target portfolio analysis, competitive intelligence reporting, and alignment of']
  owning_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: Target ownership by research org unit drives portfolio reporting, resource allocation, and therapeutic area governance. Pharma domain experts expect molecular targets to be formally assigned to a depa']
  patent_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: Target-level IP strategy is managed at the patent family level for global coverage assessment. IP portfolio reports group target protection by family. molecular_target already links to a single patent']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Molecular targets are patented inventions requiring patent protection tracking for freedom-to-operate analysis, portfolio management, and competitive intelligence in target selection decisions.']
  primary_patent_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_claim. Business justification: Specific patent claims cover target modulation methods; claim-level tracking required for FTO clearance, licensing negotiations, and assessing blocking IP in target validation stage gates.']
  therapeutic_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: Molecular targets are classified and prioritized within therapeutic area programs. Linking to the master therapeutic_area entity enables TA-level target portfolio reporting, competitive landscape anal']
  competitive_landscape_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of known competitive programs targeting this molecular target, including approved drugs, clinical-stage compounds, and disclosed research programs from competitor organizations. Business-confidential intelligence used for portfolio strategy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this molecular target record was first created in the discovery data product. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.']
  discovery_program_code varchar [note: 'Internal program or project code under which this target is being investigated. Links the target to the broader drug discovery program for portfolio management, resource allocation, and P&L tracking.']
  disease_association varchar [note: 'Specific disease or condition(s) associated with this molecular target, as supported by published literature or internal research data. May reference ICD-10/ICD-11 disease codes or MeSH disease terms.']
  druggability_assessment varchar [note: 'Scientific assessment of the target\'s amenability to small molecule or biologic modulation based on structural, biochemical, and computational analyses. Key input to portfolio prioritization and NCE/NBE strategy decisions.']
  eln_notebook_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the primary Electronic Lab Notebook (ELN) experiment record in IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics that documents the original target identification and characterization data. Provides full scientific audit trail.']
  ensembl_gene_code varchar [note: 'Ensembl stable gene identifier (e.g., ENSG00000146648) for the gene encoding the molecular target. Used for genomic cross-referencing, variant analysis, and bioinformatics pipelines.']
  expression_tissue varchar [note: 'Primary tissue(s) or cell type(s) where this molecular target is expressed at therapeutically relevant levels, as determined by transcriptomic or proteomic profiling (e.g., liver, lung epithelium, T-cells). Informs tissue selectivity and delivery strategy.']
  first_disclosed_date date [note: 'Date on which this molecular target was first formally disclosed or documented in the internal ELN or target identification database. Establishes the scientific priority date for IP purposes.']
  gene_name varchar [note: 'Official HGNC (HUGO Gene Nomenclature Committee) approved gene symbol encoding the molecular target (e.g., EGFR, TP53, BRCA1). Standard identifier used in regulatory submissions and scientific publications.']
  genetic_association_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Quantitative score (0.0000–1.0000) representing the strength of genetic evidence linking this target to the associated disease, derived from GWAS, Mendelian randomization, or Open Targets Platform data. Higher scores indicate stronger genetic validation.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent scientific or portfolio review of this target record. Ensures data currency and supports periodic target triage processes.']
  moa_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the molecular mechanism by which modulation of this target is expected to produce a therapeutic effect. Captures agonism, antagonism, inhibition, activation, or allosteric modulation details as documented in ELN.']
  moa_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the pharmacological mechanism of action for compounds acting on this target. Used to group targets by intervention strategy in SAR analysis and portfolio reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: agonist|antagonist|inhibitor|activator|allosteric_modulator|degrader|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modality varchar [note: 'Preferred drug modality for targeting this molecular target based on its structural and biochemical properties. Determines the NCE (New Chemical Entity) or NBE (New Biological Entity) classification of resulting compounds. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: small_molecule|biologic|antibody|peptide|oligonucleotide|cell_therapy|gene_therapy|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  novelty_status varchar [note: 'Classification of the target\'s novelty relative to the competitive landscape. Novel targets represent first-in-class opportunities; established targets have existing approved drugs; repurposed targets are being explored in new indications.']
  organism varchar [note: 'Biological organism in which the molecular target is expressed and studied. Typically Homo sapiens for human targets, but may include model organisms used in preclinical validation studies.']
  patent_landscape_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary of the patent landscape around this molecular target, including freedom-to-operate (FTO) considerations and known composition-of-matter or method-of-use patents. Supports IP strategy decisions.']
  pathway_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary biological signaling or metabolic pathway in which this target participates (e.g., PI3K/AKT/mTOR, RAS/MAPK, JAK/STAT). Supports pathway-based target clustering and polypharmacology analysis.']
  pdb_code varchar [note: 'RCSB Protein Data Bank accession code for the primary 3D structure of this molecular target. Used by computational chemistry and structure-based drug design (SBDD) teams for molecular docking and virtual screening.']
  prioritization_tier varchar [note: 'Portfolio prioritization tier assigned to this target by the Target Selection Committee, reflecting strategic importance, scientific confidence, and commercial potential. Tier 1 targets receive maximum resource allocation.']
  protein_structure_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a resolved 3D protein structure (X-ray crystallography, cryo-EM, or NMR) is available for this target in the Protein Data Bank (PDB) or internal structural biology repository. Directly impacts structure-based drug design (SBDD) feasibility.']
  safety_concern_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of identified safety liabilities associated with modulation of this target (e.g., hERG channel activity, hepatotoxicity risk, immunosuppression). Populated only when safety_concern_flag is True.']
  safety_concern_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether known safety liabilities or toxicological concerns have been identified for this target based on literature, genetic association studies, or internal preclinical data. Triggers mandatory safety review before progression.']
  selectivity_profile varchar [note: 'Assessment of the target\'s selectivity relative to related family members or off-target proteins. Informs compound design strategy to minimize off-target toxicity and maximize therapeutic index.']
  target_class varchar [note: 'Pharmacological class of the molecular target based on its biochemical function and structural family. Drives druggability assessment strategy and compound screening cascade design. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GPCR|kinase|nuclear_receptor|ion_channel|protease|phosphatase|epigenetic_regulator|transcription_factor|transporter|other — promote to reference product]']
  target_code varchar [note: 'Internally assigned alphanumeric business identifier for the molecular target, used as the externally-known unique code across systems (ELN, LIMS, CTMS). Distinct from the surrogate PK and from external database accession numbers.']
  target_engagement_assay varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the primary biochemical or biophysical assay used to measure compound engagement with this target (e.g., HTRF binding assay, SPR, ITC, NanoBRET). Registered in LIMS (LabWare/SampleManager) and ELN.']
  target_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable scientific name of the molecular target (e.g., EGFR, BRAF, PD-L1, CDK4/6). Serves as the primary identity label used by research scientists and in ELN records within IDBS/Dotmatics.']
  target_type varchar [note: 'Biological molecule type of the target (e.g., protein, enzyme, receptor, ion channel, nucleic acid). Determines the modality of drug intervention and screening assay design. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: protein|enzyme|receptor|ion_channel|nucleic_acid|lipid|carbohydrate|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  uniprot_accession varchar [note: 'Universal Protein Resource (UniProt) accession number uniquely identifying the protein target in the global UniProtKB database. Enables cross-referencing with external biological databases and regulatory submissions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this molecular target record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  validation_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which target validation studies were completed and the target was formally declared validated (in vitro or in vivo). Marks the transition from target identification to hit-to-lead progression stage.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle stage of target validation, reflecting the level of scientific evidence supporting the target\'s role in disease. Drives go/no-go decisions for hit-to-lead progression. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: identified|hypothetical|validated_in_vitro|validated_in_vivo|clinically_validated|deprioritized — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Master record for biological molecular targets identified during drug discovery, including proteins, enzymes, receptors, ion channels, and nucleic acids. Captures target class, gene name, UniProt/Ensembl identifiers, mechanism of action (MOA), therapeutic area relevance, target validation status, druggability assessment, and disease association. Authoritative SSOT for all target identification data entering the discovery pipeline from IDBS/Dotmatics ELN.'
}

Table discovery.compound {
  compound_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key for each compound record in the discovery compound library. Primary key for the compound master data product.']
  atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: ATC classification of discovery compounds drives formulary management, reimbursement strategy, and pharmacovigilance signal detection. \'atc_code\' is a denormalized reference to the masterdata ATC hier']
  drug_master_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.drug_master_file. Business justification: Compounds (APIs) are covered by DMFs for regulatory submissions; linkage supports IND/NDA filings, tracks manufacturing site authorization, and enables CMC strategy coordination between discovery and ']
  drug_substance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: Regulatory CMC requirement: IND/NDA submissions must trace marketed API back to discovery compound for chemistry documentation. Critical for candidate nomination handoff, regulatory filing preparation']
  fto_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.fto_analysis. Business justification: FTO clearance on specific compounds is a mandatory IP process before synthesis scale-up and IND filing. IP counsel runs compound-level FTO to confirm freedom to use the chemical structure. Every pharm']
  inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: Linking compounds to the authoritative INN registry is fundamental for regulatory submissions (IND, NDA), WHO naming conventions, and pharmacovigilance reporting. \'inn\' plain attribute is a denormaliz']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Compounds progressing to development/manufacturing are registered as materials in ERP for procurement, inventory management, batch tracking, and supply chain planning. Critical integration point betwe']
  molecular_target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.molecular_target. Business justification: compound has a STRING field \'primary_target\' which is a denormalized text reference to the compound\'s primary molecular target. In drug discovery, compounds are designed and optimized against specific']
  patent_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_claim. Business justification: Compounds must link to patent claims to track IP protection. This is essential for pharmaceutical asset valuation and freedom-to-operate analysis. No visible redundant columns to remove.']
  patent_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: Compounds belong to patent families covering composition-of-matter globally; family-level tracking supports international IP strategy, prosecution coordination, and portfolio valuation for business de']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Compounds are patented chemical matter requiring direct patent linkage for IP lifecycle management, orange book listing preparation, patent term extension applications, and generic entry risk assessme']
  therapeutic_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: Compounds are classified by therapeutic area for portfolio management, IP strategy, and compound library organization. Linking to the master therapeutic_area entity enforces consistency across discove']
  vendor_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Vendor qualification and approved vendor list (AVL) compliance requires linking purchased/sourced compounds to their business partner record. \'vendor_name\' is denormalized; the FK enables vendor audit']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Globally unique Chemical Abstracts Service (CAS) registry number assigned by the American Chemical Society to uniquely identify the chemical substance. Used for regulatory submissions, procurement, and cross-system compound lookup.']
  compound_class varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the compound by molecular modality. Drives regulatory pathway selection (NDA vs BLA), manufacturing strategy (chemical synthesis vs biologics), and analytical method requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: small_molecule|biologic|peptide|adc|oligonucleotide|cell_therapy|rna_therapy|gene_therapy — promote to reference product]']
  compound_name varchar [note: 'Internal working name or code name assigned to the compound during discovery (e.g., project code name such as \'ABC-1234\'). Used as the primary human-readable label in ELN, LIMS, and CTMS systems before an INN or USAN is assigned.']
  compound_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the compound within the discovery pipeline. \'Active\' indicates the compound is under active research; \'promoted\' indicates advancement to preclinical or clinical development; \'discontinued\' indicates the compound has been deprioritized or terminated.']
  controlled_substance_schedule varchar [note: 'U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) controlled substance schedule classification for the compound (Schedule I–V or not controlled). Determines handling, storage, procurement, and regulatory reporting requirements under the Controlled Substances Act.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp recording when the compound record was first created in the data platform (Silver Layer). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  discovery_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the compound within the drug discovery pipeline. Tracks progression from target identification through hit identification, hit-to-lead, lead optimization, and candidate selection before IND filing. Used for portfolio reporting and stage-gate decision making.']
  eln_reference_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this compound record to the originating experiment or notebook entry in the Electronic Lab Notebook (ELN) system (IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics). Provides traceability from the compound master record back to the primary research data and synthesis records.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the compound as a New Chemical Entity (NCE), New Biological Entity (NBE), biosimilar, fixed-dose combination, or prodrug. Determines regulatory exclusivity periods, patent strategy, and submission pathway under FDA, EMA, and ICH guidelines.']
  glp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the compound and its associated research data have been generated under Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) conditions as required for regulatory submissions. GLP compliance is mandatory for safety pharmacology and toxicology studies supporting IND applications.']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'Globally Harmonized System (GHS) hazard classification for the compound covering health, physical, and environmental hazards (e.g., \'Acute Toxicity Cat. 2, Carcinogen Cat. 1A\'). Required for Safety Data Sheet (SDS) preparation, occupational health risk assessment, and EHS compliance.']
  hba_count varchar [note: 'Number of hydrogen bond acceptors (HBA) in the compound structure. Key Lipinski Rule of Five parameter (threshold: ≤ 10 HBAs) used in drug-likeness assessment and oral bioavailability prediction during hit-to-lead and lead optimization.']
  hbd_count varchar [note: 'Number of hydrogen bond donors (HBD) in the compound structure. Key Lipinski Rule of Five parameter (threshold: ≤ 5 HBDs) used in drug-likeness assessment and oral bioavailability prediction during hit-to-lead and lead optimization.']
  inchi varchar [note: 'IUPAC International Chemical Identifier (InChI) string providing a standardized, layered representation of the compound\'s chemical structure. Used for cross-system compound deduplication, registry lookups, and interoperability with external databases (PubChem, ChEMBL). Classified confidential as it encodes proprietary structural IP.']
  inchi_key varchar [note: 'Fixed-length 27-character hashed representation of the InChI string (InChIKey). Used as a compact, searchable identifier for compound lookup in external databases (PubChem, ChEMBL, CAS SciFinder) and for deduplication across compound registries.']
  iupac_name varchar [note: 'Systematic chemical name of the compound following International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry (IUPAC) nomenclature rules. Provides an unambiguous, internationally recognized chemical identity for regulatory submissions and scientific publications.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which the compound record was last reviewed by a qualified scientist or data steward for accuracy, completeness, and continued relevance in the discovery portfolio. Supports data governance and periodic compound library curation activities.']
  library_membership varchar [note: 'Designation of the compound library or collection to which this compound belongs within the corporate compound library management system. Drives screening campaign design, compound availability, and resource allocation in HTS and fragment-based drug discovery programs.']
  logp decimal(8,4) [note: 'Logarithm of the octanol-water partition coefficient (LogP), a measure of the compound\'s lipophilicity. Critical SAR and ADME parameter used to predict membrane permeability, oral bioavailability, and CNS penetration. Lipinski Rule of Five threshold: LogP ≤ 5.']
  mechanism_of_action varchar [note: 'Described mechanism of action (MOA) of the compound — the biochemical interaction through which the compound produces its pharmacological effect (e.g., \'selective inhibitor of EGFR tyrosine kinase\'). Critical for target validation, patent claims, and regulatory submissions.']
  molecular_formula varchar [note: 'Empirical molecular formula of the compound expressed using Hill notation (e.g., C21H30N2O5). Represents the elemental composition and is used in regulatory submissions, analytical method development, and structural verification.']
  molecular_weight decimal(12,4) [note: 'Average molecular weight of the compound expressed in grams per mole (g/mol). Critical physicochemical property used in SAR analysis, ADME profiling, formulation development, and Lipinski Rule of Five drug-likeness assessment.']
  physical_state varchar [note: 'Physical state of the compound at standard conditions (solid, liquid, gas, semi-solid). Determines handling procedures, formulation approach, and storage requirements. Captured in the ELN and LIMS at time of compound registration.']
  purity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured chemical purity of the compound expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00%). Determined by analytical methods such as HPLC or NMR. Critical quality attribute (CQA) for assay reliability, GLP compliance, and advancement decisions in hit-to-lead progression.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date on which the compound was formally registered in the corporate compound registry (IDBS/Dotmatics). Marks the official entry of the compound into the discovery compound library and initiates the compound lifecycle tracking.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Internally assigned unique registration number for the compound within the corporate compound registry (e.g., IDBS/Dotmatics compound registration system). Serves as the primary business identifier used across discovery, CMC, and regulatory workflows.']
  rotatable_bond_count varchar [note: 'Number of rotatable bonds in the compound structure. Veber rule parameter (threshold: ≤ 10) used alongside TPSA to predict oral bioavailability and molecular flexibility in SAR and lead optimization analyses.']
  smiles varchar [note: 'Canonical SMILES (Simplified Molecular Input Line Entry System) string representing the two-dimensional chemical structure of the compound. Used in cheminformatics, HTS data analysis, SAR modeling, and AI/ML-based drug discovery workflows. Classified confidential as it encodes proprietary structural IP.']
  solubility_aqueous_um decimal(12,4) [note: 'Measured or predicted aqueous solubility of the compound expressed in micromolar (µM). Key ADME physicochemical property used in SAR analysis, formulation feasibility assessment, and drug-likeness profiling (Lipinski, Veber rules).']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Origin classification of the compound indicating how it was obtained. Synthesized compounds are produced in-house; purchased compounds are acquired from external vendors or CROs; natural products are derived from biological sources. Impacts IP strategy and CMC documentation.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Recommended storage conditions for the compound (e.g., \'-20°C, under nitrogen, protected from light, desiccated\'). Ensures compound stability and integrity in the compound library. Derived from stability data and referenced in CoA and compound library management SOPs.']
  tpsa_angstrom2 decimal(8,2) [note: 'Topological Polar Surface Area (TPSA) of the compound expressed in square angstroms (Å²). Used to predict intestinal absorption, blood-brain barrier penetration, and oral bioavailability. Veber rule threshold: TPSA ≤ 140 Å² for oral drugs.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp recording when the compound record was last modified in the data platform. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and enables change detection for downstream data consumers and AI/ML pipelines.']
  usan varchar [note: 'United States Adopted Name (USAN) assigned by the USAN Council for the compound. Used in US regulatory submissions (NDA/BLA) and US commercial labeling. May differ slightly from INN.']
  vendor_catalog_number varchar [note: 'Vendor-assigned catalog or product number for purchased compounds. Used for reorder, procurement traceability, and cross-referencing with vendor CoA and SDS documentation. Applicable only when source_type is \'purchased\'.']

  Note: 'Master record for all chemical and biological compounds in the discovery compound library, including NCEs (New Chemical Entities) and NBEs (New Biological Entities). Captures compound identity (IUPAC name, INN/USAN, registration number, CAS number), structural representation (molecular formula, molecular weight, SMILES, InChI), compound class (small molecule, biologic, peptide, ADC), source (synthesized, purchased, natural product), physicochemical properties (purity, physical state, storage conditions), and library membership. Authoritative SSOT for compound identity and structure across the discovery domain.'
}

Table discovery.assay {
  assay_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the assay master record in the discovery domain. Primary key for the assay data product. Role classification: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: Assay development and validation costs are tracked against cost centers for R&D budget reporting and financial controlling. Pharma finance teams require assay-level cost center assignment separate fro']
  cro_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: CRO vendor qualification and approved vendor list (AVL) management requires linking outsourced assays to their business partner record. \'cro_vendor_name\' is a denormalized representation; the FK enabl']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: Assay technologies are frequently licensed from third parties (e.g., licensed cell lines, detection platforms, HTRF technology). The licensing agreement governs usage rights and royalty obligations. P']
  molecular_target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.molecular_target. Business justification: Assays are designed to screen against specific molecular targets. The assay table currently has target_name and target_class as denormalized STRING attributes. Adding molecular_target_id FK allows joi']
  owning_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: Assay ownership by org unit is required for GLP compliance scope assignment, budget allocation, and departmental capacity planning. \'owning_department\' is a denormalized org unit reference that should']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Proprietary assay methods are protected by method patents. IP teams track which patents cover which assays for licensing, FTO, and CRO outsourcing decisions. A pharma IP expert would expect assay-to-p']
  therapeutic_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: Assays are designed and maintained within therapeutic area programs. Linking to the master therapeutic_area entity enables TA-level assay resource allocation, capacity planning, and portfolio reportin']
  assay_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique code assigned to the assay within the ELN or LIMS (e.g., ASY-HTS0042). Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. Used for cross-system referencing between IDBS/Dotmatics, LabWare LIMS, and SAP QM.']
  assay_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the assay as registered in the ELN (e.g., \'EGFR Kinase Inhibition HTRF Assay\'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. Used in scientific reports, regulatory submissions, and compound screening dashboards.']
  assay_type varchar [note: 'Primary scientific classification of the assay based on its biological or chemical mode of measurement. Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. Drives downstream SAR analysis workflows and compound triage decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: biochemical|cell_based|phenotypic|ADME|toxicity|binding|functional — promote to reference product]']
  automation_compatible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assay has been adapted and validated for automated liquid handling platforms (e.g., Hamilton, Tecan, Beckman Coulter). Determines eligibility for inclusion in HTS screening campaigns and impacts throughput capacity planning.']
  cell_line varchar [note: 'Name of the cell line used in cell-based assays (e.g., HEK293, HeLa, Jurkat, A549, MCF-7). Null for biochemical or cell-free assays. Required for assay reproducibility and cross-site comparability. Cell line authentication records are maintained in the ELN.']
  coefficient_of_variation_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage coefficient of variation (%CV) of the assay signal across replicate measurements, representing assay precision and reproducibility. Typically required to be ≤ 15% for HTS assays and ≤ 20% for bioanalytical assays per ICH Q2(R1). Stored from the assay validation run.']
  concentration_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for compound concentrations used in the assay (e.g., nM, µM, mM). Standardizes concentration reporting across compound screening results and SAR analysis. Required for dose-response curve fitting and IC50/EC50 calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: nM|uM|mM|mg_mL|ug_mL|ng_mL|pM — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cost_per_well_usd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Estimated reagent and consumable cost per assay well in US dollars. Used for screening campaign cost modeling, budget planning, and make-vs-buy decisions for outsourcing to CROs. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM procurement data and lab supply records.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assay master record was first created in the ELN or data platform. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance and data lineage tracking.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Physical or chemical detection principle used to measure the assay signal (e.g., fluorescence, luminescence, radioactivity, mass spectrometry). Distinct from technology_platform — platform is the instrument system, detection_method is the underlying measurement principle. Used for assay validation documentation and GLP compliance records. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fluorescence|luminescence|absorbance|radioactivity|mass_spectrometry|NMR|SPR|calorimetry — promote to reference product]']
  eln_notebook_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Electronic Lab Notebook (ELN) experiment or notebook entry in IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics where the assay development and validation data are recorded. Provides direct traceability from the data product to the source scientific record.']
  first_used_date date [note: 'Date when the assay was first deployed for compound screening or profiling activities. Marks the operational start of the assay lifecycle. Used for assay age tracking, periodic revalidation scheduling, and portfolio lifecycle management.']
  format varchar [note: 'Operational format describing how the assay is run in the screening workflow (e.g., High-Throughput Screening (HTS) primary screen, dose-response, selectivity panel). Determines throughput capacity planning and compound consumption requirements.']
  glp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assay is conducted under Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) regulations as required for regulatory submissions (IND/NDA/BLA). GLP-compliant assays require full audit trails, validated equipment, and certified personnel. Critical for preclinical safety study data submitted to FDA/EMA.']
  hit_threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the hit_threshold_value (e.g., percent inhibition, nM IC50). Ensures unambiguous interpretation of the hit-calling criterion across different assay types and readout formats.']
  hit_threshold_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numerical threshold used to classify a compound as a \'hit\' in primary screening (e.g., ≥ 50% inhibition at 10 µM). Defined during assay validation and used by automated hit-calling algorithms in the HTS data analysis pipeline. Critical for hit-to-lead progression decisions.']
  incubation_time_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard incubation duration in hours for the assay reaction or cell treatment step. Critical for assay reproducibility and scheduling of automated liquid handling workflows. Stored from the validated assay protocol.']
  last_validated_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal validation or requalification of the assay per ICH Q2(R1) or fit-for-purpose qualification criteria. Used to trigger periodic revalidation workflows and to assess whether assay data is current for regulatory submissions.']
  lims_assay_code varchar [note: 'Cross-reference identifier for this assay in the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) — LabWare or Thermo Fisher SampleManager. Enables bidirectional traceability between the discovery data product and the LIMS assay execution records.']
  multiplexed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assay measures multiple analytes or endpoints simultaneously in a single well or reaction (multiplexed assay). Multiplexed assays increase data density per compound and reduce reagent consumption. Relevant for assay cost modeling and data analysis pipeline configuration.']
  outsourced_to_cro boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assay is currently outsourced to an external Contract Research Organization (CRO) for execution. When true, the cro_vendor_name field captures the CRO identity. Impacts data governance, IP ownership, and regulatory audit trail requirements.']
  plate_format varchar [note: 'Microplate format used in the assay (e.g., 96-well, 384-well, 1536-well). Determines reagent volumes, automation compatibility, and throughput capacity. Critical for liquid handling robot programming and compound dispensing protocols.']
  protocol_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier or URL reference to the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) or assay protocol document stored in the ELN (IDBS/Dotmatics) or document management system (Veeva Vault QualityDocs). Ensures traceability from assay execution to the governing protocol version.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the assay protocol document in effect at the time of assay execution (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Required for GLP audit trails and regulatory submission traceability. Distinct from protocol_reference which is the document identifier.']
  readout_type varchar [note: 'Primary quantitative output metric reported by the assay (e.g., IC50, EC50, Ki, Kd, percent inhibition, percent activation). Determines how compound activity data is interpreted in SAR analysis and hit-to-lead progression decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IC50|EC50|Ki|Kd|percent_inhibition|percent_activation|ratio|count|viability_pct — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_submission_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assay results generated using this assay are eligible for inclusion in regulatory submissions (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA). Eligibility requires full validation per ICH Q2(R1) and GLP compliance where applicable. Governs data governance workflows for regulatory data packages.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date when the assay was formally retired and removed from active use. Null for active assays. Retirement is triggered by target deprioritization, supersession by a superior assay, or platform decommissioning. Required for data governance and historical data archival.']
  signal_window decimal(10,4) [note: 'Ratio of the maximum signal (positive control) to the minimum signal (negative control), quantifying the dynamic range of the assay. A signal window ≥ 2 is typically required for HTS qualification. Used in assay validation reports and Z-factor calculations.']
  species_origin varchar [note: 'Biological species from which the assay target, cells, or biological material is derived (e.g., human, mouse, rat, recombinant). Critical for translational relevance assessment and cross-species selectivity profiling. Impacts regulatory acceptability of preclinical data for IND submissions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: human|mouse|rat|rabbit|dog|monkey|in_vitro_recombinant — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  technology_platform varchar [note: 'Detection technology or instrument platform used to execute the assay (e.g., HTRF, AlphaScreen, Surface Plasmon Resonance (SPR), NMR, FRET, TR-FRET, Luminescence, Flow Cytometry). Critical for assay reproducibility assessment and equipment scheduling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HTRF|AlphaScreen|SPR|NMR|FRET|TR-FRET|luminescence|flow_cytometry|mass_spectrometry|fluorescence_polarization — promote to reference product]']
  temperature_celsius decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard incubation or reaction temperature in degrees Celsius for the assay. Critical for assay reproducibility across sites and instruments. Deviations from this value are flagged as out-of-specification (OOS) events in the LIMS.']
  throughput_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of compounds or samples that can be processed per day using this assay under standard HTS conditions. Serves as the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. Used for compound library screening campaign planning and resource scheduling in the discovery lab.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the assay with respect to its scientific and regulatory validation. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. \'validated\' indicates full ICH Q2(R1) compliance; \'qualified\' indicates fit-for-purpose qualification; \'development\' indicates the assay is still being optimized. Governs whether assay results can be used in regulatory submissions.']
  z_prime_factor decimal(6,4) [note: 'Statistical measure of assay quality (Z\') calculated from positive and negative control variability. Values ≥ 0.5 indicate an excellent assay suitable for HTS. Stored as the validated Z\' from the assay qualification run. Used to gate assay deployment for compound screening campaigns.']

  Note: 'Master record for biological and biochemical assays used in drug discovery screening and profiling. Captures assay name, assay type (biochemical, cell-based, phenotypic, ADME, toxicity), assay format (HTS, dose-response, selectivity), target association, assay technology platform (HTRF, AlphaScreen, SPR, NMR), assay validation status, GLP compliance flag, detection method, assay protocol reference, throughput capacity, and responsible scientist. Sourced from IDBS/Dotmatics ELN.'
}

Table discovery.hts_campaign {
  hts_campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the HTS campaign record in the discovery data product. Primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.']
  assay_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.assay. Business justification: HTS campaigns execute screening using specific assays. The campaign currently has assay_format, assay_technology, target_name, target_class as denormalized attributes. Adding assay_id FK establishes t']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: HTS campaigns are major cost events requiring cost center allocation for financial tracking, budget management, and project costing. Enables campaign cost analysis, resource planning, and financial re']
  cro_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Outsourced HTS campaigns require linking to the qualified CRO business partner for contract management, AVL compliance, and vendor performance tracking. \'cro_name\' is a denormalized business partner n']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: HTS campaigns are cost-tracked R&D projects requiring internal order assignment for budget control, variance analysis, and cost-per-screen metrics essential to pharmaceutical screening operations and ']
  molecular_target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.molecular_target. Business justification: hts_campaign has denormalized STRING fields target_name and target_class that duplicate data from the molecular_target master record. While hts_campaign already links to assay (which has molecular_tar']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent drug discovery project under which this HTS campaign is executed. Links the campaign to the broader target identification and hit-to-lead program for portfolio tracking.']
  screening_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: HTS campaigns are executed at specific screening facilities/plants; facility tracking is required for GMP compliance, capacity planning, and regulatory audit trails. Role prefix \'screening_\' distingui']
  therapeutic_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: HTS campaigns are executed within therapeutic area programs and budgets. Linking to the master therapeutic_area entity enables TA-level campaign portfolio reporting, cost tracking, and strategic resou']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the HTS campaign screening execution was completed. Used to calculate campaign duration, throughput metrics, and to trigger downstream hit confirmation workflows.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the HTS campaign screening execution actually commenced. Compared against planned_start_date to measure schedule variance and operational efficiency.']
  assay_format varchar [note: 'Scientific format of the assay used in the campaign, indicating whether the screen measures biochemical activity (purified protein), cellular response, whole-organism phenotype, direct binding (biophysical), or fragment-based interactions. Critical for interpreting hit quality and SAR analysis.']
  assay_technology varchar [note: 'Specific detection technology or instrumentation platform used in the assay (e.g., HTRF, AlphaScreen, FRET, TR-FRET, FP, luminescence, absorbance, imaging). Determines data interpretation rules and quality thresholds. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HTRF|AlphaScreen|FRET|TR-FRET|FP|luminescence|absorbance|imaging|SPR|ITC — promote to reference product]']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the HTS campaign, assigned by the ELN or LIMS system (e.g., HTS-EGFR-2024). Used for cross-system referencing in Dotmatics, LabWare, and project management tools.']
  campaign_description varchar [note: 'Free-text scientific description of the HTS campaign objectives, biological rationale, assay design, and any special conditions or deviations from standard protocols. Provides context for data interpretation and scientific review.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the HTS campaign, typically including the target name and campaign purpose (e.g., \'EGFR Kinase Primary Screen Q1 2024\'). Used in reports, dashboards, and scientific communications.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the HTS campaign, tracking progression from planning through execution to completion or cancellation. Drives operational dashboards and resource allocation decisions.']
  coefficient_of_variation_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage coefficient of variation of the neutral control wells across all campaign plates, measuring intra- and inter-plate reproducibility. Acceptable CV% thresholds are defined per assay type and are a key quality acceptance criterion.']
  compounds_screened_count varchar [note: 'Total number of unique compounds tested in this HTS campaign. Key operational metric for campaign scope, resource planning, and hit rate denominator calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the HTS campaign record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GLP data integrity regulations.']
  cro_involved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Contract Research Organization (CRO) was engaged to execute or support this HTS campaign. Drives vendor management, data transfer agreement requirements, and IP ownership tracking.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Overall data quality assessment for the HTS campaign based on Z\' factor, CV%, plate failure rate, and control performance. Determines whether campaign data is accepted for hit identification and downstream SAR analysis.']
  eln_notebook_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this HTS campaign to the corresponding experiment record in the Electronic Lab Notebook (ELN) system (IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics). Provides traceability to raw experimental data, protocols, and scientist sign-off records.']
  failed_plate_count varchar [note: 'Number of assay plates that failed quality control criteria and were excluded from hit identification analysis. High failed plate counts may trigger campaign re-screening decisions.']
  glp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this HTS campaign was conducted under Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) regulations. GLP compliance is required for studies intended to support regulatory submissions (IND/NDA). Non-GLP campaigns are exploratory research.']
  hit_count varchar [note: 'Number of compounds that met or exceeded the hit threshold criteria in this campaign. Used to calculate hit rate and to scope confirmatory screening workload.']
  hit_rate_percent decimal(6,4) [note: 'Percentage of screened compounds that qualified as hits (hit_count / compounds_screened_count × 100). Key campaign quality metric used to assess assay selectivity, library quality, and target druggability. Stored as a raw business measurement, not a derived KPI.']
  hit_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage inhibition or activation threshold used to define a \'hit\' in this campaign (e.g., ≥50% inhibition at screening concentration). Determines the stringency of hit identification and directly impacts hit count and hit rate.']
  lims_batch_code varchar [note: 'Batch or run identifier assigned by the LIMS (LabWare or Thermo Fisher SampleManager) for the sample processing associated with this HTS campaign. Enables traceability of compound samples, plate preparation, and analytical results.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled start date for the HTS campaign as defined in the project plan. Used for resource scheduling, capacity planning, and tracking schedule adherence.']
  plate_count varchar [note: 'Total number of assay plates processed during the campaign. Used for capacity planning, reagent consumption tracking, and quality control review scope.']
  plate_format varchar [note: 'Microplate format used in the HTS campaign, defining the number of wells per plate. Determines throughput capacity, liquid handling requirements, and data volume per plate.']
  positive_control_compound varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the reference compound used as the positive control in the assay (known active compound). Defines the 100% inhibition/activation benchmark for data normalization and Z\' factor calculation.']
  screen_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the screening campaign by its scientific purpose in the HTS funnel. Primary screens test the full compound library; confirmatory screens re-test hits; counter-screens eliminate false positives; dose-response screens establish potency; selectivity screens assess off-target activity.']
  screening_concentration_um decimal(10,4) [note: 'Single-point screening concentration in micromolar (µM) at which compounds are tested in the primary screen. Defines the activity threshold context for hit identification and is a critical parameter for hit rate comparability across campaigns.']
  signal_to_background_ratio decimal(8,4) [note: 'Ratio of the positive control signal to the background (negative control) signal, measuring the dynamic range of the assay. A higher S/B ratio indicates a more robust assay window. Complementary to Z\' factor for assay quality assessment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the HTS campaign record. Required for data lineage tracking, change control auditing, and Silver layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  z_prime_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Statistical measure of assay quality (Z\' factor) calculated from positive and negative control signals, ranging from 0 to 1. A Z\' ≥ 0.5 indicates an acceptable HTS assay window. Critical quality gate for campaign data acceptance per SLAS and NIH NCATS standards.']

  Note: 'Master record for High-Throughput Screening (HTS) campaigns executed against compound libraries. Captures campaign name, target or assay being screened, compound library used, screening format (primary, confirmatory, counter-screen), campaign status, start and end dates, number of compounds screened, hit rate, assay window (Z-prime factor), screening technology platform, CRO involvement flag, and campaign owner. Central operational entity for HTS execution tracking.'
}

Table discovery.screening_result {
  screening_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual screening result record in the HTS or dose-response assay campaign. Primary key for the screening_result data product in the discovery domain.']
  assay_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the assay definition used to generate this screening result, including assay type, target, and protocol version.']
  hts_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the High-Throughput Screening (HTS) campaign or screening project under which this result was generated. Enables grouping of results by campaign for SAR analysis.']
  molecular_target_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological molecular target (e.g., protein, enzyme, receptor) against which the compound was screened. Central to target identification and MOA studies.']
  primary_screening_compound_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compound (NCE or NBE) that was screened in this assay run. Links to the compound master record in the compound library.']
  coefficient_of_variation_pct decimal(10,4) [note: 'Coefficient of variation (CV%) for replicate measurements of this compound in the assay run. Measures intra-assay reproducibility; high CV% triggers data quality review.']
  compound_concentration_um decimal(18,6) [note: 'Concentration of the compound tested in the assay well, expressed in micromolar (µM). Critical for dose-response curve fitting and potency determination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this screening result record was first created in the system. Required for GLP audit trail and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record compliance.']
  curve_fit_r_squared decimal(10,6) [note: 'Coefficient of determination (R²) from the dose-response curve fitting model. Values close to 1.0 indicate a high-quality fit. Used as a data quality gate for potency value acceptance.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Data quality status assigned to this screening result based on automated QC rules and manual review. \'flagged\' indicates a potential issue requiring review; \'rejected\' excludes the result from downstream analysis.']
  ec50_nm decimal(18,6) [note: 'Half-maximal effective concentration (EC50) derived from dose-response curve fitting, expressed in nanomolar (nM). Key potency metric for agonist/activator compounds.']
  eln_notebook_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this screening result to the corresponding experiment entry in the Electronic Lab Notebook (ELN) system (IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics). Provides traceability to the original experimental record.']
  hill_slope decimal(10,4) [note: 'Hill slope (cooperativity coefficient) from the four-parameter logistic (4PL) dose-response curve fit. Values near 1 indicate simple binding; values significantly different from 1 may indicate cooperativity, aggregation, or assay artifacts.']
  ic50_nm decimal(18,6) [note: 'Half-maximal inhibitory concentration (IC50) derived from dose-response curve fitting, expressed in nanomolar (nM). Key potency metric for inhibitory compounds in SAR analysis and hit-to-lead progression.']
  instrument_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the laboratory instrument (e.g., plate reader, liquid handler, mass spectrometer) used to generate the raw signal. Supports instrument qualification tracking and systematic error investigation per GLP.']
  is_hit_to_lead_eligible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this compound result meets the predefined criteria for progression into the hit-to-lead (H2L) phase, based on potency, selectivity, and data quality thresholds.']
  ki_nm decimal(18,6) [note: 'Inhibition constant (Ki) representing the binding affinity of the compound to the target, expressed in nanomolar (nM). Derived from competitive binding or enzyme kinetics assays. Used in mechanistic SAR studies.']
  lims_result_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier from the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) for this screening result record. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational LIMS source system.']
  max_response_percent decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum observed response (top asymptote) from the dose-response curve, expressed as a percentage of control. Indicates the efficacy ceiling of the compound at the target.']
  min_response_percent decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum observed response (bottom asymptote) from the dose-response curve, expressed as a percentage of control. Used to assess baseline signal and curve quality.']
  pan_assay_interference_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the compound has been identified as a Pan-Assay Interference Compound (PAINS) — a compound that produces artifactual activity signals across multiple assays due to non-specific mechanisms. PAINS compounds are deprioritized in hit-to-lead.']
  percent_activation decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated percentage activation of the target relative to controls. Primary activity metric for agonist/activator HTS campaigns. Null for inhibition assays.']
  percent_inhibition decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated percentage inhibition of the target activity relative to positive and negative controls on the same plate. Primary activity metric for inhibitory HTS campaigns. Null for activation assays.']
  plate_barcode varchar [note: 'Unique barcode identifier of the physical assay plate on which the compound was tested. Used for traceability back to the physical plate in the LIMS and for QC investigations.']
  plate_column varchar [note: 'Numeric column designation (1–24) of the well on the assay plate. Used for spatial analysis of plate effects and systematic error detection.']
  plate_row varchar [note: 'Row designation (A–P) of the well on the assay plate. Used for spatial analysis of plate effects and edge-effect QC.']
  quality_flag_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason explaining why a data quality flag was applied (e.g., \'edge well effect\', \'reagent lot failure\', \'instrument malfunction\', \'outlier replicate\'). Required when data_quality_flag is \'flagged\' or \'rejected\'.']
  raw_signal_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Unprocessed instrument signal reading from the assay detector (e.g., fluorescence units, luminescence counts, absorbance). Preserved as the primary measurement for audit and reanalysis.']
  reagent_lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier of the key assay reagent (e.g., enzyme, antibody, cell line passage) used in this assay run. Enables reagent lot correlation analysis for systematic variability investigation.']
  replicate_number varchar [note: 'Sequential replicate index for this compound-assay combination within the same run (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Enables statistical aggregation across replicates and assessment of intra-assay variability.']
  result_classification varchar [note: 'Scientific classification of the screening result based on activity thresholds and confirmatory data. Drives hit-to-lead progression decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|inactive|inconclusive|toxic|hit|confirmed_hit|false_positive|pan_assay_interference — promote to reference product]']
  run_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the assay run was executed. Used for temporal trending, reagent lot correlation, and GLP audit trail compliance.']
  run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the assay instrument reading was captured. Supports intra-day sequencing, instrument log correlation, and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record compliance.']
  selectivity_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Quantitative selectivity score measuring the compound\'s specificity for the primary target versus off-target activities in a selectivity panel. Higher scores indicate greater selectivity. Used in SAR-driven lead optimization.']
  signal_to_background_ratio decimal(10,4) [note: 'Ratio of the positive control signal to the background (negative control) signal on the assay plate. A key plate-level QC metric; low S/B ratios indicate assay degradation or reagent failure.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this screening result record. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks lakehouse.']
  well_position varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric well position on the assay plate (e.g., A01, H12, P24) indicating the exact location of the compound in the plate layout. Standard SBS/ANSI plate format.']
  z_prime_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Z\' factor for the assay plate, a dimensionless statistical metric (range -∞ to 1) measuring assay quality based on signal window and variability. Z\' ≥ 0.5 is the accepted threshold for a valid HTS assay plate.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing individual compound screening results from HTS campaigns, dose-response assays, and selectivity panels. Captures compound, assay, and campaign references, plate/well position, raw signal, percent inhibition/activation, potency values (IC50/EC50/Ki), Hill slope, curve fit quality (R-squared), result classification (active, inactive, inconclusive, toxic, hit, confirmed hit, false positive), selectivity score, replicate number, run date, and data quality flags. Includes hit-to-lead eligibility determination. Distinct from in_vitro_study: screening_result covers primary and confirmatory screening at scale; in_vitro_study covers deeper mechanistic follow-up on selected compounds.'
}

Table discovery.sar_study {
  sar_study_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the SAR study record. Primary key for this entity.']
  assay_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.assay. Business justification: SAR (Structure-Activity Relationship) studies use specific assays to measure potency and selectivity of synthesized analogs. The study table currently has assay_type as a denormalized STRING attribute']
  best_compound_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the single best-performing compound identified from this SAR study, based on the combination of potency, selectivity, and ADME profile. Candidate for progression to the next discovery stage.']
  fto_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.fto_analysis. Business justification: FTO analyses are conducted on the chemical space explored in SAR studies to identify third-party IP blocking the series. IP and medicinal chemistry teams jointly review FTO before advancing a series. ']
  hts_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating High-Throughput Screening (HTS) campaign from which the hit compounds for this SAR study were identified. Provides lineage from HTS to lead optimization.']
  indication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: SAR studies are conducted to optimize compounds for specific disease indications. Linking to product.indication enables indication-level medicinal chemistry portfolio reporting and aligns SAR findings']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: SAR studies are discrete R&D activities requiring dedicated cost tracking via internal orders for financial planning, cost-per-analog analysis, and stage-gate decision support in medicinal chemistry o']
  lead_series_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lead chemical series under investigation in this SAR study. Links to the compound series master record in the compound library.']
  molecular_target_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological molecular target (e.g., protein, receptor, enzyme) that the SAR study is designed to modulate. Links to the target identification master record.']
  patent_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: SAR studies generate composition-of-matter IP filed as patent families covering the chemical series. IP teams link SAR findings to patent families for prosecution strategy and portfolio reporting. sar']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent drug discovery project under which this SAR study is conducted. Enables roll-up reporting at the project level.']
  therapeutic_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: SAR studies are conducted within therapeutic area medicinal chemistry programs. Linking to the master therapeutic_area entity enables TA-level medicinal chemistry resource reporting and portfolio gove']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the SAR study was actually completed and final data locked in the ELN. Compared against target completion date for schedule variance analysis.']
  adme_impact_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of how structural modifications in this SAR study impacted ADME properties including metabolic stability, permeability, solubility, plasma protein binding, and CYP inhibition. Sourced from LIMS and ELN data.']
  analogs_synthesized_count varchar [note: 'Total count of chemical analogs synthesized and tested within this SAR study. Key productivity metric for medicinal chemistry resource planning and portfolio reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SAR study record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0–100) reflecting the completeness and reliability of the experimental data captured in this SAR study, based on assay reproducibility, data completeness, and ELN audit trail integrity. Used for data governance and AI/ML model training quality gates.']
  eln_notebook_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the corresponding study record in the IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics Electronic Lab Notebook (ELN). Provides traceability to the primary research data source.']
  glp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SAR study was conducted under Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) regulations. True = GLP-compliant study; False = non-GLP exploratory research. Relevant for regulatory submission packages.']
  key_structural_features varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured description of the key chemical structural features (pharmacophores, functional groups, scaffolds, substituents) analyzed in this SAR study to drive potency, selectivity, or ADME properties.']
  mechanism_of_action varchar [note: 'Description of the biochemical mechanism by which the compounds in this series exert their pharmacological effect on the target (e.g., competitive inhibition, allosteric modulation, covalent binding).']
  metabolic_stability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lead compounds in this SAR study met the predefined metabolic stability criteria (e.g., microsomal half-life threshold). True = criteria met; False = criteria not met.']
  nce_nbe_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the compound series as a New Chemical Entity (NCE, small molecule) or New Biological Entity (NBE, biologic/large molecule) or hybrid. Determines regulatory pathway and IP strategy.']
  patent_filing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a patent application has been initiated for compounds or methods arising from this SAR study. True = patent filing in progress or completed; False = no patent action taken.']
  permeability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lead compounds demonstrated acceptable membrane permeability (e.g., Caco-2 or PAMPA assay thresholds). True = acceptable permeability achieved; False = not achieved.']
  potency_metric_type varchar [note: 'Type of biochemical potency measurement used to characterize compound activity in this SAR study (e.g., IC50 for inhibitory concentration, Ki for inhibition constant, EC50 for effective concentration).']
  potency_range_max_nm decimal(18,4) [note: 'Highest (least potent) IC50 or Ki value observed across all analogs in this SAR study, expressed in nanomolar (nM). Together with the minimum, defines the potency range of the series.']
  potency_range_min_nm decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lowest (most potent) IC50 or Ki value observed across all analogs in this SAR study, expressed in nanomolar (nM). Represents the best-in-series potency achieved.']
  progression_decision varchar [note: 'Outcome decision made at the conclusion of the SAR study regarding whether the lead series should progress to the next stage of drug discovery (e.g., lead optimization, candidate selection) or be deprioritized.']
  sar_hypothesis varchar [note: 'Scientific hypothesis describing the proposed relationship between structural features of the compound series and the observed or expected biological activity. Captured from the ELN study plan.']
  selectivity_findings varchar [note: 'Summary of selectivity data for the lead compounds against off-target proteins or related family members. Describes selectivity ratios, panel screening results, and any observed off-target liabilities.']
  selectivity_ratio decimal(10,2) [note: 'Quantitative ratio of the compound\'s potency against the primary target versus the most relevant off-target (e.g., 100-fold selective over related kinase). Key metric for candidate progression decisions.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date on which the SAR study was formally initiated, corresponding to the first experimental activity recorded in the ELN. Used for timeline tracking and portfolio planning.']
  study_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the SAR study, typically including the lead series name and therapeutic target (e.g., \'Series-7 EGFR Kinase Inhibitor Optimization\'). Used for display and search in ELN and project management tools.']
  study_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier assigned to the SAR study at initiation (e.g., SAR-ONC-202401). Used for cross-system referencing in ELN, LIMS, and project tracking systems.']
  study_phase varchar [note: 'Stage of the drug discovery funnel at which this SAR study is conducted. Aligns with the hit-to-lead and lead optimization progression framework used in medicinal chemistry.']
  study_report_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier or Veeva Vault reference for the formal SAR study report summarizing findings, conclusions, and progression recommendations. Provides traceability to the official scientific record.']
  study_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the SAR study. Drives workflow routing and portfolio reporting. \'terminated\' indicates early discontinuation; \'archived\' indicates data locked in ELN.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the SAR study is expected to be completed and key findings reported. Used for project milestone tracking and resource scheduling.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this SAR study record. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']

  Note: 'Master record for Structure-Activity Relationship (SAR) studies conducted to understand how structural modifications to a compound affect its biological activity. Captures study name, lead series identifier, target identifier, SAR hypothesis, key structural features analyzed, number of analogs synthesized, potency range observed, selectivity findings, ADME impact, study status, lead scientist, and ELN notebook reference from IDBS/Dotmatics. Core entity for medicinal chemistry optimization.'
}

Table discovery.lead_series {
  lead_series_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the lead series record. Primary key for the lead_series data product in the discovery domain.']
  best_in_class_compound_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compound record representing the best-performing compound within this lead series at the current stage, used as the benchmark for potency, selectivity, and ADME profiling comparisons.']
  fto_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.fto_analysis. Business justification: Lead series advancement requires formal FTO clearance as a stage-gate requirement. IP teams conduct series-level FTO before lead optimization investment. lead_series has ip_landscape_assessment as a t']
  hts_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating High-Throughput Screening (HTS) campaign from which the initial hits in this series were identified. Provides traceability from lead series back to the screening event.']
  indication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Lead series are optimized for specific disease indications. Linking to product.indication enables indication-level pipeline tracking and aligns lead optimization decisions with the product indication ']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Lead optimization programs in pharma are funded via dedicated internal orders covering medicinal chemistry, in vivo studies, and CRO costs. Finance tracks lead series spend separately from HTS campaig']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: Lead series may be in-licensed or out-licensed; agreement linkage tracks licensed rights, royalty obligations, milestone payments, territory restrictions, and field-of-use limitations for portfolio ma']
  molecular_target_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary molecular target (e.g., protein, enzyme, receptor) against which this lead series is designed to act. Central to target-based drug discovery and SAR analysis.']
  patent_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_claim. Business justification: Lead series scaffolds are covered by specific composition-of-matter patent claims. IP teams track which claims protect the lead scaffold for prosecution strategy and licensing negotiations. lead_serie']
  patent_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: Lead series represent patentable chemical scaffolds; patent family linkage tracks composition-of-matter protection, supports stage gate IP assessments, and enables portfolio prioritization based on IP']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.discovery_project. Business justification: Lead series are chemical series progressing through hit-to-lead and lead optimization stages within discovery projects. The series table currently has program_code as a denormalized STRING attribute. ']
  therapeutic_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: Lead series are developed within therapeutic area programs. Linking to the master therapeutic_area entity enables TA-level pipeline reporting, stage-gate review preparation, and strategic portfolio pr']
  adme_profile_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of the Absorption, Distribution, Metabolism, and Excretion (ADME) profile for the lead series, including key in vitro ADME data such as microsomal stability, permeability (Caco-2/PAMPA), plasma protein binding, and CYP inhibition flags.']
  compound_count varchar [note: 'Total number of discrete compounds synthesized and registered within this lead series. Indicates the breadth of SAR exploration and medicinal chemistry effort invested in the series.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lead series record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and GLP/21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.']
  current_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the lead series within the drug discovery pipeline, from hit confirmation through candidate nomination. Distinct from series_status in that it reflects the scientific progression stage rather than the governance lifecycle state.']
  deprioritization_date date [note: 'Date on which the lead series was formally deprioritized or stopped. Null if the series is still active. Used for portfolio analytics and resource reallocation tracking.']
  drug_likeness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite drug-likeness score for the best-in-class compound in the series, typically based on Lipinski Rule of Five parameters or QED (Quantitative Estimate of Drug-likeness). Ranges from 0 to 1; higher values indicate better drug-likeness.']
  eln_notebook_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this lead series to the primary Electronic Lab Notebook (ELN) experiment or project in IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics where the originating research data is recorded. Ensures data traceability and GLP audit trail.']
  glp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the key studies supporting this lead series have been conducted under Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) conditions as required for regulatory submission. True = GLP-compliant studies on record.']
  ip_landscape_assessment varchar [note: 'Narrative assessment of the Intellectual Property (IP) landscape for the lead series, including freedom-to-operate (FTO) status, known competitor patents covering the scaffold, and patentability assessment. Classified as confidential due to competitive sensitivity.']
  ip_status varchar [note: 'Current IP status of the lead series scaffold, indicating whether freedom-to-operate has been established, patents have been filed, or IP encumbrances exist. Classified as confidential.']
  moa_description varchar [note: 'Description of the Mechanism of Action (MOA) for the lead series, explaining how compounds in the series interact with the molecular target to produce the desired pharmacological effect.']
  modality varchar [note: 'Drug modality classification for the lead series, indicating the type of therapeutic molecule (e.g., small molecule, biologic, peptide). Drives CMC strategy, manufacturing approach, and regulatory pathway selection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: small_molecule|biologic|peptide|oligonucleotide|cell_therapy|gene_therapy|antibody_drug_conjugate|radioligand — promote to reference product]']
  molecular_weight_da decimal(8,3) [note: 'Molecular weight of the core scaffold or best-in-class compound in Daltons (Da). Key physicochemical property used in drug-likeness assessment (Lipinski Rule of Five: MW ≤ 500 Da for oral drugs).']
  nce_nbe_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the lead series as a New Chemical Entity (NCE), New Biological Entity (NBE), or New Molecular Entity (NME). Determines regulatory pathway, IP strategy, and data exclusivity eligibility.']
  nomination_date date [note: 'Date on which the lead series was formally nominated as a development candidate, marking the transition from discovery to preclinical development. Null if the series has not yet been nominated.']
  oral_bioavailability_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Best-in-class oral bioavailability percentage (%F) for the lead series from preclinical in vivo PK studies. Key ADME metric for assessing drug-likeness and oral dosing feasibility.']
  pk_profile_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of in vivo Pharmacokinetics (PK) data for the lead series, including key parameters such as oral bioavailability (%F), half-life (t½), clearance (CL), and volume of distribution (Vd) from preclinical species studies.']
  potency_assay_type varchar [note: 'Type of biochemical or cellular assay used to determine the potency benchmark value (e.g., IC50, Ki, EC50, Kd). Provides context for interpreting the potency_benchmark_nm value.']
  potency_benchmark_nm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Best-in-class potency value (IC50 or Ki) for the lead series expressed in nanomolar (nM) concentration. Represents the principal quantitative performance metric for the series and is used in stage-gate go/no-go decisions.']
  safety_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any significant safety liabilities (e.g., genotoxicity, hERG inhibition, reactive metabolites, phototoxicity) have been identified for compounds in this lead series. True = known safety liability requiring mitigation.']
  safety_liability_notes varchar [note: 'Narrative description of identified safety liabilities for the lead series, including the nature of the liability, affected compounds, and proposed mitigation strategies. Populated when safety_flag is True.']
