  sar_maturity_level varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the maturity of the Structure-Activity Relationship (SAR) understanding for the lead series, from early (limited data) to mature (well-defined SAR with predictive models). Used in stage-gate assessments.']
  scaffold_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the core chemical scaffold or chemotype defining the lead series, including key structural features relevant to Structure-Activity Relationship (SAR) analysis. Captured from ELN records.']
  selectivity_profile_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the selectivity profile for the lead series, describing off-target activity, selectivity ratios against related targets, and any known liabilities identified through selectivity panels (e.g., kinase selectivity panel, GPCR panel).']
  series_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the lead series within the compound governance system (e.g., ONCO-SER-00042). Used as the business identifier across ELN, LIMS, and medicinal chemistry platforms.']
  series_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name assigned to the lead series, typically reflecting the chemotype or scaffold class (e.g., \'Pyrimidine Kinase Inhibitor Series A\'). Used in medicinal chemistry reports and stage-gate presentations.']
  series_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the lead series was formally initiated following hit confirmation from the originating HTS campaign. Marks the beginning of the hit-to-lead phase and is used for timeline and cycle-time analytics.']
  series_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lead series within the drug discovery pipeline. Governs stage-gate decisions and resource allocation. Values: hit_to_lead (active hit confirmation and early profiling), lead_optimization (active medicinal chemistry optimization), deprioritized (paused or stopped), nominated (candidate nominated for development), on_hold (temporarily paused pending decision).']
  smiles_scaffold varchar [note: 'Simplified Molecular Input Line Entry System (SMILES) string representing the core scaffold structure of the lead series. Enables cheminformatics analysis, substructure searching, and computational chemistry workflows.']
  stage_gate_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent stage-gate committee decision was made for this lead series. Used for tracking progression timelines and portfolio reporting.']
  stage_gate_decision varchar [note: 'Most recent stage-gate committee decision for the lead series, indicating whether the series has been approved to progress (go), stopped (no_go), placed on hold, or conditionally approved pending specific criteria.']
  stage_gate_rationale varchar [note: 'Documented rationale for the most recent stage-gate decision, capturing the scientific, strategic, and risk-based reasoning of the decision committee. Supports audit trail and portfolio governance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lead series record was last modified. Supports audit trail, change tracking, and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record for chemical lead series progressing through hit-to-lead and lead optimization stages. Captures series name, scaffold/chemotype description, originating HTS campaign, primary target, series status (hit-to-lead, lead optimization, deprioritized, nominated), best-in-class compound identifier, potency benchmark, selectivity profile, ADME profile summary, IP landscape assessment, therapeutic area, program code, and stage-gate progression history with decision rationale. Central entity for tracking medicinal chemistry progression and compound governance decisions from hit confirmation through candidate nomination.'
}

Table discovery.compound_synthesis {
  compound_synthesis_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each compound synthesis record in the discovery domain. Primary key for the compound_synthesis data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — a discrete synthesis event with its own lifecycle, reagents, conditions, and outcomes.']
  compound_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compound entity that was synthesized in this experiment. Links the synthesis record to the master compound registry (NCE/NBE catalogue).']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: Synthesis activities consume reagents, labor, and equipment charged to cost centers for project costing, resource planning, and financial reporting. Essential for medicinal chemistry budget management']
  fto_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.fto_analysis. Business justification: FTO on synthesis routes is a standard pharma IP process — IP teams assess whether a synthesis method infringes third-party process patents before scale-up. This is especially critical for API manufact']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Individual synthesis activities are charged to internal orders for cost-per-compound tracking, essential for portfolio economics, make-vs-buy decisions, and CRO cost benchmarking in pharmaceutical dis']
  lead_series_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.lead_series. Business justification: Compound synthesis efforts by medicinal chemistry teams are organized by lead series. Each synthesis produces an analog within a specific chemical series. The synthesis table currently has series_code']
  master_batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.master_batch_record. Business justification: GMP-compliant preclinical supply synthesis is governed by a master batch record. Pharma domain experts expect compound_synthesis records for GMP batches to reference the controlling MBR for regulatory']
  molecular_target_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological target (protein, enzyme, receptor, or pathway) against which this compound was synthesized. Links synthesis provenance to target identification and MOA studies in the discovery domain.']
  patent_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: Process patent families protect synthesis routes globally across jurisdictions. IP teams track synthesis-to-patent-family linkage for global manufacturing FTO and licensing strategy. Family-level trac']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Synthesis routes are protected by process patents. IP teams track which synthesis methods are covered by patents for manufacturing freedom-to-operate and licensing. Pharma IP experts routinely link sy']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the drug discovery project under which this synthesis was conducted. Enables project-level portfolio reporting, resource tracking, and compound series management.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.site. Business justification: Kilo-scale and GMP preclinical supply synthesis is performed at designated manufacturing sites (pilot plants, kilo labs). Pharma operations track which manufacturing site executed each synthesis batch']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.storage_location. Business justification: Synthesized compound storage location tracking is required for chain of custody, GMP compliance, and controlled substance scheduling. \'storage_location\' plain attribute is a denormalized reference to ']
  synthesis_cro_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Contract synthesis outsourced to CROs/CMOs requires linking synthesis records to the qualified business partner for vendor management, GMP audit trails, and regulatory batch record requirements. Disti']
  synthesis_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: GMP batch records and regulatory submissions require knowing which plant performed compound synthesis. Role prefix \'synthesis_\' distinguishes from manufacturing plant context. No existing plant FK on ']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the synthesis record received formal electronic approval in the ELN by the supervising scientist. Distinct from record creation; captures the GLP-required second-person review event for data integrity.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric batch identifier assigned to the synthesized compound lot. Used for inventory tracking, biological testing assignment, and downstream traceability through the discovery pipeline. Aligns with SAP MM batch management.']
  catalyst varchar [note: 'Name and loading of the catalyst or catalytic system used in the reaction (e.g., Pd(PPh3)4, Pd2(dba)3/XPhos, DMAP). Null if the reaction is uncatalysed. Critical for SAR and process chemistry knowledge management.']
  compound_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the synthesized compound by modality. Distinguishes New Chemical Entities (NCE), New Biological Entities (NBE), peptides, oligonucleotides, PROTACs, fragments, and natural product derivatives. Drives downstream development pathway and regulatory strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NCE|NBE|peptide|oligonucleotide|PROTAC|fragment|natural_product_derivative — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this synthesis record was first created in the system (ELN or data lakehouse). Mandatory audit field for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance and GLP data integrity requirements.']
  eln_experiment_ref varchar [note: 'Unique experiment identifier from the Electronic Lab Notebook system (IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics) that documents the full experimental procedure, observations, and raw data for this synthesis. Provides traceability back to the authoritative source record.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for synthesis failure or cancellation when synthesis_status is \'failed\' or \'cancelled\'. Captures root cause information (e.g., no reaction observed, product decomposition, purification failure, starting material unavailable) to support process learning and SAR knowledge management.']
  glp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this synthesis was conducted in full compliance with Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) regulations. GLP-compliant syntheses are required for compounds entering formal preclinical safety studies. True = GLP compliant; False = non-GLP (exploratory research).']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'GHS/CLP hazard classification of the synthesized compound based on its physicochemical and toxicological properties. Governs safe handling, storage, and disposal procedures in the laboratory. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: non_hazardous|flammable|toxic|corrosive|oxidising|highly_toxic|environmental_hazard — promote to reference product]']
  hplc_purity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Purity of the synthesized compound as determined by High-Performance Liquid Chromatography (HPLC), expressed as area percentage. A Critical Quality Attribute (CQA) for compound registration and screening eligibility. Compounds typically require ≥95% purity for HTS submission.']
  molecular_formula varchar [note: 'Empirical molecular formula of the synthesized compound (e.g., C18H21N3O2). Derived from structural confirmation data. Used for compound identity verification, registry matching, and regulatory documentation.']
  molecular_weight decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated molecular weight of the synthesized compound in grams per mole (g/mol), derived from the confirmed chemical structure. Used for stoichiometric calculations, Lipinski rule-of-five assessment, and compound library profiling.']
  nmr_purity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Purity of the synthesized compound as determined by quantitative Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR) spectroscopy, expressed as percentage. Orthogonal purity assessment to HPLC; used for structural confirmation and identity verification per GLP requirements.']
  product_mass_mg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Actual mass of isolated compound product in milligrams. Used to determine availability for biological screening, ADME assays, and compound library registration. Directly linked to synthesis scale and yield.']
  purification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to purify the crude reaction product to achieve the required purity specification (e.g., column chromatography, preparative HPLC, recrystallisation, trituration). Influences final yield and purity outcomes and informs process development decisions.']
  reaction_duration_hours decimal(7,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time of the synthesis reaction in hours. A Critical Process Parameter (CPP) used in reaction optimization and SAR/process correlation studies.']
  reaction_temperature_c decimal(7,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the synthesis reaction was conducted, expressed in degrees Celsius. A Critical Process Parameter (CPP) that directly influences reaction selectivity, yield, and purity. Captured from ELN experimental conditions.']
  reagents_description varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured description of the key reagents and their stoichiometric equivalents used in the synthesis (e.g., \'K2CO3 (2.0 eq), Pd(OAc)2 (5 mol%), SPhos (10 mol%)\'). Supports reproducibility and GLP documentation requirements.']
  registered_in_library boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the synthesized compound has been registered in the corporate compound library for HTS and biological screening. True = registered and available for screening; False = not yet registered or failed registration criteria (e.g., purity < 95%).']
  smiles_notation varchar [note: 'Canonical SMILES (Simplified Molecular-Input Line-Entry System) string representing the two-dimensional chemical structure of the synthesized compound. Machine-readable format used for cheminformatics, SAR analysis, and compound database registration in Dotmatics.']
  solvent varchar [note: 'Name of the primary solvent or solvent system used in the synthesis reaction (e.g., DMF, THF, DCM, EtOH, water). Influences reaction kinetics, selectivity, and downstream purification strategy. Relevant to ICH Q3C solvent classification for later-stage development.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage condition for the synthesized compound to maintain stability and integrity (e.g., room temperature, 2–8°C, −20°C, −80°C, nitrogen atmosphere, desiccator). Derived from compound physicochemical properties and stability data.']
  structural_confirmation_method varchar [note: 'Analytical technique(s) used to confirm the chemical structure of the synthesized compound (e.g., 1H-NMR, 13C-NMR, LCMS, HRMS, IR, X-ray crystallography). Structural confirmation is mandatory for compound registration and GLP compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1H-NMR|13C-NMR|LCMS|HRMS|IR|X-ray|multiple — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  synthesis_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the synthesis reaction was initiated. Principal business event date for this transaction record. Used for timeline tracking, GLP compliance dating, and SAR trend analysis.']
  synthesis_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for this synthesis event, typically assigned by the ELN (IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics). Follows site-specific numbering conventions (e.g., MED-SYN-000123). Used for cross-referencing in lab notebooks, SAR databases, and regulatory submissions.']
  synthesis_purpose varchar [note: 'Scientific purpose for which this compound was synthesized. Classifies the synthesis within the drug discovery workflow (e.g., hit confirmation from HTS, lead optimisation, SAR exploration, ADME probe, tool compound, library synthesis). Enables portfolio analytics and resource allocation reporting.']
  synthesis_scale_mmol decimal(10,4) [note: 'Molar scale of the synthesis reaction expressed in millimoles (mmol). Determines reagent quantities, expected yield mass, and suitability for downstream biological screening (e.g., HTS vs. focused assay).']
  synthesis_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the synthesis experiment. Tracks progression from planned through execution to completion or failure. Drives workflow routing in the medicinal chemistry pipeline.']
  synthetic_method varchar [note: 'Name or classification of the synthetic methodology applied (e.g., Suzuki coupling, reductive amination, Buchwald-Hartwig amination, solid-phase synthesis). Enables SAR analysis by correlating method with yield and purity outcomes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this synthesis record. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail compliance and data integrity monitoring in GLP environments.']
  yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Isolated yield of the target compound expressed as a percentage of the theoretical maximum based on the limiting reagent. A primary quantitative outcome metric for synthesis efficiency and SAR optimization. Range: 0.00–100.00.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the synthesis of individual compounds by medicinal chemistry teams. Captures compound identifier, synthesis route identifier, synthetic method, reagents used, reaction conditions (temperature, solvent, catalyst), yield percentage, purity by HPLC/NMR, batch number, synthesis date, synthesizing chemist, ELN experiment reference from IDBS/Dotmatics, and GLP compliance flag. Tracks the manufacturing provenance of discovery compounds.'
}

Table discovery.adme_profile {
  adme_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each ADME profiling record in the discovery domain. Primary key for the adme_profile data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — captures a discrete ADME assay event with its own lifecycle and results.']
  compound_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compound under ADME evaluation, linking to the compound master record in the discovery compound library. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE category (compound is the principal resource being profiled).']
  cro_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: DMPK/ADME studies are routinely outsourced to CROs; regulatory submissions (IND) require identifying the performing laboratory as a qualified business partner. No existing CRO link on adme_profile; cr']
  drug_substance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: ADME profiles are directly associated with drug substances for IND/NDA regulatory submissions (Module 2.6 pharmacokinetics). Regulatory affairs and CMC teams require direct adme_profile→drug_substance']
  lead_series_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.lead_series. Business justification: ADME profiling is conducted specifically to support lead series optimization and progression decisions. adme_profile already has compound_id and project_id, but lacks a direct FK to the lead_series be']
  method_validation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.method_validation. Business justification: ICH M3 and FDA BMV guidance require ADME data submitted in IND/NDA to reference validated bioanalytical methods. adme_profile already has submission_id; linking to method_validation enables regulatory']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the drug discovery project under which this ADME profiling was conducted, enabling project-level aggregation of ADME data for compound progression decisions.']
  adme_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite numerical risk score (e.g., 0–100) summarizing overall ADME liability across solubility, permeability, metabolic stability, and DDI risk dimensions. Used for compound ranking and portfolio prioritization in discovery.']
  assay_concentration_um decimal(10,4) [note: 'Concentration of the compound used in the ADME assay incubation, expressed in micromolar (µM). Required for result interpretation and inter-assay comparability; standard concentrations are typically 1–10 µM.']
  assay_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the ADME assay panel was executed in the laboratory. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category (day-level precision for assay scheduling and trending).']
  bbb_kp_uu decimal(8,4) [note: 'Unbound brain-to-plasma concentration ratio (Kp,uu,brain), the quantitative measure of CNS penetration accounting for plasma protein binding. Values >0.3 indicate meaningful CNS exposure; used in CNS drug discovery programs.']
  bbb_penetration boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the compound demonstrates blood-brain barrier penetration based on in vitro BBB assay (e.g., MDCK-MDR1, hCMEC/D3) or in vivo brain-to-plasma ratio. Critical for CNS-targeted and CNS-safety assessments.']
  bioavailability_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Estimated oral bioavailability percentage (%F) derived from permeability, solubility, and metabolic stability data using predictive ADME models. Key compound progression criterion for oral drug candidates.']
  clearance_ml_min_kg decimal(10,4) [note: 'Predicted in vivo hepatic clearance in mL/min/kg, scaled from in vitro microsomal intrinsic clearance using the well-stirred liver model. Used to estimate dose and dosing interval in early drug discovery.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ADME profile record was first created in the system. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records audit trail.']
  cyp2c9_inhibition_ic50_um decimal(10,4) [note: 'Half-maximal inhibitory concentration (IC50) for CYP2C9 enzyme inhibition in µM. CYP2C9 metabolizes warfarin and NSAIDs; inhibition is a critical DDI safety signal for regulatory submissions.']
  cyp2d6_inhibition_ic50_um decimal(10,4) [note: 'Half-maximal inhibitory concentration (IC50) for CYP2D6 enzyme inhibition in µM. CYP2D6 metabolizes ~25% of marketed drugs; inhibition is a major DDI liability assessed by FDA and EMA.']
  cyp3a4_inhibition_ic50_um decimal(10,4) [note: 'Half-maximal inhibitory concentration (IC50) for CYP3A4 enzyme inhibition in µM. CYP3A4 metabolizes ~50% of marketed drugs; inhibition predicts drug-drug interaction (DDI) liability. Key regulatory safety flag.']
  cyp_inhibition_flag boolean [note: 'Composite boolean flag indicating whether the compound exhibits significant CYP inhibition risk (any CYP isoform IC50 below the regulatory concern threshold, typically <10 µM). Used for rapid compound triage and progression gating.']
  efflux_ratio decimal(8,4) [note: 'Ratio of basolateral-to-apical (B→A) over apical-to-basolateral (A→B) permeability in Caco-2 assay. Values >2 indicate active efflux transport (e.g., P-gp substrate), impacting oral bioavailability and CNS penetration.']
  eln_notebook_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this ADME profile to the corresponding experiment entry in the Electronic Lab Notebook (IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics), providing traceability to raw experimental data and GLP documentation.']
  half_life_h decimal(8,2) [note: 'Predicted or measured plasma half-life of the compound in hours, derived from in vitro microsomal clearance data using allometric scaling or measured directly in in vivo PK studies. Informs dosing frequency predictions.']
  hlm_clint_ul_min_mg decimal(10,4) [note: 'Intrinsic metabolic clearance measured in human liver microsomes (HLM), expressed as µL/min/mg protein. Used to predict in vivo hepatic clearance and half-life via well-stirred liver model. Critical for dose prediction.']
  lims_sample_code varchar [note: 'Sample identifier assigned by the LIMS (LabWare or Thermo Fisher SampleManager) for the compound sample used in this ADME assay. Enables traceability from ADME result back to physical sample chain of custody.']
  metabolic_stability_class varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of compound metabolic stability based on microsomal half-life thresholds (e.g., low: t½ <30 min, medium: 30–60 min, high: >60 min). Used in compound progression scoring and SAR triage.']
  microsomal_half_life_min decimal(8,2) [note: 'In vitro half-life of the compound in liver microsome incubation (human or rat), expressed in minutes. Derived from substrate depletion kinetics; used to classify metabolic stability as low/medium/high.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for ADME scientist observations, anomalies, assay deviations, or contextual notes relevant to interpreting the ADME profile results. Supports GLP documentation requirements.']
  permeability_caco2_nm_s decimal(10,4) [note: 'Apparent permeability coefficient (Papp A→B) measured in Caco-2 cell monolayer assay, expressed in nm/s. Predicts intestinal absorption and oral bioavailability. Key ADME parameter for BCS classification.']
  permeability_pampa_nm_s decimal(10,4) [note: 'Passive transcellular permeability measured by PAMPA assay in nm/s. Complements Caco-2 data by isolating passive diffusion component, informing SAR optimization for membrane permeability.']
  pgp_substrate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the compound is identified as a P-glycoprotein (P-gp / MDR1) efflux transporter substrate based on Caco-2 bidirectional permeability or dedicated P-gp inhibition assay. Impacts CNS penetration and oral bioavailability predictions.']
  plasma_protein_binding_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of compound bound to plasma proteins (albumin, alpha-1-acid glycoprotein) measured by equilibrium dialysis or ultrafiltration. Determines free fraction available for pharmacological activity and clearance.']
  ppb_method varchar [note: 'Analytical method used to determine plasma protein binding percentage. Rapid equilibrium dialysis (RED) is the industry standard; method choice affects result interpretation and cross-study comparability.']
  profile_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this ADME profiling run, used in ELN (IDBS/Dotmatics) and LIMS cross-referencing. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the ADME profile record from data entry through scientific review and approval. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_progress|completed|reviewed|approved|invalidated — promote to reference product if additional states are needed]']
  progression_recommendation varchar [note: 'Scientific recommendation for compound progression based on the overall ADME profile assessment. Drives hit-to-lead and lead optimization decisions. Assigned by the ADME scientist upon profile review and approval.']
  rlm_clint_ul_min_mg decimal(10,4) [note: 'Intrinsic metabolic clearance measured in rat liver microsomes (RLM), expressed as µL/min/mg protein. Supports cross-species PK scaling and preclinical-to-clinical translation of metabolic stability data.']
  solubility_method varchar [note: 'Analytical method used to determine aqueous solubility, distinguishing kinetic from thermodynamic measurements which have different interpretive implications for drug development.']
  solubility_um decimal(10,4) [note: 'Measured aqueous solubility of the compound in micromolar (µM), typically from kinetic or thermodynamic solubility assay. Critical for assessing oral bioavailability and formulation feasibility. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category.']
  species varchar [note: 'Biological species from which the biological matrix (microsomes, plasma, cells) was derived for this ADME assay. Enables cross-species PK comparison and allometric scaling for preclinical-to-clinical translation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this ADME profile record. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail requirements.']
  volume_of_distribution_l_kg decimal(8,4) [note: 'Predicted or measured apparent volume of distribution (Vd) in L/kg, reflecting the extent of compound distribution into tissues versus plasma. Informs PK/PD modeling and dose prediction.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing ADME (Absorption, Distribution, Metabolism, Excretion) profiling results for compounds under evaluation. Captures compound identifier, assay panel used, solubility value, permeability (Caco-2/PAMPA), plasma protein binding, microsomal stability (human/rat liver microsomes), CYP inhibition profile, P-glycoprotein substrate flag, blood-brain barrier penetration, half-life, clearance rate, bioavailability estimate, assay date, and ADME scientist. Critical for compound progression decisions.'
}

Business justification: Discovery projects are anchored to a drug substance (the active ingredient under development).   indication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Discovery projects are organized around specific disease indications. Linking to product.indication enables indication-level portfolio management, regulatory pathway planning, and alignment of discove']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: In SAP-based pharma R&D, a discovery project maps to a top-level internal order for cost settlement and program-level financial reporting. Child activities (HTS, SAR, synthesis) already carry internal']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: Discovery projects often operate under collaboration or in-licensing agreements; linkage tracks licensed rights, diligence obligations, milestone triggers, and royalty terms for financial planning and']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Portfolio lineage tracking: connects research program to marketed medicine for ROI analysis, pipeline reporting, strategic planning. Essential for investment decisions, productivity metrics, and comme']
  molecular_target_id varchar [note: 'Standardized identifier for the primary biological target (e.g., UniProt accession, HGNC gene symbol) the project is designed to modulate. Central to SAR analysis and MOA documentation in IDBS/Dotmatics ELN.']

  therapeutic_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: Discovery projects are organized and budgeted by therapeutic area. Linking to the master therapeutic_area entity is fundamental for portfolio management, strategic prioritization reporting, and TA-lev']
  actual_candidate_nomination_date date [note: 'Date on which the development candidate was formally nominated and the project transitioned to preclinical development. Nullable until nomination occurs. Used for cycle time analytics and portfolio performance measurement.']
  atc_code varchar [note: 'WHO ATC classification code assigned to the intended drug product based on its therapeutic target and mechanism. Used for market analysis, competitive intelligence, and regulatory classification.']
  collaboration_partner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary external collaboration partner (CRO, academic institution, or partner pharma company) if external_collaboration_flag is true. Classified confidential as it reveals strategic partnership information.']
  collaboration_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the external collaboration arrangement. Determines applicable contractual, IP, and regulatory frameworks governing data sharing and compound rights.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the discovery project record was first created in the system of record (IDBS/Dotmatics or portfolio management system). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles. Aligns with RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED canonical category.']
  eln_project_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for this discovery project in the IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics ELN system. Enables traceability from the lakehouse silver layer back to the authoritative source system for raw experimental data.']
  external_collaboration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the project involves external partners such as CROs, academic institutions, or other pharmaceutical companies. Triggers additional data governance, IP agreement, and confidentiality controls.']
  glp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the project\'s studies are conducted under GLP regulations as required for regulatory submission. GLP-compliant studies are required for safety data submitted in IND applications.']
  hts_campaign_count varchar [note: 'Number of HTS campaigns executed under this discovery project. Provides a summary indicator of screening activity volume for portfolio reporting and resource utilization analysis.']
  icd10_code varchar [note: 'Standardized ICD-10 disease classification code for the primary disease indication. Enables cross-domain linkage to clinical, regulatory, and medical affairs data and supports regulatory submissions.']
  ip_strategy_note varchar [note: 'Brief description of the IP strategy for the project, including patentability assessment, freedom-to-operate (FTO) status, and filing intentions. Classified confidential as it contains pre-filing IP strategy.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the discovery project record. Required for data lineage tracking, change control auditing, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer. Aligns with RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED canonical category.']
  moa_description varchar [note: 'Scientific description of the proposed mechanism by which the drug candidate modulates the target to achieve therapeutic effect. Captured from ELN records in IDBS/Dotmatics. Critical for IND and NDA/BLA submissions.']
  modality varchar [note: 'Type of therapeutic modality being pursued in the project. Determines applicable regulatory pathway (NDA vs BLA), manufacturing approach (CMO vs CDMO), and GLP/GMP requirements. Distinguishes NCE from NBE programs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: small_molecule|biologic|antibody|cell_therapy|gene_therapy|oligonucleotide|peptide — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  phase varchar [note: 'Current stage of the drug discovery workflow. Progresses from target identification through candidate nomination, representing the standard industry gate-based progression model. Used for portfolio stage-gate reporting and resource planning.']
  portfolio_decision varchar [note: 'Most recent portfolio governance committee decision for the project. Recorded at each stage-gate review and drives resource reallocation and pipeline reporting.']
  portfolio_decision_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent portfolio governance committee decision. Used for audit trail and decision history tracking in conjunction with portfolio_decision.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric project identifier assigned by the portfolio governance office (e.g., DISC-20240001). Used as the business key across IDBS/Dotmatics ELN, SAP project structures, and regulatory submissions. Aligns with the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER canonical category.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the drug discovery project (e.g., \'KRAS G12C Inhibitor Program\'). Classified confidential as it may reveal pipeline strategy prior to public disclosure. Aligns with the IDENTITY_LABEL canonical category.']
  project_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the discovery project as managed in the portfolio governance system. Drives portfolio dashboards and resource allocation decisions. Aligns with the LIFECYCLE_STATUS canonical category.']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the discovery project by the nature of the entity being developed. NCE (New Chemical Entity) and NBE (New Biological Entity) drive distinct regulatory and IP strategies. Aligns with CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE canonical category.']
  projected_candidate_nomination_date date [note: 'Target date by which a development candidate is expected to be nominated and handed off to preclinical development. Key milestone for pipeline planning, IND filing timelines, and portfolio forecasting.']
  sap_wbs_element varchar [note: 'SAP Project System WBS element code that maps this discovery project to the financial controlling structure in SAP S/4HANA. Enables cost allocation, budget tracking, and financial reporting at the project level.']
  sponsor varchar [note: 'Senior executive or therapeutic area head who sponsors and champions the project at the portfolio governance level. Distinct from the project leader who manages day-to-day execution.']
  start_date date [note: 'Official date on which the discovery project was formally initiated and approved by portfolio governance. Used for project duration calculations, milestone tracking, and portfolio timeline reporting.']
  strategic_priority_tier varchar [note: 'Portfolio governance classification indicating the strategic importance of the project (Tier 1 = highest priority, Tier 3 = exploratory). Drives resource allocation, budget prioritization, and executive reporting. Classified confidential as it reflects pipeline strategy.']
  target_organism varchar [note: 'Primary biological system or organism used in discovery assays and preclinical models. Relevant for GLP study design, species selection justification in IND applications, and ADME/PK modeling.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Documented reason for project termination or discontinuation (e.g., safety signal, lack of efficacy, strategic reprioritization, IP conflict). Nullable for active projects. Critical for lessons-learned analysis and portfolio retrospectives. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: safety_signal|lack_of_efficacy|strategic_reprioritization|ip_conflict|resource_constraints|competitive_landscape|regulatory_concern — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Master record for drug discovery research projects organized around one or more therapeutic targets or disease indications. Captures project name, project code, therapeutic area, disease indication (ICD-10/MeSH reference), primary and secondary target identifiers (supporting multi-target programs), project phase (target identification, target validation, hit identification, hit-to-lead, lead optimization, candidate nomination), project status, start date, projected candidate nomination date, project leader, team members, budget allocation, strategic priority tier, external collaboration references, and portfolio governance decisions. Top-level organizational entity for the discovery domain providing project-centric views of all discovery activities.'
}

  decision_date date [note: 'Date on which the nomination committee rendered its formal decision (approved, deferred, or rejected) on the development candidate nomination. May differ from nomination_date if committee review takes multiple sessions.']
  deferral_reason varchar [note: 'Documented rationale for deferral or rejection of the development candidate nomination, capturing the scientific, safety, CMC, IP, or strategic basis for the committee\'s decision. Required for governance records and future re-nomination consideration.']

  first_in_class boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the nominated development candidate represents a first-in-class mechanism of action with no approved drugs acting on the same molecular target. Informs competitive positioning, IP strategy, and regulatory designation eligibility.']
  glp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the key safety and pharmacology studies supporting this nomination were conducted in compliance with Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) regulations. GLP compliance is required for IND-enabling studies submitted to regulatory authorities.']
  handoff_date date [note: 'Date on which the nominated development candidate was formally handed off to the preclinical development team. Marks the transition from discovery to preclinical and initiates IND-enabling studies planning.']
  ip_status varchar [note: 'Current status of intellectual property protection for the nominated development candidate, including patent filing status and Freedom-to-Operate (FTO) assessment outcome. Confidential business data critical for portfolio valuation and licensing strategy.']
  moa_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the compound\'s Mechanism of Action (MOA) — how it interacts with the molecular target to produce the desired pharmacological effect. Sourced from ELN (IDBS/Dotmatics) and used in IND applications and regulatory submissions.']
  modality varchar [note: 'The drug modality class of the nominated development candidate, distinguishing New Chemical Entities (NCE) from New Biological Entities (NBE) and advanced therapy types. Determines regulatory pathway (NDA vs BLA) and CMC strategy.']
  nce_nbe_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the nominated compound as a New Chemical Entity (NCE) — a small molecule not previously approved — or a New Biological Entity (NBE) — a biologic not previously approved. Determines regulatory exclusivity, data protection, and CMC requirements.']
  nomination_committee varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the governance committee responsible for reviewing and rendering the nomination decision (e.g., Drug Discovery Committee, Portfolio Review Board, R&D Leadership Team). Supports governance traceability.']
  nomination_date date [note: 'The formal calendar date on which the development candidate was officially nominated for progression from discovery into preclinical development. This is the principal business event date for this milestone record.']
  nomination_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the development candidate nomination (e.g., DC-ONCO-2024-0042). Used in cross-functional communications, regulatory filings, and portfolio tracking systems such as IDBS/Dotmatics ELN.']
  nomination_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the development candidate nomination decision made by the nomination committee. Drives pipeline progression and resource allocation decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: approved|deferred|rejected|pending_review|withdrawn — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]']
  orphan_drug_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the nominated development candidate is potentially eligible for Orphan Drug Designation (ODD) based on the proposed indication affecting fewer than 200,000 patients in the US (or equivalent EU criteria). Triggers early regulatory strategy planning.']
  portfolio_priority varchar [note: 'Strategic priority classification assigned to the nominated development candidate by the R&D portfolio governance committee, reflecting unmet medical need, competitive landscape, commercial potential, and scientific confidence. Drives resource allocation.']
  regulatory_pathway varchar [note: 'Anticipated regulatory approval pathway for the development candidate based on the proposed indication and unmet medical need (e.g., Standard, Fast Track, Breakthrough Therapy Designation, Accelerated Approval). Drives regulatory strategy and timeline planning.']
  safety_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of early safety and tolerability findings from in vitro toxicology screens, genotoxicity assays, and any preliminary in vivo safety studies conducted under Good Laboratory Practice (GLP). Informs risk assessment for IND-enabling studies.']
  selectivity_profile varchar [note: 'Summary of the compound\'s selectivity against the primary target versus off-target proteins or receptors, derived from selectivity panel screening (e.g., kinase panel, GPCR panel). Informs safety risk and differentiation strategy.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this candidate nomination record originated or was primarily authored (e.g., IDBS E-WorkBook, Dotmatics, LIMS). Supports data lineage and Silver layer provenance tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  target_patient_population varchar [note: 'Description of the intended patient population for the proposed indication, including relevant biomarker stratification, disease stage, or demographic characteristics (e.g., adult patients with EGFR-mutant NSCLC, pediatric patients with SMA). Informs clinical trial design.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the candidate nomination record. Required for change tracking, audit compliance under 21 CFR Part 11, and incremental data pipeline processing.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the formal nomination of a development candidate (DC) compound for progression from discovery into preclinical development. Captures nominated compound identifier, lead series identifier, discovery project identifier, nomination date, therapeutic area, target, proposed indication, key efficacy data summary, key safety data summary, ADME/PK profile summary, IP status, CMC feasibility assessment, nomination decision (approved, deferred, rejected), decision committee, and handoff date to preclinical team. Critical milestone event linking discovery to clinical development pipeline.'
}

  actual_primary_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the last patient completed the primary endpoint assessment (LPLV). Recorded in CTMS and Medidata Rave. Triggers database lock procedures and Clinical Study Report (CSR) preparation.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the first patient was enrolled and received the first dose (FPFV). Recorded in Oracle/Siebel CTMS and Medidata Rave. Compared against planned_start_date to calculate enrollment delay variance.']
  analysis_population varchar [note: 'Statistical analysis population used for the primary endpoint analysis. Intent-to-Treat (ITT) is the regulatory gold standard for confirmatory trials. Per Protocol (PP) is used as a sensitivity analysis. Defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP).']
  blinding_type varchar [note: 'Level of blinding applied to the trial to minimize bias. Double-blind is the gold standard for Phase 3 confirmatory trials. Open-label is common in oncology and rare disease studies. Impacts GCP compliance requirements and unblinding procedures.']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Type of control arm used in the trial. Placebo-controlled is standard for regulatory approval; active comparator is required when placebo is unethical. Historical control is used in rare disease single-arm studies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the trial master record was first created in the clinical data lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for audit trail and data lineage purposes. Populated automatically on insert.']
  csr_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the Clinical Study Report (CSR) was finalized and approved per ICH E3 guidelines. The CSR is a mandatory component of NDA/BLA/MAA regulatory submissions. Tracked in Veeva Vault eTMF.']
  cta_reference varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority-assigned Clinical Trial Authorisation reference number for trials conducted in EU/EEA, UK (MHRA), or other ex-US jurisdictions. Supports multi-regional trial tracking.']
  database_lock_date date [note: 'Date on which the clinical trial database was locked in Medidata Rave EDC following data cleaning, query resolution, and medical review sign-off. Marks the point after which no further data changes are permitted without formal unblinding procedures. Critical milestone for regulatory submissions.']
  eclinical_system_study_reference varchar [note: 'Study identifier assigned in Medidata Rave EDC system. Used to cross-reference clinical data collected in Rave with CTMS records and the trial master record. Enables data lineage tracing from source EDC to the Silver layer.']
  etmf_vault_reference varchar [note: 'Veeva Vault eTMF study-level document identifier linking this trial master record to the corresponding eTMF study folder. Enables direct navigation to trial documentation for regulatory inspection readiness.']
  eudract_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the European Clinical Trials Database (EudraCT / CTIS) for trials conducted in the European Economic Area. Required for EMA regulatory submissions and MAA dossiers.']
  gcp_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current GCP compliance status of the trial based on internal quality oversight, audit findings, and regulatory inspection outcomes. Critical findings may trigger clinical holds. Tracked in MasterControl/TrackWise QMS and Veeva Vault.']
  ind_number varchar [note: 'FDA-assigned IND application number under which this trial is conducted. Links the trial to the regulatory submission record. Required for all Phase I–III trials in the US. May reference multiple INDs for combination studies.']
  interim_analysis_date date [note: 'Planned date for the pre-specified interim efficacy or futility analysis reviewed by the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB). Relevant for adaptive trials and trials with early stopping rules. Defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan.']
  irb_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of IRB/Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) approval for the trial protocol. Mandatory before any patient enrollment. Tracked per jurisdiction. Suspension triggers immediate halt of enrollment per GCP requirements.']
  is_adaptive_design boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the trial employs a pre-specified adaptive design allowing modifications to the trial or statistical procedures based on interim data analysis. Requires additional FDA/EMA guidance compliance per ICH E20.']
  is_pediatric_study boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the trial includes a pediatric population (patients under 18 years). Triggers Pediatric Study Plan (PIP/PSP) requirements under FDA PREA and EMA Paediatric Regulation. Impacts regulatory strategy and labeling.']
  nct_number varchar [note: 'Unique public registry identifier assigned by ClinicalTrials.gov (U.S. National Library of Medicine). Mandatory for trials subject to FDAAA 801 registration requirements. Used in regulatory submissions and public disclosure.']
  phase varchar [note: 'Regulatory development phase of the clinical trial per ICH and FDA/EMA classification. Phase 1/2 and 2/3 denote seamless adaptive designs. Phase 4 denotes post-marketing studies. Drives regulatory strategy and submission planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: phase_1|phase_1_2|phase_2|phase_2_3|phase_3|phase_4|not_applicable — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  planned_enrollment varchar [note: 'Target number of patients to be enrolled in the trial as specified in the approved protocol and IND/CTA. Basis for site activation planning, supply chain forecasting, and budget allocation in SAP.']
  planned_primary_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date for Last Patient Last Visit (LPLV) for the primary endpoint assessment. Registered in ClinicalTrials.gov as the Primary Completion Date. Drives regulatory submission timeline planning for NDA/BLA/MAA.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Planned date for First Patient First Visit (FPFV) — the target date for the first patient to be enrolled and dosed. Used for milestone planning, CRO contracting, and supply chain scheduling. Tracked against actual FPFV for variance analysis.']
  primary_endpoint varchar [note: 'The principal clinical outcome measure that the trial is powered to demonstrate (e.g., \'Overall Survival (OS)\', \'Progression-Free Survival (PFS)\', \'Objective Response Rate (ORR)\', \'Complete Remission Rate\'). Defined in the protocol and statistical analysis plan. Drives regulatory approval decisions.']
  regulatory_submission_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory submission associated with this trial\'s data package. IND for US investigational studies; NDA/BLA for US marketing applications; MAA for EU; CTA for clinical trial authorisation. Links to the regulatory.submission domain. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IND|NDA|BLA|MAA|sNDA|sBLA|CTA — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  short_title varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or acronym-based title used for internal operational reference, CTMS displays, and cross-functional communications (e.g., \'MONARCH-3\', \'KEYNOTE-522\').']
  sponsor_protocol_number varchar [note: 'Internal protocol identifier assigned by Pharmaceuticals as the trial sponsor. Used as the primary cross-system reference in Oracle/Siebel CTMS, Medidata Rave EDC, Veeva Vault eTMF, and SAP. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this master record.']
  study_design_type varchar [note: 'Fundamental design classification of the clinical trial. Determines statistical analysis plan requirements, randomization procedures, and regulatory acceptability. Adaptive designs require additional ICH E20 compliance documentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: randomized|non_randomized|single_arm|crossover|adaptive|basket|umbrella — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  title varchar [note: 'Full official title of the clinical trial as registered in ClinicalTrials.gov and EudraCT/CTIS. Used in regulatory submissions, eTMF, and public disclosures. Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this master record.']
  trial_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the clinical trial. Drives operational workflows, regulatory reporting obligations, and eTMF archival triggers. \'on_hold\' indicates a clinical hold per 21 CFR 312.42. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this master record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the trial master record was last modified in the Silver layer. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and change data capture (CDC) in Databricks.']

  Note: 'Master record for each clinical trial (study) conducted by Pharmaceuticals, covering all phases (I–IV). Captures investigational product, therapeutic area, phase, study design (randomized, blinded, open-label), primary and secondary endpoints (ORR, PFS, OS, QoL), IND/CTA reference numbers, EudraCT/NCT identifiers, sponsor, CRO partner, planned vs. actual start/end dates, GCP compliance status, regulatory submission references (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA), overall trial lifecycle state, and key operational milestones (FPFV, LPLV, database lock, CSR completion, interim analysis dates) with planned vs. actual tracking and variance commentary. Authoritative SSOT for all trial-level metadata and timeline milestones consumed by regulatory submissions and eTMF in Veeva Vault.'
}

Table clinical.protocol {
  protocol_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each versioned clinical trial protocol record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the protocol data product.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Protocol design targets specific brand indications and label claims. Enables strategic alignment between clinical development programs and commercial planning, supports evidence generation strategy, a']
  indication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Protocol defines the specific indication being studied; IND/CTA submissions and protocol amendments formally reference the indication record. Plain \'indication\' text column denormalizes the product.in']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Protocols drive R&D cost tracking via internal orders for trial execution budgets (personnel, materials, CRO costs). Standard pharma cost accounting requires protocol-level cost center assignment for ']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: Co-development and in-licensing agreements govern specific protocol conduct, endpoints, and data rights. In pharma partnerships, a licensee may run a protocol under a distinct licensing agreement from']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Protocols define study design for specific medicinal products. Required for IND/CTA submissions, regulatory dossiers (eCTD Module 5), and ensuring protocol design aligns with product CMC characteristi']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Novel protocol designs (adaptive trials, biomarker-driven selection, dosing regimens) may constitute patentable method-of-treatment inventions. Linking supports IP strategy, competitive intelligence, ']
  post_approval_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.post_approval_commitment. Business justification: A specific protocol version fulfills a post-approval commitment study requirement. Regulatory teams must link the protocol to the PAC to report fulfillment status to FDA/EMA, confirm the study design ']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.project. Business justification: Clinical protocols are authored against a specific discovery project. IND/CTA filings and regulatory submissions require tracing the protocol to its originating discovery project for portfolio governa']
  regulatory_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.application. Business justification: Protocols are written to support specific regulatory applications (IND, NDA, BLA). Regulatory teams must track which protocols support which applications for submission planning, health authority meet']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Clinical protocols must reference the approved IMP quality specification to define acceptable product parameters (potency, purity, storage) per IMPD/IND requirements. This link supports regulatory sub']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Protocol amendments must be submitted to health authorities (FDA IND amendment, EMA CTA amendment). Regulatory operations teams track which submission corresponds to each protocol version for amendmen']
  therapeutic_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: Protocol design, endpoint selection, and statistical methodology are TA-specific. Plain \'therapeutic_area\' text column denormalizes the master. Medical affairs and regulatory teams filter protocols by']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent clinical trial to which this protocol version belongs. Links the protocol to the overarching study record in the CTMS.']
  allocation_ratio varchar [note: 'Ratio of subjects assigned to each treatment arm (e.g., 1:1, 2:1, 1:1:1). Directly impacts sample size calculations and statistical power. Documented in the randomization section of the protocol.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential integer counter for protocol amendments. Zero (0) indicates the original protocol; each subsequent amendment increments by one. Supports amendment history tracking and regulatory submission traceability.']
  amendment_rationale varchar [note: 'Documented justification for protocol changes introduced in this amendment version. Required by regulatory agencies to assess the impact of changes on subject safety, data integrity, and study validity. Null for original protocol (amendment_number = 0).']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the amendment\'s regulatory significance. \'Substantial\' amendments require prior regulatory authority approval; \'non_substantial\' require notification only; \'administrative\' are minor corrections. Determines regulatory submission pathway.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor\'s internal governance (e.g., Clinical Development Committee) formally approved this protocol version for implementation. Distinct from IRB/IEC approval; triggers eTMF filing in Veeva Vault.']
  blinding_method varchar [note: 'Level of masking applied to the trial to prevent bias. Double-blind masks both subject and investigator; open-label has no masking. Critical for GCP compliance and regulatory acceptance of efficacy data.']
  clinicaltrials_gov_number varchar [note: 'National Clinical Trial (NCT) identifier assigned by ClinicalTrials.gov upon trial registration. Mandatory for FDA-regulated trials under FDAAA 801 and required for publication in peer-reviewed journals per ICMJE policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this protocol record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP data integrity standards.']
  design_type varchar [note: 'Structural design of the clinical trial. Determines how treatment arms are organized and how subjects are allocated. Informs statistical analysis plan and regulatory review strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: parallel|crossover|factorial|single_arm|basket|umbrella|platform — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dosing_regimen varchar [note: 'Description of the investigational product dose, route of administration, frequency, and duration for each treatment arm (e.g., Drug X 200mg IV Q3W for 12 cycles). Directly linked to CMC and IND/NDA/BLA submissions.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this protocol version is operationally effective at investigational sites. For amendments, this is the date sites must implement the changes. Drives site notification and training requirements.']
  eudract_number varchar [note: 'European Union Drug Regulating Authorities Clinical Trials (EudraCT) registration number assigned by EMA for clinical trials conducted in the EU/EEA. Required for MAA submissions and EU Clinical Trial Regulation compliance.']
  exclusion_criteria varchar [note: 'Conditions or characteristics that disqualify a subject from enrollment (e.g., prior therapy, comorbidities, contraindications). Complements inclusion criteria to define the eligible study population.']
  exploratory_objectives varchar [note: 'Hypothesis-generating objectives not powering the study, such as biomarker analyses, translational research endpoints, or health economics and outcomes research (HEOR) assessments. Supports future study design and RWE generation.']
  gcp_compliance_attestation boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this protocol version has been formally attested as compliant with ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice guidelines. Required for regulatory submissions and audit readiness.']
  inclusion_criteria varchar [note: 'Eligibility criteria that subjects must meet to be enrolled in the trial (e.g., age range, diagnosis, performance status, biomarker positivity). Defines the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) population. Stored as structured free text per protocol.']
  ind_number varchar [note: 'FDA-assigned Investigational New Drug (IND) application number under which this protocol is filed. Links the protocol to the regulatory submission record. Required for US clinical trial conduct under 21 CFR Part 312.']
  irb_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) granted approval for this protocol version. Milestone date for site activation and GCP compliance documentation.']
  irb_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the IRB/IEC approval for this protocol version. Required for GCP compliance and site activation. Tracked in Veeva Vault eTMF under the Trial Master File structure.']
  phase varchar [note: 'Regulatory development phase of the clinical trial as defined by ICH and FDA guidelines. Determines the study objectives, patient population size, and regulatory pathway (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA).']
  planned_sample_size varchar [note: 'Total number of subjects planned for enrollment as determined by the sample size justification in the protocol. Based on statistical power calculations for the primary endpoint. Drives site activation and enrollment planning.']
  primary_endpoint varchar [note: 'The principal clinical outcome measure used to evaluate the primary objective (e.g., Overall Survival (OS), Progression-Free Survival (PFS), Objective Response Rate (ORR)). Drives sample size calculation and statistical analysis plan.']
  primary_objective varchar [note: 'The principal scientific and clinical objective of the trial as stated in the protocol. Defines the primary endpoint and the key question the study is designed to answer (e.g., demonstrate superiority in Overall Survival).']
  protocol_number varchar [note: 'Sponsor-assigned unique protocol identifier used across all regulatory submissions, eTMF filings, and CTMS records. Externally known identifier referenced in IND/NDA/BLA/MAA dossiers.']
  protocol_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this protocol version. \'draft\' indicates work-in-progress; \'under_review\' indicates IRB/IEC or sponsor review; \'approved\' indicates fully authorized for use; \'superseded\' indicates replaced by a newer amendment; \'retired\' indicates withdrawn.']
  randomization_scheme varchar [note: 'Method used to randomly allocate subjects to treatment arms. Stratified randomization accounts for prognostic factors; adaptive randomization adjusts allocation ratios based on accumulating data. Documented in the protocol and statistical analysis plan.']
  sample_size_justification varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the statistical assumptions and power calculations supporting the planned sample size, including assumed effect size, alpha level, power, and dropout rate. Required by ICH E9(R1) and regulatory agencies.']
  sap_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier or version reference for the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) associated with this protocol version. The SAP details the pre-specified statistical methods for analyzing primary and secondary endpoints per ICH E9(R1).']
  short_title varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or lay-language title of the protocol used in operational communications, CTMS displays, and site-facing materials. Distinct from the full regulatory title.']
  stratification_factors varchar [note: 'Clinical or demographic variables used to stratify randomization to ensure balanced treatment arms (e.g., disease stage, ECOG performance status, geographic region, biomarker status). Stored as free text per protocol specification.']
  study_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the study design indicating whether the trial involves an active intervention, passive observation, or special access program. Aligns with ClinicalTrials.gov and EudraCT registration categories.']
  title varchar [note: 'Full official title of the clinical trial protocol as it appears on the protocol document and regulatory submissions. Typically includes the investigational product name, indication, and study design descriptor.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this protocol record. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP data integrity. Enables change detection in the Silver Layer lakehouse pipeline.']
  veeva_vault_doc_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault eTMF where the authoritative protocol document is stored. Enables direct traceability from the data product record to the source document in the electronic Trial Master File.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier for this protocol document (e.g., 1.0, 2.0, 2.1). Each approved amendment increments the version. Used to distinguish original protocol from subsequent amendments.']
  visit_schedule_summary varchar [note: 'High-level description of the Schedule of Assessments (SoA) including number of study visits, key assessment windows, and follow-up duration. Detailed SoA is maintained in the protocol appendix and Medidata Rave EDC configuration.']

  Note: 'Formal clinical trial protocol document master, representing each approved version of the study protocol. Tracks protocol number, version, amendment history and rationale, approval date, IRB/IEC approval reference, study objectives (primary, secondary, exploratory), inclusion/exclusion criteria, dosing regimen, visit schedule (schedule of assessments), randomization design (scheme type, stratification factors, allocation ratio, blinding method), statistical analysis plan reference, sample size justification, and GCP compliance attestation. Linked to the parent trial and managed in Veeva Vault eTMF. Each amendment creates a new versioned protocol record with change justification.'
}

Business justification: Sites receive IMP through specific distribution nodes (depots/warehouses); GDP compliance requires documented supply chain routing. Clinical supply management uses this for shipment planning, temperat']

  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Sites must comply with jurisdiction-specific regulations and inspection requirements. Business justification: Investigational sites are registered as business partners (vendors) in pharma masterdata for site fee payments, investigator grant processing, and vendor qualification audits. This is a standard accou']
  site_internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Sites incur costs tracked via internal orders for site activation, monitoring, patient recruitment, and investigator fees. Essential for trial budget management, site payment processing, and cost-per-']
  site_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Sites are owned/operated by legal entities (hospitals, research institutions, private practices). Required for clinical trial agreements (CTAs), payment processing, insurance/liability coverage, and r']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the site close-out visit was completed and the site was formally closed. All essential documents must be archived in eTMF (Veeva Vault) by this date.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the investigational site record was first created in the clinical data lakehouse. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP data integrity standards.']
  enrolled_subject_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of subjects who have been randomized and enrolled at this site to date. Compared against enrollment_target to assess site performance and trigger enrollment support actions.']
  enrollment_target varchar [note: 'Planned number of subjects to be enrolled at this site as agreed between the sponsor and principal investigator. Used for enrollment forecasting, site performance benchmarking, and IMP supply planning.']
  etmf_site_folder_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the site-level folder in the Veeva Vault eTMF system. Links the site master record to its essential document archive for GCP inspection readiness and regulatory submission support.']
  feasibility_date date [note: 'Date on which the site feasibility assessment was completed, confirming the site\'s capability, patient population, and infrastructure to conduct the trial. First milestone in the site activation timeline tracked in CTMS.']
  fpfv_actual_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the first patient first visit (FPFV) occurred at this site. Compared against fpfv_target_date to measure site activation performance and enrollment timeline adherence.']
  fpfv_target_date date [note: 'Planned date for the first patient first visit (FPFV) at this site, representing the target enrollment start. Used for enrollment forecasting and site performance tracking against the clinical development timeline.']
  gcp_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current GCP inspection status of the investigational site by a regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA). Critical for site risk assessment, regulatory submission support, and pre-approval inspection (PAI) readiness.']
  investigational_site_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the investigational site within the clinical program. Drives CRA monitoring schedules, IMP shipment eligibility, and enrollment reporting. Values: selected (identified for participation), activated (SIV complete, ready to enroll), enrolling (actively screening/randomizing subjects), on_hold (enrollment paused), closed (no further activity), terminated (early discontinuation).']
  irb_iec_approval_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current IRB/IEC approval for the trial protocol at this site expires. Triggers renewal workflow; enrollment must be suspended if approval lapses without renewal.']
  irb_iec_approval_number varchar [note: 'Official approval reference number issued by the IRB/IEC for the clinical trial protocol at this site. Mandatory for site activation and must be filed in the eTMF (Veeva Vault) prior to first patient first visit (FPFV).']
  irb_iec_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) of record responsible for ethical oversight of clinical trial activities at this site. Required for GCP compliance and eTMF documentation in Veeva Vault.']
  is_gcp_trained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all required site staff have completed GCP training as documented in the eTMF (Veeva Vault) prior to site activation. Mandatory pre-condition for site initiation per ICH E6(R2).']
  is_lead_site boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this site is designated as the lead or coordinating site for a multi-center trial. Lead sites often host the coordinating investigator and serve as the primary regulatory contact.']
  last_gcp_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent GCP inspection conducted at this site by a regulatory authority. Used to assess inspection recency and site compliance history for NDA/BLA/MAA submissions.']
  lplv_actual_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the last patient last visit (LPLV) occurred at this site, marking the end of active patient participation. Triggers site close-out visit scheduling and IMP reconciliation.']
  regulatory_authority_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory authority with jurisdiction over the investigational site\'s clinical trial activities. Determines applicable GCP guidelines, inspection readiness requirements, and submission standards (IND/NDA/BLA/MAA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|NMPA|WHO|OTHER — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  screen_failure_count varchar [note: 'Number of subjects who were screened but failed to meet eligibility criteria at this site. Used to calculate screen failure rate and assess protocol feasibility and patient population fit.']
  selection_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor formally selected the site for participation in the clinical trial, following feasibility review. Marks transition to site status \'selected\' in CTMS.']
  site_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the investigational site by institutional category, used for site selection strategy, feasibility analysis, and enrollment benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: academic_medical_center|community_hospital|private_clinic|research_center|cro_managed|veterans_affairs|pediatric_center — promote to reference product]']
  siv_date date [note: 'Date on which the Site Initiation Visit (SIV) was conducted by the Clinical Research Associate (CRA). Confirms site readiness, staff training, and IMP accountability procedures. Required before first patient enrollment.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the investigational site (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London). Used for accurate timestamp alignment of EDC data from Medidata Rave, adverse event reporting timelines, and CRA scheduling.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the investigational site record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11, and incremental data pipeline processing.']

  Note: 'Master record for each clinical investigational site (hospital, clinic, research center) participating in one or more trials. Captures site name, address, country, site number, IRB/IEC of record, site activation milestones (feasibility, selection, SIV, FPFV target), site closure date, enrollment capacity, GCP inspection history, site status (selected, activated, enrolling, on-hold, closed), regulatory authority jurisdiction, and CRA assignment for site initiation and ongoing oversight. Serves as the geographic and operational anchor for site-level enrollment, monitoring, and IMP accountability activities.'
}

Table clinical.principal_investigator {
  principal_investigator_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the principal investigator or sub-investigator master record in the clinical domain. Serves as the primary key for this entity across all downstream clinical data products.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.contract. Business justification: Each principal investigator\'s trial participation is governed by a signed investigator agreement (contract). Direct FK enables financial disclosure tracking, FMV compliance validation, and OIG/SAM scr']
  ctms_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this investigator as recorded in Oracle Clinical / Siebel CTMS. Used for cross-system reconciliation and lineage tracing back to the operational CTMS record.']
  hcp_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to hcp.hcp_master.hcp_master_id — Principal investigators are HCPs — linking PI records to HCP master enables unified view of an HCPs relationship with the company across clinical, commercial, and medical affairs activities.']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Principal investigators are registered with and subject to debarment checks by specific health authorities (FDA debarment list, EMA disqualification register). Regulatory compliance teams must link ea']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.investigational_site. Business justification: A Principal Investigator (PI) record in a CTMS is fundamentally associated with a specific investigational site — the PI conducts the trial at that site. The principal_investigator table carries per-s']
  investigator_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Principal investigators receive investigator grants and honoraria requiring registration as business partners for payment processing, financial disclosure compliance (Sunshine Act), and vendor qualifi']
  license_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: PIs hold medical licenses in specific countries. Required for regulatory compliance verification, license validation against country-specific medical boards, Form FDA 1572 validation, and investigator']
  reporter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.reporter. Business justification: Principal Investigators are primary reporters of trial SAEs/SUSARs to sponsors. Linking PI identity to PV reporter records enables causality assessment tracking, follow-up correspondence, and regulato']
  therapeutic_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: Site feasibility assessments and investigator qualification processes match PIs to trials by therapeutic area expertise. Plain \'therapeutic_area_expertise\' text column denormalizes the master. Clinica']
