  country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the investigator primarily practices and conducts clinical trial activities. Determines applicable regulatory framework (FDA, EMA, PMDA, NMPA, etc.).']
  cv_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL pointing to the investigator\'s current curriculum vitae stored in Veeva Vault eTMF. The CV is a mandatory eTMF document (TMF Reference Model artifact 01.01) required for regulatory submissions and site qualification.']
  delegation_log_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier pointing to the investigator\'s delegation of authority log stored in Veeva Vault eTMF or site trial master file. The delegation log documents which trial tasks have been assigned to sub-investigators and site staff, as required by GCP.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary professional email address of the investigator used for trial-related communications, protocol amendments, and safety notifications. Distinct from personal email; should be institutional or practice-based.']
  fda_debarment_check_date date [note: 'Date on which the investigator was last verified against the FDA Debarment List and the Office of Inspector General (OIG) exclusion database. Mandatory pre-engagement check and periodic re-verification per sponsor SOPs.']
  financial_disclosure_date date [note: 'Date on which the investigator\'s financial disclosure form (FDA Form 3454 or 3455) was most recently submitted or updated. Required for NDA/BLA submissions to FDA.']
  financial_disclosure_status varchar [note: 'Status of the investigator\'s financial disclosure form (FDA Form 3454/3455) as required under 21 CFR Part 54. Indicates whether the investigator has disclosed financial interests that could affect trial integrity. \'Disclosed\' means the form has been submitted and reviewed.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal given name of the principal investigator or sub-investigator as it appears on their medical license and regulatory submissions.']
  form_1572_date date [note: 'Date on which the investigator signed FDA Form 1572 (Statement of Investigator) for the most recent trial engagement. Form 1572 is a legal commitment by the investigator to comply with FDA regulations and GCP. Required for all IND-regulated trials in the US.']
  gcp_training_date date [note: 'Date on which the investigator most recently completed GCP training as required by ICH E6(R2). Mandatory for trial participation; triggers re-training alerts when approaching the validity window (typically 2-3 years).']
  gcp_training_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current GCP training certification expires. Investigators with expired GCP training must not conduct trial activities. Drives compliance monitoring and site qualification workflows.']
  gcp_training_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or platform that delivered the GCP training (e.g., CITI Program, TransCelerate, sponsor-internal training). Supports audit trail for regulatory inspections.']
  investigator_role varchar [note: 'GCP-defined role of the individual at the investigational site: Principal Investigator (PI) who bears overall responsibility, Sub-Investigator (sub-I) who assists under PI supervision, or Coordinating Investigator who coordinates multi-site activities.']
  investigator_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the investigator record. \'Active\' indicates the investigator is qualified and currently engaged in trial conduct. \'Disqualified\' reflects FDA or regulatory authority debarment or disqualification actions.']
  irb_registration_number varchar [note: 'Registration number of the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) with which the investigator\'s site is registered. Required for FDA-regulated trials under 21 CFR Part 56 and ICH E6(R2).']
  is_debarred boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investigator is currently listed on the FDA Debarment List or OIG exclusion database. A value of True immediately disqualifies the investigator from participating in any FDA-regulated clinical trial.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal family name of the principal investigator or sub-investigator as it appears on their medical license and regulatory submissions.']
  license_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the investigator\'s medical license expires. Triggers re-qualification workflows to ensure continued GCP-compliant trial conduct. Null if the license has no expiry.']
  license_issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory or professional body that issued the medical license (e.g., California Medical Board, General Medical Council UK, Ordre des Médecins France). Required for cross-border trial conduct verification.']
  medical_license_number varchar [note: 'State or national medical license number issued by the relevant licensing authority. Required for regulatory submissions and site qualification. Uniquely identifies the investigator\'s right to practice medicine.']
  medical_specialty varchar [note: 'Primary board-certified or recognized medical specialty of the investigator (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Cardiology, Neurology). Used to match investigator expertise to trial therapeutic area and protocol requirements.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the investigator as recorded on official credentials. Used for disambiguation when first and last names are not unique.']
  npi_number varchar [note: '10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) assigned by CMS to US-based healthcare providers. Used for US-specific investigator identification, cross-referencing with HCP databases, and commercial engagement separation.']
  orcid_identifier varchar [note: 'Persistent digital identifier (ORCID iD) assigned to the investigator by the ORCID registry. Used to uniquely identify researchers across publications, grant applications, and clinical trial registries (e.g., ClinicalTrials.gov).']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary professional phone number for the investigator, used for urgent safety communications, protocol queries, and site monitoring contact. Includes country code for international trials.']
  prior_trial_count varchar [note: 'Total number of GCP-regulated clinical trials in which the investigator has previously served as PI or sub-investigator. Key feasibility metric used by sponsors and CROs during site selection.']
  professional_title varchar [note: 'Highest relevant professional or academic degree held by the investigator (e.g., MD, PhD, PharmD). Used in regulatory submissions and delegation logs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MD|DO|PhD|MD-PhD|PharmD|MBBS|MBChB|DVM|Other — promote to reference product]']
  qualification_verified_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor or CRO last verified the investigator\'s qualifications, credentials, and GCP training as part of the site qualification or re-qualification process. Supports audit trail for regulatory inspections.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the principal investigator master record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and GCP-compliant record management per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the principal investigator master record was most recently modified. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP data integrity requirements.']
  regulatory_authority_registration_number varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by a national regulatory authority for the investigator\'s registration to conduct clinical trials (e.g., FDA investigator registration number, EudraCT investigator ID, MHRA registration). Required for IND/NDA/BLA/MAA submissions.']
  sub_specialty varchar [note: 'Secondary or sub-specialty area of clinical expertise (e.g., Hematologic Oncology, Pediatric Immunology). Provides finer-grained matching for complex or rare disease trials.']
  years_of_clinical_trial_experience varchar [note: 'Number of years the investigator has been conducting GCP-regulated clinical trials. Used during site feasibility assessments to evaluate investigator experience relative to protocol complexity.']

  Note: 'Master record for each Principal Investigator (PI) and sub-investigator responsible for conducting a clinical trial at a site. Captures investigator name, medical license number, specialty, GCP training certification date, CV reference, financial disclosure status, delegation log reference, and regulatory authority registration. Distinct from the HCP domain (which covers commercial HCP engagement) — this entity is specifically scoped to GCP-regulated trial conduct roles.'
}

Table clinical.clinical_enrollment {
  clinical_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each subject enrollment and randomization record within a clinical trial. Primary key for the enrollment data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  dispensed_inventory_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: Subject-level IMP accountability requires knowing which inventory lot was dispensed to each enrolled subject. GCP IMP accountability logs and pharmacovigilance lot traceability per subject are regulat']
  investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.investigational_product. Business justification: Enrolled subjects are assigned to specific investigational product kits at randomization. While enrollment has treatment_arm_code, linking to investigational_product provides the actual IMP kit assign']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the subject was screened and enrolled. Corresponds to the site record in Oracle CTMS / Medidata Rave.']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Tracks which medicinal product each subject received. Required for product-specific safety reporting (ICH E2A), efficacy analysis by product, and regulatory queries about subject exposure in multi-pro']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial subject (patient) record. Links to the subject master record sourced from Medidata Rave EDC.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific protocol version under which the subject was enrolled. A trial may have multiple protocol amendments; this captures the version active at enrollment.']
  rems_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.rems. Business justification: REMS programs (e.g., iPLEDGE, Clozapine REMS) require individual patient enrollment and compliance tracking. Each clinical enrollment in a REMS-governed trial must link to the REMS program for FDA-man']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent clinical trial under which this subject was screened and enrolled. Links to the trial master record in Oracle CTMS.']
  allocation_ratio varchar [note: 'The ratio of subjects allocated to each treatment arm as defined in the randomization scheme (e.g., \'1:1\', \'2:1\', \'1:1:1\'). Determines the probability of assignment to each arm. Sourced from the RTSM randomization schedule.']
  blinding_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the current blinding state of the subject\'s treatment assignment. \'blinded\' = treatment assignment concealed; \'open_label\' = treatment known from outset; \'unblinded\' = previously blinded assignment has been revealed. Critical for data integrity and regulatory compliance.']
  block_size varchar [note: 'The number of subjects per randomization block used in the blocked randomization scheme. Confidential as knowledge of block size could compromise blinding. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM randomization parameters.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject completed all protocol-required study procedures and follow-up visits. Populated only when enrollment_status = completed. Used for study close-out and final analysis datasets.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was first created in the source system (Medidata Rave RTSM or Oracle CTMS). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2) GCP guidelines.']
  ctms_subject_key varchar [note: 'The natural/business key for this subject enrollment record as assigned by Oracle Clinical / Siebel CTMS. Used for cross-system reconciliation between CTMS and Medidata Rave EDC. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  data_lock_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the subject\'s enrollment and randomization data has been locked for analysis (database lock). True = data locked and frozen for statistical analysis; False = data still open for query resolution or updates. Critical for regulatory submission readiness.']
  eligibility_confirmed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the subject has been confirmed as meeting all inclusion criteria and none of the exclusion criteria as defined in the trial protocol. True = eligible; False = not eligible or assessment pending. Drives enrollment decision in RTSM.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject was formally enrolled into the clinical trial following eligibility confirmation. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the enrollment transaction. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subject within the enrollment and randomization workflow. Drives Intent-to-Treat (ITT), modified ITT, and Per-Protocol (PP) population definitions. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM and Oracle CTMS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: screened|screen_failed|enrolled|randomized|withdrawn|completed|discontinued — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]']
  informed_consent_confirmed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a valid, signed Informed Consent Form (ICF) has been confirmed for this subject prior to any study procedures. True = ICF confirmed; False = ICF not confirmed or pending. Mandatory GCP compliance check.']
  informed_consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject signed the Informed Consent Form (ICF), confirming voluntary participation in the trial. Must precede any study-specific procedures per GCP requirements. Critical for regulatory submissions.']
  itt_population_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the subject is included in the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) analysis population. True = subject is part of the ITT population (all randomized subjects regardless of protocol adherence). Drives primary efficacy analysis per ICH E9.']
  mitt_population_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the subject is included in the modified Intent-to-Treat (mITT) analysis population. Criteria for mITT inclusion are protocol-defined (e.g., received at least one dose). Supports secondary efficacy analyses.']
  pp_population_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the subject is included in the Per-Protocol (PP) analysis population. True = subject completed the trial per protocol without major deviations. Used for sensitivity analyses in NDA/BLA/MAA submissions.']
  randomization_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned by the RTSM system at the point of randomization, linking the subject to their treatment arm assignment within the randomization schedule. Confidential during blinded phase; revealed only upon unblinding. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM.']
  randomization_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject was randomized and assigned to a treatment arm via the RTSM system. May differ from enrollment_date in trials with a run-in period. Null for screen-failed or non-randomized subjects.']
  rave_subject_key varchar [note: 'The natural/business key for this subject enrollment record as assigned by Medidata Rave EDC/RTSM. Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing back to the authoritative EDC source system.']
  screen_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Coded or free-text reason why a subject failed screening and was not enrolled. Populated only when enrollment_status = screen_failed. Used for protocol feasibility analysis and site performance monitoring. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC screen failure CRF.']
  screening_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject first presented at the investigational site for screening assessments. Marks the start of the eligibility evaluation period. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC.']
  screening_number varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to a subject at the point of screening, prior to eligibility confirmation. Format is site-specific and assigned sequentially by the RTSM system in Medidata Rave. Used to track screen failures and pre-enrollment activity.']
  stratification_factor_1 varchar [note: 'Value of the first stratification factor used to balance treatment arm assignments across key prognostic variables (e.g., disease stage, geographic region, biomarker status). Defined in the trial protocol and captured at randomization in RTSM.']
  stratification_factor_2 varchar [note: 'Value of the second stratification factor used to balance treatment arm assignments (e.g., prior therapy, performance status, age group). Null if the trial uses fewer than two stratification factors. Captured at randomization in RTSM.']
  stratification_factor_3 varchar [note: 'Value of the third stratification factor used to balance treatment arm assignments (e.g., country, mutation status, weight band). Null if the trial uses fewer than three stratification factors. Captured at randomization in RTSM.']
  subject_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to a subject upon successful enrollment into the trial. Typically formatted as site-number-sequential-ID (e.g., 001-001). Assigned by Medidata Rave RTSM upon eligibility confirmation and enrollment confirmation.']
  treatment_arm_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the treatment arm (e.g., active drug, placebo, comparator, dose level) to which the subject was assigned at randomization. Confidential in blinded trials. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM randomization schedule.']
  treatment_arm_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the treatment arm assignment (e.g., \'Drug A 100mg QD\', \'Placebo\', \'Drug B 200mg BID\'). Confidential in blinded trials. Complements treatment_arm_code for reporting and analysis.']
  unblinding_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject\'s treatment assignment was unblinded. Populated only when blinding_status = unblinded. Emergency unblinding events must be documented per GCP. Null for blinded or open-label subjects.']
  unblinding_reason varchar [note: 'Coded reason for the unblinding of the subject\'s treatment assignment. Emergency unblinding (e.g., for medical management of a Serious Adverse Event) must be documented and reported per GCP and pharmacovigilance requirements. Null for blinded or open-label subjects.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was last modified in the source system. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2). Used for incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject withdrew from the trial or was discontinued. Populated only when enrollment_status = withdrawn. Used for survival analysis (censoring) and dropout rate reporting.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Coded or free-text reason for subject withdrawal or discontinuation from the trial (e.g., adverse event, consent withdrawal, lost to follow-up, protocol deviation, investigator decision). Null for active or completed subjects. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: adverse_event|consent_withdrawal|lost_to_follow_up|protocol_deviation|investigator_decision|sponsor_decision|death|other — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each subject\'s complete enrollment and randomization journey within a clinical trial. Tracks screening number, subject number, enrollment date, informed consent confirmation, eligibility assessment (inclusion/exclusion criteria verification), screen failure reason, randomization date, randomization code, treatment arm assignment, stratification factors, blinding status, allocation ratio, block size reference, unblinding event details (if applicable), and enrollment/randomization status (screened, screen-failed, enrolled, randomized, withdrawn, completed). Supports ITT, modified ITT, and per-protocol population definitions. Authoritative SSOT for all subject-level randomization data including scheme parameters and individual assignments. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM (Randomization and Trial Supply Management) and Oracle CTMS.'
}

Table clinical.visit {
  visit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each patient visit record within the clinical trial data product on the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key.']
  clinical_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.enrollment. Business justification: Patient visits occur within the context of a specific enrollment. While visit already has participant_id, trial_id, and site_id, linking to enrollment provides the complete enrollment context includin']
  clinical_supply_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.clinical_supply_order. Business justification: At each dispensing visit, the IRT system fulfills a clinical supply order. Linking visit to clinical_supply_order supports per-visit IMP dispensation accountability logs required under GCP and IMP acc']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the visit took place. Links to the site master record in CTMS (Oracle/Siebel).']
  investigator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the principal investigator or sub-investigator who conducted or supervised this visit. Links to the investigator master record in CTMS (Oracle/Siebel).']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial subject (patient) who attended this visit. Links to the subject/patient master record in the EDC (Medidata Rave) and CTMS (Oracle/Siebel).']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial protocol version that governs the schedule of assessments for this visit. Links to the protocol master record.']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (trial) under which this visit was conducted. Links to the study master record in CTMS.']
  actual_date date [note: 'The date on which the visit actually occurred as recorded in the EDC (Medidata Rave). Corresponds to the CDISC CDASH VISDAT field. Null if the visit was missed or cancelled.']
  ae_reported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more Adverse Events (AEs) were reported at or since the previous visit (True) or not (False). Triggers downstream pharmacovigilance workflows in Argus Safety / Oracle AERS.']
  arm_name varchar [note: 'The name of the treatment arm or study arm to which the subject was assigned at the time of this visit (e.g., \'Drug A 100mg\', \'Placebo\', \'Drug A + Drug B Combination\'). Sourced from CTMS and EDC randomization records.']
  blinding_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the blinding status of the subject and/or investigator at the time of this visit. Unblinded records are restricted to authorized personnel only and are critical for data integrity in double-blind trials.']
  completion_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of protocol-required assessments and procedures that were completed at this visit (0.00–100.00). Supports partial visit analysis and data completeness reporting for regulatory submissions.']
  conmed_updated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject\'s concomitant medication list was reviewed and updated at this visit (True) or not (False). Supports safety and drug-drug interaction analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this visit record was first created in the source EDC system (Medidata Rave). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  data_entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the visit data was first entered into the EDC system (Medidata Rave) by the site staff. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2).']
  data_lock_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the visit record was locked in the EDC (Medidata Rave), preventing further data entry or modification without an authorized unlock. Null if the record has not yet been locked.']
  day_from_reference varchar [note: 'The study day number calculated from the subject\'s reference date (typically first dose date or randomization date) to the actual visit date. Corresponds to the CDISC VISITDY variable. Negative values indicate pre-dose visits.']
  deviation_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the protocol deviation severity at this visit: major (could affect subject safety or data integrity), minor (unlikely to affect safety or data integrity), or administrative (procedural/documentation only).']
  deviation_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the protocol deviation(s) noted at this visit, as recorded by the site in the EDC or CTMS. Null when deviation_flag is False.']
  deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more protocol deviations were recorded for this visit (True) or not (False). A True value triggers linkage to the protocol deviation register in the CTMS or eTMF (Veeva Vault).']
  early_termination_reason varchar [note: 'The reason the subject discontinued the study at or before this visit, if applicable. Null for non-termination visits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: adverse_event|withdrawal_of_consent|lost_to_follow_up|protocol_violation|sponsor_decision|death|other — promote to reference product]']
  edc_visit_oid varchar [note: 'The unique object identifier (OID) assigned to this visit record within the Medidata Rave EDC system. Used for traceability and reconciliation between the Silver Layer and the source EDC system.']
  epoch varchar [note: 'The trial epoch during which this visit occurred, as defined in the CDISC SDTM Trial Design model (e.g., Screening, Run-In, Treatment, Follow-Up, Post-Study). Supports epoch-level analysis.']
  informed_consent_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject\'s current informed consent form (ICF) was verified as valid and current at the time of this visit (True) or not (False). Critical for GCP compliance and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  planned_date date [note: 'The date on which the visit was scheduled to occur per the protocol Schedule of Assessments and the subject\'s randomization or enrollment anchor date.']
  query_open_count varchar [note: 'The number of data queries (discrepancies) currently open against this visit record in the EDC (Medidata Rave). A non-zero value indicates outstanding data cleaning actions required before database lock.']
  randomization_number varchar [note: 'The unique randomization number assigned to the subject at the time of randomization, as recorded in the Interactive Response Technology (IRT) system. Confidential as it may reveal treatment assignment in blinded trials.']
  sae_reported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more Serious Adverse Events (SAEs) were reported at this visit (True) or not (False). SAEs require expedited reporting to health authorities per ICH E2A and 21 CFR Part 312.32.']
  source_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Source Data Verification (SDV) has been completed for this visit by the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) or monitor (True) or not (False). Critical for GCP compliance and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this visit record was last modified in the source EDC system (Medidata Rave) or during Silver Layer processing. Supports audit trail and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  visit_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable label for the visit as defined in the protocol Schedule of Assessments (e.g., \'Screening\', \'Cycle 1 Day 1\', \'Week 12 Follow-Up\', \'End of Study\'). Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC visit folder name.']
  visit_number varchar [note: 'Sequential numeric identifier for the visit as defined in the protocol Schedule of Assessments (SoA). Unscheduled visits may carry a non-integer or supplemental numbering convention captured separately.']
  visit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the visit record indicating whether the visit was completed, missed, partially completed, cancelled, or rescheduled. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC and CTMS.']
  visit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the visit according to its role in the trial timeline. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: screening|baseline|treatment|follow_up|end_of_study|unscheduled|early_termination — promote to reference product]']
  window_lower_day varchar [note: 'The number of days before the planned visit date that defines the lower boundary of the acceptable visit window per the protocol. Used to assess visit window compliance.']
  window_upper_day varchar [note: 'The number of days after the planned visit date that defines the upper boundary of the acceptable visit window per the protocol. Used to assess visit window compliance.']
  within_window_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the actual visit date fell within the protocol-defined visit window (True) or outside it (False). Derived at ingestion from planned_date, actual_date, window_lower_day, and window_upper_day; stored for analytical efficiency.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each scheduled or unscheduled patient visit within a clinical trial, as captured in the EDC (Medidata Rave). Tracks visit number, visit name per protocol schedule of assessments, planned visit date, actual visit date, visit window compliance, visit type (screening, treatment, follow-up, end-of-study), visit status, and deviations noted. Forms the backbone of the clinical data collection timeline.'
}

Table clinical.crf_form {
  crf_form_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each CRF form definition record in the Medidata Rave electronic data capture system. Primary key for the crf_form data product.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific trial protocol version under which this CRF form definition was created and approved. Ensures form-to-protocol traceability.']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial for which this CRF form definition was designed. Links the instrument definition to its parent study in the CTMS.']
  visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled or unscheduled visit with which this CRF form is associated in the study schedule of events. Null for common forms appearing across all visits.']
  annotation_text varchar [note: 'Annotated CRF text describing the SDTM variable mappings, derivation rules, and coding instructions associated with each field on this form. Used by data management and programming teams for SDTM dataset creation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle approval state of the CRF form definition. \'draft\' indicates design in progress; \'under_review\' indicates pending sponsor/CRO review; \'approved\' indicates GCP-compliant sign-off; \'superseded\' indicates replaced by a newer version; \'retired\' indicates no longer in use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this CRF form definition received formal GCP-compliant approval. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Required for audit trail under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  cdash_domain varchar [note: 'CDISC CDASH domain code indicating the data collection standard this form adheres to (e.g., \'AE\', \'VS\', \'CM\'). Ensures harmonized data collection aligned with CDASH implementation guides.']
  completion_instructions varchar [note: 'Detailed instructions for site staff on how to complete this CRF form, including definitions, coding conventions, and data source guidance. Embedded in the EDC and printed CRF booklet.']
  completion_requirement varchar [note: 'Specifies whether completion of this CRF form is mandatory for all subjects, optional, or conditional on specific criteria (e.g., only if a prior form field triggers it). Drives data completeness monitoring and query generation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this CRF form definition record was first created in the system. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Required for audit trail under 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP.']
  data_entry_mode varchar [note: 'Specifies the data entry mode configured for this CRF form in Medidata Rave. \'single\' requires one entry; \'double\' requires independent dual entry for critical data; \'auto\' is populated programmatically from device or lab feeds.']
  edit_check_count varchar [note: 'Total number of programmed edit checks (validation rules) configured for this CRF form in Medidata Rave. Provides a measure of form complexity and data quality control coverage.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this CRF form version was retired or superseded. Null for currently active versions. Supports version lifecycle management and historical data traceability.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this CRF form version is active and available for data entry at investigational sites. Supports version management and ensures sites use the correct form version.']
  field_count varchar [note: 'Total number of data fields (questions/items) defined on this CRF form. Used for form complexity assessment, site burden estimation, and data management planning.']
  form_label varchar [note: 'Short display label for the CRF form used in the EDC user interface and printed CRF booklets. May differ from form_name for brevity in UI contexts.']
  form_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the CRF form as displayed in Medidata Rave and study documentation. Examples: \'Adverse Event Form\', \'Vital Signs\', \'Concomitant Medications\'.']
  form_oid varchar [note: 'Unique Object Identifier (OID) for the CRF form as defined in Medidata Rave and conforming to CDISC ODM standards. Used for cross-system referencing and SDTM mapping. Example: \'AE_FORM_V2\'.']
  form_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence order of this form within its associated visit or study schedule of events. Determines the display order in the EDC and printed CRF booklet.']
  form_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the CRF form by its data collection pattern in Medidata Rave. \'scheduled\' forms appear at predefined visits; \'unscheduled\' are event-driven; \'common\' appear across all visits; \'log\' forms capture repeating events (e.g., AE log); \'repeating\' forms allow multiple instances per visit.']
  form_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the CRF form definition, following semantic versioning (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.1\', \'3.0.1\'). Tracks design changes over the trial lifecycle and supports audit trail requirements.']
  is_blinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CRF form contains blinded data (e.g., treatment assignment, randomization code) that must be restricted from unblinded personnel during the trial. Supports GCP-compliant blinding controls.']
  is_repeating boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CRF form allows multiple instances per subject per visit (e.g., an Adverse Event log form where each AE is a separate instance). Drives EDC configuration in Medidata Rave.']
  is_sdv_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Source Data Verification (SDV) is required for data entered on this CRF form. Critical forms (e.g., primary endpoints, safety data) typically require 100% SDV; others may be risk-based.']
  is_signature_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an electronic signature (e-signature) is required on this CRF form by the investigator or site staff upon completion, as mandated by 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP requirements.']
  itt_population_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CRF form contributes data to the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) analysis population definition. ITT-relevant forms are critical for primary efficacy analysis and regulatory review.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this CRF form definition record was last modified. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail, change control, and GCP compliance.']
  primary_endpoint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CRF form captures data for the trial\'s primary efficacy or safety endpoint (e.g., ORR, PFS, OS). Primary endpoint forms receive heightened monitoring, SDV, and data review scrutiny.']
  rave_form_draft_version varchar [note: 'The internal Medidata Rave draft version number for this CRF form, distinct from the business version. Tracks incremental design changes within the Rave study build environment prior to promotion to production.']
  rave_study_name varchar [note: 'The study name as configured in Medidata Rave EDC system, used to identify the parent study context for this CRF form. Supports cross-referencing between the data lakehouse and the source EDC system.']
  regulatory_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data collected on this CRF form is included in regulatory submissions (NDA/BLA/MAA/IND). Drives SDTM dataset inclusion and eCTD packaging requirements.']
  safety_form_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CRF form is designated as a safety-critical form (e.g., Adverse Event, Serious Adverse Event, SUSAR reporting). Safety forms trigger expedited review workflows and pharmacovigilance data feeds.']
  sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'Two-letter CDISC SDTM domain code to which this CRF form maps for regulatory submission (e.g., \'AE\' for Adverse Events, \'VS\' for Vital Signs, \'CM\' for Concomitant Medications, \'LB\' for Laboratory). Drives SDTM dataset generation for NDA/BLA/MAA submissions.']
  sdv_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of data points on this CRF form subject to Source Data Verification (SDV) under the risk-based monitoring plan. Ranges from 0.00 (no SDV) to 100.00 (full SDV). Supports risk-based monitoring strategy.']
  source_system_form_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this CRF form record in the source EDC system (Medidata Rave). Enables lineage tracing from the Silver Layer back to the operational system of record.']
  validation_rule_set varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the edit check / validation rule set applied to this CRF form in Medidata Rave. References the programmed edit checks that enforce data quality, range checks, and cross-form consistency rules.']
  visit_oid varchar [note: 'CDISC ODM-compliant OID of the study visit to which this form is assigned, as defined in Medidata Rave. Supports cross-system visit-form linkage without requiring a resolved FK at this stage.']

  Note: 'Master record for each Case Report Form (CRF) design used in a trial, representing the electronic data capture instrument definition in Medidata Rave. Captures form name, form OID, associated visit, data domain (SDTM domain mapping), version, validation rules, completion requirements, and approval status. Distinct from CRF data (captured in subject_observation) — this is the instrument definition, not the collected data.'
}

Table clinical.subject_observation {
  subject_observation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each subject observation record in the EDC (Medidata Rave). Represents the atomic unit of clinical trial data collected per subject per visit per Case Report Form (CRF) field.']
  clinical_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.enrollment. Business justification: Clinical observations are collected for enrolled subjects. Linking subject_observation to enrollment provides treatment arm context and enables filtering observations by analysis population (ITT, mITT']
  crf_form_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CRF form definition from which this observation originates. Identifies the instrument (e.g., Vital Signs, Lab Results, Adverse Events) used to collect the data point.']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the observation was collected. Supports site-level data quality monitoring, SDV tracking, and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the enrolled trial subject (patient) from whom this observation was collected. Enables subject-level data aggregation for ITT, PP, and safety analyses.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.protocol. Business justification: Clinical observations are collected according to protocol-defined data collection standards and edit checks. Linking subject_observation to protocol enables verification that observations were collect']
  quality_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Protocol deviations identified in subject observations (protocol_deviation_flag=true) must be formally escalated to quality deviation records per GCP requirements. This FK enables direct traceability ']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial (study) under which this observation was collected. Links the observation to the parent trial protocol, phase, and regulatory submission context.']
  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: Subject observations record numeric clinical measurements requiring standardized UoM for CDISC/SDTM regulatory submissions and cross-trial data analysis. unit and standard_unit are denormalized plain-']
  visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled or unscheduled study visit during which this observation was collected. Supports visit-level data alignment and protocol deviation detection.']
  analysis_population_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates the analysis population(s) to which this observation contributes: Intent-to-Treat (ITT), Per Protocol (PP), Safety population, or excluded. Drives statistical analysis dataset (ADaM) construction and primary/secondary endpoint derivation.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this observation to its complete audit trail record in the EDC system (Medidata Rave). Enables traceability of all data changes, user actions, and timestamps per 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.']
  collection_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the observation was collected from the subject at the investigational site. Corresponds to SDTM --DTC variable. Critical for visit window compliance, protocol deviation assessment, and time-to-event endpoint calculations.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the observation was collected, including time zone offset. Required for time-sensitive measurements (e.g., PK sampling, ECG) and for accurate SDTM --STDTC derivation.']
  crf_field_name varchar [note: 'The name of the specific CRF field (data point) within the form from which this observation was collected (e.g., SYSBP, WEIGHT, AESTDTC). Corresponds to the EDC field label in Medidata Rave.']
  data_lock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this observation record has been locked in the EDC system as part of the database lock process, preventing further modifications. Locked records are used for final statistical analysis and regulatory submission datasets.']
  edc_record_reference varchar [note: 'Native record identifier assigned by the EDC system (Medidata Rave) for this observation. Used for source system traceability, audit trail reconciliation, and data migration validation.']
  entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the data was first entered into the EDC system (Medidata Rave) by the site user. Used for data entry timeliness monitoring, audit trail compliance, and GCP inspection readiness per ICH E6(R2).']
  is_baseline boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this observation represents the baseline measurement for the subject, used as the reference value for change-from-baseline calculations in efficacy endpoint derivation (e.g., tumor size at baseline for ORR). Corresponds to SDTM ABLFL flag in ADaM datasets.']
  is_unscheduled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this observation was collected during an unscheduled visit (not defined in the protocol schedule of assessments). Unscheduled observations may be excluded from primary efficacy analyses per the statistical analysis plan.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time of the most recent modification to this observation record in the EDC system. Supports audit trail tracking, data change monitoring, and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.']
  normal_range_high decimal(18,6) [note: 'The upper bound of the reference (normal) range for this observation type as defined by the central laboratory or clinical standard. Used for out-of-range flagging, safety signal detection, and SDTM STNRHI derivation.']
  normal_range_low decimal(18,6) [note: 'The lower bound of the reference (normal) range for this observation type as defined by the central laboratory or clinical standard. Used for out-of-range flagging, safety signal detection, and SDTM STNRLO derivation.']
  numeric_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The numeric representation of the observation value after unit standardization, corresponding to the SDTM STRESN variable. Used for quantitative analyses, endpoint derivation (ORR, PFS, OS), and statistical modeling in ADaM datasets.']
  observation_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the observation record within the EDC system lifecycle. Drives data cleaning workflows, lock readiness assessment, and database lock milestones. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: entered|verified|locked|queried|resolved|deleted — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]']
  out_of_range_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the numeric observation value falls outside the defined normal reference range (low or high). Triggers safety review workflows, potential AE/SAE reporting, and DSMB notification processes.']
  protocol_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this observation is associated with a protocol deviation (e.g., collected outside the visit window, wrong procedure performed). Flagged observations may be excluded from Per Protocol (PP) analysis populations.']
  query_status varchar [note: 'Status of any data query (discrepancy) raised against this observation in the EDC system. Open queries must be resolved prior to database lock. Supports data cleaning metrics and site performance monitoring.']
  query_text varchar [note: 'The text of the most recent data query raised against this observation, describing the discrepancy or clarification required from the site. Supports data management review and site communication workflows.']
  raw_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The verbatim data value as entered by the site user in the EDC system (Medidata Rave), prior to any derivation or standardization. Stored as STRING to accommodate all data types (numeric, text, coded). Represents the source data for SDV and audit trail purposes.']
  reason_for_change varchar [note: 'The documented reason provided by the site user when modifying a previously entered observation value in the EDC system. Mandatory per 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP requirements for any post-entry data correction. Stored as the most recent change reason.']
  sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM domain code to which this observation maps (e.g., VS for Vital Signs, LB for Laboratory, AE for Adverse Events, EX for Exposure). Critical for SDTM dataset generation and regulatory submission packages (NDA/BLA/MAA).']
  sdtm_variable varchar [note: 'The specific CDISC SDTM variable name to which this CRF field maps (e.g., VSSTRESN, LBORRES, AEDECOD). Enables automated SDTM dataset generation and submission-ready data transformation.']
  sdv_date date [note: 'The date on which Source Data Verification (SDV) was completed for this observation. Used for monitoring visit planning, SDV coverage reporting, and GCP audit trail documentation.']
  sdv_status varchar [note: 'Status of Source Data Verification (SDV) for this observation, indicating whether the EDC-entered value has been verified against the original source document (e.g., medical record, lab report) by the CRA or site monitor. Critical for GCP compliance and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  toxicity_grade varchar [note: 'The NCI CTCAE toxicity grade assigned to this observation when it represents an adverse finding (Grade 1=Mild, Grade 2=Moderate, Grade 3=Severe, Grade 4=Life-threatening, Grade 5=Death). Critical for safety endpoint reporting, dose modification decisions, and regulatory safety narratives.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) or monitor who performed Source Data Verification (SDV) for this observation. Supports GCP compliance documentation and monitoring visit reports.']

  Note: 'Core transactional table capturing all clinical data collected per subject per visit per CRF form in the EDC (Medidata Rave). Stores data point value, unit, SDTM variable mapping, collection date, data entry date, query status, source data verification (SDV) status, and audit trail reference. Represents the atomic unit of clinical trial data — the foundation for efficacy and safety endpoint derivation (ORR, PFS, OS, QoL) and CDISC SDTM/ADaM dataset generation.'
}

Table clinical.trial_adverse_event {
  trial_adverse_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each adverse event record reported by a subject during a clinical trial. Primary key for this transactional entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  ae_accounts_payable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_payable. Business justification: SAE processing triggers vendor invoices for emergency labs, imaging, hospitalization, and specialist consultations. Required for trial cost reconciliation, safety budget tracking, and sponsor financia']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Adverse event surveillance requires brand-level aggregation for pharmacovigilance, periodic safety update reports (PSURs), label updates, and commercial risk assessment. Essential for post-marketing s']
  clinical_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.enrollment. Business justification: Adverse events are reported for enrolled subjects and must be analyzed by treatment arm for safety assessment. Linking trial_adverse_event to enrollment enables treatment arm stratification of AE data']
  crf_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.crf_form. Business justification: Adverse events are captured via specific CRF forms (AE CRF) in the EDC system. Linking trial_adverse_event to crf_form enables traceability to the data collection instrument, supports source data veri']
  icsr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.icsr. Business justification: EDC-to-safety reconciliation (ICH E2B, FDA 21 CFR 312.32) requires linking each trial AE to its corresponding ICSR in the PV system. argus_case_reference is a denormalized text reference to the ICSR t']
  inventory_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: SUSAR and SAE causality assessments require identifying the exact inventory lot dispensed to the subject. ICH E6 GCP and pharmacovigilance regulations mandate lot-level traceability for adverse events']
  investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.investigational_product. Business justification: Adverse events require causality assessment to specific investigational products. While concomitant_medication field exists, there is no structured link to the IMP that may have caused the AE. This FK']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the adverse event was reported and the subject is enrolled.']
  labeling_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.labeling. Business justification: Unexpected SAEs and SUSARs trigger label update workflows — the AE must be assessed against the current approved labeling to determine if it is \'unexpected\'. Pharmacovigilance and regulatory affairs r']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.hcp_master. Business justification: Adverse events are often reported by treating physicians who are not the principal investigator. Regulatory authorities (FDA, EMA) require traceability of the reporting healthcare professional for cau']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: AE causality assessment requires product identification. Mandatory for pharmacovigilance (21 CFR 312.32), PSUR/PBRER preparation, product labeling updates, and regulatory safety reporting. Critical fo']
  patient_adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.adverse_event. Business justification: Pharmacovigilance reconciliation requires linking trial CRF-captured AEs (trial_adverse_event) to patient safety database records (patient.adverse_event) for SUSAR/SAE reporting, signal detection, and']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial subject (patient) who experienced the adverse event. Links to the subject/patient master record in the CTMS.']
  product_complaint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.complaint. Business justification: Product complaints reported during clinical trials (packaging defects, appearance issues, administration problems) are tracked in quality complaint system per 21 CFR 820.198. Enables trend analysis an']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.protocol. Business justification: Adverse event reporting criteria, grading scales (CTCAE), and expectedness assessments are defined in the protocol. Linking AE to protocol version enables correct application of protocol-defined AE as']
  pv_adverse_reaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.pv_adverse_reaction. Business justification: EDC-safety reconciliation (GVP Module VI) requires matching each trial AE verbatim term to its MedDRA-coded pv_adverse_reaction. meddra_preferred_term, meddra_pt_code, meddra_system_organ_class on tri']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Serious unexpected adverse events (SUSARs) trigger mandatory expedited regulatory reporting obligations. Pharmacovigilance teams must track which AEs resulted in expedited safety reports (15-day alert']
  reporting_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.investigator. Business justification: Regulatory AE reporting (MedWatch, EudraVigilance) requires identifying the reporting investigator by role, not just the HCP master. This FK enables investigator-level safety signal tracking, GCP comp']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial (study) in which the adverse event was reported. Links to the trial protocol master record.']
  visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.visit. Business justification: Adverse events are typically reported during specific patient visits. Linking AE to visit provides temporal and operational context for when the event was observed and documented. The onset_date captu']
  action_taken varchar [note: 'Action taken with the investigational medicinal product (IMP) in response to the adverse event (e.g., Dose not changed, Dose reduced, Drug interrupted, Drug withdrawn, Not applicable). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Dose not changed|Dose reduced|Drug interrupted|Drug withdrawn|Not applicable|Unknown — promote to reference product]']
  ae_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable identifier for the adverse event as assigned in the EDC system (Medidata Rave). Used for cross-referencing in CRFs, safety narratives, and regulatory submissions.']
  ae_reporting_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory reporting classification of the adverse event: AE (non-serious), SAE (Serious Adverse Event), SUSAR (Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction), or AESI (Adverse Event of Special Interest). Drives reporting timelines and workflows.']
  ae_severity varchar [note: 'Descriptive severity classification of the adverse event as recorded in the CRF. Corresponds to CTCAE grade but expressed as a categorical label for reporting and filtering.']
  ae_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the adverse event, indicating whether it is still ongoing, has resolved, resolved with residual effects, or resulted in death.']
  ae_term_verbatim varchar [note: 'The exact verbatim term as reported by the investigator or subject in the CRF prior to MedDRA coding. Preserved for audit and coding reconciliation purposes.']
  aesi_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event has been designated as an Adverse Event of Special Interest (AESI) per the trial protocol or regulatory guidance, requiring enhanced monitoring and reporting.']
  causality_assessment varchar [note: 'Investigator\'s assessment of the causal relationship between the adverse event and the investigational medicinal product (IMP). Used in safety reporting and benefit-risk evaluation.']
  concomitant_medication varchar [note: 'Name or description of any concomitant medication administered to treat or manage the adverse event. Free-text field as captured in the CRF.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adverse event record was first created in the EDC or source system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2).']
  ctcae_grade varchar [note: 'Severity grade of the adverse event per NCI CTCAE grading scale (1=Mild, 2=Moderate, 3=Severe, 4=Life-threatening, 5=Death). Mandatory for oncology trials and widely used across therapeutic areas.']
  dechallenge_result varchar [note: 'Result of withdrawing or reducing the investigational product dose in response to the adverse event. Positive dechallenge (AE improved on withdrawal) supports causal relationship.']
  ha_report_due_date date [note: 'The regulatory deadline by which the SAE or SUSAR must be reported to the relevant health authority (e.g., FDA 7-day or 15-day, EMA 7-day or 15-day). Calculated from sponsor_aware_date.']
  ha_reported_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the SAE or SUSAR was submitted to the health authority. Used to assess compliance with regulatory reporting timelines.']
  initial_report_date date [note: 'The date on which the adverse event was first reported by the investigator or site to the sponsor. Day 0 for regulatory reporting clock calculations (7-day/15-day timelines).']
  is_serious boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event meets the regulatory definition of a Serious Adverse Event (SAE) per ICH E2A criteria (death, life-threatening, hospitalization, disability, congenital anomaly, or other medically important condition).']
  is_susar boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adverse event has been classified as a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR), triggering expedited 7-day or 15-day regulatory reporting obligations to health authorities.']
  is_unexpected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event is unexpected — i.e., not listed in the current Investigator\'s Brochure (IB) or Reference Safety Information (RSI). A serious, unexpected, related AE constitutes a SUSAR requiring expedited reporting.']
  led_to_discontinuation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event led to the subject\'s permanent discontinuation from the clinical trial. Critical for ITT and safety analysis populations.']
  medical_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the medical monitor\'s review of the adverse event record. Tracks the safety review workflow from initial entry through medical sign-off.']
  medical_reviewer varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the sponsor medical monitor or physician who reviewed and assessed the adverse event record.']
  narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text medical narrative describing the adverse event, its clinical course, management, and outcome. Used in ICSR submissions and clinical study reports (CSR).']
  onset_date date [note: 'The date on which the adverse event first began or was first observed by the subject or investigator. Principal real-world event date for the AE lifecycle.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'The final or current clinical outcome of the adverse event as assessed by the investigator. Used in ICSR generation and aggregate safety reporting.']
  rave_subject_key varchar [note: 'The subject key as assigned in Medidata Rave EDC, used to cross-reference the adverse event record back to the source EDC system for data reconciliation and audit trail purposes.']
  rechallenge_result varchar [note: 'Result of re-administering the investigational product after the adverse event resolved, to assess causality. Positive rechallenge strengthens causal relationship.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the adverse event resolved or returned to baseline. Null if the event is ongoing at the time of data cut.']
  sae_criteria varchar [note: 'The specific ICH E2A seriousness criterion or criteria met by this SAE (e.g., Death, Life-threatening, Hospitalization required, Persistent disability, Congenital anomaly, Medically significant). Multiple criteria may apply. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Death|Life-threatening|Hospitalization required|Persistent disability|Congenital anomaly|Medically significant|Other — promote to reference product]']
  sponsor_aware_date date [note: 'The date on which the sponsor first became aware of the serious adverse event. Starts the regulatory reporting clock for SAE/SUSAR expedited reporting to health authorities.']
  treatment_given boolean [note: 'Indicates whether concomitant medication or other treatment was administered to manage or treat the adverse event.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adverse event record was most recently modified in the source system or Silver Layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each Adverse Event (AE) reported by a subject during a clinical trial, as captured in the EDC and CTMS. Captures AE term (MedDRA coded), onset date, resolution date, severity grade (CTCAE), seriousness flag (SAE), causality assessment, action taken (dose modification, discontinuation), outcome, and SAE/SUSAR reporting status. Distinct from the pharmacovigilance domain\'s ICSR — this is the trial-level AE record feeding into safety reporting workflows.'
}

Table clinical.trial_consent {
  trial_consent_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each informed consent event record in the clinical trial. Primary key for the trial_consent data product.']
  clinical_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.enrollment. Business justification: Informed consent is obtained during the enrollment process. Linking trial_consent to enrollment provides the complete enrollment context and enables verification that consent was obtained before enrol']
  crf_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.crf_form. Business justification: Informed consent may be captured via CRF form (consent CRF) in the EDC system, especially for electronic consent (eConsent) workflows. Linking trial_consent to crf_form enables traceability to the dat']
  informed_consent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.informed_consent. Business justification: GCP compliance and GDPR/HIPAA data privacy audits require reconciliation between trial CRF consent records (trial_consent in eTMF/EDC) and patient-level consent management records (patient.informed_co']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the consent event occurred. Links to the site master record in the CTMS.']
  investigator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Principal Investigator (PI) or sub-investigator who conducted the informed consent discussion and signed the consent form. Links to the investigator master record in the CTMS.']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (patient) who provided informed consent. Links to the subject master record in the Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS).']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.protocol. Business justification: Informed consent forms are version-controlled per protocol version. The trial_consent table has protocol_version as a string, but linking to protocol table provides the authoritative protocol version ']
  rems_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.rems. Business justification: REMS programs mandate specific patient consent elements and consent form language (e.g., iPLEDGE pregnancy prevention counseling, Clozapine agranulocytosis risk disclosure). Regulatory and clinical te']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial for which this consent was obtained. Links to the trial master record in the CTMS.']
  visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.visit. Business justification: Informed consent is typically obtained at the screening visit. Linking trial_consent to visit provides the visit context and enables verification that consent was obtained before any protocol-specific']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes or comments related to the consent event, such as special circumstances, deviations from standard consent procedures, or clarifications noted by the investigator or study coordinator.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject (or LAR) signed and dated the informed consent form, confirming voluntary agreement to participate in the trial. This is the principal business event date for the consent transaction.']
  consent_form_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the Informed Consent Form (ICF) used for this consent event, as approved by the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC). Critical for regulatory traceability when protocol amendments trigger re-consent.']
  consent_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) of the language in which the ICF was presented and signed by the subject, ensuring the subject received information in their native language per GCP requirements.']
  consent_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this consent event, used for regulatory traceability and audit purposes. Typically generated by the CTMS or EDC system (e.g., Medidata Rave or Oracle Clinical).']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the consent record. \'Active\' indicates valid consent in force; \'withdrawn\' indicates subject has revoked consent; \'superseded\' indicates replaced by a newer consent version; \'pending\' indicates awaiting subject signature; \'expired\' indicates consent period has lapsed.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the consent was electronically captured or recorded in the EDC/CTMS system (e.g., Medidata Rave), supporting 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the consent event: initial consent at enrollment, re-consent triggered by protocol amendment, assent from a minor subject, consent by a Legally Authorized Representative (LAR), or withdrawal of consent.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this consent record was first created in the system (EDC or CTMS). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP data integrity standards.']
  econsent_platform varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the eConsent platform used to capture electronic consent (e.g., Medidata Rave eConsent, Veeva Vault). Populated only when electronic_consent is True.']
  electronic_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consent was obtained via an electronic consent (eConsent) platform rather than a traditional paper-based process. Relevant for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance and audit trail requirements.']
  etmf_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in the Electronic Trial Master File (eTMF) system (e.g., Veeva Vault) for the scanned or electronically captured consent form. Enables direct linkage between the consent record and the regulatory-compliant document archive.']
  investigator_signed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Principal Investigator (PI) or delegated sub-investigator has signed the informed consent form, confirming the consent discussion was conducted per GCP requirements.']
  irb_iec_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the IRB or IEC approved the ICF version referenced in this consent event. Consent must not be obtained prior to this date to maintain GCP compliance.']
  irb_iec_approval_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the IRB or IEC approval that authorized the specific ICF version used in this consent event. Ensures GCP-compliant traceability between the consent form version and its ethics committee approval.']
  itt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject is included in the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) analysis population. Subjects who consented and received at least one dose of investigational product are typically included in the ITT population.']
  lar_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the Legally Authorized Representative (LAR) who provided consent on behalf of the subject. Populated only when lar_required is True.']
  lar_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the Legally Authorized Representative (LAR) to the trial subject (e.g., parent, legal guardian, spouse). Required when lar_required is True to establish legal authority to consent.']
  lar_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Legally Authorized Representative (LAR) was required to provide consent on behalf of the subject, applicable for minors, cognitively impaired subjects, or other legally incapacitated individuals.']
  lar_signed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Legally Authorized Representative (LAR) has signed and dated the informed consent form. Applicable only when lar_required is True.']
  optional_consent_biobank boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject provided optional consent for long-term storage and future use of biological samples in a biobank. This is a separate optional consent element from the main trial consent.']
  optional_consent_genetic boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject provided optional consent for genetic or genomic research using their biological samples. Separate from the main trial consent and biobank consent.']
  reconsent_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject provided re-consent following a protocol amendment or updated ICF version. Null if no re-consent has been required for this subject.']
  reconsent_reason varchar [note: 'Reason that triggered the re-consent requirement, such as a protocol amendment, new safety information identified during the trial, or a regulatory authority request. Null if no re-consent was required.']
  source_document_location varchar [note: 'Location reference for the original signed consent form source document, such as the eTMF folder path in Veeva Vault or physical file location at the investigational site. Supports GCP source data verification (SDV) and audit trail requirements.']
  subject_signed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has personally signed and dated the informed consent form. False if consent was provided solely by a Legally Authorized Representative (LAR) without subject co-signature.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this consent record in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data integrity monitoring per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject formally withdrew consent to participate in the trial. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. Triggers subject discontinuation workflow in the CTMS.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason provided by the subject or investigator for withdrawal of consent. Captured for regulatory reporting and subject safety follow-up purposes.']
  witness_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an impartial witness was required to be present during the consent process, applicable when the subject or LAR is unable to read or when a short-form consent is used.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each informed consent event for a trial subject, including consent form version, consent date, re-consent date (if protocol amended), subject signature confirmation, legally authorized representative details (if applicable), consent withdrawal date, and IRB/IEC approval reference for the consent version used. Ensures GCP-compliant documentation of subject autonomy and regulatory traceability.'
}

  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: IMP strength uses standardized units of measure (mg, mL, IU). Required for dose calculations, dispensing instructions, CDISC SDTM EX domain mapping, and regulatory submissions. Essential for dosing ac']
  accountability_log_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number or system identifier for the IMP accountability log maintained at the investigational site or centrally. Links to the eTMF document in Veeva Vault per ICH E6(R2) Section 8.3.15.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned batch or lot number for the specific IMP batch supplied to the trial. Essential for GMP traceability, recall management, and IMP accountability per ICH E6(R2) Section 8.3.']
  blinding_type varchar [note: 'Blinding configuration for this IMP in the trial (open-label, single-blind, double-blind, triple-blind). Determines whether subjects, investigators, and/or sponsor personnel are masked to treatment assignment.']
  cold_chain_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this IMP requires cold-chain logistics (temperature-controlled storage and transport) throughout the supply chain from manufacturer to investigational site. Drives GDP-compliant distribution planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this investigational product record was first created in the system of record (Oracle CTMS or SAP). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2).']
  dispensation_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used for IMP dispensation, return, and destruction quantities (e.g., tablet, capsule, vial, mL). Ensures consistent accountability across all quantity fields. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|vial|ampoule|mL|mg|patch|sachet|unit — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the IMP batch beyond which the product must not be dispensed to trial subjects. Mandatory field for IMP accountability and GCP compliance per ICH E6(R2).']
  gmp_certificate_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the GMP certificate or Qualified Person (QP) release certificate confirming the IMP batch was manufactured in compliance with current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP). Required for EU CTA and IND.']
  imp_code varchar [note: 'Sponsor-assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this IMP or comparator within the trial supply system. Used for dispensation tracking and accountability logs in Oracle CTMS and SAP MM.']
  impd_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier for the Investigational Medicinal Product Dossier (IMPD) submitted to EU competent authorities. Supports EMA/MHRA CTA submissions.']
  ind_number varchar [note: 'FDA-assigned Investigational New Drug (IND) application number under which this IMP is authorized for clinical investigation in the United States. Links to the regulatory.application record.']
  kit_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the IMP dispensation kit as labeled and shipped to the investigational site. Used for kit-level accountability tracking and IWRS-driven dispensation in Oracle CTMS.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date on which the IMP batch was manufactured. Used to calculate shelf life, retest dates, and to verify GMP compliance of the batch at time of dispensation.']
  matching_placebo_code varchar [note: 'IMP code of the matching placebo formulated to be indistinguishable from the active investigational product in appearance, taste, and smell. Required for double-blind trial integrity and IMP accountability.']
  mechanism_of_action varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the mechanism of action (MOA) of the IMP\'s active pharmaceutical ingredient (API), describing how it produces its pharmacological effect at the molecular or cellular level.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the product role in the trial: investigational (test article), comparator (active control), placebo (matching or non-matching), rescue medication, or auxiliary medicinal product per ICH E6(R2) definitions.']
  quantity_destroyed decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of this IMP destroyed at site or centrally, expressed in the dispensation unit. Required for complete IMP accountability reconciliation and destruction certificate documentation.']
  quantity_dispensed decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of this IMP dispensed to trial subjects from this batch, expressed in the dispensation unit (e.g., tablets, vials, mL). Core field for IMP accountability log per ICH E6(R2) Section 8.']
  quantity_returned decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of this IMP returned by trial subjects or sites, expressed in the dispensation unit. Used to calculate compliance rates and reconcile IMP accountability per GCP requirements.']
  retest_date date [note: 'Date by which the IMP batch must be retested to confirm continued suitability for use, particularly for drug substances with defined retest periods rather than expiry dates. Supports stability management.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage conditions for the IMP as specified in the CoA and IMPD/IND (e.g., 2-8°C refrigerated, 15-25°C controlled room temperature, -20°C frozen, protect from light). Critical for cold-chain management and GDP compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: refrigerated_2_8c|controlled_room_temp|frozen_minus20c|frozen_minus80c|protect_from_light|ambient — promote to reference product]']
  supply_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this IMP record in the trial supply lifecycle: available (at site, ready for dispensation), dispensed (issued to subject), returned (received back from subject), destroyed, quarantine (hold pending investigation), or expired.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this investigational product record. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP electronic records requirements.']

  Note: 'Master record for each investigational medicinal product (IMP) and comparator used in clinical trials, including drug substance name (INN/USAN), formulation, dosage form, strength, route of administration, manufacturer, batch/lot number, expiry date, storage conditions (cold-chain requirements), blinding configuration (matching placebo details), IND/IMPD reference, dispensation tracking (quantity dispensed, return, destruction), and accountability log reference. Distinct from the commercial product catalog (owned by the product domain) — this entity is scoped to GCP-regulated IMP accountability, trial supply management, and regulatory compliance per ICH E6 R2 section 8.'
}

Table clinical.trial_endpoint {
