  trial_endpoint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each trial endpoint assessment record in the clinical lakehouse silver layer.']
  clinical_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.enrollment. Business justification: Clinical endpoints are assessed for enrolled subjects and must be analyzed by treatment arm. Linking trial_endpoint to enrollment provides treatment arm context and enables filtering by analysis popul']
  crf_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.crf_form. Business justification: Clinical endpoints are captured via specific CRF forms (endpoint assessment CRF, tumor assessment CRF, response evaluation CRF). Linking trial_endpoint to crf_form enables traceability to the data col']
  hta_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.hta_submission. Business justification: HTA bodies evaluate specific clinical endpoints (primary, PRO, surrogate) for reimbursement decisions. Direct traceability from endpoint results to HTA submission is required for HTA response document']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site at which the endpoint assessment was performed.']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Endpoint results must be linked to products for efficacy claims in regulatory applications (BLA/NDA/MAA), product labeling (indications/clinical studies section), and health technology assessments. Co']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the enrolled trial subject (patient) for whom this endpoint is being assessed. Constitutes protected health information under HIPAA and GDPR.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific protocol version governing this endpoint assessment, ensuring traceability to the approved study design.']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent clinical trial under which this endpoint assessment was conducted. Links to the trial master record in the CTMS.']
  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: Trial endpoint results require standardized UoM for CDISC ADaM/SDTM submissions, cross-trial efficacy comparisons, and HTA dossier preparation. result_unit is a denormalized plain-text attribute; a FK']
  visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.visit. Business justification: Clinical endpoints are assessed at specific protocol-defined visits. Linking trial_endpoint to visit provides the complete visit context including planned vs actual date, visit window compliance, and ']
  adjudication_date date [note: 'Date on which the independent adjudication committee completed its review and issued a final determination for this endpoint assessment.']
  adjudication_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this endpoint assessment requires review by an independent adjudication committee (e.g., BICR, DSMB, IRC) due to discordance between investigator and central read, or per protocol mandate.']
  adjudication_result varchar [note: 'Final response category or concordance determination issued by the independent adjudication committee after review. Overrides investigator assessment when discordant, per protocol rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CR|PR|SD|PD|NE|concordant|discordant — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  adjudication_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the independent adjudication committee review for this endpoint assessment. Tracks workflow from pending through completed adjudication, supporting BICR and IRC processes.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the endpoint assessment was performed. This is the principal real-world event date for the endpoint record, distinct from data entry or audit timestamps.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Standardized methodology used to evaluate the endpoint (e.g., RECIST 1.1 for solid tumor response, iRECIST for immunotherapy, BICR for blinded independent central review, RANO for CNS tumors). Critical for regulatory submission credibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RECIST_1.1|iRECIST|BICR|investigator|RANO|mRECIST|PCWG3|Cheson|IWG — promote to reference product]']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the endpoint assessment record: planned (scheduled but not yet performed), completed (data captured and verified), missed (visit occurred but assessment not done), not_done (subject not available), or invalid (data quality issue).']
  baseline_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Baseline measurement value for the endpoint at study entry (e.g., sum of longest diameters at baseline for RECIST). Used to calculate percent change from baseline and determine response categories.']
  best_overall_response varchar [note: 'Best overall response achieved by the subject across all assessment timepoints up to and including this assessment, per RECIST or applicable criteria. Used in ORR and DOR calculations for regulatory submissions.']
  censoring_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for censoring a subject in time-to-event analyses when the endpoint event has not occurred (e.g., lost to follow-up, withdrawal of consent, initiation of new anticancer therapy, end of study). Required for regulatory-grade survival analyses. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lost_to_followup|withdrawal|new_anticancer_therapy|end_of_study|administrative|death_other_cause — promote to reference product]']
  confirmed_response boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the response (CR or PR) has been confirmed by a subsequent assessment at least 4 weeks later, as required by RECIST 1.1 for ORR determination in regulatory submissions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this endpoint assessment record was first created in the clinical data system. Required for GCP audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2).']
  data_lock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this endpoint record has been locked as part of the clinical database lock process prior to unblinding and statistical analysis. Locked records are immutable for regulatory submission purposes.']
  edc_record_reference varchar [note: 'Source system record identifier from Medidata Rave EDC, enabling traceability from the lakehouse silver layer back to the originating EDC record for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  endpoint_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the endpoint as primary, secondary, exploratory, or safety per the study protocol. Primary endpoints drive the primary efficacy analysis for NDA/BLA/MAA submissions.']
  endpoint_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the endpoint definition within the study (e.g., EP001, PFS_PRIMARY). Sourced from the study protocol and EDC configuration in Medidata Rave.']
  endpoint_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the clinical endpoint being assessed (e.g., Objective Response Rate, Progression-Free Survival, Overall Survival, Duration of Response, Quality of Life). Aligns with protocol-defined endpoint nomenclature.']
  endpoint_type varchar [note: 'Functional type of the endpoint indicating the nature of the measurement: efficacy (ORR, PFS, OS), safety (AE rate), pharmacokinetic (PK), pharmacodynamic (PD), biomarker, or patient-reported outcome (PRO/QoL). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: efficacy|safety|pharmacokinetic|pharmacodynamic|biomarker|patient_reported|composite — promote to reference product]']
  evaluable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject is considered evaluable for this specific endpoint at this timepoint, based on protocol-defined evaluability criteria (e.g., minimum number of prior assessments, no major protocol deviations).']
  event_date date [note: 'Date on which the endpoint event (progression, death, response) occurred. For censored subjects, this is the last known event-free date. Used in time-to-event analyses for PFS and OS.']
  event_occurred boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the endpoint event (e.g., disease progression, death) has occurred for time-to-event endpoints such as PFS and OS. False indicates censoring. Critical for Kaplan-Meier and Cox regression analyses.']
  itt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subject\'s endpoint assessment is included in the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) analysis population, the primary analysis population for most regulatory submissions.']
  nadir_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lowest (nadir) measurement value recorded for the subject across all prior assessments. Used in RECIST PD determination (≥20% increase from nadir) and DOR calculations.']
  percent_change_from_baseline decimal(10,4) [note: 'Percentage change in the endpoint measurement from the baseline value to the current assessment value. Directly used in waterfall plots and response determination per RECIST criteria.']
  pp_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subject\'s endpoint assessment is included in the Per Protocol (PP) analysis population, used as a sensitivity analysis alongside ITT in regulatory submissions.']
  query_open boolean [note: 'Indicates whether there is an open data query (discrepancy) raised against this endpoint record in the EDC system (Medidata Rave). Open queries must be resolved before database lock.']
  response_category varchar [note: 'Categorical tumor response classification per the applicable assessment criteria: Complete Response (CR), Partial Response (PR), Stable Disease (SD), Progressive Disease (PD), or Not Evaluable (NE). Directly feeds ORR calculation for NDA/BLA/MAA submissions.']
  result_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric result of the endpoint assessment (e.g., tumor size in mm, PFS duration in months, QoL score). This is the principal quantitative outcome for the endpoint record. Null for categorical-only endpoints.']
  time_to_event_days varchar [note: 'Duration in days from the study reference date (e.g., randomization date) to the endpoint event or censoring date. Used directly in PFS, OS, and DOR analyses for NDA/BLA/MAA submissions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this endpoint assessment record was most recently modified. Required for GCP audit trail compliance and data integrity monitoring under 21 CFR Part 11.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the formal assessment of primary and secondary clinical endpoints for each subject at defined timepoints. Captures endpoint name (ORR, PFS, OS, QoL, DOR), assessment date, assessment method (RECIST, BICR, investigator assessment), result value, unit, response category, and adjudication committee review status. Feeds directly into efficacy analyses for NDA/BLA/MAA submissions.'
}

Table clinical.lab_result {
  lab_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each laboratory test result record in the clinical trial data product. Primary key for the lab_result entity in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  biospecimen_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.biospecimen. Business justification: Laboratory results are derived from biological specimens. Both tables have specimen_type, but the authoritative source is biospecimen. Linking lab_result to biospecimen enables traceability from test ']
  clinical_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.enrollment. Business justification: Laboratory results are collected for enrolled subjects and must be analyzed by treatment arm. Linking lab_result to enrollment enables treatment arm stratification of lab data and filtering by analysi']
  crf_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.crf_form. Business justification: Laboratory results may be captured via CRF forms (lab CRF) in addition to direct EDC integration from central labs. Linking lab_result to crf_form enables traceability to the data collection instrumen']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site (clinical site) where the specimen was collected. Used for site-level lab data quality monitoring and GCP compliance oversight.']
  lab_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Central labs are registered business partners in pharma masterdata for vendor qualification, payment, and GLP/GCP audit trails. lab_name and lab_accreditation_number are denormalized plain-text attrib']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Safety labs and efficacy biomarkers must be attributable to specific products for regulatory submissions (ICH E3), product labeling (warnings/precautions), and post-marketing safety surveillance. Crit']
  method_validation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.method_validation. Business justification: GCP/GLP requires analytical methods used in clinical trials to be validated. Linking lab_result to method_validation supports NDA/BLA regulatory submission (analytical methods section) and audit trail']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial subject (patient) from whom the specimen was collected. Links to the subject/patient master in the CTMS or EDC system (Medidata Rave). Classified as restricted PII as it can be used to trace back to an individual trial participant.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.protocol. Business justification: Laboratory test panels, testing schedules, and normal ranges are defined in the protocol. Linking lab_result to protocol enables verification that the correct tests were performed at the correct timep']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial or study under which this laboratory result was collected. Corresponds to the protocol/study record in Oracle Clinical / Siebel CTMS or Medidata Rave.']
  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: Lab results use standardized units. Required for CDISC SDTM LB domain mapping (standard units conversion), safety analysis, cross-study comparisons, and regulatory submissions. Essential for data stan']
  visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled or unscheduled study visit during which the specimen was collected. Aligns with the visit schedule defined in the trial protocol and captured in Medidata Rave CRF.']
  clinically_significant_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the laboratory result has been assessed by the investigator as clinically significant. Corresponds to CDISC CDASH/SDTM LBCLSIG. A clinically significant abnormal result may trigger an adverse event report or protocol-defined action.']
  collection_date date [note: 'Date on which the biological specimen was collected from the trial subject. Used for study day calculation, protocol deviation assessment, and time-point alignment in efficacy and safety analyses. Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBDTC (date portion).']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Full date and time of specimen collection from the trial subject, including timezone offset. Used for precise PK/PD time-point calculations and timed lab assessments (e.g., fasting glucose, post-dose PK samples). Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBDTC (full datetime).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes associated with the laboratory result, including laboratory annotations, investigator comments on abnormal results, or data management notes. Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBCOMM. Used for contextual interpretation of results.']
  data_entry_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Case Report Form (CRF) data entry for this laboratory result in Medidata Rave EDC. Tracks the data management workflow from initial entry through verification and database lock. Used for data cleaning and trial master file completeness tracking.']
  entered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the laboratory result was entered or imported into the Medidata Rave EDC system. Used for audit trail compliance per 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2) to demonstrate timely data entry and GCP compliance.']
  fasting_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the subject was fasting at the time of specimen collection. Relevant for metabolic panels (glucose, lipids) where fasting status affects reference ranges and result interpretation. Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBFAST.']
  lab_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory result was generated by a central laboratory (contracted central lab with standardized methods across all sites) or a local laboratory (site-specific lab). Central lab results are preferred for primary efficacy and safety endpoints due to standardization.']
  loinc_code varchar [note: 'Standardized LOINC code identifying the laboratory test or observation. Enables cross-study, cross-site, and cross-system harmonization of lab test definitions. Required for regulatory submissions and interoperability with central lab data transfers.']
  normal_flag varchar [note: 'Flag indicating whether the result is within, below, or above the laboratory\'s normal reference range. Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBNRIND. Values: normal, low, high, critically_low, critically_high. Used in safety data listings and shift tables for regulatory submissions.']
  out_of_range_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the result value falls outside the laboratory\'s defined reference range (either below lower limit or above upper limit). Distinct from clinical significance — a result can be out of range but not clinically significant. Used for automated data quality checks and safety signal detection.']
  panic_value_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the result meets the laboratory\'s criteria for a panic (critical) value requiring immediate notification to the investigator or site. Supports GCP-compliant safety monitoring and timely SAE/AE reporting obligations.']
  protocol_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this laboratory result is associated with a protocol deviation (e.g., specimen collected outside the protocol-defined time window, incorrect specimen type collected). Used for ITT/PP population analysis and regulatory submission deviation summaries.']
  query_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether an open data query (discrepancy) exists for this laboratory result record in Medidata Rave EDC. Used by data managers to track unresolved data issues requiring site clarification before database lock.']
  rave_record_reference varchar [note: 'Source system record identifier from Medidata Rave EDC for this laboratory result entry. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail back to the originating EDC system.']
  reference_range_high decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper bound of the laboratory\'s normal reference range for this test, applicable to the subject\'s demographic group (age, sex). Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBSTNRHI. Used for normal/abnormal flagging and shift table generation.']
  reference_range_low decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower bound of the laboratory\'s normal reference range for this test, applicable to the subject\'s demographic group (age, sex). Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBSTNRLO. Used for normal/abnormal flagging and shift table generation.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the laboratory result was officially reported and transmitted by the laboratory to the sponsor or EDC system. Used for turnaround time monitoring, GCP compliance (timely reporting of critical values), and data transfer reconciliation.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the laboratory result record reflecting its lifecycle in the EDC and central lab workflow. Values: preliminary (initial result), final (verified and released), corrected (amended after initial release), cancelled (result voided), pending (awaiting analysis). Corresponds to HL7 observation status.']
  result_unit_standard varchar [note: 'Standardized unit of measure for the laboratory result after conversion to a common unit for cross-site and cross-study comparability. Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBSTRESU. Enables pooled analyses and regulatory submission datasets.']
  result_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The reported result value for the laboratory test as a string to accommodate both numeric and character results (e.g., \'5.4\', \'>100\', \'POSITIVE\', \'NEGATIVE\', \'TRACE\'). Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBORRES. Stored as STRING to preserve original reported value including qualifiers.']
  result_value_numeric decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric representation of the laboratory result value after standardization and unit conversion. Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBSTRESN. Used for quantitative statistical analyses, shift tables, and outlier detection. Null for non-numeric results.']
  study_day varchar [note: 'Relative study day of specimen collection calculated from the subject\'s first dose date (Day 1). Negative values indicate pre-dose assessments. Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBDY. Used for time-course analyses, PK/PD modeling, and protocol compliance assessment.']
  test_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the laboratory test panel or category (e.g., hematology, chemistry, urinalysis, coagulation). Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBCAT variable. Used for grouping and filtering in safety data review and regulatory submissions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hematology|chemistry|urinalysis|coagulation|immunology|microbiology|pharmacokinetics|other — promote to reference product]']
  test_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the laboratory test performed (e.g., Hemoglobin, Serum Creatinine, ALT, White Blood Cell Count). Sourced from the central lab data transfer file and mapped to the CRF lab panel definition in Medidata Rave.']
  test_subcategory varchar [note: 'More granular sub-classification of the laboratory test within its category (e.g., liver function tests within chemistry, CBC within hematology). Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBSCAT variable.']
  toxicity_grade varchar [note: 'Toxicity grade assigned to the laboratory abnormality per the NCI Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) grading scale (Grade 1=Mild, Grade 2=Moderate, Grade 3=Severe, Grade 4=Life-threatening, Grade 5=Death). Used for safety signal detection, dose modification decisions, and regulatory safety reporting.']
  verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the laboratory result was verified (source data verified, SDV) by the clinical monitor or data manager in Medidata Rave. Required for GCP audit trail and trial master file completeness.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each central or local laboratory test result collected during a clinical trial. Captures lab test name (LOINC coded), specimen type, collection date, result value, unit, reference range, normal/abnormal flag, clinically significant flag, out-of-range flag, and laboratory accreditation reference. Sourced from central lab data transfers into Medidata Rave. Distinct from manufacturing LIMS results — this is trial-specific clinical lab data.'
}

Table clinical.trial_milestone {
  trial_milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each trial milestone record in the clinical trial management system. Primary key for the trial_milestone data product.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Trial milestones include approval receipt events (e.g., NDA approval, MAA approval) that are critical path milestones in clinical development timelines. CTMS and regulatory systems must link the appro']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_capa. Business justification: In pharma operations, quality holds on clinical trials require CAPA completion as a prerequisite before trial resumption. A trial_milestone can represent CAPA closure as a critical path dependency. Th']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Milestones are often country-specific (country-level regulatory submissions, country site activations, country database locks). Required for country-level trial planning, regulatory timeline tracking,']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.investigational_site. Business justification: Many trial milestones are site-specific, including FPFV (First Patient First Visit) per site, LPLV (Last Patient Last Visit) per site, and site activation/closure dates. Linking trial_milestone to inv']
  lifecycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.lifecycle. Business justification: Trial milestones (FPFV, DBL, CSR completion, NDA submission) are the primary triggers for product lifecycle stage-gate decisions. Portfolio governance and R&D leadership use this link to automatically']
  patent_term_extension_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_term_extension. Business justification: Patent Term Extension (PTE/SPC) applications require precise regulatory review period dates derived from clinical trial milestones (IND filing, NDA approval). IP counsel directly references trial mile']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.plan. Business justification: Trial milestone slippage (e.g., FPFV delay, protocol amendment) directly triggers supply plan revisions in S&OP. Linking trial_milestone to supply plan enables supply planners to re-baseline productio']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Clinical milestones (FPFV, enrollment targets, database lock) trigger and constrain clinical supply production orders. Essential for supply forecasting, campaign planning, manufacturing slot allocatio']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.protocol. Business justification: Trial milestones are defined in the protocol and tracked per protocol version. Protocol amendments may change milestone definitions or timelines. Linking trial_milestone to protocol enables tracking w']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Clinical milestones (database lock, CSR completion, final study report) are prerequisites for regulatory submissions. Regulatory operations teams track submission readiness and PDUFA timelines based o']
  responsible_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: Trial milestones are owned by specific org units (regulatory affairs, clinical ops, data management) accountable for delivery. responsible_function is a denormalized plain-text attribute. Linking to o']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent clinical trial to which this milestone belongs. Links the milestone record to the trial protocol and study-level metadata.']
  actual_date date [note: 'The confirmed date on which the milestone was achieved. Populated only upon milestone completion. Used to calculate variance against planned_date and to update regulatory commitment trackers.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the milestone completion or date change was formally approved. Supports electronic audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP inspection readiness.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who formally approved the milestone completion or date change. Supports GCP audit trail requirements and electronic signature compliance under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  comments varchar [note: 'General free-text comments or notes associated with this milestone record, capturing additional context not covered by structured fields. Used by clinical operations teams for internal communication and audit trail documentation.']
  completion_evidence_type varchar [note: 'Type of documentation used to confirm milestone completion (e.g., signed memo, system-generated log from Medidata Rave, regulatory authority letter, DSMB report). Required for GCP inspection readiness and audit trail completeness.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this milestone record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under GCP and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.']
  database_lock_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the type of database lock associated with this milestone, where applicable: interim (for interim analysis), final (for primary analysis), or partial (for a subset of sites or data). Populated only for database lock milestones. Supports data management planning.']
  etmf_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this milestone to the corresponding evidence document stored in Veeva Vault eTMF (e.g., milestone completion evidence, signed confirmation letter). Supports GCP audit trail and inspection readiness.']
  forecast_date date [note: 'The current best estimate of when the milestone will be achieved, updated dynamically as the trial progresses. Distinct from planned_date (baseline) and actual_date (confirmed completion). Supports rolling forecast and portfolio timeline management.']
  health_authority varchar [note: 'The regulatory health authority to which this milestone is committed or reported (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, NMPA). Relevant when is_regulatory_commitment is True. Supports multi-regional regulatory commitment tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|NMPA|WHO|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ind_number varchar [note: 'The FDA-assigned Investigational New Drug (IND) application number associated with the clinical trial. Required for regulatory commitment tracking and FDA correspondence. Nullable for non-US trials.']
  interim_analysis_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number identifying which interim analysis this milestone represents (e.g., 1 for IA1, 2 for IA2). Populated only when is_interim_analysis is True. Supports tracking of multiple pre-specified interim analyses within a single trial.']
  is_critical_path boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this milestone lies on the critical path of the clinical trial project plan. Critical path milestones directly impact the overall trial completion date and receive elevated monitoring priority in portfolio management.']
  is_interim_analysis boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this milestone represents an interim analysis event (e.g., a pre-specified interim efficacy or futility analysis). When True, additional blinding and DSMB (Data Safety Monitoring Board) governance controls apply.']
  is_regulatory_commitment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this milestone represents a formal commitment made to a health authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA) as part of an approval condition, post-approval commitment, or REMS requirement. When True, triggers mandatory regulatory commitment tracking workflows.']
  milestone_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the milestone is a planned internal target, a contractual obligation (e.g., with a CRO/CMO), a regulatory commitment (e.g., FDA/EMA post-approval commitment), or an internal management checkpoint. Drives escalation and governance workflows.']
  milestone_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the milestone type within the organization\'s CTMS (e.g., FPFV, LPLV, DBL, CSR, IND_SUB). Used for cross-trial portfolio reporting and regulatory commitment tracking.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the clinical trial milestone (e.g., \'First Patient First Visit\', \'Last Patient Last Visit\', \'Database Lock\', \'Clinical Study Report Completion\', \'Regulatory Submission\'). Human-readable label used in portfolio dashboards and regulatory commitment trackers.']
  milestone_sequence varchar [note: 'Integer defining the chronological order of this milestone within the trial\'s project plan. Used to enforce milestone sequencing logic, detect out-of-order completions, and render Gantt-style timeline visualizations.']
  milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the milestone in its lifecycle. \'at_risk\' indicates the milestone is projected to miss its planned date. \'delayed\' indicates the actual date has passed the planned date without completion. Used for portfolio-level trial timeline management and escalation triggers.']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the milestone by functional area: enrollment (patient-facing), data_management (EDC/database activities), analysis (statistical/interim), regulatory (submission-related), safety (DSMB/safety review), or operational (site/logistics). Supports portfolio-level filtering and reporting.']
  notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stakeholder notifications (e.g., to regulatory affairs, clinical operations leadership, CRO partners) have been dispatched upon milestone completion or delay detection. Supports automated workflow orchestration.']
  planned_date date [note: 'The originally baselined target date for achieving this milestone, as defined in the clinical trial protocol or project plan. Used as the reference point for variance calculation and regulatory commitment tracking.']
  predecessor_milestone_code varchar [note: 'The milestone_code of the immediately preceding milestone that must be completed before this milestone can begin. Enables dependency-aware critical path analysis and automated scheduling in the CTMS.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region associated with this milestone for regional portfolio reporting and regulatory tracking. Used when a milestone applies to a specific region rather than a single country or globally.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this milestone record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements under GCP and 21 CFR Part 11, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks lakehouse.']
  variance_commentary varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative explaining the reason for any deviation between planned and actual/forecast milestone dates. Captures context not covered by variance_reason_code, including mitigation actions taken. Required for regulatory commitment reporting to FDA/EMA.']
  variance_days varchar [note: 'Signed integer representing the difference in calendar days between the actual (or forecast) date and the planned date. Positive values indicate delay; negative values indicate early completion. Stored as a business field to support regulatory commitment reporting without requiring runtime calculation.']
  variance_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code categorizing the root cause of any milestone variance. Used for portfolio-level trend analysis and process improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: site_activation_delay|enrollment_shortfall|protocol_amendment|regulatory_hold|safety_event|data_quality|resource_constraint|vendor_delay|other — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking key operational milestones for each clinical trial, including FPFV (First Patient First Visit), LPLV (Last Patient Last Visit), database lock date, CSR completion date, regulatory submission date, and interim analysis dates. Captures planned vs. actual dates, milestone status, responsible function, and variance commentary. Supports portfolio-level trial timeline management and regulatory commitment tracking.'
}

Table clinical.biospecimen {
  biospecimen_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the biospecimen record. Primary key for the biospecimen transactional entity.']
  accounts_payable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_payable. Business justification: Biospecimen processing and storage generate vendor invoices from central labs, biobanks, and specialty testing facilities. Required for trial cost tracking, vendor payment processing, and per-protocol']
  clinical_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.enrollment. Business justification: Biospecimens are collected from enrolled subjects and may be analyzed by treatment arm for exploratory biomarker endpoints. Linking biospecimen to enrollment enables treatment arm stratification of bi']
  crf_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.crf_form. Business justification: Biospecimen collection is documented via CRF forms (biospecimen collection CRF) in the EDC system. Linking biospecimen to crf_form enables traceability to the data collection instrument and supports s']
  discovery_assay_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.assay. Business justification: Translational medicine programs run validated discovery assays (target engagement, PD biomarker assays) on clinical biospecimens. The biospecimen record must reference which discovery assay is planned']
  dmf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.dmf. Business justification: Bioanalytical methods, reference standards, and assay validation for biospecimens are documented in Drug Master Files (Type II or III). CMC regulatory teams must reference the appropriate DMF when bio']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the biospecimen was collected. Links to the site master record in the Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS).']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Biomarker studies, PK/PD analysis, and translational research require linking specimens to the product being tested. Essential for dose-response modeling, bioequivalence studies, and supporting produc']
  parent_biospecimen_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on biospecimen (parent_biospecimen_id)']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial subject from whom this biospecimen was collected. Links to the subject master record in the clinical trial management system.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.protocol. Business justification: Biospecimen collection procedures, timing, and handling are defined in the protocol. Linking biospecimen to protocol version enables verification that collection followed the protocol-specified proced']
  reagent_inventory_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: Biospecimen processing uses reagent/consumable lots; GCP compliance (ICH E6) requires lot traceability for data integrity and result reproducibility. Role prefix distinguishes reagent lots from clinic']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: Biospecimens are shipped from investigational sites to central labs. Linking biospecimen to shipment supports chain-of-custody verification and specimen integrity documentation. shipment_tracking_numb']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.storage_location. Business justification: Biospecimens are stored at specific controlled locations (central labs, biobanks, -80°C freezers). Required for sample tracking, chain of custody, retrieval for analysis, temperature excursion monitor']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial under which this biospecimen was collected. Links to the trial master record.']
  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: Specimen volume uses standardized units (mL, µL). Required for lab processing (sufficient volume checks), aliquot planning, inventory management, and CDISC SDTM LB domain mapping. Essential for specim']
  visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the protocol-defined visit during which this biospecimen was collected. Links to the visit schedule in the Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system.']
  anatomical_site varchar [note: 'The anatomical location from which the specimen was collected, such as left arm, liver, or lung. Particularly important for tissue biopsies and tumor samples.']
  assay_planned varchar [note: 'The name or code of the assay or test planned to be performed on this specimen, such as pharmacokinetic (PK) analysis, biomarker assay, or genomic sequencing. Supports laboratory planning and resource allocation.']
  chain_of_custody_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the chain of custody for the specimen has been verified and documented. True if verified, False otherwise. Critical for regulatory compliance and data integrity.']
  collection_date date [note: 'The date on which the biospecimen was collected from the trial subject. Critical for protocol compliance and time-to-event analysis.']
  collection_method varchar [note: 'The method or procedure used to collect the specimen, such as venipuncture, biopsy, or catheterization. Ensures protocol compliance and reproducibility.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the biospecimen was collected. Required for pharmacokinetic (PK) and pharmacodynamic (PD) studies where timing is critical.']
  collection_visit_name varchar [note: 'The protocol-defined name of the visit during which the specimen was collected, such as Baseline, Week 4, End of Treatment. Aligns with the visit schedule in the Case Report Form (CRF).']
  consent_biobank_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject provided optional consent for long-term biobanking and future research use of this specimen. True if consent was provided, False otherwise.']
  consent_genetic_testing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject provided optional consent for genetic or pharmacogenomic testing on this specimen. True if consent was provided, False otherwise.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'The type of tube or container used for specimen collection, such as EDTA tube, serum separator tube, cryovial. Critical for ensuring proper specimen handling and assay validity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this biospecimen record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  deviation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of any protocol deviation that occurred during specimen collection, processing, or handling. Required for regulatory documentation.']
  fasting_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the subject was fasting at the time of specimen collection. Critical for certain laboratory tests such as glucose and lipid panels.']
  loinc_code varchar [note: 'The standardized LOINC code representing the specimen type and associated test. Enables interoperability with laboratory information systems and regulatory submissions.']
  processing_date date [note: 'The date on which the specimen was processed in the laboratory. Used to track time from collection to processing for quality control.']
  processing_method varchar [note: 'The method used to process the specimen after collection, such as centrifugation, aliquoting, or fixation. Ensures traceability of specimen handling procedures.']
  protocol_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the specimen collection deviated from the protocol-defined procedures. True if a deviation occurred, False otherwise.']
  received_date date [note: 'The date on which the specimen was received at the central laboratory or biobank. Used to verify shipment integrity and processing timelines.']
  shipment_date date [note: 'The date on which the specimen was shipped to the central laboratory or biobank. Used to track transit time and ensure cold chain compliance.']
  snomed_code varchar [note: 'The standardized SNOMED CT code representing the specimen type or anatomical source. Supports clinical terminology standardization and regulatory compliance.']
  specimen_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the biospecimen, typically a barcode or alphanumeric code assigned at collection. Used for chain-of-custody tracking and laboratory processing.']
  specimen_quality varchar [note: 'Assessment of the specimen quality upon receipt or processing. Identifies any conditions that may affect assay results or specimen usability.']
  specimen_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the biospecimen, indicating its stage in the collection, processing, storage, and analysis workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: collected|processed|stored|shipped|received|analyzed|depleted|discarded — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  specimen_subtype varchar [note: 'Further classification or detail of the specimen type, such as specific tissue origin or blood component. Provides granular categorization for specialized assays.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the biological specimen collected. Defines the nature of the sample for processing and analysis requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: blood|serum|plasma|urine|tissue|csf|saliva|bone_marrow|tumor_biopsy — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  storage_temperature varchar [note: 'The temperature condition under which the specimen is stored, such as room temperature, refrigerated (2-8°C), frozen at -20°C, frozen at -80°C, or in liquid nitrogen. Critical for specimen stability and assay integrity.']
  study_day varchar [note: 'The day relative to the reference date (typically first dose or randomization) on which the specimen was collected. Negative values indicate pre-treatment, positive values indicate post-treatment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this biospecimen record was last modified in the system. Supports audit trail and change tracking for regulatory compliance.']
  volume_collected decimal(10,3) [note: 'The quantity of specimen collected, measured in the unit specified in volume_unit. Used to determine sufficiency for planned assays.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each biological specimen (tissue biopsy, blood draw, urine, CSF) collected from a trial subject at protocol-defined timepoints. Captures specimen type, collection date, collection visit, tube/container type, volume/quantity, processing method, storage temperature, biobank location, shipment tracking to central lab, LOINC/SNOMED coding, chain-of-custody audit trail, and associated assay results reference. Critical for oncology trials requiring RECIST tumor assessments, pharmacogenomic stratification, companion diagnostic validation, and translational research endpoints. Sourced from LIMS integration with Medidata Rave EDC.'
}

Table clinical.site_activation {
  site_activation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the site_activation association']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the investigational site participating in this trial activation']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the clinical trial for which this site has been activated']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the site close-out visit was completed and the site was formally closed for this specific trial. The same site may have different closure dates across different trials.']
  enrolled_subject_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of subjects who have been randomized and enrolled at this site for this specific trial. Per-trial-per-site metric that cannot reside on the site master record.']
  enrollment_target varchar [note: 'Planned number of subjects to be enrolled at this site for this specific trial, as agreed between the sponsor and site. Belongs to the site-trial pairing, not the site master, because the same site may have different enrollment targets across different trials.']
  etmf_site_folder_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the site-level folder in the Veeva Vault eTMF system for this specific trial. Each trial generates a separate eTMF site folder per site, making this a site-trial-level reference.']
  fpfv_actual_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the first patient first visit occurred at this site for this trial. Used to calculate FPFV variance against target for operational reporting.']
  fpfv_target_date date [note: 'Planned date for the First Patient First Visit (FPFV) at this site for this trial. A key activation milestone specific to the site-trial combination.']
  gcp_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current GCP inspection status of this site for this specific trial by a regulatory authority. Inspection outcomes are trial-specific and must be tracked at the site-trial level.']
  investigational_site_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of this site within this specific trial. A site may be actively enrolling in one trial while on-hold in another, confirming this is a site-trial-level attribute.']
  irb_iec_approval_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current IRB/IEC approval for this trial protocol at this site expires. Renewal is required per trial, making this a site-trial-level attribute.']
  irb_iec_approval_number varchar [note: 'Official approval reference number issued by the IRB/IEC for this specific trial protocol at this site. Each trial requires a separate IRB/IEC approval at each site.']
  irb_iec_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Institutional Review Board or Independent Ethics Committee that approved this trial at this site. May differ by trial if a site uses different ethics committees for different protocols.']
  last_gcp_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent GCP inspection at this site for this trial. Trial-specific inspection history belongs to the site activation record.']
  lplv_actual_date date [note: 'Actual date of the Last Patient Last Visit at this site for this trial, marking the end of active enrollment and data collection at this site.']
  screen_failure_count varchar [note: 'Number of subjects screened but failing eligibility criteria at this site for this specific trial. Varies by trial protocol and site population.']
  selection_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor formally selected this site for participation in this specific trial. Marks the beginning of the site activation lifecycle for this site-trial pairing.']
  siv_date date [note: 'Date on which the Site Initiation Visit (SIV) was conducted by the CRA for this site-trial combination. A mandatory GCP milestone before first patient enrollment.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Site Activation (Trial Site) between investigational_site and trial. It captures the full operational lifecycle of a specific investigational site\'s participation in a specific clinical trial — from site selection and IRB approval through site initiation, enrollment performance, and formal site closure. Each record links one investigational_site to one trial and holds all per-trial-per-site attributes that exist only in the context of that site-trial pairing, including enrollment milestones, IRB/IEC approval details, GCP inspection status, and eTMF folder references. This is the authoritative SSOT for site-level trial operational data consumed by CTMS, eTMF, and clinical operations reporting.. Existence Justification: In pharmaceutical clinical operations, an investigational site participates in MULTIPLE clinical trials over its lifetime, and each clinical trial is conducted across MULTIPLE investigational sites simultaneously. This is the foundational operational reality of multi-site clinical trials. The business actively manages each site-trial pairing as a distinct operational entity — called a \'trial site\' or \'site activation\' — with its own enrollment targets, IRB approvals, SIV dates, FPFV milestones, and closure dates that are specific to that site-trial combination and belong to neither the site master record nor the trial master record alone.'
}

Table regulatory.submission {
  submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each regulatory submission record in the Pharmaceuticals data lakehouse. Primary key for the submission data product.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent regulatory application (e.g., NDA, BLA, MAA, IND) under which this submission sequence is filed. Each submission belongs to exactly one parent application.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Submissions are filed for specific commercial brands. Brand_id enables tracking submission activity by brand for lifecycle management, launch planning, and regulatory intelligence reporting.']
  cro_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: CROs are contracted business partners providing regulatory submission preparation and filing services. Tracking the CRO entity is essential for vendor management, quality oversight, and regulatory ins']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Submissions cover specific drug product formulations (CMC sections, dosage form/strength/route). Regulatory CMC teams track which drug_product a submission covers for CMC lifecycle management and eCTD']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory health authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, NMPA) to which this submission is directed. Determines jurisdiction-specific requirements and timelines.']
  indication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Submissions are filed for specific indications (sNDA, label update for indication expansion). submission.indication is a denormalized text field; linking to product.indication normalizes it and enable']
  inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: INN (International Nonproprietary Name) is the WHO-designated active substance identifier required in all regulatory submissions for global harmonization and pharmacovigilance. Core reference for CTD ']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Regulatory submissions incur substantial costs (filing fees, CRO services, document preparation, translation) that are tracked via internal orders for budget control and cost allocation to therapeutic']
  investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.investigator. Business justification: IND/NDA submissions require a qualified principal investigator with FDA Form 1572 on file and current GCP training. Regulatory operations teams must verify investigator qualifications (gcp_training_st']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Each regulatory submission is filed by a specific legal entity (the applicant/MAH). Tracking the submitting legal entity per submission is required for fee invoicing, compliance reporting, and regulat']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.hcp_master. Business justification: IND and NDA submissions must identify the principal investigator or medical expert responsible for clinical data. Regulatory affairs tracks investigator accountability for submission content and respo']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the drug product or drug substance master record associated with this regulatory submission, linking the submission to the medicinal product being reviewed.']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: ANDA and 505(b)(2) submissions require patent certification (21 CFR 314.94(a)(12)) citing specific patents for Paragraph I-IV declarations. Orange Book listing process mandates patent identification i']
  pricing_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.pricing_decision. Business justification: Pricing decisions reference the regulatory submission as their regulatory basis (pricing_decision.regulatory_submission_ref is a plain text denormalization). Replacing with FK enables proper join for ']
  process_validation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.process_validation. Business justification: NDA/BLA/MAA submissions include process validation data in CMC sections as supporting evidence. Regulatory CMC teams must link submissions to the specific process validation studies referenced in the ']
  responsible_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: Regulatory affairs teams are organized by org unit; submissions are assigned to specific regulatory units for workload management, resource planning, and performance reporting. A pharma regulatory ope']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.site. Business justification: Regulatory submissions are filed for specific manufacturing sites (CMC sections, PAI triggers, GMP certificates). Regulatory affairs teams must track which manufacturing site each submission covers fo']
  actual_decision_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the health authority issued its formal regulatory decision (approval, complete response, refusal, withdrawal). Used to measure review cycle time against PDUFA/EMA target dates and track regulatory performance.']
  agency_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Official tracking or docket number assigned by the health authority upon receipt of the submission (e.g., FDA STN number, EMA procedure number). Used for all official correspondence and status inquiries with the regulatory agency.']
  cover_letter_title varchar [note: 'Title or subject line of the cover letter accompanying this submission, summarizing the purpose and scope of the filing. Provides a human-readable description of the submission intent for regulatory affairs tracking and correspondence management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this submission record was first created in the data lakehouse, conforming to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory record-keeping requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  ctd_modules_included varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ICH CTD modules included in this submission (e.g., \'1,2,3,4,5\' for a full NDA; \'1,2\' for an administrative amendment). Modules: 1=Administrative/Regional; 2=Summaries; 3=Quality (CMC); 4=Nonclinical; 5=Clinical. Supports dossier completeness assessment.']
  document_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual documents included in this submission sequence. Used for dossier completeness assessment, submission quality metrics, and workload estimation for health authority reviewers.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'Pharmaceutical dosage form of the drug product covered by this submission (e.g., tablet, capsule, injection, lyophilized powder for solution, transdermal patch). Captured per ICH M4 CTD Module 1 product information.']
  dossier_format varchar [note: 'Technical format of the regulatory dossier submitted to the health authority. eCTD (Electronic Common Technical Document) is the current standard; NeeS (Non-eCTD Electronic Submission) and paper are legacy formats. Determines validation and processing requirements.']
  ectd_operation varchar [note: 'eCTD lifecycle operation code indicating how this submission sequence modifies the dossier content relative to the prior sequence. New = first submission of a document; Replace = supersedes a prior document; Append = adds to existing content; Delete = removes prior content. Conforms to ICH M8 eCTD specification.']
  fee_waiver_granted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory user fee waiver or exemption has been granted for this submission (e.g., orphan drug fee waiver, small business waiver, public health waiver). True = fee waiver approved by the health authority.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the submission within the regulatory review lifecycle. Draft = being prepared; Submitted = transmitted to agency; Under Review = agency actively reviewing; Approved = marketing authorization granted; Complete Response = FDA issued CRL requiring additional data; Refused = EMA/MHRA refusal; Withdrawn = applicant withdrew; On Hold = review paused. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product]']
  medium varchar [note: 'Physical or electronic channel used to transmit the submission to the health authority. Electronic Gateway (e.g., FDA ESG, EMA eSubmission Gateway) is the standard; physical media (CD/DVD/USB) and portal submissions are also tracked.']
  pai_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Pre-Approval Inspection (PAI) of manufacturing facilities is required as part of the review of this submission. PAIs verify GMP compliance and data integrity of manufacturing processes prior to approval. True = PAI required.']
  priority_designation varchar [note: 'Special regulatory designation granted to the submission or parent application that confers expedited review or development benefits. Includes FDA Breakthrough Therapy, Fast Track, Priority Review, Accelerated Approval, Orphan Drug designation. Multiple designations may apply; primary designation recorded here.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the health authority officially acknowledged receipt of the submission. Triggers the start of the regulatory review clock and PDUFA/EMA review timeline. May differ from submission_date due to gateway processing.']
  regulatory_pathway varchar [note: 'Regulatory review pathway designation assigned to this submission, determining review timelines and procedural requirements. Examples include FDA Standard, Priority Review, Accelerated Approval, Breakthrough Therapy, Fast Track; EMA Conditional Marketing Authorisation, Rolling Review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product]']
  rems_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program is required or proposed as part of this submission. REMS programs are FDA-mandated risk management strategies for drugs with serious safety concerns. True = REMS required/proposed.']
  review_division varchar [note: 'Name of the health authority review division or committee responsible for evaluating this submission (e.g., FDA CDER Office of Oncology Products, EMA Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP), PMDA Office of New Drug I). Determines reviewer assignment and procedural rules.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'Route by which the drug product is administered to patients as specified in this submission (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, intramuscular, topical, inhalation). Captured per ICH M4 CTD Module 1 product information.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit zero-padded eCTD sequence number uniquely identifying this submission within the parent application lifecycle (e.g., 0000 for initial submission, 0001 for first amendment). Conforms to ICH M8 eCTD technical specification.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the submission was formally transmitted to the health authority. This is the principal business event date used for regulatory timeline calculations, PDUFA date computation, and compliance tracking.']
  submission_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable external submission reference number assigned by the health authority or internal tracking system (e.g., NDA 123456/S-001, EMA/H/C/000123/0001). Used for correspondence and regulatory tracking.']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the submission event within the regulatory application lifecycle. Original = initial filing; Supplement = post-approval change; Amendment = pre-approval change; Annual Report = periodic safety/status update; Response = reply to agency information request; Withdrawal = voluntary withdrawal of application. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: original|supplement|amendment|annual_report|response|withdrawal — promote to reference product]']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Further classification of the submission type providing regulatory granularity (e.g., Prior Approval Supplement, Changes Being Effected Supplement, Type IA Variation, Type IB Variation, Type II Variation). Jurisdiction-specific categorization used for review timeline determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product]']
  target_action_date date [note: 'Regulatory target date by which the health authority is committed to completing its review and issuing a decision. For FDA submissions, this is the Prescription Drug User Fee Act (PDUFA) goal date. For EMA, this is the Day 210 opinion date. Critical milestone for commercial planning.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Clinical therapeutic area or disease category addressed by the drug product in this submission (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Cardiovascular, Neurology). Used for portfolio analytics, resource planning, and regulatory strategy alignment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this submission record was last modified in the data lakehouse, conforming to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, audit trail, and data integrity verification under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  validation_error_count varchar [note: 'Number of technical validation errors identified during eCTD dossier validation. Zero indicates a clean submission. Used to assess submission quality and track remediation effort prior to resubmission.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Technical validation status of the eCTD dossier as assessed by the health authority\'s automated validation tools (e.g., FDA Validation Criteria, EMA eCTD Validation Criteria). Passed = all technical criteria met; Failed = validation errors requiring correction; Waived = validation requirement formally waived.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Unique document identifier assigned by Veeva Vault RIM (Regulatory Information Management) system for this submission record. Enables direct cross-reference and deep-link navigation to the source system of record for submission lifecycle management.']

  Note: 'Master record and single source of truth for all regulatory submissions (filing events) to health authorities including original applications, supplements, amendments, responses to information requests, and annual reports. Each submission represents a discrete eCTD sequence filed under a parent application. Tracks submission type, sequence number, dossier format (eCTD), submission date, target agency, eCTD lifecycle operation (new, replace, append, delete), submission medium, validation status, regulatory pathway, priority designation, and current lifecycle status. Includes document-level composition tracking with CTD module/section assignment (Modules 1-5), individual document metadata (title, type, version, author, status, file format, checksum), version control, and dossier completeness assessment aligned with ICH M4 (CTD structure) and M8 (eCTD technical specification). SSOT for regulatory submission identity, lifecycle, sequence tracking, dossier content, and document inventory in Veeva Vault RIM.'
}

Table regulatory.health_authority {
  health_authority_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each health authority agency record in the regulatory master reference. Primary key for the health_authority data product.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Health authority jurisdiction defines regulatory framework, submission requirements, and inspection authority. Essential for global regulatory strategy, submission planning, and agency interaction tra']
  mailing_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.address. Business justification: health_authority.mailing_address is a plain-text denormalization of a structured address. Normalizing to masterdata.address supports submission portal management, correspondence tracking, and GMP insp']
  accepted_dossier_formats varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of submission dossier formats accepted by the health authority (e.g., \'eCTD\', \'NeeS\', \'paper\', \'eCTD v4.0\'). Drives submission preparation and formatting decisions in Veeva Vault RIM and RIMS.']
  acronym varchar [note: 'Commonly used abbreviation or acronym for the health authority (e.g., \'FDA\', \'EMA\', \'PMDA\', \'MHRA\', \'NMPA\', \'WHO\'). Used in regulatory correspondence, submission tracking, and reporting.']
  agency_name varchar [note: 'Official full legal name of the health authority agency (e.g., \'U.S. Food and Drug Administration\', \'European Medicines Agency\'). Used as the primary identity label for the agency across all regulatory activities.']
  agency_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the health authority record. \'active\' indicates the agency is currently operational and accepting submissions; \'inactive\' indicates it is no longer operational; \'merged\' indicates it has been absorbed into another agency; \'dissolved\' indicates it has been formally disbanded; \'under_review\' indicates the record is pending validation.']
  agency_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the health authority by its organizational scope and mandate. Distinguishes between national competent authorities (e.g., FDA, MHRA), supranational agencies (e.g., EMA), international organizations (e.g., WHO), regional authorities, and advisory bodies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: national_competent_authority|supranational_agency|international_organization|regional_authority|advisory_body — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  agency_website_url varchar [note: 'Official public website URL of the health authority (e.g., https://www.fda.gov, https://www.ema.europa.eu). Used for reference, guidance document retrieval, and regulatory intelligence.']
  bla_accepted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority accepts Biologics License Application (BLA) submissions or their local equivalent for marketing authorization of biological products, vaccines, and biosimilars.']
  breakthrough_designation_program boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority offers a breakthrough therapy designation or equivalent expedited development program (e.g., FDA Breakthrough Therapy, EMA PRIME scheme, MHRA Innovative Licensing and Access Pathway). Drives early engagement strategy.']
  dmf_accepted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority accepts Drug Master File (DMF) submissions for confidential disclosure of manufacturing, processing, packaging, and storage information for drug substances and excipients.']
  ectd_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority mandates submission in Electronic Common Technical Document (eCTD) format as per ICH M8 guideline. Drives submission formatting and Veeva Vault RIM configuration.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this health authority record is considered valid and active for use in regulatory submissions and compliance activities. Supports temporal validity management in the regulatory master data.']
  external_agency_code varchar [note: 'Official external identifier assigned to the health authority by an industry body or cross-reference system (e.g., DUNS number, ISO organization code, or identifier from a regulatory intelligence database). Supports cross-system reconciliation.']
  gmp_inspection_authority boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority has the mandate and capability to conduct Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) inspections of pharmaceutical manufacturing facilities. Drives inspection readiness and compliance programs.']
  ich_member boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority is a full member or observer of the International Council for Harmonisation (ICH). ICH membership signals alignment with harmonized technical guidelines for drug registration.']
  icsr_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority requires Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) submissions for pharmacovigilance and adverse event reporting. Drives Argus Safety / Oracle AERS configuration for expedited and periodic safety reporting.']
  ind_accepted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority accepts Investigational New Drug (IND) applications or their local equivalent for clinical trial authorization. Drives IND filing strategy for global clinical development programs.']
  jurisdiction_type varchar [note: 'Scope of regulatory jurisdiction exercised by the health authority — federal (e.g., FDA within the US), national (e.g., MHRA for UK), regional (e.g., state-level agencies), supranational (e.g., EMA for EU member states), or international (e.g., WHO).']
  language_of_submission varchar [note: 'Official language(s) required for regulatory submissions to this health authority (e.g., \'English\' for FDA/EMA/MHRA, \'Japanese\' for PMDA, \'Chinese (Simplified)\' for NMPA). Drives translation and localization planning for global submissions.']
  local_representative_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority requires a local regulatory representative, marketing authorization holder, or in-country agent to be designated for submissions and post-approval activities. Critical for market access planning in jurisdictions like Japan (PMDA) and China (NMPA).']
  mutual_recognition_agreements varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Mutual Recognition Agreements (MRA) or equivalence arrangements the health authority has with other regulatory bodies (e.g., \'FDA-EMA MRA\', \'PIC/S Member\'). Impacts GMP inspection reliance and submission strategy.']
  nda_accepted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority accepts New Drug Application (NDA) submissions or their local equivalent (e.g., MAA for EMA, J-NDA for PMDA) for marketing authorization of small molecule drugs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional regulatory context, special requirements, recent guidance updates, or operational notes specific to this health authority. Maintained by the Regulatory Affairs team.']
  orphan_drug_program boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority operates an orphan drug designation program providing incentives (e.g., market exclusivity, fee waivers, protocol assistance) for rare disease treatments. Relevant for oncology and rare disease therapeutic areas.']
  pai_conducted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority conducts Pre-Approval Inspections (PAI) of manufacturing facilities as part of the marketing authorization review process. Drives inspection readiness planning.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Official primary contact email address for regulatory correspondence with the health authority. Used for submission inquiries, meeting requests, and formal communications.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Official primary telephone number for the health authority\'s regulatory affairs contact office. Includes country dialing code.']
  priority_review_duration_days varchar [note: 'Expedited or priority review clock duration in calendar days for applications granted priority review designation at this health authority (e.g., 180 days for FDA Priority Review, 150 days for EMA accelerated assessment). Supports regulatory strategy for breakthrough therapies.']
  psur_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority requires Periodic Safety Update Reports (PSUR) or Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Reports (PBRER) as part of post-marketing pharmacovigilance obligations. Aligns with ICH E2C(R2) guideline.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this health authority master record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GDPR.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this health authority master record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and data currency validation under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geopolitical or regulatory region associated with the health authority (e.g., \'North America\', \'European Union\', \'Asia Pacific\', \'Latin America\', \'Middle East & Africa\'). Supports regional regulatory strategy planning and submission portfolio management.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory framework or legislation governing the health authority\'s operations (e.g., \'21 CFR Parts 210/211/312/314\' for FDA, \'Directive 2001/83/EC and Regulation 726/2004\' for EMA, \'Pharmaceutical Affairs Act\' for PMDA). Critical for compliance mapping.']
  rems_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority has a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) or equivalent risk management program framework applicable to submissions. Relevant for FDA and analogous risk management programs at other agencies.']
  standard_review_duration_days varchar [note: 'Standard regulatory review clock duration in calendar days for a standard marketing authorization application at this health authority (e.g., 365 days for FDA standard NDA review, 210 days for EMA centralized procedure). Used for regulatory timeline planning.']
  submission_portal_url varchar [note: 'Official URL of the health authority\'s electronic submission portal (e.g., FDA ESG gateway, EMA CESP portal, PMDA eCTD gateway). Used by regulatory affairs teams to route and track electronic submissions.']
  veeva_vault_agency_code varchar [note: 'Agency identifier code as configured in Veeva Vault Regulatory Information Management (RIM) system. Used to link this master record to submission tracking, dossier management, and regulatory correspondence workflows in Veeva Vault.']

  Note: 'Reference master for all global health authority agencies with which the company interacts, including FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, NMPA, WHO, and national competent authorities. Captures agency name, acronym, jurisdiction, country/region, agency type, contact details, submission portal URL, accepted dossier formats, and applicable regulatory frameworks. Enables consistent agency identification across all regulatory activities.'
}

Table regulatory.application {
  application_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the regulatory application record in the data platform. Primary key for the application entity.']
  access_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.access_strategy. Business justification: Regulatory applications drive market access strategy development; access teams plan reimbursement pathways, HTA submissions, and launch sequencing based on application status and approval timelines fo']
  atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: ATC classification in regulatory applications determines therapeutic category for health authority review, pricing negotiations, and reimbursement decisions. Required for CTD Module 1 and health econo']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: NDA/BLA/MAA applications are filed for a specific drug product formulation (dosage form, strength, route). Regulatory teams need this link for CMC section tracking and application-to-formulation trace']
  exclusivity_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.exclusivity_period. Business justification: Applications track data_exclusivity_expiry_date and market_exclusivity_expiry_date as denormalized plain attributes. Application-level exclusivity planning (NCE, orphan, pediatric) requires a proper F']
  finance_internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Regulatory applications should link to finance internal orders for regulatory program budget tracking and cost allocation. This enables accurate tracking of regulatory affairs costs by application and']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'FK to regulatory.health_authority']
  indication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.indication.
