Business justification: Applications are filed for specific therapeutic indications. application.therapeutic_indication is a denormalized text field; normalizing via FK to product.indication enables indication-level applicat']
  inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: Regulatory applications must reference INN for active substance identification, therapeutic classification, and global regulatory intelligence. Essential for NDA/BLA/MAA submissions and competitive la']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity (company or affiliate) that is the named applicant/sponsor on the regulatory application.']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: Applications for in-licensed assets are governed by licensing agreements specifying field of use, territory, and regulatory diligence obligations. Regulatory milestone payments (filing, approval) are ']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lead drug product (Drug Product / Drug Substance) associated with this regulatory application.']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: NDA/BLA applications must declare patent information for Orange Book listing per 21 CFR 314.53. Business process: patent declaration at application submission, lifecycle management, and generic entry ']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'Date on which the health authority formally accepted the application for substantive review (i.e., filing acceptance / validation date). Marks the start of the official review clock.']
  agency_application_number varchar [note: 'The official application number assigned by the health authority upon receipt of the submission (e.g., NDA 123456, BLA 761234, EMA/H/C/004321). This is the externally-known business identifier for the application.']
  applicant_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the company or entity listed as the applicant/sponsor on the regulatory application as filed with the health authority.']
  application_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory application as tracked in the regulatory information management system. Drives workflow and reporting across regulatory affairs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|refused|withdrawn|on_hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  application_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory application type filed with the health authority. IND = Investigational New Drug, NDA = New Drug Application, BLA = Biologics License Application, ANDA = Abbreviated New Drug Application, MAA = Marketing Authorization Application, sNDA/sBLA = supplemental applications, 510k = medical device premarket notification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IND|NDA|BLA|ANDA|MAA|sNDA|sBLA|510k — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the health authority granted formal approval or marketing authorization for the application. Null if not yet approved.']
  breakthrough_therapy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the FDA has granted Breakthrough Therapy Designation (BTD) for this application, enabling intensive FDA guidance and rolling review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this regulatory application record was first created in the data platform, sourced from Veeva Vault RIM or the regulatory information management system.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'The pharmaceutical dosage form of the drug product covered by this application (e.g., tablet, capsule, injection, lyophilized powder for solution). Aligns with CMC section of the dossier.']
  ectd_sequence_number varchar [note: 'The four-digit eCTD sequence number of the original submission (sequence 0000) as assigned in the eCTD lifecycle. Used to track the dossier version in Veeva Vault RIM and agency systems.']
  fast_track_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the FDA has granted Fast Track Designation for this application, facilitating more frequent interactions with FDA and eligibility for rolling review.']
  lead_division_code varchar [note: 'The FDA Office/Division or EMA Committee (e.g., CDER, CBER, CHMP, COMP) responsible for reviewing this application. Determines the review team and applicable guidance documents.']
  new_molecular_entity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the active ingredient in this application is a New Chemical Entity (NCE) or New Molecular Entity (NME) that has not previously been approved by the health authority, conferring 5-year data exclusivity in the US.']
  orphan_designation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the drug product has received Orphan Drug Designation (ODD) from the FDA or EMA for the indication covered by this application, conferring market exclusivity and fee waivers.']
  pai_outcome varchar [note: 'The outcome classification of the Pre-Approval Inspection (PAI) conducted at the manufacturing site. VAI = Voluntary Action Indicated; OAI = Official Action Indicated (may delay approval).']
  pai_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Pre-Approval Inspection (PAI) of the manufacturing facility has been required or triggered as part of the review of this application.']
  pdufa_action_date date [note: 'The target action date committed by the FDA under the Prescription Drug User Fee Act (PDUFA), or equivalent agency action deadline (e.g., EMA opinion date, PMDA review deadline). Critical milestone for commercial planning.']
  post_approval_commitment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approval of this application is subject to post-approval commitments or post-marketing requirements (PMRs/PMCs) imposed by the health authority.']
  priority_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the application has been granted Priority Review designation by the health authority, entitling it to a shorter review clock (6 months for FDA vs. standard 10 months).']
  reference_product_name varchar [note: 'For ANDA (generic) or biosimilar applications, the name of the reference listed drug (RLD) or reference biological product against which the application is filed. Null for original NDA/BLA applications.']
  regulatory_pathway varchar [note: 'The regulatory review pathway designated for this application. Determines review timelines, eligibility criteria, and post-approval commitments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|priority_review|accelerated_approval|breakthrough_therapy|fast_track|conditional_approval|exceptional_circumstances — promote to reference product]']
  rems_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the FDA has required a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program as a condition of approval for this application, due to serious safety concerns.']
  rolling_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority has granted rolling review, allowing submission and review of completed dossier sections before the full application is submitted.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'The route by which the drug product is administered to patients (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, intramuscular). Defined in the CMC and clinical sections of the dossier.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the original regulatory application dossier was formally submitted to the health authority. This is the principal business event date for the application lifecycle.']
  submission_format varchar [note: 'The format in which the regulatory dossier was submitted to the health authority. eCTD (Electronic Common Technical Document) is the current standard; NeeS and paper are legacy formats.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this regulatory application record was most recently modified in the data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'The unique document or object identifier assigned to this application record in Veeva Vault RIM, enabling traceability back to the source system of record for the regulatory dossier.']

  Note: 'Master record for a regulatory application (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, ANDA, 510k) representing the formal dossier filed with a health authority for a specific drug product. Tracks application number assigned by the agency, application type, applicant entity, lead product, therapeutic indication, regulatory pathway, orphan designation status, priority review status, PDUFA/action date, and overall approval status. One application may have multiple submissions (original + supplements).'
}

Table regulatory.submission_sequence {
  submission_sequence_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each eCTD submission sequence record within the regulatory application lifecycle.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent regulatory application (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, etc.) to which this submission sequence belongs.']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Submission sequences are filed with specific health authorities. The submission_sequence table has health_authority_code (STRING) which is a denormalized reference. Replacing with health_authority_id ']
  rims_submission_id varchar [note: 'The native submission record identifier from the source Regulatory Information Management System (RIMS), specifically Veeva Vault RIM, used for cross-system traceability and reconciliation.']
  acknowledgement_date date [note: 'Date on which the health authority formally acknowledged receipt of the submission sequence. Triggers the start of the regulatory review clock for applicable submission types.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the submission sequence was formally approved for filing by the authorized signatory, capturing the electronic signature event per 21 CFR Part 11 requirements.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the authorized signatory or regulatory affairs head who approved this submission sequence for filing with the health authority.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this submission sequence record was first created in the data platform, serving as the audit trail creation marker per 21 CFR Part 11 and data governance requirements.']
  cro_cmo_involvement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Contract Research Organization (CRO) or Contract Manufacturing Organization (CMO) contributed data or documents to this submission sequence. Relevant for supply chain transparency and regulatory accountability.']
  ctd_module varchar [note: 'The primary Common Technical Document (CTD) module addressed by this submission sequence. M1 = Regional Administrative; M2 = Summaries; M3 = Quality (CMC); M4 = Nonclinical; M5 = Clinical. Used for content classification and routing.']
  ecdt_lifecycle_operation varchar [note: 'The eCTD document lifecycle operation applied in this sequence, indicating how the submitted documents relate to prior sequences. Values: new (first submission of a document), replace (supersedes a prior document), append (adds to a prior document), delete (removes a prior document). Aligns with ICH M8 eCTD lifecycle attribute.']
  ecdt_version varchar [note: 'The version of the Electronic Common Technical Document (eCTD) specification used to structure and validate this submission sequence (e.g., 3.2.2 for legacy, 4.0 for ICH M8 compliant submissions).']
  gateway_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Unique tracking or acknowledgement number assigned by the health authority\'s electronic submission gateway (e.g., FDA ESG acknowledgement number, EMA CESP submission ID) upon receipt of the eCTD package. Used for submission traceability and correspondence.']
  information_request_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the health authority information request, deficiency letter, or complete response letter (CRL) that this submission sequence is responding to. Null if not a response submission.']
  is_resubmission boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this submission sequence is a formal resubmission following a Complete Response Letter (CRL) or refusal, as opposed to an original or routine amendment filing.']
  package_size_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total file size of the eCTD submission package in megabytes. Used for submission logistics planning, gateway transmission monitoring, and compliance with health authority file size limits.']
  pdufa_goal_date date [note: 'The target action date committed by the FDA under the Prescription Drug User Fee Act (PDUFA) for completing review of this submission. Applicable to NDA/BLA submissions filed with the FDA. Null for non-FDA or non-PDUFA submissions.']
  priority_designation varchar [note: 'Regulatory priority designation applicable to this submission, which may affect review timelines and PDUFA goal dates. Examples: Priority Review, Breakthrough Therapy, Accelerated Approval, Orphan Drug, Fast Track (FDA); PRIME (EMA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|priority|breakthrough|accelerated|orphan|fast_track|prime|conditional — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_action_date date [note: 'The date on which the health authority issued a formal regulatory action (approval, complete response letter, refusal, approvable letter) in response to this submission sequence.']
  regulatory_action_type varchar [note: 'The type of formal regulatory decision issued by the health authority for this submission sequence. Approval = marketing authorization granted; Complete Response = FDA letter requesting additional information; Approvable/Not Approvable = legacy FDA designations; Refusal = EMA/MHRA refusal of marketing authorization; Withdrawal = application withdrawn.']
  regulatory_procedure_type varchar [note: 'The regulatory procedure under which this submission is filed, particularly relevant for EMA submissions. Centralized = EMA centralized procedure; Decentralized = DCP across multiple EU member states; Mutual Recognition = MRP; National = single country national procedure. Null for FDA submissions.']
  review_clock_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory review clock officially begins for this submission sequence, as determined by the health authority. May differ from acknowledgement_date for submissions requiring filing review or completeness assessment.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit zero-padded sequence number assigned to this submission event within the regulatory application, as defined by ICH M8 eCTD specification (e.g., 0000 for original submission, 0001 for first amendment).']
  submission_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the purpose and content of this submission sequence, summarizing the regulatory objective (e.g., \'Response to FDA Complete Response Letter dated 2024-03-15 addressing CMC deficiencies in Module 3\').']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the submission sequence reflecting the health authority\'s processing state. Draft = prepared but not yet transmitted; Submitted = transmitted to health authority; Acknowledged = receipt confirmed; Under Review = active regulatory review; Accepted = approved/validated; Refused = rejected by health authority; Withdrawn = retracted by sponsor. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|acknowledged|under_review|accepted|refused|withdrawn — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the eCTD sequence package was transmitted to or received by the health authority gateway (e.g., FDA ESG, EMA CESP). Distinct from submission_date which captures only the calendar date.']
  submission_unit_count varchar [note: 'Number of submission units (eCTD submission unit packages) included in this sequence. In eCTD v4.0, a sequence may contain multiple submission units each addressing different regulatory activities.']
  supplement_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory category of a supplement submission indicating the level of change and required review pathway. PAS = Prior Approval Supplement; CBE-30 = Changes Being Effected in 30 Days; CBE-0 = Changes Being Effected immediately; Class I/II per EMA variation classification. Null for non-supplement submissions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this submission sequence record was last modified in the data platform, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  validation_error_count varchar [note: 'Number of technical validation errors identified during eCTD package validation. A value of zero indicates a clean validation pass. Used to triage submission readiness and track remediation progress.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Technical validation status of the eCTD submission package against FDA/EMA validation criteria and the eCTD specification. Pending = validation not yet run; Passed = all validation checks successful; Failed = one or more validation errors identified; Waived = validation requirement formally waived by health authority.']

  Note: 'Tracks individual eCTD submission sequences within a regulatory application, representing each discrete filing event (original submission, amendment, response to information request, annual report, supplement). Captures sequence number, submission date, submission type (original, class I/II supplement, CBE, PAS), eCTD lifecycle operation (new, replace, append), submission medium, and validation status. Aligns with ICH M8 eCTD technical specification.'
}

Table regulatory.milestone {
  milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each regulatory milestone record. Primary key for the regulatory_milestone data product.']
  access_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.access_strategy. Business justification: Regulatory milestones (PDUFA date, approval date, CRL receipt) are key inputs to access strategy timelines. Market access teams update access strategy plans based on regulatory milestone outcomes. Int']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory application (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, eCTD dossier) associated with this milestone. Enables milestone tracking at the application level.']
  brand_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand_plan. Business justification: Regulatory milestones (PDUFA dates, approval dates, submission dates) are critical inputs to brand plan timelines and launch readiness planning. The \'milestone-gated brand plan\' process is a named con']
  exclusivity_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.exclusivity_period. Business justification: Regulatory milestones (approval date, pediatric study completion, orphan designation grant) directly trigger exclusivity period start dates. Milestone-based exclusivity grant tracking requires linking']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'FK to regulatory.health_authority']
  indication_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.indication']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Regulatory milestones drive budget allocation and cost tracking for submission programs. Each milestone (PDUFA date, PAI, approval) has associated costs tracked via internal orders for financial plann']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.hcp_master. Business justification: Regulatory milestones such as investigator meetings, advisory board consultations, or expert witness preparation involve specific physicians. Business process: documenting HCP participation in critica']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the drug product or compound associated with this regulatory milestone. Links the milestone to the master product record.']
  patent_term_extension_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_term_extension. Business justification: Regulatory milestones (approval date, PDUFA date, marketing authorization date) are the primary inputs for patent term extension calculations. PTE applications reference the specific approval mileston']
  post_approval_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.post_approval_commitment. Business justification: The milestone table has a post_approval_commitment_due_date attribute, indicating milestones are used to track PAC due dates. Normalizing this by adding post_approval_commitment_id FK enables direct n']
  product_recall_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.product_recall. Business justification: Recall management milestones (recall initiation, health authority notification deadline, effectiveness check, recall closure) are tracked in regulatory milestone systems. Linking milestone to product_']
  psur_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.psur. Business justification: PSUR submissions are regulatory milestones with defined due dates tracked in submission planning. Real business process: milestone planning includes PSUR data lock points and submission deadlines. Req']
  rmp_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.rmp. Business justification: RMP submissions and updates are regulatory milestones tracked in product lifecycle planning. Real business process: milestone tracking for RMP version submissions at approval, variations, and periodic']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.site. Business justification: Regulatory milestones include PAI dates, manufacturing readiness gates, and site-specific inspection milestones. Regulatory project managers track milestones per manufacturing site for critical path m']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent regulatory submission (e.g., IND, NDA, BLA, MAA) to which this milestone belongs. Links the milestone to its governing submission record.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.territory. Business justification: Regulatory milestones (approvals, label updates) trigger territory-level commercial activities like launch preparation, sales training, and call plan updates for field force readiness.']
  validation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.validation. Business justification: Regulatory milestones track validation completion dates (PPQ, process validation, cleaning validation) critical for submission readiness. Links regulatory timeline management to quality validation exe']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'Date on which the health authority formally accepted the submission for review (i.e., filing acceptance or validation date). For FDA NDA/BLA, this is the 74-day filing acceptance date. For EMA, this is the validation date.']
  actual_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the regulatory milestone was achieved or completed. Null if the milestone has not yet been reached. Compared against planned_date and revised_target_date to calculate schedule variance.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the health authority granted marketing authorization or approval for the drug product. Applicable for approval-type milestones. Null for non-approval milestones or pending applications.']
  completion_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the outcome, key decisions, or observations recorded at the time of milestone completion. Captures health authority feedback, meeting outcomes, or approval conditions relevant to this milestone.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory milestone record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.']
  crl_receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which a Complete Response Letter (CRL) was received from the FDA, indicating the agency cannot approve the application in its current form. Null if no CRL has been issued. Critical for tracking resubmission timelines.']
  delay_reason varchar [note: 'Documented reason for any delay in achieving the milestone relative to the planned or revised target date. Captures root cause categories such as health authority information requests, manufacturing issues, clinical data gaps, or strategic reprioritization.']
  ectd_sequence_number varchar [note: 'The eCTD sequence number of the submission associated with this milestone. Four-digit zero-padded sequence number assigned to each eCTD submission cycle (e.g., \'0000\' for initial submission, \'0001\' for first amendment). Enables traceability to the specific eCTD submission lifecycle.']
  is_critical_path boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this milestone is on the critical path of the regulatory project plan. True = milestone is critical path; any delay directly impacts the overall regulatory timeline and potential approval date.']
  meeting_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date of the health authority meeting (e.g., End-of-Phase 2 meeting, pre-NDA meeting, Advisory Committee meeting). Applicable for meeting-type milestones.']
  meeting_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the health authority meeting associated with this milestone. FDA meeting types: Type A (dispute resolution/clinical hold), Type B (pre-IND, EOP1, EOP2, pre-NDA/BLA), Type C (other). ADCOM = Advisory Committee Meeting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Type_A|Type_B|Type_C|ADCOM|pre_IND|EOP1|EOP2|pre_NDA|pre_BLA|scientific_advice|other — promote to reference product]']
  milestone_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory lifecycle phase category to which this milestone belongs. Enables grouping and reporting by development phase: pre-IND activities, IND phase, pre-NDA/BLA, NDA/BLA submission and review, MAA, post-approval, or lifecycle management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_ind|ind|pre_nda_bla|nda_bla|maa|post_approval|lifecycle — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  milestone_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the type of regulatory milestone (e.g., IND_FILING, EOP2_MTG, NDA_FILING, PDUFA_DATE, ADCOM_MTG, CRL_RECEIPT, APPROVAL, PAC_DUE). Used for systematic tracking and reporting across programs.']
  milestone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the regulatory milestone, including scope, deliverables, and any specific health authority requirements or conditions associated with achieving this milestone.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the regulatory milestone (e.g., \'IND Filing Date\', \'End-of-Phase 2 Meeting\', \'NDA Filing Date\', \'PDUFA Date\', \'Advisory Committee Meeting\', \'Complete Response Letter Receipt\', \'Approval Date\', \'Post-Approval Commitment Due Date\').']
  milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current completion status of the regulatory milestone. \'planned\' = scheduled but not yet started; \'in_progress\' = activities underway; \'completed\' = milestone achieved; \'delayed\' = past target date without completion; \'cancelled\' = milestone no longer applicable; \'on_hold\' = temporarily suspended.']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the milestone type. \'submission\' = filing of a dossier or amendment; \'meeting\' = health authority meeting (e.g., EOP2, pre-NDA); \'review\' = agency review action; \'decision\' = approval, refusal, or CRL; \'commitment\' = post-approval commitment; \'inspection\' = PAI or GMP inspection; \'correspondence\' = formal regulatory correspondence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: submission|meeting|review|decision|commitment|inspection|correspondence — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pai_date date [note: 'Date of the Pre-Approval Inspection (PAI) conducted by the health authority at the manufacturing facility prior to granting marketing approval. Applicable for PAI-type milestones. Null for non-inspection milestones.']
  pdufa_date date [note: 'The FDA-assigned Prescription Drug User Fee Act (PDUFA) action date by which the FDA commits to completing its review of an NDA or BLA. Applicable only to FDA submissions. Null for non-FDA applications or non-applicable milestone types.']
  planned_date date [note: 'The originally planned or target date for achieving this regulatory milestone, as established in the regulatory project plan. Used for baseline tracking and variance analysis against actual completion.']
  priority_review_designation varchar [note: 'FDA or health authority review designation assigned to the application associated with this milestone. Affects review timelines and PDUFA dates. \'priority\' = 6-month review; \'standard\' = 10-month review; \'breakthrough\' = Breakthrough Therapy Designation; \'accelerated\' = Accelerated Approval; \'fast_track\' = Fast Track Designation; \'orphan\' = Orphan Drug Designation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|priority|breakthrough|accelerated|fast_track|orphan|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rems_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program is required as part of the approval conditions associated with this milestone. True = REMS required; False = no REMS requirement.']
  responsible_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the regulatory affairs professional or team lead responsible for managing and delivering this milestone. Used for accountability tracking and workload management in regulatory project plans.']
  revised_target_date date [note: 'Most recently revised target date for the milestone, updated when the original planned date changes due to program delays, strategy shifts, or health authority feedback. Distinct from planned_date (baseline) to enable schedule variance tracking.']
  schedule_variance_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days between the planned_date and actual_date (or current date if not yet completed). Positive value indicates delay; negative value indicates early completion. Supports regulatory timeline performance reporting.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Therapeutic area or disease indication associated with the drug product for which this regulatory milestone applies (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Cardiovascular, Neurology). Enables milestone reporting and analytics by therapeutic area.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory milestone record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.']
  veeva_vault_record_reference varchar [note: 'The unique record identifier from Veeva Vault RIM (Regulatory Information Management) system for this milestone. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the source system of record.']

  Note: 'Tracks key regulatory milestones and target dates for submissions and applications, including IND filing date, End-of-Phase 2 meeting, NDA/BLA filing date, PDUFA date, advisory committee meeting, complete response letter (CRL) receipt, approval date, and post-approval commitment due dates. Captures planned vs. actual dates, milestone type, responsible owner, and completion status. Critical for regulatory project management and timeline tracking.'
}

Table regulatory.approval {
  approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the regulatory approval record. Primary key for the approval data product.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory application (NDA, BLA, MAA, IND) that resulted in this approval. Links approval to its originating submission.']
  atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: Approved products must have ATC classification for WHO reporting, pharmacovigilance signal detection, and therapeutic substitution analysis. Core reference data for regulatory intelligence and market ']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Approvals authorize marketing of specific brands. Critical for commercial launch readiness, market access planning, and sales force enablement tied to approved brand indications.']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Marketing approvals are granted for specific drug product formulations (specific dosage form, strength, route). Regulatory teams track which drug_product received approval for post-approval lifecycle ']
  exclusivity_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.exclusivity_period. Business justification: Regulatory approval is the triggering event for exclusivity periods (NCE, orphan, pediatric). LOE planning, lifecycle management, and generic entry forecasting require linking the approval record to i']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the health authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, NMPA) that granted this approval.']
  indication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Approvals are granted for specific indications — the approved_indication text field is denormalized. Linking to product.indication normalizes this and is fundamental to approval scope management, labe']
  inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: approval.inn_name is a plain-text denormalization of the INN registry. Approvals are substance-specific; a proper FK to inn_registry normalizes this for regulatory intelligence reporting, label genera']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: Approvals for in-licensed or co-developed products are governed by licensing agreements that define commercialization rights and territory. Regulatory milestone payments triggered by approval are trac']
  mah_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: A Marketing Authorization is granted to a specific legal entity (the MAH). Tracking the MAH legal entity on each approval is fundamental for post-approval compliance, transfer of ownership transaction']
  market_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Market authorization country defines the regulatory jurisdiction, labeling requirements, pharmacovigilance obligations, and commercial rights. Essential for global regulatory strategy and lifecycle ma']
  masterdata_ndc_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.ndc_code. Business justification: NDC is the FDA-assigned unique product identifier required for US market authorization, serialization, and supply chain tracking. Essential for commercial distribution and regulatory compliance. Ndc_c']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the drug product (DP) or drug substance (DS) for which this approval was granted.']
  orange_book_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.orange_book_listing. Business justification: Orange Book listings are directly tied to NDA/BLA approvals. FDA requires approved products\' patents and exclusivities to be listed in the Orange Book referencing the approval. Regulatory affairs and ']
  pricing_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.pricing_decision.
