Pricing authorities require the approval reference to initiate price-setting processes. Pharma market acc']
  reimbursement_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.reimbursement_policy. Business justification: Regulatory approval is the direct legal basis for reimbursement policy creation. Reimbursement authorities cite the approval number and conditions when establishing coverage scope. Pharma market acces']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.site. Business justification: Marketing authorizations are granted for specific manufacturing sites listed in the approved dossier. Regulatory teams must track which sites hold manufacturing authorization per approval for supply c']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: A regulatory approval is the outcome of a specific submission (the NDA/BLA/MAA filing that was approved). While approval → application already exists, linking directly to the specific submission that ']
  trademark_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.trademark. Business justification: Regulatory approvals are granted for a specific brand name that must have valid trademark registration. Trademark clearance is a prerequisite for approval, and post-approval trademark maintenance is t']
  approval_date date [note: 'The official date on which the health authority granted the regulatory approval or marketing authorization. This is the principal real-world event date for the approval.']
  approval_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier assigned by the health authority for this approval (e.g., NDA 123456, EU/1/20/1234, PMDA approval number). This is the official regulatory reference number.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory approval. Active indicates the approval is in force; withdrawn/revoked indicates the marketing authorization has been removed; suspended indicates temporary halt pending review.']
  approval_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory approval pathway or designation granted. Full approval indicates standard review; accelerated/conditional/provisional indicate expedited pathways with post-approval commitments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: full|accelerated|conditional|provisional|priority_review|breakthrough|orphan|fast_track — promote to reference product]']
  approved_dosage_form varchar [note: 'The pharmaceutical dosage form approved for marketing (e.g., tablet, capsule, injection, oral solution, transdermal patch). Corresponds to the Finished Dosage Form (FDF) as specified in the approved label.']
  approved_patient_population varchar [note: 'Description of the patient population for whom the drug is approved, including age group, disease stage, prior treatment requirements, and any biomarker or genetic criteria (e.g., \'adults ≥18 years with EGFR-mutated NSCLC\').']
  approved_route_of_administration varchar [note: 'The approved route by which the drug product is administered to patients (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, intramuscular, topical, inhalation). As specified in the approved product label.']
  approved_strength varchar [note: 'The approved dose strength(s) of the Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API) in the drug product (e.g., \'10 mg\', \'100 mg/5 mL\', \'0.5 mg/mL\'). Multiple strengths may be listed as a delimited string.']
  certificate_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number or identifier for the official approval certificate issued by the health authority. Used to locate the physical or electronic certificate in the regulatory document management system (e.g., Veeva Vault RIM).']
  conditions varchar [note: 'Specific conditions, restrictions, or obligations attached to the approval by the health authority. Includes Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) requirements, restricted distribution programs, mandatory monitoring, or other conditions of use.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this approval record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data governance and 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']
  ectd_sequence_number varchar [note: 'The eCTD sequence number of the submission that resulted in this approval. Enables traceability from the approval back to the specific eCTD dossier version reviewed by the health authority.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the approval becomes legally binding and the product may be marketed. May differ from approval_date in cases where a transition period is granted.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the marketing authorization expires and must be renewed. Applicable in jurisdictions requiring periodic renewal (e.g., EMA 5-year renewal cycle). Null for indefinite approvals such as FDA standard approvals.']
  has_rems boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program is required as a condition of this approval. True if REMS is mandated by FDA; False otherwise.']
  label_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the approved product label (prescribing information / Summary of Product Characteristics) at the time of approval. Used to track label revisions and ensure current label compliance.']
  letter_date date [note: 'Date of the official approval letter issued by the health authority. May differ from the approval_date in cases where the letter is issued after the formal approval decision.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional regulatory notes, special circumstances, or contextual information related to this approval that is not captured in structured fields. Used by regulatory affairs teams for internal documentation.']
  orphan_drug_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approved drug product holds orphan drug designation for the approved indication. Orphan designation confers market exclusivity and regulatory incentives for rare disease treatments.']
  pai_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Pre-Approval Inspection (PAI) of manufacturing facilities was required and conducted as part of the approval process. True if PAI was mandated; False otherwise.']
  pediatric_indication boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approval includes a pediatric indication or pediatric labeling. Relevant for tracking compliance with Pediatric Research Equity Act (PREA) and Best Pharmaceuticals for Children Act (BPCA) requirements.']
  post_approval_commitment_count varchar [note: 'Number of post-approval commitments (PACs) or post-marketing requirements (PMRs) imposed by the health authority as conditions of this approval. Drives tracking of outstanding regulatory obligations.']
  regulatory_region varchar [note: 'Regulatory region or jurisdiction grouping for the approval. Used for regional reporting and portfolio management (US=FDA, EU=EMA, JP=PMDA, CN=NMPA, UK=MHRA, CA=Health Canada, AU=TGA, ROW=Rest of World). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: US|EU|JP|CN|UK|CA|AU|ROW — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  review_division varchar [note: 'The specific review division or committee within the health authority responsible for evaluating and granting this approval (e.g., FDA CDER Division of Oncology Products, EMA CHMP, PMDA Review Division).']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory submission that led to this approval. NDA (New Drug Application), BLA (Biologics License Application), MAA (Marketing Authorization Application), ANDA (Abbreviated NDA), sNDA/sBLA/sMAA (supplemental submissions). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NDA|BLA|MAA|ANDA|sNDA|sBLA|sMAA|NDS|SNDS|510k — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this approval record was last modified. Audit trail field for data governance and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault RIM (Regulatory Information Management) system where the approval certificate, approval letter, and associated regulatory documents are stored. Enables direct traceability to the source system of record.']

  Note: 'Records formal regulatory approvals and marketing authorizations granted by health authorities for drug products. Captures approval date, approval type (full, accelerated, conditional, provisional), approved indication, approved dosage form and strength, approved patient population, approval conditions, post-approval commitments, and approval certificate reference. SSOT for product approval status across all markets. Links to application and health authority.'
}

Table regulatory.ha_interaction {
  ha_interaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each health authority interaction record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete formal communication or meeting event with a health authority, with its own lifecycle, participants, and outcomes.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory application (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, or other submission) to which this health authority interaction is associated. Links the interaction to its parent regulatory dossier for chronological record-keeping.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Health authority meetings shape brand development strategy. Commercial incorporates agency feedback and agreed development plans into brand positioning and launch planning.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: ha_interaction.jurisdiction is a plain-text denormalization of country. HA interactions are jurisdiction-specific regulatory meetings; a proper FK enables interaction tracking by country, regulatory i']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the health authority entity (FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, NMPA, etc.) that is the counterparty in this interaction. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE for this transaction header.']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Health authority interactions (Type A/B/C meetings, queries, advisory committees) are conducted for specific medicinal products. ha_interaction.product_name is a denormalized text field; normalizing v']
  safety_signal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.safety_signal. Business justification: Health authority meetings (Type C, scientific advice) frequently discuss emerging safety signals and required regulatory actions. Real business process: signal detection → request meeting → discuss be']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: HA interactions (information requests, Type A/B/C meetings, queries) are frequently tied to a specific regulatory submission. The ha_interaction table already carries ectd_sequence_number and submissi']
  agency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary health authority contact or reviewer assigned to this interaction (e.g., FDA project manager, EMA rapporteur, PMDA reviewer). Enables relationship management and continuity tracking across interactions.']
  agency_reference_number varchar [note: 'The official reference or tracking number assigned by the health authority to this specific interaction or correspondence (e.g., FDA meeting request confirmation number, EMA scientific advice case number, MHRA correspondence reference). Distinct from the company\'s internal interaction_reference_number.']
  agreed_outcomes_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of agreements, agency positions, commitments, and conclusions reached during or resulting from this health authority interaction. For meetings, captures agreed minutes content. For written correspondence, captures the agency\'s decision or position. Critical for regulatory strategy and post-meeting action tracking.']
  briefing_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number or Veeva Vault document ID for the briefing document, background package, or information package submitted to the health authority in preparation for or in response to this interaction. Links to the eTMF/RIM document management system.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level assigned to this interaction record based on the sensitivity of the regulatory strategy and agency communications content. Restricted: contains highly sensitive regulatory strategy or unpublished clinical data. Confidential: standard regulatory correspondence. Internal: general meeting logistics. Public: post-approval public communications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this health authority interaction record was first created in the silver layer data product. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this transaction header. Populated automatically at ingestion time.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates the direction of the communication: inbound (initiated by the health authority, e.g., information request, deficiency letter), outbound (initiated by the company, e.g., meeting request, response letter), or bilateral (mutual, e.g., formal meeting). Essential for tracking response obligations and deadlines.']
  discussion_topics_summary varchar [note: 'A structured summary of the key topics, questions, and agenda items discussed or addressed in this health authority interaction. For meetings, captures the agenda items. For written correspondence, summarizes the subject matter. Business-confidential as it contains regulatory strategy information.']
  ectd_sequence_number varchar [note: 'The eCTD sequence number of the submission associated with this interaction (e.g., the sequence number of the meeting request package or response submission filed in the eCTD dossier). Four-digit zero-padded number per ICH M8 eCTD specification. Null if the interaction is not associated with a formal eCTD submission.']
  follow_up_actions_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interaction generated one or more follow-up action items that require tracking and completion (e.g., additional data requests, protocol amendments, label negotiations, post-approval commitments). Drives workflow routing in the regulatory affairs team.']
  follow_up_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which all follow-up actions arising from this interaction must be completed. Null if follow_up_actions_required is False or if no specific deadline was established. Used for regulatory affairs workload planning and compliance monitoring.']
  health_authority_division varchar [note: 'The specific division, office, or committee within the health authority responsible for this interaction (e.g., FDA CDER Office of Oncology Products, EMA Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP), PMDA Office of New Drug I). Enables granular tracking of agency relationships and division-specific regulatory strategies.']
  interaction_date date [note: 'The principal real-world date on which the interaction occurred — the meeting date for in-person/teleconference interactions, or the date a written communication was received or sent. Serves as the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP (date precision) for this transaction. For written correspondence, this is the document date on the letter or communication.']
  interaction_format varchar [note: 'The modality or format through which the health authority interaction took place. Distinguishes between in-person meetings, teleconferences, videoconferences, hybrid formats, and written-only communications. Critical for meeting management and regulatory strategy documentation.']
  interaction_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known tracking number or correspondence control number assigned to this interaction, either by the health authority (e.g., FDA meeting request number, EMA procedure number) or by the company\'s RIMS/Veeva Vault RIM system. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this transaction. Used for cross-referencing with agency correspondence and eCTD submissions.']
  interaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the health authority interaction record. Tracks the workflow from initial drafting through scheduling, execution, and closure. \'Overdue\' indicates a response deadline has been missed. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this transaction header. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|scheduled|in_progress|completed|cancelled|overdue — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  interaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the health authority interaction by formal category. Covers FDA meeting types (Type A, Type B, Type B EOP, Type C), EMA scientific advice, pre-submission meetings, advisory committee meetings, written correspondence types (information request, complete response letter, deficiency letter, acknowledgment letter, approval letter, agency query, general correspondence), and PAI interactions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: type_a_meeting|type_b_meeting|type_b_eop_meeting|type_c_meeting|scientific_advice|pre_submission_meeting|advisory_committee|information_request|complete_response_letter|deficiency_letter|acknowledgment_letter|approval_letter|agency_query|general_correspondence|pai_interaction — promote to reference product]']
  meeting_category varchar [note: 'For meeting-type interactions, the formal FDA meeting category (Type A: dispute resolution/clinical hold; Type B: pre-IND, end-of-phase, pre-NDA/BLA; Type C: all others) or EMA scientific advice category. Drives PDUFA response timeline obligations. Set to \'not_applicable\' for written correspondence interactions.']
  minutes_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number or Veeva Vault document ID for the official meeting minutes or written record of the interaction outcome. For written correspondence, references the agency letter or response document. Null for interactions where minutes are not yet finalized.']
  pai_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interaction is associated with a Pre-Approval Inspection (PAI) by the health authority, including inspection scheduling, outcome discussions, or corrective action follow-up. Enables filtering of PAI-related regulatory communications.']
  participant_count varchar [note: 'Total number of participants (combined company and agency attendees) in this interaction. Applicable primarily to meeting-type interactions. Used for meeting logistics and engagement analytics.']
  pdufa_clock_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interaction has an impact on the PDUFA review clock (e.g., a complete response letter resets the clock; an information request may pause it). True if the interaction affects the regulatory review timeline clock. Applicable to FDA interactions only; False or null for non-FDA interactions.']
  rems_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this health authority interaction is related to a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program, including REMS design, modification, assessment, or compliance discussions. Enables filtering of REMS-specific regulatory communications.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which the company must respond to an inbound health authority communication (e.g., information request, deficiency letter, complete response letter). Critical for compliance monitoring and PDUFA/EMA clock management. Null for outbound-only interactions.']
  response_submitted_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the company submitted its response to the health authority for inbound communications. Compared against response_due_date to assess timeline compliance. Null if no response has been submitted yet.']
  response_timeline_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the company\'s response to this health authority interaction was submitted within the required regulatory deadline (response_due_date). True if response_submitted_date is on or before response_due_date. Null if no response is required or if the response has not yet been submitted.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The originally scheduled or planned date for the interaction, particularly for meetings. Distinct from interaction_date (actual occurrence date) to enable tracking of rescheduling events and planning accuracy. Applicable primarily to meeting-type interactions.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the source system of record (Veeva Vault RIM correspondence or meeting module document ID) from which this interaction record was ingested. Enables traceability back to the operational system for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the company submitted the meeting request, briefing document, or written correspondence to the health authority. Used to calculate agency review timelines and PDUFA clock start dates. Distinct from interaction_date (when the meeting/response occurred).']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'The therapeutic area or disease indication relevant to this health authority interaction (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Cardiovascular). Enables cross-portfolio analytics and regulatory strategy alignment by therapeutic area.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this health authority interaction record was last modified in the silver layer data product. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for this transaction header. Updated on every write operation to support change data capture and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for ALL formal interactions and communications with health authorities including Type A/B/C meetings (FDA), scientific advice (EMA), pre-submission meetings, advisory committee meetings, and all written correspondence (information requests, complete response letters, deficiency letters, acknowledgment letters, approval letters, agency queries, and general regulatory correspondence). Captures interaction type, format (in-person, teleconference, written), direction (inbound/outbound), dates, participants, discussion topics, agreed outcomes, response deadlines, follow-up actions, correspondence tracking numbers, document references, and response timeline compliance. Consolidates both meeting-based and written communications into a single chronological record per application. SSOT for all health authority communications — no separate correspondence product exists. Sourced from Veeva Vault RIM correspondence and meeting modules.'
}

Table regulatory.dmf {
  dmf_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the Drug Master File (DMF) record in the regulatory data product. Primary key for the dmf entity.']
  drug_master_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.drug_master_file. Business justification: regulatory.dmf and intellectual.drug_master_file represent the same business artifact from regulatory filing and IP/licensing perspectives respectively. CMC regulatory strategy and IP protection coord']
  drug_substance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: DMFs (Drug Master Files) are regulatory filings submitted to health authorities specifically for drug substances (APIs), not finished products. Regulatory CMC review, DMF authorization letters, and cr']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Drug Master Files are filed with specific health authorities (FDA, EMA, etc.). Currently dmf has only an incoming FK from dossier_document, making it nearly siloed. Adding health_authority_id links DM']
  holder_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: DMF holders are often external suppliers or CMOs who are business partners in the ERP/MDM system. Linking DMF to business_partner enables supplier qualification tracking, GMP certificate management, a']
  holder_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: DMF holders are legal entities that own drug master files. Regulatory compliance requires tracking the legal entity for authorization letters, annual updates, and cross-referencing in NDAs/ANDAs. Hold']
  manufacturing_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: dmf.manufacturing_site_name is a plain-text denormalization of the plant. DMFs are site-specific regulatory filings; a proper FK to masterdata.plant enables GMP inspection scheduling, PAI readiness tr']
  manufacturing_site_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: DMF manufacturing site jurisdiction determines applicable GMP standards, inspection authority, and regulatory pathway. Critical for CMC assessment and supply chain risk management. Manufacturing_site_']
  patent_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: DMFs for drug substances and manufacturing processes reference the patent family protecting the CMC subject matter. Regulatory CMC strategy requires knowing which patent family covers the DMF to asses']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.site. Business justification: Drug Master Files are filed for specific manufacturing sites — the DMF covers the manufacturing process at a named site. dmf.manufacturing_site_name is a denormalized text field; replacing with site_i']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Drug Master Files are filed with health authorities via regulatory submissions. The DMF filing event is a submission, and subsequent amendments are also filed as submissions. Linking dmf to its origin']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of amendments submitted to the health authority for this DMF since the original filing date. Reflects the change history and regulatory maintenance activity for the DMF.']
  authorization_letter_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent Letter of Authorization (LoA) was issued by the DMF holder to the referencing applicant. Null if no authorization letter has been issued.']
  authorization_letter_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DMF holder has issued a Letter of Authorization (LoA) to the referencing applicant, granting the health authority permission to review the confidential DMF contents in support of an NDA, BLA, or ANDA submission.']
  cmc_section varchar [note: 'Identifies the specific Chemistry, Manufacturing, and Controls (CMC) section of the eCTD/CTD dossier (Module 3) to which this DMF contributes. Used to map DMF content to the corresponding CTD section in NDA/BLA/MAA submissions.']
  confidentiality_commitment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DMF holder has provided a formal confidentiality commitment to the referencing company, ensuring that proprietary manufacturing information disclosed in the DMF will not be shared beyond the regulatory review context.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DMF record was first created in the regulatory data product. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  deficiency_letter_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority has issued a deficiency letter (also known as a Complete Response Letter or information request) identifying gaps or issues in the DMF that must be addressed before the referencing application can be approved.']
  deficiency_response_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the DMF holder must respond to a deficiency letter issued by the health authority. Null if no deficiency letter has been received. Critical for regulatory timeline management.']
  dmf_number varchar [note: 'Official DMF number assigned by the health authority (e.g., FDA) upon receipt of the filing. Serves as the externally-known unique identifier for the DMF across regulatory submissions and CMC dossiers.']
  dmf_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the DMF with the health authority. Active indicates the DMF is in good standing and available for reference; withdrawn indicates the holder has withdrawn the filing; closed indicates the authority has closed the file.']
  dmf_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the DMF per FDA categorization: Type I (Manufacturing Site, Facilities, Operating Procedures, and Personnel), Type II (Drug Substance, Drug Substance Intermediate, and Material Used in Their Preparation, or Drug Product), Type III (Packaging Material), Type IV (Excipient, Colorant, Flavor, Essence, or Material Used in Their Preparation), Type V (FDA-Accepted Reference Information).']
  ectd_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit eCTD sequence number of the most recent submission lifecycle for this DMF. Used to track the current version of the eCTD dossier in the health authority\'s document management system.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Date on which the DMF was originally submitted and received by the health authority. Establishes the official filing date of record for regulatory and IP purposes.']
  gmp_certificate_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the current GMP certificate associated with the manufacturing site in the DMF. Used to trigger renewal activities and assess supplier qualification status.']
  gmp_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number of the current Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) certificate issued by a competent authority for the manufacturing site referenced in the DMF. Supports supplier qualification and PAI readiness.']
  internal_owner varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the internal regulatory affairs professional responsible for managing this DMF relationship, tracking updates, and coordinating authorization letters with the DMF holder.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment submitted to the health authority for this DMF. Used to assess currency of the DMF content and identify DMFs requiring review.']
  last_annual_update_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent annual update submitted to the health authority to confirm the DMF remains current and accurate. FDA requires annual updates under 21 CFR 314.420(c). Failure to update may result in DMF inactivation.']
  next_annual_update_due_date date [note: 'Calculated due date for the next mandatory annual update submission to the health authority. Used for proactive compliance tracking and regulatory calendar management.']
  pai_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Pre-Approval Inspection (PAI) for the manufacturing site or process covered by this DMF. PAI is conducted by FDA or other health authorities prior to approval of an NDA/BLA referencing this DMF. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_initiated|scheduled|completed|passed|failed|pending_response — promote to reference product]']
  strategic_importance varchar [note: 'Internal classification of the strategic importance of this DMF to the company\'s product portfolio and supply chain. Critical indicates sole-source supplier for a marketed product; high indicates key supplier for pipeline assets; medium/low for non-critical materials.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the subject matter covered by the DMF, such as the specific API, excipient, packaging component, or manufacturing site. Aligns with the \'Subject of DMF\' field in FDA eCTD submissions.']
  submission_format varchar [note: 'Format in which the DMF was submitted to the health authority: eCTD (Electronic Common Technical Document), paper, or NeeS (Non-eCTD Electronic Submission). FDA mandates eCTD for most submissions since 2017.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official title or subject description of the DMF as submitted to the health authority, describing the substance, material, site, or process covered by the filing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this DMF record in the regulatory data product. Supports change tracking, audit trail compliance, and data freshness monitoring.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault RIM (Regulatory Information Management System) corresponding to this DMF record. Enables traceability between the data product and the source system of record for regulatory document management.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date on which the DMF was formally withdrawn by the holder or closed by the health authority. Null for active DMFs. Used to manage the lifecycle of supplier relationships and assess impact on referencing applications.']

  Note: 'Drug Master File (DMF) registry tracking all DMFs filed with health authorities for APIs, excipients, packaging materials, and manufacturing sites. Captures DMF number, DMF type (Type I-V), holder name, subject of DMF, filing date, last annual update date, authorization letters issued, and associated products referencing the DMF. Supports CMC regulatory strategy and third-party supplier qualification. Aligns with 21 CFR 314.420 and EMA DMF guidelines.'
}

Table regulatory.label {
  label_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each approved product label record in the regulatory label management system. Primary key for the label data product.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Product labels are approved as part of the regulatory approval process. Each label version is tied to a specific approval or approval supplement. While label already links to submission, it should als']
  atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: Product labels must reference ATC classification for therapeutic indication consistency, pharmacovigilance coding, and regulatory alignment across markets. Critical for label change impact assessment.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Labels are brand-specific regulatory documents. Commercial teams need label content for promotional material compliance, sales training, and messaging development.']
  brand_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand_plan. Business justification: Label versions are tied to brand plan cycles — new label versions trigger promotional strategy updates and MLR re-review cycles within the brand plan. The \'label-to-brand-plan alignment\' is a named re']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: label.market_country_code is a plain-text denormalization of country. Labels are country-specific regulatory documents; a proper FK to masterdata.country is required for label management workflows, co']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Labels (SPL/SmPC) are associated with specific drug product formulations (specific dosage form, strength, route). Labeling teams need this link for SPL submission management and label lifecycle tracki']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Labels are approved by specific health authorities (FDA, EMA, PMDA, etc.). The label table has health_authority (STRING) which is a denormalized reference. Replacing with health_authority_id FK normal']
  indication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Labels cover specific approved indications; label.indications is a denormalized text field. Linking to product.indication normalizes this and enables indication-level label version tracking, critical ']
  inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: label.inn is a plain-text denormalization of the INN registry. Label content management and regulatory compliance require authoritative INN reference — the INN appears on every approved label and must']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the drug product (finished dosage form) to which this label applies. Links the label to the master product record.']
  prior_version_label_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediately preceding label version that this record supersedes. Enables reconstruction of the complete label amendment history chain for a given product-market-language combination.']
  reimbursement_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.reimbursement_policy. Business justification: The approved label defines the indication scope and restrictions that directly determine reimbursement policy conditions. Reimbursement authorities reference the specific label version when setting co']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Labels reference approved specifications (strength, dosage form, storage conditions). Ensures label claims match approved quality specifications—critical for regulatory compliance and prevents label/s']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory submission (NDA, BLA, MAA, or supplement) under which this label version was approved or submitted.']
  value_dossier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.value_dossier. Business justification: Value dossiers are built around approved label claims, indications, and clinical data. The label version is the regulatory anchor for value dossier content. Pharma HEOR teams track which label version']
  affected_sections varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of label sections modified in this version (e.g., \'Warnings and Precautions, Adverse Reactions, Dosage and Administration\'). Enables targeted review of changes and supports regulatory correspondence tracking.']
  black_box_warning boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this label version contains an FDA Boxed Warning (Black Box Warning) — the most serious warning required by the FDA for drugs with significant risk of serious or life-threatening adverse effects. True = boxed warning present; False = no boxed warning.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason driving the label change for this version. Safety Update: new safety information (e.g., new adverse event, SUSAR); New Indication: addition of a new approved therapeutic indication; Dosage Modification: change to dosing regimen; Contraindication Update: new or revised contraindications; Drug Interaction Update: new drug-drug interaction data; Pediatric/Geriatric/Pregnancy Labeling: population-specific updates; Administrative Update: non-clinical administrative corrections. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: safety_update|new_indication|dosage_modification|contraindication_update|drug_interaction_update|pediatric_labeling|geriatric_labeling|pregnancy_labeling|administrative_update — promote to reference product]']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of change that resulted in this label version. Agency-initiated: required by health authority; Company-initiated: proposed by the marketing authorization holder; Labeling Supplement: formal supplement submission; Prior Approval Supplement (PAS): requires FDA approval before implementation; Changes Being Effected (CBE): can be implemented before FDA approval; Annual Report: minor changes reported annually. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: agency_initiated|company_initiated|labeling_supplement|prior_approval_supplement|cbee|annual_report — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this label record was first created in the regulatory data system. Audit trail field capturing the initial record creation event per 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'Pharmaceutical dosage form of the drug product as stated on this label (e.g., tablet, capsule, injection, solution, cream). Reflects the finished dosage form (FDF) classification per the approved label.']
  ectd_sequence_number varchar [note: 'The eCTD sequence number of the regulatory submission dossier in which this label version was submitted to the health authority. Four-digit zero-padded sequence number per eCTD specification (e.g., 0001, 0025). Enables direct traceability to the eCTD submission package.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this label version is commercially effective and must be used on marketed product packaging. May differ from approval date due to implementation timelines or transition periods.']
  implementation_deadline date [note: 'Regulatory or company-mandated deadline by which the new label version must be implemented on marketed product packaging. Critical for compliance tracking, especially for safety-driven label changes with mandatory implementation timelines.']
  implementation_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the label implementation across markets and packaging lines. Pending: implementation not yet started; In Progress: packaging/artwork updates underway; Implemented: new label in use on marketed product; Overdue: implementation deadline passed without completion; Not Applicable: label change does not require physical implementation.']
  is_current_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this label record represents the currently approved and active version for the given product, market, language, and label type combination. True = current approved version; False = historical/superseded version. Enables efficient querying of the current label without date-range filtering.']
  label_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the label document, typically assigned by the regulatory information management system (RIMS) or Veeva Vault RIM (e.g., LBL-2024-00123). Used for cross-system traceability.']
  label_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the label document by its regulatory format and intended audience. Prescribing Information (PI) and Package Insert (PI) are US formats; Summary of Product Characteristics (SmPC) is the EU/EMA format; Patient Information Leaflet (PIL) is the patient-facing EU document; Medication Guide and Instructions for Use (IFU) are FDA-specific formats.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'IETF BCP 47 / ISO 639 language code for the language of this label version (e.g., en, fr, de, ja, zh-CN). Supports multi-language label management across global markets.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the label version within its regulatory lifecycle. Draft: in preparation; Under Review: submitted to health authority or under internal review; Approved: currently approved and in force; Superseded: replaced by a newer version; Withdrawn: removed from market; Archived: historical record retained for compliance.']
  marketing_authorization_number varchar [note: 'The marketing authorization (MA) or product license number assigned by the health authority for the approved drug product in the relevant market (e.g., NDA 123456 for FDA, EU/1/24/1234 for EMA centralized procedure). Links the label to the formal marketing authorization.']
  ndc_code varchar [note: 'FDA-assigned National Drug Code (NDC) for the drug product associated with this label. The NDC is a unique 10-digit, 3-segment number identifying the labeler, product, and trade package size. Applicable for US market labels only.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Brand/trade name of the drug product as it appears on this label version. Captured directly on the label record to preserve the exact name at the time of approval, which may differ from the current master product name.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'The regulatory or scientific basis justifying this label version change (e.g., \'Post-market safety study PMSS-2024-001\', \'PSUR cycle 5 findings\', \'FDA safety communication dated 2024-03-15\', \'Pediatric study per PREA\'). Provides the evidentiary foundation for the label amendment.']
  rems_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program is required for this drug product as referenced in this label version. True = REMS required; False = no REMS requirement.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'Approved route of administration as stated on this label (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, topical, inhalation). Reflects the regulatory-approved administration pathway for the drug product.']
  spl_document_reference varchar [note: 'FDA-assigned UUID for the Structured Product Labeling (SPL) document submitted to the FDA DailyMed system. Unique identifier for the SPL XML document in the FDA labeling repository. Applicable for US market labels only.']
  strength varchar [note: 'Strength of the active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) as stated on this label (e.g., 10 mg, 500 mg/5 mL, 100 mg/mL). Captures the exact strength designation from the approved label text.']
  superseded_date date [note: 'Date on which this label version was superseded by a newer approved version. Null for the currently active label. Enables full historical label version tracking and compliance audit trails.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Primary therapeutic area or disease area for which the drug product is approved as reflected in this label (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Cardiovascular, Neurology). Supports portfolio-level label analytics and regulatory reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this label record in the regulatory data system. Supports change detection, incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver layer, and audit trail compliance per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault PromoMats or RIM for the full label content document. Enables direct retrieval of the complete label text, formatted PDF, and XML source from the document management system.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this label document (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0). Incremented with each approved amendment. Enables full version history tracking across the label lifecycle.']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for approved product labeling (prescribing information, package insert, patient information leaflet, SmPC) and complete change history across all markets and languages. Tracks current label version, effective date, approval date, market/country, language, label type, content sections, and lifecycle status. Maintains full amendment history including change reason (safety update, new indication, dosage modification, black box warning), change type (agency-initiated, company-initiated), regulatory basis, affected sections, submission reference, and implementation status across markets. SSOT for both current approved labeling and all historical label changes — no separate label change product exists. Integrates with Veeva Vault PromoMats and RIM.'
}

Table regulatory.label_change {
  label_change_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each label change record in the regulatory labeling lifecycle management system.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Label changes are driven by or result in regulatory approvals (e.g., a safety-driven label update requires FDA approval, a variation approval mandates a label change). Linking label_change to approval']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Label changes impact promotional claims and sales messaging. Commercial must update materials and train field force when brand labels change to maintain compliance.']

Business justification: Label changes trigger brand plan updates including promotional material withdrawal, sales force communication, and revised channel strategy. The \'label change impact on brand plan\' is a named regulato']

  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: label_change.market_country_code is a plain-text denormalization of country. Label changes are country-specific regulatory actions; a proper FK enables country-level label change reporting, implementa']
  coverage_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.coverage_decision. Business justification: Safety-driven label changes (black box warnings, contraindications, REMS additions) trigger mandatory payer coverage policy reviews and updates to prior authorization criteria, directly impacting form']

Regulatory labeling teams track label changes at the drug product level for CMC-driven label updates and product-specific labeling lifecycle ma']

The label_change table has health_authority (STRING) which is a denormalized reference. Replacing with health_authority_id FK ']
  indication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Label changes are frequently indication-specific (new indication added, black box warning for specific indication, indication restriction). Linking to product.indication enables tracking which indicat']
  inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: label_change.inn is a plain-text denormalization of the INN registry. Label changes driven by safety signals or new clinical data must reference the authoritative INN for pharmacovigilance reporting a']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the drug product (finished dosage form) for which the label change applies.']
  mlr_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.mlr_review. Business justification: Label changes directly trigger MLR re-review of all affected promotional materials. The \'label-change-driven MLR review\' is a named regulatory-commercial compliance process — pharma companies must upd']
  pricing_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.pricing_decision. Business justification: Label changes affecting indication scope or safety profile trigger price renegotiations with pricing authorities. Pricing decisions reference the label change event as the regulatory basis for price r']
  primary_label_document_label_id varchar [note: 'Document management system identifier (e.g., Veeva Vault document ID) for the approved label document associated with this change, enabling direct retrieval of the label text from the document management system.']

Business justification: Safety-driven label changes often originate from recall investigations, post-market surveillance, or adverse event patterns. Linking label changes to recalls enables root cause analysis and regulatory']
  reimbursement_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.reimbursement_policy. Business justification: Label changes (new warnings, new contraindications, new indications) trigger mandatory reimbursement policy reviews. Payers must update coverage policies when the label changes. Pharma market access t']
  safety_signal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pharmacovigilance safety case or signal that initiated a safety-driven label update, such as a new warning, contraindication, or black box warning addition.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory submission (e.g., NDA supplement, BLA supplement, MAA variation) that triggered or documents this label change.']
  variation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.variation. Business justification: Post-approval variations (Type IA, IB, II, supplements) frequently mandate label changes. The variation table has an is_labeling_change flag confirming this relationship. Adding variation_id to label_']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the health authority formally approved the label change. Null for company-initiated CBE changes that do not require prior approval.']
  atc_code varchar [note: 'WHO Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) classification code for the drug product, used for standardized cross-market reporting and regulatory analytics.']
  change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the specific modifications made to the product label, including the nature of the change, the scientific or safety rationale, and the specific text added, removed, or modified.']
  change_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the label change, used for cross-referencing with regulatory submissions, health authority correspondence, and internal change control systems. Format: LC-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  change_rationale varchar [note: 'Scientific, clinical, or regulatory rationale supporting the label change, including references to clinical study data, pharmacovigilance signals, or health authority directives that justify the modification.']
  change_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the label change record, tracking progression from initial drafting through regulatory approval to market implementation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|submitted|approved|implemented|superseded|withdrawn — promote to reference product]']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nature of the label change, indicating the primary reason or category of modification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: safety_update|new_indication|dosage_modification|black_box_warning|contraindication|drug_interaction|precaution|patient_information|administrative — promote to reference product]']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, internal commentary, or contextual information related to the label change that does not fit into structured fields, such as negotiation history with health authorities or implementation challenges.']
  correspondence_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the health authority correspondence (e.g., FDA Complete Response Letter, EMA Day 120 List of Questions) that directed or acknowledged this label change.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this label change record was first created in the system, providing the audit trail entry point for the change lifecycle.']
  ectd_sequence_number varchar [note: 'The eCTD sequence number associated with the regulatory submission containing this label change, enabling traceability to the electronic dossier submitted to the health authority.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the updated label becomes legally effective and must be used in commercial distribution. May differ from approval date based on implementation timelines.']
  implementation_deadline date [note: 'The regulatory or company-mandated deadline by which the updated label must be physically implemented on all marketed product packaging and distributed to the supply chain.']
  implementation_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the physical label implementation across manufacturing and supply chain, tracking whether the updated label has been applied to product packaging and distributed.']
  initiator_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the label change was initiated by the company (e.g., based on new clinical data or pharmacovigilance signal) or mandated by a health authority (e.g., FDA, EMA directive or post-approval commitment).']
  is_black_box_warning boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this label change includes the addition, modification, or removal of a black box warning (boxed warning), which is the FDA\'s most serious warning for drug safety risks.']
  is_pv_driven boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this label change was driven by a pharmacovigilance signal, safety review, or post-market surveillance finding, as opposed to a new clinical indication or administrative update.']
  is_rems_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this label change is associated with a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program requirement, such as a new REMS, REMS modification, or REMS-driven labeling update.']
  prior_label_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the product label that was in effect immediately before this change, enabling traceability of label evolution and comparison of changes.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'The regulatory pathway, regulation, or guidance document that provides the legal or scientific basis for the label change (e.g., 21 CFR 314.70(c), EMA Article 61(3), ICH E2D post-marketing safety data management).']
  review_completed_date date [note: 'The date on which the internal cross-functional review of the label change was completed, including sign-off from Regulatory Affairs, Medical Affairs, Pharmacovigilance, and Legal.']
  sections_modified varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of label sections that were modified in this change (e.g., \'Warnings and Precautions, Dosage and Administration, Adverse Reactions\'). Aligned with the Physician Labeling Rule (PLR) section structure for US labels and SmPC sections for EU labels.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'The therapeutic area or disease category associated with the product label change (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Cardiovascular), used for portfolio-level reporting and regulatory analytics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this label change record, supporting audit trail requirements and change history tracking under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  urgency_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the urgency with which the label change must be processed and implemented, based on the severity of the safety signal or regulatory requirement. Urgent and immediate changes may require expedited health authority notification.']

  Note: 'Tracks all label changes and labeling amendments including the reason for change, change type (safety update, new indication, dosage modification, black box warning addition), regulatory basis, submission reference, effective date, and implementation status across markets. Captures the specific sections modified, change description, and whether the change was agency-initiated or company-initiated. Critical for pharmacovigilance-driven label updates and post-approval lifecycle management.'
}

Table regulatory.rems {
  rems_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the REMS program master record in the Pharmaceuticals data lakehouse.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.application. Business justification: REMS programs are mandated for specific regulatory applications (NDA/BLA). Currently rems is siloed with no FK relationships. Adding application_id links REMS to the regulatory application that requir']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: REMS programs are mandated at the time of approval and are tied to the specific approval record. This FK links REMS to the approval that imposed the requirement. The approval_date and initial_approval']
  atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: REMS programs are therapeutic class-specific risk mitigation strategies. ATC classification enables shared REMS analysis, class-wide safety signal detection, and FDA assessment reporting. Atc_code is ']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: REMS programs restrict distribution/prescribing for specific brands. Commercial operations (sampling, speaker programs, detailing) must comply with brand-specific REMS requirements.']
  brand_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand_plan. Business justification: REMS programs constrain brand plan strategy — certified pharmacy requirements, patient enrollment mandates, and communication plans are incorporated into brand planning. The \'REMS-constrained brand pl']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: FDA REMS programs are tied to specific drug product formulations (specific dosage form, route). REMS program administrators need this link to manage ETASU elements and assessment schedules at the drug']
  indication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: REMS programs are often indication-specific (e.g., REMS required for oncology indication but not other indications of the same product). Linking to product.indication enables indication-level REMS req']
  inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: rems.inn_name is a plain-text denormalization of the INN registry. REMS programs are substance-specific risk management programs; linking to inn_registry supports pharmacovigilance reporting, REMS ass']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: REMS programs require ongoing operational costs (program administration, assessments, reporting, modifications) tracked via internal orders for budget management and cost allocation to the approved pr']
  label_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.label. Business justification: REMS programs are tied to specific approved label versions — the label contains the Medication Guide and ETASU elements that are part of the REMS. The label table has a rems_required boolean confirmin']
  patient_access_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.patient_access_program. Business justification: Patient access programs must integrate REMS enrollment requirements; hub services coordinate REMS patient registration, prescriber certification verification, and pharmacy network compliance alongside']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: REMS programs are submitted to FDA as part of NDA/BLA submissions and subsequent REMS modifications are filed as supplements. Linking rems to the originating or most recent submission enables full reg']
  assessment_schedule varchar [note: 'FDA-mandated schedule for submitting REMS assessments (e.g., \'18 months, 3 years, and 7 years after approval\', or \'annually\'). Defines the cadence of program effectiveness reporting obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this REMS program master record was first created in the data lakehouse. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'Pharmaceutical dosage form of the drug product covered by the REMS (e.g., oral capsule, injectable solution, transdermal patch). Relevant to dispensing and administration risk controls.']
  ecm_submission_reference varchar [note: 'eCTD sequence number or submission reference in Veeva Vault RIM corresponding to the most recent REMS submission or modification filed with FDA. Enables traceability to the regulatory dossier.']
  etasu_elements varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific ETASU elements required under this REMS program (e.g., certified prescriber requirement, certified pharmacy requirement, patient enrollment registry, laboratory monitoring, patient agreement form). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: certified_prescriber|certified_pharmacy|patient_registry|lab_monitoring|patient_agreement|safe_use_condition — promote to reference product]']
  fda_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the FDA project manager or reviewer assigned to this REMS program. Used for official regulatory correspondence and health authority interactions.']
  goal_statement varchar [note: 'Official FDA-approved goal statement for the REMS program describing the specific safety outcome the program is designed to achieve (e.g., \'Mitigate the risk of fetal exposure to isotretinoin\').']
  has_communication_plan boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the REMS program includes a Communication Plan element targeting healthcare professionals (HCPs) about serious risks associated with the drug.']
  has_etasu boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the REMS program includes Elements to Assure Safe Use (ETASU), which are the most restrictive REMS requirements such as certified prescriber/pharmacy networks, patient registries, or laboratory testing requirements.']
  has_medication_guide boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the REMS program includes a Medication Guide as a required REMS element to be dispensed to patients at each prescription fill.']
  last_assessment_outcome varchar [note: 'FDA\'s determination following review of the most recently submitted REMS assessment report. Indicates whether the program is meeting its goals or requires modification.']
  last_assessment_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent REMS assessment report was submitted to FDA via eCTD. Used to verify compliance with the assessment schedule.']
  last_modification_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent FDA-approved modification to the REMS program. Used to track program currency and trigger downstream label and communication updates.']
  last_modification_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the most recent REMS modification as major (substantive change to ETASU or goals), minor (non-substantive change), or administrative (formatting/contact updates).']
  modification_count varchar [note: 'Total number of FDA-approved modifications made to the REMS program since initial approval. Tracks program evolution and regulatory change history.']
  next_assessment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next REMS assessment report must be submitted to FDA per the approved assessment schedule. Critical for regulatory compliance tracking.']
  program_administrator varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or vendor contracted to administer the REMS program operations (e.g., patient enrollment, prescriber certification, pharmacy certification, call center). May be an internal team or a third-party REMS administrator.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Official FDA-approved name of the REMS program as registered in Veeva Vault RIM and the FDA REMS database (e.g., \'iPLEDGE REMS\', \'TIRF REMS ACCESS\').']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the REMS program. \'active\' indicates the program is fully operational; \'under_review\' indicates FDA is evaluating a modification; \'pending_approval\' indicates initial REMS awaiting FDA approval.']
  program_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the current approved REMS program document, incremented with each FDA-approved modification (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Aligns with eCTD submission versioning in Veeva Vault RIM.']
  rems_number varchar [note: 'FDA-assigned unique program identifier for the REMS, used for official correspondence and regulatory submissions. Serves as the externally-known business identifier.']
  requires_certified_pharmacy boolean [note: 'Indicates whether pharmacies must be certified or enrolled in the REMS program before they are authorized to dispense the drug. Restricts dispensing to a controlled pharmacy network.']
  requires_certified_prescriber boolean [note: 'Indicates whether prescribers must be certified or enrolled in the REMS program before they are authorized to prescribe the drug. A key ETASU element for high-risk products.']
  requires_lab_monitoring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the REMS program mandates specific laboratory tests (e.g., pregnancy tests, liver function tests, blood counts) as a condition of prescribing or dispensing the drug.']
  requires_patient_enrollment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether patients must be enrolled in the REMS program (e.g., patient registry or patient agreement) before receiving the drug. Enables tracking of patient exposure and outcomes.']
  risk_description varchar [note: 'Summary description of the serious risk(s) that necessitate the REMS program (e.g., teratogenicity, QT prolongation, severe hepatotoxicity, opioid addiction and overdose). Derived from the FDA-approved REMS document.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'Route by which the drug product is administered to patients (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, transdermal). Informs risk communication and ETASU design.']
  shared_rems_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a shared REMS program administered jointly by multiple sponsors (e.g., brand and generic manufacturers) under a single FDA-approved REMS. Relevant for interoperability with generic entrants.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Therapeutic area classification of the drug product subject to the REMS (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Neurology, Rare Diseases, Pain Management). Used for portfolio-level REMS analytics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this REMS program master record was most recently updated in the data lakehouse. Supports audit trail and change tracking requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault RIM for the current approved REMS program document. Provides direct traceability to the source regulatory document management system.']

  Note: 'Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program master record for products requiring FDA-mandated risk management programs. Captures REMS program name, associated NDA/BLA, REMS elements (medication guide, communication plan, ETASU), program approval date, assessment schedule, enrollment requirements, certified pharmacy/prescriber requirements, and program status. Tracks REMS modifications and assessment submission history. Specific to FDA-regulated products under 21 CFR 314.520.'
}

Used for portfolio analytics and resource planning.']
  commitment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the post-approval commitment as reported to the health authority. Aligns with FDA PMR/PMC status categories: Pending (not yet started), Ongoing (in progress), Submitted (deliverable submitted to agency), Fulfilled (agency confirmed completion), Released (agency released from requirement), Delayed, or Terminated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Pending|Ongoing|Submitted|Fulfilled|Released|Delayed|Terminated — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  commitment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the commitment type: PMR (Post-Marketing Requirement — legally required under FDAAA), PMC (Post-Marketing Commitment — voluntarily agreed), Phase IV Study, Label Update, Stability Study, or Risk Management measure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PMR|PMC|Phase IV Study|Label Update|Stability Study|Risk Management|REMS|Pediatric Study|Pharmacovigilance Study|Registry Study — promote to reference product]']
  completed_milestone_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual action-level milestones or tasks that have been completed for this commitment. Used alongside milestone_count to calculate progress percentage for executive dashboards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this post-approval commitment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  delay_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for any delay in meeting the original or revised due date for this commitment. Required for agency reporting when a commitment is classified as Delayed. Supports regulatory correspondence and agency negotiations.']
  deliverable_type varchar [note: 'The type of document or output required to fulfill this commitment: Protocol (study protocol submission), Interim Report, Final Study Report, Label Update, Annual Report, or Safety Update (e.g., PSUR/PBRER). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Protocol|Interim Report|Final Study Report|Label Update|Annual Report|Safety Update|Risk Management Plan|Registry Report|Pharmacovigilance Plan — promote to reference product]']
  ectd_sequence_number varchar [note: 'The eCTD sequence number of the submission in which the deliverable for this commitment was filed with the health authority. Enables traceability to the specific eCTD submission in the regulatory dossier.']
  fulfillment_date date [note: 'The date on which the health authority confirmed that this post-approval commitment has been fulfilled or released. Null if not yet fulfilled.']
  is_pediatric_commitment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commitment was issued under pediatric legislation (PREA in the US, PDCO/PIP in the EU). True if the commitment is a pediatric study or measure required under PREA or EU Pediatric Regulation.']
  is_rems_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this post-approval commitment is associated with a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program. True if the commitment involves REMS elements such as medication guides, communication plans, or ETASU.']
  issuing_agency varchar [note: 'The regulatory health authority that issued or mandated this post-approval commitment (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, NMPA). Determines the applicable regulatory framework and reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|PMDA|MHRA|NMPA|WHO|Health Canada|TGA — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_reported_status_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent status update for this commitment was reported to the health authority (e.g., in an annual report or ad hoc correspondence). Used to track reporting currency and compliance.']
  milestone_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual action-level milestones or tasks associated with this commitment (e.g., protocol submission, interim report, final report). Provides a quick indicator of commitment complexity for planning purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, internal commentary, or agency correspondence notes relevant to this post-approval commitment. Not intended for structured data; used for operational tracking and audit trail narrative.']
  original_due_date date [note: 'The original due date for fulfillment of this post-approval commitment as specified in the approval letter or agency correspondence. Preserved for compliance tracking and variance analysis against revised due dates.']
  post_approval_commitment_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the post-approval commitment as stated in the approval letter, labeling, or agency correspondence. Captures the scope, objective, and specific deliverable required.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Internal priority classification assigned to this commitment based on regulatory risk, patient safety implications, and business impact. Drives resource allocation and escalation decisions.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'The specific statutory or regulatory authority under which this commitment was imposed (e.g., FDAAA Section 505(o)(3) for PMR, PREA for pediatric studies, EMA Article 21a for conditional approval measures). Provides legal traceability.']
  responsible_function varchar [note: 'The internal business function or department accountable for executing and delivering this post-approval commitment (e.g., Clinical Development, Regulatory Affairs, Pharmacovigilance, CMC, Medical Affairs).']
  revised_due_date date [note: 'The most current agreed-upon due date for fulfillment, reflecting any agency-approved extensions or renegotiations. Null if no revision has been made to the original due date.']
  study_number varchar [note: 'Internal or sponsor-assigned study number for the clinical or non-clinical study associated with this commitment (e.g., Phase IV study protocol number). Null for non-study commitments such as label updates.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the final deliverable (e.g., final study report, protocol, interim report) was submitted to the health authority to fulfill this commitment. Null if not yet submitted.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'The therapeutic area or disease indication to which this post-approval commitment relates (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Cardiovascular). Used for portfolio-level reporting and resource allocation.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short, descriptive title of the post-approval commitment as referenced in the approval letter or agency correspondence (e.g., \'Pediatric Safety Study in Patients 2-11 Years\').']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this post-approval commitment record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and change tracking in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'The document identifier in Veeva Vault RIM or eTMF corresponding to the submission package, approval letter, or supporting documentation for this post-approval commitment. Enables direct traceability to the source system of record.']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for all post-approval commitments, post-marketing requirements (PMR/PMC), and their granular fulfillment actions. Tracks commitment type (PMR, PMC, Phase IV study, label update, stability study), description, due dates, agency-assigned tracking number, and individual action-level tasks (protocol submission, interim reports, final study reports) with responsible functions, deliverable types, due dates, completion dates, and status. Monitors compliance with FDA PMR/PMC requirements under FDAAA and EMA post-authorization measures. Provides both executive-level commitment status and operational task-level progress tracking in a single entity. SSOT for all regulatory obligation tracking — no separate action-level product exists.'
}

While strategy is created early (potentially before application submission), it is fundamentally tied to the ap']
