  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Regulatory strategies are developed for specific drug product formulations (specific dosage form, strength, route) covering CMC strategy, filing strategy, and lifecycle planning. Regulatory strategy t']
  exclusivity_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.exclusivity_period. Business justification: Regulatory strategy explicitly incorporates exclusivity planning (NCE, orphan, pediatric designations) as core strategic levers defining the commercial protection window. Strategy-level exclusivity pl']
  indication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Regulatory strategies are indication-specific; strategy.target_indication is a denormalized text field. Normalizing via FK to product.indication enables indication-level regulatory strategy portfolio ']
  inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: strategy.inn_name is a plain-text denormalization of the INN registry. Regulatory strategy is substance-specific; linking to inn_registry enables cross-product strategy comparison, INN-based regulator']
  lead_health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Regulatory strategy identifies the lead health authority for the global filing sequence (e.g., FDA for US-first strategy, EMA for EU-first). The lead_agency STRING should be replaced with a FK to heal']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Regulatory strategy is developed for a specific legal entity as the intended applicant/MAH. Global regulatory strategy planning requires knowing which legal entity will file in each market — essential']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: Regulatory strategy for partnered or in-licensed assets must align with licensing agreement terms governing field of use, territory, and regulatory milestone obligations. Strategy documents for co-dev']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the drug product (Drug Product / Drug Substance) for which the regulatory strategy is being developed. Aligns strategy to the commercial product entity.']
  patent_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: Regulatory strategy is built around the patent estate. Filing sequence, target submission dates, and pathway selection are directly informed by patent family expiry dates and geographic coverage. Regu']
  accelerated_approval_pathway boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the regulatory strategy includes pursuit of FDA Accelerated Approval or EMA Conditional Marketing Authorization based on surrogate or intermediate clinical endpoints.']
  agency_engagement_plan varchar [note: 'Summary of planned formal and informal interactions with health authorities (e.g., Pre-IND meetings, Type B meetings with FDA, Scientific Advice with EMA, PMDA consultations). Defines the cadence and objectives of regulatory agency engagement.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the regulatory strategy was formally approved in Veeva Vault RIM. Captures the precise approval event for audit trail and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the senior Regulatory Affairs executive or governance committee who formally approved this regulatory strategy. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature compliance.']
  breakthrough_therapy_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the company is pursuing or has received FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation (BTD) for this program. BTD enables intensive FDA guidance and rolling review to expedite development.']
  competitive_landscape_notes varchar [note: 'Summary of the competitive regulatory landscape including approved products in the same indication, precedent-setting approvals, and competitor pipeline programs that may influence the regulatory strategy or review standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the regulatory strategy record was first created in the source system (Veeva Vault RIM). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  development_phase varchar [note: 'Current clinical development phase of the drug program at the time this regulatory strategy is authored. Determines the regulatory interactions and submission milestones applicable. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: preclinical|phase_1|phase_2|phase_3|phase_3b|pre_submission|post_approval — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ectd_module_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the eCTD module and section where the regulatory strategy documentation is filed within the submission dossier (e.g., Module 1.2 - Product Overview). Aligns strategy to the ICH eCTD structure.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this version of the regulatory strategy is considered active and binding for the development program. Marks the start of the strategy\'s applicability period.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this version of the regulatory strategy is superseded or retired. Null for currently active strategies. Supports version lifecycle management in Veeva Vault RIM.']
  fast_track_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FDA Fast Track Designation is being pursued or has been granted for this program. Fast Track facilitates development and expedites review of drugs treating serious conditions with unmet medical need.']
  global_filing_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordered sequence of regulatory agency submissions planned globally, describing the order in which dossiers will be filed across jurisdictions (e.g., FDA first, then EMA, then PMDA). Supports launch sequencing and resource planning.']
  key_regulatory_risks varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the principal regulatory risks identified for this program, including data gaps, safety signals, manufacturing concerns, or precedent-setting issues that could delay or prevent approval.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the regulatory strategy record was most recently updated in the source system. Tracks document revision history for compliance and version control purposes.']
  orphan_drug_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Orphan Drug Designation (ODD) is being pursued or has been granted by FDA or EMA. ODD provides market exclusivity, fee waivers, and tax credits for drugs targeting rare diseases.']
  prime_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EMA PRIority MEdicines (PRIME) designation is being pursued or has been granted. PRIME provides enhanced EMA support for medicines that offer a major therapeutic advantage over existing treatments.']
  priority_markets varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes representing the priority markets targeted for initial regulatory submissions (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU, JPN). Drives global filing sequence planning.']
  regulatory_pathway_type varchar [note: 'The planned regulatory approval pathway for the drug program (e.g., NDA 505(b)(1), NDA 505(b)(2), BLA, MAA, Abbreviated NDA, 510(k)). Determines the submission type and data package requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NDA_505b1|NDA_505b2|ANDA|BLA|MAA|NDS|JNDA|sNDA|sBLA|sMAA — promote to reference product]']
  rems_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program is anticipated or required by FDA as part of the approval conditions for this drug. REMS programs manage known or potential serious risks.']
  sakigake_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether PMDA Sakigake designation is being pursued for this program in Japan. Sakigake provides priority review and early consultation for innovative drugs addressing serious diseases.']
  strategic_rationale varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the strategic reasoning behind the chosen regulatory pathway, filing sequence, and expedited program designations. Documents the decision-making basis for regulatory governance review.']
  strategy_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for the regulatory strategy document as assigned in Veeva Vault RIM. Used for cross-functional referencing and regulatory correspondence tracking.']
  strategy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory strategy document within Veeva Vault RIM. Drives workflow routing and version control. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|under_review|approved|active|superseded|retired — promote to reference product if additional statuses are needed]']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Primary submission type targeted by this regulatory strategy (e.g., IND, NDA, BLA, MAA). Defines the regulatory dossier format and applicable agency requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IND|NDA|BLA|MAA|sNDA|sBLA|sMAA|ANDA|DMF|other — promote to reference product]']
  target_approval_date date [note: 'Anticipated date of regulatory approval from the lead agency based on standard or priority review timelines. Drives commercial launch planning and supply chain readiness.']
  target_submission_date date [note: 'Planned date for the primary regulatory submission (e.g., NDA, BLA, MAA) to the lead agency. Used for milestone tracking and commercial launch timeline planning.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Broad therapeutic area classification for the drug program (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Cardiovascular). Used for portfolio-level regulatory planning and resource allocation.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the regulatory strategy document, typically including the compound name, indication, and target market. Human-readable label used in Veeva Vault RIM and regulatory governance meetings.']
  tpp_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the Target Product Profile (TPP) describing the desired label claims, patient population, dosing regimen, safety profile, and differentiation from existing therapies. Anchors the regulatory strategy to commercial objectives.']
  vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'The unique document identifier assigned by Veeva Vault RIM to the regulatory strategy document. Enables direct cross-reference and deep-linking to the source document in the regulatory information management system.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the regulatory strategy document following major.minor versioning convention. Tracks document evolution through review and approval cycles in Veeva Vault RIM.']

  Note: 'Documents the regulatory strategy for each drug development program, capturing the planned regulatory pathway, target product profile (TPP), key regulatory risks, agency engagement plan, global filing sequence, priority markets, expedited program designations pursued (Breakthrough, Fast Track, PRIME, Sakigake), competitive landscape considerations, and strategic rationale. Serves as the authoritative regulatory plan linking development programs to submission plans and commercial launch timelines. Managed within Veeva Vault RIM.'
}

The designation is tied to the application throughout development. The agency_reference']

Business justification: Regulatory designations (orphan, breakthrough, fast track) are granted based on therapeutic area and unmet need. ATC classification enables competitive intelligence and portfolio strategy analysis. At']

Business justification: Regulatory designations (orphan, breakthrough, fast track) directly shape brand plan strategy — affecting exclusivity periods, pricing strategy, and market access planning. Business justification: Special regulatory designations (Breakthrough Therapy, Fast Track, Orphan Drug, PRIME) are integral components of the regulatory strategy. The strategy table has breakthrough_therapy_designation, fast']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory submission (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA) under which this designation was requested or linked.']
  accelerated_approval_pathway boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this designation is associated with or enables the FDA Accelerated Approval pathway based on a surrogate or intermediate clinical endpoint.']
  agency_meeting_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent or scheduled health authority meeting (e.g., Type B meeting with FDA, PRIME scientific advice meeting with EMA) related to this designation.']
  benefits varchar [note: 'Free-text summary of the regulatory and commercial benefits conferred by this designation (e.g., rolling review, priority review voucher, tax credits, market exclusivity, fee waivers, intensive FDA guidance).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, context, or remarks related to the designation, including agency feedback, internal decisions, or strategic considerations not captured in structured fields.']
  confirmatory_trial_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a post-approval confirmatory clinical trial is required as a condition of the designation or associated approval pathway (e.g., Accelerated Approval post-market requirement).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory designation record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  designation_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number assigned by the health authority upon granting the designation (e.g., FDA Orphan Drug Designation number, Breakthrough Therapy designation reference). Serves as the externally-known business identifier for this designation.']
  designation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory designation, tracking progression from initial request through grant, potential withdrawal, revocation, or expiry.']
  designation_type varchar [note: 'Category of the special regulatory designation or expedited program. Covers FDA and international agency programs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Breakthrough Therapy|Fast Track|Accelerated Approval|Priority Review|Orphan Drug Designation|Rare Pediatric Disease|Qualified Infectious Disease Product|EMA PRIME|PMDA Sakigake|Conditional Marketing Authorization|Exceptional Circumstances — promote to reference product]']
  development_phase varchar [note: 'Clinical development phase of the drug at the time the designation was requested, reflecting the stage of the program (e.g., Phase 1, Phase 2, Phase 3).']
  ectd_module_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the eCTD module and section where the designation information is documented within the regulatory submission dossier (e.g., Module 1.13 for US regional information).']
  exclusivity_period_years decimal(4,1) [note: 'Duration in years of market exclusivity conferred by the designation upon approval (e.g., 7 years for FDA Orphan Drug, 10 years for EMA Orphan). Null if no exclusivity is associated with this designation type.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the designation expires or lapses, if applicable (e.g., Rare Pediatric Disease voucher validity, Orphan Drug Exclusivity end date). Null for designations with no defined expiry.']
  grant_date date [note: 'Date on which the health authority officially granted the designation. Null if the designation has not yet been granted.']
  obligations varchar [note: 'Free-text summary of post-grant obligations and commitments associated with the designation (e.g., pediatric study requirements, annual prevalence reporting for orphan drugs, post-market confirmatory trials for accelerated approval).']
  orphan_disease_prevalence decimal(10,2) [note: 'Reported prevalence of the orphan condition per 10,000 population as submitted in the Orphan Drug Designation (ODD) request. Applicable only when designation_type is Orphan Drug Designation or Rare Pediatric Disease.']
  priority_review_voucher_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this designation confers eligibility for a Priority Review Voucher (PRV) upon approval (applicable to Rare Pediatric Disease and Tropical Disease designations under FDA).']
  request_date date [note: 'Date on which the company formally submitted the designation request to the health authority.']
  rolling_review_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the designation enables rolling review of the marketing application (i.e., submission of completed dossier modules before the full application is ready), as applicable for Fast Track and EMA PRIME designations.']
  strategic_importance varchar [note: 'Internal classification of the strategic importance of this designation to the company\'s regulatory strategy and commercial planning (e.g., Critical for launch timeline, High for market access).']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Broad therapeutic area classification for the indication covered by the designation (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Infectious Diseases).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory designation record was last modified, supporting audit trail and change tracking requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault RIM for the designation request package, grant letter, or associated regulatory correspondence, enabling traceability to the source document management system.']
  voucher_redeemed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Priority Review Voucher (PRV) associated with this designation has been redeemed or sold. Applicable only when priority_review_voucher_eligible is True.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date on which the designation was voluntarily withdrawn by the sponsor or revoked by the health authority. Null if not withdrawn.']

  Note: 'Tracks special regulatory designations and expedited program grants for drug products including FDA Breakthrough Therapy, Fast Track, Accelerated Approval, Priority Review, Orphan Drug Designation (ODD), Rare Pediatric Disease, EMA PRIME, and PMDA Sakigake. Captures designation type, granting agency, request date, grant date, expiry date, associated product and indication, and designation-specific benefits and obligations. Critical for regulatory strategy and commercial planning.'
}

  brand_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand_plan. Business justification: Variations (new indications, new strengths, label updates) directly trigger brand plan revisions and new promotional cycles.   country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: variation.market_country_code is a plain-text denormalization of country. Variations are country-specific regulatory submissions; a proper FK to masterdata.country is essential for variation managemen']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Variations (Type I/II, CBE, PAS) are filed to change a specific drug product\'s approved characteristics. Regulatory CMC teams need this link to track which drug_product formulation is subject to the v']
  drug_substance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance.   master_batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.master_batch_record.   medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the medicinal product affected by this variation or supplement.']

The variation is the regulatory trigger for the pricing decision. Pharma market acces']
  product_recall_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.product_recall. Business justification: Recall-driven regulatory variations are a named process in pharma: a product recall (e.g., labeling error, contamination) necessitates a Type II variation or supplement submission. Regulatory teams mu']
  safety_signal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.safety_signal. Business justification: Type II variations are often triggered by validated safety signals requiring product information updates. Real business process: signal validation → regulatory variation to update SmPC/label/RMP. Vari']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.site. Business justification: Post-approval variations are frequently site-specific (site additions, manufacturing process changes at a site). Regulatory teams track which manufacturing site a variation applies to for site-specifi']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Variations (post-approval changes) are submitted to health authorities via formal submissions. The variation record tracks the change request, while submission tracks the filing event. Adding submissi']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'Date on which the health authority formally accepted the variation submission as valid for review (validation/acceptance check passed).']
  affected_ctd_sections varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of eCTD Common Technical Document (CTD) module and section references impacted by this variation (e.g., 3.2.S.2.2, 3.2.P.3.3, 2.7.1). Sourced from Veeva Vault RIM dossier mapping.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the health authority granted approval or positive opinion for the variation or supplement.']
  change_category varchar [note: 'High-level business category of the change being submitted, used for portfolio analytics and workload management across regulatory affairs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manufacturing|quality|clinical|labeling|administrative|safety|pharmacovigilance|packaging — promote to reference product]']
  change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the proposed change covered by this variation or supplement, including the nature of the modification to the approved product dossier.']
  clock_stop_days varchar [note: 'Total number of days the regulatory review clock was stopped due to requests for supplementary information (List of Questions / List of Outstanding Issues) from the health authority, used for accurate timeline tracking.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional regulatory affairs notes, internal observations, or contextual information relevant to the variation that does not fit structured fields.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this variation record was first created in the system, providing audit trail for data governance and regulatory compliance.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'Pharmaceutical dosage form of the product subject to this variation (e.g., tablet, capsule, solution for injection), as defined in the marketing authorization.']
  ectd_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit eCTD sequence number assigned to the variation submission within the application lifecycle, used for dossier version control and health authority tracking.']
  group_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this variation to a grouped variation package when multiple changes are submitted together under EMA grouping or FDA bundling provisions.']
  implementation_date date [note: 'Date on which the approved change was operationally implemented in manufacturing, labeling, or other affected business processes.']
  implementation_deadline date [note: 'Regulatory or internal deadline by which the approved variation must be implemented, as mandated by the health authority decision or internal policy.']
  implementation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the operational implementation of the approved variation change across manufacturing, labeling, and supply chain.']
  is_grouped boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this variation is submitted as part of a grouped variation package (multiple changes submitted together in a single application under EMA grouping rules).']
  is_labeling_change boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this variation includes a change to the approved product labeling (SmPC, PIL, or US prescribing information), triggering label management workflows.']
  is_safety_driven boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this variation was initiated as a result of a pharmacovigilance signal, safety review, or post-market safety commitment, triggering expedited regulatory processing.']
  questions_received_date date [note: 'Date on which the health authority issued a List of Questions (LoQ) or Request for Information (RFI) during the review of this variation, initiating a clock stop.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory guideline, condition number, or legal basis under which the variation is classified (e.g., EMA Annex II condition number, ICH Q12 established conditions, FDA regulation citation).']
  response_submitted_date date [note: 'Date on which the company submitted its response to the health authority\'s List of Questions or Request for Information, restarting the regulatory review clock.']
  strength varchar [note: 'Approved strength or concentration of the active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) in the product subject to this variation (e.g., 10 mg, 100 mg/mL).']
  target_approval_date date [note: 'Planned or projected date by which health authority approval of the variation is expected, based on regulatory clock and review timelines.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Therapeutic area or disease area associated with the product subject to this variation (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases), used for portfolio reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this variation record was last modified, supporting audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer.']
  variation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier assigned to the variation or supplement by the health authority or internal regulatory tracking system (e.g., EMA variation number, FDA supplement number, or internal RIMS reference).']
  variation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the variation or supplement from initial drafting through health authority decision and implementation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|refused|withdrawn|implemented — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  variation_type varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the variation or supplement. EMA categories include Type IA (minor, immediate notification), Type IAIN (immediate notification), Type IB (minor, 30-day review), and Type II (major). FDA categories include CBE-0 (Changes Being Effected immediately), CBE-30 (Changes Being Effected in 30 days), and PAS (Prior Approval Supplement). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Type_IA|Type_IAIN|Type_IB|Type_II|CBE_0|CBE_30|PAS|Minor|Major — promote to reference product]']
  vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Unique document identifier in Veeva Vault RIM corresponding to the variation dossier or submission package, enabling direct traceability to the source system record.']

  Note: 'Tracks post-approval variations and supplements to marketing authorizations including Type IA, IB, and II variations (EMA), CBE-0, CBE-30, and PAS supplements (FDA), and equivalent changes in other markets. Captures variation type, change description, affected CTD sections, submission date, approval date, implementation date, and regulatory basis. Manages the lifecycle of approved product changes across global markets. Sourced from Veeva Vault RIM variation management module.'
}

Business justification: Access strategy is country-specific and directly tied to the product registration in that country. Registration status, holder, and conditions shape the access strategy. Pharma market access teams tra']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating regulatory application (NDA, BLA, MAA, etc.) that resulted in this product registration.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: A country-level product registration is the direct outcome of a regulatory approval. The product_registration table tracks the marketing authorization at country level, which is granted based on an ap']
  atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: Product registrations require ATC classification for national formulary inclusion, reimbursement coding, and therapeutic substitution policies. Core to market access and pricing strategy. Atc_code is ']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Registrations authorize brand marketing in specific countries. Commercial launch planning and territory allocation require registration status visibility by brand and market.']
  brand_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand_plan. Business justification: Country-level product registration directly enables and is referenced by the country-specific brand plan. The \'registration-to-launch brand planning\' process is a named market entry workflow — brand p']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Product registration jurisdiction determines regulatory authority, renewal requirements, serialization mandates, and market access. Core to commercial operations and compliance tracking. Country_code ']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Product registrations are for specific drug product formulations (specific dosage form, strength, route) in specific countries. Regulatory teams track which drug_product is registered per country for ']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Product registrations are granted by specific health authorities. The product_registration table has health_authority_code (STRING) which is a denormalized reference to the health_authority table. Rep']
  holder_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. The MAH/registration holder is a legal entity; a proper FK is required for regist']
  hta_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.hta_submission.   masterdata_ndc_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.ndc_code. Required for serialization compliance and supply chain integrity. Ndc_code is d']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the global drug product master record for which this country-level registration applies.']

Regulatory teams track which packaging configurations are re']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Patent linkage at the product registration level is a direct regulatory requirement in multiple jurisdictions (EU patent linkage register, Canada patent register). Regulatory affairs teams must associ']
  pricing_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.pricing_decision.   submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission.   trademark_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.trademark. Business justification: Product registrations use trademarked brand names in specific jurisdictions.   approved_indication varchar [note: 'Full text of the approved therapeutic indication(s) as granted by the health authority and reflected in the current product label/SmPC. Defines the authorized use of the product in this market.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this product registration record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data governance and lineage tracking.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'Approved pharmaceutical dosage form of the registered drug product as stated in the marketing authorization (e.g., film-coated tablet, solution for injection, capsule, lyophilized powder). Aligns with the Finished Dosage Form (FDF) designation.']
  ectd_sequence_number varchar [note: 'The eCTD sequence number of the most recent approved submission associated with this registration. Used to identify the current approved dossier version in the eCTD lifecycle.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current marketing authorization or product registration expires if not renewed. This is the EFFECTIVE_UNTIL date for the MASTER_AGREEMENT role. Null for registrations that are valid indefinitely (e.g., post-5-year renewal in EU where no further expiry applies).']
  first_commercial_date date [note: 'Date on which the product was first commercially launched and placed on the market in this country following registration. Used to track sunset clause compliance and commercial launch timelines.']
  orphan_designation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product registration carries an orphan drug designation in the registered country, conferring market exclusivity and regulatory incentives for rare disease treatments.']
  post_approval_commitment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this registration carries outstanding post-approval commitments or conditions imposed by the health authority (e.g., Phase IV studies, risk management measures, label updates). True = open commitments exist.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date on which the marketing authorization or product registration was officially granted by the health authority. This is the EFFECTIVE_FROM date for the MASTER_AGREEMENT role — the date the registration became binding.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official marketing authorization or product registration number assigned by the health authority in the country of registration (e.g., NDA number, EU MA number, NMPA approval number). This is the externally-known business identifier for the registration.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the product registration in the given country. Active = valid and commercially available; Suspended = temporarily halted by authority or holder; Withdrawn = permanently removed; Expired = lapsed without renewal; Pending Renewal = renewal submitted and under review.']
  rems_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program is required for this product registration in the applicable market. Applicable primarily to FDA-regulated products.']
  renewal_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the health authority granted approval of the renewal application, extending the validity of the marketing authorization.']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'Calendar date by which the renewal application must be submitted to the health authority to prevent registration lapse. Critical for renewal calendar management and commercial continuity planning.']
  renewal_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the regulatory renewal fee payable to the health authority for this registration renewal. Expressed in the local currency of the health authority jurisdiction.']
  renewal_fee_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the renewal fee amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, CNY).']
  renewal_fee_status varchar [note: 'Payment status of the regulatory renewal fee due to the health authority for this registration renewal cycle. Unpaid or overdue fees can result in registration lapse.']
  renewal_outcome varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent renewal application for this registration. Approved = renewal granted; Refused = health authority denied renewal; Withdrawn by Holder = MAH withdrew the renewal application; Pending = renewal submitted and under review; Not Submitted = renewal not yet submitted.']
  renewal_submission_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the renewal application dossier was submitted to the health authority. Used to confirm timely submission and calculate submission lead time against the renewal due date.']
  renewal_type varchar [note: 'Type of renewal cycle applicable to this registration as defined by the health authority. Five-year = standard EU/ICH 5-year renewal; Annual = annual renewal required (e.g., certain PMDA registrations); Sunset Clause = registration lapses if product not marketed within defined period; Indefinite = no renewal required after initial grant; Not Applicable = renewal not applicable in this jurisdiction.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'Approved route of administration for the registered drug product as stated in the marketing authorization (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, topical, inhalation).']
  strength varchar [note: 'Approved strength(s) of the active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) as stated in the marketing authorization (e.g., 10 mg, 100 mg/mL, 500 mg/5 mL). Multiple strengths may be listed as a delimited string if covered under a single registration number.']
  sunset_clause_date date [note: 'Date by which the product must be placed on the market in the registered country to avoid automatic lapse of the registration under the sunset clause provision. Applicable in EU and certain other jurisdictions where a registration lapses if the product is not marketed within 3 years of grant.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Therapeutic area classification of the registered product (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Cardiovascular, Neurology). Used for portfolio analytics and commercial reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this product registration record was most recently updated in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and incremental ETL processing.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault RIM (Regulatory Information Management) system corresponding to the registration dossier and supporting documents for this product registration. Enables traceability to the source system of record.']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for country-level product registration master tracking the marketing authorization status, complete renewal lifecycle, and maintenance calendar of each drug product in each country/market. Captures registration number, country, registration holder, registration date, expiry date, approved indication, approved dosage forms and strengths, local brand name, and registration status (active, suspended, withdrawn, expired). Includes complete renewal tracking: renewal type (5-year, annual, sunset clause), renewal due dates, submission dates, approval dates, fee payment status, and renewal outcomes. Manages the renewal calendar across all markets to prevent registration lapse. SSOT for global product registration footprint, renewal compliance, and commercial continuity — no separate renewal product exists.'
}

Table regulatory.dmf_cross_reference {
  dmf_cross_reference_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the dmf_cross_reference association']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory application (NDA, BLA, ANDA, MAA) that cites the DMF.']
  dmf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the Drug Master File (DMF) being cross-referenced in the regulatory application.']
  access_granted_date date [note: 'Date on which the applicant was formally granted access to reference the DMF content within their application. Marks the effective start of the cross-reference authorization for this DMF-application pair.']
  authorization_letter_date date [note: 'Date on which the DMF holder issued the Letter of Authorization (LoA) to the applicant for this specific cross-reference. This date is specific to the DMF-application pairing and does not belong on either entity alone.']
  authorization_letter_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DMF holder has issued a Letter of Authorization (LoA) to the applicant for this specific DMF-application cross-reference. Belongs to the cross-reference relationship, not to the DMF or application alone.']
  authorization_letter_reference varchar [note: 'The document reference number of the Letter of Authorization issued by the DMF holder for this specific DMF-application cross-reference. Unique to each LoA issued.']
  cross_reference_number varchar [note: 'The FDA-assigned identifier for this specific DMF citation within the regulatory application dossier. Assigned by the agency upon receipt and used to track the cross-reference in the review process.']
  cross_reference_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this DMF cross-reference authorization within the application. Tracks whether the authorization is active, has been withdrawn by the DMF holder, or is pending receipt of the LoA.']
  referencing_application_count varchar [note: 'Number of regulatory applications (NDA, BLA, ANDA, MAA) that currently reference this DMF. Indicates the strategic importance of the DMF to the product portfolio and the breadth of supplier dependency. [Moved from dmf: This is a derived count of how many applications reference this DMF. It is a denormalized summary that can be computed from the dmf_cross_reference association table (COUNT of application_id grouped by dmf_id). Storing it on the DMF entity creates a maintenance burden and data integrity risk. It should be removed from dmf and derived from this association.]']

  Note: 'This association product represents the formal Cross-Reference Authorization between a Drug Master File (DMF) and a regulatory application. It captures the operational record of a sponsor\'s authorization to cite a specific DMF within a specific application, including the Letter of Authorization (LoA) lifecycle, FDA-assigned cross-reference identifiers, and access grant status. Each record links one DMF to one regulatory application and carries attributes that exist only in the context of this specific DMF-application cross-reference relationship, in accordance with 21 CFR 314.420 and FDA DMF guidance.. Existence Justification: In pharmaceutical regulatory practice, a single DMF (e.g., an API Drug Master File) is formally cross-referenced by multiple NDA/BLA/ANDA applications from one or more sponsors, and a single application routinely references multiple DMFs (e.g., one for the API, one for the excipient, one for the packaging material, one for the manufacturing site). This cross-referencing process is a formally managed business activity: the DMF holder must issue a Letter of Authorization (LoA) to each referencing applicant, the FDA tracks each cross-reference explicitly, and each authorization has its own lifecycle, reference number, and correspondence history. The relationship is not derivable from transactional joins — it is an operational record that regulatory affairs professionals actively create, update, and manage.'
}

Table manufacturing.production_order {

  formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.formulation. Business justification: Production orders are executed against a specific approved formulation version.   line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_line. Business justification: Production orders are executed on a specific manufacturing line within a site.   masterdata_ndc_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.masterdata_ndc_code.   packaging_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.packaging_configuration. Packaging line scheduling and regulatory serializa']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_plan. Business justification: Production orders execute supply plans in S&OP process. MRP systems require linking scheduled production to the supply plan version that authorized it for capacity planning and variance analysis.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: SAP PP production orders are plant-specific objects. Plant-level assignment enables manufacturing cost center allocation, GMP inspection scope determination, and regulatory reporting by manufacturing ']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_site. Business justification: Production orders are executed at a specific manufacturing site. The production_order table currently has production_facility_code (STRING) which should be normalized to a FK relationship with manufac']
  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: Production order quantities require standardized UoM from MDM master for yield reconciliation, batch record accuracy, and regulatory submission consistency. unit_of_measure is a plain string on produc']
  actual_finish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when all production operations were completed and order was confirmed. Captured from Manufacturing Execution System (MES) at final operation completion.']
  actual_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual quantity produced and confirmed at order completion. May differ from planned quantity due to yield loss, in-process rejections, or overproduction within acceptable limits.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when production order execution commenced. Captured from Manufacturing Execution System (MES) at first operation start. Critical for cycle time analysis and Current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP) batch record documentation.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Unique batch or lot number assigned to the production output. Critical identifier for traceability, quality control testing, Certificate of Analysis (CoA), and pharmacovigilance. Must be unique per material and comply with batch numbering conventions per 21 CFR Part 211.188.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who created the production order record. Required for audit trail and accountability per 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the production order record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data governance and compliance.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calculated expiration date for the manufactured batch based on approved shelf life and manufacturing date. Critical for inventory management, distribution planning, and regulatory compliance. Must be printed on product labeling per 21 CFR Part 201.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who last modified the production order record. Required for audit trail and accountability per 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the production order record was last updated. Audit trail field for tracking changes to order details, status transitions, or confirmations.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the production order. Human-readable order number used across manufacturing execution systems and batch records.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the production order in the manufacturing execution workflow. Created indicates order planning complete; released indicates order approved for execution; in_progress indicates active manufacturing; confirmed indicates production complete pending quality review; technically_complete indicates all operations and quality checks complete; closed indicates final settlement and batch record archived; cancelled indicates order terminated without execution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|released|in_progress|confirmed|technically_complete|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the production order indicating the type of manufacturing operation: process order for Drug Substance (DS) production, production order for Drug Product (DP) formulation, packaging order for fill-finish and labeling, rework order for reprocessing, validation batch for process qualification, or exhibit batch for regulatory submission samples.']
  pat_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Process Analytical Technology (PAT) tools are used for real-time monitoring and control of Critical Process Parameters (CPP) and Critical Quality Attributes (CQA) during manufacturing. True for processes with inline/online/at-line analytical instrumentation per FDA PAT Guidance.']
  planned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Target quantity to be produced as specified in the production order. Represents the batch size defined in the master batch record or Bill of Materials (BOM).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Scheduling priority for the production order. Urgent for critical supply shortages or regulatory commitments; high for commercial launch or clinical trial material; normal for routine replenishment; low for strategic inventory build.']
  qbd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the production order follows Quality by Design (QbD) principles with defined design space, Critical Process Parameters (CPP), and Critical Quality Attributes (CQA) per ICH Q8/Q9/Q10. True for QbD-enabled processes with enhanced process understanding and control strategy.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of material reconciliation comparing issued raw materials and components against actual consumption and finished goods produced. Reconciliation is required per 21 CFR Part 211.188 to detect significant unexplained discrepancies that may indicate diversion, contamination, or process issues.']
  regulatory_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether material from this production order will be used for regulatory submission purposes such as stability studies, exhibit batches for New Drug Application (NDA), Biologics License Application (BLA), or Marketing Authorization Application (MAA). Requires enhanced documentation and retention per regulatory requirements.']
  retest_date date [note: 'Date by which Drug Substance (DS) or Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API) must be retested to confirm continued compliance with specifications. Applicable primarily to bulk API and intermediates stored before further processing.']
  rework_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the production order involves rework or reprocessing of previously manufactured material that did not meet specifications. Rework must be performed according to approved procedures and documented per 21 CFR Part 211.115.']
  scheduled_finish_date date [note: 'Planned date for production order completion including all manufacturing operations and in-process quality checks.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date for production order execution to begin. Used for capacity planning and material staging.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material scrapped or rejected during production due to Out of Specification (OOS) results, equipment failures, or process deviations. Scrap must be documented and investigated per Current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP) requirements.']
  validation_batch_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the production order is a validation batch used for process qualification, process performance qualification (PPQ), or continued process verification. Validation batches require enhanced documentation and are used to demonstrate process capability and reproducibility.']
  yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated yield percentage representing actual quantity produced divided by planned quantity, expressed as a percentage. Used for process performance monitoring and variance analysis. Deviations outside acceptable yield ranges may trigger investigation per Current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP).']

  Note: 'Core manufacturing execution record representing a discrete production order for drug substance (DS), drug product (DP), or packaging/labeling operations. Captures order type (process order, production order, packaging order), planned/actual quantities, scheduled and actual start/finish dates, production facility, manufacturing line, order status (created, released, confirmed, technically complete), priority, batch size, unit of measure, material/SKU, NDC code (for packaging), reconciliation data, and linkage to master batch record. Central transactional entity for all manufacturing execution aligned with SAP PP module.'
}

Table manufacturing.batch_record {
  batch_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the electronic batch record (eBR). Primary key for the batch record entity. This is the system-generated surrogate key for the batch manufacturing record.']
  batch_size_unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: Batch size UoM in GMP batch records must be standardized for yield calculations, regulatory submissions, and cross-site comparisons. batch_size_uom is a plain string; normalizing to masterdata.unit_of']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Every batch is manufactured for a specific brand. Critical for serialization compliance, track-and-trace systems, product complaint investigations, recall management, and commercial supply chain visib']
  brand_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand_plan. Business justification: GMP batch records must be traceable to the commercial brand plan authorizing production campaigns. Supports supply-commercial audit trails, regulatory inspection readiness, and annual product review (']
  drug_master_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.drug_master_file. Business justification: Batches manufactured using DMF-registered API, excipients, or processes must reference the DMF for regulatory traceability, CMC compliance verification, letter of authorization validation, and support']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Batch records document manufacturing of specific drug products. Essential for batch disposition, regulatory traceability, product genealogy, and linking manufacturing execution to product specificatio']
  formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.formulation. Business justification: GMP requires each batch record to document the approved formulation version manufactured. Regulatory inspections, batch disposition decisions, and annual product quality reviews all require this direc']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Batch production costs for validation, clinical trials, and development batches are allocated to internal orders for proper project accounting, cost tracking, and capitalization assessment.']
  investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.investigator. Business justification: Clinical supply chain management requires tracking which principal investigator received each investigational batch. FDA 21 CFR Part 312 and ICH E6 GCP require traceability of investigational product ']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_line. Business justification: Batch records are executed on a specific manufacturing line. The batch_record table has production_line_code (STRING) which should be normalized to a FK to manufacturing_line. This enables access to l']
  master_batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved master batch record (MBR) that defines the standard manufacturing procedure, formula, and process parameters for this product. The MBR is the template from which this batch record is executed.']
  masterdata_ndc_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.masterdata_ndc_code. Business justification: Batch records for US-marketed products require NDC code for FDA lot distribution reports, DSCSA serialization compliance, and drug recall traceability. Linking batch_record to masterdata_ndc_code enab']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: GMP batch records (21 CFR Part 211.188, EU GMP Annex 11) must document the specific material being manufactured including material number, grade, and specifications. Linking batch_record to material m']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Batch records are released against the approved medicinal product registration. QP certification, batch disposition, and regulatory batch certificates reference the medicinal product. Annual product q']
  packaging_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.packaging_configuration. Business justification: Batch records for finished products must document the packaging configuration used for serialization compliance, labeling verification, and batch release. Regulatory batch certificates and QP release ']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: SAP MM batch master records are plant-specific. Batch records require plant assignment for lot release decisions, GMP inspection scope, and regulatory lot distribution reporting. Plant-level batch tra']
  process_validation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.process_validation. Business justification: Validation batches are executed as part of a formal process validation study (Stage 2 — Process Performance Qualification per FDA Process Validation Guidance 2011). Each batch_record that is a validat']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Batch records are the execution records for production orders. Each batch_record is created to document the manufacturing execution of a specific production_order. This is a standard child→parent rela']
  pv_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.pv_product. Business justification: Batch disposition decisions require knowing the pv_product\'s REMS requirements, expedited reporting flags, and RMP commitments. PV teams trace safety signals back to specific manufactured batches via ']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_site. Business justification: Batch records are executed at a specific manufacturing site. Currently batch_record has manufacturing_site_code and manufacturing_site_name (STRING) which are redundant with the manufacturing_site mas']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: GMP regulatory requirement (21 CFR Part 211, EU GMP): every executed batch record must reference the approved product specification it was manufactured against. Essential for batch release, regulatory']
  supply_inventory_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: Manufacturing batch records must link to supply inventory lots to enable end-to-end traceability from production to distribution. This is critical for product recalls, quality investigations, and supp']
  transfer_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.transfer_price. Business justification: Inter-site/inter-entity batch manufacturing requires transfer pricing for OECD/tax compliance. Each batch record must reference the applicable transfer price to support intercompany valuation, tax fil']
  actual_yield_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual quantity of product produced at the end of the manufacturing process. Compared against theoretical yield to calculate yield percentage and identify process inefficiencies or material losses.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The unique business identifier assigned to the manufacturing batch. This is the externally-known batch identifier used across all manufacturing, quality, and regulatory documentation. Must be unique and traceable per cGMP requirements.']
  batch_size_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The planned or target quantity of product to be manufactured in this batch. Expressed in the unit of measure specified in batch_size_uom. Used for yield calculations and material planning.']
  batch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the batch. In-process indicates active manufacturing; Released indicates QA approval for distribution; Rejected indicates failed quality specifications; Quarantine indicates pending investigation or testing; On-hold indicates temporary suspension; Expired indicates past shelf life.']
  batch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the batch by manufacturing stage. Drug Substance (DS) refers to API production; Drug Product (DP) refers to formulated finished dosage form (FDF). Intermediate refers to in-process materials.']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal CAPA investigation was required for this batch due to deviations, out-of-specification (OOS) results, or quality issues. True indicates CAPA was initiated; False indicates no CAPA required.']
  deviation_count varchar [note: 'The number of manufacturing deviations or non-conformances documented during the production of this batch. Deviations require investigation and impact assessment per cGMP. Zero indicates no deviations occurred.']
  disposition_approver_name varchar [note: 'The name of the qualified quality unit personnel who approved the final disposition decision. Must be an authorized individual per the quality management system (QMS).']
  disposition_date date [note: 'The date on which the final disposition decision was made by the quality unit. Marks the completion of batch review and approval process.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'The final quality decision on the batch. Release indicates approval for distribution; Reject indicates failure to meet specifications; Rework indicates batch will be reprocessed; Destroy indicates batch will be disposed of. This is the authoritative disposition outcome.']
  disposition_rationale varchar [note: 'The documented justification for the disposition decision. For rejections, includes the specific quality failures or deviations. For releases, confirms all specifications and cGMP requirements were met. Required for regulatory compliance and audit trail.']
  environmental_monitoring_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether environmental monitoring results (viable and non-viable particulates, temperature, humidity) were within acceptable limits throughout the manufacturing process. Critical for sterile and aseptic manufacturing per cGMP.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which the batch should not be used or distributed. Calculated based on manufacture date plus approved shelf life from stability studies. Required on all drug product labels per regulatory requirements.']
  formulation_version varchar [note: 'The version number of the product formulation used for this batch. Tracks changes to the formula, process parameters, or specifications. Critical for change control and regulatory submissions (CMC section).']
  gmp_classification varchar [note: 'The GMP classification under which this batch was manufactured. cGMP (current Good Manufacturing Practice) is required for commercial and late-stage clinical batches. GMP applies to earlier development batches. Non-GMP may apply to research-only materials.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the batch manufacturing was completed. Used for regulatory labeling, shelf-life calculations, and expiry date determination. Distinct from manufacturing_end_timestamp which includes time precision.']
  manufacturing_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when manufacturing operations for this batch were completed. Marks the end of the production cycle before quality testing and release. Used to calculate cycle time and production efficiency.']
  manufacturing_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when manufacturing operations for this batch commenced. Marks the beginning of the batch production cycle. Critical for batch genealogy and process timeline documentation.']
  pat_monitoring_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Process Analytical Technology (PAT) tools were used for real-time monitoring and control of critical quality attributes (CQA) during this batch. PAT enables continuous quality verification per FDA PAT guidance.']
  qbd_design_space_compliance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all critical process parameters (CPP) remained within the approved QbD design space during manufacturing. True indicates full compliance; False indicates deviation requiring investigation. Applicable for products developed under QbD principles per ICH Q8.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the material reconciliation process comparing input materials consumed against output product and documented waste. Discrepancies require investigation per cGMP. Reconciled indicates all materials are accounted for within acceptable limits.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this batch record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data governance and compliance. Distinct from manufacturing_start_timestamp which reflects the physical production start.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this batch record was last modified in the system. Audit trail field for data governance and compliance. Updated whenever any field in the batch record is changed.']
  regulatory_submission_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory submission (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA) under which this batch was manufactured. Links the batch to the approved regulatory dossier and ensures manufacturing is conducted per approved CMC specifications.']
  retest_date date [note: 'The date by which the batch must be re-examined to ensure it still meets quality specifications. Primarily applicable to drug substances (API) and intermediates. If testing confirms specifications are met, the material may continue to be used.']
  sterility_assurance_level varchar [note: 'The probability of a single viable microorganism being present on a product unit after sterilization. Expressed as SAL-10^-N (e.g., SAL-10^-6 means one in a million probability). Required for sterile products. Not applicable for non-sterile products.']
  sterilization_method varchar [note: 'The sterilization method applied to this batch, if applicable. Required for sterile drug products and certain medical devices. Not applicable for non-sterile products. Method must be validated per regulatory requirements.']
  theoretical_yield_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The expected quantity of product that should be produced based on the master batch record formula and input materials, assuming 100% efficiency with no losses. Used as the baseline for yield percentage calculation.']
  yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The ratio of actual yield to theoretical yield expressed as a percentage. Calculated as (actual_yield_quantity / theoretical_yield_quantity) * 100. Deviations from expected yield ranges trigger investigations per cGMP.']

  Note: 'Electronic batch record (eBR) representing the complete manufacturing history of a specific batch of drug substance or drug product, as required under 21 CFR Parts 210/211 and cGMP. Captures batch number, product code, batch size, manufacturing date, expiry date, retest date, manufacturing site, batch status (in-process, released, rejected, quarantine), master batch record reference, yield data (theoretical vs actual, yield percentage, reconciliation status), disposition decision (release/reject with approver and rationale), material consumption actuals, parent/child batch genealogy links, and campaign reference. Serves as the primary cGMP compliance document and SSOT for batch lifecycle from production through disposition. Sourced from MES and SAP PP/QM.'
}
