Business justification: MBR batch size specifications require standardized UoM for regulatory submissions (NDA/MAA CMC), process validation protocols, and cross-site technology transfers. batch_size_uom is a plain string; no']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: MBRs define manufacturing processes for specific brands. Links technical manufacturing documentation to commercial product lifecycle management, launch planning, and regulatory submission tracking. Es']
  drug_master_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.drug_master_file. Business justification: MBRs for API or proprietary excipient manufacturing must link to holder\'s DMF for regulatory submissions, supply chain qualification, change control coordination, and ensuring manufacturing process al']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the drug product (DP) or drug substance (DS) that this master batch record defines manufacturing instructions for.']
  drug_substance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: MBRs define API/drug substance used in formulation. Critical for API sourcing strategy, DMF linkage, regulatory submissions (CTD Module 3), and ensuring correct active ingredient specification. Suppor']
  formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.formulation. Business justification: The MBR is the master manufacturing document and must reference the approved formulation. Technology transfer, change control, and regulatory CMC filings all require the MBR to be directly linked to t']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: An MBR for a licensed product must reference the licensing agreement to ensure manufacturing procedures remain within licensed field-of-use, batch size, and territory constraints. Compliance audits an']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Master batch records define the manufacturing process for a specific material. GMP regulations require MBRs to reference the material master for version control, change control, and regulatory submiss']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: MBRs define manufacturing processes that must reference covering process/formulation patents for technology transfer documentation, licensing agreement compliance, regulatory filings, and IP strategy ']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: MBRs are plant-specific in SAP PP/QM for GMP compliance. Plant assignment enables site-specific regulatory approval tracking, technology transfer management, and GMP inspection readiness. Pharma QMS s']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing site or facility where this master batch record is used. Supports multi-site operations and Contract Manufacturing Organization (CMO) / Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) oversight.']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: ICH Q8/GMP document control: an MBR is authored against a specific approved product specification defining in-process controls and release criteria. Pharma QA experts expect this link for MBR approval']
  superseded_by_mbr_master_batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer master batch record that supersedes this version, enabling version lineage tracking.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval status of this master batch record: pending, approved, or rejected by quality assurance and regulatory affairs.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the quality assurance (QA) manager or authorized person who approved this master batch record.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which this master batch record was formally approved by quality assurance.']
  batch_size_maximum decimal(18,3) [note: 'The maximum allowable batch size that can be manufactured using this master batch record, supporting scale flexibility.']
  batch_size_minimum decimal(18,3) [note: 'The minimum allowable batch size that can be manufactured using this master batch record, supporting scale flexibility.']
  batch_size_standard decimal(18,3) [note: 'The standard or nominal batch size for which this master batch record is designed, expressed in the unit of measure defined in batch_size_uom.']
  cgmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this master batch record is compliant with current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP) regulations per 21 CFR Parts 210 and 211.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this master batch record was first created in the system.']
  document_location varchar [note: 'The storage location or URI of the full master batch record document in the document management system (Veeva Vault QualityDocs or MasterControl).']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'The finished dosage form (FDF) of the product: tablet, capsule, injection, suspension, solution, cream, ointment, or powder. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|injection|suspension|solution|cream|ointment|powder — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this master batch record becomes effective and can be used for manufacturing operations.']
  equipment_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of the equipment required for executing this master batch record, including specific equipment types, models, or identifiers.']
  expected_yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The expected theoretical yield for this batch as a percentage, used to calculate acceptable yield ranges and detect deviations.']
  formulation_details varchar [note: 'Detailed formulation information including active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) and excipient quantities, ratios, and specifications. Business-confidential intellectual property.']
  in_process_control_specifications varchar [note: 'Summary of in-process control specifications and acceptance criteria that must be met during batch execution, including critical quality attributes (CQAs).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this master batch record was last modified or updated.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the master batch record: draft, under review, approved, active (in use), superseded (replaced by newer version), obsolete, or retired. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|under_review|approved|active|superseded|obsolete|retired — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  manufacturing_process_type varchar [note: 'The type of manufacturing process covered by this master batch record: API synthesis, formulation, fill-finish, packaging, blending, granulation, coating, or lyophilization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: api_synthesis|formulation|fill_finish|packaging|blending|granulation|coating|lyophilization — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  mbr_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique business identifier for this master batch record, used across manufacturing, quality, and regulatory systems.']
  mbr_title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title or name of the master batch record, typically including product name and dosage form.']
  minimum_yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum acceptable yield percentage below which a batch is considered out of specification (OOS) and requires investigation.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next periodic review of this master batch record.']
  pat_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Process Analytical Technology (PAT) is implemented for real-time monitoring and control of critical process parameters (CPPs) and critical quality attributes (CQAs).']
  process_description varchar [note: 'High-level description of the manufacturing process steps covered by this master batch record, including key operations and sequence.']
  qbd_approach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this master batch record was developed using Quality by Design (QbD) principles per ICH Q8/Q9/Q10.']
  regulatory_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory approval document (NDA, BLA, MAA, or ANDA number) under which this master batch record is authorized for commercial manufacturing.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'The periodic review cycle in months for this master batch record, ensuring ongoing compliance and relevance per quality management system (QMS) requirements.']
  strength varchar [note: 'The strength or potency of the active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) in the finished dosage form, e.g., \'500mg\', \'10mg/mL\'.']
  superseded_date date [note: 'The date on which this master batch record was superseded by a newer version and is no longer used for new batches.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'The version number of this master batch record, incremented with each revision. Format: major.minor (e.g., 1.0, 2.1).']

  Note: 'The approved master batch record (MBR) template defining the standard manufacturing instructions, formulation, process steps, critical process parameters (CPPs) with target values and acceptance criteria, critical quality attributes (CQAs), in-process control specifications, equipment requirements, and expected yields for a specific product and batch size. Represents the recipe or master formula from which individual batch records are generated. Tracks version number, approval status, effective date, superseded date, product code, dosage form, batch size range, and regulatory approval linkage. Serves as the SSOT for all parameter specifications — parameter names, types, target values, control limits (upper/lower), specification limits, and acceptance criteria. Actual measured values during execution are recorded in process_parameter_result, not here. Managed in Veeva Vault QualityDocs or MasterControl.'
}

Table manufacturing.site {
  site_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the manufacturing site. Primary key for the manufacturing site master data entity.']
  address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.address. Business justification: Manufacturing sites have a physical address required for GMP inspection correspondence, regulatory submissions, and logistics. Address fields on site are denormalized from masterdata.address. Normaliz']
  capacity_unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: Site capacity planning and OEE reporting require standardized UoM from the MDM master. capacity_unit_of_measure is a plain string on site; normalizing to masterdata.unit_of_measure ensures cross-site ']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Site country drives regulatory authority mapping (FDA vs EMA vs PMDA), serialization requirements, and GMP inspection authority. country_code on site is a denormalized string; linking to masterdata.co']
  drug_master_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.drug_master_file. Business justification: Manufacturing sites are listed as approved manufacturing locations in DMFs. When a site\'s GMP status changes, the DMF must be updated. Regulatory affairs teams use this link to identify which DMFs are']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Manufacturing sites are owned/operated by a legal entity for FDA establishment registration, EMA authorization, financial consolidation, and GMP certification. Regulatory filings (e.g., 21 CFR 207) re']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: Manufacturing sites belong to organizational units for budget allocation, headcount reporting, and GMP-regulated org hierarchy. Pharma quality management systems require org unit assignment to sites f']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: Manufacturing sites map to SAP plant master records for GMP inspection tracking, capacity planning, and regulatory submissions (FDA establishment, EMA site number). Pharma ERP integration (SAP PP/MM) ']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.profit_center. Business justification: Manufacturing sites are mapped to profit centers for site-level P&L reporting, manufacturing cost variance analysis, and financial controlling. Pharma finance teams require profit center assignment pe']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturing site became operational and began commercial production. Used for calculating site age, depreciation schedules, and historical performance analysis.']
  capacity_units_per_year decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual production capacity of the site measured in units appropriate to the site type (e.g., kilograms of API, millions of tablets, thousands of vials). Used for capacity planning, supply chain optimization, and network strategy.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturing site ceased operations or was decommissioned. Null for active sites. Used for historical record keeping and regulatory reporting of discontinued manufacturing locations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the manufacturing site record was first created in the master data system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  ema_authorization_number varchar [note: 'EMA manufacturing authorization number or EudraGMP identifier for sites manufacturing medicinal products for the European market. Required for GMP compliance verification.']
  ema_authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the EMA manufacturing authorization indicating whether the site is actively authorized to manufacture for EU markets, authorization has expired, application is pending, authorization is not required, or has been suspended.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email contact for the manufacturing site used for regulatory correspondence, quality notifications, and business communications.']
  environmental_monitoring_program boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site has an active environmental monitoring program for tracking viable and non-viable particulates, temperature, humidity, and other environmental conditions in classified manufacturing areas. Required for sterile and aseptic manufacturing operations.']
  fda_registration_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current FDA establishment registration expires. FDA registrations must be renewed annually between October 1 and December 31.']
  fda_registration_number varchar [note: 'FDA establishment registration number assigned to the site. Required for all facilities manufacturing drugs for the US market per 21 CFR Part 207. Must be renewed annually.']
  fda_registration_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the FDA establishment registration indicating whether the site is actively registered, registration has expired, renewal is pending, registration is not required, or registration has been suspended.']
  gmp_certification_status varchar [note: 'Overall current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP) certification status of the site indicating compliance with pharmaceutical quality standards. Certified means site has passed recent GMP inspection, expired means certification has lapsed, pending means inspection scheduled, not certified means site has not been inspected or failed inspection, conditional means certification granted with specific conditions or CAPA requirements.']
  iso_9001_certification_date date [note: 'Date when the site received ISO 9001 certification. ISO 9001 certificates are typically valid for three years with annual surveillance audits.']
  iso_9001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site holds ISO 9001 Quality Management System certification. While not required for pharmaceutical manufacturing, ISO 9001 certification demonstrates commitment to quality management best practices.']
  last_gmp_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent GMP inspection conducted by any regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, etc.). Used to track inspection frequency and predict next inspection window.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the manufacturing site record was last updated in the master data system. Critical for change tracking and data governance.']
  mes_site_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the site in the Manufacturing Execution System (MES). Used to link batch records, equipment data, and process parameters to the physical manufacturing location.']
  next_gmp_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Expected or scheduled date for the next GMP inspection. Typically inspections occur every 2-3 years for routine surveillance or more frequently for sites with compliance issues or new product approvals.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the manufacturing site indicating whether the facility is actively producing, temporarily offline, permanently closed, being built, in validation phase, or suspended due to regulatory or quality issues.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the site is owned and operated internally by the company, operated by a Contract Manufacturing Organization (CMO), a Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO), or a joint venture partnership.']
  pat_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site has Process Analytical Technology (PAT) capabilities for real-time monitoring and control of critical process parameters (CPP) and critical quality attributes (CQA) during manufacturing. PAT enables continuous process verification and improved process understanding.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the manufacturing site. Used for regulatory authority communications, audit scheduling, and emergency contact.']
  pics_membership_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the site is located in a PIC/S member country, enabling mutual recognition of GMP inspections across participating regulatory authorities. Member status facilitates international regulatory acceptance.']
  qbd_implementation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the level of Quality by Design (QbD) principles implementation at the site. QbD is a systematic approach to pharmaceutical development that begins with predefined objectives and emphasizes product and process understanding based on sound science and quality risk management.']
  quality_management_system varchar [note: 'Primary Quality Management System platform deployed at the site for managing deviations, CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action), change control, and document management. Critical for compliance and audit readiness.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the manufacturing site used across enterprise systems and regulatory submissions. Typically alphanumeric code assigned by corporate master data management.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Official legal or business name of the manufacturing facility as registered with regulatory authorities and used in regulatory submissions.']
  site_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary manufacturing capability of the site. API manufacturing produces active pharmaceutical ingredients (drug substance), formulation creates drug product, fill-finish performs sterile filling operations, packaging performs secondary packaging, combination sites perform multiple operations, and analytical testing sites perform quality control testing.']

  Note: 'Master data for physical manufacturing facilities including owned sites and CMO/CDMO contract sites. Captures site name, site code, address, country, site type (API manufacturing, formulation, fill-finish, packaging, combination), GMP certification status, regulatory approval status (FDA, EMA, PMDA registered), site capacity, site owner (internal vs CMO/CDMO), ERP plant code, and operational status. Serves as the SSOT for manufacturing location master data within the manufacturing domain.'
}

Table manufacturing.line {
  line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the manufacturing line. Primary key.']
  cmo_cdmo_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: CMO/CDMO-operated lines must reference the qualified business partner for vendor qualification tracking, GMP audit scheduling, and contract manufacturing oversight. cmo_cdmo_name is a denormalized str']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: Production lines are assigned to cost centers for manufacturing cost allocation, OEE financial reporting, and SAP CO controlling. cost_center on line is a plain denormalized string; linking to masterd']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Manufacturing lines are dedicated or validated for specific drug products (dedicated biologics lines, controlled substance lines). Site master files and regulatory submissions document which drug prod']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Production lines are capitalized fixed assets in pharma CAPEX accounting, tracked separately from individual equipment. Depreciation schedules, asset impairment reviews, and FDA/EMA facility registrat']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Production lines have dedicated internal orders for maintenance, qualification, and operational cost tracking. Pharma finance teams use line-level internal orders for OEE cost analysis and maintenance']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing site where this line is located. Links to the site master data for facility-level context.']
  automation_level varchar [note: 'Degree of automation for the manufacturing line. Continuous manufacturing represents advanced Process Analytical Technology (PAT) and Quality by Design (QbD) implementations.']
  capacity_kg_per_batch decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum batch size capacity in kilograms for bulk processing lines such as granulation, blending, or Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API) synthesis. Null for unit-based lines.']
  capacity_units_per_hour decimal(18,2) [note: 'Rated production capacity of the line expressed as units per hour. For solid dosage forms, this represents tablets or capsules per hour; for liquids, vials or bottles per hour.']
  changeover_time_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average time in hours required to perform a product changeover on this manufacturing line, including cleaning, setup, and line clearance activities per Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) requirements.']
  cleaning_validation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of cleaning validation for the manufacturing line. Critical for preventing cross-contamination and ensuring compliance with Current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP) per 21 CFR Part 211.']
  cleanroom_classification varchar [note: 'ISO 14644 or European Union (EU) Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) Annex 1 cleanroom classification for the manufacturing line environment. Critical for sterile and aseptic manufacturing operations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ISO-5|ISO-6|ISO-7|ISO-8|Grade-A|Grade-B|Grade-C|Grade-D|non-classified — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cmo_cdmo_operated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this manufacturing line is operated by a Contract Manufacturing Organization (CMO) or Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) rather than owned and operated internally.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturing line was commissioned and became operational. Used for asset lifecycle management and depreciation tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this manufacturing line record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  decommissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturing line was decommissioned and taken out of service. Null for active lines.']
  dosage_form_type varchar [note: 'Type of Finished Dosage Form (FDF) produced on this manufacturing line. Aligns with regulatory submissions and product master data. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|injectable|lyophilized-vial|suspension|solution|cream|ointment|patch|inhaler — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  environmental_monitoring_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the manufacturing line requires environmental monitoring for viable and non-viable particulates per Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) Annex 1 for sterile manufacturing.']
  equipment_train_composition varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of major equipment units that comprise the manufacturing line, such as \'Mixer-Granulator-Dryer-Mill\' or \'Filling Machine-Capper-Labeler\'. Supports equipment traceability and maintenance planning.']
  inspection_outcome varchar [note: 'Outcome classification from the most recent regulatory inspection. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) uses classifications such as No Action Indicated (NAI), Voluntary Action Indicated (VAI), and Official Action Indicated (OAI).']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent preventive or corrective maintenance activity performed on the manufacturing line. Supports equipment reliability and Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this manufacturing line record was last updated. Audit trail field for change tracking.']
  line_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the manufacturing line, used in production scheduling, batch records, and Manufacturing Execution System (MES). Typically alphanumeric and unique within a site.']
  line_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the manufacturing line, such as \'Tablet Compression Line 3\' or \'Sterile Fill-Finish Suite A\'.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the manufacturing line by its primary production function. Defines the type of manufacturing operation performed on this line. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: granulation|blending|compression|coating|filling|lyophilization|packaging|inspection|labeling|serialization — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  mes_integration_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the manufacturing line is integrated with the Manufacturing Execution System (MES) for real-time batch tracking, Critical Process Parameter (CPP) monitoring, and electronic batch record generation.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this manufacturing line record. Supports audit trail and compliance with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature requirements.']
  next_scheduled_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the next planned preventive maintenance activity. Used for production scheduling and capacity planning to avoid conflicts with manufacturing campaigns.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special considerations, or operational remarks about the manufacturing line.']
  oee_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) percentage for this manufacturing line. OEE is a key performance indicator combining availability, performance, and quality metrics.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the manufacturing line. Indicates whether the line is available for production, actively running, undergoing maintenance, or out of service.']
  pat_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the line is equipped with Process Analytical Technology (PAT) tools for real-time monitoring and control of Critical Quality Attributes (CQA) and Critical Process Parameters (CPP).']
  qualification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current qualification or validation of the manufacturing line expires and requalification is required. Critical for compliance with Current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP) and Quality by Design (QbD) principles.']
  regulatory_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory inspection by Food and Drug Administration (FDA), European Medicines Agency (EMA), or other regulatory authority. Critical for Pre-Approval Inspection (PAI) and ongoing compliance tracking.']
  serialization_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the manufacturing line is equipped with serialization and track-and-trace capabilities to comply with Drug Supply Chain Security Act (DSCSA) and Falsified Medicines Directive (FMD) requirements.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Operating shift pattern for the manufacturing line. Determines production capacity and labor planning requirements.']
  validated_status varchar [note: 'Current validation state of the manufacturing line under Computer System Validation (CSV) and process validation requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) guidelines.']

  Note: 'Master data for individual production lines, suites, or manufacturing units within a site. Captures line code, line name, line type (granulation, blending, compression, coating, filling, lyophilization, packaging), associated site, capacity (units/hour or kg/batch), equipment train composition, cleanroom classification, validated status, current operational status (available, in-use, under maintenance, decommissioned), and qualification expiry date. Enables production scheduling and capacity planning at the line level.'
}

Table manufacturing.equipment {
  equipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the manufacturing equipment asset. Primary key.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Major GMP equipment has dedicated budgets for qualification programs, calibration, and preventive maintenance requiring linkage for equipment lifecycle cost tracking and capital planning decisions.']
  capacity_unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: Equipment capacity specifications require standardized UoM for cross-site comparability and GMP qualification documentation. capacity_unit_of_measure is a plain string; normalizing to masterdata.unit_']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: GMP-critical equipment is capitalized as fixed assets requiring depreciation calculation, net book value tracking, asset retirement planning, and regulatory documentation for FDA/EMA inspections.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific manufacturing line or production area where this equipment is assigned. Nullable for equipment not assigned to a specific line (e.g., utilities, mobile equipment).']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: Equipment is managed at plant level in SAP PM (Plant Maintenance) for calibration scheduling, GMP qualification records, and regulatory inspection readiness. Plant-level equipment master data is a sta']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing site or facility where this equipment is installed.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original purchase cost of the equipment in the currency specified in acquisition_cost_currency.']
  acquisition_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the acquisition cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  asset_criticality varchar [note: 'Business criticality classification of the equipment based on impact to production, product quality, and regulatory compliance. Critical assets require highest priority for maintenance and spare parts availability.']
  calibration_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between required calibrations for this equipment, as defined in the calibration program.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current calibration status of the equipment. Indicates whether the equipment\'s measurement and control systems are within acceptable tolerances.']
  capacity_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Nominal production capacity or throughput of the equipment. The unit of measure is specified in capacity_unit_of_measure.']
  clean_room_classification varchar [note: 'ISO 14644-1 clean room classification of the area where the equipment is installed. Applicable for equipment in controlled environments for sterile or aseptic manufacturing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: iso_3|iso_4|iso_5|iso_6|iso_7|iso_8|non_classified — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment was commissioned and made ready for qualification activities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this equipment master record was first created in the system.']
  csv_status varchar [note: 'Validation status for computerized equipment systems per 21 CFR Part 11 requirements. Applicable to equipment with electronic controls, data acquisition, or automated processes.']
  equipment_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of equipment by operational function: production (API/DP manufacturing), packaging (primary/secondary), quality control (testing/inspection), utilities (water/HVAC/compressed air), or support (material handling/storage).']
  equipment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the equipment including its purpose, key features, and operational characteristics.']
  equipment_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the equipment asset (e.g., Reactor-101, Tablet Press Line 3, Lyophilizer A).']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the equipment based on its function in the manufacturing process. Includes primary manufacturing equipment, utilities, and support systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reactor|blender|granulator|tablet_press|capsule_filler|coating_machine|lyophilizer|autoclave|filling_machine|packaging_line|labeling_machine|inspection_system|fermentor|centrifuge|dryer|mill|sifter|homogenizer|mixer|sterilizer|isolator|laminar_flow_hood|clean_room_hvac|water_system|compressed_air_system|nitrogen_generator|other — 27 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  gmp_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this equipment is classified as GMP-critical, meaning it directly impacts product quality, safety, or efficacy and requires enhanced qualification, calibration, and change control.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment was physically installed at the manufacturing site.']
  iq_completion_date date [note: 'Date when Installation Qualification was successfully completed and approved.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment was last calibrated.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this equipment master record was last updated.']
  last_pm_date date [note: 'Date when the last scheduled preventive maintenance was completed on this equipment.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s model number or designation for the equipment.']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next calibration of the equipment to maintain measurement accuracy and GMP compliance.']
  next_pm_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next preventive maintenance activity on this equipment.']
  next_qualification_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic requalification of the equipment to maintain GMP compliance.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the equipment. Operational indicates the equipment is available for production use; under_maintenance indicates scheduled or unscheduled maintenance; quarantined indicates the equipment is restricted from use pending investigation; decommissioned indicates permanent removal from service. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: operational|under_maintenance|quarantined|decommissioned|idle|startup|shutdown — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  oq_completion_date date [note: 'Date when Operational Qualification was successfully completed and approved.']
  pq_completion_date date [note: 'Date when Performance Qualification was successfully completed and approved, confirming the equipment consistently performs as intended in the production environment.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current qualification status of the equipment per Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) requirements. Tracks progression through Installation Qualification (IQ), Operational Qualification (OQ), and Performance Qualification (PQ). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_qualified|iq_in_progress|iq_complete|oq_in_progress|oq_complete|pq_in_progress|pq_complete|qualified|requalification_required — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sap_equipment_number varchar [note: 'Equipment master record number in SAP PM module used for maintenance planning, work orders, and asset tracking.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer to this specific equipment unit.']
  tag_number varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric tag number assigned to the equipment for identification and tracking purposes in the manufacturing facility. This is the business identifier used in operations and maintenance.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer\'s warranty coverage for this equipment expires.']

  Note: 'Master data for manufacturing equipment assets including reactors, blenders, granulators, tablet presses, filling machines, lyophilizers, autoclaves, and packaging lines. Captures equipment ID (tag number), equipment name, equipment type, manufacturer, model, serial number, installation date, qualification status (IQ/OQ/PQ), next qualification due date, calibration status, next calibration due date, associated manufacturing line and site, current status (operational, under maintenance, quarantined, decommissioned), and SAP PM equipment number. Supports equipment scheduling and GMP compliance.'
}

Table manufacturing.equipment_qualification {
  equipment_qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the equipment qualification record. Primary key for this entity.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_capa. Business justification: Equipment qualification failures mandate CAPA initiation per GMP. equipment_qualification has capa_reference_number (denormalized text). Replacing with a proper FK to quality_capa enables direct trace']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Equipment qualification (IQ/OQ/PQ) protocols define product-specific acceptance criteria for drug products manufactured on that equipment. Regulatory submissions, site transfer activities, and requali']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing equipment being qualified. Links to the equipment master record.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: IQ/OQ/PQ qualification activities are significant cost events tracked via internal orders in pharma ERP. Finance teams capitalize or expense qualification costs based on the internal order; this link ']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific production line where the equipment operates. Provides manufacturing context for the qualification.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing facility where the equipment is installed and qualified. Links to the site master data.']
  vendor_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Equipment qualification requires tracking the vendor/supplier who performed or supported qualification activities. FDA 21 CFR Part 211 and EU GMP require vendor qualification status. vendor_name is de']
  acceptance_criteria_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of the key acceptance criteria defined in the qualification protocol. Defines the pass/fail thresholds for qualification tests.']
  capa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) is required based on qualification results. True if failures or significant deviations necessitate formal corrective action.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional comments, observations, or notes related to the qualification activity. Captures context not covered by structured fields.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created this qualification record. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this qualification record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for record lifecycle tracking.']
  csv_category varchar [note: 'Computer System Validation (CSV) category per Good Automated Manufacturing Practice (GAMP) 5 guidelines. Category 3 (non-configured products), Category 4 (configured products), and Category 5 (custom applications) require different validation approaches.']
  deviation_count varchar [note: 'Number of deviations from the qualification protocol that occurred during execution. Each deviation requires documentation and impact assessment per Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP).']
  engineering_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the Engineering department formally approved the qualification from a technical perspective.']
  engineering_approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the Engineering representative who reviewed and approved the technical aspects of the qualification.']
  execution_end_date date [note: 'Date when qualification testing activities were completed. Marks the conclusion of all qualification tests defined in the protocol.']
  execution_start_date date [note: 'Date when qualification testing activities commenced. Marks the beginning of the qualification execution phase.']
  gmp_impact_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the equipment based on its impact on product quality and Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) compliance. GMP Critical equipment directly impacts product quality and requires full qualification.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified this qualification record. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance and audit trail.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this qualification record was last modified. Audit trail field for change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  next_requalification_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic requalification of the equipment. Ensures ongoing compliance with Current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP) requirements.']
  qualification_lead_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the qualification lead for communication and coordination purposes.']
  qualification_lead_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual responsible for leading and coordinating the qualification activities. Primary point of contact for the qualification project.']
  qualification_outcome varchar [note: 'Final result of the qualification activity indicating whether the equipment met all acceptance criteria defined in the qualification protocol.']
  qualification_protocol_number varchar [note: 'Unique protocol document number that defines the qualification test plan and acceptance criteria. Externally-known business identifier for this qualification activity.']
  qualification_scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the qualification scope including systems, subsystems, and Critical Process Parameters (CPP) or Critical Quality Attributes (CQA) being validated.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the qualification activity. Tracks progression from planning through execution to final outcome. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Planned|In Progress|Completed|Passed|Failed|On Hold|Cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  qualification_type varchar [note: 'Type of qualification activity performed. IQ (Installation Qualification) verifies equipment is installed correctly; OQ (Operational Qualification) verifies equipment operates within specified parameters; PQ (Performance Qualification) verifies equipment consistently produces quality product under normal operating conditions.']
  quality_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the Quality Assurance unit formally approved the qualification results and released the equipment for production use.']
  quality_approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the Quality Assurance (QA) representative who reviewed and approved the qualification results. Required for Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) compliance.']
  regulatory_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this qualification is part of a regulatory submission package (New Drug Application (NDA), Biologics License Application (BLA), or Marketing Authorization Application (MAA)). True if qualification data will be submitted to regulatory authorities.']
  regulatory_submission_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the regulatory submission (New Drug Application (NDA), Biologics License Application (BLA), Marketing Authorization Application (MAA)) that includes this qualification data.']
  risk_assessment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the risk assessment document that informed the qualification strategy. Aligns with Quality by Design (QbD) principles.']
  test_cases_executed_count varchar [note: 'Total number of test cases executed during the qualification activity. Provides a measure of qualification scope and thoroughness.']
  test_cases_failed_count varchar [note: 'Number of test cases that did not meet acceptance criteria during qualification execution. Triggers investigation and Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) if applicable.']
  test_cases_passed_count varchar [note: 'Number of test cases that met acceptance criteria during qualification execution. Used to calculate pass rate.']
  validation_report_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the validation report that summarizes qualification results, deviations, and conclusions. Links to the formal documentation package.']
  vendor_qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current qualification status of the equipment vendor. Vendors must be qualified before their equipment can be used in Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) production.']

  Note: 'Records of equipment qualification activities including Installation Qualification (IQ), Operational Qualification (OQ), and Performance Qualification (PQ) as required under cGMP and 21 CFR Part 211. Captures qualification type, equipment reference, qualification protocol number, execution date, qualification outcome (pass/fail), next requalification date, approver, and associated validation report reference. Tracks the qualification lifecycle for each piece of equipment to ensure GMP compliance and readiness for production use.'
}

  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Production schedules are aligned to manufacturing budgets for capacity planning and financial forecasting. Pharma S&OP processes require schedule-to-budget linkage to track planned vs. actual manufact']

Business justification: Demand-driven production planning: formulary coverage decisions directly trigger or adjust production schedules. Once a payer coverage decision is confirmed, manufacturing schedules are activated to m']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the drug substance (DS) or drug product (DP) scheduled for production.']

Business justification: Production scheduling requires the formulation to allocate correct materials, equipment configurations, and line setups. Campaign scheduling and capacity planning are formulation-specific; changeovers']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific manufacturing line or production line within the site for which this schedule applies.']
  master_batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.master_batch_record.   packaging_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.packaging_configuration. Business justification: Packaging line scheduling is driven by packaging configuration (pack size, serialization requirements, line speed). Production schedules for packaging operations must reference the packaging configura']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: Production schedules are plant-specific in SAP PP for capacity planning and MRP. Plant assignment enables plant-level capacity utilization reporting, GMP campaign planning, and regulatory manufacturin']
  primary_production_campaign_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production campaign plan to which this schedule belongs, enabling grouping of related production runs.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production order scheduled on this manufacturing line during the planning period.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing site or plant where production is scheduled to occur.']
  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: Production schedule quantities require standardized UoM for capacity planning, supply-demand balancing, and S&OP reporting. unit_of_measure is a plain string on production_schedule; normalizing to mas']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the production schedule was approved for execution.']
  batch_quantity_planned varchar [note: 'Number of batches planned for production during the scheduled period.']
  batch_size_planned decimal(18,3) [note: 'Planned quantity or batch size for the scheduled production run, expressed in the product\'s unit of measure.']

  capacity_utilization_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned capacity utilization percentage for the manufacturing line during the scheduled period.']
  cgmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production schedule has been reviewed and confirmed to comply with cGMP requirements per 21 CFR Parts 210 and 211.']
  cmo_cdmo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production schedule involves a CMO or CDMO partner rather than internal manufacturing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this production schedule record was first created in the system.']
  downtime_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the scheduled downtime window (e.g., preventive maintenance, line cleaning, product changeover). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: maintenance|cleaning|changeover|validation|calibration|inspection|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  downtime_window_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'End timestamp of any scheduled downtime window for maintenance, cleaning, or changeover activities.']
  downtime_window_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Start timestamp of any scheduled downtime window for maintenance, cleaning, or changeover activities.']
  executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the production schedule was marked as executed or completed.']
  frozen_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the production schedule was frozen, preventing further changes without formal change control.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this production schedule record was last modified or updated.']
  pat_implementation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Process Analytical Technology is implemented for real-time monitoring during the scheduled production.']
  planning_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the planning horizon covered by this production schedule.']
  planning_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the planning horizon covered by this production schedule.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this production schedule, influencing resource allocation and sequencing decisions.']
  qbd_principles_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Quality by Design principles have been applied in the development of this production schedule.']
  schedule_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the production schedule, capturing special instructions, constraints, or considerations.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the production schedule indicating its approval and execution state.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Type of production schedule indicating the planning level (e.g., master schedule, operational schedule).']
  schedule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the production schedule, enabling tracking of schedule revisions and changes over time.']
  scheduled_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned duration of the production run in hours, calculated from scheduled start to end timestamps.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned end date and time for the production run on the manufacturing line.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned start date and time for the production run on the manufacturing line.']
  supply_chain_planning_system varchar [note: 'Name of the supply chain planning system that generated or influenced this production schedule (e.g., Kinaxis RapidResponse, Blue Yonder).']

  Note: 'Planned production schedule for manufacturing lines and sites over a defined planning horizon. Captures schedule version, planning period (start/end date), site, manufacturing line, scheduled production orders, planned batch quantities, scheduled downtime windows, campaign plan, and schedule status (draft, approved, frozen, executed). Integrates with SAP PP capacity planning and Kinaxis RapidResponse supply chain planning. Enables capacity utilization analysis and production campaign management.'
}

Table manufacturing.process_parameter {
  process_parameter_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the process parameter definition. Primary key for the process parameter entity.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing batch for which this parameter measurement was recorded. Links parameter measurements to specific batch execution records.']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Critical process parameters (CPPs) are defined per drug product for PAT-enabled monitoring and regulatory CMC submissions. Direct linkage supports product-level parameter trending, process capability ']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the measuring instrument, sensor, or equipment used to capture this parameter value. Required for traceability, calibration verification, and Computer System Validation (CSV) compliance.']
  manufacturing_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the deviation record if this parameter measurement triggered an Out of Specification (OOS) or Out of Trend (OOT) investigation. Links to the Quality Management System (QMS) deviation tracking system.']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: ICH Q8 QbD: critical process parameters (CPPs) are defined within the design space established in the product specification. process_parameter has upper/lower specification limits but no FK to the gov']
  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: Critical process parameters (CPPs) require standardized UoM for regulatory submissions (NDA/MAA), process validation reports, and cross-site comparability studies. unit_of_measure is a plain string; n']
  actual_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The actual measured or recorded value of this parameter during batch execution. Captured from Manufacturing Execution System (MES), PAT instruments, or manual operator entry. Used for batch record documentation and process validation.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator or QA comments regarding this parameter measurement, including explanations for deviations, corrective actions taken, or contextual information for batch record documentation.']
  conformance_status varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the measured value meets the defined control and specification limits. Out of Specification (OOS) or Out of Trend (OOT) results trigger investigation and Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) processes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this process parameter record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for regulatory compliance and data lineage.']
  criticality_justification varchar [note: 'Scientific rationale and risk assessment documentation explaining why this parameter is classified as critical or non-critical. Required for regulatory submissions and Quality by Design (QbD) documentation per ICH Q8.']
  data_integrity_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash or checksum of the parameter measurement record to ensure data integrity and detect unauthorized modifications. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature and audit trail requirements.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The system or application from which this parameter data originated (e.g., SAP PP, MES, LIMS, PAT instrument software). Required for data lineage and audit trail per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this parameter specification version was superseded or retired. Null for currently active specifications. Supports historical batch record interpretation and regulatory compliance.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this parameter specification version became effective. Used to determine which specification applies to historical batch records and supports regulatory audit trails.']
  investigation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this parameter measurement requires formal investigation due to exceeding control or specification limits. True triggers Quality Assurance (QA) review and potential Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this process parameter record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP).']
  lower_control_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The lower boundary of the normal operating range for this parameter. Values below this trigger investigation but may not require batch rejection. Used for statistical process control and continuous process verification.']
  lower_specification_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The minimum acceptable value for this parameter as defined in regulatory filings and approved specifications. Values below this limit may result in batch rejection or deviation investigation per Current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP).']
  measurement_frequency varchar [note: 'The required frequency or timing for measuring this parameter during batch execution. Defines sampling plan and monitoring requirements per the approved batch record. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: continuous|per_batch|hourly|per_shift|daily|on_demand|at_process_step — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'The method by which this parameter is measured or recorded. Indicates whether data is captured through Process Analytical Technology (PAT), manual operator entry, or laboratory testing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manual_entry|automated_sensor|pat_instrument|laboratory_analysis|inline_measurement|atline_measurement|offline_measurement — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this parameter measurement was recorded. Critical for establishing batch record chronology and supporting 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.']
  parameter_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the parameter within the manufacturing process. Used for cross-referencing with Manufacturing Execution System (MES) and Process Analytical Technology (PAT) instruments.']
  parameter_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the process parameter (e.g., Mixing Temperature, Compression Force, pH Level, Dissolution Rate).']
  parameter_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the parameter based on its impact on product quality. Critical Process Parameters (CPPs) have direct impact on Critical Quality Attributes (CQAs) and require stringent control per Quality by Design (QbD) principles.']
  parameter_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the parameter specification. Incremented when control limits, specification limits, or other parameter attributes are changed through the change control process. Supports regulatory change history requirements.']
  pat_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this parameter is monitored using Process Analytical Technology (PAT) instruments for real-time or near-real-time measurement and control. True if PAT is implemented for this parameter.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether deviations or out-of-specification results for this parameter must be reported to regulatory authorities (FDA, EMA, etc.) as part of annual product reviews or post-approval change protocols.']
  target_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The intended setpoint or target value for this parameter as defined in the master batch record or process development documentation. Represents the optimal operating point.']
  upper_control_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The upper boundary of the normal operating range for this parameter. Values above this trigger investigation but may not require batch rejection. Used for statistical process control and continuous process verification.']
  upper_specification_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The maximum acceptable value for this parameter as defined in regulatory filings and approved specifications. Values above this limit may result in batch rejection or deviation investigation per Current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP).']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this parameter measurement was reviewed and verified by Quality Assurance (QA) or supervisory personnel. Part of the batch record review and release process.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Quality Assurance (QA) or supervisory personnel who reviewed and verified this parameter measurement. Required for Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) batch record review.']

  Note: 'Defines and records Critical Process Parameters (CPPs) and non-critical process parameters for manufacturing processes, including both the specification (target values, control limits, specification limits) from the master batch record and the actual measured values recorded during batch execution from MES, PAT instruments, or manual entry. Captures parameter name, type (CPP vs non-critical), process step, target value, control/specification limits, unit of measure, measurement frequency, PAT indicator, and per-batch actual measurements with timestamps, instrument IDs, and pass/fail status. Supports QbD principles, process validation, CPV trending, and regulatory inspection readiness per ICH Q8/Q9/Q10.'
}

Table manufacturing.process_parameter_result {
  process_parameter_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the process parameter result record. Primary key for this entity.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing batch in which this parameter was measured. Links to the batch master record.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the instrument, sensor, or equipment used to capture the measurement. Links to equipment master for calibration and qualification tracking.']
  manufacturing_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the deviation or OOS investigation record if this measurement triggered a deviation. Null if no deviation occurred.']
  process_parameter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parameter definition in the master batch record or parameter library. Links to the parameter specification.']
  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: Process parameter results require standardized UoM for statistical process control (SPC), OOS investigations, and regulatory batch record review. unit_of_measure is a plain string; normalizing to mast']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable batch number or lot number for traceability and regulatory reporting. Denormalized for query performance.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or observations recorded by the operator or system regarding the measurement, including any contextual notes or anomalies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this process parameter result record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'System or source from which the measurement data originated: MES (Manufacturing Execution System), SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition), LIMS (Laboratory Information Management System), Manual Entry, PAT System, ELN (Electronic Lab Notebook).']
  deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement triggered a process deviation or Out of Specification (OOS) event requiring investigation and CAPA.']
  evaluation_result varchar [note: 'Result of comparing the measured value against specification limits: Pass (within limits), Fail (out of specification), Warning (approaching limits), Not Evaluated (no limits defined).']
  instrument_code varchar [note: 'Human-readable code or tag number of the instrument or equipment (e.g., TMP-101, PRS-205). Denormalized for traceability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this process parameter result record was last modified. Audit trail field for change tracking.']
  lower_control_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower acceptable limit for the parameter as defined in the master batch record or process specification. Used for pass/fail evaluation.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Actual measured value of the process parameter as recorded during batch execution. This is the primary result field.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the parameter was measured: Manual (operator entry), Automated (MES/SCADA), PAT (real-time analyzer), Laboratory (offline test).']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the parameter measurement was taken or recorded. Critical for batch chronology and process validation.']
  operator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the operator or technician who recorded the measurement. Denormalized for audit and compliance reporting.']
  pat_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement was captured using Process Analytical Technology (PAT) real-time monitoring tools per FDA PAT guidance.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the parameter result record: Active (current and valid), Superseded (replaced by corrected value), Voided (invalidated), Archived (historical, no longer active).']
  sampling_point varchar [note: 'Physical location or sampling point within the process equipment where the measurement was taken (e.g., Inlet, Outlet, Mid-Point, Probe Location).']
  target_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Target or setpoint value for the parameter as defined in the master batch record. Used for comparison and deviation detection.']
  test_method_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the validated test method or Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) used for the measurement (e.g., SOP-QC-1234, USP Method 621).']
  upper_control_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper acceptable limit for the parameter as defined in the master batch record or process specification. Used for pass/fail evaluation.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the measurement verification by a supervisor or quality assurance: Pending (awaiting review), Verified (approved), Rejected (invalid), Under Review (investigation in progress).']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the measurement was verified or approved by a supervisor or quality assurance personnel.']

  Note: 'Actual measured values of critical process parameters (CPPs), non-critical process parameters, and in-process control (IPC) results recorded during batch manufacturing execution. Captured from MES, PAT instruments, SCADA systems, or manual entry in the electronic batch record. Captures batch reference, process step, parameter name, parameter type (CPP, non-critical, IPC), measured value, unit of measure, measurement timestamp, measurement method, instrument ID, operator ID, pass/fail evaluation against specification limits defined in the master batch record, deviation flag, and PAT indicator. This entity is the sole SSOT for all in-process parameter measurements — parameter specifications (targets, control limits, acceptance criteria) are owned by master_batch_record while this entity owns all actuals. Provides the complete CPP/CQA data trail for each batch, supporting QbD principles, process validation (Stage 2/3), continued process verification (CPV) trending, statistical process control, and regulatory inspection readiness per ICH Q8/Q9/Q10 and 21 CFR 211.188.'
}

Table manufacturing.manufacturing_deviation {
  manufacturing_deviation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the manufacturing deviation record. Primary key.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing batch affected by this deviation.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated CAPA record initiated to address the root cause and prevent recurrence.']

Business justification: Regulatory-reportable manufacturing deviations on DMF-covered processes require notification to the DMF holder and potentially the regulatory authority. This link enables identification of DMF amendme']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the drug product (DP) or drug substance (DS) affected by the deviation.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific equipment or instrument involved in the deviation, if applicable.']
  formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.formulation. Business justification: Deviations are tied to a specific formulation version (excipient out-of-spec, process step failure). Root cause analysis, CAPA, and regulatory reportable deviation assessments require knowing which fo']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Deviation investigation costs for clinical or validation batches are charged to project-specific internal orders for accurate project cost tracking and capitalization assessment.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.line. Business justification: Manufacturing deviations occur in specific production lines or suites. The current manufacturing_deviation table has a manufacturing_area (STRING) free-text field, but no structured FK to the line mas']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Regulatory reportable deviations (field alerts, annual product quality reviews) are tracked at the medicinal product level. Pharmacovigilance signal detection and APQR aggregate deviations by medicina']
  pv_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.pv_product. Business justification: At deviation triage, teams must assess regulatory reportability against the pv_product\'s safety profile (REMS, RMP risk minimization measures, expedited reporting thresholds). The existing deviation→i']
  quality_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: In pharma QMS, a manufacturing deviation (MES/shop-floor system) triggers a formal quality deviation record in the QMS (e.g., TrackWise). Cross-system traceability between manufacturing execution devi']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing site or facility where the deviation occurred.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable batch number or lot number associated with the deviation for traceability and reporting.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the deviation record was formally closed after all investigations, actions, and approvals were completed.']
  cmo_cdmo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the deviation occurred at a contract manufacturing organization (CMO) or contract development and manufacturing organization (CDMO) rather than an internal facility.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the deviation record was first created in the system.']
  detection_date date [note: 'Date when the deviation was first identified or detected.']
  detection_phase varchar [note: 'Manufacturing or quality control phase during which the deviation was detected: in-process (during production), packaging (during fill-finish or labeling), qc_testing (during in-process or final QC), release_testing (during batch release), stability_testing (during ongoing stability studies), post_release (after product distribution).']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the deviation was first identified, providing granular traceability for time-sensitive deviations.']
  deviation_category varchar [note: 'Secondary classification or sub-category providing additional granularity within the deviation type (e.g., temperature excursion, equipment calibration failure, material identity mix-up).']
  deviation_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the deviation, typically system-generated or manually assigned following organizational numbering convention. Used for external reference and tracking.']
  deviation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the deviation by root category: process (procedure not followed), equipment (malfunction or failure), material (raw material or component issue), environmental (cleanroom or facility condition), documentation (batch record or SOP error), personnel (training or human error).']
  immediate_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of containment or corrective actions taken immediately upon detection to prevent further impact or recurrence during the batch.']
  impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Evaluation of the deviations impact on product quality, safety, efficacy, and compliance. Includes assessment of affected batches, potential patient risk, and regulatory implications.']
  investigation_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the deviation investigation was completed and root cause determined.']
  investigation_start_date date [note: 'Date when formal investigation of the deviation was initiated.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the deviation record was last updated or modified.']
  manufacturing_deviation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the deviation event, including what occurred, when it was observed, and the circumstances surrounding the event.']
  manufacturing_deviation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the deviation: open (newly identified), under_investigation (root cause analysis in progress), pending_capa (awaiting corrective action), closed (investigation and actions complete), cancelled (determined not to be a deviation).']
  occurrence_date date [note: 'Estimated or known date when the deviation actually occurred, which may differ from detection date.']
  process_step varchar [note: 'Specific manufacturing process step or operation during which the deviation occurred (e.g., blending, granulation, tablet compression, sterile filtration, lyophilization).']
  product_disposition varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision for the affected batch or product: released (approved for distribution), rejected (destroyed or returned), reworked (reprocessed per approved procedure), quarantined (held pending further evaluation), pending_investigation (disposition not yet determined).']
  qms_system_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier from the source quality management system (MasterControl, TrackWise, or other QMS) for integration and traceability.']
  regulatory_report_date date [note: 'Date when the deviation was reported to regulatory authorities, if applicable.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the deviation must be reported to regulatory authorities (FDA, EMA, etc.) based on severity, product impact, or compliance requirements.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the identified root cause after investigation (e.g., human error, equipment failure, material defect, procedure inadequacy, environmental control failure).']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the root cause identified through investigation, including contributing factors and evidence supporting the conclusion.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Impact classification of the deviation: critical (direct impact on product quality, safety, or efficacy), major (potential impact requiring investigation), minor (no expected impact on product quality).']

  Note: 'Records unplanned departures from approved manufacturing procedures, specifications, or batch records during production. Captures deviation number, batch reference, deviation type (process, equipment, material, environmental, documentation), description, severity (critical, major, minor), detection date, detection phase (in-process, packaging, QC testing), root cause category, immediate containment actions, impact assessment, CAPA reference, closure date, and regulatory reportability flag. Integrates with MasterControl/TrackWise QMS. Distinct from quality CAPA records owned by the quality domain.'
}

  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. The existing medicinal_product FK is broader; dru']

Essential for API procurement, material master data, cost rollup, and linking manufacturing material requirements']
  formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.formulation. Business justification: BOMs operationalize formulations for manufacturing. Links formulation design (R&D) to manufacturing execution (production). Essential for change control, formulation version management, material requi']

Royalty calculation, licensed field-of-use compliance, and cost-']

The bill_of_materials table has master_batch_record_reference (STRING) which should be ']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: BOMs reference the output material (finished drug product or intermediate) being manufactured. SAP MM/PP BOM management requires material master linkage for costing, procurement planning, and GMP comp']

  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. This link is required for BOM approval workfl']
