Business justification: Supplier qualification involves testing incoming drug substances, excipients, and materials against approved specifications. The approved_material_scope text field captures scope broadly, but a direct']
  approval_workflow_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the supplier qualification approval workflow. Draft indicates initial creation; pending review indicates awaiting Quality Assurance (QA) review; QA review indicates under active review; QA approved indicates final approval granted; rejected indicates qualification denied.']
  approved_material_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the materials, drug substances, drug products, or components that the supplier is qualified to provide. Includes specific material codes, grades, and specifications covered under this qualification.']
  approved_service_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the services that the supplier is qualified to perform. Includes manufacturing operations, testing services, validation activities, clinical trial services, or other contracted services covered under this qualification.']
  audit_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Frequency in months at which the supplier site must be audited. Determined by risk tier and regulatory requirements. Critical suppliers may require annual audits; low-risk suppliers may be audited every 3-5 years.']
  capa_count_last_12_months varchar [note: 'Number of Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) records initiated for the supplier in the last 12 months. Indicates frequency of quality issues requiring formal corrective action.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, notes, or observations related to the supplier qualification. May include audit findings summary, conditional approval terms, or special handling instructions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this supplier qualification record was first created in the Quality Management System (QMS). Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance.']
  deviation_count_last_12_months varchar [note: 'Number of quality deviations reported by or attributed to the supplier in the last 12 months. Used as a key performance indicator for supplier quality oversight.']
  gmp_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Assessment of the supplier\'s compliance with current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP) regulations. Compliant indicates full adherence to GMP requirements; non-compliant indicates deficiencies identified; conditional indicates compliance with minor observations; under review indicates ongoing assessment.']
  gmp_responsibility_matrix varchar [note: 'Detailed allocation of Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) responsibilities between the pharmaceutical company and the supplier. Defines who is responsible for manufacturing, testing, release, stability, change control, deviation management, Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA), and regulatory reporting.']
  initiated_date date [note: 'Date on which the supplier qualification process was initiated. Marks the start of the qualification lifecycle.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent on-site or remote audit conducted at the supplier facility. Used to track audit schedule compliance and determine next audit due date.']
  last_audit_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent supplier audit. Approved indicates no significant findings; approved with observations indicates minor findings that do not impact qualification; conditional indicates findings requiring Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) before full approval; failed indicates critical findings resulting in disqualification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this supplier qualification record was last modified in the Quality Management System (QMS). Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next supplier audit. Calculated based on audit frequency and last audit date.']
  next_requalification_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next supplier re-qualification activity. Calculated based on re-qualification frequency and last qualification date.']
  oos_count_last_12_months varchar [note: 'Number of Out of Specification (OOS) results reported for materials or services provided by the supplier in the last 12 months. Critical quality metric for supplier performance.']
  performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative performance rating of the supplier based on quality metrics including on-time delivery, Certificate of Analysis (CoA) accuracy, deviation rate, Out of Specification (OOS) incidents, and Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) closure timeliness. Typically scored on a scale of 0-100.']
  qa_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the Quality Assurance (QA) review was completed and the supplier qualification was approved or rejected.']
  qualification_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the supplier qualification became effective and the supplier was approved for use under Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) operations.']
  qualification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the supplier qualification expires and re-qualification is required. Nullable for suppliers with ongoing qualification subject to periodic review.']
  qualification_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the supplier qualification record. Externally-known unique number assigned by the Quality Management System (QMS) for tracking and audit purposes.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the supplier qualification. Approved indicates full qualification; conditional indicates limited approval with restrictions; disqualified indicates supplier is not approved for use; pending indicates qualification in progress; suspended indicates temporary hold; expired indicates qualification period has lapsed.']
  qualification_type varchar [note: 'Method used to qualify the supplier. Audit-based indicates on-site or remote audit conducted; questionnaire indicates self-assessment questionnaire; Certificate of Analysis (CoA) review indicates qualification based on CoA evaluation; hybrid indicates combination of methods; risk-based indicates qualification approach scaled to supplier risk tier.']
  quality_agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quality agreement. Master indicates overarching agreement covering multiple products or services; product-specific indicates agreement for a single drug product or drug substance; service-specific indicates agreement for defined services; site-specific indicates agreement tied to a particular manufacturing or testing site.']
  regulatory_inspection_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority that conducted the most recent inspection. Examples include U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), European Medicines Agency (EMA), Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA), Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency (PMDA).']
  regulatory_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory inspection conducted by U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), European Medicines Agency (EMA), or other regulatory authority at the supplier facility.']
  regulatory_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Most recent regulatory inspection classification for the supplier facility. No Action Indicated (NAI) indicates no objectionable conditions; Voluntary Action Indicated (VAI) indicates objectionable conditions that do not meet threshold for regulatory action; Official Action Indicated (OAI) indicates objectionable conditions warranting regulatory action; not inspected indicates no recent regulatory inspection on record.']
  requalification_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Frequency in months at which the supplier must be re-qualified. Typical values are 12, 24, or 36 months depending on supplier risk tier and regulatory requirements.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification of the supplier based on the criticality of materials or services provided, regulatory impact, and supplier performance history. Critical indicates highest risk requiring stringent oversight; high indicates significant risk; medium indicates moderate risk; low indicates minimal risk.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this supplier qualification record originated. Typically MasterControl, TrackWise, or Veeva Vault QualityDocs.']
  supplier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supplier based on the materials or services provided. Contract Manufacturing Organization (CMO) manufactures drug product; Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) provides development and manufacturing; Contract Research Organization (CRO) conducts research and clinical trials; Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API) supplier provides drug substance; excipient supplier provides inactive ingredients; packaging supplier provides primary or secondary packaging materials; equipment supplier provides manufacturing equipment; service provider offers validation, testing, or consulting services. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cmo|cdmo|cro|api_supplier|excipient_supplier|packaging_supplier|equipment_supplier|service_provider — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Quality qualification, agreement, and ongoing oversight records for approved suppliers, CMOs, CDMOs, and CROs providing materials or services under GMP. Captures supplier qualification status (approved/conditional/disqualified), qualification type (audit-based, questionnaire, CoA review), approved material/service scope, quality agreement details (GMP responsibilities matrix, regulatory market coverage, agreement type, effective/expiry dates, review cycle, counterparty obligations), re-qualification schedule, risk tier classification, performance metrics, and approval workflow. Serves as the single quality record for supplier relationships — distinct from procurement vendor master. Sourced from MasterControl/TrackWise and Veeva Vault QualityDocs.'
}

Table supply.demand_plan {
  demand_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the demand plan record in the pharmaceutical supply chain planning system.']
  access_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.access_strategy. Business justification: Demand planning in pharma is directly driven by market access strategy — formulary coverage scope, reimbursement approval dates, and launch sequencing all define the addressable patient population. Su']
  atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: demand_plan.atc_code is a denormalized ATC classification. Linking to masterdata.atc_classification enables therapeutic-area demand aggregation and WHO ATC-based market analysis reports used in pharma']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: S&OP consensus demand planning in pharma is brand-driven. The demand plan must reference the brand it forecasts to reconcile commercial revenue targets with supply capacity. A pharma supply planner wo']
  brand_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand_plan. Business justification: The S&OP process explicitly reconciles demand plans against brand plan volume and revenue targets. Linking demand_plan to brand_plan enables consensus forecast approval workflows and plan-vs-actual va']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Demand plans are created at country-market level for market access and regulatory forecasting. A pharma demand planner always scopes forecasts by country to align with local regulatory and reimburseme']
  distribution_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_network. Business justification: A demand plan is a statistical/consensus forecast for pharmaceutical SKUs across specific distribution channels and network nodes. demand_plan has distribution_channel (string) but no FK to the distri']
  exclusivity_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.exclusivity_period. Business justification: Demand forecasts must incorporate exclusivity expiry dates (orphan, pediatric, data exclusivity) as these drive generic entry timing. Critical for commercial forecasting with regulatory exclusivity co']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: Demand plans for in-licensed products must reference the licensing agreement to apply contractual volume caps, minimum purchase obligations, and territory-specific demand constraints. Royalty calculat']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pharmaceutical product (Drug Product/Drug Substance) associated with this demand plan, enabling cross-SKU product-level demand aggregation.']
  patent_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: Demand planning for LOE scenarios requires patent family-level analysis (earliest/latest expiry across jurisdictions) to model demand cliffs accurately. A patent family spans multiple countries; plann']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Demand planning must account for patent expiry dates (LOE events) to forecast generic competition impact on volumes. Critical for commercial forecasting and LOE planning cycles in pharmaceuticals.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing or distribution site for which this demand plan is scoped, supporting multi-site supply network planning.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pharmaceutical SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this demand plan is generated. Covers both commercial and clinical supply SKUs.']
  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: demand_plan.uom is a denormalized plain-text UOM. Linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure enforces 3NF and enables consistent UOM conversion in supply planning reports. Pharma planners require validated']
  adjusted_forecast_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Demand quantity after commercial, medical affairs, or supply chain planner adjustments to the statistical baseline. Reflects market intelligence, launch events, tender wins, or clinical trial enrollment changes applied on top of the baseline.']
  baseline_forecast_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Statistically generated baseline demand quantity for the SKU in the forecast period, produced by the statistical forecasting engine (Kinaxis/Blue Yonder) using historical sales, shipment, or consumption data without manual override.']
  cold_chain_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU requires cold chain (temperature-controlled) logistics and storage throughout the supply chain. Critical for biologics, vaccines, and temperature-sensitive pharmaceutical products. Drives GDP-compliant distribution planning.']
  consensus_approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the demand plan was formally approved through the S&OP consensus process. Marks the transition to frozen/authoritative demand signal for supply execution. Compliant with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record requirements.']
  consensus_forecast_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final consensus-approved demand quantity agreed upon during the S&OP/IBP consensus meeting, representing the authoritative demand signal used for supply planning and inventory positioning. Populated only after plan_status reaches consensus_approved.']
  demand_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the demand signal by supply chain purpose: commercial (market sales), clinical (investigational product for trials), compassionate use (expanded access programs), sample (promotional/medical samples), or launch (pre-launch inventory build). Drives planning rules and inventory segmentation.']
  demand_signal_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the primary demand signal used to generate or adjust this forecast (e.g., Kinaxis RapidResponse statistical engine, Blue Yonder, SAP APO, manual planner input, CRM pipeline, market research, or tender/contract data). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kinaxis|blue_yonder|sap_apo|manual|crm|market_research|tender — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'The trade channel through which the pharmaceutical product reaches the end customer (e.g., wholesale distributor, direct hospital supply, retail pharmacy, specialty pharmacy, government/tender, export). Influences demand pattern and supply routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wholesale|direct|hospital|retail_pharmacy|specialty_pharmacy|government|export|e_commerce — promote to reference product]']
  forecast_accuracy_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Measured forecast accuracy for the prior equivalent period, expressed as a percentage (0–100). Calculated as 1 minus the Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) for the SKU-period. Used to assess planning reliability and trigger model recalibration.']
  forecast_bias decimal(18,4) [note: 'Systematic directional error in the forecast for the prior equivalent period, measured as actual minus forecast quantity. Positive bias indicates under-forecasting; negative bias indicates over-forecasting. Used for continuous improvement of demand planning accuracy.']
  forecast_granularity varchar [note: 'The time granularity at which the demand forecast quantities are expressed (weekly, monthly, or quarterly buckets). Determines how forecast_period_date is interpreted.']
  forecast_period_date date [note: 'The specific time bucket (month, week, or quarter start date) to which the demand quantities in this record apply. Each row represents one SKU-period combination within the planning horizon.']
  lot_size_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard manufacturing lot or batch size quantity for the SKU, used to convert demand quantities into production orders and assess capacity requirements. Expressed in the same UOM as forecast quantities.']
  ndc_code varchar [note: 'FDA National Drug Code (NDC) for the pharmaceutical product/package, used for US market demand planning, regulatory reporting, and supply chain traceability. Format: labeler-product-package (5-4-2).']
  override_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining why the adjusted forecast deviates from the statistical baseline (e.g., new tender win, product launch, competitor entry, clinical trial enrollment change, market withdrawal). Required when adjusted_forecast_qty differs from baseline_forecast_qty. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tender_win|product_launch|competitor_entry|clinical_enrollment|market_withdrawal|promotional_event|regulatory_change — promote to reference product]']
  plan_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this demand plan record was first created in the system, providing the audit trail start point for the planning record lifecycle.']
  plan_last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this demand plan record, supporting audit trail requirements and change management tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH Q10.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the demand plan, used for cross-system reference in Kinaxis RapidResponse, Blue Yonder, and SAP S/4HANA. Follows the enterprise demand plan numbering convention.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the demand plan record, tracking progression from initial statistical generation through consensus approval and freeze for supply execution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|consensus_approved|frozen|superseded — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the demand plan, enabling tracking of iterative revisions within a planning cycle (e.g., v1.0, v2.1). Supports version-controlled consensus planning workflows.']
  planner_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the demand planner documenting assumptions, market intelligence, adjustment rationale, or risk factors influencing the forecast. Supports S&OP review and audit trail documentation.']
  planning_cycle varchar [note: 'The cadence of the demand planning cycle in which this plan was generated (e.g., monthly S&OP cycle, quarterly business review, weekly rolling horizon). Determines the review and approval schedule.']
  planning_horizon_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the demand planning horizon covered by this plan. Defines the end of the forecast period, typically 12–24 months for pharmaceutical commercial planning.']
  planning_horizon_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the demand planning horizon covered by this plan. Defines the start of the forecast period for supply planning and inventory positioning.']
  reorder_point_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Inventory level at which a replenishment order should be triggered for this SKU, calculated from lead time demand plus safety stock. Used in SAP S/4HANA MRP and Kinaxis supply planning.']
  safety_stock_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum buffer inventory quantity maintained to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions for this SKU in the planning period. Expressed in the same UOM as forecast quantities. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM or Kinaxis.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Approved shelf life of the pharmaceutical SKU in days from the date of manufacture. Critical for demand planning to ensure inventory is consumed within expiry, minimizing write-offs and ensuring patient safety compliance.']
  statistical_model_type varchar [note: 'The statistical forecasting algorithm applied to generate the baseline forecast (e.g., exponential smoothing, ARIMA, Croston, moving average, machine learning ensemble). Sourced from Kinaxis RapidResponse or Blue Yonder model configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: exponential_smoothing|arima|croston|moving_average|holt_winters|ml_ensemble — promote to reference product]']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum permitted storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this pharmaceutical SKU. Used in conjunction with minimum storage temperature to define the acceptable temperature range for GDP-compliant cold chain management.']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum required storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this pharmaceutical SKU. Used for cold chain planning, GDP compliance, and distribution network configuration (e.g., -80°C for mRNA vaccines, 2–8°C for biologics).']
  supply_chain_tier varchar [note: 'Identifies the supply chain tier to which this demand plan applies: Drug Substance (DS/API), Drug Product (DP/FDF), Finished Goods (packaged product), or Packaging Components. Enables multi-tier demand disaggregation from finished goods back to API and raw materials.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'The therapeutic area classification of the pharmaceutical product (e.g., oncology, immunology, rare diseases, cardiovascular, neurology). Used for demand segmentation and portfolio-level planning analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oncology|immunology|rare_diseases|cardiovascular|neurology|infectious_disease|metabolic — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Statistical and consensus-based demand forecast for pharmaceutical SKUs across commercial and clinical supply chains. Captures baseline forecast, adjusted forecast, consensus-approved quantities, planning horizon, and demand signals from Kinaxis RapidResponse/Blue Yonder. Tracks forecast version, approval status, planning cycle, and demand classification (commercial, clinical, compassionate use). Serves as the authoritative demand signal for supply planning and inventory positioning.'
}

Table supply.plan {
  plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the supply plan record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  access_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.access_strategy. Business justification: Supply plans are built against market access milestones — planned launch dates, covered markets, and formulary tier targets from the access strategy determine production timing and quantities. S&OP an']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Supply plans are created per brand to meet commercial demand forecasts and launch timelines. Links manufacturing capacity planning to brand lifecycle stage, LOE dates, and promotional spend budgets fo']
  brand_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand_plan. Business justification: Supply plans must be built against a specific brand plan version to ensure production quantities align with commercial revenue and volume targets. S&OP reconciliation and capacity planning reports req']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: S&OP budget alignment: supply plans drive production and procurement spend that must reconcile with approved budgets during Sales & Operations Planning. Finance validates supply plan quantities and co']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: Supply plans are linked to cost centers for financial planning, budget allocation, and manufacturing cost tracking. Pharma finance teams require plan-to-cost-center linkage for standard costing and va']
  demand_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand plan record that drives this supply plan. Links supply plan to the corresponding demand forecast from the S&OP process, enabling demand-supply reconciliation and gap analysis.']
  distribution_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_network. Business justification: A supply plan (constrained/unconstrained) targets a specific distribution network node for receipt or deployment of drug substance, drug product, or finished goods. plan already has storage_location_i']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pharmaceutical product (drug substance, drug product, or FDF) covered by this supply plan. Links to the master product/material record in SAP MM or the product master data product.']
  exclusivity_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.exclusivity_period. Business justification: Supply planning aligns production with exclusivity periods to optimize inventory before generic competition. Essential for lifecycle supply planning and avoiding excess inventory at exclusivity expiry']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Supply plans are owned by a legal entity for intercompany transfer pricing, financial consolidation, and GMP regulatory compliance. Pharma supply planners must attribute plans to the responsible legal']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: Supply plans for in-licensed or out-licensed products must reference the licensing agreement to enforce supply obligations, minimum batch commitments, territory restrictions, and diligence obligations']
  packaging_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.packaging_configuration. Business justification: Production plans in pharma must specify the packaging configuration to schedule packaging lines, procure primary/secondary materials, and plan serialization capacity. A supply planning expert expects ']
  patent_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: Supply plans are built with LOE scenario planning requiring patent family-level analysis — the family\'s latest expiry date across all jurisdictions determines the true supply planning horizon. Individ']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Supply plans reference patents to align production capacity with exclusivity periods and avoid overproduction pre-LOE. Essential for capacity planning around patent cliffs and inventory optimization.']
  plan_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing site (internal or CMO/CDMO) responsible for producing the planned quantity. Links to the site master data record. Supports GDP compliance and site-level capacity planning.']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.discovery_project. Business justification: Manufacturing capacity planning and tech transfer activities require visibility to pipeline projects for scale-up planning, site selection, and supply network design. Essential for phase-appropriate s']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Supply plans in pharma are executed at SKU level for market-specific production scheduling, launch planning, and commercial supply commitments. sku_code is a denormalized plain attribute; FK to sku en']
  source_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating site for transfer supply type plans. Identifies the site from which material will be transferred to the receiving/destination site. Relevant for inter-site and inter-company supply transfers.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the receiving site or distribution center for transfer supply type plans. Identifies where the material will be received after inter-site transfer or CMO/CDMO delivery.']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.trial. Business justification: Supply plans are created for specific clinical trials. A direct trial_id FK on supply.plan enables trial-level supply planning reports, capacity allocation by trial, and S&OP integration where trial s']
  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: plan.quantity_uom is a denormalized plain-text UOM. Linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure enforces 3NF and ensures validated UOM master data is used in production planning and regulatory batch records']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supply plan was formally approved by the authorized supply chain planner or manager. Part of the electronic audit trail required under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH Q10.']
  batch_size decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard or planned batch size for the manufacturing run, expressed in the quantity UOM. Critical for GMP compliance, batch record generation, and capacity utilization calculations. Aligns with the validated batch size in the manufacturing process.']
  capacity_constraint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this supply plan record is constrained by manufacturing capacity limitations (True) or represents an unconstrained theoretical plan (False). Distinguishes constrained from unconstrained plan scenarios.']
  capacity_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of available manufacturing capacity consumed by this supply plan at the designated manufacturing site. Used for capacity management, bottleneck identification, and CMO contract utilization tracking.']
  cold_chain_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the planned material requires cold-chain storage and transportation (True) to maintain product integrity. Triggers cold-chain logistics requirements in GDP-compliant distribution planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supply plan record was first created in the source planning system (Kinaxis RapidResponse, Blue Yonder, or SAP PP). Part of the audit trail for plan lifecycle tracking.']
  gdp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supply plan has been reviewed and confirmed as compliant with Good Distribution Practice (GDP) requirements for the planned distribution lanes and storage conditions.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supply plan record was most recently modified in the source planning system. Supports change tracking, plan versioning, and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Total planned lead time in calendar days from production start (or purchase order placement) to material availability at the destination site. Includes manufacturing, QC release, and logistics transit time. Used for MRP scheduling.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pharmaceutical material covered by this supply plan: Drug Substance (DS), Drug Product (DP), Finished Dosage Form (FDF), Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API), excipient, or packaging component. Aligns with CMC categorization.']
  ndc_code varchar [note: 'National Drug Code assigned by the FDA uniquely identifying the drug product, labeler, and package size. Required for US commercial supply planning and distribution. Applicable to FDF and DP material types.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for supply planners to document assumptions, constraints, risk factors, or exceptions associated with this supply plan record. Supports S&OP decision documentation and audit trail.']
  number_of_batches varchar [note: 'Total number of manufacturing batches planned within the planning horizon for this supply plan record. Used for capacity loading, CMO scheduling, and GMP batch record planning.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the supply plan, sourced from Kinaxis RapidResponse, Blue Yonder, or SAP PP (e.g., SP-2024-00123). Used for cross-system traceability and communication with CMOs/CDMOs.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the supply plan. Draft indicates work-in-progress; approved indicates sign-off by supply chain leadership; frozen indicates locked for execution; superseded indicates replaced by a newer version; cancelled indicates plan is void.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supply plan indicating whether it reflects capacity and material constraints (constrained) or theoretical demand without constraints (unconstrained). Consensus plan represents the agreed S&OP output. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: constrained|unconstrained|consensus|rough_cut|master — promote to reference product]']
  planned_production_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total planned production or procurement quantity for the material within the planning horizon, expressed in the base unit of measure (e.g., kg for DS/API, units for FDF/DP). Core quantitative output of the supply plan.']
  planned_production_start_date date [note: 'The scheduled start date for the manufacturing or procurement activity. Used for capacity planning, CMO scheduling, and lead time management. Derived from planned receipt date minus production lead time.']
  planned_receipt_date date [note: 'The date on which the planned quantity is expected to be received at the destination site or made available in inventory. Used for inventory availability calculations and demand fulfillment scheduling.']
  planning_cycle varchar [note: 'Identifier for the S&OP or supply planning cycle in which this plan was generated (e.g., \'2024-Q3-Cycle2\', \'Monthly-2024-09\'). Enables tracking of plan evolution across planning cycles and version comparison.']
  planning_horizon_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the planning horizon covered by this supply plan. Defines the latest date for which planned production or receipt quantities are captured. Typically 12–36 months for pharmaceutical supply planning.']
  planning_horizon_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the planning horizon covered by this supply plan. Defines the earliest date for which planned production or receipt quantities are captured. Typically aligned with the S&OP cycle.']
  reorder_point_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Inventory level at which a replenishment order is triggered for the material at the destination site. Used in SAP MM MRP reorder point planning to ensure continuous supply availability.']
  safety_stock_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Planned safety stock quantity for the material at the destination site, expressed in the quantity UOM. Represents the buffer inventory maintained to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions. Configured in SAP MM MRP.']
  scenario varchar [note: 'Scenario classification for the supply plan, supporting what-if analysis and risk planning. Base is the primary plan; optimistic and pessimistic reflect demand upside/downside; risk_adjusted incorporates supply risk mitigations.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Remaining shelf life in calendar days for the planned material at the time of planned receipt. Critical for pharmaceutical supply planning to ensure product reaches patients with adequate remaining shelf life per GDP and customer requirements.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage temperature condition for the planned material (e.g., ambient 15-25°C, refrigerated 2-8°C, frozen -20°C, ultra-frozen -80°C). Drives cold-chain logistics and warehouse configuration requirements.']
  supply_chain_segment varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the supply plan pertains to the commercial supply chain or the clinical trial supply chain. Clinical supply plans are subject to IND/CTA requirements and GCP; commercial plans are subject to GMP and GDP.']
  supply_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the sourcing strategy for the planned supply: make (internal manufacturing), buy (external procurement from CMO/CDMO), or transfer (inter-site or inter-company stock transfer). Drives MRP logic in SAP PP and Kinaxis.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the supply plan (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Supports plan versioning and comparison between constrained and unconstrained scenarios across planning cycles.']

  Note: 'Constrained and unconstrained supply plan for drug substance (DS), drug product (DP), and finished dosage form (FDF) across manufacturing sites and CMOs/CDMOs. Captures planned production quantities, planned receipt dates, supply source, planning horizon, capacity constraints, and plan version. Integrates with Kinaxis RapidResponse/Blue Yonder and SAP PP. Tracks plan status (draft, approved, frozen) and supply type (make, buy, transfer).'
}

Table supply.inventory_lot {
  inventory_lot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each lot/batch-level inventory record in the pharmaceutical supply chain. Primary key for the inventory_lot data product.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Inventory valuation, expiry management, and revenue recognition in pharma require brand-level aggregation. Enables commercial finance to track inventory by brand for gross-to-net calculations, sample ']
  drug_master_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.drug_master_file. Business justification: Each inventory lot of an API or drug product is manufactured under a specific DMF. QA lot release and disposition decisions reference the DMF to confirm GMP compliance and manufacturing site authoriza']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Inventory lots represent physical stock of specific drug products (formulation+strength+dosage form). Essential for batch traceability, GDP compliance, recall execution, and regulatory lot disposition']
  investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.investigator. Business justification: GCP drug accountability logs (required by ICH E6 and FDA 21 CFR Part 312) track which investigator received, used, and returned each clinical supply lot. inventory_lot has is_clinical_supply flag but ']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Every inventory lot must reference its material master for specifications, regulatory data, storage requirements, quality parameters. Material_number is denormalized identifier; FK enforces referentia']
  packaging_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.packaging_configuration. Business justification: Lots are manufactured in specific packaging configurations. Required for serialization hierarchy (GTIN/NDC mapping), regulatory compliance reporting, and distribution channel planning where package si']
  patient_access_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.patient_access_program. Business justification: Inventory lots designated for patient access programs (free goods, compassionate use stock) are segregated and tracked separately for program cost accounting, regulatory reporting, and benefit cap man']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.plan. Business justification: An inventory lot is created as a result of executing a supply plan (planned production or planned receipt). Linking inventory_lot.plan_id → plan.plan_id connects the physical lot to the supply plan th']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: Inventory lots are held at plants requiring plant master data for GDP authorization, GMP status, capacity management, regulatory inspections. Plant_code is denormalized identifier; FK enforces referen']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: GMP batch traceability and recall management require tracing any inventory lot back to the production order that created it. FDA 21 CFR Part 211 and EU GMP Annex 15 mandate full batch genealogy. No ex']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Inventory lots in pharma are managed at SKU level for market-specific stock allocation, reimbursement tracking, and serialization compliance. sku_code is a denormalized plain attribute; FK to sku enab']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.storage_location. Business justification: Lots are stored at specific storage locations requiring master data for GDP compliance, capacity management, temperature zones, DEA authorization. Storage_location_code is denormalized identifier; FK ']
  supplier_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Pharma GMP requires traceability of each inventory lot to its supplier for quality investigations, CAPA management, and supplier qualification audits. inventory_lot.supplier_lot_number implies a suppl']
  transfer_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.transfer_price. Business justification: Intercompany inventory valuation: when lots are transferred between legal entities, the approved transfer price governs the intercompany cost applied to that lot. Finance and tax teams reconcile lot u']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.trial. Business justification: Clinical inventory lots (is_clinical_supply=true) are allocated to specific trials. Linking inventory_lot to trial supports IMP accountability reconciliation, expiry management per trial, and regulato']
  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: inventory_lot.unit_of_measure is a denormalized plain-text UOM. Linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure enforces 3NF and ensures GMP-compliant UOM validation for batch release and regulatory submissions']
  capa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the associated Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record in the Quality Management System (QMS) if this lot is subject to an active quality investigation or deviation. Links to MasterControl or TrackWise QMS.']
  coa_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Certificate of Analysis (CoA) issued by the manufacturer or supplier for this lot, confirming that the lot meets all specified quality attributes and release criteria. Required for GMP compliance and regulatory inspections.']
  cold_chain_break_date date [note: 'Date on which a cold chain break or temperature excursion was first detected for this lot. Populated only when temperature_excursion_flag is True. Used to initiate quality impact assessment and determine lot disposition.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the unit_cost valuation (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Enables multi-currency inventory valuation reporting across global manufacturing and distribution sites.']
  days_until_expiry varchar [note: 'Number of days remaining until the lot\'s expiry date, calculated at the time of data extraction. Used for near-expiry inventory alerts, FEFO prioritization, and supply chain risk management. Negative values indicate expired lots.']
  dea_schedule varchar [note: 'DEA controlled substance schedule classification (I through V) for this lot, if applicable. Determines the level of security, storage, and record-keeping requirements. Null for non-controlled substances.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the lot is no longer suitable for use, as determined by stability studies and approved shelf-life specifications. Critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory management, GDP compliance, and regulatory reporting. Corresponds to SAP batch expiry date (VFDAT field).']
  is_clinical_supply boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this lot is designated for use in clinical trials (investigational product supply) rather than commercial distribution. Clinical supply lots are subject to additional regulatory controls under GCP and IND/CTA requirements.']
  is_controlled_substance boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this lot contains a DEA-scheduled controlled substance requiring additional security, storage, and regulatory reporting obligations under the Controlled Substances Act.']
  is_gdp_compliant boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this lot\'s storage and handling at the current location is confirmed compliant with Good Distribution Practice (GDP) requirements, including temperature monitoring, security, and documentation standards.']
  last_movement_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent inventory movement (goods receipt, goods issue, transfer posting, or stock transfer) recorded for this lot in SAP. Used for slow-moving inventory analysis and supply chain performance monitoring.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned alphanumeric identifier for the production lot or batch. Enables full lot/batch traceability from raw material receipt through finished goods distribution. Corresponds to SAP batch number (CHARG field). Required for GMP compliance and recall management.']
  lot_status varchar [note: 'Current quality and disposition status of the lot in the inventory management system. Drives whether the lot is available for use, shipment, or must be held pending quality review. Maps to SAP batch status (unrestricted-use, quality inspection, blocked). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: unrestricted|quality_hold|quarantine|rejected|released|destroyed — promote to reference product]']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date on which the lot was manufactured or the production process was initiated. Used for shelf-life calculation, lot traceability, and regulatory reporting. Corresponds to SAP batch manufacturing date (HSDAT field).']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the material held in this lot. Distinguishes between Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API), excipient, packaging component, drug substance (DS), drug product (DP), and finished dosage form (FDF/finished good). Drives storage, handling, and regulatory requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: API|excipient|packaging_component|drug_substance|drug_product|finished_good — promote to reference product]']
  ndc_code varchar [note: 'FDA-assigned National Drug Code in 5-4-2 format uniquely identifying the drug product, including labeler, product, and package codes. Applicable to finished drug products and drug substances registered with the FDA. Null for raw materials and packaging components.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP purchase order number associated with the procurement of this lot from an external supplier. Enables financial reconciliation, goods receipt matching, and procurement traceability.']
  quantity_available decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net quantity of the lot available for new allocations, calculated as quantity_on_hand minus quantity_reserved minus quantity_on_quality_hold. Represents the actionable inventory for supply planning and order fulfillment.']
  quantity_on_hand decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total physical quantity of the lot currently in stock at the storage location, expressed in the base unit of measure. Represents the gross inventory position before reservations. Sourced from SAP inventory management (MARD/MCHB tables).']
  quantity_on_quality_hold decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the lot currently blocked due to quality inspection, Out-of-Specification (OOS) investigation, or quality hold pending Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) resolution. Not available for use or shipment.']
  quantity_reserved decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the lot that has been reserved for specific production orders, sales orders, or clinical trial supply requests but not yet physically consumed or shipped. Reduces available quantity for new allocations.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which the lot was received at the current storage location via goods receipt (GR) posting in SAP. Marks the start of the lot\'s residency at the site and triggers incoming quality inspection workflows.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory lot record was first created in the source system (SAP) or the data lakehouse. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and data lineage tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this inventory lot record in the source system or data lakehouse. Supports change data capture (CDC), audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11, and data freshness monitoring.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date on which the lot was formally released by Quality Assurance (QA) for use in manufacturing, clinical supply, or commercial distribution following successful completion of all required quality control testing and review.']
  retest_date date [note: 'Date by which the lot must be retested to confirm continued compliance with specifications before further use or distribution. Applicable primarily to APIs, drug substances, and excipients with defined retest periods per ICH Q7 and approved specifications.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing site, distribution center, or third-party logistics (3PL) location where this lot is physically held. Enables multi-site inventory visibility and GDP compliance tracking.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage temperature condition for this lot as specified in the product\'s approved labeling and storage requirements. Drives cold-chain logistics planning and GDP compliance. Values: ambient (15–25°C), refrigerated (2–8°C), frozen (-20°C), controlled_room_temp (20–25°C), ultra_low (-60 to -80°C).']
  supplier_lot_number varchar [note: 'Original lot or batch number assigned by the external supplier, Contract Manufacturing Organization (CMO), or Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO). Enables traceability back to the supplier\'s manufacturing records and Certificate of Analysis (CoA).']
  temperature_excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this lot has experienced a documented temperature excursion during storage or transit that may impact product quality and requires quality impact assessment per GDP and cold-chain management procedures.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard or moving average cost per unit of measure for this lot as recorded in SAP. Used for inventory valuation, COGS calculation, and financial reporting. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'SAP inventory valuation method applied to this lot for financial costing purposes. Determines how the lot\'s value is calculated in the general ledger (standard price, moving average price, or actual cost). Relevant for COGS reporting.']

  Note: 'Lot/batch-level inventory record for raw materials (APIs, excipients), packaging components, drug substance, drug product, and finished goods held at manufacturing sites, distribution centers, and 3PL locations. Captures lot number, NDC, SKU, material number, quantity on hand, quantity reserved, quantity available, storage location, storage condition (ambient, refrigerated, frozen), expiry date, retest date, lot status (unrestricted, quality hold, quarantine, rejected, released), and GDP compliance flags. SSOT for lot-level inventory traceability.'
}

Table supply.warehouse_receipt {
  warehouse_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the warehouse receipt record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  clinical_supply_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.clinical_supply_order. Business justification: warehouse_receipt has a clinical_supply_flag boolean indicating clinical supply receipts, but no FK to the clinical_supply_order that triggered the receipt. Adding clinical_supply_order_id normalizes ']
  distribution_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_network. Business justification: Warehouse receipts occur at specific distribution network nodes (plants, warehouses). The plant_code (STRING) should be replaced with distribution_network_id FK to distribution_network.distribution_ne']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Goods receipt in pharma requires drug product identification for QP release decisions, storage condition assignment, and GMP-compliant inspection lot creation. A pharma supply chain expert expects war']
  inventory_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: Warehouse receipts create or update inventory lot records upon material receipt. The batch_number (STRING) should be replaced with inventory_lot_id FK to inventory_lot.inventory_lot_id. Material attri']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Goods receipt must validate against material master specifications (storage conditions, quality inspection requirements, batch management rules). Critical for GDP compliance, COA verification, and qua']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: Goods receipts occur at specific plants requiring plant master data for GMP certification status, FDA establishment identifier, capacity validation. Essential for receipt authorization, quality inspec']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: A warehouse receipt is the physical confirmation of an inbound shipment arriving at a pharmaceutical manufacturing site, warehouse, or distribution center. Linking warehouse_receipt.shipment_id → ship']
  stock_transfer_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.stock_transfer_order. Business justification: A warehouse receipt at the receiving plant is the completion event for a stock transfer order. Linking warehouse_receipt.stock_transfer_order_id → stock_transfer_order.stock_transfer_order_id enables ']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.storage_location. Business justification: warehouse_receipt.storage_location is a denormalized plain-text field. Linking to masterdata.storage_location enables GMP-compliant goods receipt posting to validated storage zones and supports GDP co']
  supplier_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Warehouse receipts must be traceable to the supplying business partner for GMP supplier qualification, incoming inspection management, and purchase order reconciliation. No supplier FK currently exist']
  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: warehouse_receipt.unit_of_measure is a denormalized plain-text UOM. Linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure enforces 3NF and ensures validated UOM master data for GMP goods receipt documentation and reg']
  actual_receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time the physical materials arrived at the receiving dock or facility. Distinct from the SAP posting date; captures the real-world event time for GDP compliance, cold-chain monitoring, and traceability. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'The carrier\'s bill of lading number for the inbound shipment. Provides the legal transport document reference linking the receipt to the logistics carrier record. Used for freight claims, GDP transport documentation, and supply chain traceability.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'The name of the logistics carrier or third-party logistics provider (3PL) responsible for transporting the materials to the receiving site. Used for GDP carrier qualification tracking and freight performance management.']
  clinical_supply_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received material is designated for clinical trial supply (investigational product) rather than commercial supply. True = clinical trial supply subject to IND/GCP requirements; False = commercial supply. Determines applicable regulatory framework and handling procedures.']
  coa_reference varchar [note: 'The document reference number or identifier for the vendor\'s Certificate of Analysis (CoA) accompanying the shipment. The CoA confirms the material meets specification. Required for GMP compliance and regulatory traceability. Stored in Veeva Vault or LIMS document management.']
  coc_reference varchar [note: 'The document reference number for the vendor\'s Certificate of Compliance (CoC), confirming the material was manufactured in accordance with GMP and applicable regulatory requirements. Distinct from the CoA which covers analytical test results.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this warehouse receipt record was first created in the source system or data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'The vendor\'s delivery note or packing slip number accompanying the shipment. Used for physical receipt verification, discrepancy identification, and document archiving per GMP requirements.']
  gdp_compliance_status varchar [note: 'The GDP compliance check status for this receipt, confirming that the material was transported and received in accordance with Good Distribution Practice guidelines. \'compliant\' = all GDP checks passed; \'non_compliant\' = one or more GDP deviations identified; \'pending_review\' = GDP review in progress.']
  gr_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP Material Document number generated upon posting the goods receipt in SAP S/4HANA MIGO (transaction MIGO/MB01). Serves as the primary business identifier linking this receipt to the source ERP transaction. Format follows SAP material document numbering (e.g., 5000012345).']
  gr_document_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year associated with the SAP GR material document number. Required because SAP material document numbers are unique only within a fiscal year. Combined with gr_document_number to form the unique SAP key.']
  inbound_delivery_number varchar [note: 'SAP inbound delivery document number (transaction VL31N/VL32N) associated with this goods receipt, if applicable. Used when the receipt is triggered by an advance shipping notice (ASN) from the vendor. May be null for direct PO-based receipts.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'The SAP Quality Management (QM) inspection lot number automatically created upon goods receipt posting for materials subject to quality inspection. Links the warehouse receipt to the quality inspection process in SAP QM and LIMS. Null if no inspection is required.']
  po_line_item varchar [note: 'The specific line item number within the purchase order against which this goods receipt is posted. A single PO may have multiple line items for different materials or delivery schedules.']
  po_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The ordered quantity as specified in the purchase order line item. Used to calculate receipt variance (received vs. ordered) and to identify over/under deliveries requiring vendor notification or tolerance check.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting posting date of the goods receipt in SAP, determining the fiscal period in which the inventory and accounting entries are recorded. This is the principal business event date for this transaction.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'The SAP purchase order number against which this goods receipt is posted. Links the physical receipt to the procurement commitment. Used for three-way matching (PO, GR, invoice) in accounts payable.']
  quarantine_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received material has been placed in quarantine status pending quality inspection or investigation. True = material is quarantined and not available for use; False = material is not in quarantine. Triggers quality inspection workflow in LIMS and QMS.']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the warehouse receipt. \'posted\' = GR document successfully posted in SAP; \'reversed\' = GR has been reversed/cancelled; \'blocked\' = receipt blocked pending resolution; \'under_inspection\' = quality inspection in progress; \'released\' = inspection complete and stock released for use.']
  received_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual quantity of material physically received and counted at the receiving dock, expressed in the base unit of measure. Used for inventory posting, three-way matching, and discrepancy reporting against the PO quantity.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'The SAP stock type assigned to the received material upon posting. \'unrestricted\' = available for use/sale; \'quality_inspection\' = held pending QC inspection (default for GMP materials); \'blocked\' = blocked stock due to quality issue or hold; \'returns\' = returned goods stock.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'The required storage condition for the received material, determining the appropriate storage area and cold-chain handling requirements. \'ambient\' = room temperature; \'refrigerated\' = 2–8°C; \'frozen\' = -20°C or below; \'controlled_room_temp\' = 15–25°C; \'controlled_substance\' = DEA-regulated controlled substance requiring secure storage.']
  temperature_excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a temperature excursion was detected during transport or at the time of receipt. True = temperature excursion occurred (requires investigation and potential rejection); False = no temperature excursion detected. Critical for cold-chain materials (biologics, vaccines, temperature-sensitive APIs).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this warehouse receipt record was last modified in the source system or data platform. Tracks changes to status, inspection results, or corrections. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Record of physical receipt of materials at a pharmaceutical manufacturing site, warehouse, or distribution center against a purchase order or inbound delivery. Captures GR document number, posting date, material, lot number, received quantity, storage location, vendor, CoA reference, GDP compliance check status, temperature excursion flag, and quarantine status. Aligns with SAP MM goods receipt (MIGO). Triggers quality inspection and lot creation in LIMS.'
}

Table supply.goods_issue {
  goods_issue_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for the goods issue record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the system-generated identifier for each material outbound movement or consumption event.']
  clinical_supply_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.clinical_supply_order. Business justification: goods_issue has clinical_trial_number as a free-text string, but clinical supply issuances should be linked to the structured clinical_supply_order record. Adding clinical_supply_order_id → clinical_s']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: goods_issue.cost_center is a denormalized plain-text field. Linking to masterdata.cost_center enables accurate financial posting of material movements to validated cost centers for manufacturing cost ']
  delivery_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.delivery_order. Business justification: Goods issue documents are created as part of delivery order fulfillment. The delivery_document_number (STRING) should be replaced with a proper FK to delivery_order.delivery_order_id. This establishes']
  distribution_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_network. Business justification: Goods issue transactions occur at specific distribution network nodes. The plant_code (STRING) should be replaced with distribution_network_id FK to distribution_network.distribution_network_id. This ']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: GMP-relevant goods issues in pharma manufacturing must reference the drug product for batch traceability, cost accounting by product, and controlled substance movement reporting. Regulatory audits req']
  inventory_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: Goods issue transactions consume inventory from specific lots. The batch_number (STRING) should be replaced with inventory_lot_id FK to inventory_lot.inventory_lot_id. Material identifiers and descrip']
  issuing_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.storage_location. Business justification: goods_issue.issuing_storage_location is a denormalized plain-text field. Linking to masterdata.storage_location enables GMP-compliant goods issue posting from validated storage zones and supports GDP ']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Goods issue automatic accounting: every goods issue (material consumption, intercompany transfer, destruction) generates a journal entry for inventory cost accounting (COGS, write-offs). SAP MM-FI int']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Material issuance requires material master data for valuation, specifications, handling instructions, controlled substance schedules. Essential for inventory accounting, GMP compliance, and movement t']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.profit_center. Business justification: goods_issue.profit_center is a denormalized plain-text field. Linking to masterdata.profit_center enables accurate P&L attribution of material movements for segment reporting and intercompany transfer']
  receiving_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: Material issuances to receiving plants require plant master data for GMP status, capacity, regulatory authorization. Receiving_plant is denormalized code; FK enables plant qualification verification a']
  stock_transfer_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.stock_transfer_order. Business justification: A goods issue is the sending-side event for a stock transfer order — when materials leave the source plant/warehouse as part of an inter-site transfer. goods_issue has order_reference_number (string) ']
  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: goods_issue.unit_of_measure is a denormalized plain-text UOM. Linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure enforces 3NF and ensures validated UOM master data for GMP batch documentation and financial goods m']
  cold_chain_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the issued material requires cold-chain temperature-controlled handling and transportation (e.g., 2-8°C refrigerated, -20°C frozen, -80°C ultra-cold for biologics and mRNA products). When True, GDP cold-chain documentation including temperature monitoring records must accompany the shipment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the goods issue document was first created and posted in SAP (CPUDT + CPUTM in MKPF). Provides the precise audit timestamp for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance and GMP batch record documentation. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the material cost amount (WAERS in SAP). Represents the local currency of the issuing plant. Required for multi-currency financial consolidation and cross-border supply chain cost reporting.']
  destruction_certificate_number varchar [note: 'The certificate of destruction document number issued by a licensed waste disposal or pharmaceutical destruction contractor when materials are scrapped or destroyed (movement type 551/552). Required for GMP compliance, DEA controlled substance destruction reporting, and environmental regulatory compliance. Populated only for destruction goods issues.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date the goods issue document was physically created or the actual date of the material movement (BLDAT in SAP). May differ from posting_date when backdated postings occur. Used for audit trail and GMP documentation compliance.']
  gi_document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known SAP material document number assigned to the goods issue posting (e.g., 4900012345). This is the primary business identifier referenced in GMP batch records, cost accounting entries, and supply chain traceability. Corresponds to MBLNR in SAP MSEG/MKPF tables.']
  gi_document_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the goods issue material document was posted in SAP (MJAHR). Required in combination with gi_document_number to uniquely identify a material document in SAP MM.']
  gi_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the goods issue document. \'posted\' indicates the GI is active and inventory has been reduced; \'reversed\' indicates a reversal document has been posted (movement type 262/552/602); \'blocked\' indicates the document is on quality hold; \'cancelled\' indicates the document was voided prior to posting.']
  is_controlled_substance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the issued material is a DEA-scheduled controlled substance requiring additional regulatory documentation, DEA Form 222 or CSOS electronic ordering, and controlled substance inventory reconciliation. When True, the goods issue must comply with DEA 21 CFR Part 1304 record-keeping requirements.']
  is_gmp_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this goods issue is subject to Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) documentation and traceability requirements. True for materials used in GMP-regulated manufacturing, clinical trial supply, or quality testing. False for non-GMP materials such as office supplies, maintenance items, or non-regulated consumables. Drives GMP batch record inclusion and regulatory audit scope.']
  issued_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of material issued in the base unit of measure (ERFMG in SAP MSEG). Represents the actual amount consumed, shipped, scrapped, or transferred. Drives inventory reduction, cost accounting entries, and GMP batch record material consumption documentation. Must reconcile with production order component requirements or delivery quantities.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the goods issue record was last modified in the source system or Silver Layer. Tracks subsequent changes such as reversal postings, status updates, or data corrections. Required for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail completeness per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  material_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total cost value of the goods issue calculated as issued_quantity multiplied by the material valuation price at time of posting (DMBTR in SAP MSEG in local currency). Represents the COGS or cost allocation amount posted to the consuming cost center or production order. Critical for financial reporting, profitability analysis, and manufacturing cost tracking.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP MM movement type code that classifies the nature of the goods issue (BWART in MSEG). Drives inventory valuation, account determination, and GMP documentation requirements. Key values: 261=Goods issue to production order (consumption), 551=Scrapping/destruction, 601=Goods issue for delivery/customer shipment, 311=Transfer posting within plant. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 261|262|551|552|601|602|311|312|901|902 — promote to reference product]']
  movement_type_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the SAP movement type (e.g., \'Goods Issue to Production Order\', \'Goods Issue for Delivery\', \'Scrapping\', \'Sample Withdrawal\', \'Transfer Posting\'). Supports business reporting and GMP batch record documentation without requiring SAP configuration knowledge.']
  order_reference_number varchar [note: 'The reference number of the consuming or receiving order associated with the goods issue. For production consumption (movement type 261), this is the SAP production order number (AUFNR). For clinical supply, this is the clinical trial supply order number. For customer delivery, this is the SAP sales order number (VBELN). Central to GMP batch record traceability and cost allocation.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the order type associated with the goods issue. Determines the business process context: production_order/process_order for manufacturing consumption, sales_order for commercial customer delivery, clinical_supply_order for investigational product distribution to trial sites, transfer_order for inter-plant movements, destruction_order for controlled substance or expired material disposal.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting and inventory posting date of the goods issue (BUDAT in SAP). Determines the period in which inventory reduction and cost accounting entries are recorded. Critical for period-end closing, GMP batch record dating, and regulatory traceability.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'The SAP reason code (GRUND) providing additional classification for the goods issue movement, particularly for scrapping, destruction, or non-standard consumption. Examples include: expired material destruction, quality rejection scrap, clinical trial sample, regulatory submission sample, stability testing sample. Supports GMP deviation tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  receiving_storage_location varchar [note: 'For intra-plant transfer postings (movement type 311), the destination storage location within the same plant. Tracks material movement between warehouse zones, e.g., from quarantine to released stock, from bulk storage to dispensing area, or from cold-chain to ambient staging.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External or cross-system reference document number associated with the goods issue (XBLNR in SAP). May reference a purchase order, clinical trial supply request, destruction certificate, quality disposition document, or regulatory sample request. Enables cross-system traceability between SAP and CTMS, LIMS, or QMS systems.']
  reversal_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP material document number of the reversal posting (STBLG) if this goods issue has been reversed. A reversal restores inventory and reverses cost accounting entries. Populated only when gi_status = \'reversed\'. Required for GMP audit trail and financial period correction documentation.']
  special_stock_indicator varchar [note: 'SAP special stock indicator (SOBKZ) identifying whether the issued material belongs to a special stock category. Key values: E=Sales order stock (customer-specific), Q=Project stock, K=Consignment stock at customer, W=Vendor consignment stock. Empty value indicates standard own stock. Critical for clinical trial supply (project stock) and consignment management.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'The quality/availability stock type from which the material was issued (INSMK in SAP). Indicates whether material was issued from unrestricted (released) stock, quality inspection stock, blocked stock, or restricted-use stock. Critical for GMP compliance — goods issues from quality inspection or blocked stock require QA authorization and deviation documentation.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'The inventory valuation method applied to the goods issue for cost accounting purposes (BWTAR in SAP). Determines how the material consumption cost is calculated: standard cost (standard price from material master), moving average price (MAP), or actual cost. Impacts COGS calculation and financial reporting accuracy.']

  Note: 'Record of material consumption or outbound movement from inventory including issuance to production orders, clinical trial supply kits, sample distribution, destruction, quality testing, or customer delivery. Captures GI document number, posting date, material, lot number, issued quantity, movement type (production consumption, sales delivery, sample, scrap, transfer posting), cost center or order reference, and issuing storage location. Aligns with SAP MM goods issue (MIGO). Reduces inventory on hand and triggers cost accounting entries. Supports GMP batch record material consumption documentation.'
}

Critical for GDP-compliant transfers, serialization verification, and rec']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.plan. Business justification: Stock transfer orders in pharmaceutical supply chains are typically generated from constrained supply plans (e.g., Kinaxis RapidResponse or SAP APO planned orders). Linking stock_transfer_order.plan_i']
  receiving_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: Stock transfers to receiving plants require plant master data for GMP certification, capacity, DEA registration. Receiving_plant_code is denormalized; FK ensures receiving plant qualification, capacit']
  sending_distribution_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_network. Business justification: Stock transfer orders move materials between distribution network nodes. The sending_plant_code (STRING) should be replaced with sending_distribution_network_id FK to distribution_network.distribution']
  sending_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.storage_location. Business justification: stock_transfer_order.sending_storage_location is a denormalized plain-text field. Linking to masterdata.storage_location ensures the sending location is a validated GMP/GDP storage zone, required for ']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Stock transfer orders in pharma move market-specific SKUs between sites and distribution nodes. SKU-level identification is required for serialization chain-of-custody, market authorization compliance']
  transfer_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.transfer_price. Business justification: Intercompany STO pricing: stock transfer orders between legal entities require an approved transfer price for intercompany billing and tax compliance. Finance and supply chain jointly manage STO-to-tr']
