Linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure enforces 3NF and ensures validated UOM master data for interplant transfer documentation an']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason for cancellation of the stock transfer order. Populated only when transfer_status is \'cancelled\'. Supports root cause analysis and supply chain performance improvement.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the qualified logistics carrier or 3PL provider responsible for transporting the pharmaceutical materials between locations. Must be a GDP-qualified carrier for pharmaceutical shipments.']
  cold_chain_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pharmaceutical material requires cold-chain handling during transport (e.g., refrigerated 2-8°C or frozen -20°C/-80°C conditions). Triggers cold-chain logistics protocols, temperature monitoring requirements, and qualified carrier selection.']
  controlled_substance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material being transferred is a DEA-scheduled controlled substance. When true, additional regulatory controls apply including DEA Form 222 or CSOS electronic ordering, chain of custody documentation, and secure transport requirements.']
  dea_schedule varchar [note: 'Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) schedule classification for controlled substances (I through V). Null for non-controlled materials. Determines the level of regulatory control and documentation required for the transfer.']
  delivery_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP outbound delivery document number (transaction VL10) created from the stock transfer order for goods issue processing. Links the STO to the physical shipment execution in SAP WM/SD.']
  gdp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stock transfer order has been executed in full compliance with Good Distribution Practice (GDP) requirements, including qualified carrier use, temperature monitoring, chain of custody documentation, and deviation management.']
  goods_issue_date date [note: 'Actual date on which goods issue was posted at the sending plant, marking the departure of materials from the source location. Triggers inventory reduction at the sending plant and initiates in-transit stock.']
  goods_receipt_date date [note: 'Actual date on which goods receipt was posted at the receiving plant, confirming arrival and acceptance of the transferred materials. Triggers inventory increase at the receiving plant and closes the in-transit stock.']
  issued_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity of material for which goods issue (GI) has been posted at the sending plant. May differ from transfer_quantity in cases of partial goods issue. Corresponds to SAP movement type 351/641 posted quantity.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the stock transfer order record was last modified in the source system. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data loading in the lakehouse.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP inventory management movement type code used for the stock transfer. Key values: 301 (one-step plant-to-plant), 303/305 (two-step transfer order), 351 (goods issue for STO), 641/647 (goods issue with delivery). Determines accounting and inventory impact.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the stock transfer order. Critical transfers may relate to patient safety supply continuity, clinical trial supply, or shortage situations. Drives scheduling and resource allocation in supply chain planning.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason code explaining why the stock transfer was initiated. Examples include demand replenishment, safety stock replenishment, inter-company transfer, clinical trial supply, product recall, quality hold relocation, or returns processing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: demand_replenishment|safety_stock|inter_company|clinical_supply|recall|quality_relocation|returns|balance_stock — promote to reference product]']
  received_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity of material for which goods receipt (GR) has been posted at the receiving plant. May differ from issued_quantity due to transit losses, damage, or rejection. Corresponds to SAP movement type 101/105 posted quantity.']
  receiving_storage_location varchar [note: 'SAP storage location code within the receiving plant where the materials will be placed upon goods receipt. Identifies the specific warehouse area, cold-room, quarantine zone, or controlled substance vault at the destination site.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The date by which the receiving plant requires the materials to arrive. Used for supply chain planning, scheduling goods issue, and measuring on-time delivery performance. Corresponds to the SAP STO delivery date field.']
  shipping_condition varchar [note: 'Shipping service level or condition required for the transfer. Determines carrier selection, transit time expectations, and special handling requirements. Corresponds to SAP SD shipping condition configuration.']
  sto_creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the stock transfer order was created in the source system (SAP MM). Represents the business event timestamp of order initiation. Used for lead time analysis and supply chain performance reporting.']
  sto_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business document number assigned by SAP MM for the inter-plant or inter-site stock transfer order. Corresponds to the SAP Purchase Order number used for STO processing (document type UB or NB).']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'SAP stock type of the material at the sending location prior to transfer. Indicates whether the material is in unrestricted use, quality inspection (QI) stock, blocked stock, or restricted-use stock. Impacts GDP compliance and release status.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage and transport temperature condition for the pharmaceutical material. Drives cold-chain logistics planning and carrier qualification. Values: ambient (15-25°C), refrigerated (2-8°C), frozen (-20°C), deep_frozen (-80°C), controlled_room_temp (20-25°C).']
  supply_chain_type varchar [note: 'Distinguishes whether the stock transfer is part of the commercial supply chain or the clinical trial supply chain. Clinical trial transfers require additional traceability and compliance controls per GCP and IND/CTA requirements.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned tracking number or waybill number for the shipment associated with this stock transfer order. Enables real-time shipment visibility and chain of custody documentation for GDP compliance.']
  transfer_category varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the transfer based on the sending and receiving location types: plant-to-plant, distribution center to plant, plant to distribution center, DC-to-DC, or involving a third-party logistics (3PL) provider. Supports network-level supply chain analytics.']
  transfer_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the pharmaceutical material ordered for transfer in the base unit of measure. Represents the planned transfer quantity as specified in the STO line item.']
  transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stock transfer order. Tracks progression from creation through goods issue, in-transit, goods receipt, and closure or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|open|goods_issued|in_transit|partially_received|received|cancelled|blocked — promote to reference product]']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the transfer is a one-step transfer (goods issue and receipt posted simultaneously) or a two-step transfer (goods issue posted at sending plant, goods receipt posted separately at receiving plant). Aligns with SAP MM movement types 301/303/305.']

  Note: 'Inter-site or inter-plant stock transfer order for pharmaceutical materials between manufacturing sites, distribution centers, and 3PL locations. Captures transfer order number, sending plant/storage location, receiving plant/storage location, material, lot number, transfer quantity, transfer type (one-step, two-step), GDP compliance requirements, cold-chain requirements, and transfer status. Aligns with SAP MM/WM stock transfer order management.'
}

Table supply.shipment {
  shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the shipment record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the supply.shipment data product.']
  business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the logistics carrier or freight forwarder responsible for transporting this shipment. Maps to SAP SD forwarding agent (TDLNR).']
  clinical_supply_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.clinical_supply_order. Business justification: Shipments can fulfill clinical supply orders. This FK links shipments to the clinical orders they fulfill. No columns to remove as shipment doesn\'t currently have denormalized clinical order attribute']
  delivery_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the outbound or inbound delivery document associated with this shipment. Maps to SAP SD Delivery (LIKP/LIPS) or SAP MM Stock Transfer Order delivery.']
  destination_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: shipment.destination_country_code is a denormalized plain-text field. Linking to masterdata.country enables import permit validation, serialization requirement checks (DSCSA/EU FMD), and customs compl']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Pharma shipments require drug product traceability for GDP compliance, DSCSA/FMD serialization verification, and import/export regulatory declarations. Shipments can exist without delivery orders (e.g']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.investigational_site. Business justification: IMP shipments are delivered to specific investigational sites. Linking shipment to investigational_site enables GDP delivery confirmation per site, supports site-level IMP inventory reconciliation, an']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Shipping requires material specifications for packaging, handling, regulatory compliance (hazmat, controlled substances), temperature requirements. Essential for carrier selection, customs documentati']
  origin_distribution_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_network. Business justification: Shipments originate from distribution network nodes (plants, warehouses, DCs). The origin_location_code (STRING) should be replaced with origin_distribution_network_id FK to distribution_network.distr']
  patient_access_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.patient_access_program. Business justification: Shipments fulfilling compassionate use, expanded access, or free-goods patient access programs must be traceable to the program for cost accounting, regulatory reporting, and program budget reconcilia']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.patient. Business justification: Decentralized clinical trials ship investigational products directly to patients\' homes. Logistics systems must track destination patient for delivery coordination, temperature monitoring accountabili']
  payer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.payer_account. Business justification: Shipments to specialty pharmacies, PBMs, and direct payer channels must be tracked by payer account for contract compliance verification, chargeback reconciliation, and rebate claim validation. Essent']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Shipments in pharma are executed at SKU level for market-specific regulatory compliance (country-specific NDC, reimbursement codes). Direct clinical supply shipments may not have a delivery order, req']
  stock_transfer_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Stock Transfer Order (STO) when this shipment represents an inter-site transfer. Applicable for both one-step and two-step STO processing in SAP MM. Null for customer-facing outbound deliveries.']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.trial. Business justification: All IMP shipments are made under a specific trial. Linking shipment to trial enables supply operations to report total shipment activity per trial for IND annual reports, clinical supply cost reconcil']
  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: shipment.quantity_uom is a denormalized plain-text UOM. Linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure enforces 3NF and ensures validated UOM master data for customs declarations and GDP-compliant shipment doc']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Confirmed date the goods were received and accepted at the destination. Populated upon proof-of-delivery (POD) confirmation. Used for on-time delivery performance measurement and GDP compliance documentation.']
  cold_chain_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment requires temperature-controlled cold-chain logistics. True for biologics, vaccines, insulin, and other temperature-sensitive pharmaceutical products. Triggers mandatory temperature monitoring and GDP cold-chain documentation requirements.']
  controlled_substance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains DEA-scheduled controlled substances (e.g., opioids, Schedule II-V narcotics). Triggers DEA Form 222 or CSOS electronic order requirements and enhanced chain-of-custody documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment record was first created in the source system (SAP SD/MM). Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance. Corresponds to SAP ERDAT/ERZET fields.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Official customs entry or export declaration number assigned by the customs authority for cross-border shipments. Required for import/export compliance tracking and regulatory audit trails. Null for domestic shipments.']
  customs_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment crosses international borders and requires customs import/export documentation (commercial invoice, packing list, import license, health certificate). True for cross-border shipments.']
  destination_location_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier (SAP Plant code, DC code, customer account, or clinical site number) of the destination receiving the shipment. Used for delivery confirmation and traceability.']
  destination_location_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of destination receiving the shipment. Drives GDP compliance requirements, cold-chain monitoring thresholds, and customs documentation needs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: plant|distribution_center|3pl_warehouse|distributor|wholesaler|hospital|pharmacy|clinical_trial_site — promote to reference product]']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned or carrier-confirmed estimated date of arrival at the destination. Used for supply chain planning, cold-chain monitoring window calculations, and customer service commitments. Corresponds to SAP planned delivery date (LFDAT).']
  gdp_compliance_status varchar [note: 'GDP compliance assessment status for this shipment. \'compliant\' = no deviations detected; \'deviation_open\' = temperature excursion or handling deviation under investigation; \'deviation_closed\' = deviation resolved with CAPA; \'pending_review\' = awaiting QA assessment of monitoring data.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the shipment in kilograms, including packaging and pallets. Required for carrier freight billing, dangerous goods declarations (IATA/IMDG), and customs documentation.']
  hazardous_goods_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains dangerous goods (e.g., flammable solvents, cytotoxic agents, radioactive materials) subject to IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations (DGR) or IMDG Code. Triggers mandatory DG documentation and packaging requirements.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) defining the transfer of risk and responsibility between shipper and consignee. Determines who bears cost and risk during transit. Corresponds to SAP SD Incoterms (INCO1). Critical for customs valuation and insurance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'Named place or port associated with the Incoterms code, specifying the exact point of risk/cost transfer (e.g., \'Rotterdam\' for FOB Rotterdam). Corresponds to SAP SD Incoterms Part 2 (INCO2).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the shipment record. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail compliance. Corresponds to SAP AEDAT/AEZET fields.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot or batch number of the pharmaceutical goods being shipped. Critical for GMP traceability, recall management, and CoA linkage. Corresponds to SAP batch number (CHARG). Enables end-to-end lot traceability from manufacturing to patient.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country from which the shipment originates. Used for customs documentation, import/export compliance, and GDP lane qualification.']
  origin_location_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of origin location from which the shipment departs. Supports routing logic and GDP compliance tracking across the pharmaceutical distribution network.']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned Progressive (PRO) number or airway bill number (AWB) / bill of lading (BOL) number used to track the shipment within the carrier\'s system. Primary reference for carrier communication and proof-of-delivery retrieval.']
  serialization_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the drug product serialization data (2D DataMatrix barcodes at unit/case level) has been verified and reported to the applicable track-and-trace system (DSCSA in US, FMD in EU) prior to shipment. Required for commercial pharmaceutical distribution.']
  shipment_date date [note: 'Actual date the goods were physically dispatched from the origin location. The principal business event date for this transaction. Corresponds to SAP goods issue posting date (WADAT_IST). Used for GDP compliance, shelf-life calculations, and delivery performance KPIs.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the shipment, sourced from SAP SD (transaction VT01N/VT02N). Used for cross-system traceability and carrier communication. Corresponds to SAP Shipment Number (TKNUM).']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shipment. \'planned\' = shipment created but not yet dispatched; \'in_transit\' = goods picked up by carrier; \'delivered\' = confirmed receipt at destination; \'cancelled\' = shipment voided; \'on_hold\' = blocked pending resolution (e.g., GDP deviation, customs hold); \'returned\' = goods returned to origin.']
  shipped_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of goods dispatched in this shipment, expressed in the base unit of measure (UoM). For finished goods, typically in units (EA) or cases. For API/DS, typically in kg. Must reconcile with delivery document quantity.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable storage and transit temperature in degrees Celsius for this shipment. Defined by the drug product\'s approved storage conditions (e.g., 8°C for refrigerated, 25°C for controlled room temperature). Exceedances trigger GDP deviation investigations.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable storage and transit temperature in degrees Celsius for this shipment. Defined by the drug product\'s approved storage conditions (e.g., 2°C for refrigerated products, -20°C for frozen biologics). Used to configure temperature monitoring device alarms.']
  temperature_monitor_device_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the temperature data logger or monitoring device (e.g., Sensitech TempTale, Berlinger ELPRO) placed within the shipment packaging. Used to retrieve temperature excursion data post-delivery for GDP compliance review.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the goods movement. \'outbound_customer\' = commercial delivery to distributor/wholesaler/hospital/pharmacy; \'inter_site_one_step\' = direct stock transfer between plants; \'inter_site_two_step\' = STO with intermediate goods receipt; \'3pl_transfer\' = movement to/from third-party logistics provider; \'returns\' = reverse logistics; \'clinical_trial_supply\' = IMP shipment to clinical trial site. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: outbound_customer|inter_site_one_step|inter_site_two_step|3pl_transfer|returns|clinical_trial_supply — promote to reference product]']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transport used for this shipment. Drives cold-chain packaging requirements, transit time expectations, and customs documentation. \'air\' = air freight; \'road\' = truck/van; \'sea\' = ocean freight; \'rail\' = rail freight; \'courier\' = express parcel; \'multimodal\' = combination of modes.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'United Nations (UN) four-digit number identifying the hazardous substance or article being shipped (e.g., UN3373 for Biological Substance Category B, UN1170 for ethanol). Required on dangerous goods declarations when hazardous_goods_flag is True.']
  volume_m3 decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total volumetric measurement of the shipment in cubic metres. Used for freight cost calculation (chargeable weight), container/truck capacity planning, and cold-chain packaging validation.']

  Note: 'Physical movement record and SSOT for all pharmaceutical goods movements including outbound customer deliveries, inter-site stock transfers (one-step and two-step), 3PL movements, and returns. Captures shipment number, origin and destination (plant, DC, 3PL, distributor, wholesaler, hospital, pharmacy, clinical trial site), carrier, mode of transport, transfer type, NDC, lot numbers, shipped quantity, shipment date, estimated/actual delivery date, incoterms, cold-chain requirements (temperature range, monitoring device ID), GDP compliance status, customs/import documentation, and stock transfer processing status. Subsumes stock transfer order functionality — inter-site transfers are modeled as shipments with transfer_type indicating one-step or two-step processing. Aligns with SAP SD shipment management and SAP MM stock transfer processing.'
}

Table supply.delivery_order {
  delivery_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the outbound delivery order record in the pharmaceutical supply chain lakehouse (Silver Layer). Primary key for this entity.']
  business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the ship-to party (customer, distribution center, hospital, pharmacy, or clinical site) that is the physical destination of this delivery.']
  commercial_contract_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.contract_account. Business justification: Supply delivery orders should link to commercial contract accounts for customer shipments and order fulfillment. This enables integrated order-to-cash process and customer account management across co']
  distribution_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_network. Business justification: A delivery order is fulfilled from a specific distribution network node (warehouse or DC). No FK currently links delivery_order to distribution_network. Adding distribution_network_id → distribution_n']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: GDP-compliant delivery order processing requires direct drug product identification for controlled substance scheduling, FEFO compliance, and cold chain verification. A pharma logistics expert would e']
  inventory_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: Delivery orders are fulfilled from specific inventory lots. The batch_number (STRING) should be replaced with inventory_lot_id FK to inventory_lot.inventory_lot_id. Material identifiers, SKU, and expi']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pharmaceutical material (finished drug product, API, or packaging component) being delivered. Corresponds to SAP Material Master (MM60).']
  patient_access_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.patient_access_program. Business justification: Specialty pharmacy and free-goods delivery orders are fulfilled under patient access programs. Program cost accounting, compliance reporting, and benefit cap tracking require linking each delivery ord']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.plan. Business justification: A delivery order is the fulfillment execution document for a supply plan. Linking delivery_order.plan_id → plan.plan_id connects the outbound delivery to the supply plan that drove it, enabling plan-v']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Delivery orders in pharma are fulfilled at SKU level (market-specific pack, strength, country). sku_code is a denormalized plain attribute; replacing with FK to sku enables market-specific stock alloc']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse or storage location from which goods are issued. Corresponds to SAP WM storage location.']
  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: delivery_order.unit_of_measure is a denormalized plain-text UOM. Linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure enforces 3NF and ensures validated UOM master data for delivery documentation, FEFO compliance, a']
  actual_goods_issue_date date [note: 'The actual date on which goods issue was posted in SAP, representing the physical departure of pharmaceutical goods from the warehouse. Critical for lot traceability and GDP compliance.']
  clinical_supply_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delivery is for clinical trial investigational product (IP) supply rather than commercial distribution. Clinical supply deliveries require additional chain-of-custody and IND compliance documentation.']
  cold_chain_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delivery requires temperature-controlled cold-chain logistics (refrigerated or frozen transport). Triggers cold-chain carrier assignment and temperature monitoring requirements.']
  controlled_substance_schedule varchar [note: 'DEA schedule classification (I–V) for controlled substance pharmaceutical products in this delivery. Required for DEA 222 form compliance and controlled substance chain-of-custody tracking. \'not_applicable\' for non-controlled products.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery order record was first created in the source system (SAP SD). Used for audit trail and data lineage in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  delivery_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known SAP outbound delivery document number (e.g., 80XXXXXXXX) generated by SAP SD VL01N. Used as the primary business reference across warehouse, logistics, and customer-facing communications.']
  delivery_priority varchar [note: 'Priority classification for warehouse and logistics execution of this delivery. Emergency and clinical priorities trigger expedited picking, packing, and carrier assignment for patient safety or clinical trial continuity.']
  delivery_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of pharmaceutical product to be delivered as specified in the delivery order. Expressed in the base unit of measure (e.g., EA for each, BT for bottle, VL for vial).']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery order reflecting warehouse execution progress. Progresses from created through picking, packing, and goods issue posting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|picking_in_progress|picked|packing_in_progress|packed|goods_issue_posted|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  delivery_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the delivery document indicating the nature of the movement: outbound (commercial sale), returns (reverse logistics), stock_transfer (inter-site or inter-DC), clinical_supply (investigational product for clinical trials), or sample (promotional/medical samples). Aligns with SAP delivery type (LF, LR, NL, etc.).']
  fefo_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch/lot selected for this delivery complies with FEFO (First Expiry First Out) inventory rotation policy, ensuring the earliest-expiring stock is shipped first. Mandatory for pharmaceutical GDP compliance.']
  goods_issue_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity for which goods issue has been posted in SAP, representing the confirmed shipped quantity. Used for inventory reconciliation and revenue recognition.']
  goods_issue_status varchar [note: 'Status of the SAP goods issue (GI) posting for this delivery, which triggers inventory reduction and revenue recognition. Fully posted indicates stock has been legally transferred to the customer.']
  goods_issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when goods issue was posted in SAP SD, capturing the exact moment of inventory transfer. Required for cold-chain time-in-transit calculations and GDP audit trails.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the delivery in kilograms, including product, primary packaging, secondary packaging, and pallet. Required for freight booking, dangerous goods documentation, and carrier compliance.']
  handling_unit_count varchar [note: 'Number of handling units (pallets, cartons, shipper boxes) packed for this delivery. Used for freight cost calculation, carrier booking, and GDP packing documentation.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pharmaceutical product in this delivery is classified as a hazardous material (e.g., cytotoxic, controlled substance, radioactive pharmaceutical) requiring special handling, labeling, and transport documentation.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the transfer of risk and responsibility between seller and buyer for this delivery. Determines who bears cost and risk during transport. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the delivery order record in the source system. Supports incremental data loading and change data capture in the lakehouse pipeline.']
  packing_status varchar [note: 'Packing execution status indicating whether the picked pharmaceutical goods have been packed into shipping units (cartons, pallets, shipper boxes) for this delivery.']
  picked_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity of pharmaceutical product physically picked from warehouse storage bins. May differ from delivery quantity in cases of partial picks or stock discrepancies.']
  picking_status varchar [note: 'Warehouse picking execution status indicating whether stock has been physically selected from storage bins for this delivery. Corresponds to SAP WM transfer order status.']
  planned_delivery_date date [note: 'The scheduled date on which the pharmaceutical goods are planned to be delivered to the ship-to party. Used for supply chain planning and customer service level monitoring.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'SAP shipment document number (VT01N) associated with this delivery, representing the physical transport leg after goods issue. A single shipment may consolidate multiple delivery orders. Distinct from the delivery order which is the warehouse execution instruction.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage and transport temperature condition for the pharmaceutical product (e.g., refrigerated 2-8°C, frozen -20°C, ambient 15-25°C). Critical for cold-chain logistics planning and GDP compliance.']
  temperature_excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a temperature excursion (deviation from required storage condition) was recorded during the picking, packing, or pre-shipment staging of this delivery. Triggers quality investigation per GDP.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned tracking number (e.g., UPS, FedEx, DHL tracking ID) for the physical shipment associated with this delivery. Used for last-mile visibility and customer delivery confirmation.']
  volume_m3 decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total volume of the delivery in cubic metres. Used for freight capacity planning, cold-chain container sizing, and carrier booking.']

  Note: 'Outbound delivery document representing the fulfillment instruction for pharmaceutical finished goods against commercial orders or stock transfer requests. Captures delivery number, delivery type (outbound, returns, stock transfer), ship-to party, delivery date, material, lot number, delivery quantity, picking status, packing status, goods issue status, carrier assignment, and FEFO (First Expiry First Out) compliance flag. Aligns with SAP SD outbound delivery (VL01N). Represents the warehouse execution instruction — distinct from shipment which tracks the physical movement after goods issue.'
}

Table supply.distribution_network {
  distribution_network_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each node in the pharmaceutical distribution network topology. Serves as the primary key for this master resource record.']
  address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.address. Business justification: distribution_network has denormalized address, city, latitude, longitude plain-text fields. Linking to masterdata.address enforces 3NF and enables validated address master data for GDP site qualificat']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: distribution_network.country_code is a denormalized plain-text field. Linking to masterdata.country enables GDP authorization validation by country, distribution license requirement checks, and serial']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Distribution network nodes are operated by or contracted to a legal entity (own warehouse or 3PL entity). Pharma GDP compliance requires knowing the responsible legal entity for each node for regulato']
  manufacturing_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.site. Business justification: Supply network design and S&OP planning require knowing which manufacturing site feeds each distribution node. GDP compliance routing and capacity allocation depend on this site-to-network relationshi']
  operator_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Distribution network nodes operated by 3PL providers must be linked to the operator business_partner for GDP qualification management, contract compliance, and 3PL performance reporting. distribution_']
  clinical_supply_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node is qualified and authorized to handle clinical trial investigational medicinal products (IMPs) in addition to commercial supply. Clinical supply nodes must comply with GCP and GMP requirements.']
  cold_chain_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node holds a valid cold chain certification from a recognized body (e.g., IATA CEIV Pharma, GDP cold chain qualification). Required for handling temperature-sensitive biologics, vaccines, and specialty medicines.']
  controlled_substance_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node is authorized by the DEA (or equivalent national authority) to store and distribute controlled substances. Nodes without this authorization cannot be assigned to controlled substance distribution lanes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution network node record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.']
  dea_registration_number varchar [note: 'DEA registration number authorizing the node to handle Schedule I–V controlled substances. Required for US-based nodes handling controlled pharmaceutical products. Null for non-US nodes or nodes not handling controlled substances.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this distribution network node is scheduled to cease operations or be decommissioned. Null for currently active nodes with no planned end date. Used for network transition planning.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this distribution network node became or is scheduled to become operationally active in the supply network. Used for time-phased network planning and historical network topology analysis.']
  gdp_authorization_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the GDP wholesale distribution authorization expires. Supply planning systems must alert when expiry is approaching to prevent distribution through unauthorized nodes.']
  gdp_authorization_number varchar [note: 'Official GDP wholesale distribution authorization number issued by the competent regulatory authority (e.g., MHRA, national competent authority). Required for regulatory traceability and audit purposes.']
  gdp_authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current GDP authorization status of the node as required by EU GDP Guidelines (2013/C 343/01) and equivalent national regulations. Authorized nodes are eligible to handle medicinal products. Expired or suspended nodes must be excluded from active distribution lanes.']
  handling_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days required for internal processing activities at the node (receiving, quality inspection, pick/pack, dispatch preparation). Added to lead times in supply planning calculations.']
  hazmat_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node is certified to store and handle hazardous materials including cytotoxic compounds, flammable solvents, and other regulated substances. Relevant for oncology API and certain excipient handling.']
  inbound_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of calendar days required for inbound shipments to arrive at this node from its upstream supply source. Used as a planning parameter in Kinaxis RapidResponse and SAP APO/IBP for replenishment scheduling.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution network node record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  multi_temperature_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node can simultaneously handle multiple temperature zones (e.g., both ambient and refrigerated storage). Enables assignment of mixed-temperature product portfolios to a single node in supply network planning.']
  network_tier varchar [note: 'Hierarchical tier classification of the node within the distribution network topology. Tier 1 = primary manufacturing/central DC; Tier 2 = regional DC; Tier 3 = local/last-mile node. Drives network optimization and replenishment hierarchy in supply planning.']
  node_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this distribution network node across supply chain planning systems (Kinaxis RapidResponse, Blue Yonder, SAP APO/IBP). Used as the business key for cross-system integration and network lane definitions.']
  node_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the distribution network node (e.g., \'Frankfurt Regional DC\', \'Chicago 3PL Hub\', \'Singapore Manufacturing Site\'). Used in supply chain planning dashboards and network topology reports.']
  node_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the distribution network node. Inactive or suspended nodes are excluded from supply planning runs. Nodes under qualification are pending GDP or regulatory authorization review.']
  node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the network node by its functional role in the pharmaceutical supply chain. Drives planning logic, GDP compliance requirements, and lane eligibility in Kinaxis RapidResponse and SAP APO/IBP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manufacturing_site|distribution_center|3pl_node|wholesaler|direct_to_pharmacy|clinical_depot|hospital_pharmacy|specialty_pharmacy — promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing operational notes, special handling instructions, or qualifications relevant to this distribution network node. Examples include specific GDP deviation history, special import/export restrictions, or infrastructure constraints.']
  operator_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entity operating the distribution node. Distinguishes between internally operated sites and third-party operators such as 3PL (Third-Party Logistics), CMO (Contract Manufacturing Organization), CDMO (Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization), or wholesalers.']
  outbound_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of calendar days required for outbound shipments to depart this node and reach the next downstream node or customer. Used for delivery commitment and supply planning in Kinaxis RapidResponse.']
  pallet_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of standard pallets (EUR/ISO pallet) the node can accommodate simultaneously. Used for operational capacity planning and inbound/outbound throughput modeling in supply network planning tools.']
  quarantine_zone_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node has a dedicated physical quarantine zone for holding materials pending quality release, investigation, or returns processing. Required for GDP-compliant nodes handling pharmaceutical products.']
  region varchar [note: 'Internal geographic region classification for the node (e.g., \'EMEA\', \'APAC\', \'North America\', \'LATAM\'). Used for regional supply planning segmentation in Kinaxis RapidResponse and Blue Yonder.']
  sap_plant_code varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP plant code assigned to this distribution network node in SAP S/4HANA MM/WM/PP modules. Used for cross-system integration between supply planning tools (Kinaxis, Blue Yonder) and SAP ERP for inventory and production order management.']
  sap_storage_location_code varchar [note: 'SAP storage location code within the plant, representing the primary storage location for this node in SAP S/4HANA WM. Used for inventory posting, goods receipt, and goods issue transactions.']
  serialization_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node is equipped to scan, verify, and process serialized pharmaceutical packaging as required by DSCSA (US) and EU FMD (Falsified Medicines Directive) track-and-trace regulations.']
  storage_capacity_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total physical storage capacity of the node measured in cubic meters. Used for capacity planning, network optimization, and inventory allocation decisions in Kinaxis RapidResponse and SAP WM.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Primary temperature storage zone capability of the node. Defines the cold-chain handling classification: ambient (15–25°C), CRT (Controlled Room Temperature, 15–30°C), refrigerated (2–8°C), frozen (-20°C), or ultra-low temperature (-80°C). Critical for product-to-node assignment in supply planning.']
  warehouse_zone_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct storage zones configured within the node (e.g., quarantine zone, approved stock zone, returns zone, controlled substance vault). Reflects the physical warehouse layout complexity in SAP WM.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the pharmaceutical distribution network topology including manufacturing sites, distribution centers, 3PL nodes, wholesalers, direct-to-pharmacy lanes, and warehouse storage zones with bin/rack/zone configuration. Captures node type, node name, geographic region, GDP authorization status, cold-chain capability (temperature zones: ambient, CRT, 2-8°C, -20°C, -80°C), controlled substance handling authorization, storage capacity, physical warehouse layout, lead times, and network lane definitions. Used for supply network planning in Kinaxis RapidResponse/Blue Yonder and SAP APO/IBP.'
}

Adding distribution_network_id → distribution_network.distribution_network']

Business justification: GCP/ICH E6 drug accountability requires every clinical supply order to be traceable to the responsible investigator.
