  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The date on which the clinical supply was confirmed as received at the investigational site. Compared against required_delivery_date to assess supply chain performance and site readiness.']
  approved_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of IMP or ancillary supply formally approved for fulfilment after clinical supply review. May differ from ordered_quantity due to stock availability, protocol amendments, or QP release constraints.']
  blinding_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the blinding methodology applied to this supply order as defined in the clinical protocol. Determines labelling, packaging, and handling requirements for the IMP to maintain trial integrity.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for cancellation when order_status is \'Cancelled\'. Captures business justification for audit trail and supply planning analysis (e.g., protocol amendment, site closure, stock recall).']
  cold_chain_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this supply order requires cold-chain logistics (True) due to temperature-sensitive IMP storage and transport conditions. Triggers cold-chain packaging, monitoring, and carrier selection in the distribution workflow.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this clinical supply order record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and EU Annex 11.']
  dispensing_unit varchar [note: 'Description of the dispensing unit configuration for the IMP as packaged for clinical trial use (e.g., \'Blinded kit of 28 tablets\', \'1 vial 10mg/mL\'). Supports site pharmacist dispensing and accountability records.']
  export_permit_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the export permit or authorisation issued by the originating country\'s regulatory authority for the shipment of IMP across international borders for clinical trial purposes.']
  import_permit_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the import permit issued by the relevant national regulatory authority (e.g., FDA, EMA member state, PMDA) authorising the importation of the IMP into the destination country for clinical trial use.']
  ind_cta_number varchar [note: 'The regulatory filing reference number (IND number in the US per 21 CFR Part 312, or CTA number in the EU per Regulation 536/2014) under which this clinical supply order is authorised. Required for import/export permit applications and regulatory compliance.']
  irt_system_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or transaction ID from the Interactive Response Technology (IRT) / Interactive Web Response System (IWRS) that triggered or confirmed this supply order. Links the supply event to the randomization and drug management system.']
  is_blinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this supply order is subject to blinding requirements (True) or is open-label (False). Used as a quick filter for blinding compliance checks and supply handling procedures.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this clinical supply order record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Required for change control audit trail under 21 CFR Part 11 and EU Annex 11.']
  order_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the clinical supply order was formally placed or submitted by the clinical supply team. This is the principal business event date for the order.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable order reference number assigned by the clinical supply management system (e.g., Kinaxis RapidResponse or SAP MM) to uniquely identify this supply order across systems and with third-party logistics providers.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the clinical supply order, tracking progression from initial creation through fulfilment or cancellation. Drives supply chain workflow in Kinaxis RapidResponse / SAP MM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Draft|Submitted|Approved|In Preparation|Shipped|Delivered|Cancelled|On Hold — promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supply order by the nature of the material being ordered. IMP = investigational medicinal product; Comparator = approved reference drug; Placebo = matching placebo; Ancillary = non-IMP study supplies; Open-Label Extension = OLE phase supply; Rescue Medication = emergency/rescue supply.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of IMP or ancillary supply units requested in this order, expressed in the unit of measure defined on the IMP material master (e.g., vials, tablets, kits).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the clinical supply order indicating the urgency of fulfilment. Emergency orders may be triggered by unplanned site demand, protocol deviations, or patient safety requirements.']
  qp_release_date date [note: 'The date on which the Qualified Person (QP) formally certified and released the IMP batch for clinical use. Required for EU GMP Annex 13 compliance and batch disposition records.']
  qualified_person_release boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IMP batch included in this order has been certified and released by a Qualified Person (QP) as required by EU GMP Annex 13 and Directive 2001/20/EC before dispatch to the clinical site.']
  randomization_kit_number varchar [note: 'The unique kit number assigned by the Interactive Response Technology (IRT) or randomization system to the blinded supply kit. Links the physical supply to the randomization schedule and subject assignment. Critical for blinded trial integrity.']
  required_delivery_date date [note: 'The date by which the clinical supply must arrive at the investigational site to support the trial schedule. Used for supply chain planning and lead-time management in Kinaxis RapidResponse.']
  shipment_date date [note: 'The date on which the clinical supply was physically dispatched from the originating depot or manufacturing site. Used for GDP compliance, transit time tracking, and delivery performance monitoring.']
  shipped_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual quantity of IMP or ancillary supply physically dispatched from the manufacturing site or depot. May differ from approved_quantity due to last-minute stock adjustments or partial shipments.']
  shipping_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country from which the clinical supply is dispatched. Used for import/export compliance, customs documentation, and GDP route planning.']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum permissible storage and transport temperature in degrees Celsius for the IMP in this order, as specified in the IMP dossier and CoA. Used to configure cold-chain monitoring thresholds and excursion alerts.']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum permissible storage and transport temperature in degrees Celsius for the IMP in this order, as specified in the IMP dossier and CoA. Used to configure cold-chain monitoring thresholds.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'The courier or logistics provider\'s shipment tracking reference number, enabling real-time monitoring of the clinical supply in transit. Supports GDP compliance and cold-chain excursion investigation.']

  Note: 'Supply order for investigational medicinal products (IMPs), comparators, and ancillary supplies destined for clinical trial sites. Captures IMP material code, protocol number, study site, randomization kit number, ordered quantity, blinding requirements, expiry date, cold-chain requirements, import/export permit references, and order status. Distinct from commercial sales orders due to IND/CTA regulatory requirements, blinding, and randomization constraints.'
}

Table supply.product_recall {
  product_recall_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the pharmaceutical product recall event record. Primary key for the product_recall data product in the supply domain.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Product recalls are initiated against a specific regulatory approval — the recall targets the approved product/indication. Regulatory affairs teams track which approval is being recalled for health au']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: Product recalls are initiated based on quality findings documented in batch records. FDA 21 CFR Part 7 recall submissions require direct reference to the implicated batch record. No existing column on']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Recalls are managed at brand level; commercial teams coordinate customer notifications, field force communications, and market impact assessments. Links recall events to brand positioning, promotional']
  brand_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand_plan. Business justification: Product recalls must be assessed against the active brand plan to quantify commercial revenue impact, update forecasts, and trigger regulatory/commercial communications. FDA recall effectiveness repor']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Product recalls are executed per country with country-specific regulatory notification requirements (FDA, EMA, PMDA). A country FK on product_recall is essential for regulatory notification tracking a']
  coverage_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.coverage_decision. Business justification: Product recall regulatory process requires notifying payers and suspending affected coverage decisions. Market access teams must identify which coverage decisions are impacted by a recall for regulato']
  drug_master_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.drug_master_file. Business justification: During a product recall, regulatory teams reference the DMF to identify manufacturing site details, API specifications, and CMC data relevant to the recall root cause. Recall regulatory submissions to']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Recalls target specific drug product formulations/strengths, not just medicinal product families. Batch-level recall execution requires drug_product specificity for lot identification, customer notifi']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Recall cost collection: product recalls require a dedicated internal order (cost collector) to capture all recall-related costs — logistics, destruction, customer credits, regulatory fees — for financ']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Product recalls are initiated and managed by a specific legal entity responsible for regulatory notifications and liability. Pharma regulatory affairs teams require legal entity attribution for recall']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.line. Business justification: Recalls are frequently scoped to a specific manufacturing line (e.g., contamination event on Line 3). FDA recall scope definition and effectiveness checks require direct line-level scoping on the reca']
  manufacturing_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_deviation. Business justification: Manufacturing deviations are a primary trigger for product recalls. Regulatory CAPA closure and FDA/EMA recall submissions require direct linkage between the causative deviation and the resulting reca']
  master_batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.master_batch_record. Business justification: Recalls triggered by a defective MBR version (wrong formulation, incorrect specifications) require direct linkage to the implicated MBR. QA must identify all batches produced under that MBR version fo']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Recalls must link to material master for affected specifications, regulatory data, distribution history. Critical for recall scope determination, effectiveness checks, regulatory reporting, and custom']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master product record for the pharmaceutical product subject to the recall. Links to the product master data entity for product name, formulation, and therapeutic area details.']
  packaging_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.packaging_configuration. Business justification: Recalls in pharma are often packaging-configuration-specific (e.g., a specific blister pack size with a defective closure). FDA/EMA recall notifications require precise packaging identification. Direc']
  patient_access_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.patient_access_program. Business justification: When a product recall is initiated, patient access programs dispensing the recalled product must be immediately suspended and enrolled patients notified. Patient safety compliance requires direct trac']
  pv_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.pv_product. Business justification: Product recalls are often triggered by safety signals (ICSRs, SUSARs) and require coordination between supply chain and pharmacovigilance for regulatory authority notifications (FDA, EMA). Links recal']
  reimbursement_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.reimbursement_policy. Business justification: Regulatory recall notifications must identify affected reimbursement policies for suspension or amendment. Health authorities and payers require traceability from recall events to active reimbursement']
  safety_signal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.safety_signal. Business justification: Product recalls in pharma are formally triggered by validated safety signals. Regulatory agencies (FDA, EMA) require traceability from recall action back to the originating safety signal. PV and suppl']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing site where the affected lots were produced. Enables traceability back to the production facility for root cause investigation and regulatory inspection purposes.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Product recalls in pharma are executed at SKU level — the specific market pack (country, strength, pack size) determines customer notification scope and regulatory reporting. sku_code is denormalized;']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.trial. Business justification: Recall management requires identifying all active clinical trials using the recalled product/lot. Linking product_recall to trial enables recall teams to notify sponsors, pause dosing, and file regula']
  affected_markets varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3-letter country codes (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU) identifying the countries or markets where the recalled product was distributed and where recall actions must be executed.']
  cold_chain_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recalled product requires cold-chain handling (temperature-controlled logistics) during the recovery and return process. Critical for biologics, vaccines, and temperature-sensitive drug products.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the product recall record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance and data lineage tracking.']
  customer_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which direct customers (wholesalers, distributors, hospitals, pharmacies) were formally notified of the recall via recall notification letters or electronic communications.']
  customer_notification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the customer notification process indicating whether recall notification letters have been sent to all affected customers in the distribution chain.']
  distribution_level varchar [note: 'The level in the distribution chain to which the recall extends. Consumer: product reached end patients/consumers; Retail: product at retail pharmacy level; Wholesale: product at wholesale distributor level; Hospital: product at hospital/clinic level; User: product at healthcare professional level.']
  effectiveness_check_level varchar [note: 'FDA-defined effectiveness check level indicating the depth and percentage of consignees to be audited to verify recall notification receipt and product return. Level A: 100% of consignees; Level B: some percentage; Level C: 10% of consignees; Level D: 2% of consignees; Not Required: for certain recall types.']
  expiration_date_range varchar [note: 'The expiration date range (e.g., \'2024-01 to 2025-06\') covering all affected lot numbers included in the recall. Assists customers and distributors in identifying recalled product on shelves and in inventory.']
  gdp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recall execution is being conducted in compliance with Good Distribution Practice (GDP) guidelines, including proper documentation, chain of custody, and temperature-controlled handling of recovered product where applicable.']
  health_hazard_assessment varchar [note: 'Summary of the health hazard evaluation conducted by the company\'s medical and regulatory teams to assess the risk to patients from the defective product. Supports recall classification and regulatory submission under 21 CFR Part 7.']
  initiating_agency varchar [note: 'The regulatory authority or company entity that initiated or mandated the recall. Identifies whether the recall was company-initiated (voluntary) or directed by a specific regulatory body such as FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, or NMPA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|NMPA|WHO|Company — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the product recall record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and enables change tracking for regulatory inspection readiness.']
  ndc_code varchar [note: 'The National Drug Code (NDC) of the affected drug product in the standard 5-4-2 format. Uniquely identifies the labeler, product, and package configuration of the recalled pharmaceutical product as registered with the FDA.']
  press_release_date date [note: 'The date on which the company issued a public press release or public notification regarding the recall. Required for Class I and certain Class II recalls to inform consumers and healthcare professionals.']
  product_disposition varchar [note: 'The approved method for disposing of recovered recalled product. Options include destruction, return to manufacturer, reprocessing, relabeling (where permitted), or quarantine pending further evaluation.']
  quantity_distributed decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of the affected product that was distributed to the market prior to the recall initiation, expressed in the standard unit of measure (e.g., units, bottles, vials). Used to calculate recall effectiveness and track recovery progress.']
  quantity_recovered decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of the recalled product that has been physically returned, destroyed, or otherwise accounted for as of the last update. Used to calculate recall effectiveness percentage and support regulatory reporting.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure applicable to quantity_distributed and quantity_recovered fields (e.g., EA=Each, BT=Bottle, VL=Vial, AMP=Ampoule, PKG=Package, KG=Kilogram, L=Liter). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|BT|VL|AMP|PKG|KG|L — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  recall_class varchar [note: 'FDA-defined classification indicating the severity of the health hazard associated with the recalled product. Class I: serious adverse health consequences or death; Class II: temporary or medically reversible adverse health consequences; Class III: unlikely to cause adverse health consequences.']
  recall_closure_date date [note: 'The date on which the recall was formally closed or terminated by the regulatory authority or the company after all recall activities, effectiveness checks, and documentation requirements were completed.']
  recall_effectiveness_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of distributed product that has been successfully recovered or accounted for, calculated as (quantity_recovered / quantity_distributed) * 100. Key metric for FDA recall effectiveness checks and regulatory reporting under 21 CFR Part 7.']
  recall_initiated_date date [note: 'The date on which the recall was formally initiated by the company or ordered by the regulatory authority. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the recall lifecycle.']
  recall_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, regulatory correspondence summaries, or contextual information relevant to the recall event that is not captured in structured fields.']
  recall_number varchar [note: 'Externally-assigned recall identification number issued by the regulatory authority (e.g., FDA recall number) or internally assigned by the company for voluntary recalls. Serves as the primary business identifier for tracking and referencing the recall event across regulatory submissions and communications.']
  recall_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code categorizing the root cause or reason for the recall (e.g., contamination, labeling error, sub-potency, sterility failure, foreign particulate). Aligns with FDA recall reason taxonomy for regulatory reporting and trend analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Contamination|Labeling Error|Sub-Potency|Super-Potency|Sterility Failure|Foreign Particulate|Stability Failure|Packaging Defect|Microbial Contamination|Other — promote to reference product]']
  recall_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the specific defect, violation, or safety concern that triggered the recall. Provides the full technical and regulatory context beyond the recall reason code, including root cause findings from CAPA investigations.']
  recall_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the recall indicating whether the recall covers the entire national market, specific regions, international markets, or is limited to a specific manufacturing or distribution site.']
  recall_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the recall event. Pending: recall initiated but not yet active; Active: recall in progress with field corrections underway; Monitoring: recall effectiveness checks ongoing; Terminated: FDA has terminated the recall; Closed: all recall activities completed and documented.']
  recall_strategy varchar [note: 'Description of the recall strategy approved by the regulatory authority or company, including the depth of recall, communication methods, effectiveness check methodology, and disposition instructions for recovered product.']
  recall_type varchar [note: 'Categorization of the recall initiation type. Voluntary recalls are company-initiated; Mandatory recalls are ordered by a regulatory authority; Market Withdrawal involves minor violations not subject to legal action; Stock Recovery involves products not yet distributed to the market.']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the company formally notified the relevant regulatory authority (e.g., FDA, EMA) of the recall initiation. Critical compliance milestone tracked under 21 CFR Part 7 and equivalent international regulations.']
  regulatory_submission_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the regulatory submission (e.g., NDA, BLA, MAA) associated with the recalled product. Links the recall event to the product\'s regulatory dossier for compliance tracking in RIMS.']

  Note: 'Pharmaceutical product recall event record initiated by regulatory authority mandate or voluntary company action. Captures recall number, recall class (Class I, II, III per FDA), affected NDC, affected lot numbers, recall scope (market, distribution level), recall reason, regulatory agency notification date, press release date, customer notification status, quantity recovered, and recall closure date. Supports lot traceability and regulatory reporting obligations under 21 CFR Part 7.'
}

Table supply.serialization_record {
  serialization_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for serialization_record']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: Serialized units must trace to manufacturing batch. DSCSA and EU FMD regulations mandate linking serial numbers to batch records for product authentication, verification, and targeted recall execution']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Serialization compliance (DSCSA, FMD) is brand-specific; commercial teams use serialization data for product authentication, counterfeit prevention, and market-specific regulatory reporting. Links tra']
  business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trading partner (manufacturer, wholesaler, distributor, pharmacy) that currently holds legal ownership of the serialized unit. Updated at each DSCSA transaction event (Transfer of Ownership). Core to DSCSA chain-of-custody tracking.']
  commissioning_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: Serialization commissioning occurs at plants; plant master data provides GMP certification, FDA establishment identifier, regulatory market authorization. Distinct from manufacturing_site_id (manufact']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: serialization_record.market_country_code is a denormalized plain-text field. Linking to masterdata.country enables EU FMD and DSCSA serialization compliance validation by market country and supports r']
  dispensing_hcp_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.master. Business justification: DSCSA track-and-trace and anti-diversion programs require serialized units to be traceable to the dispensing or administering HCP. Role-prefix \'dispensing_\' distinguishes from patient_id already on th']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pharmaceutical finished goods product master record. Links the serialized unit to the product\'s regulatory approvals, storage conditions, and supply chain attributes.']
  inventory_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: Serialization records track individual serialized units that belong to specific inventory lots. The lot_number (STRING) should be replaced with inventory_lot_id FK to inventory_lot.inventory_lot_id. N']
  investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.investigational_product. Business justification: Serialized IMP packs must be linked to the specific investigational product for DSCSA/FMD track-and-trace compliance. Regulatory serialization requirements for clinical supplies mandate that each seri']
  packaging_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.packaging_configuration. Business justification: Serialization occurs at specific packaging levels (bottle, carton, case, pallet). Packaging configuration defines GTIN structure, aggregation hierarchy, and regulatory serialization requirements (DSCS']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.patient. Business justification: Clinical supply serialization systems track which specific serialized unit (GTIN/serial) was dispensed to which patient. Required for accountability, returns reconciliation, regulatory inspections, an']
  primary_serialization_commissioning_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing or packaging site where the serial number was commissioned. Used for GDP compliance, site-level traceability, and regulatory reporting to FDA/EMA.']
  product_recall_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the recall event record when recall_flag is TRUE. Links the serialized unit to the specific recall notification, recall class, and remediation actions. Populated only when the unit is subject to an active recall.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.sales_order. Business justification: DSCSA (Drug Supply Chain Security Act) compliance requires tracing serialized pharmaceutical units to the sales transaction. Pharma serialization teams link serial numbers to sales orders for transact']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: A serialization record tracks the unit-level and case-level serialization status of pharmaceutical finished goods. When a serialized unit is shipped, the serialization record should be linked to the s']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: DSCSA (US) and FMD (EU) serialization regulations require saleable unit identification at SKU level. Serialization records must reference the SKU to support verification, aggregation hierarchy, and re']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical location (warehouse, distribution center, pharmacy, hospital) where the serialized unit is currently held. Used for inventory visibility, GDP compliance, and cold-chain monitoring.']
  treatment_exposure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.treatment_exposure. Business justification: Links serialized investigational product units to documented treatment administration events in eCRF. Enables verification that lot/serial numbers recorded in exposure logs match dispensed units, crit']
  cold_chain_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this serialized unit requires cold-chain storage and transportation (e.g., 2-8°C refrigerated or -20°C frozen). Drives cold-chain logistics routing, temperature monitoring requirements, and GDP compliance checks.']
  commissioning_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the serial number was commissioned (activated and associated with a physical product unit) at the manufacturing or packaging site. Marks the start of the serialized unit\'s lifecycle. Required for DSCSA and EU FMD event reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this serialization record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Represents the audit trail creation timestamp for the Silver Layer record, distinct from the commissioning event timestamp.']
  decommissioning_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for decommissioning the serialized unit. Required for EU FMD decommissioning event reporting to the European Medicines Verification System (EMVS). Supports pharmacovigilance and recall management workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: recall|destruction|sample|theft|damage|dispensed|exported|other — promote to reference product]']
  decommissioning_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the serialized unit was decommissioned (removed from the active supply chain). Populated when serialization_status transitions to \'decommissioned\' or \'destroyed\'. Required for EU FMD and DSCSA event reporting.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'Pharmaceutical dosage form of the product (e.g., tablet, capsule, injection, solution). Used for supply chain classification, storage condition assignment, and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|injection|solution|cream|patch|inhaler|suspension — promote to reference product]']
  epcis_event_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the most recent GS1 EPCIS event associated with this serialized unit. Links to the EPCIS event repository for full event history (ObjectEvent, AggregationEvent, TransactionEvent). Used for DSCSA and EU FMD interoperability.']
  gdp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the current handling and storage of this serialized unit is compliant with Good Distribution Practice (GDP) requirements. Set to FALSE if a GDP deviation has been recorded for this unit (e.g., temperature excursion, unauthorized handling).']
  gtin varchar [note: '14-digit GS1 Global Trade Item Number identifying the pharmaceutical product at the trade item level. Encoded in the 2D DataMatrix barcode on the package. Used for product identification in DSCSA transaction data and EU FMD verification.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this serialization record was most recently updated in the enterprise data platform. Tracks the latest status change, verification event, or ownership transfer recorded against this serialized unit.']
  last_verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent verification event performed against the medicines verification system (NMVS/EMVS). Used to track verification frequency and identify units that have not been recently verified.']
  pack_size varchar [note: 'Quantity and unit of the pharmaceutical product contained in the individual saleable unit (e.g., \'30 tablets\', \'10 mL vial\', \'1 pre-filled syringe\'). Used for dispensing verification and supply chain planning.']
  packaging_level varchar [note: 'Level of the packaging hierarchy this serialization record represents. \'unit\' is the individual saleable unit (SGTIN); \'bundle\' is a multi-pack; \'case\' is a shipping case (SSCC); \'pallet\' is a pallet-level aggregation (SSCC). Drives aggregation hierarchy logic.']
  parent_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the immediate parent container in the aggregation hierarchy (e.g., the case serial number for a unit, or the pallet serial number for a case). Enables reconstruction of the full unit→case→pallet aggregation tree for DSCSA and EU FMD compliance.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Commercial or brand name of the pharmaceutical product associated with this serialized unit. Included for operational readability and reporting without requiring a join to the product master.']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this serialized unit is subject to an active product recall. Set to TRUE when the unit\'s lot number is included in a recall notification. Enables rapid identification and quarantine of affected units across the supply chain.']
  regulatory_market_code varchar [note: 'Regulatory market authorization code or product registration number applicable to the market country. Links the serialized unit to the specific marketing authorization (NDA/BLA/MAA) under which it was released for that market.']
  saleable_return_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this serialized unit has been returned from the market and assessed as eligible for re-entry into the saleable supply chain. Requires verification against the NMVS/EMVS and GDP assessment before re-release.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric serial number assigned to an individual saleable unit or case during commissioning. Conforms to GS1 SGTIN or SSCC encoding standards. Required for DSCSA unit-level traceability and EU FMD verification at point of dispense.']
  serialization_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the serialized unit within the track-and-trace system. \'commissioned\' = activated at manufacturing; \'active\' = in supply chain; \'shipped\' = dispatched to trading partner; \'returned\' = returned from market; \'decommissioned\' = intentionally removed; \'destroyed\' = physically destroyed.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating serialization system or track-and-trace platform (e.g., SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals, Tracelink, Serialized Inventory Management System) that generated this record. Used for data lineage and system reconciliation.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage condition for the serialized unit as specified in the product\'s approved labeling and stability data. Drives warehouse zone assignment, cold-chain logistics, and GDP compliance monitoring.']
  supply_chain_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the serialized unit belongs to the commercial supply chain or the clinical trial supply chain. Clinical trial units require additional IND/CTA traceability. Compassionate use units require special regulatory tracking.']
  transaction_statement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a valid DSCSA Transaction Statement has been received and verified for this serialized unit from the upstream trading partner. Required under DSCSA Section 582 for each change of ownership in the pharmaceutical supply chain.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent verification check of the serialized unit against the national or European medicines verification system (NMVS/EMVS). \'verified\' = authentication passed; \'failed\' = authentication failed (potential falsification); \'pending\' = verification in progress; \'not_verified\' = not yet checked.']

  Note: 'Unit-level and case-level serialization record for pharmaceutical finished goods compliant with DSCSA (Drug Supply Chain Security Act) and EU FMD (Falsified Medicines Directive) requirements. Captures serial number, GTIN, NDC, lot number, expiry date, aggregation hierarchy (unit→case→pallet), commissioning event, decommissioning event, verification status, and trading partner transaction history. Enables end-to-end track-and-trace across the pharmaceutical supply chain.'
}

Table supply.cold_chain_record {
  cold_chain_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cold chain monitoring record. Primary key for the cold_chain_record product.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Cold chain excursions require brand-level disposition decisions and regulatory reporting. Commercial teams need brand-level cold chain deviation metrics for product quality KPIs and customer/regulator']
  carrier_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: cold_chain_record.carrier_name is a denormalized plain-text field. Linking to masterdata.business_partner enables carrier qualification tracking, GDP deviation attribution to specific carriers, and ca']
  distribution_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_network. Business justification: cold_chain_record has location_code (string) and location_type identifying where the temperature reading was taken, but no FK to the distribution_network. Adding distribution_network_id → distribution']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Temperature monitoring requirements and excursion thresholds are drug product-specific (formulation-dependent stability profiles). Product link required for deviation assessment, disposition decisions']
  inventory_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: Cold chain monitoring records track temperature/humidity for specific inventory lots. The lot_number (STRING) should be replaced with inventory_lot_id FK to inventory_lot.inventory_lot_id. Material id']
  investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.investigational_product. Business justification: Cold-chain excursions affecting IMP shipments must be assessed against the specific investigational product\'s stability profile for QP disposition decisions. GDP regulations require linking temperatur']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Temperature monitoring must reference material storage requirements (min/max thresholds, excursion tolerances) from material master. Essential for excursion assessment, disposition decisions, and GDP ']
  preceding_cold_chain_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on cold_chain_record (preceding_cold_chain_record_id)']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the shipment or delivery if the monitoring occurred during transit. Links to shipment records for end-to-end traceability.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.storage_location. Business justification: Cold chain deviations occurring at a storage location must be linked to the validated storage zone for GDP deviation management, CAPA initiation, and re-qualification decisions. cold_chain_record.stor']
  capa_reference varchar [note: 'The reference number of the CAPA investigation initiated in response to the temperature excursion, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cold chain monitoring record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  deviation_reference varchar [note: 'The reference number of the quality deviation or non-conformance record associated with the temperature excursion event.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The category of temperature monitoring device used to capture the reading.']
  disposition_decision_date date [note: 'The date when the quality disposition decision was made following excursion investigation and impact assessment.']
  disposition_rationale varchar [note: 'The documented justification and scientific rationale for the disposition decision, including stability data and risk assessment results.']
  disposition_status varchar [note: 'The quality disposition decision for the product following temperature monitoring or excursion investigation. Determines whether product can be released for use.']
  excursion_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The total duration in minutes that the temperature or humidity remained outside the acceptable range during the excursion event.']
  excursion_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the temperature or humidity returned to within the acceptable range, marking the end of the excursion event.']
  excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this reading represents a temperature or humidity excursion outside the acceptable range. True indicates an excursion event.']
  excursion_severity varchar [note: 'The severity classification of the temperature excursion based on the magnitude and duration of the deviation from acceptable range.']
  excursion_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the temperature or humidity first exceeded the acceptable threshold, marking the beginning of the excursion event.']
  gdp_compliance_status varchar [note: 'The assessment of whether the cold chain monitoring and handling met GDP requirements and regulatory standards.']
  humidity_reading_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The relative humidity percentage recorded by the monitoring device, if humidity monitoring is required for the product.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cold chain monitoring record was most recently modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'The category of location where the temperature monitoring occurred, indicating the supply chain segment.']
  monitoring_device_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the temperature monitoring device or data logger used to capture the reading. Required for device calibration traceability.']
  monitoring_event_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the temperature monitoring event or data collection session. Used for traceability and audit purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, observations, or contextual information related to the temperature monitoring event or excursion.']
  reading_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the temperature or humidity reading was captured by the monitoring device. Critical for excursion analysis.']
  regulatory_report_date date [note: 'The date when the temperature excursion event was reported to the relevant regulatory authority, if reporting was required.']
  regulatory_reportable boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the temperature excursion event requires reporting to regulatory authorities such as FDA, EMA, or other health agencies.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serialization identifier for the individual package or pallet being monitored, if serialization is implemented.']
  storage_condition_required varchar [note: 'The required storage condition classification for the pharmaceutical product as defined in the product specification and regulatory approval.']
  storage_zone varchar [note: 'The specific storage zone, room, or area within the facility where the product was located during monitoring.']
  supply_chain_segment varchar [note: 'The segment of the pharmaceutical supply chain where the temperature monitoring occurred, enabling segment-specific analysis.']
  temperature_max_threshold_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum acceptable temperature in degrees Celsius for the product storage condition. Readings above this trigger an excursion event.']
  temperature_min_threshold_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum acceptable temperature in degrees Celsius for the product storage condition. Readings below this trigger an excursion event.']
  temperature_reading_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'The actual temperature measurement in degrees Celsius recorded by the monitoring device at the reading timestamp.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'The carrier-assigned tracking number for the shipment, enabling correlation with carrier systems and proof of delivery.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing temperature monitoring data points and excursion events for cold-chain pharmaceutical products during storage and transit, including sensor readings, excursion duration, and disposition decisions'
}

Table supply.recall_lot_scope {
  recall_lot_scope_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the recall_lot_scope association']
  inventory_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific inventory lot included within the scope of this recall action.']
  product_recall_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the parent product recall event that initiated the scope of this lot inclusion.']
  affected_quantity_per_lot decimal [note: 'The quantity of this specific inventory lot that falls within the scope of the recall. Distinct from product_recall.quantity_distributed, which is the aggregate across all lots. Required for per-lot effectiveness check reporting to the FDA.']
  disposition_per_lot varchar [note: 'The approved disposition method for this specific lot as determined by quality review. May differ from the recall-level product_disposition on the parent recall record if lot-specific conditions (e.g., cold chain break, partial quantity) warrant a different disposition decision.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the holder or custodian of this specific inventory lot has been formally notified of the recall. Tracked per lot because lots may be held at different locations (3PL, distributor, hospital) requiring separate notification actions.']
  recovery_date date [note: 'The date on which this specific inventory lot was physically recovered, confirmed destroyed, or otherwise resolved within the recall process. Per-lot date required for FDA effectiveness check documentation.']
  recovery_status varchar [note: 'Current recovery status for this specific lot within the recall. Tracked independently per lot as different lots may be at different stages of recovery simultaneously. Cannot reside on inventory_lot (which reflects current inventory state) or product_recall (which is aggregate).']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational scoping event between a product_recall and a specific inventory_lot in pharmaceutical recall management. It captures the per-lot tracking required by FDA 21 CFR Part 7 effectiveness checks, recording the affected quantity, recovery status, disposition method, and notification state for each individual lot included in a recall action. Each record links one product_recall to one inventory_lot and carries attributes that exist only in the context of that specific recall-lot pairing — they cannot reside on the recall header (which is aggregate) or the inventory lot (which reflects current inventory state).. Existence Justification: In pharmaceutical recall operations, a single recall event explicitly targets multiple specific inventory lots (e.g., all lots of NDC 12345-678-90 manufactured between Jan–Jun 2024), and a single inventory lot can be subject to multiple recalls across its lifecycle (e.g., a lot recalled for labeling deficiency and later for contamination). The FDA\'s recall effectiveness check process requires tracking each affected lot individually with its own recovery status, disposition, and notification record. This is an operationally managed relationship — recall coordinators actively create, update, and close records per lot — not an analytical derivation.'
}

Table pharmacovigilance.icsr {
  icsr_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for the Individual Case Safety Report record in the pharmacovigilance data product.']

  candidate_nomination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.candidate_nomination. Business justification: ICSRs for investigational compounds must link to the candidate nomination that initiated the clinical program, enabling traceability from adverse events back to the nomination decision for IND safety ']
  clinical_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.clinical_enrollment. Business justification: ICSRs from blinded trials require tracing the exact enrollment record (treatment arm, randomization code) for unblinding decisions and causality assessment per ICH E6 GCP. icsr already has patient_id ']

  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Case-level product traceability: ICSR is the core safety case record. Linking to drug_product enables case-level traceability to the exact formulation for regulatory submissions, signal detection, and']
  hco_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.hco_master. Business justification: ICSRs are submitted by healthcare institutions (hospitals, clinics). Linking icsr to hco_master enables site-level safety signal detection, institutional case aggregation, and regulatory reporting. A ']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. A proper FK enables authority-level ICSR volume reporting, submission deadline track']
  indication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Structured indication-level case analysis: icsr has suspect_product_indication as plain text. Linking to indication normalizes this to a structured FK enabling indication-level case aggregation for PS']
  inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: ICSRs report adverse reactions to specific active substances identified by INN — a core E2B(R3) data element. Linking to inn_registry enables signal detection by substance across products, supports WH']
  investigational_compound_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.compound. Business justification: ICSRs for investigational products in clinical trials must reference the specific discovery compound for IND safety reporting, causality assessment, and DSUR preparation. FDA and EMA require traceabil']
  investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.investigator. Business justification: Trial-sourced ICSRs must identify the responsible investigator per ICH E2A/GCP. The investigator record holds GCP training status and 1572 data required for regulatory submissions. master_id alone is ']
  manufacturing_batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: Pharmacovigilance ICSRs should link to manufacturing batch records for product quality complaints and batch-related adverse events. This enables integrated safety and quality investigation and support']
  manufacturing_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: Manufacturing site identification required for GMP deviation investigations, site-specific signal detection, and quality-safety interface. Links safety events to production locations for batch-related']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.hcp_master. Business justification: ICSRs require linking to reporting HCP for follow-up queries, credential verification, specialty-based causality assessment, and regulatory submission completeness per ICH E2B requirements. Role prefi']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'FK to patient.patient.patient_id — Safety-to-patient linkage enables longitudinal safety analysis — ICSRs must link to patient records for duplicate detection, medical history context, and regulatory case processing (ICH E2B R3).']
  processing_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: ICSR cases are assigned to specific PV org units (regional safety teams) for case processing and regulatory submission. PV case management systems track org unit ownership for workload reporting, GCP ']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.project. Business justification: Investigational-phase ICSRs must be traceable to the originating discovery project for IND aggregate safety reporting (21 CFR 312.32) and portfolio-level safety reviews. pv_product does not always exi']
  pv_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.pv_product. Business justification: ICSR reports adverse events for a specific product under PV surveillance. The pv_product_id FK links to the authoritative PV product master. Remove suspect_product_name as it normalizes to pv_product.']
  reporter_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Reporter country determines regulatory reporting obligations, timelines (15-day vs 90-day rules), and format requirements (E2B versions). Critical for pharmacovigilance compliance and case routing to ']
  reporter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.reporter. Business justification: Every ICSR has a reporter who submitted it. This is a critical missing link. The reporter_id FK allows joining to get reporter qualification, type, and contact details. Remove primary_reporter_type as']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.sales_rep. Business justification: ICSRs collected by field sales reps must reference the reporting rep for follow-up management, training compliance tracking, and sunshine act reporting. Field force AE reporting is a standard pharma w']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: ICSRs are transmitted to health authorities via E2B gateway submissions. Linking icsr to regulatory.submission enables tracking of ICSR regulatory submission deadlines, transmission status, and compli']
  submitting_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: ICSRs are submitted by a specific MAH legal entity — a core E2B(R3) regulatory requirement. The sender_organisation plain-text field is a denormalization of legal_entity. A proper FK enables regulator']
  ae_onset_date date [note: 'Date of onset of the adverse event/reaction as reported by the reporter. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) E.i.4. Used for time-to-onset analysis, dechallenge/rechallenge assessment, and signal detection.']
  ae_outcome varchar [note: 'Outcome of the adverse event at the time of reporting. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) E.i.7. Fatal outcome triggers mandatory expedited reporting and mortality signal analysis.']
  case_narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text medical narrative summarizing the adverse event, patient history, suspect drug details, clinical course, and outcome. Authored by the medical reviewer. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) H.1. Classified confidential as it may contain de-identified but sensitive clinical information.']
  case_nullification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this case version represents a nullification (retraction) of a previously submitted ICSR. True = nullification report submitted to health authorities. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) C.1.11.1.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the ICSR within the pharmacovigilance case management system (Argus Safety). Drives SLA monitoring for 7-day and 15-day regulatory reporting deadlines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|in_triage|in_processing|pending_review|closed|archived|nullified — promote to reference product]']
  case_version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version counter for the ICSR record. Version 1 = initial report; subsequent integers represent follow-up reports with new or amended information. Nullification reports are tracked via case_nullification_flag. Supports full version history per ICH E2B(R3) C.1.2 and 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail requirements.']
  causality_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of the causal relationship between the suspect drug and the adverse event, using WHO-UMC causality categories. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) G.k.9.i. Drives listedness and SUSAR determination. Assessed by the company\'s medical reviewer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ICSR record was first created in the pharmacovigilance system (Argus Safety / Oracle AERS). Supports 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail and GDPR data processing records.']
  dechallenge_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of dechallenge — whether the adverse event resolved or improved upon discontinuation of the suspect drug. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) G.k.9.i.3a. Key input for causality assessment and benefit-risk evaluation.']
  duplicate_assessment_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the duplicate case detection assessment performed during intake triage. Duplicate cases are linked to the master case and suppressed from regulatory submission counts. Critical for accurate signal detection and aggregate reporting.']
  edc_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of SAE reconciliation between the pharmacovigilance database (Argus Safety) and the clinical trial EDC system (Medidata Rave). Applicable when report_type = \'clinical_trial\'. Ensures consistency between safety database and clinical database per ICH E6(R2) GCP requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the ICSR record in the source system. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail compliance and Silver layer incremental data loading.']
  listedness_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the adverse drug reaction is listed (expected) in the current Reference Safety Information (RSI) — Company Core Data Sheet (CCDS) or approved product labeling. Unlisted + serious = SUSAR (Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction), triggering expedited reporting. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) G.k.9.i.']
  literature_reference varchar [note: 'PubMed Identifier (PMID) or Digital Object Identifier (DOI) for literature-sourced ICSRs. Populated when report_type = \'literature\'. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) C.4.r.1. Supports systematic literature review (SLR) traceability.']
  minimum_criteria_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ICSR meets the four ICH E2B minimum criteria for a valid case: (1) identifiable reporter, (2) identifiable patient, (3) suspect drug, (4) adverse event. Cases not meeting minimum criteria are tracked but not submitted. Corresponds to ICH E2D minimum information requirements.']
  most_recent_info_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent follow-up information received for this case, resetting the regulatory clock for follow-up submissions. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) C.1.5.']
  patient_age_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for patient_age_value. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) D.2.2b. Supports neonatal and pediatric cases where age is expressed in days, weeks, or months.']
  patient_age_value decimal(5,2) [note: 'De-identified patient age at the time of the adverse event onset, expressed as a numeric value. Must be used in conjunction with patient_age_unit. Stored per GDPR/HIPAA de-identification standards. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) D.2.2a.']
  patient_sex varchar [note: 'Biological sex of the patient as reported. De-identified demographic field per GDPR/HIPAA. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) D.5. Used in signal detection and benefit-risk assessment stratification.']
  patient_weight_kg decimal(6,2) [note: 'Patient body weight in kilograms at the time of the adverse event, as reported. De-identified per GDPR/HIPAA. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) D.3. Used for dose-weight relationship analysis in signal detection.']
  primary_ae_meddra_pt varchar [note: 'MedDRA (Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities) Preferred Term (PT) coding for the primary adverse event/reaction reported. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) E.i.2.1b. Standardized coding enables cross-case signal detection and regulatory submission.']
  primary_ae_meddra_soc varchar [note: 'MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) for the primary adverse event, derived from the MedDRA PT hierarchy. Supports aggregate signal detection by organ system and PSUR/PBRER tabulation requirements.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date the ICSR was first received by the company (MAH/sponsor), establishing the regulatory clock start date (Day 0) for 7-day expedited and 15-day expedited reporting obligations. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) C.1.4.']
  rechallenge_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of rechallenge — whether the adverse event recurred upon re-administration of the suspect drug after dechallenge. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) G.k.9.i.3b. Strengthens causality evidence and is critical for SUSAR assessment in clinical trials.']
  regulatory_submission_deadline date [note: 'Calculated deadline date by which the ICSR must be submitted to the relevant health authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, NMPA, TGA). Derived from receipt_date plus applicable reporting window (7 or 15 calendar days for expedited reports; 90 days for non-expedited). SLA-critical field for compliance monitoring.']
  regulatory_submission_status varchar [note: 'Current submission status of the ICSR to the applicable health authority. Tracks compliance against 7-day and 15-day expedited reporting deadlines. Overdue status triggers escalation per PV SLA management procedures.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ICSR source channel per ICH E2B(R3) C.1.3. Drives regulatory reporting obligations and processing workflows. Spontaneous reports from HCPs/patients, literature-sourced cases (PMID/DOI), clinical trial SAEs, solicited reports from patient support programs, and other sources.']
  sender_case_number varchar [note: 'Internal case number assigned by the company (MAH/sponsor) in the source system (Argus Safety / Oracle AERS). Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) C.1.8.1. Used for internal tracking and reconciliation with the worldwide unique case ID.']
  serious_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event meets ICH E2A seriousness criteria (death, life-threatening, hospitalization, disability, congenital anomaly, or medically significant). True = Serious Adverse Event (SAE). Drives expedited 7-day or 15-day regulatory reporting obligation. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) E.i.3.2.']
  seriousness_criteria varchar [note: 'Specific ICH E2A seriousness criterion or criteria met by the adverse event. Multiple criteria may apply. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) E.i.3.2a-f. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: death|life_threatening|hospitalization|disability|congenital_anomaly|medically_significant — promote to reference product]']
  source_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the adverse event occurred. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) C.1.17.1. Determines applicable regulatory authority for submission routing (FDA, EMA, PMDA, NMPA, TGA, MHRA).']
  susar_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case qualifies as a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) — i.e., serious, unexpected (unlisted), and with a reasonable causal relationship to the investigational medicinal product. True = SUSAR requiring 7-day (fatal/life-threatening) or 15-day expedited reporting to health authorities and ethics committees.']
  worldwide_unique_case_number varchar [note: 'Globally unique case identifier assigned at intake, conforming to ICH E2B(R3) C.1.1 format (sender identifier + case number). Used for cross-regional deduplication and regulatory submission tracking across FDA MedWatch, EudraVigilance, J-ICSR, and NMPA.']

  Note: 'Individual Case Safety Report — the primary transactional record in pharmacovigilance. Captures all adverse event/adverse drug reaction reports from healthcare professionals, patients, literature, clinical trials, and spontaneous sources. Stores case identifiers (worldwide unique case ID), report type (spontaneous, literature, clinical trial, solicited, SAE), source channel, seriousness criteria, listedness assessment, causality assessment, narrative, regulatory clock start date, case processing status, and submission compliance. For literature-sourced cases, stores publication reference (PMID/DOI). For clinical trial cases, stores reconciliation status with EDC, dechallenge/rechallenge results, and site details. Includes version history tracking (initial, follow-up, nullification) with full audit trail per ICH E2B and 21 CFR Part 314. Includes de-identified patient demographics (age, sex, weight, medical history) per GDPR/HIPAA. Includes intake triage, minimum criteria validation, duplicate assessment outcome, and case assignment. SSOT for all individual safety cases. Supports regional requirements for FDA (MedWatch), EMA (EudraVigilance), PMDA (J-ICSR), NMPA, and TGA reporting. SLA-critical: 7-day and 15-day regulatory reporting deadlines.'
}

Table pharmacovigilance.pv_adverse_reaction {
  pv_adverse_reaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each adverse reaction record within the pharmacovigilance system. Primary key for the pv_adverse_reaction data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.adverse_event. Business justification: PV-coded adverse reactions must be reconciled against EDC-captured adverse events per GVP Module VI. A direct FK from pv_adverse_reaction to patient.adverse_event enables reaction-level EDC reconcilia']
  atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: Linking adverse reactions to ATC therapeutic class enables signal detection by drug category, benefit-risk analysis, and PSUR aggregate reporting. Medical coding teams perform MedDRA-to-ATC cross-tabu']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Adverse reactions aggregated by brand for safety surveillance, label update decisions, and competitive safety positioning. Essential for brand risk management committees and periodic benefit-risk eval']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Reaction-to-product traceability: pv_adverse_reaction must reference the specific drug_product for signal detection across formulations, labeling update decisions, and expedited regulatory reporting. ']
  icsr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) to which this adverse reaction belongs. One ICSR may contain multiple adverse reaction records. Supports header-detail navigation for case processing.']
  indication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Indication-level benefit-risk: adverse reactions must be evaluated against the approved indication for listedness determination and PSUR benefit-risk summaries. EMA/FDA require indication-specific saf']
  inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: Adverse reactions are coded against specific active substances (INN) for signal detection and aggregate safety analysis. Linking pv_adverse_reaction to inn_registry enables substance-level disproporti']
  label_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.label. Business justification: Listedness determination for each adverse reaction requires reference to the current approved label. Linking pv_adverse_reaction to regulatory.label enables automated listedness_determination against ']
  pv_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the suspect medicinal product (drug product) associated with this adverse reaction. Links to the master product record for the drug under pharmacovigilance surveillance.']
  action_taken varchar [note: 'Action taken with the suspect medicinal product in response to the adverse reaction: dose reduced, drug withdrawn, dose not changed, not applicable, unknown, or dose increased. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) field B.4.k.8 and is required for regulatory ICSR submissions.']
  case_narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text medical narrative describing the clinical course of this adverse reaction, including relevant medical history, concomitant medications, treatment, and outcome. Required for regulatory ICSR submissions and medical review. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) field H.1.']
  causality_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of the causal relationship between the suspect drug and the adverse reaction, using WHO-UMC or company standard causality categories. Drives listedness determination, SUSAR classification, and signal detection weighting.']
  causality_method varchar [note: 'The methodology or algorithm used to perform the causality assessment (e.g., WHO-UMC scale, Naranjo algorithm, company-specific algorithm). Supports audit trail and methodological consistency across cases.']
  coded_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the medical coder or system process that performed or confirmed the MedDRA coding. Required for audit trail under 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance. Stores the user login or system account name.']
  coding_date date [note: 'Date on which the MedDRA coding was completed for this adverse reaction. Used for coding turnaround time metrics, audit trail, and compliance with regulatory submission timelines.']
  coding_method varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the MedDRA coding was performed automatically by the safety system (auto-coded), manually by a medical coder (manual review), or auto-coded and subsequently confirmed by manual review. Supports coding quality metrics and audit trail requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this adverse reaction record was first created in the pharmacovigilance safety database. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and GVP Module VI. Supports case processing timeline metrics.']
  ctcae_grade varchar [note: 'Severity grade assigned per the NCI Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE), ranging from 1 (mild) to 5 (death). Applicable primarily for oncology clinical trial adverse events. Null for non-oncology or post-market cases where CTCAE grading is not applicable.']
  dechallenge_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of dechallenge (withdrawal of the suspect drug) on the adverse reaction. Positive dechallenge (reaction improved or resolved after withdrawal) supports causality. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) field B.4.k.18.']
  is_serious boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adverse reaction meets the regulatory definition of a Serious Adverse Event (SAE) per ICH E2A criteria. True if any seriousness criterion is met. Drives expedited reporting timelines (7-day or 15-day) to health authorities.']
  is_susar boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adverse reaction has been classified as a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR). True when the reaction is serious, unexpected (unlisted), and there is a reasonable causal relationship to the investigational medicinal product. Triggers mandatory expedited reporting to health authorities and ethics committees.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this adverse reaction record. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11. Used to track follow-up updates and case amendments in the safety database.']
  listedness_determination varchar [note: 'Determination of whether this adverse reaction is listed (expected) or unlisted (unexpected) in the current Reference Safety Information (RSI), such as the Company Core Data Sheet (CCDS) or Investigator Brochure (IB). Unlisted serious reactions in clinical trials constitute Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reactions (SUSARs) requiring expedited reporting.']
  meddra_hlgt_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA High Level Group Term (HLGT) code, one level above HLT in the MedDRA hierarchy. Supports broader therapeutic area grouping for signal detection and regulatory submissions.']
  meddra_hlt_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA High Level Term (HLT) code representing the grouping level above the Preferred Term. Used for aggregate signal detection and periodic safety update report (PSUR) tabulations.']
  meddra_pt_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) numeric code assigned to this adverse reaction after coding. The PT is the primary level used for regulatory reporting, signal detection, and line listing generation. Mandatory for ICSR submissions to FDA and EMA.']
  meddra_pt_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) label corresponding to the meddra_pt_code. Stored denormalized for reporting and line listing generation without requiring a join to the MedDRA reference table.']
  meddra_soc_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) code representing the highest level of the MedDRA hierarchy. Used for regulatory line listings, PSUR summary tabulations, and risk management plan (RMP) categorization.']
  meddra_version varchar [note: 'Version of the MedDRA dictionary used to code this adverse reaction (e.g., 26.0, 26.1). Critical for regulatory submissions and recoding exercises when MedDRA versions are updated. Required for reproducibility of signal detection analyses.']
  medical_confirmation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse reaction has been medically confirmed by a healthcare professional (HCP). Medically confirmed cases carry greater weight in signal detection and regulatory reporting. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) field A.1.7.']
  onset_date date [note: 'Date on which the adverse reaction first began. Used to calculate time-to-onset from drug exposure, supports causality assessment, and is required for ICSR regulatory submissions. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) field B.2.i.4.']
  outcome_code varchar [note: 'Standardized outcome of the adverse reaction at the time of reporting. Values align with ICH E2B(R3) outcome codes: recovered/resolved, recovering/resolving, not recovered/not resolved, fatal, unknown, recovered with sequelae. Mandatory for regulatory submissions.']
  reaction_duration_days varchar [note: 'Duration of the adverse reaction in days, calculated from onset to resolution. Stored as a business attribute when explicitly reported by the reporter or calculated during case processing. Supports time-to-resolution analytics and benefit-risk assessments.']
  reaction_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number of this adverse reaction within the parent ICSR, enabling ordering and identification of multiple events within a single case. Corresponds to the reaction/event sequence in ICH E2B(R3) message structure.']
  reaction_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow processing status of this adverse reaction record within the pharmacovigilance case management system: new (received, not yet processed), under review, coded (MedDRA coding complete), medically reviewed, closed (finalized), or deleted (voided). Drives case management workflow in Argus Safety.']
  rechallenge_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of rechallenge (re-administration of the suspect drug after withdrawal) if performed. Positive rechallenge (reaction recurred) strengthens causality. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) field B.4.k.19.']
  regulatory_report_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory reporting obligation triggered by this adverse reaction: 7-day expedited (unexpected fatal/life-threatening SUSAR), 15-day expedited (other serious unexpected), periodic (PSUR/PBRER), non-expedited, or not reportable. Drives submission workflow and deadline tracking.']
  reporting_source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the original source from which this adverse reaction was reported: spontaneous (unsolicited), clinical trial, published literature, regulatory authority, or other. Determines applicable reporting timelines and regulatory obligations. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) field A.1.4.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the adverse reaction resolved or ended. Null if the event is ongoing, fatal, or outcome is unknown. Used to calculate event duration and supports outcome determination. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) field B.2.i.6.']
  seriousness_criteria varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of applicable seriousness criteria met by this adverse reaction per ICH E2A: death, life-threatening, hospitalization, disability/incapacity, congenital anomaly, medically significant. Supports regulatory reporting classification and expedited reporting decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: death|life_threatening|hospitalization|prolonged_hospitalization|disability|congenital_anomaly|medically_significant — promote to reference product]']
  severity_description varchar [note: 'Narrative severity classification of the adverse reaction using standard WHO/ICH terminology: mild, moderate, severe, life-threatening, or fatal. Complements CTCAE grade for non-oncology cases and post-market surveillance where CTCAE grading is not used.']
  signal_detection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adverse reaction record has been flagged as contributing to a potential pharmacovigilance signal under review. Set by the signal detection process in the safety database. Supports signal management workflow and regulatory signal reporting obligations.']
  time_to_onset_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from the first dose of the suspect drug to the onset of the adverse reaction. A key pharmacovigilance metric used in signal detection, causality assessment, and benefit-risk evaluation. Derived from drug start date and reaction onset date during case processing.']
  verbatim_term varchar [note: 'The exact adverse event term as reported by the original reporter (patient, healthcare professional, or literature source) before MedDRA coding. Preserved verbatim for coding audit trail and signal detection. Required per ICH E2B(R3) field B.2.i.1.']

  Note: 'Granular adverse event record linked to an ICSR. Captures the specific event term (MedDRA coded at PT, HLT, HLGT, SOC levels with version tracking), verbatim reporter term, onset date, resolution date, outcome (recovered, not recovered, fatal, unknown), seriousness criteria, severity grade (CTCAE where applicable), and listedness determination against reference safety information. Includes coding metadata: auto-coded vs manual review, coding date. One ICSR may contain multiple adverse events. Supports signal detection aggregation by MedDRA term and regulatory line listing generation.'
}

Table pharmacovigilance.susar {
  susar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the SUSAR record in the pharmacovigilance data product. Primary key for the silver-layer lakehouse entity.']
