  adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.adverse_event. Business justification: A SUSAR classification originates from a clinical adverse event. Linking susar directly to patient.adverse_event enables expedited reporting traceability — regulators (EMA, FDA) require the source cli']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.application. Business justification: SUSARs are reported under a specific IND/CTA application. Linking susar to regulatory.application enables IND-level SUSAR tracking and annual IND safety report compilation per 21 CFR 312.33, tying eac']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: SUSARs tracked by brand for expedited regulatory reporting (FDA, EMA) and brand-specific safety monitoring during clinical development. Critical for development safety update reports (DSURs) and inves']
  drug_substance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: Substance-level SUSAR aggregation: SUSARs are reported per investigational product but aggregated by active substance across trials for development safety update reports (DSURs). Linking to drug_subst']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: SUSARs are reported to specific health authorities (FDA via IND, EMA via EudraCT/CTIS). A proper FK to health_authority enables authority-specific SUSAR reporting compliance tracking, deadline managem']
  icsr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) from which this SUSAR was identified. Links the SUSAR to its originating safety case in Argus Safety / Oracle AERS.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: SUSAR investigations in clinical trials generate specific costs for expedited assessments, unblinding procedures, and regulatory submissions. Finance tracks these via internal orders linked to trial b']
  investigational_compound_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.compound. Business justification: SUSARs are filed specifically for investigational compounds under an IND. Direct FK to discovery.compound is required for expedited 7/15-day IND safety reporting and replaces the denormalized investig']
  investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.investigational_product. Business justification: SUSARs reference the specific IMP batch and formulation for causality assessment and regulatory submission. investigational_product_name, investigational_product_dose, investigational_product_route on']
  investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.investigator. Business justification: ICH E2A and GCP require sponsors to notify the responsible investigator of SUSARs within defined timelines. Linking susar to investigator enables automated investigator notification workflows and prov']
  manufacturing_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: SUSARs involving investigational products require manufacturing plant identification for quality/manufacturing investigations and batch recall decisions. Linking SUSAR to the manufacturing plant of th']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.patient. Business justification: SUSARs (Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reactions) in clinical trials must be traceable to the specific trial subject for expedited regulatory reporting to FDA (IND safety reports), EMA (EudraVig']
  processing_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: SUSARs require expedited regulatory reporting (7/15-day clock) and are owned by specific PV org units. Org unit assignment supports submission deadline tracking, regulatory accountability, and GCP ins']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.project. Business justification: SUSARs are filed under a specific IND which belongs to a discovery project. Regulatory teams must associate SUSARs with the development project for IND annual reports, DSUR preparation, and clinical p']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.protocol. Business justification: SUSARs must be reported against the specific protocol version active at the time of the event per FDA 21 CFR 312.32 and EMA GVP Module VI. susar has trial_id but not protocol_id; the protocol version ']
  pv_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.pv_product. Business justification: A SUSAR is always associated with a specific investigational medicinal product under PV surveillance. The susar table captures investigational_product_name as a free-text string but lacks a structured']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: SUSARs must be expedited-reported under the IND/CTA regulatory submission. Linking susar to regulatory.submission enables IND safety reporting compliance tracking per 21 CFR 312.32 and EudraCT require']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial in which the SUSAR occurred. Used to associate the SUSAR with the relevant Investigational New Drug (IND) application and trial protocol.']
  argus_case_number varchar [note: 'The unique case number assigned by the Argus Safety / Oracle AERS pharmacovigilance system for this SUSAR. Serves as the system-of-record identifier for cross-system traceability.']
  assigned_treatment_arm varchar [note: 'The treatment arm to which the subject was assigned (e.g., investigational product, comparator, placebo), populated after unblinding. Required for causality assessment and regulatory reporting when unblinding has occurred.']
  blinding_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the clinical trial was blinded at the time the SUSAR was identified, and if so, whether unblinding occurred for this case. Unblinding for SUSAR reporting is a critical regulatory and trial integrity decision.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this SUSAR record was first created in the pharmacovigilance system (Argus Safety / Oracle AERS). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP.']
  ema_eudravigilance_submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the SUSAR was submitted to the EMA via the EudraVigilance Clinical Trial Module (EVCTM). Null if not yet submitted or not applicable.']
  expectedness_determination varchar [note: 'The sponsor\'s determination of whether the adverse reaction is expected or unexpected based on comparison against the current Investigator\'s Brochure (IB) or Reference Safety Information (RSI). An \'unexpected\' determination is a prerequisite for SUSAR classification.']
  fda_ind_submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the SUSAR was submitted to the FDA as an IND safety report under 21 CFR Part 312.32. Null if not yet submitted or not applicable. Used for SLA compliance tracking.']
  follow_up_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the follow-up report for this SUSAR (e.g., 1 for first follow-up, 2 for second). Zero or null for initial reports. Used to track the version history of regulatory submissions.']
  initial_receipt_date date [note: 'The date on which the sponsor first received valid minimum information about the adverse reaction, triggering the start of the expedited reporting clock. Day 0 for 7-day and 15-day SLA calculations per ICH E2A.']
  investigator_causality varchar [note: 'The treating investigator\'s assessment of the causal relationship between the investigational product and the adverse reaction, as reported on the Case Report Form (CRF). Captured separately from sponsor causality per ICH E2A requirements.']
  is_fatal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse reaction resulted in the death of the subject. A fatal outcome triggers the 7-day expedited reporting clock and is a primary driver of reporting urgency.']
  is_life_threatening boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse reaction was life-threatening at the time of occurrence (i.e., the subject was at immediate risk of death). Together with is_fatal, determines applicability of the 7-day reporting clock.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this SUSAR record was most recently modified in the pharmacovigilance system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and change tracking for regulatory submissions.']
  medical_review_date date [note: 'The date on which the qualified medical reviewer completed the medical assessment of this SUSAR. Part of the audit trail for regulatory compliance.']
  mhra_submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the SUSAR was submitted to the UK Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA). Null if not applicable.']
  narrative_text varchar [note: 'The free-text clinical narrative describing the SUSAR, including the sequence of events, medical history, treatment, and outcome. Required for all regulatory submissions per ICH E2B(R3). Classified as confidential due to sensitive clinical content.']
  pmda_submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the SUSAR was submitted to the Japan Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency (PMDA). Null if not applicable.']
  reaction_meddra_pt_code varchar [note: 'The MedDRA (Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities) Preferred Term code representing the primary adverse reaction. Mandatory for regulatory submissions to FDA, EMA, MHRA, and PMDA.']
  reaction_meddra_pt_name varchar [note: 'The human-readable MedDRA Preferred Term name corresponding to the reaction_meddra_pt_code. Stored for reporting clarity and to avoid repeated lookups against the MedDRA dictionary.']
  reaction_meddra_soc_name varchar [note: 'The MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) under which the primary adverse reaction is classified. Used for signal detection, aggregate analysis, and PSUR/PBRER reporting by therapeutic area.']
  reaction_onset_date date [note: 'The date on which the suspected unexpected serious adverse reaction first manifested in the subject. Used for temporal causality assessment and time-to-onset analysis.']
  reaction_outcome varchar [note: 'The outcome of the adverse reaction at the time of reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: recovered|recovering|not_recovered|recovered_with_sequelae|fatal|unknown — promote to reference product]']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SUSAR record within the pharmacovigilance workflow. Drives workflow routing in Argus Safety and regulatory submission tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|under_review|submitted|follow_up_pending|closed|invalidated — promote to reference product]']
  reporting_clock_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this SUSAR is subject to a 7-day expedited reporting clock (fatal or life-threatening) or a 15-day reporting clock (all other serious unexpected reactions). Drives SLA enforcement and regulatory deadline calculation.']
  reporting_deadline_date date [note: 'The calculated calendar date by which the SUSAR must be submitted to all applicable health authorities, derived from initial_receipt_date plus the applicable reporting clock (7 or 15 calendar days). SLA-critical field with criminal liability for non-compliance.']
  seriousness_criteria varchar [note: 'The ICH E2A seriousness criterion or criteria met by this adverse reaction, which qualifies it as serious. Multiple criteria may apply. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fatal|life_threatening|hospitalization_required|prolonged_hospitalization|persistent_disability|congenital_anomaly|medically_significant — promote to reference product]']
  sponsor_causality varchar [note: 'The sponsor\'s assessment of the causal relationship between the investigational product and the adverse reaction. A causality of \'related\' or \'not assessable\' (where benefit of doubt applies) is required for SUSAR classification.']
  subject_age varchar [note: 'The age of the clinical trial subject in years at the time the adverse reaction occurred. Required for regulatory submissions and demographic analysis. Classified as restricted PHI.']
  subject_sex varchar [note: 'The biological sex of the clinical trial subject as recorded in the case report. Required for ICH E2B(R3) regulatory submissions and demographic safety analysis. Classified as restricted PHI.']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this SUSAR record represents an initial report, a follow-up report with additional information, or a final report. Tracks the reporting lifecycle across multiple submissions to health authorities.']
  trial_phase varchar [note: 'The phase of the clinical trial in which the SUSAR occurred (Phase I through Phase IV). Influences regulatory reporting obligations and signal assessment context.']
  unblinding_date date [note: 'The date on which the treatment assignment was unblinded for this SUSAR case, if applicable. Null for open-label trials or cases where unblinding was not performed. Critical for audit trail and trial integrity documentation.']
  worldwide_unique_case_number varchar [note: 'The globally unique case identifier assigned per ICH E2B(R3) standards, formatted as country code + organisation identifier + local case number. Used for regulatory submissions and cross-authority deduplication.']

  Note: 'Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction record for clinical trial expedited reporting. Captures SUSARs identified from clinical trial ICSRs meeting expedited criteria: unexpected (not in IB/RSI), serious, and at least possibly causally related. Stores parent ICSR reference, trial phase, investigational product, expectedness determination against current IB, causality assessment by sponsor and investigator, 7-day (fatal/life-threatening) or 15-day reporting clock, and submission status to authorities (FDA IND safety report, EMA SUSAR, MHRA, PMDA). SLA-critical: 7-day and 15-day expedited reporting deadlines with criminal liability for non-compliance.'
}

Table pharmacovigilance.sae_report {
  sae_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the SAE report record in the pharmacovigilance safety database. Primary key for this entity.']
  adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.adverse_event. Business justification: SAE reports in PV are directly derived from serious adverse events in the clinical EDC system. sae_report.edc_case_reference and edc_reconciliation_status confirm this reconciliation process; a direct']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.application. Business justification: SAE reports under clinical trials are filed under the IND/CTA application. Linking sae_report to regulatory.application enables application-level SAE tracking for annual safety reports and regulatory ']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: SAEs in clinical trials linked to investigational brand for development safety database, regulatory submission packages (NDA/BLA), and brand-level safety profile characterization. Required for integra']
  clinical_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.clinical_enrollment. Business justification: SAE reports from blinded trials require the enrollment record to identify treatment arm for unblinding and causality assessment. sae_report has patient_id and trial_id but cannot identify the specific']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: SAE-to-product traceability: sae_report documents a serious adverse event for a specific drug product. Linking to drug_product enables product-level SAE tracking for regulatory submissions, labeling u']
  hco_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.hco_master. Business justification: SAE reports originate from clinical trial sites (HCOs). Linking sae_report to hco_master enables site-level SAE aggregation for IND/CTA regulatory submissions and site performance monitoring — a stand']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: sae_report.submission_authority is a denormalized plain attribute. A proper FK to health_authority enables authority-specific SAE reporting compliance dashboards, deadline tracking, and submission por']
  icsr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.icsr. Business justification: An SAE report is a primary source document that feeds into an ICSR (Individual Case Safety Report). In PV operations, an SAE report from a clinical trial is processed and converted into an ICSR for re']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: SAE investigations require budget allocation for additional medical reviews, site follow-up visits, and causality assessments. Internal orders capture these incremental costs, enabling trial financial']
  investigational_compound_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.compound. Business justification: SAE reports during clinical trials reference the investigational compound directly. For pre-approval compounds not yet registered as pv_products, a direct FK to discovery.compound is required for IND ']
  investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.investigational_product. Business justification: SAE reports from clinical trials must reference the specific IMP batch for causality assessment and potential product recall decisions. suspect_drug_dose and suspect_drug_route on sae_report are denor']
  investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.investigator. Business justification: IND/CTA SAE reporting regulations require identification of the responsible investigator. The investigator record carries GCP certification, protocol deviation history, and 1572 status — all required ']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.hcp_master. Business justification: SAE reports in clinical trials identify the site investigator who reported the event. Essential for query resolution, site communication, medical review, and regulatory inspection readiness per GCP re']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the patient or clinical trial subject associated with this SAE. Links to the master patient/subject record for demographic and medical history context.']
  processing_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: SAE reports are processed and owned by specific PV org units (regional safety teams). Org unit assignment is required for GCP audit trails, regulatory submission accountability, workload management, a']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.project. Business justification: SAE reports must be associated with the discovery/development project for aggregate safety analysis, DSUR preparation, and clinical program oversight. Safety operations teams routinely query SAEs by p']
  pv_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the suspect medicinal product (drug substance or drug product) associated with the SAE. Links to the product master for INN, ATC code, formulation, and regulatory approval details.']
  reporter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the healthcare professional, patient, or other reporter who submitted the SAE. Links to the reporter master record for qualification, contact, and reporter type details.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.sales_rep. Business justification: SAE reports received via field sales reps must be tracked to the collecting rep for follow-up obligations, AE reporting compliance audits, and rep training records. Pharma companies are required to tr']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: SAE reports under clinical trials are filed under the IND/CTA regulatory submission. Linking sae_report to regulatory.submission enables IND safety reporting compliance tracking per 21 CFR 312.32, tyi']
  susar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.susar. Business justification: The sae_report table has an is_susar boolean flag indicating that certain SAE reports qualify as SUSARs. When is_susar=true, the SAE report should be directly linked to the corresponding SUSAR record ']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial or study in which the SAE occurred. Links to the clinical trial master record for protocol, phase, and IND/CTA details. Null for spontaneous post-market reports.']
  visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.visit. Business justification: SAE reports from clinical trials are identified at a specific visit. Linking sae_report to the visit enables EDC-safety reconciliation and source data verification (SDV), a GCP requirement. sae_report']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric case identifier assigned by the pharmacovigilance system (Argus Safety/Oracle AERS) upon initial receipt of the SAE. Used for cross-system tracking, regulatory submissions, and reconciliation with EDC systems such as Medidata Rave.']
  causality_assessment varchar [note: 'Medical assessment of the causal relationship between the suspect medicinal product and the SAE, based on WHO-UMC or investigator assessment criteria. Drives SUSAR classification and regulatory reporting obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SAE report record was first entered into the pharmacovigilance safety database (Argus Safety/Oracle AERS). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GVP.']
  dechallenge_result varchar [note: 'Outcome observed when the suspect drug was withdrawn or dose reduced (dechallenge). A positive dechallenge (event resolved on withdrawal) supports a causal relationship. Required field in ICH E2B(R3) ICSR submissions.']
  edc_case_reference varchar [note: 'Case or subject reference number from the clinical trial EDC system (e.g., Medidata Rave subject ID or AE sequence number) used to reconcile the SAE record between the safety database and the clinical database.']
  edc_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of reconciliation between this SAE record in the pharmacovigilance safety database (Argus Safety) and the corresponding record in the clinical trial EDC system (Medidata Rave). Required per ICH E2D guidance to ensure consistency between clinical and safety databases.']
  is_fatal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SAE resulted in the death of the patient. Triggers mandatory expedited 7-day reporting to health authorities and escalation to senior medical review.']
  is_susar boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SAE has been classified as a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR), requiring expedited 7-day (fatal/life-threatening) or 15-day reporting to health authorities and notification to investigators.']
  listedness varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the adverse reaction is listed (expected) in the current Reference Safety Information (RSI) such as the Investigator Brochure or SmPC. Determines whether the event qualifies as unexpected for SUSAR classification.']
  meddra_pt_code varchar [note: 'Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Preferred Term code assigned to the primary SAE event term. Standard coding used for regulatory submissions, signal detection, and aggregate safety analysis.']
  meddra_pt_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable MedDRA Preferred Term label corresponding to the meddra_pt_code. Displayed in safety reports, regulatory submissions, and signal detection outputs.']
  meddra_soc_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA System Organ Class code representing the highest-level anatomical or physiological classification of the SAE. Used for aggregate safety reporting and PSUR tabulations.']
  medical_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the medical officer review of the SAE case. Tracks progression from initial triage through physician causality assessment and case narrative approval.']
  narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text medical narrative summarizing the SAE case including patient history, event description, clinical course, treatment, and outcome. Contains protected health information (PHI). Submitted as part of the ICSR to health authorities.']
  onset_date date [note: 'Date on which the serious adverse event first began or was first observed by the patient or healthcare professional. Used to calculate time-to-onset from first drug exposure.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Clinical outcome of the SAE at the time of reporting or case closure. Drives case narrative, aggregate safety reporting, and benefit-risk assessment in PSUR/PBRER.']
  patient_age_at_onset varchar [note: 'Age of the patient in years at the time the serious adverse event began. Used for demographic analysis, signal detection, and population-specific risk assessment.']
  patient_sex varchar [note: 'Biological sex of the patient as recorded at the time of the SAE. Used for demographic stratification in signal detection and aggregate safety reporting (PSUR/PBRER).']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which the company first received valid minimum information about the SAE (Day 0 for regulatory reporting clock). SLA-critical: initial SAE notification must be processed within 24 hours of site awareness per ICH E2D.']
  rechallenge_result varchar [note: 'Outcome observed when the suspect drug was re-administered after the initial adverse event (rechallenge). A positive rechallenge (event recurred) strongly supports causality. Required field in ICH E2B(R3) ICSR submissions.']
  regulatory_submission_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the ICSR/SUSAR was submitted to the health authority. Compared against regulatory_submission_due_date to assess compliance with reporting timelines.']
  regulatory_submission_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the SAE/SUSAR must be submitted to the relevant health authority (FDA, EMA, etc.). Calculated from receipt_date based on applicable reporting timeline (7-day for fatal/life-threatening SUSAR; 15-day for other SUSARs; 30-day for non-expedited).']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the SAE report from initial receipt through medical review, causality assessment, and regulatory submission. Drives SLA monitoring and submission queue management.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SAE report by its originating source. Determines applicable regulatory reporting timelines and submission pathways (e.g., 7-day vs 15-day expedited reporting).']
  seriousness_criteria varchar [note: 'One or more ICH E2A seriousness criteria met by this event: death, life-threatening, hospitalization (initial or prolonged), disability/incapacity, congenital anomaly/birth defect, or medically important event. Multiple criteria may apply. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: death|life_threatening|hospitalization|prolonged_hospitalization|disability|congenital_anomaly|medically_important — promote to reference product]']
  site_awareness_date date [note: 'Date on which the investigational site or sponsor first became aware of the SAE. Used to calculate the 24-hour initial notification SLA and the 7/15-day regulatory reporting clock per ICH E2A and GCP requirements.']
  time_to_onset_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between the first dose of the suspect medicinal product and the onset of the SAE. Key pharmacovigilance metric used in signal detection, aggregate analysis, and benefit-risk evaluation.']
  treatment_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the treatment(s) administered to manage the SAE, including concomitant medications, procedures, or interventions. Captured in the case narrative.']
  treatment_given boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specific treatment was administered to the patient in response to the SAE. Relevant for case narrative, medical review, and aggregate safety reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the SAE report record. Supports audit trail, version control, and reconciliation workflows per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  worldwide_unique_case_number varchar [note: 'Globally unique case identifier formatted per ICH E2B(R3) standard (country code + organisation identifier + local case number). Required for electronic ICSR submissions to FDA, EMA, and other health authorities.']

  Note: 'Serious Adverse Event report record capturing SAEs from clinical trials, post-market surveillance, and spontaneous sources. Stores SAE classification criteria (death, life-threatening, hospitalization, disability, congenital anomaly, medically important), time to onset, treatment given, dechallenge/rechallenge results, reporter information, site/investigator details, and reconciliation status with clinical trial EDC systems. Tracks the SAE from initial receipt through medical review, causality assessment, and regulatory submission. Supports reconciliation workflows between clinical database and PV safety database per ICH E2D guidance. SLA-critical: initial SAE notification within 24 hours of site awareness.'
}

Table pharmacovigilance.pv_submission {
  pv_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each pharmacovigilance regulatory submission record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory application (NDA, BLA, MAA, IND) under which this pharmacovigilance submission is filed. Links to the regulatory.application product. Enables traceability between safety submissions and the marketing authorisation or investigational application.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Periodic safety reports (PSURs, PADERs) and signal-driven submissions pertain to specific marketing approvals. Linking pv_submission to approval enables authority-level benefit-risk tracking tied to t']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Regulatory safety submissions (ICSRs, PSURs, SUSARs) must reference commercial brand for health authority tracking, compliance audit trails, and brand-level submission history. Essential for regulator']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Submission-level product tracking: pv_submission records regulatory submissions to health authorities. Linking to drug_product enables product-level submission tracking for regulatory intelligence, co']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Every PV submission targets a specific health authority. pv_submission.health_authority_code is a denormalized plain attribute. A proper FK enables authority-specific PV submission compliance dashboar']
  original_submission_pv_submission_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the original pv_submission_id when this record represents a resubmission, follow-up, or amendment. Null for original submissions. Enables reconstruction of the full resubmission history chain.']
  primary_pv_icsr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) that is the subject of this submission, when submission_type is an ICSR variant. Links to the source safety case record in Argus Safety / Oracle AERS. Null for aggregate report submissions (PSUR, PBRER, DSUR).']
  psur_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.psur. Business justification: Some PV submissions are for PSUR reports (submission_type indicates report type). Add psur_id FK (nullable - only populated for PSUR submission types). This links the submission transaction to the PSU']
  pv_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the medicinal product (Drug Product / Drug Substance) that is the subject of the safety report or aggregate submission. Links to the master product record.']
  responsible_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: PV submissions are executed by specific regulatory affairs or PV org units. Org unit ownership is required for submission workload reporting, regulatory deadline accountability, and inspection readine']
  rmp_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.rmp. Business justification: Some PV submissions are for RMP reports. Add rmp_id FK (nullable - only populated for RMP submission types). Remove rmp_version as it normalizes to rmp.version_number.']
  sae_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.sae_report. Business justification: SAE reports generate regulatory submissions. Add sae_report_id FK (nullable - only populated for SAE submission types). This links the submission transaction to the SAE master record.']
  safety_signal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pharmacovigilance safety signal record that prompted or is associated with this submission, where applicable. Links to the signal detection and management record. Null for routine ICSR submissions not linked to a formal signal.']
  submission_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Submission target country determines format (E2B R2/R3, CIOMS), gateway (EudraVigilance, FDA ESG, MHRA Yellow Card), and timelines. Operational requirement for submission routing and compliance tracki']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: PV submissions (E2B transmissions, PSURs, RMPs) are formal regulatory submissions tracked in the regulatory dossier. Linking pv_submission to regulatory.submission enables reconciliation of PV transmi']
  submitting_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: The legal entity making a PV submission is a core regulatory requirement — health authorities require MAH legal entity identification on all submissions. sender_organisation and sender_identifier are ']
  susar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.susar. Business justification: SUSAR records generate regulatory submissions (expedited reporting). Add susar_id FK (nullable - only populated for SUSAR submission types). One SUSAR can have multiple submissions (initial + follow-u']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.trial. Business justification: Expedited IND safety reports (15-day reports) are submitted to health authorities in the context of a specific clinical trial per FDA 21 CFR 312.32. Linking pv_submission to trial enables regulatory t']
  variation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.variation. Business justification: Safety-driven variations (urgent safety restrictions, Type II variations) generate pv_submission records for authority notification. Linking pv_submission to variation enables end-to-end tracking of s']
  acknowledgement_code varchar [note: 'Technical acknowledgement code returned by the health authority gateway per HL7 v2 / E2B messaging standards. AA = Application Accept; AE = Application Error; CA = Commit Accept; CE = Commit Error; TA = Technical Accept; TE = Technical Error. Drives automated error handling and resubmission workflows.']
  acknowledgement_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the technical acknowledgement (ACK) message was received from the health authority gateway confirming successful receipt of the submission. Distinct from substantive acceptance. Used for SLA compliance evidence.']
  argus_case_number varchar [note: 'The case number from the Argus Safety / Oracle AERS pharmacovigilance system that is the source of record for this submission. Enables direct traceability from the submission record back to the full safety case in the operational PV system.']
  authority_case_number varchar [note: 'The case or report reference number assigned by the receiving health authority upon acceptance of the submission (e.g., EudraVigilance case number, FDA FAERS case ID). Used for follow-up queries and regulatory correspondence. Populated after authority acceptance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pharmacovigilance submission record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and 21 CFR Part 11 / Annex 11 electronic records compliance.']
  date_of_first_receipt date [note: 'The date the company first received the minimum valid safety information constituting a reportable case (Day 0 for regulatory clock purposes). This is the anchor date from which the 7-day or 15-day expedited reporting deadline is calculated for ICSRs. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) C.1.4.']
  days_to_deadline varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days remaining between the current date and the regulatory_deadline at the time of last record update. Negative values indicate overdue submissions. Used for SLA monitoring dashboards and automatic escalation triggers. Note: this is a snapshot value captured at record update time, not a live calculation.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Structured error code returned by the health authority gateway or E2B validation engine upon rejection or technical failure (e.g., EudraVigilance business rule error code, FAERS conformance error code). Enables automated error categorisation and routing to the appropriate remediation team.']
  gateway_portal varchar [note: 'The specific electronic gateway or web portal used to transmit this submission to the health authority. Determines technical format and protocol requirements. EudraVigilance Gateway and EVWEB are EMA channels; FAERS Gateway is the FDA channel for ICSRs; MHRA Sentinel is the UK post-Brexit channel. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EUDRAVIGILANCE_GATEWAY|EUDRAVIGILANCE_EVWEB|FAERS_GATEWAY|MHRA_SENTINEL|PMDA_GATEWAY|NMPA_GATEWAY|VIGIBASE_WHO|DIRECT_EMAIL|PAPER — promote to reference product]']
  is_expedited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this submission is subject to expedited regulatory reporting requirements (7-day or 15-day clock). True = expedited; False = periodic/non-expedited. Drives SLA escalation logic and compliance monitoring.']
  is_on_time boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the submission was transmitted on or before the regulatory_deadline. True = submitted on time; False = submitted late. Key compliance KPI field populated at time of submission. Used for regulatory compliance reporting and CAPA tracking.']
  is_susar boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this submission relates to a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) from a clinical trial, which carries specific expedited reporting obligations to both health authorities and investigators under ICH E2A and GCP regulations.']
  message_format varchar [note: 'The technical format standard used for the submission message. E2B_R2_XML = ICH E2B(R2) XML format (used by some national authorities); E2B_R3_XML = ICH E2B(R3) XML format (required by EMA EudraVigilance and FDA from 2022); ECTD = Electronic Common Technical Document format (for aggregate reports); PDF = portable document format for ad-hoc communications; PAPER = legacy paper submission.']
  regulatory_deadline date [note: 'The mandatory regulatory deadline date by which this submission must be transmitted to the health authority, calculated from the date of first receipt (Day 0) of the safety information. For expedited ICSRs: Day 7 (fatal/life-threatening) or Day 15 (serious). For PSURs/PBRERs: per approved data lock point schedule. SLA-critical field used for automatic escalation.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured description of the reason(s) for rejection returned by the health authority gateway or authority reviewer when submission_status is REJECTED. Includes E2B validation error codes and human-readable explanations. Used for root cause analysis and CAPA.']
  report_period_end date [note: 'End date of the data collection period (Data Lock Point, DLP) for aggregate reports (PSUR, PBRER, PADER, DSUR). Defines the cut-off date for safety data included in the report. Null for individual case (ICSR) submissions.']
  report_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the data collection period covered by this submission. Applicable primarily to aggregate reports (PSUR, PBRER, PADER, DSUR) defining the International Birth Date (IBD) or period start. Null for individual case (ICSR) submissions.']
  report_type_qualifier varchar [note: 'Qualifier indicating whether this is an initial report, a follow-up update to a previously submitted case, or a nullification (withdrawal) of a previously submitted report. Corresponds directly to ICH E2B(R3) C.1.2 field.']
  responsible_qppv varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the Qualified Person for Pharmacovigilance (QPPV) responsible for this submission under EU GVP regulations. The QPPV bears legal accountability for the completeness and accuracy of pharmacovigilance submissions to the EMA and EU member states.']
  resubmission_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this submission has been resubmitted to the health authority following a rejection or technical error. Zero indicates the original submission was accepted without resubmission. Used to track submission quality and identify systemic data quality issues.']
  submission_date timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world timestamp when the pharmacovigilance report was transmitted to the health authority gateway or portal. This is the legally significant submission event time used for regulatory deadline compliance calculations.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pharmacovigilance submission workflow. DRAFT = in preparation; PENDING = queued for transmission; SUBMITTED = transmitted to health authority gateway; ACKNOWLEDGED = technical acknowledgement received from gateway; ACCEPTED = substantively accepted by authority; REJECTED = rejected with errors requiring correction; RESUBMITTED = corrected and resubmitted after rejection; WITHDRAWN = voluntarily withdrawn by the company. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DRAFT|PENDING|SUBMITTED|ACKNOWLEDGED|ACCEPTED|REJECTED|RESUBMITTED|WITHDRAWN — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pharmacovigilance submission by regulatory report type. ICSR_EXPEDITED_7DAY = fatal/life-threatening Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) requiring 7-calendar-day reporting; ICSR_EXPEDITED_15DAY = serious ICSR requiring 15-calendar-day reporting; ICSR_NON_EXPEDITED = non-expedited periodic ICSR; PSUR = Periodic Safety Update Report; PBRER = Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Report; PADER = Periodic Adverse Drug Experience Report; DSUR = Development Safety Update Report. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ICSR_EXPEDITED_7DAY|ICSR_EXPEDITED_15DAY|ICSR_NON_EXPEDITED|PSUR|PBRER|PADER|DSUR|AD_HOC_SAFETY_COMM|REMS_ASSESSMENT|SUSAR — promote to reference product]']
  submission_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of this submission for a given safety case or aggregate report. Version 1 = initial submission; Version 2+ = follow-up or amendment submissions. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) C.1.2 (report type: initial/follow-up/nullification).']
  transmission_reference varchar [note: 'Unique message transmission identifier assigned by the health authority gateway at the point of receipt (e.g., EudraVigilance message ID, FAERS transmission reference). Distinct from the authority_case_number; this is the technical envelope identifier used for gateway-level tracing and acknowledgement matching.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pharmacovigilance submission record was most recently modified in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and 21 CFR Part 11 / Annex 11 electronic records compliance.']

  Note: 'Regulatory submission record for all pharmacovigilance reports submitted to health authorities worldwide. Covers expedited individual case reports (7-day fatal/life-threatening, 15-day serious), periodic aggregate reports (PSUR, PBRER, PADER, DSUR), and ad-hoc safety communications. Stores submission type, target authority, submission date, acknowledgement status, error handling, submission format (E2B R2/R3 XML, eCTD), gateway/portal details, submission reference number, resubmission history, and compliance status. Supports all major regulatory gateways including EudraVigilance (EVWEB/gateway), FAERS (FDA), MHRA Sentinel, PMDA gateway, and NMPA. Tracks authority-specific technical requirements (E2B R3 for EMA, E2B R2 for some national authorities). SLA-critical: regulatory deadline compliance with automatic escalation.'
}

Table pharmacovigilance.psur {
  psur_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the PSUR/PBRER/PADER/DSUR master record in the pharmacovigilance data lakehouse.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing authorisation application (NDA/BLA/MAA) or investigational application (IND) under which this periodic safety report is submitted.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: PSURs are tied to specific marketing approvals. The approval record contains approval_date (international birth date reference), approved_indication, and conditions the PSUR must address. Direct link ']
  atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: PSURs are structured around ATC classifications for benefit-risk assessment and PSUSA grouping. ATC code is a core PSUR data element per ICH E2C(R2) and EMA PSUSA procedures. Linking psur to atc_class']
  drug_substance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: ICH E2C regulatory requirement: PSUR is a substance-based periodic safety report. Linking psur to drug_substance is fundamental to PSUR structure — the report covers all formulations of the active sub']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary health authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, NMPA) to which this PSUR is submitted. Determines applicable regulatory requirements and submission format.']
  indication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Indication-level PSUR benefit-risk: EMA and FDA require PSUR benefit-risk evaluation structured by approved indication. Linking psur to indication enables indication-specific cumulative safety summari']
  inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: PSURs are written for a specific active substance identified by INN — a core ICH E2C(R2) requirement. The INN is the primary identifier for PSUR scope definition and PSUSA procedures. Linking psur to ']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: PSUR preparation is a discrete, budgeted PV activity (medical writing, data analysis, regulatory affairs). Finance tracks actual vs. planned costs per PSUR cycle via internal orders. Pharma finance co']
  label_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.label. Business justification: PSURs assess whether the current label accurately reflects the benefit-risk profile. Linking psur to the label version under review enables tracking of label_update_required decisions against the spec']
  mah_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: PSUR is submitted by specific MAH legal entity. Determines signatory authority, regulatory obligations, and submission routing. Critical for regulatory compliance and multi-entity portfolio management']
  milestone_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.milestone_payment. Business justification: In pharma licensing and co-development agreements, PSUR data lock points or favorable benefit-risk conclusions trigger contractual milestone payments. Finance milestone_payment tracks these triggering']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: PSURs reference patent status for benefit-risk assessment context, especially when evaluating whether formulation changes covered by new patents affect safety profile. Patent expiry impacts market dyn']
  post_approval_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.post_approval_commitment. Business justification: PSURs are frequently submitted as fulfillment of post-approval commitments. Linking psur to post_approval_commitment enables tracking of PSUR-type commitment fulfillment status, a key regulatory compl']
  pv_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the medicinal product (drug substance or drug product) that is the subject of this periodic safety report.']
  responsible_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: PSURs are authored and owned by specific PV/regulatory org units. Org unit assignment is required for PSUR schedule management, resource planning, QPPV accountability, and regulatory audit — a standar']
  submission_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: PSURs are submitted to health authorities in specific countries/regions. Country is a core PSUR scheduling and submission tracking attribute per ICH E2C(R2). A proper FK to country enables regional PS']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: PSURs are submitted as formal regulatory submissions in the eCTD dossier. Linking psur to regulatory.submission enables tracking of PSUR submission sequence numbers, validation status, and eCTD operat']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.trial. Business justification: PSURs must reference ongoing and completed clinical trials contributing safety data in the reporting interval per GVP Module VII. A direct FK to the primary trial enables the PSUR author to pull trial']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the QPPV (or designated signatory) formally approved the PSUR for regulatory submission. Represents the final internal milestone before submission and is an auditable event per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  benefit_risk_conclusion varchar [note: 'Overall benefit-risk evaluation conclusion for the product as determined by the medical reviewer for this PSUR period. A finding of unfavourable or inconclusive may trigger regulatory action, label update, or risk management plan revision.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PSUR record was first created in the pharmacovigilance data lakehouse, providing the audit trail creation event per 21 CFR Part 11 and GDPR data governance requirements.']
  cumulative_case_count varchar [note: 'Total number of Individual Case Safety Reports (ICSRs) received from the IBD through the data lock point across all reporting intervals, providing the full post-marketing safety case history for the product.']
  cumulative_exposure_patient_years decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total cumulative patient-years of exposure to the product from IBD through data lock point, calculated from treatment duration data. Used as the primary denominator for incidence rate calculations in benefit-risk assessment.']
  cumulative_exposure_patients varchar [note: 'Estimated cumulative number of patients exposed to the product from the IBD through the data lock point, derived from post-marketing surveillance, clinical trials, and sales data. Used as denominator for cumulative incidence rate calculations.']
  data_lock_point date [note: 'The cut-off date up to which all safety data (adverse events, literature, clinical studies) are included in this PSUR. No new data arising after this date is incorporated into the current report period.']
  draft_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the initial draft of the PSUR was completed by the lead author and made available for internal review. Used to track preparation milestones against the submission deadline.']
  ha_assessment_outcome varchar [note: 'Outcome of the health authority\'s assessment of this PSUR, indicating any regulatory action required. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|no_action|label_update|rmp_update|referral|suspension|withdrawal — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  international_birth_date date [note: 'The date of first marketing authorisation for the product in any country worldwide, used as the anchor date for calculating PSUR submission deadlines and reporting intervals per ICH E2C(R2). SLA-critical field.']
  interval_case_count varchar [note: 'Total number of Individual Case Safety Reports (ICSRs) received during the current reporting interval (between interval_start_date and data_lock_point), including both spontaneous and solicited reports.']
  interval_end_date date [note: 'End date of the current PSUR reporting interval, which is typically equal to the data lock point. Defines the close of the reporting window for interval case counts and exposure data.']
  interval_sae_count varchar [note: 'Number of Serious Adverse Events (SAEs) reported during the current reporting interval. SAEs include fatal, life-threatening, hospitalisation-requiring, or permanently disabling events per ICH E2A criteria.']
  interval_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the current PSUR reporting interval (the period covered by this report). Together with interval_end_date defines the reporting window for interval case counts and exposure data.']
  interval_susar_count varchar [note: 'Number of Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reactions (SUSARs) identified during the current reporting interval. SUSARs require expedited reporting and are tracked separately for signal detection purposes.']
  label_update_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the safety findings in this PSUR require an update to the product label (SmPC, Package Insert, or Patient Information Leaflet). A True value initiates a label change management workflow.']
  medical_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the medical reviewer completed the clinical and benefit-risk review of the PSUR draft. Milestone in the PSUR preparation workflow tracked against the submission deadline.']
  new_safety_signals_count varchar [note: 'Number of new safety signals identified during the reporting interval through signal detection activities (disproportionality analysis, literature review, spontaneous reports). Drives benefit-risk re-evaluation.']
  psusa_procedure_number varchar [note: 'EMA-assigned procedure number for the PSUR Single Assessment (PSUSA) process applicable to centrally authorised products in the EU. Used to track the PSUSA workflow and outcome in Veeva Vault RIM and RIMS.']
  qc_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which quality control review of the PSUR was completed, verifying accuracy of case counts, exposure data, and regulatory formatting prior to QPPV approval and submission.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to this PSUR/PBRER/PADER/DSUR submission, used in regulatory correspondence and tracking systems such as Argus Safety and Veeva Vault RIM.']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle workflow state of the PSUR record, tracking progression from initial drafting through medical review, QC, approval, and regulatory submission. Drives SLA monitoring and escalation workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_review|qc_review|approved|submitted|accepted|withdrawn — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the periodic aggregate safety report: PSUR (Periodic Safety Update Report), PBRER (Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Report per ICH E2C R2), PADER (Periodic Adverse Drug Experience Report per FDA), DSUR (Development Safety Update Report for investigational products), or PSUSA (PSUR Single Assessment for EU centralised procedure).']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Regulatory-mandated submission frequency for this PSUR, which varies by product age, risk profile, and authority requirements. Drives automated SLA deadline calculation from the IBD.']
  reporting_period_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordinal number of this PSUR reporting period for the product (e.g., 1st, 2nd, 3rd PSUR), enabling chronological tracking of the full PSUR history for a product.']
  rmp_update_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the benefit-risk findings in this PSUR necessitate an update to the product\'s Risk Management Plan (RMP). A True value triggers a formal RMP revision workflow in the pharmacovigilance system.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Native record identifier from the originating pharmacovigilance system (Argus Safety, Oracle AERS, or Veeva Vault RIM) for this PSUR record, enabling traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the PSUR was submitted to the health authority. Compared against submission_deadline to determine on-time or late submission for regulatory compliance tracking.']
  submission_deadline date [note: 'Mandatory regulatory deadline by which this PSUR must be submitted to the applicable health authority, calculated from the IBD and reporting frequency. SLA-critical field monitored for compliance.']
  submission_format varchar [note: 'Electronic or paper format used for regulatory submission of this PSUR. eCTD (Electronic Common Technical Document) is the standard for FDA and EMA submissions; NeeS is a non-eCTD electronic submission format.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this PSUR record in the data lakehouse, supporting audit trail requirements and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and GDPR.']

  Note: 'Periodic aggregate safety report master record covering PSUR, PBRER (ICH E2C R2), PADER (FDA), and DSUR (development phase) for each product-indication combination. Captures data lock point, reporting interval, international birth date (IBD), cumulative exposure data, interval and cumulative case summaries, signal summary, benefit-risk evaluation conclusion, and submission status per applicable authority. Tracks preparation milestones, author assignments, medical review, QC, and approval workflow. Supports EU PSUR single assessment (PSUSA) process, FDA PADER requirements, and ICH DSUR for investigational products. Includes structured benefit-risk assessment sections. SLA-critical: fixed regulatory submission deadlines based on IBD.'
}

Table pharmacovigilance.rmp {
  rmp_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the Risk Management Plan record in the pharmacovigilance data lakehouse.']
  access_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.access_strategy. Business justification: RMP risk minimization commitments (REMS, additional monitoring) are mandatory inputs to market access strategy formulation. HTA bodies and payers require RMP alignment before granting reimbursement. M']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory application (NDA, BLA, MAA) to which this RMP is attached or submitted as a condition of approval.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing authorization or approval record that mandated or is associated with this RMP.']
  authority_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: RMPs are approved by health authorities in specific countries. Country of the approving authority is a core RMP tracking attribute per EU GVP Module V. A proper FK enables country-specific RMP version']
  drug_substance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: RMP safety specification: EMA GVP Module V requires the RMP safety specification to characterize the active substance\'s known and potential risks. Linking rmp to drug_substance enables substance-level']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: RMPs are submitted to and assessed by specific health authorities (EMA PRAC, national competent authorities). rmp.health_authority_code is a denormalized plain attribute. A proper FK enables authority']
  indication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Indication-specific risk minimization: RMP risk minimization measures (educational materials, REMS, restricted access) are defined per approved indication. EMA GVP Module V requires indication-level r']
  inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: RMPs are written for a specific active substance identified by INN — a core EU GVP Module V requirement. The INN defines the RMP scope and is required for EMA submission. Linking rmp to inn_registry i']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: RMP preparation, maintenance, and effectiveness evaluation are significant, budgeted PV activities. Each RMP version update and submission cycle incurs costs (consultants, regulatory affairs, medical ']
  mah_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Risk Management Plan ownership by legal entity determines regulatory accountability, update obligations, and PRAC submission responsibility. Essential for lifecycle safety management and regulatory co']
  patient_access_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.patient_access_program. Business justification: RMP risk minimization measures frequently mandate patient access programs (controlled distribution, pregnancy prevention registries, educational programs). The market patient_access_program operationa']
  previous_version_rmp_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediately preceding version of this RMP, enabling full version chain reconstruction and audit trail for regulatory inspections.']
  responsible_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: RMPs are managed by specific PV/regulatory org units responsible for risk minimization activities. Org unit ownership is required for RMP update scheduling, effectiveness evaluation accountability, an']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: RMPs are submitted as part of the regulatory dossier (eCTD Module 1.8.2). Linking rmp to regulatory.submission enables tracking which submission sequence included the RMP version, supporting variation']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RMP document with the relevant health authority: draft (in preparation), submitted (filed with authority), approved (accepted by authority), rejected (not accepted), superseded (replaced by newer version), or withdrawn.']
  argus_rmp_code varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this RMP record as stored in Argus Safety / Oracle AERS, used for data lineage, reconciliation, and integration with the pharmacovigilance safety database.']
  authority_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the health authority formally approved or accepted this version of the RMP. Null if not yet approved.']
  benefit_risk_conclusion varchar [note: 'Overall benefit-risk conclusion documented in this RMP version, reflecting the current assessment of the product\'s benefit-risk balance as evaluated by the marketing authorization holder and/or health authority.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this RMP record was first created in the pharmacovigilance data system, supporting audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and EU GVP Module I.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this version of the RMP becomes operationally effective and binding for pharmacovigilance activities and risk minimization measures.']
  effectiveness_evaluation_plan varchar [note: 'Description of the plan to evaluate the effectiveness of risk minimization measures, including key performance indicators, data sources, and evaluation timelines as required by EU GVP Module XVI.']
  identified_risk_count varchar [note: 'Number of important identified risks documented in the safety specification of this RMP version. An identified risk is one for which there is adequate evidence of an association with the medicinal product.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this RMP record in the data system, used for change tracking, data lineage, and regulatory audit trail compliance.']
  missing_information_count varchar [note: 'Number of missing information items documented in the safety specification, representing gaps in knowledge about the safety of the medicinal product (e.g., use in pregnancy, pediatric use).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next periodic review and potential update of the RMP, as agreed with the health authority or per internal PV schedule.']
  pass_commitment_count varchar [note: 'Number of Post-Authorization Safety Study (PASS) commitments included in this RMP version, representing studies imposed as conditions of marketing authorization or undertaken voluntarily.']
  potential_risk_count varchar [note: 'Number of important potential risks documented in the safety specification of this RMP version. A potential risk is one for which there is some basis for suspicion but insufficient evidence to confirm.']
  pv_plan_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the pharmacovigilance plan for this RMP includes only routine PV activities (spontaneous reporting, PSUR/PBRER) or also includes additional PV activities such as PASS studies, registries, or enhanced surveillance.']
  rems_program_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the FDA-approved REMS program associated with this RMP, if applicable (e.g., iPLEDGE for isotretinoin, TIRF REMS Access Program).']
  rems_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) is required by the FDA as part of the US risk management plan for this product. Applicable only for FDA submissions.']
  risk_minimization_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether risk minimization measures in this RMP are limited to routine measures (product labeling, SmPC, PIL) or also include additional risk minimization measures (educational materials, controlled access programs, REMS for US).']
  rmp_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the RMP document, used in regulatory submissions and authority correspondence (e.g., RMP-2024-001).']
  rmp_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the RMP format based on the regulatory authority template: EU GVP Module V (EMA/MHRA), FDA Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS), PMDA RMP (Japan), or WHO RMP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EU_RMP|FDA_REMS|PMDA_RMP|MHRA_RMP|WHO_RMP — promote to reference product]']
  safety_specification_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the safety specification section of the RMP, covering the epidemiology of the indication, non-clinical safety findings, clinical trial safety data, and post-marketing experience.']
  scope_indication varchar [note: 'The approved or proposed therapeutic indication(s) covered by this RMP, as defined in the marketing authorization or clinical development program (e.g., treatment of relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma).']
  scope_population varchar [note: 'Description of the target patient population covered by this RMP, including age groups, special populations (pediatric, geriatric, renal/hepatic impairment), and any population restrictions.']
  signal_detection_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier(s) to pharmacovigilance signal detection records or PRAC signal assessments that triggered or informed this RMP version update.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date on which this version of the RMP was formally submitted to the health authority as part of a regulatory dossier or standalone submission.']
  superseded_date date [note: 'Date on which this RMP version was superseded by a newer version. Null for the currently active version.']
  title varchar [note: 'Full descriptive title of the Risk Management Plan document, typically including the product name, indication, and version.']
  veeva_vault_doc_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault RIM (Regulatory Information Management) where the RMP document is stored and managed as part of the regulatory dossier.']
  version_change_rationale varchar [note: 'Documented rationale for updating this RMP version, including the trigger (e.g., new safety signal, PSUR outcome, authority request, new indication, PASS completion) and summary of changes from the previous version.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the RMP document (e.g., 1.0, 2.3), incremented with each regulatory submission or significant update. Supports version history tracking.']

  Note: 'Risk Management Plan master record. Captures RMP version, scope (product, indication, population), safety specification (important identified risks, important potential risks, missing information), pharmacovigilance plan activities (routine and additional), risk minimization measures (routine and additional including REMS for US), effectiveness evaluation plan, and PASS commitments. Tracks RMP approval status per authority (EMA, MHRA) and version history. Supports EU GVP Module V template, FDA REMS requirements, and PMDA RMP format. Links to resulting PV actions and PASS study commitments.'
}

Table pharmacovigilance.safety_signal {
  safety_signal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the safety signal record in the pharmacovigilance system. Primary key for the safety_signal data product.']
  atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: Signal detection and prioritization by therapeutic class enables risk-benefit assessment, class-effect evaluation, and regulatory communication strategy. Supports signal management workflows and PRAC ']
  brand_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand_plan. Business justification: Validated safety signals that require label changes or risk minimization directly impact brand plans. Signal management teams formally notify brand plan owners; this link enables commercial impact ass']
  compound_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.compound. Business justification: Safety signals during clinical development are compound-specific before a pv_product is established. Signal management teams need direct compound traceability for investigational product signal assess']
  drug_substance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: Substance-level signal aggregation: safety signals are frequently detected at the active substance level across multiple formulations and markets. Linking to drug_substance enables cross-product signa']
  indication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Indication-specific benefit-risk: safety signals are evaluated per approved indication to determine whether the benefit-risk balance is affected. PRAC signal assessments and PSUR referrals require ind']
  inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: Safety signals are detected for specific active substances (INN). Signal management databases (EudraVigilance, FAERS) track signals by INN/substance across products. Linking safety_signal to inn_regis']
  label_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.label. Business justification: Safety signals with label_change_recommended=true must reference the specific label version to be updated. This enables signal management workflow to directly identify and queue the affected label for']
  molecular_target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.molecular_target. Business justification: Safety signal validation requires molecular target review. Signal assessment committees evaluate target biology, pathway, tissue expression, and known class effects to determine if the signal is mecha']
  owning_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: Safety signals are assessed and owned by specific PV org units (signal management team, therapeutic area safety team). Org unit ownership is required for signal triage workflow, PRAC notification acco']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Safety signal management requires IP teams to assess whether a label change triggered by a signal affects patent claims or exclusivity. Signal closure reports and regulatory action decisions reference']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.project. Business justification: Safety signals are managed at the development project level for portfolio safety reviews, DSUR signal sections, and go/no-go decisions on clinical progression. Signal management teams routinely aggreg']
  pv_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the medicinal product (drug substance or drug product) associated with this safety signal. Links to the master product entity for the implicated compound.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Safety signals requiring regulatory action result in formal submissions (label changes, variations, PSUR referrals). Linking safety_signal to the resulting regulatory submission enables end-to-end sig']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.trial. Business justification: Safety signals detected from clinical trial data must be traceable to the originating trial for PRAC notification and signal triage (GVP Module IX). safety_signal has source_reference_number as text b']
  analysis_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the data analysis period used for signal detection. Together with analysis_period_start_date, defines the cumulative or interval data window reviewed.']
  analysis_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the data analysis period used for signal detection. Defines the temporal window of adverse event data reviewed to identify the signal.']
  assessment_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the formal signal assessment findings, including methodology applied, data sources reviewed, clinical interpretation, and conclusions. Supports regulatory dossier preparation and PRAC submissions.']
  benefit_risk_impact varchar [note: 'Overall benefit-risk impact determination resulting from the signal assessment. Indicates whether the signal changes the benefit-risk balance of the product as favorable, unfavorable, uncertain, or neutral.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the safety signal was formally closed following completion of all assessment activities and regulatory actions. Represents the end of the signal management lifecycle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the safety signal record was first created in the pharmacovigilance system (Argus Safety / Oracle AERS). Used for audit trail and data lineage compliance per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  data_source_database varchar [note: 'Name of the specific pharmacovigilance database or data source from which the signal was detected (e.g., FDA FAERS, WHO VigiBase, EudraVigilance, company spontaneous database, published literature). Supports traceability of signal origin.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to identify the safety signal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: disproportionality_analysis|clinical_review|machine_learning|literature_review|spontaneous_report|regulatory_authority — promote to reference product]']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the safety signal was first detected or identified, representing the principal real-world event time. Critical for SLA tracking of PRAC rapporteur notification timelines and regulatory reporting deadlines.']
  ebgm_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Empirical Bayes Geometric Mean (EBGM) — a Bayesian disproportionality metric used in the FDA FAERS database analysis. EBGM ≥2 is a commonly applied threshold for signal detection.']
  fatal_case_count varchar [note: 'Number of fatal outcome cases among the cases contributing to this safety signal. Fatal cases receive highest priority in signal triage and may trigger expedited regulatory notification.']
  ic_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Information Component (IC) — a Bayesian disproportionality metric used by the WHO Uppsala Monitoring Centre (UMC) VigiBase. Positive IC values indicate a drug-event association stronger than expected by chance.']
  label_change_recommended boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the signal assessment has resulted in a formal recommendation to update the product label (SmPC, USPI, or equivalent). Triggers regulatory submission activities.']
  meddra_pt_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) code representing the primary adverse event or medical concept associated with this safety signal. Standardized coding per MedDRA hierarchy for regulatory submissions and signal tracking.']
  meddra_pt_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable MedDRA Preferred Term name corresponding to the meddra_pt_code. Provides the standardized medical terminology label for the safety signal event.']
  meddra_soc_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) code for the primary adverse event, providing the highest-level classification in the MedDRA hierarchy. Used for signal clustering and therapeutic area analysis.']
  prac_notification_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the PRAC rapporteur was notified of the safety signal. Compared against prac_notification_due_date to assess SLA compliance.']
  prac_notification_due_date date [note: 'SLA-critical deadline by which the PRAC rapporteur must be notified of the validated safety signal per EMA regulatory requirements. Drives escalation workflows and compliance monitoring.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Triage priority assigned to the safety signal based on clinical seriousness, signal strength, and public health impact. Drives SLA timelines for assessment completion and regulatory notification.']
  prr_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Proportional Reporting Ratio (PRR) — a disproportionality analysis metric quantifying the ratio of the proportion of reports for a drug-event combination versus all other drugs. Values >2 with chi-squared >4 and ≥3 cases typically indicate a signal.']
  psur_referral_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this safety signal has been referred for inclusion and evaluation in the next Periodic Safety Update Report (PSUR) / Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Report (PBRER).']
  regulatory_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the safety signal assessment has determined that a regulatory action is required (e.g., label update, DHPC, REMS modification, product withdrawal). Drives downstream regulatory affairs workflows.']
  regulatory_action_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory action determined to be required based on the signal assessment outcome. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: label_update|rems_modification|dhpc|psur_referral|product_withdrawal|contraindication_addition|none — promote to reference product]']
  rmp_update_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the signal assessment outcome requires an update to the product\'s Risk Management Plan (RMP). Triggers regulatory affairs and pharmacovigilance team coordination.']
