  ror_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Reporting Odds Ratio (ROR) — a disproportionality analysis metric representing the odds of a specific adverse event being reported for a drug versus all other drugs. Complements PRR in signal detection.']
  serious_case_count varchar [note: 'Number of Serious Adverse Event (SAE) cases among the total cases contributing to this signal. Serious cases include fatal, life-threatening, hospitalization, disability, or congenital anomaly outcomes per ICH E2A.']
  signal_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the safety signal including the nature of the concern, clinical context, patient population affected, and initial evidence summary. Supports signal triage and assessment documentation.']
  signal_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier assigned to the safety signal upon creation in Argus Safety / Oracle AERS. Used for cross-system referencing, regulatory correspondence, and PRAC rapporteur notifications.']
  signal_source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the data source from which the safety signal was detected. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spontaneous_database|literature|clinical_trial|real_world_data|social_media|health_authority — promote to reference product]']
  signal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the safety signal per GVP Module IX workflow: new (detected, not yet triaged), under_evaluation (active assessment in progress), validated (confirmed as a genuine safety concern), refuted (assessed and dismissed), closed (final disposition reached and documented).']
  signal_title varchar [note: 'Concise, human-readable title describing the safety signal, typically formatted as [Drug Name] and [Adverse Event Term]. Used in regulatory correspondence, PRAC submissions, and internal safety committee communications.']
  signal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the signal as new (not previously known), strengthened (existing signal with increased evidence), weakened (existing signal with decreased evidence), or refuted (previously identified signal now dismissed).']
  source_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier from the originating data source (e.g., FAERS case number, EudraVigilance report ID, literature PubMed ID, clinical trial NCT number) that triggered or contributed to the signal detection.']
  triage_date date [note: 'Date on which the safety signal was formally triaged by the pharmacovigilance team to determine priority and assign for full assessment. Marks the transition from detection to active evaluation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the safety signal record. Supports audit trail requirements and tracks signal assessment progression through the lifecycle.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date on which the safety signal was formally validated as a genuine safety concern following initial assessment. Triggers downstream regulatory notification and benefit-risk evaluation activities.']

  Note: 'Safety signal record representing a new or evolving safety concern identified through signal detection activities. Captures signal source (spontaneous database, literature, clinical trial, real-world data), detection method (disproportionality analysis, clinical review, machine learning), MedDRA-coded term, signal strength metrics (PRR, ROR, EBGM, IC), and signal status lifecycle (new, under evaluation, validated, refuted, closed). Includes formal assessment records: methodology, data sources reviewed, benefit-risk impact evaluation, proposed label change recommendation, regulatory action required, PRAC/PSUR referral status, and final disposition. Each signal supports multiple assessment iterations. Tracks the full signal management workflow from detection through triage, validation, prioritization, assessment, and closure or regulatory action per GVP Module IX. SLA-critical: PRAC rapporteur notification timelines.'
}

  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: pv_product.authorization_number is a denormalized attribute mapping to a marketing authorization. A proper FK to regulatory.approval enables the QPPV to track which approved products are under active ']
  atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Replaces text atc_code with proper master data refer']

A direct FK enables period-based PV planning queries without t']

  indication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.indication.   controlled_substance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product contains a controlled substance subject to DEA scheduling requirements. True = controlled substance; False = not a controlled substance. Impacts PV reporting and distribution controls.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the PV product master record was first created in the pharmacovigilance system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and EU GVP Module II.']
  ema_product_number varchar [note: 'EMA-assigned product number for centrally authorised products in the EU. Used for EudraVigilance submissions, PSUR repository submissions, and EU regulatory correspondence.']
  eudravigilance_mp_code varchar [note: 'EudraVigilance Medicinal Product Dictionary (EVMPD) identifier assigned by EMA for the product. Required for electronic ICSR submissions to EudraVigilance and EU regulatory reporting.']
  expedited_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product is subject to expedited (7-day or 15-day) Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) reporting obligations to health authorities. Drives case processing timelines in Argus Safety.']
  international_birth_date date [note: 'International Birth Date (IBD) — the date of first marketing authorisation granted worldwide for the active substance. Serves as the reference date for PSUR/PBRER data lock point scheduling per ICH E2C(R2).']
  orphan_designation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product holds an orphan drug designation for a rare disease indication. True = orphan designation granted; False = no orphan designation. Affects PV reporting thresholds and PSUR requirements.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the medicinal product by its scientific/regulatory category (e.g., small molecule, biologic, vaccine, gene therapy, cell therapy, combination product). Drives applicable regulatory framework and PV requirements.']
  psmf_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this product to the applicable Pharmacovigilance System Master File (PSMF) that describes the PV system governing this product. Mandatory for EU GVP Module II compliance.']
  psur_due_date date [note: 'Next scheduled due date for submission of the Periodic Safety Update Report (PSUR) or Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Report (PBRER) to the relevant health authority. Calculated from IBD and submission frequency.']
  psur_frequency varchar [note: 'Regulatory-mandated frequency for Periodic Safety Update Report (PSUR) / Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Report (PBRER) submission for this product. Drives the PSUR scheduling calendar.']
  pv_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the product within the pharmacovigilance system. Drives case processing eligibility, signal detection inclusion, and PSUR scheduling. Active = currently under PV surveillance; Suspended = temporarily halted; Withdrawn = marketing authorisation withdrawn; Under_review = under regulatory review; Archived = historical record only.']
  rems_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) is required by the FDA for this product. True = REMS is mandated; False = REMS not required. Applicable to US-authorised products only.']
  rmp_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Risk Management Plan (RMP) is required for this product by the applicable health authority. True = RMP is mandated; False = RMP not required. Drives risk minimisation activity tracking.']
  signal_detection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product is currently included in the active signal detection and management programme. True = included in signal detection runs; False = excluded (e.g., archived or withdrawn products).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the PV product master record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and EU GVP Module II.']

  Note: 'Pharmacovigilance product master — the authoritative PV-domain view of medicinal products under safety surveillance. Captures product name, INN, ATC code, MAH (Marketing Authorization Holder), authorization numbers per country/region, formulation, route of administration, reference safety information document reference, international birth date (IBD), PSMF reference, QPPV assignment, and PV system governance metadata. Distinct from the commercial product catalog — this is the PV-specific product registration used for case processing, signal detection, PSUR scheduling, and regulatory reporting. Includes PSMF-level metadata: PV system description, organizational structure for pharmacovigilance, contracted party references, and compliance status per EU GVP Module II.'
}

Table pharmacovigilance.suspect_drug {
  suspect_drug_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the suspect or concomitant drug record within an Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR). Primary key of this entity.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: suspect_drug.drug_authorization_number is a denormalized attribute mapping to a marketing authorization. A proper FK to regulatory.approval enables traceability from adverse event case to the specific']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Suspect drugs in case reports linked to brand for causality assessment, brand-specific safety profiling, and competitive safety benchmarking. Supports brand safety narrative development and medical af']
  compound_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.compound. Business justification: The suspect drug in an ICSR for an investigational compound must link directly to the discovery compound for case processing, duplicate detection, and aggregate analysis. suspect_drug links to pv_prod']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Core PV traceability: suspect_drug must link to the exact drug_product formulation for signal detection, batch-level safety analysis, and regulatory submissions. PV experts expect this link to identif']
  drug_substance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: Substance-level signal detection: the same API appears in multiple formulations. Linking suspect_drug to drug_substance enables cross-product signal aggregation by active ingredient, a standard PV and']
  icsr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) to which this suspect or concomitant drug record belongs. One ICSR may contain multiple drug records.']
  inventory_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: PV case processing requires tracing a suspect drug\'s implicated batch to the actual inventory_lot for distribution scope assessment, cold chain compliance review, and batch recall decisions. Regulator']
  investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.investigational_product. Business justification: The suspect drug in a trial ICSR is the investigational product. Linking enables batch-level traceability for causality assessment and potential recall decisions per ICH E2B R3. suspect_drug has verba']
  label_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.label. Business justification: Listedness assessment in ICSR processing requires knowing the current approved label for the suspect drug. Linking suspect_drug to regulatory.label enables automated listedness determination against t']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.hcp_master. Business justification: Tracking prescribing physician for suspect drugs enables prescriber-pattern analysis in signal detection, targeted DHPC distribution, and prescriber follow-up for dechallenge/rechallenge outcomes. Cri']
  masterdata_ndc_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.ndc_code. Business justification: US ICSRs require NDC-level product identification for FDA submissions. Enables precise product matching, regulatory compliance, and integration with FDA Structured Product Labeling (SPL) for listednes']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Suspect drugs must link to material master for batch traceability, quality investigations, and manufacturing deviation correlation. Enables root cause analysis when batch_lot_number indicates potentia']
  pv_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.pv_product. Business justification: Suspect drugs in ICSRs should link to the PV product master for authoritative product data. Add pv_product_id FK. Remove product_name, inn_name, atc_code as they normalize to pv_product (verified pv_p']
  treatment_exposure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.treatment_exposure. Business justification: Batch/lot-level safety investigations require tracing a PV suspect drug record to the specific treatment_exposure (lot_number, dose_administered, administration_date). This link is essential for batch']
  action_taken varchar [note: 'Action taken with the suspect or concomitant drug in response to the adverse event (e.g., drug withdrawn, dose reduced, dose not changed, unknown). Critical for dechallenge assessment and causality evaluation. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.8.']
  coding_status varchar [note: 'Status of the WHO Drug Dictionary coding process for this drug record: coded (coding complete), pending (awaiting coding), not coded (unable to code), auto-coded (coded by automated system), or manually coded (coded by a pharmacovigilance specialist).']
  country_of_administration varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the drug was administered to the patient. Used for regional signal detection and regulatory reporting stratification.']
  cumulative_dose_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total cumulative dose of the drug received by the patient from start to stop date (or to the date of the adverse event). Used in dose-response analyses and exposure-based signal detection. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.5a.']
  cumulative_dose_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the cumulative dose amount (e.g., mg, g, IU). Must be interpreted in conjunction with cumulative_dose_amount. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.5b.']
  dechallenge_result varchar [note: 'Outcome observed after the drug was withdrawn or dose reduced (dechallenge): whether the adverse event improved or resolved. A positive dechallenge supports causality. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.9.i.3.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'Physical form of the drug product administered to the patient (e.g., tablet, capsule, injection, patch, solution). Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.4.r.9.2. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|injection|solution|patch|cream|powder|suspension|inhaler|suppository — promote to reference product]']
  dose_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric quantity of the drug administered per dose as reported. Must be interpreted in conjunction with dose_unit. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.4.r.1a.']
  dose_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the dose amount (e.g., mg, mcg, g, mg/kg, IU, mL). Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.4.r.1b. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mg|mcg|g|mg/kg|IU|mL|mg/mL|% — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dosing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the drug was administered (e.g., once daily, twice daily, weekly). Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.4.r.3. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: once daily|twice daily|three times daily|four times daily|weekly|biweekly|monthly|as needed|continuous|every 8 hours — promote to reference product]']
  drug_role varchar [note: 'Role of the drug in the adverse event case: suspect (primary drug under investigation), concomitant (taken alongside but not suspected), interacting (suspected drug-drug interaction), or treatment (used to treat the adverse event). Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.1.']
  drug_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number identifying the position of this drug record within the parent ICSR, used for ordering and referencing multiple drugs in a single case (e.g., drug 1, drug 2). Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.1.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the drug product batch administered to the patient. Used in product quality investigations to assess whether an expired product may have contributed to the adverse event. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.4.r.8.']
  indication varchar [note: 'Medical condition or disease for which the drug was prescribed or taken, coded using MedDRA terminology. Supports indication-stratified signal detection and benefit-risk assessment. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.7.r.2b.']
  indication_verbatim varchar [note: 'Indication for drug use as reported verbatim by the reporter, prior to MedDRA coding. Preserved for traceability and coding audit. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.7.r.2a.']
  is_blinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the drug identity is blinded (True) in the context of a clinical trial (e.g., randomized controlled trial where the patient or investigator does not know the assigned treatment). Affects SUSAR reporting and unblinding procedures.']
  is_company_product boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the drug is a product owned or marketed by the reporting company (True) or a competitor/third-party product (False). Determines regulatory reporting obligations and case ownership.']
  parent_drug_code varchar [note: 'WHO Drug Dictionary parent drug code representing the active moiety or parent compound, used to group drug products sharing the same active substance for aggregate signal detection and PSUR analyses.']
  rechallenge_result varchar [note: 'Outcome observed after the drug was reintroduced following dechallenge (rechallenge): whether the adverse event recurred. A positive rechallenge is strong evidence of causality. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.9.i.4.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this suspect or concomitant drug record was first created in the pharmacovigilance system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GVP Module VI.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this suspect or concomitant drug record was last modified in the pharmacovigilance system. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11 and GVP Module VI.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'Route by which the drug was administered to the patient (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, intramuscular, topical). Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.4.r.10.2. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|intravenous|subcutaneous|intramuscular|topical|inhalation|transdermal|intrathecal — promote to reference product]']
  source_system_drug_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of this drug record in the originating pharmacovigilance source system (e.g., Argus Safety case drug ID or Oracle AERS drug sequence identifier). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the system of record.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date on which the patient started taking the drug. Used to calculate drug exposure duration and temporal relationship to adverse event onset. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.4.r.4.']
  stop_date date [note: 'Date on which the patient stopped taking the drug. Nullable if the drug was ongoing at the time of the report. Used to calculate exposure duration and assess dechallenge timing. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.4.r.5.']
  time_to_onset_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between the drug start date and the onset of the adverse event. A key temporal metric for causality assessment and signal detection algorithms (e.g., disproportionality analysis).']
  verbatim_drug_name varchar [note: 'Drug name exactly as reported by the reporter (patient, healthcare professional, or other source) before any coding or standardization. Preserved for traceability and audit. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.2.2.']
  who_drug_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned by the WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD) to the coded medicinal product. Used for product-level case aggregation, line listing, and signal detection analytics.']
  who_drug_name varchar [note: 'Standardized drug name as coded against the WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD), representing the preferred term for the medicinal product. Enables cross-case aggregation and signal detection.']

  Note: 'Suspect and concomitant drug record within an ICSR. Captures drug name (verbatim and WHO Drug Dictionary coded), drug role (suspect, concomitant, interacting), dose, frequency, route, indication, start/stop dates, action taken (drug withdrawn, dose reduced, dose not changed, unknown), dechallenge result, rechallenge result, and batch/lot number. One ICSR may have multiple suspect and concomitant drugs. Enables product-level case aggregation for signal detection, line listing generation, and exposure-based analyses.'
}

Table pharmacovigilance.reporter {
  reporter_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the pharmacovigilance reporter record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the reporter master data entity.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Reporter country determines follow-up feasibility, data privacy regulations (GDPR vs others), contact protocols, and language requirements. Operational necessity for case management and regulatory com']
  hco_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.hco_master. Business justification: Reporters (pharmacists, hospital physicians) are affiliated with HCOs. Linking reporter to hco_master supports institutional-level reporting pattern analysis, follow-up management, and GDPR data stewa']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.hcp_master. Business justification: When reporter is HCP (is_hcp flag), linking to hcp_master enables license verification, specialty validation for causality assessment, NPI matching for regulatory submissions, and structured follow-up']
  master_reporter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the canonical/master reporter record when this record has been identified as a duplicate. Null if this record is itself the master. Supports deduplication and case re-linking workflows in the PV system.']
  masterdata_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.address. Business justification: Structured address linkage enables standardized contact management, duplicate reporter detection, and GDPR compliance (right to erasure). Replaces fragmented address fields with master data reference ']
  anonymized_reporter_code varchar [note: 'Privacy-compliant pseudonymous identifier assigned to the reporter in lieu of personal identifiers, enabling case aggregation and follow-up management without exposing PII. Mandatory under GDPR and HIPAA for PV processing.']
  argus_reporter_code varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for the reporter as assigned by Argus Safety / Oracle AERS. Used for lineage tracing and deduplication against the operational PV system.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the reporter provided or withdrew consent to be contacted for follow-up. Required for GDPR audit trail demonstrating lawful basis for processing personal data.']
  consent_to_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reporter has provided explicit consent to be contacted for follow-up queries regarding the adverse event report. Mandatory consent gate under GDPR Article 7 before any follow-up communication is initiated.']
  contact_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for follow-up communication as indicated by the reporter. Drives the follow-up workflow in Argus Safety. \'no_contact\' indicates the reporter has declined further contact, which must be respected per GDPR consent obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reporter record was first created in the PV system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 for electronic records in regulated pharmaceutical environments.']
  department varchar [note: 'Department or unit within the reporter\'s organization (e.g., Oncology Ward, Clinical Pharmacology, Drug Safety). Supports routing of follow-up queries to the correct organizational unit.']
  duplicate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reporter record has been identified as a potential or confirmed duplicate of another reporter record. Duplicate reporters must be resolved before case processing to prevent double-counting in aggregate safety analyses.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the reporter for electronic follow-up correspondence. Classified as restricted PII. Used only for PV case follow-up; not shared with commercial systems. Consent-gated per GDPR.']
  family_name varchar [note: 'Last/family name of the individual reporter. Stored in restricted classification per GDPR and HIPAA. Used for follow-up correspondence and duplicate detection. May be withheld or anonymized per reporter consent.']
  fax varchar [note: 'Fax number of the reporter or their institution, used for formal follow-up correspondence in jurisdictions where fax remains a regulatory-accepted communication channel (e.g., Japan PMDA submissions).']
  first_report_date date [note: 'Date on which the reporter submitted their first adverse event report to the company. Used for longitudinal reporter relationship analysis and tenure-based reliability assessment.']
  follow_up_deadline date [note: 'Target date by which a follow-up response from the reporter is required to meet regulatory submission timelines (e.g., 15-day expedited reporting for SUSARs and SAEs). Calculated based on initial receipt date and applicable regulatory clock.']
  follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether outstanding follow-up queries have been sent to this reporter and a response is pending. Drives the follow-up management workflow in Argus Safety to ensure timely case completion within regulatory timeframes.']
  gdpr_erasure_date date [note: 'Date on which the GDPR Right to Erasure request was processed and PII fields were anonymized. Null if no erasure request has been received. Required for GDPR compliance audit trail.']
  gdpr_erasure_requested boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reporter has submitted a Right to Erasure (Right to be Forgotten) request under GDPR Article 17. When true, PII fields must be anonymized while retaining the anonymized case record for pharmacovigilance regulatory obligations under GDPR Article 17(3)(c).']
  given_name varchar [note: 'First/given name of the individual reporter. Stored in restricted classification per GDPR and HIPAA. Used for follow-up correspondence and duplicate detection. May be withheld or anonymized per reporter consent.']
  is_hcp boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reporter is a qualified Healthcare Professional (HCP). HCP-reported cases carry higher regulatory weight and may trigger expedited 15-day reporting obligations to FDA, EMA, and other health authorities.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-2 language code representing the reporter\'s preferred communication language. Used to route follow-up correspondence to appropriately qualified multilingual PV staff and to select translated follow-up letter templates.']
  last_report_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent adverse event report submitted by this reporter. Used to identify active versus dormant reporters and to support follow-up prioritization.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'Professional license or registration number of the HCP reporter (e.g., medical license, pharmacy registration). Used to verify reporter qualification and credibility. Stored as confidential business data.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for PV case processor notes regarding the reporter, such as communication history, special handling instructions, or context about the reporter\'s relationship to the case. Not transmitted in regulatory submissions.']
  organization_name varchar [note: 'Name of the institution, hospital, clinic, regulatory authority, or company from which the reporter submitted the adverse event report. Relevant for institutional reporters and regulatory authority submissions.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number of the reporter for follow-up contact. Classified as restricted PII. Stored in E.164 international format where possible. Used exclusively for PV case follow-up activities.']
  qualification varchar [note: 'Professional qualification of the reporter, particularly relevant for Healthcare Professional (HCP) reporters. Determines the clinical credibility weighting of the report and regulatory classification. ICH E2B(R3) field A.2.1.2. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: physician|pharmacist|nurse|dentist|other_health_professional|consumer|unknown — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  regulatory_authority_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, NMPA) when the reporter is a regulatory body. Used to route mandatory ICSR submissions and track authority-initiated follow-up requests.']
  reliability_score varchar [note: 'Internal PV team assessment of the reporter\'s historical reliability based on completeness and accuracy of prior submissions. Used to weight signal detection analysis and prioritize follow-up resources. Assigned by the PV case processor.']
  reporter_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the reporter record within the PV system. Inactive or blocked reporters are excluded from new case assignments. Supports duplicate detection and data quality management.']
  reporter_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reporter by their relationship to the adverse event. Drives regulatory reporting obligations and follow-up workflows. ICH E2B(R3) field A.2.1.1. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: healthcare_professional|patient_consumer|lawyer|regulatory_authority|literature|other — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the original source through which the adverse event report was received. Determines regulatory handling requirements; e.g., literature-sourced reports require medical literature monitoring per GVP Module VI. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spontaneous|literature|clinical_trial|regulatory_authority|solicited|other — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  specialty varchar [note: 'Medical or clinical specialty of the HCP reporter (e.g., Oncology, Cardiology, Neurology). Supports signal detection analysis by therapeutic area and enables targeted follow-up by medically qualified personnel.']
  total_reports_submitted varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of Individual Case Safety Reports (ICSRs) submitted by this reporter. Used for duplicate detection, reporter profiling, and reliability assessment. Updated upon each new case linkage.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the reporter record. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GDPR Article 30 record-keeping obligations.']

  Note: 'Reporter master data — the individual or organization who submitted an adverse event report. Captures reporter type (healthcare professional, patient/consumer, lawyer, regulatory authority, literature), qualification (physician, pharmacist, nurse, dentist, consumer), country, institution, anonymized reporter identifier (per GDPR/HIPAA), contact preference for follow-up, and reporter reliability assessment. Enables reporter-level case aggregation, duplicate detection, follow-up management, and compliance with ICH E2B reporter data requirements. Distinct from HCP master — this is the PV-specific reporter identity used solely for case processing.'
}

  safety_signal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the validated safety signal that triggered this pharmacovigilance action. Links the action to its upstream signal detection record for traceability and regulatory reporting.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: PV actions (label updates, DHPC, REMS modifications) result in regulatory submissions.
