  trial_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.trial. Business justification: Regulatory PV actions (label updates, DHPC, protocol amendments) are often triggered by safety findings from a specific clinical trial. Linking pv_action to trial enables traceability from regulatory ']
  variation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.variation. Business justification: Safety-driven PV actions result in Type II variations (EU) or Prior Approval Supplements (US). Linking pv_action to the resulting variation enables tracking of safety-driven variation timelines and re']
  action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the pharmacovigilance action, including the specific risk minimization measures to be implemented, rationale, and expected patient safety impact. Captured from Argus Safety case narrative or Veeva Vault RIM action record.']
  action_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the PV action, formatted as PVA-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. Used in regulatory correspondence, authority notifications, and cross-system tracking within Argus Safety/Oracle AERS and Veeva Vault RIM.']
  action_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pharmacovigilance action, tracking progression from initiation through completion or cancellation. Drives workflow routing in safety management systems.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory or safety action being taken. Includes labeling updates (CCDS/SmPC/USPI changes), Dear Healthcare Professional Communication (DHPC), restricted indication, market suspension/withdrawal, Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) modification, Risk Management Plan (RMP) update trigger, and Post-Authorization Safety Study (PASS) initiation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: labeling_update|dhpc|restricted_indication|market_suspension|market_withdrawal|rems_modification|rmp_update|pass_initiation — promote to reference product]']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the pharmacovigilance action was fully implemented and closed. Compared against target_completion_date to assess timeliness and regulatory compliance. Nullable until action is completed.']
  argus_case_number varchar [note: 'Source system case or action number from Argus Safety/Oracle AERS, the operational system of record for pharmacovigilance. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the source PV system.']
  authority_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the relevant health authority (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, NMPA, etc.) was formally notified of this pharmacovigilance action. Nullable when notification is not required or not yet submitted.']
  authority_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pharmacovigilance action requires formal notification to one or more health authorities (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, etc.) prior to or upon implementation. Drives regulatory submission workflows.']
  benefit_risk_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of how this pharmacovigilance action affects the overall benefit-risk balance of the medicinal product. Informs PSUR/PBRER benefit-risk conclusions and regulatory authority communications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PV action record was first captured in the system (Argus Safety/Oracle AERS or Veeva Vault). Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.']
  dhpc_distribution_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the Dear Healthcare Professional Communication (DHPC) distribution when action_type is dhpc. Indicates whether the communication is national, regional, or global. Not applicable for non-DHPC actions.']
  effectiveness_assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the effectiveness assessment of this pharmacovigilance action was completed or is scheduled. Supports periodic benefit-risk evaluation and regulatory reporting cycles.']
  effectiveness_assessment_outcome varchar [note: 'Outcome of the post-implementation effectiveness assessment, indicating whether the pharmacovigilance action successfully minimized the identified risk. Informs subsequent signal management and RMP update decisions.']
  effectiveness_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Status of the post-implementation effectiveness assessment for this pharmacovigilance action. Tracks whether the action achieved its intended risk minimization objective, as required by EMA GVP Module XVI and FDA REMS assessment requirements.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date on which this pharmacovigilance action was escalated to senior management or the QPPV. Nullable when escalation_flag is false. Supports governance and oversight reporting.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pharmacovigilance action has been escalated due to missed deadlines, critical safety findings, or authority pressure. Triggers executive and QPPV notification workflows.']
  implementation_evidence_ref varchar [note: 'Document reference number or Veeva Vault document ID for the evidence package demonstrating that the pharmacovigilance action has been implemented (e.g., updated label version, DHPC distribution confirmation, REMS modification approval letter).']
  initiated_date date [note: 'The date on which the pharmacovigilance action was formally initiated or triggered. Represents the principal real-world business event timestamp (day-level precision) for regulatory timeline tracking and compliance reporting.']
  labeling_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of labeling document affected when action_type is labeling_update. Identifies whether the Company Core Data Sheet (CCDS), Summary of Product Characteristics (SmPC), US Prescribing Information (USPI), Patient Information Leaflet (PIL), or package insert is being updated. Null or not_applicable for non-labeling actions.']
  labeling_section_affected varchar [note: 'Specific section(s) of the labeling document being updated (e.g., Section 4.4 Special Warnings, Section 4.8 Undesirable Effects, Boxed Warning, Contraindications). Applicable only for labeling_update action type.']
  notified_authorities varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of health authority codes (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, NMPA) that have been or must be notified of this action. Supports multi-jurisdictional regulatory tracking.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Risk-based priority classification of the pharmacovigilance action, determining escalation urgency and resource allocation. Critical actions typically involve immediate patient safety risks requiring expedited regulatory response.']
  regulatory_deadline_date date [note: 'Mandatory deadline imposed by a health authority (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, NMPA) for completion or notification of this pharmacovigilance action. Distinct from the internally-set target_completion_date. Non-compliance may result in regulatory sanctions.']
  rems_program_name varchar [note: 'Name of the FDA-approved Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program being modified when action_type is rems_modification. Nullable for non-REMS actions.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Organizational unit or party accountable for implementing and completing this pharmacovigilance action. Determines workflow ownership and escalation paths within the safety management system. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pv_team|regulatory_affairs|medical_affairs|manufacturing|commercial|external_cro|external_cmo — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the pharmacovigilance action must be fully implemented. Drives SLA monitoring, regulatory deadline tracking, and escalation workflows. Distinct from actual completion date.']
  triggering_event_reference varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured reference number identifying the specific triggering event (e.g., signal case number, PSUR submission number, authority correspondence reference). Provides cross-system traceability.']
  triggering_event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the upstream event or finding that necessitated this PV action. Distinguishes between signal validation outcomes, PSUR/PBRER findings, RMP periodic reviews, health authority requests, SUSAR clusters, out-of-specification (OOS) trends, and literature review findings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: signal_validation|psur_finding|rmp_review|authority_request|susar_cluster|oos_trend|literature_review — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this PV action record. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and GVP Module I.']
  veeva_vault_doc_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault RIM or QualityDocs for the regulatory submission package, labeling document, or DHPC associated with this action. Supports document management and regulatory submission traceability.']

  Note: 'Pharmacovigilance action record capturing regulatory and safety actions taken in response to validated signals, PSUR findings, RMP updates, or authority requests. Action types include: labeling updates (CCDS, SmPC, USPI changes), DHPC (Dear Healthcare Professional Communication), restricted indication, market suspension/withdrawal, REMS modification, RMP update trigger, and PASS study initiation. Tracks action type, triggering event, responsible party, target completion date, authority notification requirement, implementation evidence, and effectiveness assessment. Links signals, PSURs, and RMPs to their downstream regulatory consequences.'
}

Table pharmacovigilance.pv_agreement {
  pv_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the pharmacovigilance agreement record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity. Role: MASTER_AGREEMENT.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: PV agreements between MAH and licensee/partner define PV responsibilities for a specific approved product. Linking pv_agreement to approval enables MAH to track which approvals have active PV agreemen']
  brand_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand_plan. Business justification: PV agreements with co-promotion or licensing partners govern safety data exchange obligations tied to specific brand plans. Commercial and PV teams need this link to align PSUR responsibilities, signa']
  counterparty_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: The counterparty in a PV agreement is a registered business partner in the MDM system (licensee, co-marketing partner, CRO). Linking to business_partner enables counterparty data quality management, a']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: PV agreements are negotiated as part of or directly reference licensing agreements — ICSR exchange timelines, SUSAR reporting obligations, and PSUR responsibilities are defined within the licensing de']
  mah_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Pharmacovigilance agreements are contracts between legal entities defining safety data exchange obligations. MAH legal entity linkage foundational for contract management, audit trail, and regulatory ']
  managing_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: PV agreements are managed by specific PV org units (alliance management, PV operations). Org unit ownership is required for compliance review scheduling, ICSR exchange monitoring, and PV agreement aud']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.project. Business justification: PV agreements with partners/licensees are negotiated per development program and govern safety data exchange obligations for a specific project. Compliance teams need to associate PV agreements with d']
  pv_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.pv_product. Business justification: A PV agreement governs safety data exchange obligations for specific medicinal products. The pv_agreement table has territory_scope and counterparty fields but no structured FK to the product it cover']
  royalty_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.royalty_agreement. Business justification: Pharmacovigilance agreements with licensors/licensees for in-licensed or out-licensed products often reference the same commercial relationship as royalty agreements. Linking enables tracking of safet']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.site. Business justification: PV agreements with CMO/CDMO partners specify which manufacturing sites are covered by ICSR exchange and SUSAR reporting obligations (ICH E2D, EU GVP Module I). Linking pv_agreement to the manufacturin']
  territory_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: PV agreements have a defined territory scope and governing law country. counterparty_country and governing_law_country are plain-text denormalizations of country. A proper FK enables territory-based P']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique reference number assigned to the pharmacovigilance agreement (e.g., SDEA-2024-0042). Used in regulatory correspondence, audit trails, and partner communications. Maps to the document number in Veeva Vault RIM or Argus Safety agreement registry.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pharmacovigilance agreement. DRAFT = under negotiation; ACTIVE = in force and binding; SUSPENDED = temporarily paused by mutual consent or regulatory action; TERMINATED = permanently ended before expiry; EXPIRED = reached end date without renewal; UNDER_REVIEW = undergoing periodic review or amendment.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pharmacovigilance agreement by its legal and operational nature. SDEA = Safety Data Exchange Agreement; PVA = Pharmacovigilance Agreement; CO_VIGILANCE = co-vigilance arrangement; CO_PROMOTION = co-promotion partner PV agreement; AFFILIATE = local affiliate PV agreement. Determines the regulatory obligations and reporting obligations applicable to each party.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment to the pharmacovigilance agreement. Nullable if no amendments have been made since original execution. Used to track agreement evolution and trigger re-review workflows.']
  argus_agreement_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier used in Argus Safety / Oracle AERS to link this pharmacovigilance agreement to the partner configuration for automated ICSR routing and exchange. Enables traceability between the agreement record and the operational safety system configuration.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance monitoring status of the pharmacovigilance agreement based on periodic reviews, audits, and KPI tracking. COMPLIANT = all obligations met; NON_COMPLIANT = one or more obligations breached; UNDER_REVIEW = compliance assessment in progress; PENDING_AUDIT = scheduled for compliance audit. Supports GVP Module I oversight requirements.']
  counterparty_pv_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the designated pharmacovigilance contact at the counterparty organisation. Used for operational safety data exchange communications and escalation notifications.']
  counterparty_pv_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the designated pharmacovigilance contact person at the counterparty organisation responsible for safety data exchange under this agreement. Required for operational communication and escalation.']
  counterparty_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the counterparty by their operational role in the pharmaceutical value chain. CRO = Contract Research Organization; CMO = Contract Manufacturing Organization; CDMO = Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization; LICENSEE = product licensee; CO_PROMOTER = co-promotion partner; AFFILIATE = local affiliate entity; DISTRIBUTOR = distribution partner. Drives applicable GVP obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CRO|CMO|CDMO|LICENSEE|CO_PROMOTER|AFFILIATE|DISTRIBUTOR — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pharmacovigilance agreement record was first created in the system. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per model conventions. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GVP Module I.']
  data_exchange_format varchar [note: 'Specifies the technical format used for exchanging Individual Case Safety Reports (ICSRs) between parties. E2B_R2 = ICH E2B(R2) XML format; E2B_R3 = ICH E2B(R3) FHIR-based format; PAPER = paper-based exchange; PROPRIETARY = system-specific format; NOT_SPECIFIED = format not yet defined. Drives technical integration requirements with Argus Safety/Oracle AERS.']
  data_exchange_method varchar [note: 'Technical transmission method used for exchanging safety data between parties. EVWEB = EMA EudraVigilance Web Trader; GATEWAY = automated gateway-to-gateway; SFTP = secure file transfer; EMAIL = encrypted email; MANUAL = manual entry; API = direct API integration. Determines system configuration in Argus Safety.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the pharmacovigilance agreement becomes legally binding and operational obligations commence. Determines the start of mandatory safety data exchange timelines. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd per model conventions.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the pharmacovigilance agreement ceases to be in force unless renewed or extended. Nullable for open-ended agreements. Used to trigger renewal workflows and compliance alerts. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd per model conventions.']
  icsr_exchange_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days within which Individual Case Safety Reports (ICSRs) must be exchanged between the MAH and the counterparty following initial receipt. Typically 7 days for fatal/life-threatening SUSARs and 15 days for other serious cases per GVP Module VI. Drives compliance monitoring and SLA tracking.']
  is_gdpr_dpa_in_place boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a GDPR-compliant Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is in place with the counterparty covering the personal data exchanged as part of pharmacovigilance activities. Mandatory for EU-based safety data exchange under GDPR Article 28.']
  language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-2 language code of the governing language of the pharmacovigilance agreement document (e.g., EN for English, DE for German, FR for French). Relevant for legal interpretation and translation obligations.']
  last_compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent periodic compliance review conducted for this pharmacovigilance agreement. Used to track review frequency and identify overdue reviews per GVP Module I requirements for oversight of outsourced PV activities.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this pharmacovigilance agreement record. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per model conventions. Supports change tracking and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  next_compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the next periodic compliance review of this pharmacovigilance agreement is scheduled. Drives workflow scheduling and resource planning for the PV compliance team.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, special conditions, derogations, or operational notes relevant to this pharmacovigilance agreement that are not captured in structured fields. Used by PV operations and regulatory affairs teams.']
  psur_responsible_party varchar [note: 'Designates which party bears primary responsibility for authoring and submitting Periodic Safety Update Reports (PSURs) / Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Reports (PBRERs) to regulatory authorities. MAH = Marketing Authorisation Holder; COUNTERPARTY = partner organisation; JOINT = shared responsibility; NOT_APPLICABLE = PSURs not in scope of this agreement.']
  qppv_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Qualified Person Responsible for Pharmacovigilance (QPPV) designated by the MAH under this agreement. The QPPV is a mandatory regulatory role under EU GVP legislation responsible for the PV system. Captured for regulatory compliance and audit purposes.']
  regulatory_submission_responsible_party varchar [note: 'Designates which party is responsible for submitting safety reports (ICSRs, SUSARs, PSURs, RMPs) to national competent authorities (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, etc.) for products in scope. Determines accountability in regulatory inspections and audits.']
  renewal_notification_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days before the agreement expiry date that a renewal notification must be issued to the counterparty. Drives automated alerting in the PV agreement management system to prevent lapses in safety data exchange coverage.']
  rmp_responsible_party varchar [note: 'Designates which party is responsible for maintaining and updating the Risk Management Plan (RMP) for products covered under this agreement. Relevant for EU-authorised products where RMP is mandatory per GVP Module V.']
  signal_management_responsible_party varchar [note: 'Designates which party is responsible for signal detection, assessment, and management activities for products covered under this agreement. MAH = Marketing Authorisation Holder; COUNTERPARTY = partner; JOINT = shared; NOT_APPLICABLE = signal management not in scope. Aligns with GVP Module IX obligations.']
  susar_exchange_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days within which Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reactions (SUSARs) must be exchanged between parties. Typically 7 days for fatal/life-threatening and 15 days for non-fatal/non-life-threatening SUSARs per GVP Module VI and ICH E2D. Separate from general ICSR exchange timelines.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the pharmacovigilance agreement was formally terminated prior to its scheduled expiry date. Nullable if the agreement has not been terminated. Triggers wind-down procedures for safety data exchange and regulatory notification obligations.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for early termination of the pharmacovigilance agreement (e.g., product withdrawal, partnership dissolution, regulatory action, mutual agreement). Required for regulatory audit trail and compliance documentation.']
  territory_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the geographic territories or regulatory jurisdictions covered by this agreement (e.g., specific countries, regions, or global). Determines which national competent authorities are in scope for regulatory reporting obligations under this agreement.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the executed pharmacovigilance agreement document stored in Veeva Vault (RIM or QualityDocs module). Enables direct navigation to the source document for audit and review purposes. Aligns with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the pharmacovigilance agreement document (e.g., 1.0, 2.1). Incremented upon each amendment or renewal. Supports document lifecycle management in Veeva Vault and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']

  Note: 'Pharmacovigilance agreement record governing safety data exchange obligations between the MAH and partners. Covers SDEAs (Safety Data Exchange Agreements), PVAs (Pharmacovigilance Agreements), and co-vigilance arrangements with licensees, CROs, CMOs, co-promotion partners, and local affiliates. Captures agreement type, counterparty, effective/expiry dates, reporting timelines (days to exchange), product scope, responsible party designations (PSUR authorship, signal management, regulatory submission), and compliance monitoring status. Required under GVP Module I and ICH E2E for all outsourced PV activities.'
}

Table pharmacovigilance.signal_case_series {
  signal_case_series_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the signal_case_series association']
  icsr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the Individual Case Safety Report contributing to the signal']
  safety_signal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the safety signal that this ICSR contributes to']
  case_contribution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ICSR is a contributing (numerator) case in the disproportionality analysis for the signal, as opposed to a background reference case. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  case_count varchar [note: 'Total number of Individual Case Safety Reports (ICSRs) or adverse event cases contributing to this safety signal at the time of detection or most recent assessment. Used as a threshold criterion alongside disproportionality metrics. [Moved from safety_signal: The aggregate case_count on safety_signal is a derived summary of the signal_case_series records. While the aggregate may be retained on safety_signal for performance/reporting purposes, the authoritative case-level data now lives in signal_case_series. If retained, it should be treated as a denormalized cache, not the source of truth.]']
  detection_run_date date [note: 'Date of the specific signal detection run during which this ICSR was identified as contributing to the safety signal. Belongs to the relationship, not to the signal or the ICSR alone. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  disproportionality_contribution_score decimal [note: 'Per-case contribution score to the aggregate disproportionality metric (PRR/ROR/EBGM/IC) for this signal. Quantifies how much this individual ICSR influences the signal strength. Belongs to the relationship, not to either entity alone. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  meddra_pt_code_matched varchar [note: 'The specific MedDRA Preferred Term code from the ICSR that matched the signal\'s MedDRA PT during the detection run. An ICSR may have multiple MedDRA terms; this records which one drove the case\'s inclusion in this signal\'s case series. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Signal Case Series relationship between a safety_signal and an icsr. It captures the operational linkage between a pharmacovigilance safety signal and each Individual Case Safety Report that contributes to or supports that signal, as managed per GVP Module IX and ICH E2E. Each record documents one ICSR\'s participation in one signal detection run, including its contribution score, MedDRA match, and case contribution status. This is the authoritative source for case-level drill-down, regulatory queries, and signal validation workflows in the pharmacovigilance system.. Existence Justification: In pharmacovigilance, a safety signal is identified by aggregating and analyzing multiple ICSRs — a single signal can be supported by hundreds of individual case reports, and a single ICSR can contribute to multiple signals (e.g., the same adverse event case may contribute to a signal for Drug A alone and a signal for a Drug A + Drug B interaction). The business actively manages this \'signal case series\' as a first-class operational concept in safety databases like Argus Safety and Oracle AERS, used for regulatory queries, signal validation, and case-level drill-down. The relationship carries its own data (contribution score, detection run date, MedDRA match flag) that belongs to neither the signal nor the ICSR alone.'
}

Table product.medicinal_product {
  medicinal_product_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the medicinal product. Primary key for this entity.']
  atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: Products classified by ATC for therapeutic categorization, formulary management, pharmacoepidemiology, reimbursement. Replaces denormalized atc_code string with FK to ATC master for hierarchical analy']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: medicinal_product is the high-level commercial/regulatory entity (the authorized product with brand name, regulatory approvals, market authorizations), while drug_product is the physical finished dosa']
  inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: Products linked to INN for international nomenclature, regulatory harmonization (IDMP), scientific communication. Replaces denormalized inn string with FK to INN registry for standardized substance id']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: Medicinal products commercialized under in-licensing agreements require this link for royalty reporting, territory rights management, and regulatory submission compliance. BD and legal teams track whi']
  mah_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Marketing Authorization Holder (MAH) is regulatory requirement for all marketed medicines. Tracks legal entity responsible for product registration, pharmacovigilance, regulatory compliance per jurisd']
  masterdata_ndc_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.ndc_code. Business justification: Medicinal products in US market reference NDC for regulatory compliance (FDA product listing), reimbursement (billing codes), distribution (supply chain tracking). Enables NDC-level regulatory intelli']
  patent_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: Product-level patent family tracking enables portfolio-wide IP strategy, multi-jurisdiction LOE forecasting, competitive intelligence, and integrated patent estate management across global markets.']
  reference_product_id varchar [note: 'For biosimilar products only: the medicinal_product_id of the reference biologic (originator product) to which this biosimilar has demonstrated similarity. Null for non-biosimilar products.']
  registration_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Products registered in specific countries/markets for regulatory compliance (market authorization tracking), commercial operations (launch planning), pharmacovigilance (country-specific safety reporti']
  therapeutic_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: medicinal_product has therapeutic_area as a STRING attribute that should reference the therapeutic_area master table for standardized classification.']
  trademark_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.trademark. Business justification: Every marketed product requires trademark protection linkage for brand management, regulatory submissions (proprietary name approval), commercial operations, and trademark enforcement/watch services.']
  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: Products have base UOM for inventory management (stock keeping unit), dosing calculations (DDD), regulatory filings (strength specifications). Enables UOM standardization across systems, inventory val']
  analytical_similarity_outcome varchar [note: 'For biosimilar products only: the outcome of the analytical similarity assessment comparing the biosimilar to the reference product across structural, functional, and physicochemical characteristics. Not Applicable for non-biosimilar products.']
  controlled_substance_schedule varchar [note: 'The DEA controlled substance schedule classification if the medicinal product contains a controlled substance (Schedule I through V). Not Controlled if the product does not contain controlled substances.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this medicinal product record was first created in the master product catalog system.']
  development_stage varchar [note: 'The current stage of the medicinal product in the drug development lifecycle, from Discovery through Preclinical, Clinical Phases (I, II, III), Regulatory Submission (NDA/BLA/MAA), Approval, Launch, or Discontinuation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Discovery|Preclinical|Phase I|Phase II|Phase III|NDA Submitted|BLA Submitted|MAA Submitted|Approved|Launched|Discontinued — promote to reference product]']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'The date on which the medicinal product was discontinued or withdrawn from the market. Null if the product is still active.']
  discontinuation_reason varchar [note: 'The primary reason for discontinuation or withdrawal of the medicinal product from the market. Not Applicable if the product is still active.']
  exclusivity_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which regulatory exclusivity periods (e.g., NCE exclusivity, orphan exclusivity, pediatric exclusivity, biologics exclusivity) expire, preventing approval of competing products.']
  extrapolation_status varchar [note: 'For biosimilar products only: indicates the extent to which clinical data from one indication of the reference product has been extrapolated to support approval for other indications of the biosimilar. Not Applicable for non-biosimilar products.']
  first_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the medicinal product received its first regulatory approval from any major regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, etc.).']
  interchangeability_designation varchar [note: 'For biosimilar products only: indicates whether the FDA has designated this biosimilar as interchangeable with the reference product, allowing automatic substitution at the pharmacy level. Not Applicable for non-biosimilar products.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this medicinal product record was most recently updated in the master product catalog system.']
  launch_date date [note: 'The date on which the medicinal product was first made commercially available in any market.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'The current operational lifecycle status of the medicinal product in the master product catalog: Active (available for manufacturing/commercial operations), Inactive (not currently marketed), Withdrawn (removed from market), Suspended (temporarily halted), or Under Review (regulatory or quality hold).']
  moa varchar [note: 'The biochemical or physiological mechanism by which the medicinal product produces its therapeutic effect (e.g., TNF-alpha inhibitor, PD-1 inhibitor, HMG-CoA reductase inhibitor).']
  originator_bla_number varchar [note: 'For biosimilar products only: the FDA BLA number of the reference biologic product (e.g., BLA125057 for Humira). Null for non-biosimilar products.']
  originator_maa_number varchar [note: 'For biosimilar products only: the EMA Marketing Authorization Application number of the reference biologic product. Null for non-biosimilar products.']
  orphan_drug_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the medicinal product has received Orphan Drug designation from the FDA or EMA for treatment of a rare disease affecting fewer than 200,000 patients (US) or fewer than 5 in 10,000 (EU).']
  patent_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the primary patent protection for the medicinal product expires, after which generic or biosimilar competition may enter the market.']
  pediatric_indication boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the medicinal product has an approved indication for use in pediatric populations (patients under 18 years of age).']
  prescription_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the medicinal product requires a prescription from a licensed healthcare professional for dispensing (true for Rx products, false for OTC products).']
  product_name varchar [note: 'The commercial brand name or proprietary name under which the medicinal product is marketed (e.g., Humira, Keytruda, Lipitor).']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the medicinal product by regulatory and development category: New Chemical Entity (NCE), New Biological Entity (NBE), Biosimilar, Vaccine, Generic, Over-The-Counter (OTC), or Medical Device Combination product. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NCE|NBE|Biosimilar|Vaccine|Generic|OTC|Medical Device Combination — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  qbd_approach boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the medicinal product was developed using Quality by Design (QbD) principles with defined design space, critical quality attributes (CQAs), and critical process parameters (CPPs).']
  regulatory_designation varchar [note: 'Special regulatory designations granted to the medicinal product (e.g., Orphan Drug, Breakthrough Therapy, Fast Track, Priority Review, Accelerated Approval, PRIME designation). Multiple designations may be concatenated.']
  rems_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the FDA has mandated a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program for this medicinal product to ensure that benefits outweigh risks.']
  shelf_life_months varchar [note: 'The approved shelf life of the medicinal product under specified storage conditions, expressed in months from date of manufacture.']
  storage_conditions varchar [note: 'The required storage conditions for the medicinal product to maintain stability and quality (e.g., Store at 2-8°C, Store below 25°C, Protect from light, Do not freeze).']
  usan varchar [note: 'The United States Adopted Name for the active substance, assigned by the USAN Council.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing an authorized or investigational medicinal product (branded drug, generic, biosimilar, vaccine, or consumer health product). Captures INN/USAN, brand name, ATC classification code, mechanism of action (MOA), therapeutic area, product type (NCE, NBE, biosimilar, vaccine, OTC), development stage, lifecycle status, and for biosimilar products: reference biologic product linkage (reference_product_id, originator BLA/MAA number, interchangeability designation, extrapolation status, analytical similarity assessment outcome). Aligned with ISO IDMP Medicinal Product concept. Serves as the top-level node in the pharmaceutical product hierarchy (medicinal product → drug product → SKU) referenced by manufacturing, commercial, regulatory, and supply chain domains.'
}

Table product.drug_substance {
  drug_substance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the drug substance (active pharmaceutical ingredient). Primary key for the drug substance master record.']
  atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: APIs classified by ATC for regulatory submissions (CTD Module 2.3), therapeutic class analysis, competitive intelligence. Replaces denormalized atc_code with FK to ATC master for API portfolio analysi']
  drug_master_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.drug_master_file. Business justification: Drug substances are primary subjects of Type II DMFs. Critical for regulatory submissions, supplier audits, and API manufacturing documentation. Removes denormalized dmf_number and dmf_type.']
  inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: APIs must reference INN registry for standardized nomenclature, regulatory filings (CTD Module 3), pharmacovigilance (substance identification). Replaces denormalized inn_name with FK to INN master fo']
  manufacturer_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: API manufacturer legal entity required for Drug Master File (DMF) holder identification, supplier qualification, regulatory filings (CTD Module 3), GMP compliance tracking. Replaces denormalized manuf']
  manufacturing_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: API manufacturing site assignment is a core supply chain and GMP compliance process. Regulatory filings (DMF, IMPD) require identification of the manufacturing plant for each drug substance. A pharma ']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Drug substances are represented as materials in ERP/SAP for procurement, inventory management, and batch tracking. Linking the scientific drug_substance record to its ERP material master is essential ']
  origin_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Country of origin for drug substances is a regulatory requirement under ICH Q11 and import/export regulations. FDA and EMA require declaration of the country where the API is manufactured. Pharma doma']
  patent_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: Drug substances are protected by patent families (composition-of-matter, polymorph, synthesis patents). Portfolio management and LOE planning operate at the patent family level. IP teams track which p']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: API-level patent protection distinct from formulation patents. Critical for FTO analysis, CMC regulatory strategy, supplier qualification, and manufacturing site IP clearance.']
  therapeutic_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: A drug substance (API) is developed and classified within a specific therapeutic area (e.g., oncology, immunology). The drug_substance table currently stores therapeutic_class as a free-text string, w']
  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: APIs measured in specific UOM for manufacturing (batch records), specifications (release testing), regulatory filings (CTD Module 3). Enables UOM standardization for API inventory, yield calculations,']
  adme_profile_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of the drug substance\'s ADME characteristics including absorption rate, distribution volume, metabolic pathways, and excretion routes.']
  bcs_classification varchar [note: 'BCS classification of the drug substance based on solubility and permeability characteristics (Class I: high solubility/high permeability, Class II: low solubility/high permeability, Class III: high solubility/low permeability, Class IV: low solubility/low permeability).']
  cas_registry_number varchar [note: 'Unique numerical identifier assigned by the Chemical Abstracts Service to the drug substance for unambiguous chemical identification.']
  chemical_name varchar [note: 'Systematic IUPAC chemical name describing the molecular structure of the drug substance according to international nomenclature standards.']
  controlled_substance_schedule varchar [note: 'DEA controlled substance schedule classification if the drug substance is subject to controlled substance regulations, or \'Not Controlled\' if not applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the drug substance master record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current drug substance specification or master record version became effective for use in manufacturing and regulatory submissions.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current drug substance specification or master record version is superseded or retired, if applicable.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the drug substance master record is currently active and available for use in formulation, manufacturing, and regulatory activities.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the drug substance master record was last updated or modified.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current stage in the drug substance development and commercial lifecycle, from discovery through discontinuation.']
  moa_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary of the drug substance\'s mechanism of action, describing how it produces its pharmacological effect at the molecular or cellular level.']
  molecular_formula varchar [note: 'Chemical formula representing the number and type of atoms in one molecule of the drug substance (e.g., C8H9NO2).']
  molecular_weight decimal(10,4) [note: 'Molecular weight of the drug substance expressed in grams per mole (g/mol), calculated from the molecular formula.']
  nce_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the drug substance has been designated as a New Chemical Entity by regulatory authorities, qualifying for extended data exclusivity periods.']
  orphan_drug_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the drug substance has received orphan drug designation for treatment of rare diseases affecting fewer than 200,000 patients.']
  particle_size_specification varchar [note: 'Defined particle size distribution range or specification for the drug substance, critical for dissolution and bioavailability (e.g., D50 < 10 microns).']
  pharmacopoeial_monograph varchar [note: 'Reference to the official pharmacopoeial monograph (USP, EP, JP, or other) that defines quality standards for the drug substance.']
  physical_form varchar [note: 'Physical state or form of the drug substance at standard conditions (e.g., crystalline solid, liquid, lyophilized powder).']
  polymorphic_form varchar [note: 'Specific crystalline or amorphous form of the drug substance, identifying the polymorph used in manufacturing (e.g., Form I, Form II, anhydrous, hydrate).']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Summary of regulatory approval status across major markets (FDA, EMA, PMDA, etc.), indicating where the drug substance has received marketing authorization.']
  retest_period_months varchar [note: 'Time period in months after which the drug substance should be re-examined to ensure it still meets specifications before use in manufacturing.']
  salt_form varchar [note: 'Chemical salt form of the drug substance if applicable (e.g., hydrochloride, sodium, sulfate), or \'free base\' or \'free acid\' if not a salt.']
  solubility_profile varchar [note: 'Aqueous solubility characteristics of the drug substance across physiologically relevant pH ranges, critical for formulation development and bioavailability.']
  specification_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the current quality specification document defining acceptance criteria for the drug substance.']
  stability_profile varchar [note: 'Summary of the drug substance\'s stability characteristics under various storage conditions, including temperature and humidity sensitivity.']
  stereochemistry varchar [note: 'Three-dimensional spatial arrangement of atoms in the drug substance molecule, including chirality and isomeric configuration (e.g., R/S designation, E/Z configuration).']
  storage_conditions varchar [note: 'Recommended storage conditions for the drug substance to maintain quality and stability (e.g., store at 2-8°C, protect from light, store under inert atmosphere).']
  synthesis_route_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the chemical synthesis route or manufacturing process used to produce the drug substance, enabling change control and traceability.']
  usan_name varchar [note: 'United States Adopted Name assigned by the USAN Council, representing the official generic name used in the United States for the drug substance.']

  Note: 'Master record for the active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) or drug substance (DS) as defined in ICH Q7. Captures chemical name, molecular formula, molecular weight, CAS registry number, INN/USAN designation, pharmacopoeial monograph reference (USP/EP/JP), physical form, stereochemistry, polymorphic form, salt form, particle size specification, ADME profile summary, BCS classification, and regulatory Drug Master File (DMF) reference number. Represents the DS layer in the DS/DP hierarchy and is the single source of truth for API chemical identity referenced by manufacturing, quality, regulatory, and formulation records.'
}

  therapeutic_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: drug_product has therapeutic_area as a STRING attribute that should reference the therapeutic_area master table for standardized classification.']
  trademark_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.trademark. Business justification: Drug products have brand names protected by trademarks.   unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Replaces denormalized strength_unit with FK to UOM master for s']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the drug product received initial regulatory approval from the primary health authority (e.g., FDA, EMA).']

  cmc_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary of the chemistry, manufacturing, and controls information for the drug product, including formulation composition and manufacturing process overview.']
  controlled_substance_schedule varchar [note: 'The DEA controlled substance schedule classification if the drug product contains a controlled substance (Schedule I through V), or non-controlled if not applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this drug product record was first created in the system.']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'The date on which the drug product was discontinued or withdrawn from the market, if applicable.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'The physical form in which the drug product is manufactured and administered (e.g., tablet, capsule, injection, solution, suspension, cream, ointment, transdermal patch). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|injection|solution|suspension|cream|ointment|patch — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  excipients varchar [note: 'List of inactive ingredients (excipients) used in the drug product formulation, such as binders, fillers, preservatives, and colorants.']
  formulation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the drug product formulation based on release characteristics (e.g., immediate release, extended release, delayed release, modified release, controlled release).']
  launch_date date [note: 'The date on which the drug product was first made commercially available in the market.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle stage of the drug product (e.g., development, approved, marketed, discontinued, withdrawn from market).']
  moa varchar [note: 'Description of the biochemical or physiological mechanism by which the drug product exerts its therapeutic effect.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this drug product record was last modified or updated.']
  package_size varchar [note: 'The quantity of dosage units per package (e.g., 30 tablets, 100 mL, 10 vials).']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'The primary packaging configuration for the drug product (e.g., bottle, blister pack, vial, ampule, prefilled syringe, tube).']
  patent_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the primary patent protection for the drug product expires, opening the market to generic competition.']
  prescription_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the drug product requires a prescription (Rx), is available over-the-counter (OTC), or is behind-the-counter.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Internal product code assigned to the drug product for identification across manufacturing, regulatory, and commercial systems. Typically alphanumeric and unique within the organization.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'The commercial or proprietary name of the drug product as it appears on labeling and marketing materials.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the drug product by regulatory and commercial category (e.g., branded, generic, biosimilar, vaccine, over-the-counter, medical device combination product).']
  qbd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the drug product was developed using Quality by Design principles as defined by ICH Q8.']
  rems_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the drug product is subject to a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy program required by the FDA to ensure safe use.']
  rld_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this drug product is designated as a Reference Listed Drug by the FDA for bioequivalence studies.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'The path by which the drug product is administered to the patient (e.g., oral, intravenous, intramuscular, subcutaneous, topical, transdermal).']
  shelf_life_months varchar [note: 'The approved shelf life of the drug product from date of manufacture, expressed in months, under specified storage conditions.']
  storage_conditions varchar [note: 'The required storage conditions for the drug product to maintain stability and quality (e.g., store at 2-8°C, protect from light, store below 25°C).']
  strength varchar [note: 'The amount of active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) per unit of dosage form, expressed with unit of measure (e.g., 500 mg, 10 mg/mL, 5%).']
  strength_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric value of the active pharmaceutical ingredient strength for computational and analytical purposes.']
  usan varchar [note: 'The United States Adopted Name for the active pharmaceutical ingredient, assigned by the USAN Council.']

  Note: 'Master record for the finished drug product (DP) or finished dosage form (FDF) derived from one or more drug substances. Captures dosage form (tablet, capsule, injection, etc.), route of administration, strength, formulation type, reference listed drug (RLD) linkage, shelf life, storage conditions, and CMC summary. Bridges the DS/DP hierarchy and is referenced by manufacturing batch records, regulatory submissions, and commercial SKUs.'
}

Table product.formulation {
  formulation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the formulation record. Primary key for the formulation entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: Formulation development activities are tracked against cost centers for R&D capitalization, project accounting, and budget control. Pharma companies must allocate formulation development costs to spec']
  development_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: Formulation development site tracking for technology transfer documentation, scale-up studies, process validation, site-specific design space. Critical for CMC regulatory submissions (CTD Module 3), m']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent drug product that this formulation defines. Links to the finished dosage form (FDF) in the product master catalog.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Formulation development projects are tracked via internal orders for cost accumulation, budget control, and capitalization eligibility determination. This link enables pharmaceutical companies to trac']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: Formulations developed using licensed technology platforms (e.g., drug delivery systems, nanoparticle formulations) are governed by licensing agreements. Royalty calculations and IP ownership determin']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Formulations have distinct patent protection (composition, process). Essential for lifecycle management, line extension strategy, and generic defense through formulation patents.']
  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: Formulation unit dose strength UOM for manufacturing instructions (batch records), specifications (release criteria), regulatory filings. Replaces denormalized unit_dose_strength_uom with FK to UOM ma']
  api_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) in the formulation. Most formulations have 1 API; combination products have 2 or more.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who approved this formulation. Typically a quality assurance manager or regulatory affairs lead.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when this formulation was formally approved for use in manufacturing or clinical trials. Represents a key lifecycle milestone.']
  batch_size_target decimal(18,3) [note: 'Planned or standard batch size for manufacturing this formulation, expressed in the unit specified by batch_size_uom. Used for scale-up calculations and manufacturing planning.']
  batch_size_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the batch size (e.g., kg for bulk powder, L for liquid, units for finished dosage forms). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kg|L|units|tablets|capsules|vials|syringes — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  bioequivalence_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to bioequivalence or bioavailability studies conducted for this formulation, particularly relevant for generic formulations or formulation changes requiring bridging studies.']
  composition_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the formulation composition, including all active and inactive ingredients, their roles, and quantities. Supports CMC Module 3 documentation and regulatory submissions.']
  cpp_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of critical process parameters (CPPs) identified for this formulation. CPPs are process parameters whose variability has an impact on a critical quality attribute (CQA) and therefore should be monitored or controlled.']
  cqa_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of critical quality attributes (CQAs) for this formulation. CQAs are physical, chemical, biological, or microbiological properties that should be within an appropriate limit, range, or distribution to ensure the desired product quality.']
  created_date date [note: 'Date when this formulation record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for formulation lifecycle management.']
  design_space_description varchar [note: 'Textual summary of the QbD design space, including key input variables, process parameters, and their acceptable ranges. References supporting studies and risk assessments.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'Physical form in which the drug product is manufactured and administered (e.g., tablet, capsule, injectable solution). Aligns with FDA and EMA dosage form terminologies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|solution|suspension|emulsion|cream|ointment|gel|patch|injection|lyophilized_powder|suppository — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ectd_module_reference varchar [note: 'Specific eCTD module and section reference where this formulation is documented (e.g., \'3.2.P.1\', \'3.2.P.3.3\'). Facilitates traceability to regulatory documentation.']
  excipient_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct inactive ingredients (excipients) in the formulation. Includes fillers, binders, disintegrants, lubricants, coatings, preservatives, and other functional excipients.']
  formulation_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the formulation, used across manufacturing, regulatory, and quality systems. Typically follows internal coding conventions for Chemistry Manufacturing and Controls (CMC) documentation.']
  formulation_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the formulation, often including the drug product name, strength, and dosage form (e.g., \'Compound XYZ 50mg Tablet Formulation\').']
  formulation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the formulation. Tracks progression from early development through commercial manufacturing and potential discontinuation.']
  formulation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the formulation based on its release mechanism and pharmacokinetic profile. Critical for regulatory submissions and manufacturing process design.']
  gmp_grade_required varchar [note: 'Indicates the GMP grade required for manufacturing this formulation: clinical (for investigational products), commercial (for marketed products), or both (for formulations used in both contexts).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified this formulation record. Part of audit trail for compliance and quality management.']
  last_modified_date date [note: 'Date when this formulation record was last modified. Tracks changes to formulation specifications, status, or metadata.']
  manufacturing_process_type varchar [note: 'Primary manufacturing process or technology used to produce this formulation (e.g., wet granulation for tablets, aseptic filling for injectables). Influences equipment selection and process validation strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wet_granulation|dry_granulation|direct_compression|coating|lyophilization|aseptic_filling|emulsification|spray_drying — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  patent_protected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this formulation is covered by one or more patents, providing intellectual property protection. Links to patent records in the intellectual property domain.']
  pharmacopoeial_standard varchar [note: 'Primary pharmacopoeial standard to which this formulation conforms (USP = United States Pharmacopeia, EP = European Pharmacopoeia, JP = Japanese Pharmacopoeia, BP = British Pharmacopoeia, IP = Indian Pharmacopoeia, ChP = Chinese Pharmacopoeia).']
  qbd_design_space_defined boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Quality by Design (QbD) design space has been established for this formulation, defining the multidimensional combination of input variables and process parameters that ensure quality.']
  regulatory_submission_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory submission (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA) in which this formulation was described or approved. Links formulation records to regulatory dossiers in RIMS.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'Intended pathway by which the drug product is administered to the patient. Critical for formulation design and regulatory classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|intravenous|intramuscular|subcutaneous|topical|transdermal|inhalation|ophthalmic|otic|rectal|vaginal — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  scale_up_factor decimal(10,2) [note: 'Ratio of commercial batch size to development batch size, indicating the degree of scale-up achieved. Used to assess scale-up risk and process robustness.']
  shelf_life_months varchar [note: 'Approved or proposed shelf life for the formulation in months, based on stability data. Defines the period during which the product remains within specifications under defined storage conditions.']
  stability_profile_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of stability characteristics for this formulation, including storage conditions, shelf life, and key degradation pathways. References formal stability studies conducted per ICH Q1A guidelines.']
  storage_conditions varchar [note: 'Recommended storage conditions for the formulation (e.g., \'Store at 2-8°C\', \'Store below 25°C\', \'Protect from light\'). Critical for maintaining product quality and stability.']
  technology_transfer_status varchar [note: 'Status of technology transfer activities for this formulation, tracking the transfer of manufacturing knowledge and processes from development to commercial manufacturing sites or to CMOs/CDMOs.']
  unit_dose_strength decimal(18,6) [note: 'Quantity of active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) per unit dose (e.g., 50 mg per tablet, 100 mg/mL for solution). Expressed in the unit specified by unit_dose_strength_uom.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the formulation, tracking iterative changes during development, scale-up, and optimization. Follows semantic versioning (e.g., 1.0, 1.1, 2.0).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who created this formulation record. Part of audit trail for compliance and quality management.']

  Note: 'Detailed formulation record specifying the qualitative and quantitative composition (Q1/Q2) of a drug product, including all active and inactive ingredients, their pharmacopoeial grades, functional roles, and quantities per unit dose and per batch. Tracks formulation version history, Quality by Design (QbD) design space parameters, critical process parameters (CPP), and critical quality attributes (CQA). Supports CMC Module 3 documentation, scale-up, and technology transfer.'
}

  excipient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.excipient. Business justification: formulation_ingredient contains excipient-related attributes that should be normalized to the excipient master table. When an ingredient is an excipient (not an API), this FK allows retrieval of autho']
