Regulatory submissions require referencing the excipient DMF for LOA (letter of authorization). The existing `dmf_number` plain attribute is a denormalized']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Excipient material master integration for procurement (PO creation, vendor management), inventory management (stock levels, retest dates), batch traceability (genealogy), MRP planning. Enables ERP int']
  origin_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Country of origin for excipients is a regulatory requirement, especially for animal-derived excipients (TSE/BSE risk assessment per EMA guidelines) and for import compliance. Pharma domain experts exp']
  parent_excipient_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on excipient (parent_excipient_id)']
  unit_of_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: Excipient specifications (particle size, quantity per unit dose) require a unit of measure reference for formulation accuracy and pharmacopoeial compliance. Pharma domain experts expect excipient mast']
  allergen_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the excipient contains or is derived from known allergens. Critical for patient safety labeling and formulation risk assessment.']
  allergen_type varchar [note: 'Specific allergen classification if allergen_flag is true. Examples include lactose (milk-derived), gelatin (animal-derived), or gluten.']
  animal_derived_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the excipient is derived from animal sources. Important for TSE/BSE risk assessment and religious/dietary considerations.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Unique CAS registry number assigned to the excipient for chemical substance identification. Critical for regulatory submissions and safety data sheets.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the change control or deviation record authorizing the creation or modification of this excipient master record. Ensures traceability and GMP compliance.']
  chemical_name varchar [note: 'IUPAC or systematic chemical name of the excipient. Provides precise chemical identity for regulatory and scientific documentation.']
  coa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Certificate of Analysis must accompany each lot of excipient received from the supplier. Standard requirement for GMP compliance.']
  compendial_grade varchar [note: 'Pharmacopoeial grade or quality standard to which the excipient conforms. Indicates the regulatory monograph governing quality specifications.']
  compendial_monograph varchar [note: 'Specific pharmacopoeial monograph reference number or title that defines quality standards, test methods, and acceptance criteria for the excipient.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this excipient master record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data governance.']
  dmf_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the DMF submission type. Type II is most common for excipients, covering manufacturing site, process, and controls.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this excipient master record becomes effective and available for use in formulation and manufacturing activities.']
  excipient_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or material master code for the excipient used across manufacturing, procurement, and formulation systems. Typically aligned with ERP material master.']
  excipient_name varchar [note: 'Common or trade name of the excipient as used in formulation documentation and manufacturing records.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which this excipient master record is no longer valid for use. Null if the record remains active indefinitely.']
  functional_category varchar [note: 'Primary functional role of the excipient in drug product formulation. Defines the excipient\'s purpose in the dosage form.']
  gmo_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the excipient is derived from genetically modified organisms. Required for labeling in certain jurisdictions.']
  gmp_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current GMP compliance status of the excipient manufacturing site based on regulatory inspections and audit findings.']
  halal_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the excipient has been certified as Halal-compliant for use in markets requiring Islamic dietary compliance.']
  inn_name varchar [note: 'WHO-assigned International Nonproprietary Name for the excipient, if applicable. Used for global regulatory harmonization.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this excipient master record is currently active and available for use in formulation development and manufacturing operations.']
  kosher_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the excipient has been certified as Kosher-compliant for use in markets requiring Jewish dietary compliance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this excipient master record was last updated. Audit trail field for data governance and change tracking.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle stage of the excipient in the organization\'s master data system. Determines availability for new formulation development and manufacturing use.']
  molecular_formula varchar [note: 'Chemical molecular formula representing the elemental composition of the excipient.']
  molecular_weight decimal(10,3) [note: 'Molecular weight of the excipient in Daltons or grams per mole. Critical for formulation calculations and quality control.']
  particle_size_specification varchar [note: 'Defined particle size range or distribution specification for the excipient. Critical quality attribute affecting flowability, dissolution, and content uniformity.']
  physical_form varchar [note: 'Physical state or form of the excipient as received and used in manufacturing. Impacts handling, storage, and processing requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: powder|granule|liquid|gel|paste|solid|semi_solid — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory approval status of the excipient for use in pharmaceutical products. Varies by jurisdiction and intended use.']
  retest_period_months varchar [note: 'Time period in months after which the excipient must be retested to confirm continued compliance with specifications before use in manufacturing.']
  solubility_profile varchar [note: 'Description of the excipient\'s solubility characteristics in various solvents (water, ethanol, etc.). Critical for formulation development and process design.']
  specification_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference or identifier for the approved quality specification defining acceptance criteria, test methods, and release requirements for the excipient.']
  stability_profile varchar [note: 'Summary of the excipient\'s stability characteristics under various environmental conditions (temperature, humidity, light). Informs storage and handling requirements.']
  storage_conditions varchar [note: 'Recommended storage conditions for the excipient to maintain quality and stability. Includes temperature range, humidity control, and light protection requirements.']

  Note: 'Master record for pharmaceutical excipients used in drug product formulations, capturing functional category, grade, compendial status, and supplier qualification'
}

Table patient.patient {
  patient_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for patient']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Patient country of residence drives regulatory reporting requirements (EMA regional submissions), data localization compliance (GDPR, local privacy laws), enrollment eligibility criteria, and pharmaco']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the patient\'s residential address. Used for patient contact, investigational product shipment, and patient support services. Protected health information (PHI).']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the patient\'s residential address (apartment, suite, unit number). Optional field for complete address capture.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the patient\'s residential address. Protected health information (PHI) when combined with other identifiers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the patient record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for regulatory compliance and data lineage.']
  ctms_subject_number varchar [note: 'Subject identifier assigned by the Clinical Trial Management System (Oracle Clinical or Siebel CTMS). Used to link patient records to clinical trial enrollment and visit data.']
  data_classification_level varchar [note: 'Classification level of the patient record based on sensitivity and regulatory requirements. Determines access controls and handling procedures.']
  data_privacy_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient has provided consent for data processing and sharing under GDPR and HIPAA regulations. True if consent obtained, false otherwise.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the patient. Used for age calculation, eligibility determination, and patient identification. Protected health information (PHI).']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'Date when the patient discontinued participation in a clinical trial or patient support program. Used for retention analysis and follow-up planning.']
  discontinuation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for patient discontinuation from clinical trial or patient support program. Critical for safety analysis and protocol deviation tracking.']
  edc_subject_number varchar [note: 'Patient identifier in the Electronic Data Capture system (Medidata Rave). Links patient to case report forms (CRFs) and clinical trial data collection.']
  ehr_mrn varchar [note: 'Medical record number from external electronic health record systems. Used for real-world data (RWD) integration and health economics outcomes research (HEOR).']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for patient communication, electronic consent delivery, and patient support program engagement. Protected health information (PHI).']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the patient was enrolled in a clinical trial or patient support program. Marks the start of the patient\'s participation lifecycle.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current enrollment status of the patient in clinical trials. Tracks progression through trial phases from screening to completion or discontinuation.']
  ethnicity varchar [note: 'Self-reported ethnicity of the patient. Required for clinical trial diversity reporting and subgroup analysis per FDA and EMA guidelines.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first name of the patient. Protected health information (PHI) subject to HIPAA and GDPR privacy regulations.']
  gender_identity varchar [note: 'Self-identified gender of the patient. Captured separately from biological sex to support inclusive clinical research and patient-centered care.']
  gpid varchar [note: 'Global patient identifier used across all systems and geographies to uniquely identify a patient. Pseudonymized token for cross-system linkage while maintaining HIPAA and GDPR compliance.']
  informed_consent_date date [note: 'Date when informed consent was obtained from the patient. Required for GCP compliance and regulatory audit trails.']
  informed_consent_status varchar [note: 'Status of informed consent for clinical trial participation or data usage. Critical for GCP compliance and patient rights protection.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last name (surname or family name) of the patient. Protected health information (PHI) subject to HIPAA and GDPR privacy regulations.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the patient. Optional field for patient identity verification.']
  mpi_token varchar [note: 'Master patient index linkage token used to resolve patient identity across disparate source systems. Enables patient matching and deduplication while preserving privacy.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'Nationality of the patient represented as ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code. Used for regulatory reporting and cross-border clinical trial management.']
  patient_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the patient in clinical trials or patient support programs. Tracks patient engagement and follow-up requirements.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for patient communication, appointment reminders, and adherence monitoring. Protected health information (PHI).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code or ZIP code of the patient\'s residential address. Protected health information (PHI) when combined with other identifiers.']
  primary_language varchar [note: 'Primary language spoken by the patient. Used for informed consent delivery, patient communication, and patient-reported outcomes (PRO) instrument selection.']
  pseudonymization_token varchar [note: 'Pseudonymized identifier used to protect patient identity in research datasets and analytics. Enables HIPAA and GDPR compliant data sharing while maintaining linkability.']
  pv_case_patient_reference varchar [note: 'Patient identifier in the pharmacovigilance safety reporting system (Argus Safety or Oracle AERS). Links patient to adverse event reports and ICSRs.']
  race varchar [note: 'Self-reported race of the patient. Required for clinical trial diversity reporting and subgroup safety and efficacy analysis per FDA and EMA guidelines.']
  sex varchar [note: 'Biological sex assigned at birth. Used for clinical trial eligibility, safety analysis, and pharmacokinetic (PK) / pharmacodynamic (PD) assessments.']
  specialty_pharmacy_code varchar [note: 'Patient identifier in specialty pharmacy and patient support program systems. Used for copay assistance, adherence monitoring, and patient services.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region of the patient\'s residential address. Used for geographic analysis and regulatory reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the patient record was last updated. Part of audit trail for regulatory compliance and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing a patient or clinical trial participant. Single source of truth for patient identity across clinical trials, pharmacovigilance, and market access programs. Captures demographics (date of birth, sex, gender, race, ethnicity), contact information, primary language, nationality, country of residence, patient status (active, deceased, lost-to-follow-up), global patient identifier (GPID), cross-system identifiers (CTMS subject number, EDC ID, PV case patient ID, specialty pharmacy ID, EHR MRN), pseudonymization token for HIPAA/GDPR compliance, master patient index (MPI) linkage, consent status flag, data privacy classification, and audit metadata. Authoritative record for all downstream patient-linked data products.'
}

Essential for linking tr']
  molecular_target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.molecular_target. Business justification: Patient stratification and trial eligibility in oncology/immunology are defined by molecular target (e.g., HER2, EGFR). Clinical operations and enrollment reports require linking enrolled patients to ']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the patient or clinical trial subject enrolled. Links to the master patient record.']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the principal investigator responsible for the patient at the enrollment site.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.registry. Business justification: Patient enrollments can be for clinical trials OR patient registries. The existing trial_id FK handles clinical trial enrollments, but registry enrollments need a separate FK to link to the registry m']
  regulatory_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.application. Business justification: Clinical trials operate under regulatory applications (IND in US, CTA in EU). Enrollment records must link to the regulatory application for protocol compliance tracking, safety reporting obligations,']
  reimbursement_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.reimbursement_policy. Business justification: Benefits verification at enrollment requires knowing which reimbursement policy applies — determines prior authorization requirements, step therapy, and coverage restrictions. Hub services and special']
  rems_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.rems. Business justification: REMS programs with patient enrollment ETASU elements (e.g., iPLEDGE, TIRF REMS) require mandatory patient registration before dispensing. Patient enrollment records must reference the REMS program to ']
  support_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the patient support program in which the patient is enrolled']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the clinical trial, patient support program, or patient registry into which the patient is enrolled.']
  benefit_amount_received decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative financial assistance amount (in USD) that this specific patient has received through this specific program enrollment. Tracked for benefit cap enforcement, Sunshine Act reporting, and program ROI analysis.']
  blinding_status varchar [note: 'Blinding status of the patient enrollment: open_label (no blinding), single_blind (patient blinded), double_blind (patient and investigator blinded), triple_blind (patient, investigator, and sponsor blinded), unblinded (emergency unblinding occurred).']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel or source through which the patient enrolled in this program. Used for channel effectiveness analysis and marketing attribution.']
  cohort_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier for the enrollment cohort or wave. Used in dose-escalation studies, adaptive trials, or phased enrollment programs to track enrollment groups.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date the patient provided informed consent to participate in the trial or program.']
  consent_to_program_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient has provided informed consent specific to this program\'s data collection, communication, and benefit processing. Separate from general clinical trial consent. Required for HIPAA compliance and program operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which this enrollment record originated (e.g., Oracle Clinical, Medidata Rave, Veeva Vault CTMS). Critical for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  disenrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the patient was disenrolled from this support program. Null for active enrollments. Used for benefit period closure and retention analytics.']
  disenrollment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description for why the patient was disenrolled from this program (e.g., treatment completed, patient request, insurance change to government payer, benefit maximum reached, non-compliance). Critical for retention analysis and program optimization.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'Date of the disposition event (e.g., date of withdrawal, date of completion, date of death, date of screen failure).']
  disposition_event_type varchar [note: 'Type of disposition event that ended or changed the patients enrollment status: completed (finished protocol per plan), withdrawn (voluntary or investigator-initiated discontinuation), lost_to_followup (contact lost), death (patient died), screen_failure (did not meet eligibility), protocol_deviation (removed due to protocol violation).']
  disposition_primary_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason code for the disposition event. Standardized per CDISC Controlled Terminology (e.g., adverse event, lack of efficacy, protocol violation, patient decision, lost to follow-up, death, completed per protocol).']
  disposition_secondary_reason varchar [note: 'Secondary or contributing reason code for the disposition event. Provides additional context beyond the primary reason.']
  eligibility_verification_date date [note: 'Date when patient eligibility for this program was verified (insurance type confirmed as commercial, prescription verified, etc.). Required for compliance documentation and audit trail.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date the patient was formally enrolled into the trial, program, or registry after meeting eligibility criteria and providing informed consent.']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the patient at enrollment. May be the subject number, screening number, or program enrollment number used in clinical documentation.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the patient enrollment: screened (undergoing screening), screen_failed (did not meet eligibility), enrolled (consented and enrolled), randomized (assigned to treatment arm), active (receiving treatment), completed (finished protocol), withdrawn (voluntarily discontinued), lost_to_followup (contact lost), deceased (patient died). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: screened|screen_failed|enrolled|randomized|active|completed|withdrawn|lost_to_followup|deceased — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  enrollment_type varchar [note: 'Type of enrollment program: clinical_trial (interventional study), support_program (patient assistance program), registry (observational registry), expanded_access (pre-approval access), compassionate_use (emergency use).']
  icf_version varchar [note: 'Version of the informed consent form signed by the patient at enrollment. Tracks consent document revisions and re-consent requirements.']
  itt_population_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the patient is included in the Intent-to-Treat analysis population. True if the patient was randomized and received at least one dose of study drug.']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Date of the last documented contact with the patient. Used for tracking lost-to-follow-up and vital status.']
  last_dose_date date [note: 'Date the patient received their last dose of investigational product. Critical for safety follow-up and disposition tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  mitt_population_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the patient is included in the Modified Intent-to-Treat analysis population. Criteria vary by protocol but typically require randomization and at least one post-baseline efficacy assessment.']
  patient_enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the patient was enrolled in this specific support program. Critical for program eligibility, benefit period calculation, and compliance reporting.']
  patient_enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this enrollment. Tracks progression from pending through active participation to completion or disenrollment. Used for operational reporting and benefit eligibility determination.']
  pp_population_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the patient is included in the Per-Protocol analysis population. True if the patient completed the protocol without major protocol deviations.']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Patient\'s preferred method of contact for communications related to this specific program enrollment. May differ from general patient contact preferences.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version of the clinical trial protocol under which the patient was enrolled. Critical for tracking protocol amendments and their impact on enrollment cohorts.']
  randomization_date date [note: 'Date the patient was randomized to a treatment arm in a clinical trial. Null for non-randomized studies or support programs.']
  randomization_number varchar [note: 'Unique randomization number assigned to the patient at randomization. Used to maintain blinding and treatment assignment.']
  record_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this enrollment record originated (e.g., PSP hub platform, specialty pharmacy system, CRM). Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or regulatory jurisdiction where the patient was enrolled (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific). Used for stratified analysis and regulatory submissions.']
  safety_population_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the patient is included in the safety analysis population. Typically true if the patient received at least one dose of investigational product.']
  screen_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason the patient failed screening and was not enrolled. Typically coded using standardized reason codes (e.g., inclusion/exclusion criteria not met, withdrew consent, adverse event during screening).']
  screening_date date [note: 'Date the patient began the screening process for eligibility assessment.']
  stratification_factors varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of stratification factors used at randomization (e.g., disease stage, prior therapy, biomarker status). Critical for ensuring balanced treatment assignment.']
  treatment_arm varchar [note: 'Treatment arm or cohort to which the patient was assigned (e.g., active drug, placebo, dose level, combination therapy). May be coded to maintain blinding.']
  unblinding_date date [note: 'Date the patient was unblinded (if emergency unblinding occurred). Null if patient remains blinded or study is open-label.']
  unblinding_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for emergency unblinding (e.g., serious adverse event requiring treatment decision, patient safety, regulatory request).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was last updated in the system.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing a participant\'s enrollment into a clinical trial, patient support program, or patient registry, including full disposition lifecycle. Tracks enrollment type (clinical_trial, support_program, registry), enrollment date, randomization date/number, assigned treatment arm, site/investigator identifiers, enrollment status lifecycle (screened, enrolled, randomized, completed, withdrawn, screen-failed), screen failure reason, protocol/ICF version at enrollment. Disposition tracking: disposition event type (completed, withdrawn, lost-to-follow-up, death, screen failure, protocol deviation withdrawal), disposition date, primary and secondary reason codes (standardized per CDISC CT), last dose date, last contact date, and analysis population assignment flags (ITT, mITT, PP, safety). Single source of truth for both enrollment and disposition — serves as authoritative source for CDISC SDTM DM/DS domain derivation and CSR population summaries.'
}

Table patient.informed_consent {
  informed_consent_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for informed_consent']
  consenting_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the principal investigator or sub-investigator who obtained the informed consent. Applicable for clinical trial consents.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Consent jurisdiction determines applicable data privacy regulation (GDPR vs HIPAA vs local), consent form requirements, withdrawal rights, data subject access request processing, and regulatory compli']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the investigational site where clinical consent was obtained. Applicable for clinical trial consents. Null for non-trial privacy consents.']
  label_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.label. Business justification: Informed consent documents for commercial patient programs must reference the current approved label version for product information disclosed to patients. Traceability between ICF and label version i']
  patient_access_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to market.patient_access_program. Business justification: Expanded access, compassionate use, and named patient programs require separate informed consent distinct from clinical trial consent. Linking informed_consent to patient_access_program enables tracki']
  patient_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.patient_enrollment. Business justification: Informed consent is obtained in the context of a specific enrollment episode. Adding patient_enrollment_id links each consent record (including re-consent events) to the exact enrollment it governs, e']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the patient or clinical trial subject who provided consent. Links to the patient master data product.']
  regulatory_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.application. Business justification: ICF versions must align with protocol amendments submitted to health authorities under the regulatory application. Regulatory inspections verify ICF version matches approved protocol version. Critical']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the clinical trial associated with this consent. Applicable for clinical trial consents (ICF). Null for non-trial privacy consents.']
  biomarker_substudy_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient consented to participate in biomarker or translational research sub-studies. True if consented, False if declined or not applicable.']
  consent_category varchar [note: 'Category of consent record. Distinguishes between clinical informed consent (ICF), re-consent events, consent withdrawals, GDPR privacy consent, HIPAA authorization, and data sharing consent.']
  consent_channel varchar [note: 'Channel or interface through which consent was captured. Distinguishes between web portal, mobile app, paper form, in-person at site, telephonic, or email.']
  consent_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the signed consent document stored in the document management system (e.g., Veeva Vault, eTMF). Used for regulatory inspection readiness.']
  consent_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the consent expires or is no longer valid. Null for consents without expiration. Applicable for time-limited privacy consents or trial-specific consents.']
  consent_language varchar [note: 'Language in which the informed consent form was presented to the patient. ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (e.g., EN, ES, FR, DE, JA).']
  consent_method varchar [note: 'Method by which consent was obtained. Distinguishes between paper-based, electronic consent (eConsent), verbal consent, or telephonic consent.']
  consent_obtained_date date [note: 'Date when the informed consent was obtained from the patient or legally authorized representative (LAR). Primary business event timestamp for consent lifecycle.']
  consent_obtained_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when consent was obtained. Used for eConsent systems and audit trail. Complements consent_obtained_date with time-of-day precision.']
  consent_purpose varchar [note: 'Specific purpose for which consent was obtained. Examples: clinical_trial_participation, data_processing, research, marketing, data_sharing, pharmacovigilance, patient_support_program. Free-text or controlled vocabulary.']
  consent_signature_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the patient or LAR signed the consent. Examples: wet_signature (ink on paper), electronic_signature, digital_signature (PKI-based), biometric, verbal_recorded.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consent. Tracks whether consent is pending signature, obtained, withdrawn by patient, expired, declined, or revoked.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'Specific type or sub-category of consent. Examples: main_study, sub_study_genetic, sub_study_biomarker, future_research, data_processing, marketing, third_party_sharing. Provides granular classification within consent_category.']
  consent_withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date when the patient withdrew consent. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. Critical for GCP compliance and data subject rights.']
  data_subject_rights_fulfillment_date date [note: 'Date when the data subject rights request was fulfilled or completed. Null if request is pending or no request exists.']
  data_subject_rights_request_date date [note: 'Date when the data subject rights request was received. Null if no request. Critical for GDPR compliance (30-day response requirement).']
  data_subject_rights_request_type varchar [note: 'Type of data subject rights request associated with this consent record. Examples: access, erasure, portability, rectification, restriction, objection. Null if no request. Applicable for GDPR compliance.']
  econsent_platform varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the eConsent platform or system used to capture electronic consent. Examples: Medidata Rave, Veeva Vault, Florence eBinders. Null for paper consents.']
  future_use_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient consented to future use of their data or biological samples for research purposes beyond the current study. True if consented, False if declined.']
  gdpr_lawful_basis varchar [note: 'GDPR Article 6 or Article 9 lawful basis for processing personal data. Examples: consent, legitimate_interest, legal_obligation, vital_interests, public_task, explicit_consent_special_category. Applicable for EU/EEA patients.']
  genetic_substudy_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient consented to participate in genetic or genomic sub-studies. True if consented, False if declined or not applicable.']
  hipaa_authorization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether HIPAA authorization was obtained for use and disclosure of protected health information (PHI). True if authorization obtained, False if not applicable or declined. Applicable for US patients.']
  icf_version varchar [note: 'Version number or identifier of the informed consent form document used. Critical for regulatory traceability and protocol amendment tracking. Applicable for clinical trial consents.']
  lar_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the legally authorized representative or caregiver who provided consent on behalf of the patient. Applicable for pediatric populations or incapacitated patients.']
  lar_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the LAR to the patient. Examples: parent, legal guardian, healthcare proxy, power of attorney, caregiver.']
  re_consent_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for re-consenting the patient. Examples: protocol_amendment, new_safety_information, icf_version_update, regulatory_requirement. Applicable when consent_category is re_consent.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this consent record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this consent record was last updated in the system. Audit trail field for change tracking and compliance.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by the patient for withdrawing consent. Examples: adverse_event, personal_reasons, lack_of_efficacy, relocation, lost_to_followup. Free-text or controlled vocabulary.']
  withdrawal_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of consent withdrawal. Indicates whether patient withdrew from entire study, data use only, biological sample use only, or future contact. Critical for GCP compliance.']
  witness_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the impartial witness who observed the consent process. Required when patient or LAR cannot read, or as per local regulations.']
  witness_signature_date date [note: 'Date when the impartial witness signed the informed consent form. Null if no witness was required.']

  Note: 'Unified consent lifecycle management covering clinical informed consent (ICF), re-consent events, consent withdrawals, and all data privacy authorizations. Clinical consent: ICF version, consent/withdrawal dates, consenting investigator, witness, method (paper, eConsent), language, sub-study consent flags (genetic, biomarker, future use). Privacy consent: GDPR Article 6/9 lawful basis, HIPAA authorization, consent purpose (data processing, research, marketing, data sharing), data subject rights requests (access, erasure, portability, rectification), request/fulfillment dates, jurisdiction (EU, US, UK, JP), consent channel (web, paper, verbal). Captures consent category (clinical_icf, re-consent, withdrawal, privacy_gdpr, privacy_hipaa, data_sharing), LAR/caregiver reference for pediatric populations, and full audit trail. Single source of truth for ALL consent types — clinical and privacy. Mandatory for GCP compliance, regulatory inspection readiness, HIPAA/GDPR compliance, and data subject rights fulfillment.'
}

  concomitant_medication_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the concomitant medication record. Primary key for this entity.']

Normalizing this with an adverse_event_id FK replaces the free-text rel']
  atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: Concomitant medications are classified by ATC for drug interaction analysis, pharmacovigilance signal detection, and regulatory submission narratives (CDISC CM domain). The existing atc_code column is']
  crf_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.crf_form. Business justification: CDISC SDTM CM domain submission requires source CRF traceability for concomitant medication records. Clinical data management and source data verification (SDV) processes depend on linking CM records ']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Concomitant medications are pharmaceutical products in material master for drug-drug interaction analysis, contraindicated medication detection, protocol deviation flagging, and safety signal detectio']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Concomitant medications must link to product master for drug-drug interaction analysis, contraindication checking, protocol deviation assessment, and safety signal detection in pharmacovigilance.   patient_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.patient_enrollment. Business justification: Concomitant medications are recorded during a specific study or program period. Linking to patient_enrollment provides the full enrollment context (treatment arm, protocol version, population flags) n']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the clinical trial subject or patient taking the concomitant medication. Links to the patient or trial subject master record.']
  prescribing_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.master.   protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.protocol_deviation. Business justification: The concomitant_medication table has a protocol_deviation_flag BOOLEAN field indicating whether the medication constitutes a protocol deviation. Normalizing this with a protocol_deviation_id FK replac']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the clinical trial or study in which the concomitant medication was recorded. Links to the trial master record.']
  visit_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the clinical trial visit or encounter during which the concomitant medication was reported or recorded.']
  action_taken varchar [note: 'Action taken with the concomitant medication in response to an adverse event or safety concern (e.g., none, dose reduced, dose increased, temporarily stopped, permanently stopped).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or additional notes regarding the concomitant medication, including clarifications, special circumstances, or investigator remarks.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the concomitant medication record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for regulatory compliance and data lineage.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The source from which the concomitant medication data was captured (e.g., Case Report Form (CRF), electronic diary, medical record, subject interview, pharmacy record).']
  dose decimal(18,4) [note: 'The numeric dose amount of the concomitant medication administered per administration event. Used with dose_unit to specify complete dosing information.']
  dose_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the medication dose (e.g., mg, g, mcg, mL, IU, tablets, capsules). Standardized units for dose quantification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mg|g|mcg|mL|L|IU|units|tablets|capsules|drops|puffs|patches — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency or schedule of concomitant medication administration (e.g., once daily, twice daily, as needed, every 8 hours). Free-text or standardized frequency code.']
  indication varchar [note: 'The medical condition, symptom, or reason for which the concomitant medication is being taken. Free-text description of the therapeutic indication.']
  medication_class varchar [note: 'The therapeutic or pharmacological class of the concomitant medication (e.g., antibiotic, antihypertensive, analgesic, anticoagulant). Used for safety signal detection and drug-drug interaction screening.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified the concomitant medication record. Part of audit trail for regulatory compliance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the concomitant medication record was last modified. Part of audit trail for regulatory compliance and data lineage.']
  ongoing_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the concomitant medication is still being taken by the subject at the time of data capture or database lock. True if ongoing, False if stopped.']
  prior_to_study_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the concomitant medication was started before the subject enrolled in the clinical trial. True if started prior, False if started during study.']
  prohibited_concomitant_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the concomitant medication is prohibited per the clinical trial protocol. True if prohibited, False if allowed. Critical for protocol deviation tracking.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the concomitant medication record in the Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system (e.g., draft, submitted, verified, locked, query open, query resolved).']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'The route by which the concomitant medication is administered to the subject (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, intramuscular, topical, inhalation).']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the subject began taking the concomitant medication. Critical for temporal analysis of drug-drug interactions and adverse event causality assessment.']
  stop_date date [note: 'The date on which the subject stopped taking the concomitant medication. Null if medication is ongoing at time of data capture.']
  who_drug_code varchar [note: 'Standardized WHO Drug Dictionary code for the concomitant medication. Used for global pharmacovigilance reporting and drug-drug interaction analysis.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who created the concomitant medication record. Part of audit trail for regulatory compliance.']

  Note: 'Records all concomitant medications taken by a participant during a study or program period. Captures WHO Drug-coded medication name, ATC classification code, dose, dose unit, route of administration, frequency, start date, stop date, ongoing flag, indication for use, and whether the medication is a prohibited concomitant per protocol. Critical for safety assessment, drug-drug interaction analysis, and pharmacovigilance case narratives.'
}

Table patient.adverse_event {
  adverse_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for adverse_event']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.application. Standard p']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: Regulatory pharmacovigilance requirement. When adverse events are reported post-market or in trials, FDA/EMA mandate traceability to manufacturing batch for investigation, recall assessment, and root ']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Adverse events must be tracked by commercial brand for pharmacovigilance, regulatory reporting (periodic safety updates), commercial risk assessment, and launch readiness. Supports brand safety profil']
  compound_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to discovery.compound. Business justification: Pharmacovigilance requires linking adverse events to the specific investigational compound administered. Essential for safety signal detection, causality assessment, regulatory reporting (IND safety r']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Pharmacovigilance reporting timelines (15-day SUSAR, expedited ICSRs) are country-specific per ICH E2B(R3) and local regulations. A country FK on adverse_event enables country-level PV compliance repo']
  crf_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.crf_form. Business justification: CDISC SDTM AE domain submission requires source CRF traceability. Patient-level AE records must link to the AE CRF page in the EDC for regulatory audit trail, SDV, and data reconciliation with the cli']
  investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational product or study drug that the patient was receiving at the time of the adverse event.']
  label_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.label. Business justification: Adverse event \'expectedness\' classification — a core ICSR and PSUR requirement — is determined by comparing the AE against the current approved label. Pharmacovigilance teams must reference the label ']
  labeling_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.labeling. Business justification: Pharmacovigilance expectedness assessment (ICSR, SUSAR, PSUR) requires comparing each AE against the Reference Safety Information in the approved label version in force at event onset. Direct FK enabl']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Pharma PV teams track which li']
  manufacturing_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_deviation.   medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Critical for pharmacovigila']
  patient_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.patient_enrollment. Business justification: Adverse events must be contextualized within the specific enrollment episode for pharmacovigilance reporting. The enrollment context (treatment arm, randomization number, blinding status, population f']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the patient or clinical trial subject who experienced the adverse event.']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.principal_investigator. Business justification: ICH E6 GCP and FDA 21 CFR 312.64 require the reporting PI to be identified for each SAE/SUSAR. patient.adverse_event.master_id links to hcp.master (person identity) but not the trial-specific PI role;']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Individual Case Safety Reports (ICSRs) and expedited AE reports to health authorities are submitted as regulatory submissions.   rems_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.rems. Business justification: FDA REMS assessments require analysis of adverse events for REMS-covered products to evaluate whether risk minimization elements are working. AEs must be linked to the REMS program to support periodic']
  reporting_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.master. Business justification: Pharmacovigilance and MedWatch/EudraVigilance regulatory reporting require traceability of the HCP who reported the adverse event. The \'reported_by\' plain-text field is a denormalization of hcp.master']
  treatment_exposure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.treatment_exposure. Business justification: An adverse event is causally linked to a specific treatment exposure event (the dose/administration that triggered it). Adding treatment_exposure_id normalizes the treatment_administered string field ']
  trial_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial during which the adverse event occurred. Null for post-market surveillance events.']
  action_taken_study_drug varchar [note: 'Action taken with the investigational product or study drug in response to the adverse event.']
  ae_term varchar [note: 'The verbatim description of the adverse event as reported by the patient, investigator, or healthcare professional.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Unique case identifier assigned by the pharmacovigilance system for regulatory reporting. Used for tracking and submission to regulatory authorities.']
  causality_assessment varchar [note: 'Investigator or sponsor assessment of the causal relationship between the adverse event and the investigational product or study drug.']
  duration_days varchar [note: 'The duration of the adverse event in days, calculated from onset date to resolution date. Null if ongoing or fatal.']
  expectedness varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event was expected based on the current Investigator Brochure or product labeling.']
  meddra_pt_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA preferred term code assigned to the adverse event for standardized medical terminology and regulatory reporting.']
  meddra_pt_name varchar [note: 'MedDRA preferred term name describing the adverse event in standardized medical terminology.']
  meddra_soc_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA system organ class code representing the highest level of the MedDRA hierarchy for the adverse event.']
  meddra_soc_name varchar [note: 'MedDRA system organ class name representing the body system affected by the adverse event.']
  onset_date date [note: 'The date when the adverse event first occurred or was first observed by the patient or investigator.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the adverse event at the time of reporting or study completion.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this adverse event record was first created in the data system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this adverse event record was last modified or updated in the data system.']
  regulatory_report_date date [note: 'The date when the adverse event was reported to the regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, etc.). Applicable for SAEs and SUSARs.']
  report_date date [note: 'The date when the adverse event was first reported to the sponsor or investigator.']
  reporting_timeline_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event was reported to regulatory authorities within the required timeline (e.g., 7 days for fatal/life-threatening SAEs, 15 days for other SAEs).']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date when the adverse event resolved, recovered, or was no longer present. Null if ongoing or fatal.']
  seriousness_criteria_congenital_anomaly boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event resulted in a congenital anomaly or birth defect.']
  seriousness_criteria_death boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event resulted in death.']
  seriousness_criteria_disability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event resulted in persistent or significant disability or incapacity.']
  seriousness_criteria_hospitalization boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event resulted in initial or prolonged inpatient hospitalization.']
  seriousness_criteria_life_threatening boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event was life-threatening at the time of the event.']
  seriousness_criteria_medically_important boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event was considered medically important and may jeopardize the patient or require intervention to prevent one of the other serious outcomes.']
  seriousness_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event meets regulatory criteria for seriousness (death, life-threatening, hospitalization, disability, congenital anomaly, or other medically important condition).']
  severity_grade varchar [note: 'Severity grade of the adverse event using CTCAE scale: Grade 1 (Mild), Grade 2 (Moderate), Grade 3 (Severe), Grade 4 (Life-threatening), Grade 5 (Death).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system from which the adverse event record was captured (e.g., Argus Safety for pharmacovigilance, Medidata Rave for clinical trial EDC).']
  study_day varchar [note: 'The study day (relative to first dose or randomization) on which the adverse event occurred. Negative values indicate pre-treatment events.']
  susar_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event qualifies as a SUSAR (serious, unexpected, and suspected to be related to the investigational product), requiring expedited reporting to regulatory authorities.']

  Note: 'Captures all adverse events (AEs) and serious adverse events (SAEs) reported by or for a participant during a clinical trial or post-market surveillance program. Records MedDRA-coded preferred term (PT) and system organ class (SOC), onset date, resolution date, severity grade (CTCAE), seriousness criteria (death, hospitalization, life-threatening, etc.), causality assessment, action taken with study drug, outcome, SAE flag, SUSAR flag, and reporting timeline compliance status. Feeds into Argus Safety / Oracle AERS for regulatory reporting.'
}

Table patient.treatment_exposure {
  treatment_exposure_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the treatment exposure record. Primary key for this entity.']
