  annual_cap_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum total compensation in USD that can be paid to the HCP under this contract in a calendar year. Enforces anti-kickback compliance and prevents excessive payments.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'Business unit or division responsible for this contract. Used for cost allocation and organizational reporting.']
  compliance_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the compliance department approved this contract. Required for audit trails and regulatory inspections.']
  compliance_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of compliance review for this contract. Must be approved before contract execution and payment processing.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the contract. Used for legal reference, payment processing, and audit trails.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contract. Controls whether payments can be processed and services can be rendered.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contract based on the nature of services provided by the HCP. Determines applicable compliance rules and FMV benchmarks.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the contract is executed. Determines applicable regulatory requirements and FMV benchmarks.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contract record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this contract. Supports multi-currency operations and Sunshine Act reporting.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contract expires or terminates. Nullable for open-ended contracts. Used to prevent payments beyond contract term.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes legally binding and services may commence. Used for compliance validation and payment authorization.']
  exclusion_screening_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent exclusion screening was performed. Must be refreshed monthly per OIG guidance.']
  exclusion_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of screening the HCP against government exclusion lists (OIG, GSA SAM, FDA Debarment). Must be cleared before contract execution and payment.']
  fda_debarment_screening_result varchar [note: 'Result of screening against the FDA Debarment List. Debarred individuals cannot participate in drug development or approval processes.']
  fmv_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology used to determine the FMV rate for this contract. Documents compliance with anti-kickback regulations and ensures defensible compensation.']
  fmv_rate_type varchar [note: 'Unit basis for the FMV compensation rate. Determines how payments are calculated and validated against caps.']
  honorarium_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed honorarium amount in USD for per-engagement or per-deliverable contracts. Used for speaker programs and advisory board participation.']
  hourly_rate_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum hourly compensation rate in USD for services rendered under this contract. Part of the FMV range used for compliance validation.']
  hourly_rate_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum hourly compensation rate in USD for services rendered under this contract. Part of the FMV range used for compliance validation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contract record was last updated. Used for audit trails and change tracking.']
  legal_review_date date [note: 'Date when the legal department completed review of this contract. Required for audit trails and contract governance.']
  legal_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of legal department review for this contract. Must be approved before contract execution.']
  oig_screening_result varchar [note: 'Result of screening against the OIG List of Excluded Individuals and Entities. HCPs on the exclusion list cannot receive federal healthcare program payments.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which payments are made to the HCP under this contract. Used for cash flow planning and compliance tracking.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Description of payment schedule, invoicing requirements, and disbursement conditions. Defines when and how the HCP will be compensated.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the HCP will provide services under this contract. Used for FMV rate benchmarking and compliance segmentation.']
  sam_screening_result varchar [note: 'Result of screening against the GSA System for Award Management exclusion list. Ensures HCP is not debarred from federal contracts.']
  scope_of_services varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the services the HCP will provide under this contract. Defines deliverables, expectations, and compliance boundaries.']
  specialty varchar [note: 'Medical specialty of the HCP relevant to this contract. Used for FMV rate benchmarking and compliance validation.']
  sunshine_act_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether payments under this contract must be reported under the Physician Payments Sunshine Act (Open Payments). True for most HCP contracts.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for contract termination if status is terminated. Used for compliance analysis and vendor relationship management.']

  Note: 'Contracts, agreements, and associated fair market value (FMV) rate cards between the pharmaceutical company and HCPs for services including consulting, advisory boards, speaker programs, clinical research, and medical education. Captures contract type, effective dates, scope of services, FMV rate ranges by specialty/service/region, FMV methodology, hourly rate min/max, annual caps, honorarium rate, payment terms, compliance approval status, legal review status, and debarment/exclusion screening results (OIG, GSA SAM, FDA Debarment List checks). Supports anti-kickback compliance validation (ensuring compensation within FMV bounds), exclusion screening before financial transfers, and Sunshine Act reporting for all contracted HCP payments.'
}

Table hcp.investigator {
  investigator_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the clinical trial investigator record. Primary key for the investigator data product.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Clinical investigator payments and site costs are budgeted at trial level for financial planning, commitment tracking, and variance analysis. Essential for clinical trial budget management and ensurin']
  hco_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hcp.hco_master. Business justification: Clinical trial investigators conduct trials at specific healthcare organizations (academic medical centers, research hospitals, clinics). While investigator has org_unit_id for internal organizational']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the HCP master data product. Links the investigator record to the underlying healthcare professional identity.']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Investigator qualification, FDA Form 1572 execution, IRB approval, and GCP training are tied to a specific investigational or approved medicinal product. Clinical operations teams track investigator-t']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: Clinical operations organizational units manage investigator relationships and trial site oversight. Required for operational accountability, resource allocation, and performance management in clinica']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Clinical investigators named as inventors on patents arising from company-sponsored research require tracking for IP ownership verification, inventor compensation obligations, Sunshine Act reporting, ']
  primary_therapeutic_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: Investigators are qualified and tracked by therapeutic area expertise for clinical trial site selection, feasibility assessment, and investigator database management. Essential for clinical operations']
  active_trials_count varchar [note: 'Number of clinical trials currently active for this investigator. Used to assess investigator capacity and workload.']
  average_enrollment_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average number of subjects enrolled per month by this investigator. Calculated metric used for trial planning and site selection.']
  conflict_of_interest_declared boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investigator has declared any financial or other conflicts of interest that could affect trial conduct or data integrity.']
  cv_last_updated_date date [note: 'Date when the investigator\'s curriculum vitae was last updated in the system.']
  cv_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a current curriculum vitae is on file for the investigator. CV is required for regulatory submissions and site qualification.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system from which the investigator record was created or last updated (e.g., Oracle Clinical, Veeva Vault, CTMS).']
  disqualification_date date [note: 'Date when the investigator was disqualified from conducting clinical trials, either by regulatory authority or company decision.']
  disqualification_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for investigator disqualification. May include regulatory violations, data integrity issues, or non-compliance with GCP.']
  external_source_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this investigator in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  fda_1572_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a signed FDA Form 1572 (Statement of Investigator) is on file. Required for all US-based clinical trials.']
  fda_1572_signature_date date [note: 'Date when the investigator signed the FDA Form 1572 for the current protocol assignment.']
  financial_disclosure_date date [note: 'Date when the investigator submitted their financial disclosure form for the current trial or reporting period.']
  financial_disclosure_status varchar [note: 'Status of the investigator\'s financial disclosure submission (FDA Form 3454/3455). Required to identify potential conflicts of interest.']
  gcp_training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the investigator completed their most recent GCP training certification.']
  gcp_training_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the investigator\'s current GCP training certification expires. Investigators must renew training to remain qualified.']
  gcp_training_status varchar [note: 'Status of the investigator\'s GCP training certification. GCP training is mandatory for all clinical trial investigators per ICH guidelines.']
  initiated_trial_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investigator has conducted or is conducting investigator-initiated trials (IIT) with company products. IITs are investigator-led studies.']
  investigator_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the investigator for tracking across clinical trials. May be company-specific or protocol-specific.']
  investigator_role varchar [note: 'Role of the investigator on clinical trials. Principal investigator (PI) has primary responsibility; sub-investigators and co-investigators support the PI.']
  investigator_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the investigator. Active investigators are qualified and available for trial assignments. Disqualified status indicates regulatory action by FDA or other authority.']
  irb_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the IRB approved the investigator to conduct clinical research at their site.']
  irb_approval_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current IRB approval expires. Investigators must obtain renewal to continue trial activities.']
  irb_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of IRB approval for the investigator to conduct clinical trials at their affiliated site. IRB approval is required before trial initiation.']
  kol_investigator_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investigator is also classified as a Key Opinion Leader (KOL) in their therapeutic area. KOL investigators have additional engagement opportunities.']
  last_performance_review_date date [note: 'Date when the investigator\'s performance was last formally reviewed by clinical operations or medical affairs.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Overall performance rating assigned to the investigator based on enrollment, data quality, compliance, and site management. Used for future site selection decisions.']
  protocol_deviation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of protocol deviations reported for this investigator across all trials. Quality metric for investigator performance assessment.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date when the investigator was formally qualified to conduct clinical trials for the company. Represents completion of all qualification requirements.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this investigator record was first created in the data product. Audit trail field.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this investigator record was last updated. Audit trail field for change tracking.']
  serious_protocol_deviation_count varchar [note: 'Number of serious protocol deviations reported for this investigator. Serious deviations may impact subject safety or data integrity.']
  sunshine_act_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether payments or transfers of value to this investigator must be reported under the Physician Payments Sunshine Act (Open Payments).']
  total_subjects_enrolled varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of subjects enrolled by this investigator across all trials. Key performance indicator for investigator productivity.']
  total_trials_conducted varchar [note: 'Total number of clinical trials the investigator has conducted as PI or sub-investigator. Performance metric for investigator selection.']

  Note: 'Clinical trial investigator records for HCPs serving as principal investigators (PIs) or sub-investigators on company-sponsored or investigator-initiated trials. Captures investigator role, GCP training status, CV on file, financial disclosure (Form FDA 3455), IRB approval status, site affiliation, therapeutic area expertise, enrollment performance metrics, and trial participation history. Provides the HCP-domain view of investigator identity and qualifications; clinical trial operational data (protocol, enrollment, visits) resides in the clinical domain. Supports regulatory submissions (Form FDA 1572) and clinical operations planning.'
}

Table intellectual.patent {
  patent_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each patent record in the IP portfolio. Primary key for the patent master data product.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Patents are jurisdiction-specific; essential for freedom-to-operate analysis, market entry planning, and regulatory strategy in global pharmaceutical operations.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Patents are owned by legal entities; critical for IP ownership tracking, portfolio valuation, M&A due diligence, and financial reporting of intangible assets.']
  masterdata_atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: Patents are classified by therapeutic area; essential for portfolio analysis, competitive benchmarking, and therapeutic area-specific IP strategy.']
  masterdata_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: Patents incur maintenance costs allocated to cost centers; required for IP budget management, financial planning, and cost allocation in pharmaceutical R&D.']
  masterdata_inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: Patents claim specific INNs; essential for patent landscaping, FTO analysis, and molecule-patent linkage in pharmaceutical IP management.']
  masterdata_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.profit_center. Business justification: Patents are assigned to therapeutic areas/product lines represented by profit centers; critical for portfolio valuation, segment reporting, and strategic IP management.']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Patents cover specific materials (APIs, formulations); essential for product development, supply chain planning, and patent-product linkage in pharmaceutical operations.']
  patent_family_id varchar [note: 'Identifier grouping all related patent applications and grants that share a common priority filing across multiple jurisdictions. Used to manage global IP portfolio coherence and track international prosecution strategy.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: Manufacturing process patents are associated with specific plants; required for technology transfer, GMP compliance, and site-specific IP protection.']
  adjusted_expiry_date date [note: 'Final effective expiry date after applying all patent term adjustments (PTA), patent term extensions (PTE) for regulatory review delays, and supplementary protection certificates (SPC). Represents the true end of patent exclusivity for commercial planning purposes.']
  annuity_paid_through_date date [note: 'Date through which patent annuity (maintenance) fees have been paid, confirming the patent is in good standing. Used by IP operations to monitor fee payment compliance and prevent unintentional lapse.']
  asset_classification varchar [note: 'Broader intellectual property asset classification for this record. Primarily \'patent\' for this product, but also supports trade secrets and know-how records anchored to the patent master per the SSOT design. Enables unified IP portfolio reporting across asset types.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this patent record was first created in the IP portfolio system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.']
  data_exclusivity_expiry date [note: 'Date on which regulatory data exclusivity (RDE) expires, preventing generic/biosimilar applicants from referencing the originator\'s clinical data. In the US, NCEs receive 5 years; biologics receive 12 years under BPCIA. In the EU, the 8+2+1 rule applies. Complements patent expiry in LOE planning.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calculated or legally determined date on which patent protection lapses, typically 20 years from the filing date subject to patent term adjustments (PTA), patent term extensions (PTE), and supplementary protection certificates (SPC). The primary LOE (Loss of Exclusivity) planning anchor for lifecycle management and generic/biosimilar entry strategy.']
  external_counsel_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or name of the external law firm or patent attorney managing prosecution, maintenance, or litigation for this patent. Supports coordination between in-house IP team and outside counsel.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Date on which the patent application was formally submitted to the patent office. Establishes the priority date for novelty assessment and is the anchor for calculating patent term and exclusivity periods.']
  fto_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Current freedom-to-operate (FTO) analysis status for this patent relative to the company\'s commercial activities. Cleared indicates no infringement risk identified; at_risk indicates potential third-party patent conflict; blocked indicates active infringement concern; under_review indicates ongoing FTO analysis. Critical for launch readiness and litigation risk management.']
  grant_date date [note: 'Date on which the patent was officially granted by the patent office, conferring enforceable rights to the assignee. Null for applications still under examination. Used to calculate patent term adjustments (PTA) and supplementary protection certificate (SPC) eligibility.']
  legal_status varchar [note: 'Current legal standing of the patent in its jurisdiction lifecycle. Pending indicates application under examination; granted indicates active protection; abandoned/lapsed indicates failure to prosecute or maintain; opposed indicates third-party challenge; litigated indicates active court proceedings. Drives LOE planning and portfolio valuation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|granted|abandoned|expired|lapsed|opposed|litigated — promote to reference product]']
  litigation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this patent is currently subject to active litigation, including Paragraph IV ANDA challenges, inter partes review (IPR), post-grant review (PGR), or district court infringement proceedings. Triggers escalated monitoring and legal counsel coordination.']
  maintenance_fee_due_date date [note: 'Next scheduled date by which maintenance (annuity) fees must be paid to the patent office to keep the patent in force. Failure to pay results in lapse of patent rights. Critical for IP portfolio maintenance operations.']
  orange_book_listed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this patent is listed in the FDA Approved Drug Products with Therapeutic Equivalence Evaluations (Orange Book). Orange Book listing triggers Paragraph IV certification obligations for ANDA filers and is central to Hatch-Waxman litigation strategy.']
  orphan_drug_exclusivity_expiry date [note: 'Date on which orphan drug market exclusivity expires, granting 7 years (US) or 10 years (EU) of market protection for drugs treating rare diseases. Distinct from patent expiry and critical for rare disease portfolio lifecycle planning.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the company\'s relationship to the patent. Owned indicates full proprietary ownership; licensed_in indicates rights acquired from a third party; licensed_out indicates rights granted to a third party; co_owned indicates joint ownership; assigned indicates transfer of ownership has occurred.']
  patent_number varchar [note: 'Officially assigned patent number issued by the relevant patent office (e.g., USPTO, EPO, WIPO). Serves as the primary external business identifier for the patent. Format varies by jurisdiction (e.g., US10123456B2, EP3456789A1).']
  patent_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the patent by the nature of the invention. Composition of matter covers the active molecule (NCE/NBE); method of use covers therapeutic indications; formulation covers drug product design; process covers manufacturing methods; polymorph and salt form cover physical-chemical variants. Critical for LOE (Loss of Exclusivity) strategy and generic/biosimilar entry defense.']
  pediatric_exclusivity_expiry date [note: 'Date on which the 6-month pediatric exclusivity extension expires, granted by the FDA under the Best Pharmaceuticals for Children Act (BPCA) for conducting pediatric studies. Attaches to and extends both patent and data exclusivity periods.']
  priority_date date [note: 'Earliest claimed priority date, typically the date of the first-filed application in the patent family (provisional or national phase). Determines prior art cutoff and is critical for freedom-to-operate (FTO) analyses and validity challenges.']
  prosecution_status varchar [note: 'Detailed status of the patent application within the prosecution workflow at the patent office. Provides granular tracking beyond legal_status for IP counsel and patent attorneys managing active applications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: filed|examination|office_action|response_pending|allowed|granted|abandoned — promote to reference product]']
  purple_book_listed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this patent is listed in the FDA Purple Book (List of Licensed Biological Products). Relevant for biologics and biosimilar entry defense under the Biologics Price Competition and Innovation Act (BPCIA).']
  spc_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC) expires, representing the final end of patent-based market exclusivity in the relevant EU jurisdiction. Null if no SPC applies.']
  spc_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC) granted in EU member states to extend patent protection beyond the standard 20-year term to compensate for regulatory review time. Null if no SPC has been granted.']
  term_adjustment_days varchar [note: 'Number of days added to the patent term by the USPTO as Patent Term Adjustment (PTA) to compensate for delays in patent prosecution caused by the USPTO. Directly impacts the effective expiry date calculation.']
  term_extension_days varchar [note: 'Number of days added to the patent term under 35 U.S.C. § 156 (US) to compensate for time lost during FDA regulatory review of the drug product. Maximum extension is 5 years; total remaining term cannot exceed 14 years post-approval.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Primary therapeutic area or disease category addressed by the patented compound or method (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Cardiovascular, Neuroscience). Aligns with the company\'s R&D portfolio structure and supports commercial lifecycle planning.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official title of the patent as filed and granted by the patent office. Describes the invention in human-readable form and is used for portfolio reporting and IP management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this patent record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  veeva_vault_doc_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this patent record to the corresponding document stored in Veeva Vault (RIM or QualityDocs module), where the patent application, grant certificate, and related correspondence are archived. Supports traceability to the system of record for IP documents.']

  Note: 'Core master record for all pharmaceutical patents owned or licensed by the company. Captures patent number, title, filing/grant/expiry dates, patent family reference, jurisdiction, patent type (composition of matter, method of use, formulation, process, polymorph, salt form), covered compound (INN/USAN), therapeutic area, legal status, and assignee. Also serves as the anchor for trade secrets and know-how records via asset classification. SSOT for the IP portfolio — anchors lifecycle management, LOE planning, litigation defense, and generic/biosimilar entry strategy.'
}

Table intellectual.patent_claim {
  patent_claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual patent claim record within the intellectual property portfolio management system.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Patent claims are jurisdiction-specific; critical for patent prosecution strategy, litigation planning, and claim scope analysis in pharmaceutical IP management.']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Specific patent claims cover specific products. Essential for FTO analysis, claim construction in litigation, Orange Book listing accuracy, and claim-level infringement assessment. Removes denormalize']
  drug_substance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: Composition-of-matter and salt-form patent claims cover specific drug substances. IP teams need to link patent claims to drug substances for FTO analysis, claim scope assessment, and validity challeng']
  formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.formulation. Business justification: Formulation patent claims (e.g., extended-release, nanoparticle) cover specific formulations. IP teams link patent claims to formulations for FTO clearance, claim mapping during ANDA challenges, and d']
  masterdata_atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: Patent claim portfolio segmentation by ATC class is required for competitive intelligence reports and IP strategy by therapeutic area. A pharma IP expert expects claims to be classifiable by ATC. No e']
  masterdata_inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: IP portfolio management requires linking patent claims to the specific INN (active substance) they protect. FTO analysis and regulatory submissions depend on knowing which INN each claim covers. No ex']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Manufacturing FTO clearance requires linking patent claims to the specific material (API or formulation) they cover. Supply chain IP risk assessment depends on this link. No existing material FK on pa']
  orange_book_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.orange_book_listing. Business justification: patent_claim has is_orange_book_listed (BOOLEAN) indicating whether the specific claim is listed in the Orange Book. The actual listing record is in orange_book_listing. Adding a FK normalizes this — ']
  outside_counsel_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Outside counsel firms handling patent claim prosecution are business partners in the vendor master. Linking enables legal spend management and AP payment processing. outside_counsel_firm is a denormal']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent patent record to which this claim belongs. Each patent may contain multiple independent and dependent claims.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent amendment to this claim was filed or entered. Null if the claim has never been amended. Used to track prosecution history and assess prosecution history estoppel in infringement proceedings.']
  application_number varchar [note: 'The official application number assigned by the patent office upon filing (e.g., US 16/123,456 or EP 19 123 456.7). Used to track prosecution history, office actions, and correspondence with patent counsel. Serves as the primary external identifier for the application.']
  challenge_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the claim is subject to an active or completed post-grant challenge. IPR (Inter Partes Review) and opposition proceedings can invalidate individual claims independently. Critical for generic/biosimilar entry defense and Paragraph IV ANDA litigation strategy.']
  claim_amendment_version varchar [note: 'Version number tracking amendments made to the claim text during prosecution or post-grant proceedings (e.g., reexamination, IPR). Version 1 represents the originally filed claim; subsequent versions reflect amendments. Used to maintain prosecution history and support claim construction arguments.']
  claim_category varchar [note: 'Subject matter category of the claim defining what is being protected. Composition claims protect chemical or biological entities (NCE, NBE, API); method/use claims protect therapeutic uses or manufacturing processes; formulation claims protect drug product (DP) compositions; dosage form claims protect FDF configurations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: composition|method|process|apparatus|use|formulation|dosage_form|biological — promote to reference product]']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the claim within the patent document as filed or granted (e.g., Claim 1, Claim 2). Claim numbering follows USPTO and EPO conventions and is used in legal proceedings, IPR petitions, and freedom-to-operate analyses.']
  claim_priority_date date [note: 'The earliest effective filing date from which this claim\'s priority is established, including priority claims under Paris Convention or PCT. The priority date determines prior art cutoff and is critical for validity assessments and patent term calculations.']
  claim_scope_classification varchar [note: 'Business classification of the claim\'s legal breadth and coverage. Broad claims cover wide ranges of embodiments; narrow claims are limited to specific embodiments. Genus claims cover a class of compounds or methods; species claims cover a specific member. Used in lifecycle management and generic/biosimilar entry defense strategy.']
  claim_text varchar [note: 'Full verbatim text of the claim as it appears in the granted patent or pending application. This is the legally operative language used in infringement assessments, IPR petitions, inter partes review proceedings, and freedom-to-operate (FTO) analyses. Classified confidential due to strategic IP sensitivity.']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Structural classification of the claim indicating whether it stands alone (independent) or references and narrows a prior claim (dependent or multiple dependent). Independent claims define the broadest scope; dependent claims add limitations. Critical for infringement analysis and claim charting.']
  cpc_classification_code varchar [note: 'The Cooperative Patent Classification (CPC) code assigned by USPTO or EPO, providing more granular technical classification than IPC. Used in patent landscape analyses, freedom-to-operate searches, and competitive intelligence for drug discovery and CMC domains.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this patent claim record was first created in the IP management system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this patent claim record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements. Veeva Vault RIM is the primary system of record for IP and regulatory data in the pharmaceutical enterprise.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The calculated expiration date of the patent claim, accounting for standard 20-year patent term from filing date, patent term adjustments (PTA), and patent term extensions (PTE) granted for regulatory review delays under Hatch-Waxman or SPC in Europe. Critical for lifecycle management and loss-of-exclusivity (LOE) planning.']
  filing_date date [note: 'The date on which the patent application containing this claim was filed with the relevant patent office. Used to calculate patent term and determine prior art under AIA first-inventor-to-file rules.']
  fto_relevance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this claim has been identified as relevant in one or more freedom-to-operate (FTO) analyses. FTO-relevant claims require monitoring for potential infringement by third-party products or by the company\'s own pipeline compounds.']
  grant_date date [note: 'The date on which the patent containing this claim was officially granted by the patent office. Marks the start of the enforceable patent term. Null for pending applications.']
  ipc_classification_code varchar [note: 'The International Patent Classification (IPC) code assigned to this claim, indicating the technical field of the invention (e.g., A61K for pharmaceutical preparations, A61P for therapeutic activity). Used for patent landscape analyses, competitive intelligence, and prior art searches.']
  ipr_petition_number varchar [note: 'The official PTAB (Patent Trial and Appeal Board) or EPO Opposition Division case number for any active or completed inter partes review (IPR) or opposition proceeding challenging this claim. Null if no IPR or opposition has been filed. Critical for litigation tracking and generic/biosimilar defense strategy.']
  is_purple_book_listed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this claim is associated with a patent listed in the FDA Purple Book (Lists of Licensed Biological Products). Purple Book listing is relevant for biosimilar entry defense under the Biologics Price Competition and Innovation Act (BPCIA) patent dance provisions.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this patent claim record was most recently modified in the IP management system. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and data synchronization with Veeva Vault RIM and external patent docketing systems.']
  litigation_case_reference varchar [note: 'Court case number or docket reference for any active or completed patent infringement litigation involving this claim (e.g., Paragraph IV ANDA litigation, biosimilar patent dance disputes). Classified confidential due to legal privilege sensitivity. Null if no litigation is associated.']
  molecule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the molecule or biological entity protected by this claim. NCE (New Chemical Entity) for small molecules; NBE (New Biological Entity) for large molecule biologics; biosimilar for follow-on biologics; vaccine, gene therapy, or cell therapy for advanced therapy medicinal products (ATMPs). Determines applicable regulatory pathway and exclusivity provisions.']
  parent_claim_number varchar [note: 'For dependent claims, the claim number of the parent claim from which this claim depends. Null for independent claims. Used to reconstruct claim dependency trees for infringement and validity analyses.']
  patent_office_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the patent office where this claim was filed or is being prosecuted. USPTO (US), EPO (European), UKIPO (UK post-Brexit), JPO (Japan), CNIPA (China), WIPO (PCT international). Determines applicable procedural rules and examination standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USPTO|EPO|UKIPO|JPO|CNIPA|WIPO|PMDA — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  patent_term_extension_months varchar [note: 'Number of months of patent term extension granted to compensate for regulatory review delays during NDA/BLA/MAA approval processes. In the US, PTE is granted under 35 U.S.C. § 156 (Hatch-Waxman); in Europe, via Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC). Directly impacts loss-of-exclusivity (LOE) date calculations.']
  prosecution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the claim within the patent prosecution lifecycle at the relevant patent office. Pending claims are under examination; allowed claims have been approved but not yet granted; granted claims are part of an issued patent; rejected claims received a final office action; abandoned claims were not pursued further.']
  spc_number varchar [note: 'The official reference number of the Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC) granted in EU member states for this claim. SPCs extend effective patent protection for up to 5 years post-patent expiry for medicinal products that required regulatory approval. Null if no SPC has been granted.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'The therapeutic area or disease indication covered by this claim (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Cardiovascular). Aligns with the company\'s portfolio therapeutic areas and is used for lifecycle management, LOE planning, and commercial strategy coordination.']
  validity_status varchar [note: 'Current legal standing of the claim. Valid claims are enforceable; invalid claims have been found unpatentable; cancelled claims were withdrawn during prosecution or post-grant proceedings; amended claims were modified during IPR or reexamination; under_review indicates active challenge proceedings; stayed indicates enforcement is paused pending litigation outcome.']

  Note: 'Individual claims within a granted or pending patent, capturing claim number, claim type (independent, dependent), claim text, claim scope classification (broad, narrow, genus, species), and validity status. Critical for freedom-to-operate analyses, infringement assessments, and generic/biosimilar entry defense strategies. Each claim has its own legal standing and may be challenged independently during inter partes review (IPR) or opposition proceedings.'
}

Table intellectual.patent_family {
  patent_family_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the patent family record in the Pharmaceuticals IP portfolio system. Primary key for this entity.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Patent families are owned by legal entities; essential for portfolio valuation, corporate structure reporting, and strategic IP management across business units.']
  masterdata_atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: Patent families are organized by therapeutic area; required for strategic portfolio management, competitive intelligence, and therapeutic area investment decisions.']
  masterdata_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: Patent families are managed by business units with cost center allocation; essential for portfolio budgeting, cost tracking, and financial accountability.']
  masterdata_inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: Patent families cover specific INNs; required for competitive intelligence, portfolio strategy, and molecule-specific IP protection planning.']
  masterdata_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.profit_center. Business justification: Patent families align with business segments; essential for strategic portfolio management, segment P&L, and therapeutic area performance tracking.']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: IP portfolio management links patent families to the materials (APIs/formulations) they protect, enabling supply chain IP risk assessment and licensing scope determination. A pharma IP expert expects ']
  priority_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Priority country is fundamental to patent family strategy and filing decisions; required for PCT strategy and global patent portfolio management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the patent family record was first created in the IP data system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  data_exclusivity_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which regulatory data exclusivity (RDE) protection expires for the drug covered by this patent family. Data exclusivity prevents generic/biosimilar applicants from referencing the originator\'s clinical data for a defined period (e.g., 5 years NCE in US, 8+2+1 years in EU).']
  dmf_reference_number varchar [note: 'The FDA Drug Master File (DMF) number or equivalent regulatory dossier reference associated with the API or formulation covered by this patent family. Links IP protection to the regulatory submission record.']
  earliest_expiry_date date [note: 'The earliest expiration date among all granted member patents in the family, before any patent term extensions or supplementary protection certificates. Critical for generic/biosimilar entry defense strategy planning.']
  family_code varchar [note: 'Internally assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the patent family within the IP management system. Used for cross-referencing across Veeva Vault RIM and SAP IP modules.']
  family_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name assigned to the patent family, typically reflecting the invention title or the compound/technology it covers (e.g., \'Compound X Formulation Family\').']
  family_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the patent family. \'Active\' indicates at least one member patent is in force; \'Pending\' indicates applications under examination; \'Expired\' indicates all members have lapsed; \'Abandoned\' indicates prosecution was discontinued. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|pending|expired|abandoned|licensed_out|litigated — promote to reference product]']
  family_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the patent family grouping methodology. \'Simple\' groups patents sharing the same priority; \'Extended\' includes equivalents; \'INPADOC\' follows the European Patent Office INPADOC definition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: simple|extended|INPADOC|domestic|international — promote to reference product]']
  filing_date date [note: 'The date on which the patent family record was formally established in the IP management system, typically corresponding to the priority application filing date. Used for portfolio age analysis and lifecycle tracking.']
  freedom_to_operate_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Freedom-to-Operate (FTO) analysis for this patent family. \'Cleared\' indicates no blocking third-party patents identified; \'At Risk\' indicates potential infringement risk; \'Blocked\' indicates a blocking patent exists; \'Under Review\' indicates analysis in progress.']
  granted_member_count varchar [note: 'Number of patent applications within the family that have been granted (issued) patents across all jurisdictions. Distinguishes granted protection from pending applications for portfolio strength assessment.']
  invention_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of invention covered by the patent family. New Chemical Entity (NCE) covers novel small molecules; New Biological Entity (NBE) covers biologics; formulation covers drug product compositions; process covers manufacturing methods; method_of_use covers therapeutic indications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NCE|NBE|formulation|process|combination|device|method_of_use — promote to reference product]']
  invention_title varchar [note: 'The official title of the invention as stated in the priority or PCT application. Reflects the technical scope of the patent family and is used for portfolio reporting and IP strategy communications.']
  jurisdiction_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct national or regional jurisdictions in which the patent family has at least one active member application or granted patent. Measures geographic breadth of IP protection.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the patent family record. Supports change tracking, audit compliance under 21 CFR Part 11, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  latest_expiry_date date [note: 'The latest expiration date among all granted member patents in the family, including any patent term extensions (PTE) or supplementary protection certificates (SPC). Defines the outer boundary of IP exclusivity for lifecycle management.']
  licensing_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the patent family is subject to a licensing arrangement. \'Licensed In\' means the company holds a license from a third party; \'Licensed Out\' means the company has granted a license to a third party; \'Cross Licensed\' indicates a bilateral arrangement.']
  litigation_status varchar [note: 'Current litigation status associated with this patent family. Tracks whether the family is subject to active patent infringement suits, inter partes review (IPR), or post-grant review (PGR) proceedings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|threatened|filed|settled|judgment_for_patentee|judgment_against — promote to reference product]']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual patent applications and granted patents that are members of this family across all jurisdictions. Indicates the breadth of global IP coverage for the invention.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for IP counsel to record strategic observations, prosecution history highlights, licensing negotiation context, or lifecycle management decisions relevant to the patent family. Not intended for structured data.']
  orange_book_listed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more patents in this family are listed in the FDA Approved Drug Products with Therapeutic Equivalence Evaluations (Orange Book). Orange Book listing triggers 30-month stay provisions against ANDA filers under Hatch-Waxman.']
  orphan_drug_exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invention covered by this patent family has been granted orphan drug designation and associated market exclusivity (7 years in the US, 10 years in the EU). Relevant for rare disease portfolio strategy.']
  patent_register_listed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether patents in this family are listed on the EMA European Patent Register or national patent registers linked to marketing authorisations, triggering data exclusivity and market protection provisions.']
  patent_term_extension_granted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Patent Term Extension (PTE) or Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC) has been granted for any member of this family, compensating for regulatory review time under FDA or EMA processes.']
  pct_application_number varchar [note: 'The international application number filed under the Patent Cooperation Treaty (PCT) administered by WIPO, if applicable. Format: PCT/[country code][year]/[serial number]. Enables simultaneous patent protection in multiple jurisdictions.']
  pct_filing_date date [note: 'The international filing date of the PCT application, if applicable. Determines the 30/31-month national phase entry deadline for member jurisdictions.']
  pct_publication_number varchar [note: 'The WIPO publication number assigned to the PCT international application upon publication (e.g., WO2023/123456). Used for public disclosure tracking and prior art searches.']
  pediatric_exclusivity_expiry_date date [note: 'The expiry date of pediatric exclusivity granted under the Best Pharmaceuticals for Children Act (BPCA) in the US or the Paediatric Regulation in the EU, adding 6 months of additional exclusivity to qualifying patents.']
  portfolio_segment varchar [note: 'Strategic classification of the patent family within the IP portfolio. \'Core\' patents protect primary commercial products; \'Defensive\' patents block competitor freedom to operate; \'Licensing\' patents are monetized through out-licensing; \'Acquired\' patents came through M&A.']
  priority_application_number varchar [note: 'The application number of the earliest-filed patent application that establishes the priority date for the family. Typically a national or regional application number (e.g., US provisional or EP application).']
  priority_date date [note: 'The filing date of the earliest priority application in the family. Establishes the effective date of invention disclosure for novelty and prior art assessments under Paris Convention.']
  responsible_ip_counsel varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the internal IP attorney or external patent counsel responsible for managing prosecution and maintenance of this patent family. Used for accountability and workload management in the IP department.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'The primary therapeutic area or disease indication covered by the invention (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Cardiovascular). Aligns with the company\'s portfolio therapeutic area taxonomy for lifecycle management reporting.']

  Note: 'Groups related patents and patent applications filed across multiple jurisdictions that share a common priority application. Tracks the priority application number, priority date, PCT application number, family type (simple, extended, INPADOC), and member count. Enables portfolio-level analysis of global IP coverage for a given invention and supports lifecycle management across jurisdictions.'
}

Table intellectual.exclusivity_period {
  exclusivity_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each regulatory exclusivity period record in the pharmaceutical intellectual property portfolio.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Exclusivity is jurisdiction-specific; essential for lifecycle management, LOE planning, and market-specific revenue forecasting in pharmaceutical commercialization.']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Exclusivity periods apply to specific drug products (dosage form and strength combinations). FDA exclusivity codes are assigned at the drug product level. Regulatory teams need this link for LOE plann']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Exclusivity periods are granted to and held by a specific legal entity (Marketing Authorization Holder). Regulatory submissions, LOE planning, and legal entity IP asset reporting all require knowing w']
  masterdata_atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: Exclusivity is granted for specific therapeutic indications; required for competitive intelligence, market analysis, and therapeutic area-specific LOE planning.']
  masterdata_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: LOE (loss of exclusivity) defense activities and exclusivity maintenance costs are tracked against cost centers for IP budget management and financial planning. No existing cost_center FK on exclusivi']
  masterdata_inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: Exclusivity is granted for specific INNs; critical for lifecycle management, molecule-specific LOE planning, and regulatory exclusivity tracking.']
  masterdata_ndc_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.ndc_code. Business justification: Exclusivity protects specific NDC-coded products; critical for LOE forecasting, generic entry planning, and product-specific revenue protection.']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pharmaceutical product (Drug Product or Drug Substance) to which this exclusivity period applies.']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'FK to intellectual.patent.patent_id — Exclusivity periods are often tied to specific patents — linking enables IP lifecycle management and loss-of-exclusivity (LOE) planning critical for commercial strategy.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the NDA, BLA, or MAA was approved by the granting authority, which typically triggers the start of the exclusivity period. Serves as the anchor date for exclusivity calculations.']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'The proprietary/trade name of the drug product under which the exclusivity was granted and the product is marketed. Used for commercial lifecycle management and trademark coordination.']
  challenge_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this exclusivity period is subject to a legal or regulatory challenge. Paragraph IV = ANDA Paragraph IV certification challenge under Hatch-Waxman; Inter Partes = IPR/PGR challenge at USPTO; Court Order = court-ordered modification; Under Review = authority-initiated review. Critical for generic entry defense strategy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this exclusivity period record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and SOX compliance.']
  data_package_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory data package submitted with the application. Full = complete standalone dossier (NCE/NBE); 505(b)(2) = hybrid relying on published literature or prior approvals; Hybrid = EU hybrid application; Bibliographic = based on published scientific literature. Determines applicable exclusivity type and duration.']
  dmf_number varchar [note: 'FDA Drug Master File (DMF) number associated with the drug substance or drug product covered by this exclusivity, where applicable. Supports CMC (Chemistry Manufacturing and Controls) linkage and regulatory audit.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'The pharmaceutical dosage form of the product covered by this exclusivity (e.g., tablet, capsule, injection, solution, patch). Relevant because exclusivity may be dosage-form specific.']
  exclusivity_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the exclusivity period indicating whether it applies to a small molecule (NCE/NME), biologic (NBE), orphan drug designation, pediatric extension, or combination product.']
  exclusivity_code varchar [note: 'Internally assigned unique alphanumeric code identifying this exclusivity period record, used for cross-system referencing and lifecycle management tracking.']
  exclusivity_duration_months varchar [note: 'Total duration of the exclusivity period expressed in months as granted by the regulatory authority (e.g., 84 months for US orphan drug, 120 months for EU orphan drug, 144 months for US biologics). Supports duration validation and comparison across jurisdictions.']
  exclusivity_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory exclusivity period expires, after which generic or biosimilar applicants may reference the innovator data. Critical for lifecycle management and generic/biosimilar entry defense planning.']
  exclusivity_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory exclusivity period commences, typically aligned with the date of first approval (NDA/BLA/MAA approval date) or orphan designation date depending on exclusivity type.']
  exclusivity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the exclusivity period. Active = currently in force; Expired = past expiry date; Withdrawn = voluntarily or involuntarily revoked by authority; Challenged = subject to legal or regulatory challenge; Pending = granted but not yet effective; Suspended = temporarily halted pending review.']
  exclusivity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory exclusivity type granted. NCE = New Chemical Entity (5 years US); ODE = Orphan Drug Exclusivity (7 years US / 10 years EU); PED = Pediatric Exclusivity Extension (6 months); DATA = Data Exclusivity for new indication (3 years US); BIOL = Biologics Exclusivity (12 years US); REMS = Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy exclusivity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NCE|ODE|PED|DATA|BIOL|REMS|NME|PATENT_LINKAGE — promote to reference product]']
  first_generic_entry_date date [note: 'The projected or actual date on which the first generic (ANDA) or biosimilar (351(k) BLA) product may enter the market following expiry or challenge of this exclusivity. Used for lifecycle management, revenue forecasting, and LOE (Loss of Exclusivity) planning.']
  granting_authority varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority that granted this exclusivity period (e.g., FDA for US market, EMA for EU market, PMDA for Japan, MHRA for UK post-Brexit). Determines jurisdiction and applicable exclusivity rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|PMDA|MHRA|NMPA|WHO|DEA — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  indication varchar [note: 'The specific disease or condition for which the product is approved and for which this exclusivity was granted. Particularly important for new indication (3-year data exclusivity) and orphan drug exclusivity where exclusivity is indication-specific.']
  is_orphan_designated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product holds an orphan drug designation from FDA (Orphan Drug Act) or EMA (Regulation EC 141/2000), which is a prerequisite for orphan drug exclusivity (ODE). Distinct from exclusivity_type as designation may exist without exclusivity being granted.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this exclusivity period record, supporting change tracking and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records regulations.']
  legal_counsel_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from legal counsel regarding this exclusivity period, including litigation strategy, challenge responses, and coordination with patent attorneys. Classified as confidential due to potential attorney-client privilege content.']
  lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle management stage of the product relative to this exclusivity period. Pre-Approval = exclusivity anticipated but not yet granted; Active Protection = exclusivity in force; LOE Watch = within 24 months of expiry requiring active monitoring; Post-Expiry = exclusivity has lapsed.']
  loe_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk level for Loss of Exclusivity (LOE) based on proximity to expiry, challenge status, and competitive pipeline. Critical for commercial lifecycle management and strategic planning.']
  orphan_designation_number varchar [note: 'Official orphan drug designation number assigned by FDA (ODA) or EMA (EC Regulation 141/2000) when exclusivity_type is ODE. Required for orphan drug exclusivity validation and regulatory reporting.']
  patent_linkage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this exclusivity period is linked to a listed patent in the FDA Orange Book or equivalent patent register, enabling coordination between patent protection and regulatory exclusivity for lifecycle management.']
  pediatric_extension_expiry_date date [note: 'The adjusted expiry date reflecting the 6-month pediatric exclusivity extension, if granted. Null if pediatric_extension_granted is False.']
  pediatric_extension_granted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a 6-month pediatric exclusivity extension (PED) has been granted under the Best Pharmaceuticals for Children Act (BPCA) or EU Paediatric Regulation, extending the base exclusivity or patent term by 6 months.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'The specific statutory or regulatory provision under which this exclusivity was granted (e.g., 21 CFR 314.108(b)(2) for NCE, Orphan Drug Act Section 527, BPCIA Section 7002, EU Regulation 141/2000 Article 8). Supports legal and regulatory audit trails.']
  rems_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) is required as a condition of approval for this product, which may confer additional exclusivity protections or market access restrictions relevant to generic entry.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'The route by which the drug product is administered (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, topical). Exclusivity may be route-specific, particularly for new indication or new formulation exclusivity.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this exclusivity period data was sourced (e.g., Veeva Vault RIM, FDA Orange Book feed, EMA EPAR, SAP S/4HANA). Supports data lineage and Silver layer provenance tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'The therapeutic area or disease category associated with the approved indication (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Neurology, Cardiovascular). Used for portfolio segmentation and LOE impact analysis by therapeutic area.']

  Note: 'Tracks all regulatory exclusivity periods granted to pharmaceutical products, distinct from patent protection. Covers orphan drug exclusivity (7 years US / 10 years EU), data exclusivity (5 years NCE / 3 years new indication), pediatric exclusivity extension (6 months), new chemical entity exclusivity, biologics exclusivity (12 years US), and REMS exclusivity. Captures exclusivity type, granting authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA), start date, expiry date, product reference, and associated NDA/BLA/MAA number. Critical for lifecycle management and generic/biosimilar entry defense.'
}

Table intellectual.trademark {
  trademark_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each trademark registration record in the pharmaceutical intellectual property portfolio.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Trademarks are registered by jurisdiction; required for brand protection strategy, market entry planning, and global brand portfolio management.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Trademarks are registered to legal entities; required for brand ownership tracking, legal compliance, and M&A due diligence in pharmaceutical branding.']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: trademark has licensing_flag (BOOLEAN) indicating the trademark is subject to a licensing arrangement. The governing licensing agreement is tracked in licensing_agreement, which covers all IP licensin']
  masterdata_atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: Trademarks are associated with therapeutic categories; required for brand strategy, market positioning, and therapeutic area-specific brand portfolio management.']
  masterdata_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: Trademark maintenance costs (annuity fees, watch services, opposition proceedings) are tracked against cost centers for IP budget management and financial reporting. A pharma IP controller expects tra']
  masterdata_inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: Trademarks are brand names for INN-based drugs. EMA and FDA require INN alongside brand name on packaging; regulatory submissions map trademark to INN. inn_name on trademark is a denormalized represen']
  masterdata_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.profit_center. Business justification: Trademarks are associated with profit centers for brand P&L reporting and licensing revenue attribution under IFRS. Brand management requires profit-center-level trademark portfolio reporting. No exis']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Brand trademarks are associated with specific materials (finished drug products) for packaging artwork management and supply chain labeling compliance. Artwork approval workflows require trademark-to-']
  application_number varchar [note: 'The official application number assigned by the trademark office at the time of filing, prior to registration. Used to track the application through examination and opposition proceedings.']
  colour_claim varchar [note: 'Description of any specific colour or colour combination claimed as a feature of the trademark (e.g., Pantone codes, RGB values, or textual colour descriptions). Relevant for figurative and combined marks where colour is a distinctive element.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the trademark record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  disclaimer_text varchar [note: 'Any disclaimer of exclusive rights to specific elements of the trademark (e.g., generic or descriptive terms within a combined mark) as required or accepted by the trademark office during examination.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the current trademark registration term expires and renewal must be filed to maintain protection. Critical for lifecycle management and brand protection continuity.']
  external_counsel_firm varchar [note: 'Name of the external law firm or IP agent responsible for managing the trademark prosecution and maintenance in the relevant jurisdiction. Supports coordination with legal counsel on prosecution strategy and renewal deadlines.']
  filing_date date [note: 'The date on which the trademark application was officially filed with the relevant trademark office. Establishes priority date for the mark in the jurisdiction.']
  goods_services_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the goods and/or services covered by the trademark registration, as specified in the application. For pharmaceutical trademarks, typically describes the therapeutic area, dosage form, and indication of the drug product covered.']
  internal_reference_code varchar [note: 'Internal company reference code or docket number assigned to the trademark record in the company\'s IP management system (e.g., Veeva Vault RIM or internal IP docketing system). Enables cross-referencing with internal IP portfolio management tools.']
  litigation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the trademark is currently involved in active litigation or legal proceedings (True), such as infringement actions, cancellation proceedings, or inter partes disputes. Triggers coordination with legal counsel.']
  madrid_international_number varchar [note: 'The WIPO-assigned international registration number for trademarks filed under the Madrid Protocol. Populated only when madrid_protocol_flag is True.']
  madrid_protocol_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this trademark registration was obtained through the WIPO Madrid Protocol international registration system (True) or through a direct national/regional filing (False). Affects renewal procedures and management workflows.']
  mark_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the trademark by its nature: word mark (text only), figurative mark (logo/image only), combined mark (text and image), three-dimensional mark (shape), sound mark, or colour mark. Drives brand protection strategy and enforcement scope. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: word_mark|figurative_mark|combined_mark|three_dimensional_mark|sound_mark|colour_mark — promote to reference product]']
  nice_class_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Nice Classification class numbers (1–45) under which the trademark is registered. For pharmaceutical trademarks, typically includes Class 5 (pharmaceutical preparations) and potentially Class 10 (medical devices) or Class 44 (medical services). Defines the scope of goods and services covered by the mark.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional remarks, prosecution history notes, strategic comments, or legal observations relevant to the trademark record that are not captured in structured fields.']
  office_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the trademark office or registry where the application was filed or registration is held (e.g., USPTO for United States, EUIPO for European Union, UKIPO for United Kingdom, JPO for Japan, CNIPA for China, WIPO for international registrations). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USPTO|EUIPO|UKIPO|JPO|CNIPA|WIPO|CIPO|IP_AUSTRALIA — promote to reference product]']
  opposition_deadline_date date [note: 'The date by which third parties may file an opposition to the trademark application in the relevant jurisdiction. Critical for monitoring the application\'s progress toward registration and for assessing third-party challenge risk.']
  opposition_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any opposition proceedings against the trademark application: not_opposed (no opposition filed), opposition_filed (third-party opposition pending), opposition_withdrawn (opponent withdrew), or opposition_decided (proceeding concluded by the trademark office).']
  owner_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the trademark owner\'s country of domicile or incorporation, as recorded in the trademark register.']
  priority_claim_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction in which the priority application was originally filed, as claimed under the Paris Convention.']
  priority_claim_date date [note: 'The date of the earliest priority application claimed under the Paris Convention, if the trademark application claims priority from a prior filing in another jurisdiction. Establishes the effective priority date for multi-jurisdictional filings.']
  product_launch_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary market where the associated pharmaceutical drug product is commercially launched or planned for launch under this trademark. Links trademark protection to commercial launch activities.']
  registration_date date [note: 'The date on which the trademark was officially registered by the trademark office. Marks the start of the initial registration term and triggers renewal obligation tracking.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'The official registration number assigned by the trademark office upon successful registration of the mark. Serves as the externally-known unique identifier for the trademark in the relevant jurisdiction.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the trademark registration: pending (application filed, under examination), registered (officially granted), opposed (subject to third-party opposition proceedings), cancelled (registration revoked), expired (lapsed due to non-renewal), or abandoned (application withdrawn or lapsed). Critical for brand protection and commercial launch readiness.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'The date by which the renewal application must be submitted to the trademark office to avoid lapse of the registration. May differ from expiry_date due to grace period provisions.']
  renewal_reminder_date date [note: 'The internal reminder date set for initiating the trademark renewal process, typically set a defined number of months before the official renewal_date. Supports proactive renewal management and avoidance of unintentional lapse.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'The therapeutic area or disease category for which the associated drug product is indicated (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Cardiovascular). Supports portfolio segmentation and brand strategy analytics.']
  trademark_name varchar [note: 'The brand name, word mark, or slogan that is the subject of the trademark registration (e.g., a proprietary drug brand name). Serves as the primary human-readable identity label for the trademark record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the trademark record was last modified in the system. Supports audit trail requirements, change tracking, and incremental data processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  use_anywhere_date date [note: 'The date on which the trademark was first used anywhere (not necessarily in commerce) in connection with the identified goods/services. Relevant for establishing priority in use-based trademark systems.']
  use_in_commerce_date date [note: 'The date on which the trademark was first used in commerce in connection with the goods/services identified in the registration. Required for use-based trademark systems (e.g., USPTO) and relevant for establishing priority and defending against cancellation for non-use.']
  watch_service_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a trademark watch service is active for this mark (True), monitoring for conflicting third-party applications in the relevant jurisdictions. Supports proactive brand protection strategy.']

  Note: 'Master record for all pharmaceutical trademark registrations including brand names, logos, trade dress, and slogans. Captures trademark name, registration number, filing date, registration date, expiry/renewal date, jurisdiction, Nice classification, goods and services description, trademark type (word mark, figurative, combined), owner entity, and current status (pending, registered, opposed, cancelled). Supports brand protection strategy and commercial launch activities.'
}

Table intellectual.drug_master_file {
  drug_master_file_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the Drug Master File record in the enterprise data platform (Silver Layer). Primary key for this entity.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: DMFs are filed with country-specific regulatory authorities; required for regulatory compliance, supply chain qualification, and market-specific manufacturing authorization.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: DMFs are held by legal entities; required for regulatory submissions, supply chain qualification, and vendor management in pharmaceutical manufacturing.']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: Drug Master Files (DMFs) contain confidential manufacturing and formulation information. DMF holders often grant access to third parties (e.g., generic manufacturers, partners) via licensing agreement']
  masterdata_atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: DMFs are classified by ATC for regulatory portfolio management and therapeutic area reporting. atc_code on drug_master_file is a denormalized representation of atc_classification.atc_code. Proper FK e']
  masterdata_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: DMF maintenance costs (annual updates, regulatory submissions, amendment fees) are tracked against cost centers for regulatory affairs budget management and R&D cost capitalization under IAS 38. No ex']
  masterdata_inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: DMFs describe specific INNs; required for regulatory submissions, molecule-DMF linkage, and supply chain qualification in pharmaceutical manufacturing.']
  masterdata_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.profit_center. Business justification: DMFs support commercial products; their costs and associated revenues are attributed to profit centers for product P&L reporting and segment financial reporting. No existing profit_center FK on drug_m']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: DMFs describe specific materials; required for regulatory compliance, procurement, and material-DMF linkage in pharmaceutical supply chain management.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: DMFs reference manufacturing sites; essential for regulatory submissions, supply chain qualification, and site-specific manufacturing authorization.']
  api_manufacturer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DMF holder is the manufacturer of the Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API) covered by this DMF. True for Type II DMFs where the holder manufactures the drug substance. Used to distinguish API manufacturers from intermediates suppliers, excipient suppliers, and packaging material suppliers.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'CAS Registry Number uniquely identifying the chemical substance covered by the DMF. Used for substance identification in regulatory submissions, safety databases, and supply chain qualification. Applicable primarily to Type II (API/DS) and Type IV (excipient) DMFs.']
  cmc_section_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific CMC (Chemistry, Manufacturing, and Controls) section of the eCTD submission (Module 3) that this DMF supports. Used to map the DMF to the corresponding regulatory dossier section for NDA/BLA/ANDA/MAA submissions.']
  confidentiality_commitment_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority acknowledged the confidential nature of the DMF content. The FDA treats DMF content as confidential trade secret information. This date records when the confidentiality commitment was formally established, supporting IP protection and trade secret management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DMF record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.']
  cross_reference_count varchar [note: 'Number of active regulatory applications (NDA, BLA, ANDA, IND, MAA) that currently cross-reference this DMF. Indicates the commercial and regulatory criticality of the DMF; a high count signals high supply chain dependency and elevated risk if the DMF is withdrawn or goes inactive.']
