  dmf_number varchar [note: 'Official DMF number assigned by the regulatory authority (e.g., FDA) upon receipt of the DMF submission. Serves as the primary external identifier used in cross-references within NDA, BLA, ANDA, and IND applications. Format varies by authority (e.g., FDA assigns a numeric DMF number).']
  dmf_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the DMF with the regulatory authority. \'active\' indicates the DMF is in good standing and available for cross-reference; \'inactive\' indicates it has not been updated within the required period; \'withdrawn\' indicates the holder has formally withdrawn the file; \'under_review\' indicates a pending agency review; \'closed\' indicates the file has been administratively closed.']
  dmf_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the DMF per FDA categorization: Type I (Manufacturing Site, Facilities, Operating Procedures, and Personnel — discontinued by FDA but retained for legacy), Type II (Drug Substance, Drug Substance Intermediate, and Material Used in Their Preparation, or Drug Product), Type III (Packaging Material), Type IV (Excipient, Colorant, Flavor, Essence, or Material Used in Their Preparation), Type V (FDA-Accepted Reference Information). Drives CMC submission strategy and third-party qualification workflows.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'Physical form of the drug product or substance covered by the DMF (e.g., tablet, capsule, injectable solution, lyophilized powder, cream, patch). Relevant primarily for Type II (Drug Product) and Type III (Packaging) DMFs. Aligns with FDA/EMA dosage form terminology. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|injectable_solution|lyophilized_powder|cream|patch|suspension|gel|suppository|inhalation_powder — promote to reference product]']
  ectd_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit eCTD sequence number assigned to the DMF submission or amendment within the eCTD lifecycle. Used to track the submission history and version control of the DMF in the regulatory authority\'s electronic submission gateway.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the DMF became effective and available for cross-reference by applicants in regulatory submissions (NDA, BLA, ANDA, IND). May differ from submission date if the authority requires a review period before the file is deemed active.']
  fda_establishment_identifier varchar [note: 'FDA Establishment Identifier (FEI) number assigned to the manufacturing facility by the FDA. Used to link the DMF to the FDA\'s establishment registration database, enabling GMP inspection history lookup and import alert monitoring.']
  gmp_certificate_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the current GMP certificate for the manufacturing site associated with this DMF. Triggers renewal workflows and supplier qualification reviews. An expired GMP certificate may block use of the DMF in new regulatory submissions.']
  gmp_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number of the current GMP certificate issued by a recognized regulatory authority or inspection body (e.g., FDA, EMA, PIC/S member authority) for the manufacturing site associated with this DMF. Used for supplier qualification and regulatory compliance verification.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment or annual update submitted to the regulatory authority for this DMF. FDA requires annual updates to maintain active status. Tracks currency of the file and triggers compliance alerts when updates are overdue.']
  letter_of_authorization_date date [note: 'Date on which the DMF holder issued the Letter of Authorization (LOA) permitting the company to cross-reference this DMF in its regulatory submissions. Relevant when the company is the applicant referencing a third-party DMF.']
  letter_of_authorization_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Letter of Authorization (LOA) from the DMF holder is required before an applicant can cross-reference this DMF in their regulatory submission. True for most Type II and Type IV DMFs where the holder controls access to confidential manufacturing information.']
  manufacturing_site_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country where the manufacturing site is located. Used for import/export compliance, country-of-origin tracking, supply chain risk management, and regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  next_annual_update_due_date date [note: 'Calculated due date for the next mandatory annual update submission to maintain active DMF status with the regulatory authority. Derived from the last amendment date plus 12 months. Used for compliance monitoring and proactive submission planning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional regulatory, operational, or strategic notes related to this DMF. May include information about pending amendments, agency correspondence, supplier negotiations, or lifecycle management decisions not captured in structured fields.']
  proprietary_holder_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the company itself (Pharmaceuticals) is the holder of this DMF (True) or whether it is a third-party supplier DMF that the company cross-references (False). Drives different workflows: internal DMFs require internal maintenance; third-party DMFs require supplier management and LOA tracking.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'The regulatory authority to which the DMF was submitted (e.g., FDA for US, EMA for EU, PMDA for Japan, MHRA for UK, NMPA for China). Determines applicable regulatory requirements, format standards (e.g., eCTD vs. paper), and review timelines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|PMDA|MHRA|NMPA|WHO|Health Canada|TGA — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rims_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Internal tracking number assigned to this DMF record within the company\'s Regulatory Information Management System (RIMS), such as Veeva Vault RIM. Used for internal workflow management, submission tracking, and audit trail purposes.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'Intended route of administration for the drug substance or product covered by the DMF (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, topical, inhalation). Applicable for Type II DMFs. Aligns with FDA/EMA route of administration terminology. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|intravenous|subcutaneous|intramuscular|topical|inhalation|transdermal|intrathecal — promote to reference product]']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the DMF was first submitted to the regulatory authority. Establishes the official filing date and is used to calculate review timelines, annual update obligations, and IP/exclusivity period calculations.']
  submission_format varchar [note: 'Format in which the DMF was submitted to the regulatory authority: \'eCTD\' (Electronic Common Technical Document — mandatory for FDA since 2017 for most submissions), \'paper\' (legacy paper-based submission), or \'NeeS\' (Non-eCTD Electronic Submission — used in some jurisdictions). Determines document management and archival requirements.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Primary therapeutic area or disease indication associated with the drug substance covered by this DMF (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Cardiovascular, Neurology). Used for portfolio management, lifecycle planning, and IP strategy alignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oncology|immunology|rare_diseases|cardiovascular|neurology|infectious_diseases|metabolic|respiratory|dermatology|hematology — promote to reference product]']
  title varchar [note: 'Official descriptive title of the DMF as submitted to the regulatory authority. Typically includes the substance or material name, manufacturer name, and scope of the file (e.g., \'Drug Substance DMF for [INN] manufactured at [Site]\').']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DMF record was most recently updated in the enterprise data platform. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and data freshness monitoring in compliance with 21 CFR Part 11.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault RIM (Regulatory Information Management) system where the DMF submission package and associated correspondence are stored. Enables traceability between the enterprise data platform record and the source regulatory document management system.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date on which the DMF holder formally withdrew the DMF from the regulatory authority. Populated only when dmf_status is \'withdrawn\'. Triggers downstream impact assessment for any NDA/BLA/ANDA applications that cross-reference this DMF.']

  Note: 'Master record for Drug Master Files (DMFs) submitted to regulatory authorities. Covers Type I-V DMFs (manufacturing site, drug substance/API, packaging, excipient, reference information). Captures DMF number, type, holder, submission/amendment dates, regulatory authority, active status, and associated substance. Supports CMC submissions and third-party API/excipient qualification for regulatory filings.'
}

Table intellectual.licensing_agreement {
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the licensing agreement record in the intellectual property portfolio system.']
  licensee_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: The licensee in a licensing agreement is a business partner in the vendor/customer master. Linking enables royalty payment processing, credit management, and partner relationship management. licensee_']
  licensor_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Licensing agreements require tracking licensor legal entity for contract management, revenue recognition, and intercompany royalty accounting in pharmaceutical partnerships.']
  masterdata_atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: Licensing portfolio is segmented by ATC therapeutic class for business development reporting, deal valuation, and portfolio strategy. BD teams report licensing deal value by therapeutic area using ATC']
  masterdata_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: License agreements generate costs/revenues allocated to cost centers; required for P&L reporting, royalty expense tracking, and business unit accountability.']
  masterdata_inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: Licensing agreements for pharmaceutical compounds are tied to specific INNs (active substances). Royalty calculation, FTO analysis, and regulatory compliance tracking require substance-level licensing']
  masterdata_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.profit_center. Business justification: License agreements are assigned to business segments; required for revenue recognition, segment P&L reporting, and royalty income allocation.']
  territory_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: License territories define geographic scope; essential for revenue allocation, royalty calculation, and compliance with territorial restrictions in pharmaceutical licensing.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to the licensing agreement, used in correspondence with counterparties, legal counsel, and regulatory filings. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the licensing agreement. Draft: under negotiation. Active: fully executed and in force. Suspended: temporarily paused by mutual consent or breach notice. Terminated: ended before natural expiry. Expired: reached end of term without renewal. Drives deal management workflows and financial reporting.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the licensing agreement by deal structure. In-licensing: rights acquired from a third party. Out-licensing: rights granted to a third party. Cross-licensing: mutual exchange of IP rights. Co-development: joint development with shared IP. Collaboration: broad research or commercialization partnership. Drives revenue recognition treatment under ASC 606.']
  change_of_control_provision boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement contains a change-of-control clause that grants termination or renegotiation rights upon acquisition or merger of either party. True = change-of-control provision exists; False = no such provision. Critical for M&A due diligence and IP portfolio valuation.']
  commercial_milestone_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate value of all commercial milestone payments (e.g., first commercial sale, annual net sales thresholds) defined in the agreement, expressed in the agreement currency. Supports commercial launch planning and ASC 606 variable consideration estimation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the licensing agreement record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED audit trail required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance and SOX financial reporting controls.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all financial obligations under the agreement (upfront payments, milestones, royalties) are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, GBP). Required for multi-currency royalty accounting and foreign exchange hedging.']
  development_milestone_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate value of all development-stage milestone payments (e.g., IND submission, Phase I/II/III completion) defined in the agreement, expressed in the agreement currency. Supports clinical development budget planning and ASC 606 variable consideration estimation.']
  diligence_obligations varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the commercially reasonable efforts or best efforts diligence obligations imposed on the licensee to develop and commercialize the licensed product. Failure to meet diligence obligations may trigger termination rights. Used for partner relationship management and compliance monitoring.']
  dispute_resolution_mechanism varchar [note: 'Contractually agreed mechanism for resolving disputes between the parties. Arbitration: binding arbitration under specified rules (e.g., ICC, AAA). Litigation: resolution through courts of the governing jurisdiction. Mediation: non-binding mediation before escalation. Expert-determination: technical disputes resolved by independent expert.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the licensing agreement becomes legally binding and operative. Marks the start of the agreement term for royalty accrual, exclusivity period calculation, and milestone tracking. Corresponds to EFFECTIVE_FROM for the MASTER_AGREEMENT role.']
  exclusivity_type varchar [note: 'Scope of exclusivity granted under the license. Exclusive: licensee is the sole permitted user in the territory/field. Non-exclusive: licensor may grant same rights to others. Co-exclusive: limited number of licensees permitted. Semi-exclusive: licensor retains right to practice but not to license others. Critical for generic/biosimilar entry defense strategy.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date on which the licensing agreement was formally signed and executed by all parties. May differ from the effective date if the agreement has a retroactive or future effective date. Used for legal record-keeping and audit trails.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the licensing agreement naturally expires at the end of its agreed term. Nullable for perpetual or open-ended agreements. Drives lifecycle management alerts, renewal negotiations, and IP exclusivity planning. Corresponds to EFFECTIVE_UNTIL for the MASTER_AGREEMENT role.']
  field_of_use varchar [note: 'Specific therapeutic area, indication, or application domain within which the licensee is permitted to use the licensed IP. Examples: \'oncology\', \'rare diseases\', \'human therapeutics only\', \'diagnostic use\'. Restricts the scope of the license and is critical for exclusivity analysis and sublicensing rights management.']
  governing_law varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of the agreement. Determines applicable contract law, dispute resolution procedures, and regulatory compliance obligations.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the licensing agreement record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED audit trail required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance and change control tracking.']
  legal_counsel_reference varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the internal or external legal counsel responsible for drafting, negotiating, and maintaining the agreement. Used for coordination on patent litigation, IP enforcement, and contract amendments.']
  licensed_asset_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the intellectual property assets covered by the license, including compound names (INN/USAN), patent numbers, know-how, trade secrets, trademarks, or biological sequences. Supports IP portfolio management and freedom-to-operate analysis.']
  licensed_asset_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary intellectual property asset type covered by the agreement. Patent: granted patent rights. Know-how: proprietary technical knowledge. Trademark: brand or trade name rights. Data-package: regulatory data exclusivity or clinical data. Biological-sequence: gene or protein sequence rights. Trade-secret: confidential proprietary information. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: patent|know-how|trademark|data-package|biological-sequence|trade-secret|formulation|process — promote to reference product]']
  milestone_count varchar [note: 'Total number of contractually defined milestone events (development, regulatory, and commercial) specified in the agreement. Used for milestone tracking dashboards, deal management reporting, and ASC 606 performance obligation identification.']
  regulatory_milestone_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate value of all regulatory milestone payments (e.g., NDA/BLA/MAA submission, FDA/EMA approval) defined in the agreement, expressed in the agreement currency. Supports regulatory affairs planning and ASC 606 variable consideration estimation.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Identifies which party bears primary responsibility for conducting development activities (clinical trials, regulatory submissions, manufacturing scale-up) under the agreement. Licensor: licensor retains development responsibility. Licensee: licensee assumes development responsibility. Joint: shared responsibility as defined in the agreement.']
  right_of_first_negotiation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement grants either party a right of first negotiation (ROFN) for additional territories, indications, or follow-on compounds. True = ROFN exists; False = no ROFN. Relevant for lifecycle management and business development strategy.']
  royalty_base varchar [note: 'The contractual basis on which royalties are calculated. Net-sales: royalties applied to net sales after standard deductions. Gross-sales: royalties applied to gross sales before deductions. Net-revenue: royalties applied to net revenue. Units-sold: royalties calculated per unit sold. Determines the royalty accounting methodology.']
  royalty_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'Contractual royalty rate expressed as a decimal fraction of net sales (e.g., 0.050000 = 5.0%) payable by the licensee to the licensor. Used for royalty accrual calculations, revenue recognition under ASC 606/IFRS 15, and P&L forecasting. Null if the agreement uses a tiered royalty structure captured in a separate milestone/royalty schedule.']
  sap_contract_number varchar [note: 'Contract number assigned in SAP S/4HANA (SD or FI module) for financial processing of royalty payments, milestone invoicing, and upfront fee recognition. Enables reconciliation between the IP portfolio system and the ERP for royalty accounting and revenue recognition.']
  sublicensing_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensee has the contractual right to sublicense the licensed IP to third parties (e.g., CMOs, CDMOs, or sub-distributors). True = sublicensing permitted; False = sublicensing prohibited. Impacts CMO/CDMO engagement and supply chain structuring.']
  termination_conditions varchar [note: 'Summary of the contractual conditions under which either party may terminate the agreement, including material breach cure periods, insolvency triggers, regulatory failure events, and convenience termination rights. Used by legal counsel for contract management and risk assessment.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the licensing agreement was terminated prior to its natural expiry, whether by mutual consent, breach, or exercise of termination rights. Null if the agreement has not been terminated. Used for financial close-out and IP rights reversion tracking.']
  total_deal_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total potential deal value including upfront payments, all milestone payments, and estimated royalties over the agreement term, expressed in the agreement currency. Used for deal valuation, P&L forecasting, and investor disclosure. Represents the gross deal value before probability-weighting.']
  total_milestone_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all contractually defined milestone payment amounts across all milestone events in the agreement, expressed in the agreement currency. Represents the maximum milestone-based consideration achievable under the agreement. Used for deal valuation and revenue recognition planning.']
  upfront_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'One-time upfront license fee or signing payment due at execution of the agreement, expressed in the agreement currency. Recognized as revenue or capitalized as an intangible asset depending on the nature of the license under ASC 606/IFRS 15. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this MASTER_AGREEMENT.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the fully executed agreement document stored in Veeva Vault (RIM or QualityDocs module). Enables direct navigation from the data product record to the source legal document for audit, due diligence, and regulatory inspection purposes. Aligns with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.']

  Note: 'Records all IP licensing agreements (in-licensing, out-licensing, cross-licensing, co-development, collaboration) including deal terms, milestone obligations, and milestone payment tracking. Captures agreement type, parties, licensed assets, territory, field of use, exclusivity scope, royalty structure, milestone events (regulatory filing, IND submission, phase completion, NDA approval, first commercial sale, sales thresholds), milestone descriptions, trigger events, milestone due dates, achieved dates, milestone payment amounts, currency, payment status, responsible party, sublicensing rights, and termination conditions. Supports deal management, milestone tracking, revenue recognition (ASC 606), royalty accounting, and partner relationship management.'
}

Table intellectual.royalty_payment {
  royalty_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each royalty payment transaction record within the intellectual property portfolio management system.']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Royalty payments are calculated on net sales of specific drug products (by dosage form and strength). Finance and BD teams need drug_product-level granularity for royalty reporting, audit reconciliati']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Every royalty payment (in or out) generates a GL posting for revenue/expense recognition. Finance links royalty payments to journal entries for reconciliation, audit trail, SOX compliance, and revenue']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensing agreement under which this royalty payment is made or received. Links the payment to the governing contract terms.']
  masterdata_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: Royalty payments are posted to cost centers for financial controlling and budget variance analysis. cost_center_code on royalty_payment is a denormalized representation of cost_center.cost_center_code']
  masterdata_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.profit_center. Business justification: Royalty payments are attributed to profit centers for product P&L reporting and segment financial reporting under IFRS 15 revenue recognition. Finance requires profit-center-level royalty income/expen']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Royalty payments calculated on product-specific net sales. Essential for financial reporting, deal reconciliation, and product-level P&L attribution. Removes denormalized licensed_product_name.']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the patent or intellectual property asset (NCE, NBE, formulation, or process patent) that is the subject of the royalty-bearing license.']
  payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.payment_run. Business justification: Royalty disbursements are executed via treasury payment runs. Linking royalty_payment to payment_run enables reconciliation of IP royalty payments against bank payment batches — required for treasury ']
  primary_royalty_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party granting the license (the entity receiving royalty payments in an outbound scenario or paying in an inbound scenario).']
  royalty_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.royalty_agreement. Business justification: Royalty payments are calculated under a specific finance royalty_agreement governing rates, tiers, and territory. This FK enables royalty accounting reconciliation, audit trail, and rate-tier validati']
  adjustment_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'Any adjustments applied to the calculated royalty amount, such as corrections from prior period reconciliations, audit findings, or contractual true-ups. Positive values increase the payment; negative values reduce it.']
  audit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this royalty payment record has been flagged for formal royalty audit review, typically triggered by discrepancies between reported sales and expected royalty amounts or by contractual audit rights.']
  bank_reference_number varchar [note: 'Bank-issued transaction reference number or wire transfer confirmation number associated with the royalty payment. Used for bank reconciliation and payment verification.']
  calculation_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the royalty calculation period for which net sales or units sold are being reported and royalties computed. Together with start date defines the reporting window per the licensing agreement.']
  calculation_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the royalty calculation period (e.g., quarter or year) for which net sales or units sold are being reported and royalties computed. Aligns with licensing agreement reporting obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this royalty payment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the royalty payment transaction (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY). Determines the denomination of all monetary amounts in this record.']
  deductions_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'Total allowable deductions applied to gross sales to arrive at net sales, as defined in the licensing agreement. Typically includes chargebacks, rebates, returns, freight, and applicable taxes.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for disputing the royalty payment amount, applicable when payment_status is \'disputed\'. Documents the basis for disagreement between licensor and licensee for legal and audit purposes.']
  due_date date [note: 'Contractual deadline by which the royalty payment must be made as specified in the licensing agreement. Used to track overdue payments and trigger escalation workflows.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'Foreign exchange rate applied to convert the transaction currency to the company\'s functional reporting currency at the time of payment. Required for multi-currency licensing agreements and consolidated financial reporting.']
  functional_currency_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'Total payment amount converted to the company\'s functional reporting currency using the applicable exchange rate. Used for consolidated P&L reporting and SOX financial controls.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'SAP General Ledger account code to which this royalty payment is posted for financial reporting purposes. Distinguishes royalty income (inbound) from royalty expense (outbound) in the P&L.']
  gross_sales_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'Total gross sales revenue generated from the licensed product during the calculation period, before any deductions. Used as the starting point for net sales calculation per the licensing agreement definition.']
  licensed_territory varchar [note: 'Geographic territory or market for which the royalty payment applies, as defined in the licensing agreement. May be a country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3), region, or \'GLOBAL\'. Supports territory-level royalty analytics.']
  minimum_royalty_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'Contractual minimum royalty amount guaranteed per period as specified in the licensing agreement, regardless of actual sales performance. Applicable when the calculated royalty falls below this floor.']
  net_sales_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'Net sales revenue for the licensed product during the calculation period, calculated as gross sales minus allowable deductions per the licensing agreement definition. Forms the royalty basis for net-sales-based agreements.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the royalty payment was executed (funds transferred or received). Represents the principal real-world business event date for this transaction.']
  payment_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the royalty payment is inbound (received by the company as licensor) or outbound (paid by the company as licensee). Critical for P&L classification and financial reporting.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The financial instrument or mechanism used to execute the royalty payment (e.g., wire transfer, ACH, check, offset against receivables, or netting against other obligations).']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this royalty payment transaction, typically matching the SAP FI document number or bank remittance reference used for reconciliation and audit trail.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the royalty payment transaction. Tracks progression from draft calculation through approval, payment execution, and final settlement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending|approved|paid|disputed|cancelled — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the royalty payment reconciliation process, indicating whether the payment has been matched against the licensee\'s royalty report and confirmed as accurate. Supports royalty audit workflows.']
  royalty_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'Calculated royalty amount due for the calculation period, derived by applying the royalty rate to the applicable royalty basis (net sales, gross sales, or units sold). This is the principal monetary fact of the transaction.']
  royalty_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the royalty statement or sales report submitted by the licensee that forms the basis for this royalty payment calculation. Links the payment to the supporting documentation.']
  royalty_tier_label varchar [note: 'Label identifying the applicable royalty tier when the licensing agreement specifies tiered rates based on cumulative net sales thresholds (e.g., \'Tier 1: 0-$100M\', \'Tier 2: $100M-$500M\'). Null if the agreement uses a flat rate.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Therapeutic area classification of the licensed drug product (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases). Supports portfolio-level royalty analytics and strategic lifecycle management decisions.']
  total_payment_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'Final net royalty payment amount after applying the royalty rate to the basis, enforcing any minimum royalty floor, and incorporating adjustments. This is the amount actually paid or received.']
  units_sold decimal(20,4) [note: 'Number of units of the licensed product sold during the calculation period. Used as the royalty basis for unit-based royalty agreements. Expressed as DECIMAL to accommodate partial units or volume-based measures.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this royalty payment record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'Amount of withholding tax deducted from the royalty payment at source, as required by applicable tax treaties between the licensor and licensee jurisdictions. Relevant for cross-border licensing arrangements.']
  withholding_tax_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'Applicable withholding tax rate (expressed as a decimal fraction) applied to the royalty payment based on the tax treaty between the relevant jurisdictions. Used to validate withholding tax amount calculations.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of royalty payments made or received under licensing agreements. Captures payment date, payment direction (inbound/outbound), royalty basis (net sales, units sold), royalty rate applied, calculation period, gross sales, net sales, deductions, royalty amount, currency, payment status, and reconciliation status. Supports financial reporting, royalty audits, and compliance with licensing agreement terms.'
}

Table intellectual.fto_analysis {
  fto_analysis_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each Freedom-to-Operate (FTO) analysis or competitive IP watch record. Primary key for this entity.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: FTO analyses are jurisdiction-specific; critical for market entry risk assessment, product launch planning, and patent landscape analysis in target markets.']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: FTO opinions are product-specific for launch risk assessment, design-around strategy, and IP clearance. Essential for R&D and commercial decision-making. Removes denormalized product identifiers.']
  drug_substance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: FTO analyses are conducted for drug substances (NCEs, biologics) before IND filing and manufacturing scale-up. IP teams need to link FTO analyses to specific drug substances to assess freedom-to-opera']
  formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.formulation. Business justification: FTO analyses are conducted for specific formulations before technology transfer and manufacturing scale-up. IP teams need to link FTO analyses to formulations to assess freedom-to-operate for formulat']
  law_firm_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: FTO analysis law firms are business partners in the vendor master. Linking enables legal spend tracking, AP payment processing, and outside counsel management. law_firm_name is a denormalized business']
  masterdata_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: FTO analyses are charged to requesting business units; required for cost allocation, chargeback processing, and legal spend tracking in pharmaceutical IP.']
  masterdata_inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: FTO analyses are conducted for specific active ingredients (INNs) to assess freedom to operate before product launch. Substance-level FTO portfolio management and regulatory clearance tracking require']
  patent_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: FTO analyses in pharma are commonly scoped to entire patent families rather than individual patents, since a competitor product must clear all family members across jurisdictions. fto_analysis has no ']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Freedom-to-Operate analyses are conducted with respect to specific blocking patents. fto_analysis currently has no FK to patent despite being the core IP asset being analyzed. One patent can be the su']
  affected_product_names varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of proprietary product names or INNs that are commercially affected by the FTO analysis findings or the competitor IP watch activity. Supports portfolio-level impact assessment and lifecycle management decisions.']
  analysis_date date [note: 'The date on which the FTO analysis was formally conducted or the opinion was rendered by the responsible attorney or firm. Represents the principal business event date for this record.']
  analysis_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to the FTO analysis or IP watch record, used for tracking across legal, regulatory, and business functions. Format: FTO-YYYY-NNNNN.']
  analysis_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the FTO analysis record: draft (initiated, not yet reviewed), in_review (under attorney or committee review), completed (final opinion issued), superseded (replaced by a newer analysis), or cancelled.']
  analysis_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the Freedom-to-Operate analysis or competitive IP watch record, summarizing the product, technology, or process under assessment.']
  analysis_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the analysis record: FTO assessment (go/no-go commercialization opinion), IP watch (ongoing competitor monitoring), patent landscape (broad mapping), clearance opinion (legal freedom-to-use), or freedom-to-use. Drives workflow and reporting.']
  competitor_filing_date date [note: 'Date on which the competitor filed the patent application, ANDA, biosimilar application, or other regulatory/IP submission being monitored. Critical for calculating response timelines and exclusivity defense windows.']
  competitor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the competitor company whose IP activities are being monitored in this watch record (e.g., generic manufacturer filing an ANDA, biosimilar applicant, or third-party patent filer). Central to competitive intelligence.']
  competitor_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number of the competitor\'s filing being monitored, such as an ANDA application number, biosimilar BLA number, patent publication number, or IPR petition number. Enables direct lookup in regulatory and patent databases.']
  conducting_attorney varchar [note: 'Name of the internal patent attorney or external legal counsel responsible for conducting and signing off on the FTO analysis. Critical for attorney-client privilege tracking and legal accountability.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level assigned to the FTO analysis record, governing access controls and information sharing: restricted (attorney-client privilege, highly sensitive), confidential (internal legal/IP team only), internal (general internal use), or public. Aligns with enterprise data governance policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this FTO analysis record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 requirements.']
  design_around_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the FTO analysis concluded that a design-around strategy is required to avoid infringement of identified blocking patents before commercialization can proceed.']
  dmf_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Drug Master File (DMF) associated with the drug substance or manufacturing process under FTO assessment, if applicable. Links the IP analysis to the CMC regulatory submission package.']
  exclusivity_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the relevant regulatory exclusivity period (orphan drug, data exclusivity, pediatric, NCE, etc.) expires for the assessed product. Determines the earliest date a competitor could receive approval for a generic or biosimilar.']
  exclusivity_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory exclusivity relevant to the FTO analysis or IP watch record: orphan_drug, data_exclusivity, pediatric, new_chemical_entity (NCE), biological, or market exclusivity. Informs the window of protection against generic or biosimilar entry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: orphan_drug|data_exclusivity|pediatric|new_chemical_entity|biological|market — promote to reference product]']
  final_opinion varchar [note: 'The formal legal opinion rendered by the conducting attorney: clear (free to operate without infringement risk), conditional_clear (free to operate subject to design-arounds or licensing), blocked (cannot commercialize without infringing), inconclusive (insufficient information), or pending (opinion not yet issued).']
  licensing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the FTO analysis concluded that a license must be obtained from a third-party patent holder before the assessed product or technology can be commercialized.']
  litigation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the FTO analysis or IP watch record is associated with active or anticipated patent litigation (e.g., Paragraph IV ANDA challenge, IPR petition, patent infringement suit). Triggers escalation to senior legal counsel.']
  patent_expiry_relevance_date date [note: 'The earliest expiry date among the blocking patents identified in the FTO analysis, representing the date after which the primary IP barrier may no longer apply. Critical for lifecycle management and generic/biosimilar entry defense planning.']
  recommended_actions varchar [note: 'Narrative description of recommended next steps arising from the FTO analysis, such as design-around strategies, licensing negotiations, patent challenge (IPR/PGR), freedom-to-use licensing, or further legal investigation. Drives IP strategy execution.']
  requestor_department varchar [note: 'Business department or function that initiated the FTO analysis request (e.g., R&D, Commercial, Regulatory Affairs, Legal). Used for workload tracking and cross-functional reporting.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date by which the FTO analysis or IP watch record must be reviewed and updated to reflect changes in the patent landscape, competitor activities, or product development status. Supports proactive IP portfolio management.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall IP infringement risk level assigned by the conducting attorney following the FTO analysis: high (significant blocking IP identified, commercialization at risk), medium (some risk, mitigation possible), low (minor risk, manageable), or negligible (no material blocking IP found).']
  risk_rationale varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation supporting the assigned risk level, summarizing the key blocking patents, claim analysis findings, and legal reasoning that led to the risk determination. Supports legal review and business decision-making.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the scope of the FTO analysis, including the specific product features, process steps, formulation parameters, or technology elements assessed for potential third-party IP infringement.']
  technology_area varchar [note: 'Broad technology or therapeutic area classification of the subject matter under FTO assessment (e.g., oncology, immunology, rare diseases, formulation, manufacturing process, delivery device). Supports portfolio-level IP analytics.']
  threat_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of the competitive threat posed by the monitored competitor activity: critical (immediate commercial impact expected), high (significant risk to market exclusivity), medium (moderate risk requiring active response), low (limited near-term impact), or monitoring (no immediate action required).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this FTO analysis record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail, version control, and change management per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the FTO analysis document (e.g., 1.0, 2.1), tracking revisions and updates to the analysis over time. Supports document control and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  watch_type varchar [note: 'For competitive IP watch records, classifies the type of competitor activity being monitored: patent_filing (new patent applications), anda_submission (Abbreviated New Drug Application), biosimilar_application (biosimilar filing), patent_challenge (IPR/PGR/opposition), regulatory_filing, or trademark. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: patent_filing|anda_submission|biosimilar_application|patent_challenge|regulatory_filing|trademark — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Freedom-to-Operate (FTO) analysis and competitive IP watch records. Covers FTO assessments determining whether a product, process, or technology can be commercialized without infringing third-party IP rights, plus ongoing monitoring of competitor IP activities (patent filings, ANDA/505(b)(2) submissions, biosimilar applications, patent challenges). Captures analysis title, scope, requestor, conducting attorney or firm, analysis date, product or technology assessed, jurisdictions covered, identified blocking patents, risk level, recommended actions, final opinion, watch type, competitor identity, filing dates, reference numbers, technology area, affected products, and threat assessment. Supports go/no-go decisions for product development, commercial launch, and proactive IP defense strategy.'
}

Table intellectual.patent_litigation {
  patent_litigation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each patent litigation case record in the intellectual property portfolio management system.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Patent litigation cases require dedicated legal budgets for outside counsel, expert witnesses, and settlement reserves. Finance links litigation cases to budget records to track actual vs. planned spe']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Litigation occurs in specific jurisdictions; required for legal strategy, financial reserves estimation, and paragraph IV defense planning in pharmaceutical patent disputes.']
  defendant_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Patent litigation is filed by or against specific internal legal entities. Legal entity-level litigation exposure reporting and financial provisioning (IAS 37) require knowing which legal entity is th']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: ANDA paragraph IV patent litigation is filed against specific drug products (NDA holders). Legal teams track litigation cases by drug product for revenue-at-risk analysis, 30-month stay management, an']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.invoice. Business justification: Patent litigation generates outside counsel invoices that must be linked to the specific litigation case for legal spend tracking, budget variance analysis, and SOX-compliant legal cost reporting. Pha']
  masterdata_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: Litigation costs are allocated to business units; essential for legal spend tracking, forecasting, and financial impact assessment of patent disputes.']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Patent litigation targets specific products (Paragraph IV, biosimilar challenges). Essential for commercial risk assessment, legal spend tracking, revenue-at-risk forecasting. Removes denormalized pro']
  orange_book_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.orange_book_listing. Business justification: Patent litigation in pharmaceuticals is often triggered by Paragraph IV certifications challenging Orange Book-listed patents. Linking patent_litigation to orange_book_listing tracks which Orange Book']
  outside_counsel_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Outside counsel firms managing patent litigation are business partners in the vendor master. Linking enables legal spend management, matter management integration, and AP payment processing. outside_c']
  patent_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: Patent litigation in pharma frequently involves entire patent families — a Paragraph IV ANDA challenge or IPR petition may target all family members across jurisdictions simultaneously. patent_litigat']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: A patent litigation case is fundamentally about asserting or defending a specific patent. patent_litigation currently has asserted_patent_numbers as a denormalized STRING, but a proper FK to the prima']
  actual_legal_spend_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative actual legal fees and costs incurred to date for this litigation matter, expressed in US dollars. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI/CO module legal spend tracking. Used for legal budget management and P&L reporting.']
  appeal_court varchar [note: 'Name of the appellate court or tribunal hearing the appeal (e.g., U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit, U.S. Supreme Court, EPO Board of Appeal). Null if no appeal has been filed.']
  appeal_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an appeal has been filed following the trial court or PTAB judgment. True if an appeal is pending or has been decided; False if no appeal was filed.']
  asserted_patent_numbers varchar [note: 'Semicolon-delimited list of patent numbers (e.g., US9,123,456; US10,234,567) asserted or challenged in this litigation. These are the Orange Book-listed or Blue Book-listed patents at issue. Links to the patent portfolio for expiry and claim analysis.']
  case_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal legal team notes, strategic observations, and case-specific commentary. May include attorney work product summaries, risk assessments, and strategic recommendations. Classified as confidential due to potential attorney-client privilege content.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Official court-assigned or tribunal-assigned docket number uniquely identifying the litigation case (e.g., civil action number, IPR petition number, PGR petition number). Serves as the primary external business identifier for cross-referencing with legal counsel and court records.']
  case_priority varchar [note: 'Internal priority classification assigned to the litigation case based on revenue at risk, strategic importance, and likelihood of adverse outcome. Critical cases receive executive-level oversight; high cases are monitored by senior IP counsel; medium and low cases follow standard legal management processes.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the litigation case by legal mechanism. Paragraph IV ANDA challenges arise under Hatch-Waxman; IPR and PGR are USPTO trial proceedings; patent infringement suits are district court actions; declaratory judgment actions are filed by generic/biosimilar challengers; biosimilar patent dance refers to BPCIA exchange proceedings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: paragraph_iv_anda|inter_partes_review|post_grant_review|patent_infringement|declaratory_judgment|biosimilar_patent_dance — promote to reference product]']
  court_or_tribunal varchar [note: 'Name of the judicial or administrative body presiding over the case (e.g., U.S. District Court for the District of Delaware, USPTO Patent Trial and Appeal Board (PTAB), U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit, European Patent Office Opposition Division).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this patent litigation record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking.']
  estimated_liability_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Management\'s best estimate of the potential financial exposure or liability associated with this litigation case, expressed in US dollars. Used for legal reserve provisioning, financial disclosure under ASC 450 (contingencies), and SOX compliance. Includes potential damages, lost profits, and legal costs.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Date on which the complaint, petition, or challenge was formally filed with the court or tribunal. For Paragraph IV ANDA challenges, this is the date the ANDA filer notified the NDA holder. Critical for calculating the 30-month stay period and litigation timelines.']
  first_filer_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ANDA challenger is the first filer entitled to 180-day generic exclusivity under the Hatch-Waxman Act. True if the defendant holds first-filer status; False otherwise. Relevant for assessing the competitive landscape and timing of generic entry.']
  institution_decision_date date [note: 'Date on which the PTAB issued its decision on whether to institute the IPR or PGR trial. A decision to institute means the petition met the threshold for review. Null for non-PTAB proceedings.']
  ipr_petition_number varchar [note: 'USPTO-assigned petition number for Inter Partes Review proceedings before the Patent Trial and Appeal Board (PTAB). Format follows USPTO convention (e.g., IPR2023-01234). Null for non-IPR case types.']
  judgment_date date [note: 'Date on which the court or tribunal issued its final judgment, final written decision (for PTAB proceedings), or order. Null if the case has not yet reached judgment.']
  lead_outside_counsel varchar [note: 'Full name of the lead attorney at the outside counsel firm responsible for managing this litigation matter. Used for escalation, communication, and legal budget accountability.']
  legal_budget_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved annual or total budget allocated for legal fees and costs associated with this litigation matter, expressed in US dollars. Used for variance analysis against actual legal spend and budget reforecasting.']
  litigation_status varchar [note: 'Current procedural status of the litigation case reflecting its lifecycle stage. Active cases are ongoing; settled cases have reached agreement; dismissed cases were terminated without judgment; judgment_entered reflects a court ruling; appealed cases are under appellate review; stayed cases are paused pending other proceedings; closed cases are fully resolved. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|settled|dismissed|judgment_entered|appealed|stayed|closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  next_key_milestone varchar [note: 'Description of the next significant procedural or substantive milestone in the litigation (e.g., claim construction hearing, expert discovery deadline, summary judgment motion, PTAB oral argument, appellate briefing deadline). Used for litigation calendar management.']
  next_milestone_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next key procedural or substantive milestone in the litigation. Used for proactive legal calendar management and escalation triggers.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome or resolution of the litigation case. Patent upheld means the challenged patent survived; patent invalidated means the patent was found invalid or unenforceable; infringement found or non-infringement found reflects district court rulings; settled indicates out-of-court resolution; dismissed indicates termination without merits ruling; consent judgment reflects agreed court order. Null for active cases. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: patent_upheld|patent_invalidated|infringement_found|non_infringement_found|settled|dismissed|consent_judgment — promote to reference product]']
  paragraph_iv_notice_date date [note: 'Date on which the company received the formal Paragraph IV certification notice from the ANDA filer, as required under the Hatch-Waxman Act. Triggers the 45-day window to file suit and the 30-month stay. Null for non-Paragraph IV case types.']
  plaintiff_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the party initiating the litigation (typically the innovator/NDA holder asserting patent rights, or the generic/biosimilar applicant filing a declaratory judgment action). May be the company itself or a subsidiary.']
  revenue_at_risk_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated annual revenue at risk if the litigation outcome is unfavorable and generic or biosimilar entry occurs earlier than the patent expiry date. Expressed in US dollars. Critical input for lifecycle management decisions and commercial strategy.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which a settlement agreement was executed between the parties, if the case was resolved through settlement rather than judgment. Null for cases resolved by judgment or still active.']
  settlement_entry_date date [note: 'Agreed date on which the generic or biosimilar entrant is permitted to launch its product under the terms of the settlement agreement. Critical for lifecycle management and revenue forecasting. Null for non-settlement outcomes.']
  suit_filed_date date [note: 'Date on which the NDA holder filed the patent infringement suit in response to the Paragraph IV notice, within the 45-day statutory window. Null for case types where the company is the defendant or for non-Hatch-Waxman proceedings.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Therapeutic area or disease category associated with the drug product at the center of the litigation (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Cardiovascular, Neuroscience). Enables portfolio-level analysis of litigation exposure by therapeutic area.']
  thirty_month_stay_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the automatic 30-month stay of FDA approval of the ANDA expires under the Hatch-Waxman Act. Calculated from the date the NDA holder received the Paragraph IV certification notice. Null for non-Hatch-Waxman case types. Critical for generic entry defense strategy.']
  trial_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date of the trial or oral hearing before the court or tribunal. For PTAB proceedings, this is the oral argument date. Null if trial has not been scheduled or the case settled prior to trial.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this patent litigation record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking for compliance purposes.']

  Note: 'Tracks active and historical patent litigation cases including Paragraph IV ANDA challenges, inter partes review (IPR) petitions, post-grant review (PGR), patent infringement suits, and declaratory judgment actions. Captures case number, court or tribunal, case type, filing date, parties (plaintiff, defendant), asserted patents, accused products, litigation status, key milestones, outside counsel, estimated liability, and outcome. Critical for generic/biosimilar entry defense strategy and legal budget management.'
}

Table intellectual.patent_prosecution {
  patent_prosecution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each patent prosecution record in the portfolio management system. Primary key for the patent_prosecution data product.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Patent prosecution is jurisdiction-specific; each prosecution record corresponds to a national/regional patent office. Prosecution cost tracking by jurisdiction and regulatory authority mapping requir']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Patent prosecution costs (annuity fees, filing fees, attorney costs) are tracked against SAP internal orders for IP cost controlling and capitalization decisions. Pharma IP finance teams require this ']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Patent prosecution is conducted on behalf of a specific legal entity (the patent applicant/owner). Legal entity-level IP asset reporting and transfer pricing of IP rights require this link. No existin']
  masterdata_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: Patent prosecution costs (annuity fees, attorney fees, filing fees) are tracked against cost centers for IP budget management and R&D cost capitalization under IAS 38. IP controllers require cost-cent']
  patent_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: Patent prosecution records track individual patent applications. Patent families group related applications filed across multiple jurisdictions that share a common priority. Each prosecution record sh']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Patent prosecution tracks the application process from filing through grant. When a patent is granted, the prosecution record should link to the resulting patent master record. The grant_number in pat']
  paying_agent_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Annuity paying agents are business partners in the vendor master; linking enables automated annuity payment processing and vendor spend analytics. paying_agent_name is a denormalized business_partner.']
  abandonment_date date [note: 'Date the application was formally abandoned, either expressly by the applicant or by operation of law (e.g., failure to respond to office action within statutory period or failure to pay issue fee). Null unless prosecution_stage = \'abandoned\'.']
  annuity_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the annuity or maintenance fee for the current fee year must be paid to maintain the patent in force. Failure to pay by this date (or within the grace period) results in patent lapse. Critical docketing field.']
  annuity_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base annuity or maintenance fee amount due for the current fee year, in the currency specified by annuity_fee_currency. Used for IP budget forecasting and cost tracking across the patent portfolio.']
  annuity_fee_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the annuity fee amount (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, GBP, CNY). Required for multi-currency portfolio cost consolidation and budget reporting.']
  annuity_fee_year varchar [note: 'The renewal/annuity year number for which the most recent maintenance fee obligation applies (e.g., year 3, year 7, year 12). Annuity years are counted from the filing date in most jurisdictions. Used for fee schedule management and budget forecasting.']
  annuity_grace_period_end_date date [note: 'Last date on which the annuity fee can be paid with a surcharge before the patent lapses. Typically 6 months after annuity_due_date in most jurisdictions. After this date, the patent is irrevocably lapsed unless restoration procedures are available.']
  annuity_payment_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the annuity or maintenance fee was paid to the patent office or paying agent. Used to confirm timely payment and to calculate any applicable surcharges.']
  annuity_payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status of the annuity/maintenance fee obligation for the current fee year. Pending = not yet due or awaiting payment; Paid = confirmed payment received; Overdue = past due date, within grace period; Lapsed = grace period expired without payment; Waived = fee waived per applicable rules.']
  annuity_surcharge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Late payment surcharge amount applied when the annuity fee is paid after the due date but within the grace period. Tracked separately from the base fee for cost analysis and to identify patterns of late payment.']
  application_number varchar [note: 'Official application number assigned by the patent office upon filing (e.g., US16/123456, EP21700001.2, PCT/US2023/012345). Serves as the primary external identifier for tracking prosecution status across jurisdictions.']
  application_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the patent application by filing type. Utility = standard non-provisional; Provisional = 12-month placeholder; PCT = Patent Cooperation Treaty international; Continuation/Divisional/CIP = derived from parent application. Determines prosecution rules and term calculations. CIP = Continuation-In-Part.']
  claim_count varchar [note: 'Total number of claims in the current version of the patent application or granted patent, including independent and dependent claims. Affects official filing fees in many jurisdictions and indicates the breadth of IP protection sought.']
  cpc_classification varchar [note: 'Cooperative Patent Classification (CPC) code assigned by USPTO or EPO (e.g., A61K 31/4745). More granular than IPC; used for patent analytics, freedom-to-operate (FTO) searches, and competitive landscape mapping.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this patent prosecution record was first created in the IP docketing system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and SOX compliance.']
  examiner_art_unit varchar [note: 'Patent office art unit or examination division to which the application is assigned (e.g., USPTO Art Unit 1627 for pharmaceutical compositions). Informs prosecution strategy based on art unit allowance rates and examiner tendencies.']
  examiner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the patent examiner assigned to the application at the patent office. Used for prosecution strategy planning, examiner history analysis, and interview request management.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calculated date on which the patent expires, typically 20 years from the filing date subject to any patent term extensions (PTE), supplementary protection certificates (SPC), or pediatric exclusivity extensions. Critical for lifecycle management, generic entry forecasting, and LOE (loss of exclusivity) planning.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Date the patent application was officially filed with the patent office. Establishes the priority date for prior art purposes and is the anchor for calculating the 20-year patent term under most jurisdictions. Critical for patent term extension (PTE) and supplementary protection certificate (SPC) calculations.']
  grant_date date [note: 'Date the patent was officially granted by the patent office. Marks the start of enforceable patent rights and triggers the annuity/maintenance fee schedule in most jurisdictions. Null until prosecution_stage = \'granted\'.']
  independent_claim_count varchar [note: 'Number of independent claims in the application or granted patent. Independent claims define the broadest scope of protection and are the primary focus of validity and infringement analyses.']
  invention_type varchar [note: 'Pharmaceutical classification of the invention being patented. NCE (New Chemical Entity) = novel small molecule; NBE (New Biological Entity) = biologic/biosimilar; formulation = drug product composition; process = manufacturing/synthesis method; method_of_use = therapeutic indication; combination = multi-component therapy. Critical for lifecycle management and generic/biosimilar entry defense strategy.']
  ipc_classification varchar [note: 'International Patent Classification (IPC) code assigned to the application (e.g., A61K 31/00 for pharmaceutical preparations). Used for patent landscape analysis, prior art searches, and competitive intelligence. Multiple codes may be stored as a comma-separated string.']
  last_office_action_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent office action received from the patent office. Used to calculate the statutory response deadline and to trigger docketing alerts for prosecution attorneys.']
  law_firm_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external patent law firm engaged for prosecution of this application, if applicable. Used for vendor management, cost tracking, and conflict-of-interest monitoring across the IP portfolio.']
  office_action_count varchar [note: 'Total number of office actions (rejections, objections, or requirements) received from the patent office during prosecution of this application. Indicates prosecution complexity and is used for cost estimation and strategy assessment.']
  office_action_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the most recent office action received. Restriction = requirement to elect claims; Non-final = first substantive rejection; Final = final rejection after response; Advisory = after final response; Allowance/Notice of Allowance = claims approved for grant.']
  parent_application_number varchar [note: 'Application number of the parent patent application from which this application claims priority (applicable for continuation, divisional, or CIP applications). Used to reconstruct the patent family hierarchy and assess claim scope relative to the parent.']
  patent_office_code varchar [note: 'Two- or three-letter code identifying the national or regional patent office handling the application (e.g., US = USPTO, EP = EPO, JP = JPO/PMDA, CN = CNIPA, WO = WIPO PCT). Drives jurisdiction-specific deadline rules and annuity schedules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: US|EP|JP|CN|WO|GB|DE|FR|CA|AU|IN|KR|BR|MX|RU — promote to reference product]']
  payment_confirmation_reference varchar [note: 'Official confirmation or receipt reference number issued by the patent office or paying agent upon successful annuity fee payment. Serves as proof of payment for audit and compliance purposes.']
  pct_application_number varchar [note: 'PCT international application number (e.g., PCT/US2023/012345) if this national/regional application entered the national phase from a PCT filing. Links the national phase application to the international PCT prosecution record.']
  priority_date date [note: 'Earliest claimed priority date for the application, typically the filing date of the first-filed provisional or parent application in the priority chain. Governs prior art cutoff and is used in freedom-to-operate (FTO) analyses and patent validity assessments.']
  prosecution_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the patent application in the prosecution workflow lifecycle. Drives docketing actions, deadline calculations, and portfolio status reporting. Values: filed, examination, office_action_received, response_submitted, allowance, granted, abandoned. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: filed|examination|office_action_received|response_submitted|allowance|granted|abandoned — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  prosecution_strategy varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the prosecution strategy adopted for this application, including claim amendment approach, interview strategy, appeal considerations, and alignment with the broader IP lifecycle management plan. Attorney-client privileged content.']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date the patent application was published by the patent office, typically 18 months after the earliest priority date. Marks the point at which the application enters the public domain and becomes citable prior art.']
  publication_number varchar [note: 'Official publication number assigned by the patent office upon publication of the application (e.g., US20230123456A1, WO2023/012345A1). Distinct from the application number; used for public citation and prior art searches.']
  response_deadline date [note: 'Statutory or extended deadline by which a response to the most recent office action must be filed to avoid abandonment. Calculated from last_office_action_date per jurisdiction-specific rules. Critical docketing field — failure to meet this deadline results in application abandonment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this patent prosecution record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization with source docketing systems (e.g., Veeva Vault RIM, Anaqua), and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Tracks patent application prosecution workflow from filing through grant or abandonment, including all annuity and maintenance fee payment obligations across all jurisdictions. Captures application number, prosecution stage, patent office, examiner, office actions received, response deadlines, prosecution attorney, prosecution strategy, and complete annuity/maintenance fee history (fee year, due dates, amounts, currencies, payment status, paying agents, confirmation receipts, grace periods, surcharge amounts). Failure to pay annuities results in patent lapse. Supports docketing, deadline management, fee compliance, cost tracking, portfolio maintenance operations, and annuity budget forecasting.'
}

Table intellectual.patent_term_extension {
  patent_term_extension_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each patent term extension or supplementary protection certificate record in the intellectual property portfolio. Primary key for this entity.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: PTEs are jurisdiction-specific; critical for patent expiry forecasting, lifecycle management, and market-specific exclusivity planning in pharmaceutical commercialization.']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: PTEs granted for specific approved drug products (formulation-level precision beyond existing medicinal_product_id). Essential for LOE forecasting and regulatory strategy. Removes denormalized inn.']
  drug_substance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: Patent term extensions are granted for the active ingredient (drug substance) that was subject to regulatory review. FDA PTE and EU SPC applications reference the specific drug substance. Regulatory a']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: PTE/SPC applications are filed by the Marketing Authorization Holder (a specific legal entity). Legal entity-level IP asset reporting and regulatory submission tracking require this link. No existing ']
  masterdata_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: PTE/SPC application costs and associated legal fees are tracked against cost centers for IP budget management and R&D capitalization. IP finance teams require cost-center-level PTE spend reporting. No']
  masterdata_inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: PTEs and SPCs are granted for specific active substances (INNs). Substance-level exclusivity management and LOE planning require INN linkage. atc_code on patent_term_extension is a classification, not']
  medicinal_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the drug product (Drug Product / Finished Dosage Form) for which the marketing authorization was obtained and which forms the basis of the extension application. Links the extension to the commercial product in the master data.']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying base patent record in the IP portfolio for which this term extension or SPC is being sought. Links the extension to the specific patent being extended.']
  adjusted_expiry_date date [note: 'The new patent expiry date after application of the granted term extension or SPC. Calculated as base_patent_expiry_date plus granted_extension_days. This is the Loss of Exclusivity (LOE) date for the product in this jurisdiction and is the primary output used in lifecycle management and generic/biosimilar entry defense strategies.']
  applicant_delay_days varchar [note: 'Number of days deducted from the regulatory review period due to delays attributable to the applicant (e.g., failure to respond to FDA requests within the prescribed period). Reduces the calculated extension duration under 35 U.S.C. § 156(c)(1). Tracked for audit and dispute resolution purposes.']
  application_number varchar [note: 'The official application number assigned by the granting authority (e.g., USPTO, EPO, UKIPO, JPO) when the patent term extension or SPC application was filed. Serves as the primary external business identifier for tracking the application through the regulatory process.']
  approval_phase_start_date date [note: 'For US PTE calculations: the date on which the regulatory approval phase began, typically the date of NDA/BLA submission to the FDA. Used to calculate the approval phase component of the regulatory review period under 35 U.S.C. § 156(g)(1)(B).']
  atc_code varchar [note: 'The WHO Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) classification code assigned to the active substance covered by the extension. Provides standardized classification for cross-portfolio analytics, competitive intelligence, and regulatory reporting.']
  base_patent_expiry_date date [note: 'The original expiry date of the underlying base patent before any term extension or adjustment is applied. Used as the baseline for calculating the adjusted expiry date and the duration of the extension period.']
  base_patent_number varchar [note: 'The official patent number of the underlying patent for which the term extension or SPC is sought (e.g., US10,123,456 or EP1234567). For SPC purposes, this is the \'basic patent\' as defined under Article 1(c) of EU Regulation 469/2009. Retained as a denormalized field for direct reference without joining to the patent table.']
  calculated_extension_days varchar [note: 'The number of days of patent term extension calculated based on the regulatory review period formula. For US PTE: one-half of the testing phase plus the approval phase, capped at 5 years with remaining patent life not exceeding 14 years post-approval. For EU SPC: period between IND filing and MA grant minus 5 years, capped at 5 years.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this patent term extension record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking in compliance with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.']
  data_exclusivity_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which regulatory data exclusivity (also known as data protection) expires for the product in this jurisdiction. In the EU, this is 8+2+1 years from first authorization; in the US, 5 years for NCEs or 12 years for biologics. Tracked alongside PTE/SPC to determine the complete exclusivity landscape.']
  decision_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the patent term extension or SPC application. Tracks the application from initial drafting through filing, examination, and final decision by the granting authority.
