Critical for LOE (Loss of Exclusivity) date calculation and portfolio management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|filed|under_review|granted|refused|withdrawn|appealed|lapsed|invalidated — promote to reference product]']
  external_counsel_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or name of the external patent law firm or legal counsel managing this patent term extension or SPC application. Used for coordination of legal strategy, correspondence tracking, and matter management. Marked confidential as it reflects privileged legal relationships.']
  filing_date date [note: 'The date on which the patent term extension or SPC application was formally filed with the granting authority. For US PTE, must be filed within 60 days of first marketing approval. For EU SPC, must be filed within 6 months of marketing authorization grant. Critical for determining application validity.']
  grant_date date [note: 'The date on which the granting authority officially granted the patent term extension or SPC. Null if the application has not yet been decided or was refused/withdrawn. Used to confirm the effective start of the extended protection period.']
  granted_extension_days varchar [note: 'The actual number of days of patent term extension officially granted by the granting authority. May differ from the calculated extension if the authority applies different determinations of the regulatory review period or applicant delay deductions. Null until the extension is formally granted.']
  granting_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the national or regional intellectual property office responsible for examining and granting the patent term extension or SPC (e.g., USPTO, EPO, UKIPO, JPO, CIPO, IP Australia). Determines procedural rules and timelines.']
  loe_date date [note: 'The effective Loss of Exclusivity (LOE) date for the product in this jurisdiction, accounting for all applicable exclusivity mechanisms (PTE/SPC, pediatric extension, orphan drug exclusivity, data exclusivity). This is the single most critical date for commercial planning, generic/biosimilar entry defense, and lifecycle management strategy. Represents the date after which generic or biosimilar competition may legally enter the market.']
  marketing_authorization_date date [note: 'The date on which the first marketing authorization for the product was granted in the relevant jurisdiction. This date is critical for calculating the regulatory review period and the permissible extension duration under Hatch-Waxman PTE and EU SPC frameworks.']
  marketing_authorization_number varchar [note: 'The official marketing authorization number (NDA number, BLA number, EU MA number, or national equivalent) that forms the regulatory basis for the extension application. For EU SPC, this is the first authorization to place the product on the market in the Community per Article 3(b) of Regulation 469/2009.']
  mechanism_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the patent term extension mechanism. PTE = US Hatch-Waxman Patent Term Extension under 35 U.S.C. § 156; SPC = EU/UK Supplementary Protection Certificate under EC Regulation 469/2009; national_equivalent = country-specific analogous mechanisms (e.g., Japan, Canada, Australia); pediatric_extension = additional 6-month pediatric exclusivity; data_exclusivity = regulatory data protection period. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PTE|SPC|national_equivalent|pediatric_extension|data_exclusivity|regulatory_data_protection — promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, legal strategy notes, authority correspondence summaries, or other relevant information pertaining to this patent term extension or SPC record that is not captured in structured fields. Used by IP counsel and regulatory affairs teams.']
  opposition_date date [note: 'The date on which a third-party opposition, invalidity action, or equivalent challenge was filed against this patent term extension or SPC. Null if no opposition has been filed. Used to track litigation timelines and coordinate with legal counsel.']
  opposition_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a third party (e.g., generic manufacturer, biosimilar applicant) has filed an opposition, invalidity action, or equivalent challenge against this patent term extension or SPC. True triggers escalation to the legal team for litigation management and LOE date reassessment.']
  orphan_drug_exclusivity boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product covered by this extension also benefits from orphan drug exclusivity (7 years in the US under the Orphan Drug Act, 10 years in the EU under Regulation (EC) No 141/2000). Used in conjunction with PTE/SPC to determine the effective exclusivity cliff date.']
  pediatric_extension_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this patent term extension or SPC is eligible for an additional 6-month pediatric extension under the US Best Pharmaceuticals for Children Act (BPCA) or EU Paediatric Regulation (EC) No 1901/2006. True if a Written Request (US) or Paediatric Investigation Plan (EU) has been completed and accepted.']
  pediatric_extension_granted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the additional 6-month pediatric extension has been formally granted on top of the base PTE or SPC. Distinct from eligibility — this flag confirms the authority has approved the pediatric supplement. When True, the adjusted_expiry_date should reflect the additional 6 months.']
  regulatory_review_period_days varchar [note: 'The total number of days of the regulatory review period as determined by the granting authority. For US PTE, this is the period from IND filing to NDA/BLA approval, reduced by any applicant-caused delays. Directly determines the maximum permissible extension duration under 35 U.S.C. § 156.']
  spc_product_definition varchar [note: 'For EU/UK SPC applications only: the definition of the \'product\' as per Article 1(b) of EU Regulation 469/2009, specifying the active ingredient or combination of active ingredients of a medicinal product. This definition determines the scope of SPC protection and is critical for freedom-to-operate analyses and generic entry defense.']
  testing_phase_start_date date [note: 'For US PTE calculations: the date on which the regulatory review testing phase began, typically the date of IND (Investigational New Drug) application submission. Used to calculate the testing phase component of the regulatory review period under 35 U.S.C. § 156(g)(1).']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'The therapeutic area or disease indication for which the drug product covered by the extension is approved (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Cardiovascular, Neurology). Used for portfolio analytics, lifecycle management planning, and commercial strategy alignment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this patent term extension record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking for IP portfolio management and regulatory compliance.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'The document identifier in Veeva Vault RIM (Regulatory Information Management) where the official extension application documents, correspondence with the granting authority, and supporting regulatory dossier are stored. Enables direct navigation from this record to the source regulatory documents.']

  Note: 'Records all patent term extension and supplementary protection mechanisms across global jurisdictions that compensate for regulatory review time lost during drug approval. Covers US Hatch-Waxman Patent Term Extension (PTE), EU/UK Supplementary Protection Certificates (SPC), Japanese patent term extension, and equivalent national mechanisms. Captures application number, mechanism type (PTE, SPC, national equivalent), filing date, patent reference, product reference, marketing authorization reference, regulatory review period, calculated/granted extension duration, adjusted expiry date, granting authority, decision status, SPC-specific fields (basic patent, product definition per Article 1(b)), and pediatric extension eligibility. Single source of truth for all patent term adjustments — critical for accurate LOE date calculation across all markets.'
}

Table intellectual.orange_book_listing {
  orange_book_listing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each Orange Book listing record in the intellectual property portfolio system. Primary key for this data product.']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Orange Book listings reference specific drug products (NDC-level). Essential for generic competition monitoring, Paragraph IV strategy, and patent-product linkage accuracy. Removes denormalized identi']
  exclusivity_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.exclusivity_period. Business justification: FDA Orange Book listings include both patent and exclusivity information. The exclusivity_code and exclusivity_expiry_date (STRING, DATE) should be replaced with a FK to exclusivity_period.exclusivity']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Orange Book listings are held by specific legal entities (NDA holders). Legal entity-level IP portfolio reporting and paragraph IV litigation tracking require this link. applicant_name is a denormaliz']
  masterdata_atc_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: Orange Book listings are classified by ATC for therapeutic area portfolio management and competitive intelligence. atc_code on orange_book_listing is a denormalized representation of atc_classificatio']
  masterdata_inn_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: Orange Book listings are for specific drug substances (INNs). Substance-level patent linkage analysis and generic entry monitoring (paragraph IV challenges) require INN linkage. A pharma IP expert exp']
  masterdata_ndc_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.ndc_code. Business justification: Orange Book listings reference specific NDC codes; required for regulatory compliance, paragraph IV defense, and product-patent linkage tracking.']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: FDA Orange Book listings reference specific patents that protect approved drug products. The patent_number (STRING) should be replaced with a FK to patent.patent_id to link the regulatory listing to t']
  anda_first_filer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a first-filer ANDA applicant has been identified for this patent listing, triggering the 180-day generic exclusivity period under Hatch-Waxman. True if a first filer has been designated; False otherwise. Affects the timing of subsequent generic approvals.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this Orange Book listing record was first created in the data product. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  delisting_date date [note: 'Date on which the patent or exclusivity was removed from the FDA Orange Book, either voluntarily by the NDA holder or by FDA order. Null if the listing remains active. Relevant for tracking generic entry eligibility changes.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'Physical form of the finished dosage form (FDF) as listed in the Orange Book (e.g., tablet, capsule, injection, solution, patch). Determines therapeutic equivalence evaluation and generic substitutability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|injection|solution|patch|cream|suspension|powder|gel|suppository — promote to reference product]']
  first_generic_eligibility_date date [note: 'Earliest date on which a generic drug product may receive final FDA approval, accounting for both patent expiry and exclusivity expiry dates. Derived from the later of patent expiry, exclusivity expiry, and 30-month stay expiry. Critical for lifecycle management and loss-of-exclusivity (LOE) planning.']
  listing_date date [note: 'Date on which the patent or exclusivity was first listed in the FDA Orange Book by the NDA holder. Establishes the effective date of the listing for Paragraph IV certification and 30-month stay calculations.']
  listing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Orange Book listing record. \'Active\' indicates the patent or exclusivity is currently listed and enforceable. \'Delisted\' indicates the NDA holder has removed the listing. \'Withdrawn\' indicates FDA-initiated removal. \'Pending\' indicates submission under review. \'Corrected\' indicates an amendment was filed.']
  litigation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of patent infringement litigation associated with this Orange Book listing, typically arising from a Paragraph IV certification. Tracks whether litigation is active, settled, or resolved by court judgment. Informs LOE risk assessment and legal strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|active|settled|judgment_for_patentee|judgment_for_challenger|appeal_pending|dismissed — promote to reference product]']
  market varchar [note: 'Regulatory market or jurisdiction for which this listing record applies. \'US\' for FDA Orange Book; \'EU\' for EMA Article 10 reference product listing; \'UK\' for MHRA; \'JP\' for PMDA; \'CN\' for NMPA. Enables multi-market IP portfolio management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: US|EU|UK|JP|CN|CA|AU|BR|IN|KR — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  nda_number varchar [note: 'FDA-assigned application number (NDA, ANDA, or BLA) under which the drug product is approved and listed in the Orange Book. Governs which patents and exclusivities are associated with the approved product.']
  orphan_drug_designation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the listed drug product holds an FDA Orphan Drug Designation (ODD), conferring 7-year market exclusivity upon approval. True if ODD is active; False otherwise. Relevant for rare disease portfolio strategy and generic entry defense.']
  paragraph_iv_date date [note: 'Date on which the first Paragraph IV certification was received by the NDA holder for this listed patent. Initiates the 45-day window to file patent infringement suit and triggers the 30-month stay clock if suit is filed.']
  paragraph_iv_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Paragraph IV certification has been filed by an ANDA applicant challenging this listed patent as invalid, unenforceable, or not infringed. True triggers the 45-day notice period and potential 30-month stay of ANDA approval.']
  patent_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the listed patent expires as published in the FDA Orange Book. This date directly governs the earliest date a generic applicant may receive final approval absent a Paragraph IV challenge or court ruling.']
  patent_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the listed patent as covering the drug substance (active ingredient), drug product (formulation/composition), or method of use. Determines the nature of Paragraph IV challenges and carve-out labeling strategies.']
  patent_use_code varchar [note: 'FDA-assigned use code (e.g., U-001) that identifies the specific approved method of use covered by the listed patent. Required for method-of-use patents; determines the scope of Paragraph IV certifications and carve-out labeling eligibility.']
  pediatric_exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a 6-month pediatric exclusivity extension has been granted under the Best Pharmaceuticals for Children Act (BPCA), attaching to all existing patents and exclusivities for the listed product. True if pediatric exclusivity is in effect.']
  reference_listed_drug_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this NDA product is designated as the Reference Listed Drug (RLD) against which ANDA applicants must demonstrate bioequivalence. True if the product is the RLD; False if it is a non-RLD listed product.']
  rems_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the listed drug product is subject to an FDA-approved Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS). REMS requirements may affect generic applicant ability to demonstrate shared REMS compliance, impacting generic entry timelines.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'Route by which the drug product is administered (e.g., oral, intravenous, topical, subcutaneous). Used in therapeutic equivalence evaluations and generic entry assessments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|intravenous|subcutaneous|intramuscular|topical|inhalation|transdermal|ophthalmic — promote to reference product]']
  spc_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC) expires in the relevant EU/UK jurisdiction. Represents the effective end of patent-based market protection for the listed product in that market.']
  spc_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC) granted in EU/UK jurisdictions to extend patent protection beyond the standard 20-year term for medicinal products. Applicable for EU and UK market listings; null for US Orange Book entries.']
  strength varchar [note: 'Labeled strength of the drug product as listed in the Orange Book (e.g., \'10 mg\', \'500 mg/5 mL\'). Strength is a key differentiator for therapeutic equivalence and generic substitution eligibility.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the NDA holder submitted the patent or exclusivity information to the FDA for Orange Book listing. Must occur within 30 days of NDA approval or patent grant per 21 CFR Part 314.53(d).']
  therapeutic_equivalence_code varchar [note: 'FDA therapeutic equivalence evaluation code assigned to the drug product in the Orange Book (e.g., AB, AA, BC, BX). \'A\' codes indicate therapeutically equivalent to the RLD; \'B\' codes indicate not therapeutically equivalent. Drives pharmacy substitution decisions and formulary management.']
  thirty_month_stay_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the 30-month stay of ANDA approval expires following a timely-filed patent infringement suit in response to a Paragraph IV certification. After this date, FDA may grant final ANDA approval regardless of ongoing litigation outcome.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this Orange Book listing record was last modified in the data product. Supports change tracking, audit compliance under 21 CFR Part 11, and Silver Layer data freshness monitoring.']
  veeva_vault_doc_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the corresponding Orange Book listing submission document stored in Veeva Vault RIM (Regulatory Information Management). Enables traceability between the data record and the source regulatory document.']

  Note: 'Records of patents and exclusivities listed in the FDA Orange Book (Approved Drug Products with Therapeutic Equivalence Evaluations) for each NDA/ANDA product. Captures NDA number, product name, active ingredient, dosage form, strength, listed patent number, patent expiry date, exclusivity code, exclusivity expiry date, and listing status. Directly governs generic entry timelines and Paragraph IV challenge eligibility. Analogous listings for EU (Article 10 reference) and other markets are also captured.'
}

Table intellectual.licensed_patent_schedule {
  licensed_patent_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the licensed_patent_schedule association']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the licensing agreement under which this patent is licensed. Identifies the deal, parties, territory, and financial terms governing this patent\'s inclusion.']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific patent covered under this licensing agreement. Identifies the IP asset whose rights are being granted.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this specific patent\'s coverage under the licensing agreement becomes operative. May differ from the agreement\'s overall effective_date when patents are added to an existing agreement via amendment (e.g., a continuation patent granted after deal signing is added to the schedule mid-term).']
  exclusivity_type varchar [note: 'Scope of exclusivity granted for this specific patent under this agreement. A single licensing agreement may grant exclusive rights to one patent and non-exclusive rights to another in the same schedule. Cannot reside on licensing_agreement (which captures overall deal exclusivity) or on patent (which may be licensed exclusively to one partner and non-exclusively to another).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this patent\'s coverage under the agreement terminates. May be driven by the patent\'s own expiry, the agreement\'s expiry, or a negotiated earlier termination for this specific patent. Enables LOE planning at the patent-agreement intersection level.']
  licensed_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative or structured description of which patent claims, fields of use, or indications are licensed under this agreement for this specific patent. A patent may be partially licensed (e.g., only method-of-use claims, not composition claims). Cannot reside on the agreement (varies per patent) or on the patent (varies per agreement).']
  royalty_rate_per_patent decimal [note: 'The royalty rate (expressed as a percentage of net sales or a fixed per-unit amount) applicable specifically to this patent under this agreement. In multi-patent deals, individual patents may carry different royalty rates reflecting their relative commercial importance. Cannot reside on licensing_agreement (which may aggregate rates) or on patent (which may be licensed at different rates to different partners).']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this patent\'s inclusion in the licensed patent schedule. Supports tracking of patents added, removed, or suspended from coverage during the agreement term without requiring deletion of the historical record.']
  sublicensing_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensee is contractually permitted to sublicense this specific patent to third parties under this agreement. Sublicensing rights may be granted for some patents in a schedule but not others, particularly where the licensor retains tighter control over foundational composition-of-matter patents.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Contract between licensing_agreement and patent. It captures the formal schedule of intellectual property assets covered under each licensing agreement, recording the specific patents licensed, the per-patent royalty rate, the scope of licensed claims, exclusivity type, and sublicensing rights for each patent-agreement combination. Each record links one licensing_agreement to one patent with attributes that exist only in the context of this contractual relationship — they cannot reside on the agreement (which spans multiple patents) nor on the patent (which may be licensed under multiple agreements with different terms).. Existence Justification: In pharmaceutical IP management, a single licensing agreement routinely covers a portfolio of multiple patents (e.g., a composition-of-matter patent plus a method-of-use patent plus a formulation patent), and a single patent can be licensed under multiple separate agreements to different licensees in different territories or fields of use. The business actively manages this relationship as a \'Licensed Patent Schedule\' — a formal exhibit attached to every licensing agreement that lists each covered patent with its own royalty rate, effective dates, and exclusivity scope. This is not an analytical correlation; it is an operationally managed, legally binding schedule that deal managers create, amend, and terminate throughout the agreement lifecycle.'
}

Table finance.general_ledger {
  general_ledger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each general ledger account balance record in the Silver layer. Serves as the primary key for this data product.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: GL accounts are defined within a chart of accounts — fundamental master data relationship for account maintenance, financial statement mapping, and SOX compliance reporting in pharma ERP systems.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: GL accounts are assigned to cost centers for R&D cost tracking, overhead allocation, and GMP manufacturing cost reporting. The plain cost_center column is denormalized; FK enables proper cost accounti']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Each GL account belongs to a legal entity (company code) for statutory financial reporting, intercompany reconciliation, and IFRS/US-GAAP consolidation. Pharma finance experts expect every GL account ']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.profit_center. Business justification: GL accounts are assigned to profit centers for segment P&L reporting by therapeutic area in pharma. The plain profit_center column is a denormalized code; proper FK enables segment reporting and manag']
  account_group varchar [note: 'Account group code that controls the number range and field selection for the GL account (e.g., \'OPEX\', \'CAPX\', \'COGS\', \'RDEX\'). Used to segment accounts by business function such as R&D expense, COGS, or capital expenditure in pharmaceutical reporting.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the GL account master record: active (postings allowed), blocked (postings temporarily prevented), or marked_for_deletion (pending archival). Supports SOX-compliant account master data governance.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'SAP account type classification: A=Assets, D=Customers (Accounts Receivable), K=Vendors (Accounts Payable), M=Materials, S=General Ledger. Determines posting rules and balance sheet/income statement classification.']
  balance_sheet_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the GL account is a balance sheet account (True) or a profit and loss (P&L) income statement account (False). Balance sheet accounts carry forward balances; P&L accounts are reset at year-end. Critical for financial statement classification.']
  business_area varchar [note: 'SAP business area code used for internal segment reporting across legal entities. Supports cross-company-code P&L and balance sheet reporting by business division (e.g., Pharmaceuticals, Consumer Health, Vaccines).']
  chart_of_accounts varchar [note: 'Identifier for the chart of accounts framework applied to this GL account (e.g., \'INT1\' for international, \'USGP\' for US GAAP). Supports multi-GAAP reporting across the pharmaceutical group.']
  closing_balance decimal(28,6) [note: 'The GL account balance at the end of the fiscal period in transaction currency, calculated as opening balance plus debits minus credits (or vice versa depending on account type). Authoritative balance for trial balance, balance sheet, and income statement reporting.']
  closing_balance_gc decimal(28,6) [note: 'The GL account closing balance translated into the group consolidation currency. Used for consolidated financial statements, group P&L reporting, and multi-entity consolidation eliminations.']
  closing_balance_lc decimal(28,6) [note: 'The GL account closing balance translated into the company code local currency. Used for statutory reporting and local GAAP compliance in each legal entity\'s jurisdiction.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code representing the legal entity or organizational unit for which the ledger balance is recorded. Aligns with the legal entity structure of the pharmaceutical group for statutory and consolidated reporting.']
  company_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the local (company code) currency in which balances are reported for statutory purposes. Distinct from transaction currency to support currency translation and consolidation eliminations.']
  credit_amount decimal(28,6) [note: 'Total credit postings to the GL account during the fiscal period in transaction currency. Sourced from aggregated journal entry postings in SAP FI. Used in trial balance and double-entry bookkeeping validation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the transaction currency in which the GL balance amounts are originally recorded (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, GBP). Required for multi-currency pharmaceutical operations across global markets.']
  debit_amount decimal(28,6) [note: 'Total debit postings to the GL account during the fiscal period in transaction currency. Sourced from aggregated journal entry postings in SAP FI. Used in trial balance and double-entry bookkeeping validation.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period number (1–16, where periods 13–16 are special closing periods) within the fiscal year for which the balance is recorded. Supports period-end close, monthly P&L reporting, and SOX-compliant financial close tracking.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year (e.g., 2024) for which the GL account balance is recorded. Aligns with the company\'s fiscal year variant as configured in SAP. Used for annual financial close, SOX compliance, and year-over-year comparisons.']
  fs_item varchar [note: 'Financial statement item code mapping the GL account to a specific line in the balance sheet or income statement (e.g., P&L by therapeutic area, balance sheet line). Supports multi-GAAP consolidation and statutory reporting.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'SAP functional area code classifying the GL account balance by business function (e.g., \'MFGC\' for manufacturing/COGS, \'RDEX\' for R&D expense, \'SELL\' for selling expense, \'GADM\' for general and administrative). Supports cost-of-goods-sold (COGS) and P&L by function reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MFGC|RDEX|SELL|GADM|CLIN|REGL|PROC — promote to reference product]']
  gaap_standard varchar [note: 'The accounting standard under which this ledger balance is reported (US_GAAP, IFRS, LOCAL_GAAP, or IFRS_AND_US_GAAP for dual-reporting ledgers). Supports multi-GAAP reporting requirements for the pharmaceutical group\'s global legal entities.']
  gl_account_name varchar [note: 'Short descriptive name of the GL account as defined in the chart of accounts (e.g., \'Research and Development Expense\', \'API Inventory\', \'Accrued Clinical Trial Costs\'). Used in financial reports and trial balance outputs.']
  gl_account_number varchar [note: 'The chart-of-accounts account number uniquely identifying the GL account within the company code. Used as the primary business identifier for account lookup, trial balance, and financial statement mapping.']
  group_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the group (consolidation) currency used in consolidated financial statements (e.g., USD for a US-headquartered pharmaceutical group). Supports group-level P&L and balance sheet consolidation.']
  intercompany_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account balance contains intercompany transactions (True) that require elimination during group consolidation. Supports consolidation eliminations for the pharmaceutical group\'s multi-entity structure.']
  last_posted_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which a journal entry posting was made to this GL account within the fiscal period. Used for account activity monitoring, dormant account identification, and period-end close validation.']
  opening_balance decimal(28,6) [note: 'The GL account balance at the start of the fiscal period in transaction currency. Represents the carried-forward closing balance from the prior period. Used in trial balance preparation and period-end close processes.']
  period_close_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the financial period close process for this GL account balance: open (period active), in_progress (close activities underway), closed (period-end close completed), or locked (period locked against further postings). Supports SOX-compliant financial close tracking.']
  posting_block boolean [note: 'Indicates whether postings to this GL account are currently blocked (True) at the company code level. A blocked account prevents new journal entries, used during period-end close or account restructuring.']
  rd_capitalization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the GL account balance is subject to R&D capitalization rules under IFRS IAS 38 (True) or expensed as incurred under US GAAP ASC 730 (False). Critical for pharmaceutical companies managing the boundary between R&D expense and intangible asset capitalization.']
  recon_account_type varchar [note: 'Indicates if this GL account is a reconciliation account and its type: D=Accounts Receivable (Customers), K=Accounts Payable (Vendors), A=Assets, or empty for non-reconciliation accounts. Reconciliation accounts aggregate subledger balances into the GL.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this GL account balance record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and SOX-compliant record-keeping requirements.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this GL account balance record was last modified in the Silver layer data product. Supports incremental data loading, change tracking, and SOX-compliant audit trail requirements.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Segment identifier for IFRS 8 / ASC 280 segment reporting (e.g., \'Oncology\', \'Immunology\', \'Rare Disease\', \'Consumer Health\'). Enables disaggregated P&L and asset reporting by operating segment for external financial disclosures.']
  sox_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account is in scope for SOX Section 302/404 internal controls over financial reporting (ICFR). SOX-relevant accounts require documented controls, testing, and sign-off during the annual audit cycle.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'SAP tax code associated with the GL account for VAT/GST and withholding tax determination. Relevant for pharmaceutical entities operating across multiple tax jurisdictions. Supports indirect tax reporting and compliance.']
  trading_partner varchar [note: 'Company code of the intercompany trading partner for balances involving intercompany transactions. Used to identify and eliminate intercompany balances during group consolidation. Populated only when intercompany_flag is True.']

  Note: 'Central repository for all financial accounting entries across the enterprise, representing the authoritative chart of accounts, account balances, and period-end ledger positions. Captures account master data (account number, account type, account group, currency), period balances (opening, debit totals, credit totals, closing balance by period), company code, profit center allocations, cost center references, and currency translations as sourced from SAP FI module. Serves as SSOT for trial balance, balance sheet, income statement, and SOX-compliant period-end close processes across all legal entities in the pharmaceutical group. Supports consolidation eliminations, multi-GAAP reporting (US GAAP and IFRS), and financial close status tracking. Does NOT own individual postings (owned by journal_entry) — owns aggregate balances and account structure.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each journal entry record in the general ledger. Primary key for the journal_entry data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  accounts_payable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_payable. Business justification: Journal entries generated by AP postings should reference the source AP document. This is a nullable FK since not all journal entries are AP-related. Strengthens accounts_payable connections to journa']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: Cost center on journal entries drives R&D capitalization decisions, overhead absorption, and pharma program cost reporting. The plain cost_center column is denormalized; FK enables cost center hierarc']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Journal entries related to fixed asset transactions (acquisition, depreciation, disposal, impairment) should reference the specific asset. This is a nullable FK since not all journal entries are asset']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Journal entries post to specific GL accounts. Each JE line item must reference the GL account it impacts. The existing gl_account field is a STRING code; adding general_ledger_id establishes the prope']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Journal entries that charge costs to internal orders should reference the internal order. This is a nullable FK since not all journal entries are internal-order-related. Connects internal_order to jou']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.invoice. Business justification: Invoice posting in SAP FI creates journal entries. Linking journal_entry to invoice provides direct traceability from accounting transactions to the originating invoice document. This supports revenue']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Journal entries are posted within a legal entity for statutory reporting, intercompany elimination, and tax filing. Pharma companies post entries across multiple legal entities; this FK is required fo']
  payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.payment_run. Business justification: Payment runs in SAP FI generate clearing journal entries when vendor payments are executed. Linking journal_entry to payment_run enables reconciliation between payment execution records and the result']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.profit_center. Business justification: Profit center on journal entries is required for segment P&L reporting by therapeutic area and product line. The plain profit_center column is denormalized; FK enables management reporting and IFRS 8 ']
  royalty_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.royalty_agreement. Business justification: Journal entries are posted for royalty accruals, royalty payments, and sublicense fee recognition. Linking journal_entry directly to royalty_agreement enables traceability from individual accounting t']
  amount_group_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the journal entry translated into the corporate group reporting currency (typically USD or EUR) for consolidated P&L reporting by therapeutic area, brand, and geography.']
  amount_local_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the journal entry translated into the company code\'s local (functional) currency using the applicable exchange rate. Used for statutory financial reporting and local tax compliance.']
  amount_transaction_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary amount of the journal entry in the original transaction currency. For debit entries this is positive; for credit entries this is negative (SAP sign convention). Core monetary fact for the posting.']
  assignment_field varchar [note: 'SAP assignment field (ZUONR) used for sorting and matching open items in accounts payable, accounts receivable, and bank reconciliation. Typically populated with invoice number, payment reference, or contract number for automated clearing.']
  business_area varchar [note: 'SAP business area code representing an organizational unit for which an internal balance sheet and P&L can be drawn up. Used for segment reporting across geographies and therapeutic areas.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code identifying the legal entity for which the journal entry is posted. Enables P&L reporting by legal entity and supports multi-entity consolidation across the pharmaceutical group.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'SAP indicator specifying whether the line item is a debit (S=Soll/Debit) or credit (H=Haben/Credit) posting. Fundamental double-entry bookkeeping attribute required for GL balance validation and trial balance generation.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date of the original source document (invoice date, payment date, contract date) that triggered the journal entry. May differ from posting_date; used for aging analysis and vendor/customer terms calculation.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'SAP-assigned unique accounting document number within the company code and fiscal year. Serves as the externally-known business identifier for the journal entry, used in audit trails and reconciliation.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'SAP document type code classifying the nature of the journal entry (e.g., SA=General Ledger, KR=Vendor Invoice, DR=Customer Invoice, AA=Asset Posting, ZP=Payment Clearing, ML=Material Ledger). Drives posting rules and account determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SA|KR|DR|AA|ZP|ML|AB|KZ|DZ|WA|WE|RE — promote to reference product]']
  entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the journal entry record was created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Foreign currency exchange rate applied to translate the transaction currency amount to local currency at the time of posting. Sourced from SAP exchange rate tables per the applicable rate type (e.g., M=standard, B=bank buying rate).']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month 01–12, plus special periods 13–16 for year-end adjustments) within the fiscal year. Drives period-end close processes, P&L reporting by period, and financial close calendar management.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which the journal entry belongs, as defined in the SAP fiscal year variant. Used for annual financial reporting, R&D capitalization tracking, and SOX compliance period assignment.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'SAP functional area classifying the business function of the posting (e.g., R&D, Manufacturing, Sales & Distribution, General & Administrative). Supports cost-of-goods-sold (COGS) analysis and functional P&L reporting per IFRS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: R&D|Manufacturing|Sales|Marketing|G&A|Regulatory|Medical Affairs|Supply Chain — promote to reference product]']
  gl_account varchar [note: 'SAP GL account number to which the journal entry line is posted. Determines the financial statement line item (P&L or balance sheet). Supports COGS by product, R&D capitalization, and therapeutic area P&L analysis.']
  header_text varchar [note: 'Free-text description at the journal entry header level explaining the business purpose of the posting (e.g., \'Q3 R&D accrual – Oncology Phase III\', \'API procurement – CMO settlement\'). Supports audit review and financial close documentation.']
  is_reversal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this journal entry is itself a reversal of a prior posting (True) or an original entry (False). Used to filter reversals from net financial reporting and to identify accrual reversal patterns in period-end close.']
  last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the journal entry was last modified or reversed. Supports change audit trail requirements under SOX Section 404 and 21 CFR Part 11 for electronic records integrity.']
  line_item_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line item number within the journal entry document (e.g., 001, 002). Uniquely identifies each debit/credit line within the document for reconciliation, audit, and subledger matching.']
  line_item_text varchar [note: 'Free-text description at the individual line item level providing context for the specific debit or credit posting (e.g., \'API batch #XYZ123 – COGS\', \'Phase III trial accrual – CRO milestone\'). Supports granular audit review.']
  local_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the company code\'s local (functional) currency. Used for statutory reporting in the country of the legal entity.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the journal entry is posted to the general ledger. Determines the fiscal period and year to which the transaction is assigned. Critical for period-end close and financial reporting.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the journal entry in the general ledger lifecycle. \'posted\' = fully committed to GL; \'parked\' = saved but not posted; \'held\' = incomplete draft; \'reversed\' = offset entry created; \'cleared\' = matched against open items; \'blocked\' = pending approval.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External reference number from the source document (e.g., vendor invoice number, purchase order number, contract reference, or regulatory submission reference). Enables cross-system reconciliation and audit traceability.']
  reversal_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the reversing journal entry if this posting has been reversed. Populated only for reversed entries; null otherwise. Supports audit trail completeness and period-end close validation.']
  subledger_type varchar [note: 'Identifies the subledger from which the journal entry originates: AP (Accounts Payable), AR (Accounts Receivable), asset accounting, tax accounting, material ledger, or none (direct GL posting). Enables subledger-to-GL reconciliation.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The calculated tax amount (VAT, GST, sales tax) in local currency for the journal entry line. Supports indirect tax return filing, tax provision calculations, and multi-jurisdiction tax compliance reporting.']
  tax_base_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The taxable base amount in local currency on which the tax is calculated. Required for indirect tax return preparation and multi-jurisdiction VAT/GST compliance reporting.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'SAP tax code identifying the applicable tax rate and type (e.g., VAT, sales tax, withholding tax) for the posting. Drives automatic tax calculation and links to tax jurisdiction for multi-jurisdiction indirect tax compliance.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the tax jurisdiction (country, state, county, city) applicable to the transaction for multi-jurisdiction indirect tax reporting. Required for US sales tax (Vertex/Avalara integration) and international VAT compliance.']
  tax_return_period varchar [note: 'The reporting period (YYYY-MM) to which this tax posting belongs for indirect tax return filing purposes. May differ from the accounting fiscal period; used for VAT/GST return preparation across multiple tax jurisdictions.']
  transaction_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the original transaction (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, GBP). The currency in which the business transaction was originally denominated, before conversion to local or group currency.']
  vat_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the tax posting type for indirect tax reporting: input VAT (recoverable on purchases), output VAT (collected on sales), deferred VAT (timing difference), exempt, zero-rated, or not applicable. Supports multi-jurisdiction VAT compliance.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of withholding tax (WHT) deducted at source for cross-border payments to vendors, royalty recipients, or service providers. Relevant for pharmaceutical royalty accounting, CRO/CMO payments, and international tax treaty compliance.']

  Note: 'Individual accounting transactions posted to the general ledger with full header and line-item detail, including tax-specific postings. Header captures posting date, document type, reference number, fiscal year/period, currency, and posting user. Line items capture GL account, cost center, profit center, debit/credit amounts in transaction and local currency, tax code, tax jurisdiction, taxable base amount, tax amount, withholding tax, assignment field, and line item text. Encompasses all subledger postings (AP, AR, asset, tax) and manual adjustments. Tax postings carry additional attributes: tax jurisdiction, tax return reference, VAT/sales tax/deferred tax classification, and indirect tax reporting period for multi-jurisdiction compliance. Supports audit trails required under SOX and 21 CFR Part 11, financial close processes, and granular P&L analysis by therapeutic area, brand, and geography.'
}

Table finance.internal_order {
  internal_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the SAP CO internal order record in the Databricks Silver layer. Primary key for this data product.']
  drug_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Internal orders are created for drug product CMC development, stability studies, and tech transfer activities. Linking internal_order to drug_product enables product-level R&D cost tracking and capita']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Internal orders for R&D projects are created within a legal entity for capitalization eligibility, transfer pricing, and statutory cost reporting. Pharma R&D orders must be entity-attributed for tax c']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: Internal orders are owned by organizational units (R&D departments, manufacturing divisions) for program management reporting and budget allocation. Pharma R&D program tracking requires org unit attri']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility associated with this internal order. Relevant for CAPEX orders tied to facility investments or manufacturing overhead orders. Corresponds to WERKS in SAP AUFK.']
  primary_internal_responsible_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center organizationally responsible for managing and monitoring this internal order. Corresponds to KOSTV in SAP AUFK. Used for P&L reporting by therapeutic area and organizational unit.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the profit center associated with this internal order, enabling P&L reporting by therapeutic area, brand, or geography. Supports management accounting and ROI analysis for drug development programs.']
  therapeutic_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: Internal orders in pharma R&D are organized by therapeutic area for cost allocation, budget variance reporting, and portfolio investment tracking. therapeutic_area on internal_order is a plain text de']
  actual_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative actual costs posted to this internal order to date in the controlling area currency. Sourced from SAP CO actual cost line items (COEP table). Used for budget vs. actual reporting, R&D spend tracking, and CAPEX lifecycle governance.']
  actual_finish_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the internal order activity was completed (technically completed or closed). Used for variance analysis between planned and actual project duration and for R&D capitalization period determination.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved budget allocated to this internal order in the controlling area currency. Represents the authorized spending limit for the R&D program, capital project, or overhead activity. Used for budget vs. actual variance analysis and availability control.']
  budget_profile varchar [note: 'SAP CO budget profile code assigned to this internal order, defining the budgeting rules, availability control tolerance levels, and fiscal year distribution method. Corresponds to PROFL in SAP AUFK.']
  capitalization_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether costs accumulated on this internal order are eligible for capitalization as an intangible asset under IAS 38 or ASC 350. Typically set to True for Phase 3 clinical development orders meeting the six IAS 38 criteria. Drives R&D capitalization tracking and CAPEX lifecycle governance.']
  commitment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total open commitment value (purchase orders, contracts, and reservations) posted against this internal order but not yet invoiced. Represents future obligated spend. Sourced from SAP CO commitment management (COOI table). Critical for total exposure reporting.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI company code representing the legal entity under which this internal order is managed. Enables multi-entity financial consolidation and statutory reporting. Corresponds to BUKRS in SAP AUFK.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'SAP CO controlling area code that defines the organizational unit within which cost accounting is performed. Determines the currency and fiscal year variant applicable to this internal order. Corresponds to KOKRS in SAP AUFK.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the internal order record was first created in SAP CO. Corresponds to ERDAT and ERZET fields in SAP AUFK. Used for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the controlling area currency in which budget, actual, commitment, and plan amounts are expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). Corresponds to WAERS in SAP AUFK.']
  deletion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this internal order has been flagged for deletion in SAP CO. Deletion-flagged orders are excluded from active reporting but retained for audit trail purposes. Corresponds to LOEKZ in SAP AUFK.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'SAP fiscal year variant code defining the fiscal year and period structure applicable to this internal order (e.g., K4 for calendar year, V3 for April–March). Determines period-end processing and budget distribution. Corresponds to PERIV in SAP AUFK.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'SAP functional area classifying the business function of the internal order (e.g., Research and Development, Manufacturing, Sales and Marketing, General and Administrative). Supports P&L by function and R&D capitalization under ASC 730 / IAS 38. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RD|MFG|SGA|MKTG|CLIN|REG|QA — promote to reference product]']
  investment_measure_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the internal order as a capital expenditure (CAPEX), operating expense (OPEX), R&D expense (RD_EXPENSE), or capitalizable R&D (RD_CAPITAL) measure. Drives balance sheet vs. P&L treatment and supports CAPEX lifecycle governance and ROI analysis.']
  last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to this internal order record in SAP CO. Corresponds to AEDAT and AEZET fields in SAP AUFK. Used for change tracking and SOX audit trail.']
  long_description varchar [note: 'Extended narrative description of the internal order providing full business context, scope, and objectives. Supports R&D capitalization documentation and audit trail requirements under SOX and 21 CFR Part 11.']
  order_category varchar [note: 'SAP internal order category code distinguishing overhead orders (01), investment orders (04), accrual orders (02), and statistical orders (10/20). Drives settlement and budget management behavior in SAP CO.']
  order_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable short description of the internal order, typically identifying the R&D program, clinical trial phase, capital project, or overhead activity being tracked. Corresponds to KTEXT in SAP AUFK.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'SAP-assigned alphanumeric identifier for the internal order, used as the externally-known business key across finance, R&D, and project management systems. Corresponds to the AUFNR field in SAP CO table AUFK.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current system status of the internal order in SAP CO. CRTD=Created, REL=Released (costs can be posted), TECO=Technically Completed (no further postings), CLSD=Closed (settled and locked), DLT=Deletion Flagged, LKD=Locked. Governs cost posting eligibility and settlement processing.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'SAP order type code classifying the purpose of the internal order (e.g., R&D expense, capital expenditure, overhead, clinical trial cost collection, manufacturing overhead). Corresponds to AUART in SAP AUFK. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RD01|CAPEX|OVHD|CLIN|MFG|MKTG — promote to reference product]']
  overhead_key varchar [note: 'SAP CO overhead key assigned to this internal order, determining the overhead surcharge rates applied during overhead calculation (costing sheet). Used for allocating indirect manufacturing or R&D overhead costs to the order. Corresponds to ZUONR in SAP AUFK.']
  plan_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total planned cost amount for this internal order in the controlling area currency, as entered in SAP CO planning. Supports budget vs. plan vs. actual three-way variance analysis for R&D program financial governance.']
  planned_finish_date date [note: 'Planned completion date for the activity or project represented by this internal order. Used for R&D program milestone tracking, CAPEX project scheduling, and budget period governance. Corresponds to PLSEZ in SAP AUFK.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date for the activity or project represented by this internal order. Used for timeline planning of R&D programs, clinical trials, and capital projects. Corresponds to PLFAZ in SAP AUFK.']
  program_phase varchar [note: 'Drug development lifecycle phase associated with this internal order. Drives R&D capitalization eligibility (costs may be capitalized from Phase 3 under IAS 38 criteria), ROI analysis, and budget governance. Aligns with ICH M4 eCTD structure.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date on which the internal order was released in SAP CO (status changed to REL), enabling cost postings to begin. Marks the start of the active cost collection period. Corresponds to IDAT2 in SAP AUFK.']
  settlement_receiver_type varchar [note: 'Category of the primary settlement receiver for this internal order. CTR=Cost Center, AUC=Asset Under Construction, WBS=WBS Element, PSG=Profitability Segment, GL=G/L Account, ORD=Internal Order. Determines the accounting treatment of settled costs.']
  settlement_rule_type varchar [note: 'Defines how costs accumulated on this internal order are settled to receivers (cost centers, G/L accounts, assets, WBS elements, profitability segments). FUL=Full settlement, PER=Periodic settlement, MAN=Manual settlement. Governs period-end cost allocation and R&D capitalization postings.']
  statistical_order_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this internal order is a statistical order (costs posted in parallel for reporting purposes only, without affecting the primary cost object). Statistical orders are used for management reporting overlays such as therapeutic area P&L without impacting actual cost flows.']

  Note: 'SAP CO internal order used to collect, monitor, and settle costs for specific projects, R&D programs, clinical trials, or capital expenditure initiatives. Captures order type (R&D, capex, overhead), settlement rule, budget profile, actual costs, commitment values, responsible cost center, project/WBS reference, and order lifecycle status. References milestone_payment records for contractual obligations but does NOT own milestone payment terms or trigger events. Supports R&D capitalization tracking, ROI analysis for drug development programs, and CAPEX lifecycle governance.'
}

Table finance.accounts_payable {
  accounts_payable_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each accounts payable record in the SAP FI-AP module, representing a single vendor invoice or payment obligation. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the vendor bank account used for payment execution, referencing the SAP FI-AP bank details record. Sensitive financial data subject to PCI and SOX controls.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal cost center (e.g., R&D, Manufacturing, Clinical Operations) to which the payable is allocated for P&L reporting by therapeutic area or function.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: AP transactions post to GL accounts (liability accounts, expense accounts). The existing gl_account_code is a STRING; adding general_ledger_id FK links AP to the GL account master record. This further']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: AP invoices are posted within a legal entity for statutory payables reporting, VAT filing, and intercompany AP reconciliation. Pharma companies manage AP across multiple legal entities requiring entit']
  payment_run_id varchar [note: 'The SAP automatic payment program run identifier (F110 run ID) that generated the payment for this invoice. Enables batch payment reconciliation and audit trail for automated payment processing.']
  royalty_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.royalty_agreement. Business justification: AP invoices for royalty payments (when company is licensee) should reference the governing royalty agreement for compliance, audit, and accrual tracking. Cardinality: Many AP invoices can reference on']
  vendor_business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: AP invoices are received from vendors who are business partners (CROs, CMOs, raw material suppliers). Pharma AP processing requires vendor master linkage for payment terms, GMP qualification status, a']
  warehouse_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.warehouse_receipt. Business justification: Three-way match (PO/GR/Invoice) is mandatory in pharma procure-to-pay. AP invoices are verified against goods receipts for quantity/quality confirmation before payment approval. Regulatory and SOX con']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP FI clearing document number (AUGBL) that offsets the open payable item upon payment or credit memo application. Used for open item management and reconciliation.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The SAP FI company code (BUKRS) representing the legal entity within the pharmaceutical group that is the obligor for this payable. Supports intercompany reconciliation and legal entity P&L reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the accounts payable record was first created in the SAP FI-AP system, used for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the vendor invoice (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). Supports multi-currency payables for global pharmaceutical supply chain and cross-border licensing transactions.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cash discount amount captured if payment is made within the discount period defined in the payment terms (e.g., 2% discount for payment within 10 days). Supports working capital optimization analytics.']
  discount_due_date date [note: 'The last date by which payment must be made to qualify for the early payment discount defined in the payment terms. Used by treasury for dynamic discounting and supply chain finance decisions.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment must be made to the vendor to satisfy the invoice obligation and avoid late payment penalties. Derived from invoice date and payment terms (e.g., Net 30, Net 60).']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The accounting period (MONAT, 1–16 including special periods) within the fiscal year in which the invoice is posted in SAP FI, used for monthly close and accrual management.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year (GJAHR) in which the invoice is posted in SAP FI, used for period-end close, annual financial reporting, and SOX compliance.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The SAP FI general ledger account (HKONT) to which the invoice line is posted, determining the expense or liability classification in the chart of accounts (e.g., R&D expense, COGS, API raw material).']
  goods_receipt_number varchar [note: 'The SAP MM goods receipt document number (MBLNR) confirming receipt of goods or services against the purchase order. Required for three-way match (PO-GR-Invoice) in GMP-compliant pharmaceutical procurement.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total gross amount of the vendor invoice before deduction of discounts, withholding tax, or other adjustments, as stated on the invoice document. Represents the full obligation amount.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date printed on the vendor invoice document (BLDAT in SAP), representing the principal business event date for the payable obligation. Used for aging analysis and payment term calculation.']
  invoice_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the goods or services invoiced, as provided by the vendor or entered during invoice processing in SAP FI-AP. Supports spend categorization and audit review.']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor invoice by business nature. Milestone and royalty types are specific to pharmaceutical licensing and CRO/CMO contracts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|credit_memo|debit_memo|milestone|royalty|advance|recurring — promote to reference product]']
  is_intercompany boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payable is an intercompany transaction between legal entities within the pharmaceutical group, requiring elimination in consolidated financial statements per IFRS 10.']
  is_rd_capitalized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the expenditure represented by this payable has been capitalized as an intangible asset under IAS 38 (development phase costs meeting capitalization criteria), as opposed to expensed immediately as R&D.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net amount payable to the vendor after deducting withholding tax and applicable discounts from the gross invoice amount. Represents the actual cash outflow obligation.']
  payment_block_indicator varchar [note: 'SAP payment block key (ZLSPR) indicating whether the invoice is blocked from automatic payment processing. Common values: R=Manual review required, B=Blocked by purchasing, A=Blocked for audit, V=Verified/released, blank=Not blocked.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment was executed and the invoice was cleared in SAP FI-AP. Used for cash flow reporting, days payable outstanding (DPO) calculation, and vendor relationship management.']
  payment_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP FI document number of the outgoing payment transaction that cleared this invoice (AUGBL). Populated upon payment execution; null for open/blocked invoices.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument used to settle the vendor invoice (e.g., ACH for domestic US transfers, SWIFT/wire for international payments, SEPA for EU transactions, check for legacy vendors).']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the accounts payable record indicating whether the invoice obligation is open, partially settled, fully paid, blocked for payment, cancelled, or under dispute. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|partially_paid|paid|blocked|cancelled|disputed — promote to reference product]']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'The SAP payment terms key (ZTERM) defining the agreed payment schedule, discount conditions, and due date calculation rules (e.g., NT30 = Net 30 days, 2/10NT30 = 2% discount if paid within 10 days).']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the invoice was posted to the SAP general ledger (BUDAT), determining the accounting period for financial reporting and period-end close.']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'The internal SAP FI document number (BELNR) assigned upon posting of the vendor invoice in the general ledger. Serves as the primary system-of-record identifier within SAP.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The value-added tax (VAT) or sales tax amount included in the vendor invoice, as calculated by SAP FI tax determination. Used for tax reporting and input tax recovery.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'The pharmaceutical therapeutic area (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Neuroscience) to which the payable is attributed, enabling P&L reporting and R&D spend analysis by therapeutic area as required by finance leadership.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the accounts payable record in SAP FI-AP, supporting audit trail requirements under SOX and 21 CFR Part 11.']
  vendor_category varchar [note: 'Pharmaceutical industry classification of the vendor type. Enables spend analytics by vendor category (e.g., CRO spend for clinical operations, CMO/CDMO spend for outsourced manufacturing, licensor for royalty and milestone obligations). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CRO|CMO|CDMO|raw_material_supplier|licensor|service_provider|logistics|other — promote to reference product]']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of withholding tax (WHT) deducted from the vendor payment, applicable to royalty payments, milestone payments to licensors, and cross-border service fees per local tax regulations.']

  Note: 'Vendor invoice, payment obligation, and payment execution records managed within SAP FI-AP module. Captures vendor invoice details (invoice number, date, due date, payment terms, gross amount, tax amount, withholding tax, payment block status), payment execution (payment document, payment date, payment method including ACH/wire/check, bank account, clearing document, payment run ID), and settlement status. Covers payables to CROs, CMOs, CDMOs, raw material suppliers, licensors (royalty and milestone payments), and service providers in the pharmaceutical supply chain.'
}

Table finance.accounts_receivable {
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each accounts receivable record in the SAP FI-AR module. Serves as the primary key for the silver layer lakehouse table. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer (wholesaler, distributor, hospital network, or government payer) associated with this receivable record. Maps to the SAP FI-AR customer master (debtor account).']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: AR records post to specific GL accounts (revenue, receivables). accounts_receivable has gl_account_code as a denormalized string. Adding general_ledger_id FK normalizes this to the canonical GL accoun']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: AR records belong to a legal entity for revenue recognition, statutory reporting, and intercompany AR elimination. Pharma revenue by legal entity is required for country-level market access and pricin']
  masterdata_ndc_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.masterdata_ndc_code. Business justification: AR line items in pharma are tied to specific NDC codes for product-level revenue recognition, chargeback reconciliation, and government price reporting (Medicaid best price). The plain product_ndc is ']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.profit_center. Business justification: AR is attributed to profit centers for therapeutic area revenue reporting and segment P&L. The plain profit_center column is denormalized; FK enables product-line revenue analytics required for pharma']
  royalty_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.royalty_agreement. Business justification: AR invoices for royalty receipts (when company is licensor) should reference the governing royalty agreement for revenue recognition and compliance. Cardinality: Many AR invoices can reference one roy']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: AR in pharma tracks revenue by SKU for gross-to-net calculations, chargeback processing, and revenue recognition by product. product_ndc is a denormalized text field on AR; replacing with sku_id FK en']
  ar_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the receivable. \'open\' = outstanding balance; \'partially_cleared\' = partial payment received; \'cleared\' = fully paid or offset; \'disputed\' = customer dispute raised; \'written_off\' = bad debt write-off applied; \'in_dunning\' = active dunning process initiated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|partially_cleared|cleared|disputed|written_off|in_dunning — promote to reference product]']
  billing_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer invoice was issued (billing document date). Used as the principal business event date for revenue recognition under ASC 606 / IFRS 15. Corresponds to SAP BLDAT.']
  chargeback_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total chargeback deductions claimed by wholesalers or distributors against this invoice, representing the difference between the contract price and the wholesale acquisition cost (WAC). Critical for pharmaceutical gross-to-net revenue calculations.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'Date on which this AR item was fully cleared (settled) in SAP FI-AR. Corresponds to SAP AUGDT. Null for open or partially cleared items. Used for DSO and cash flow reporting.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP document number of the clearing transaction (payment, credit memo, or offset) that fully or partially settled this AR item. Corresponds to SAP AUGBL field in BSAD. Null for open items.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP company code identifying the legal entity (pharmaceutical subsidiary or affiliate) that owns this AR document. Drives legal entity P&L reporting and SOX compliance segregation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this AR record was first created in the SAP FI-AR system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for audit trail and SOX compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the AR document (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). Represents the transaction currency in which the invoice was issued to the customer. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Business segment classification of the customer for this receivable. Supports P&L analysis by channel and revenue recognition reporting under ASC 606 / IFRS 15. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wholesaler|distributor|hospital_network|government_payer|retail_pharmacy|specialty_pharmacy — promote to reference product]']
  days_overdue varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days this AR item is past its due date as of the last refresh date. Calculated as (refresh_date - due_date) for open items. Used for aging bucket classification and credit risk assessment.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible SAP FI-AR accounting document number uniquely identifying this receivable transaction within the general ledger. Corresponds to BELNR in SAP BSID/BSAD tables.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the AR document: customer invoice, credit memo (e.g., chargebacks, rebates), debit memo, down payment request, incoming payment receipt, or revenue accrual entry. Maps to SAP document type (BLART). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_invoice|credit_memo|debit_memo|down_payment|payment_receipt|accrual — promote to reference product]']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the customer is contractually required to remit payment per the agreed payment terms. Calculated from billing date and payment terms. Used for dunning and aging analysis.']
  dunning_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent dunning notice was issued to the customer for this overdue AR item. Corresponds to SAP MAHND. Used to track collections activity and avoid duplicate dunning.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Current dunning escalation level (0–4) assigned to this AR item by the SAP dunning program. Level 0 = no dunning; higher levels indicate increasing overdue severity. Drives collections escalation for wholesalers and distributors.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Foreign exchange rate applied at posting date to translate the document currency amount to local currency. Sourced from SAP exchange rate table (TCURR). Required for multi-currency pharmaceutical operations.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which this AR document was posted in SAP FI-AR. Used for period-end close, SOX financial reporting, and P&L by therapeutic area and geography.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross amount of the AR document before tax deductions, chargebacks, or rebate adjustments. Represents the face value of the customer invoice in the document currency. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this AR record in the SAP FI-AR system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for change tracking and SOX audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net invoice amount translated into the company code local currency (e.g., USD for US entity) using the SAP exchange rate at posting date. Required for consolidated P&L reporting and SOX compliance.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter code for the company code local currency used for translation of the AR document amount (e.g., USD for US legal entity).']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net receivable amount after deducting tax from the gross amount. Represents the revenue-recognized value for ASC 606 / IFRS 15 reporting. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET.']
  open_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining unpaid balance on this AR document at the time of last update. Equals gross_amount minus all clearing payments and credit memos applied. Used for aging reports and collections management.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the customer remitted payment (e.g., bank transfer, ACH, wire transfer, check). Maps to SAP payment method key (ZLSCH). Used for cash application and treasury reconciliation.']
  payment_receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which the incoming customer payment was received and applied against this AR document. Null if no payment has been received. Used for Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) calculation.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP payment terms key (e.g., NT30, NT60, 2/10NET30) defining the discount period, discount percentage, and net due date for this receivable. Drives due date calculation and early payment discount eligibility.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the AR document was posted to the general ledger in SAP FI. Determines the accounting period for financial reporting. Corresponds to SAP BUDAT.']
  posting_period varchar [note: 'Accounting period (01–12 for regular months, 13–16 for special closing periods) within the fiscal year in which this AR document was posted. Aligns with SAP FI period control.']
  rebate_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual rebate amount accrued or applied against this AR document for government payers (Medicaid, Medicare) or commercial payer contracts. Part of gross-to-net revenue deductions under ASC 606.']
  recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which revenue was or is scheduled to be recognized in the income statement per ASC 606 / IFRS 15 performance obligation criteria. May differ from billing date for deferred or accrued revenue entries.']
